english_verse: “A virgin will have a baby boy, and he will be called Immanuel,” which means “God is with us.”
translation_verse: “Di ke, ɔdisami gi óte onyime e, aakɔ̀ limɛ ɛ, gi aapɔ obinyime e, lɛ biaxwa yɛ si Imanuel,” gi lɛ ese linu si “Aya ɔlɛ ni blɔ.”

english_verse: From David to the time of the exile in Babylonia, the ancestors of Jesus were: David, Solomon , Rehoboam, Abijah, Asa, Jehoshaphat, Jehoram; Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, Manasseh, Amon, Josiah, and Jehoiachin and his brothers.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yosia apɔ Yexonya ni yɛ beneminyime ba ní ba Babilonia aboyo la me kuzezeo lipoe le me e.

english_verse: From the exile to the birth of Jesus, his ancestors were: Jehoiachin, Shealtiel, Zerubbabel, Abiud, Eliakim, Azor, Zadok, Achim; Eliud, Eleazar, Matthan, Jacob, and Joseph, the husband of Mary, the mother of Jesus, who is called the Messiah.
translation_verse: Nite si Yakob apɔ Yosef. Yosef yedze eze Maria a. Lɛ Maria ɛ, yɛ apɔ Yesu gi yɛ onu Kristo e.

english_verse: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Azur apɔ Zadok, Zadok apɔ Yaxin, lɛ Yaxin apɔ Elihud i.

english_verse: This is how Jesus Christ was born. A young woman named Mary was engaged to Joseph from King David's family. But before they were married, she learned that she was going to have a baby by God's Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo yɛ kupɔpɔɔ mɛ eboe la ɛtrɛ nite: Beʋi yene Maria litukpo ɛki Yosef. Pɔ xe ableke biakpɛ likpɔ ɛ, amɔ si yɛ akɔ limɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ kooko.

english_verse: Joseph was a good man and did not want to embarrass Mary in front of everyone. So he decided to quietly call off the wedding.
translation_verse: Yenye Yosef eze ɔnɔ gi abitɛ ní Aya yɛ isã nɛ aʋa a, niteme si ɔ́pɛ si yikpɛ ɔdzɛɛ kuye e ní banima aŋu mɛ. Lɛ loso e, atrɔ si yɛ ayurɔ yɛ ní kikpokpo me.

english_verse: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
translation_verse: Agi azeegyo eboa la su edi i, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu isu ya abasɛ Yosef ní kulila mɛ. Lɛ akpɛ ɔna ní yɛ lipo si, “Yosef, Dawid yebie, wukíse lifu ní Maria kugbanɔɔ su u, lɛ ese linu si yɛ limɛ nɛ ɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ lidɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Then after her baby is born, name him Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.”
translation_verse: Atrápɔ obinyime gi biaxwa yɛ si Yesu lɛ ese linu si yɛ atrábu yɛ benete ba ní ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Kɔ eboa la ya pɔ́ ɛba la mɛ, tɔ biakɔ bloni liboe lɛ gi Aya apani aplɛ gani yɛ oboedoplɛɛ aʋa sì,

english_verse: After Joseph woke up, he and Mary were soon married, just as the Lord's angel had told him to do.
translation_verse: Agi Yosef enyime ní sra sɛ mɛ ɛ, abitɛ ní Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ yɛ liboe le aʋa. Niteme si agbani Maria.

english_verse: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
translation_verse: Pɔ ɛ, yɛ ni yɛ béze edi zɛkɔ nani lipoe lɛ gi apɔ yebinyime tɔpu yɛ pɔ́ ɛ. Lɛ Yosef axwa obi ye linyi si Yesu i.

english_verse: When Jesus was born in the village of Bethlehem in Judea, Herod was king. During this time some wise men from the east came to Jerusalem
translation_verse: Agi bɛpɔ Yesu ní Betlehem ní Yudea, ní Okusi ye Herodes lipoe le me e, beboete ba bɛdɔ ní kewetu ya ɛba Yerusalem, lɛ beʋi sì

english_verse: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
translation_verse: “Nifɔ kwiamɔ Yudanɛ ma Okusi ye gi bɛpɔ ɔ? Kwimɔ yɛ liŋui nɛ, lɛ kwiba si kwitrakpɛ litukpo kesea ɛki yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When King Herod heard about this, he was worried, and so was everyone else in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Agi Okusi ye Herodes enu liboe lɛ ya ɛ, yɛ ɔna nɔ ɛhwa. Lɛ Yerusalem banɛ ma pɔ́ fɛ ɛ, liboe le lihwa ni ba a.

english_verse: Herod brought together the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses and asked them, “Where will the Messiah be born?”
translation_verse: Lɛ axwa kusɔliɔ baka ba petee ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba pɔ́ akpɛ likpɔ, lɛ apamí liboe le me nì ba si, “Nifɔ biapɔ Kristo ye?”

english_verse: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Ní Betlehem, ní Yudea, lɛ ese linu si, Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ aŋwlimi a plɛ si:

english_verse: ‘Bethlehem in the land of Judea, you are very important among the towns of Judea. From your town will come a leader, who will be like a shepherd for my people Israel.’ ”
translation_verse: ‘Lɛ wɔ Betlehem ní Yuda kesea aʋa ɛ, wɔ wonu ɔma sisami nɔ ní Yuda ikusima nɛ kusi; pɔ lɛ wɔ mɛ ɔnɔgbanɔɛ gi yɛ atrá gbani mɛ Israel ɔma nɔ aadɔ.’ ”

english_verse: Herod secretly called in the wise men and asked them when they had first seen the star.
translation_verse: Lɛ Herodes axwa beboete ba tsinɔ ní kutsatsa mɛ ní kikpokpo me, lɛ apami lipoe lɛ klɔ gi liŋui nɛ lido e nì ba mɔmɔmɔ.

english_verse: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
translation_verse: Le kede e, akpɛ ba atsisi Betlehem, lɛ asi ba sì, “Mlazɛpɛ́ obi ye mɔmɔmɔ; tɔ xegi mlɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, mledzi ba atɔ mlagasi mɛ, tɔ mɛ fɛ maatrɛ tɔ miikpɛ litukpo kesea ɛki yɛ.”

english_verse: When the men went into the house and saw the child with Mary, his mother, they knelt down and worshiped him. They took out their gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh and gave them to him.
translation_verse: Gi beti iku ní kepea mɛ pɔ́ ɛ, bɛmɔ obi ye nì yene Maria; lɛ bedu ba aklamasi na kesea lɛ bɛkpɛ etukpo kesea ɛki obi ye. Lɛ bebu ba bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi bize sika, kotoklobo ani lifi yɛ ní ba igudo le me ɛki obi ye e.

english_verse: Later they were warned in a dream not to return to Herod, and they went back home by another road. The Escape to Egypt
translation_verse: Agi Aya ebu abasɛ ba ní kulila mɛ si bakídzi atrɛ ní Herodes klɔ tɔ ɛ, bɛga kude dzedze lɛ bɛtrɛ ní ba kepea ɛ. Yosef ese ni Yesu ni Maria atsinɔ Egipte

english_verse: After the wise men had gone, an angel from the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up! Hurry and take the child and his mother to Egypt! Stay there until I tell you to return, because Herod is looking for the child and wants to kill him.”
translation_verse: Agi bɛsɛ pɔ́ ɛ, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu isu ya abasi Yosef ní kulila mɛ si, “Herodes atrápɛ obi ye si yiye ye, lɛ loso e, yɔ tɔ gbani obi ye ni yene tɔ sé trɛ ní Egipte. Mleze niklɔ kɔzɛna nani lipoe lɛ klɔ gi maasi mlɔ si mlasɛ edzi i.”

english_verse: That night, Joseph got up and took his wife and the child to Egypt,
translation_verse: Niteme si Yosef ayɔ, lɛ agbani obi ye ni yene elesi la lɛ eseni ba atsinɔ ní Egipte e.

english_verse: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
translation_verse: Beze ní Egipte kɔzɛna nani Herodes litsepoe le. Lɛ loso e, liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ apani si “Maxwa Mobi ye adɔ ní Egipte e,” liba lɛ mɛ. Herodes aki si beye bebinyimenuvɔ ba petee ní Betlehem

english_verse: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
translation_verse: Agi Herodes amɔ si beboete ba gi bɛdɔ kewetu ya bekyi ye exla ɛ, amɔ kusuyeʋi nyani nyani. Lɛ loso e, akpɛ ɔsã si beye obinyime kakaa gi azɛ agɔ taʋa pleno ní Betlehem ani imaɛ nɛ klɔ gi iklani lɔ ɛ. Ebu ɔkliɔ lɔ klɔ kɔkpɛ liboe lɛ klɔ gi enu adɔ ní bababa ʋa onu ní ɔnuvɔɛ lipɔpoe le su i.

english_verse: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
translation_verse: Niteme liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ Yeremya apani aplɛ ɛ,

english_verse: “In Ramah a voice was heard crying and weeping loudly. Rachel was mourning for her children, and she refused to be comforted, because they were dead.” The Return from Egypt
translation_verse: liba lɛ mɛ si, “Ole ɔtɔ eyi ní Rama, gi onu kubemi gagla ole. Raxel eebemi yɛ bebi ba; ɔ́dimɛ si bayɔ yɛ ɔna a, lɛ ese linu si yɛ bebi ba pɔ́ betse.” Yosef, Maria ani Yesu bedzi dɔni Egipte

english_verse: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
translation_verse: Ní Herodes kutseo kede e, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu isu ya abasi Yosef ní kulila mɛ ní Egipte,

english_verse: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
translation_verse: lɛ apani si, “Yɔ, gbani obi ye ni yene, atɔ dzitrɛ ní Israel kesea aʋa, lɛ ese linu si banama gi bɛpɛ si beye obi ye e, betse.”

english_verse: Joseph got up and left with them for Israel.
translation_verse: Loso e Yosef ayɔ, lɛ agbani obi ye ni yene, atsinɔ ní Israel kesea ʋa ɛ.

english_verse: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Yosef enu si Arxelao yɛ ɛɛŋa kukusi yo ní yɛka Herodes kutse o kede ní Yudea ɛ, ese lifu ne ki niklɔ kutrɛtrɛ. Agi bɛkpɛ yɛ ɔsã ní kulilaa mɛ loso e, ekyi atrɛ ní Galilea kesea mɛ.

english_verse: and they went to live there in the town of Nazareth. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “He will be called a Nazarene.”
translation_verse: Lɛ atsa kepe ní ɔma ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Nazaret mɛ ɛ. Nite si liboe lɛ gi bedo eplɛ gani beboedoplɛ ba ʋa si “Banima biaxwa Yɛ si Nazaret Ɔnɛ ɛ” liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Do something to show you have really given up your sins.
translation_verse: Mlabitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He said, “Turn back to God! The kingdom of heaven will soon be here.”
translation_verse: Apani si, “Mlekyi ní mlɔ ìna nɛ mɛ, lɛ ese linu si lifunu ne Kaʋaŋaa ketii pɔnɔ!”

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Lɛ kposɔ sipi liwu Yohanes akpɛ, lɛ akɔ kagaplekpa ɔgɛmi akɔ anya yɛ kasɛa mɛ ɛ. Yɛ kiŋaŋaɛ kize akikli ni liŋwafu mɛ kìbu.

english_verse: From Jerusalem and all Judea and from the Jordan River Valley crowds of people went to John.
translation_verse: Banima bɛdɔ ní Yerusalem ni Yudea ani Yɔdan onipe lo su gi bɛba yɛ klɔ.

english_verse: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛdra ba ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, xe akpɛ kuni aki ba ní Yɔdan onipe lo me e.

english_verse: Many Pharisees and Sadducees also came to be baptized. But John said to them: You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Agi Yohanes amɔ Farisianɛ ma ni Zadukianɛ ma ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ gi bɛzɛba yɛ klɔ si ɔkpɛ kuni ɔki ba ɛ, “eʋi ba si, mlɔ banama gi mlɔ etukpo la mɛ alɛ gaglaa ŋwisi bagblaga ʋa kinɛ ɛ, nyaŋwɛ asi mlɔ si mlese ní Aya yɛ kutuheheo gi kwitraba onu na a?

english_verse: An ax is ready to cut the trees down at their roots. Any tree that doesn't produce good fruit will be chopped down and thrown into a fire.
translation_verse: Bɛkɔ kawɛa ɛtrɔ ní ise le igu ne aʋa kooko; nite si ose kakaa gi óyo esebi gbã ɛ, biate lo kɔŋwia kpɛ́ ní kifu yɛ mɛ.

english_verse: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: Evu yɛ kitɛɛ gi atrákɔ ɛ pí amu na petee e. Atrákpɛ yɛ amu na likpɔ ní owle lo me, kɔ akpɛ itsɔ lɛ ni kifu monimoni ye me.” Yohanes akpɛ kuni aki Yesu

english_verse: Jesus left Galilee and went to the Jordan River to be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yesu ádɔ Galilea lɛ aba Yohanes klɔ ní Yordan onipe lo kutu si tɔ Yohanes ɔkpɛ kuni yo ɔki yɛ.

english_verse: But John kept objecting and said, “I ought to be baptized by you. Why have you come to me?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yohanes apɛ si yitsi kude kí Yesu. Lɛ loso asi yɛ si, “Wɔ́ linu si wɔakpɛ kuni ki mɛ. Lɛ wɔ boŋu wɔba mɔ klɔ a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “For now this is how it should be, because we must do all God wants us to do.” Then John agreed.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ebu lɛ onu aki yɛ si, “Ki tɔ lize nite abla. Niteme si, kwiabitɛ Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa.” Liki si Yohanes adimɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So Jesus was baptized. And as soon as he came out of the water, the sky opened, and he saw the Spirit of God coming down on him like a dove.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ gi Yohanes akpɛ kuni yo aki Yesu pɔ ɛ, Yesu azeedo ní kuni yo me ko e, lɛ lifunu ne lidra ɛ, lɛ amɔ Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi eeple edɔ ŋwisi abronuma kinɛ ní yɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Then a voice from heaven said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ole ɔtɔ ɛpani dɔni lifunu ne si, “Mobi ye liɛ ya yɛ madimɛ nɔ yɛ liboe liku me aŋu u!”

english_verse: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu enu si bɛkpɛ Yohanes ni ligba lɛ ɛ, atrɛ ní Galilea.

english_verse: But instead of staying in Nazareth, Jesus moved to Capernaum. This town was beside Lake Galilee in the territory of Zebulun and Naphtali.
translation_verse: Asɛ ní Nazaret lɛ adzɛ eze ní Kapernaum, gi onu ɔma ɔtɔ ní Galilea limu ne kutu ní Zebulon ni Naftali kuseo me e.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone without eating for
translation_verse: Agi Yesu aza kuʋɛ ewe avitene elesi ni kemize pɔ́ ɛ, kuʋɛ kwiku ye.

english_verse: Then the devil came to him and said, “If you are God's Son, tell these stones to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Ozimizi ye aba yɛ klɔ ɔ; lɛ asi yɛ si, “Xe Aya Yebi wonu i, kɔlɔ ki tɔ akla la ya edzì bidɔŋaŋa aki wɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The Scriptures also say, ‘Don't try to test the Lord your God!’ ”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Pɔ̀ Aya Liboe le lipani dzɛ si, ‘Ɔkaɛ gi onu wɔ Aya ɛ, wukífeke ye edi i.’ ”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
translation_verse: Lɛ dzɛ ɛ, Ozimizi ye atsini Yesu ní lito gyɔgyɔ ɛtɔ aʋa a. Lɛ ebu kelea mɛ bèkusi ʋa pɔ́ ani ba kuʋidi yo abasi yɛ.

english_verse: The devil said to him, “I will give all this to you, if you will bow down and worship me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Ozimizi ye asi yɛ si, “Xegi wodu aklamasi kese xe wɔkpɔ̀ mɛ ɛ, maakɔ bekusi ʋa ya petee ɔki wɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu lɛ onu si, “Sɛ ní mɛ su, Wɔ Satana, Liboe le lipani si, ‘Ɔkaɛ gi onu wɔ Aya ɛ, yɛ ko wɔasumu; lɛ yɛ lixwɛ ko wɔawa a!’ ”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said,
translation_verse: Nite yɛ kigaglaɛ kize, atɔ Yesaya Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ yɛ liboe le liba lɛ mɛ si,

english_verse: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
translation_verse: “Zebulon kesea ni Naftali kesea, kuse gi kuli axu la kudeo aʋa ɛ ní Yordan kewetu ya, Galilea, banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ba kese!

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ lɛ bɛyurɔ ba dzikle lɛ bekuni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu azɛɛga aza ní Galilea limu ne kutu yɔ ɛ, amɔ beneminyime tiaʋa gi benu bakpábu i. Simon gi bɛzɛxwa fɛ si Petro e, ni yenemi ye Andrea. Bezee bu akpa la nì dzikle.

english_verse: Jesus walked on until he saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat with their father, mending their nets. Jesus asked them to come with him.
translation_verse: Yesu atrɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ lɛ azɛɛmɔ beneminyime tiaʋa dzedze e. Ba beze Yakobo ni Yohanes, gi benu Zebedeo yɛ bebi ba. Beze ní ba katapokpa me ni ba ɔkaɛ gi bɛzɛlulɔ ba dzikle ye e. Niteme si Yesu axwa ba kyɛ.

english_verse: At once they left the boat and their father and went with Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Luke
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, bɛsɛ ní katapokpa ni ba ɔkaɛ su lɛ bekuni ni Yesu kude e. Yesu abasɛ bidɔ, apani Liboe Gbã nɛ, lɛ ayɔni banyanya ʋa kyɛ ɛ

english_verse: Jesus went all over Galilee, teaching in their synagogues and preaching the good news about God's kingdom. He also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Yesu aklanɔ ní Galilea petee. Abasi bidɔ ní Yudanɛ ma itsa klɔ. Apani Kaʋaŋaa su Liboe Gbã nɛ, lɛ ayɔni banama su gi bànya ede ede na eze e.

english_verse: News about him spread all over Syria, and people with every kind of sickness or disease were brought to him. Some of them had a lot of demons in them, others were thought to be crazy, and still others could not walk. But Jesus healed them all.
translation_verse: Benu Yesu unu ní Siria kesea petee me nite si banima bɛmani banyanya ba petee ní yɛ klɔ dɔni ikɔ ikɔ lɛ. Bɛzɛnyà bànya ede ede: kusutà, ifuɛfuɛ zimizi, kukpakpanya ani ɔnɔse kapɛ tiele kutsetse. Lɛ Yesu ayɔni ba petee e.

english_verse: Large crowds followed Jesus from Galilee and the region around the ten cities known as Decapolis. They also came from Jerusalem, Judea, and from across the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Anɔkpi la ɛdɔ Galilea, Dekapolis, Yerusalem, Yudea ani kuseo liɔ klɔ gi kuli ní Yɔdan kewetu ya ɛ, ekuni yɛ kude.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on the side of a mountain and sat down. Blessings (Luke
translation_verse: Agi Yesu amɔ anɔkpi la ɛ, emu ketokpaa lɛ eze ní kesea ɛ. Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛkpɛ likpɔ ní yɛ su i.

english_verse: You must obey God's commands better than the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law obey them. If you don't, I promise you will never get into the kingdom of heaven. Anger
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ si xegi mlɔ kubitɛɔ ní Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa a, gi kɔ kiaki si mlɔ liboe liagyɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ, xe kúsani Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma kiniɛ ɛ, mlatrátani kuti iku ní lifunu ne Kaʋaŋaa mɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní kusuyeʋi su u

english_verse: So if you are about to place your gift on the altar and remember that someone is angry with you,
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xegi wɔɛtrɛ ní bidɔ ɔpɔʋi kɔ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa ɛki Aya a, xe wɔplɛ aŋu si liboe lilɛ ní wɔ onemi ye me ni wɔ ɛ,

english_verse: and he taught them:
translation_verse: Lɛ ekpese ba bidɔ kubasɛ si,

english_verse: God blesses those people who depend only on him. They belong to the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: “Isami iku banama gi bɛmɔ e gyesi si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kigagla kíma ní ba awla, lɛ ese linu si lifunu ne Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, ba kanɛ kenu.”

english_verse: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
translation_verse: Isami iku banama gi bɛwa awowoe la ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya aayɔ ba ina a.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are humble. The earth will belong to them!
translation_verse: “Isami iku banama gi bɛbubɔ isu ya ɛ, ba kaŋasea kiaze kesea petee.

english_verse: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
translation_verse: Isami iku banama gi bizedi banɔ dzedze ba asigaga la ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya aadi ba asigaga a.

english_verse: God blesses those people whose hearts are pure. They will see him!
translation_verse: Isami iku banama gi ba ina nɛ mɛ idra ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ba biaze ní Aya aŋu na mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: You are the light for the whole world. A city built on top of a hill cannot be hidden,
translation_verse: “Mlalɛ ŋwisi kani aki kelea mɛ. Ɔma gi bɛtsa ní lito aʋa ɛ, óʋikpo o.

english_verse: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, mɛɛsi mlɔ si xegi mlɛbitɛ kedoplɛ ɛ, mlakíta ka ese. Mlakítani lifunu ne e, lɛ ese linu si lɛ linu Aya yɛ likusigbo le e.

english_verse: The earth is God's footstool, so don't swear by the earth. Jerusalem is the city of the great king, so don't swear by it.
translation_verse: Mlakítani kesea, lɛ ese linu si ka kenu Aya yɛ ikli lɛ odu klɔ ɔ. Mlakítani Yerusalem kyɛ ɛ lɛ ese linu si onu Okusi ʋidi ye yɛ ɔma.

english_verse: You are the salt for everyone on earth. But if salt no longer tastes like salt, how can it make food salty? All it is good for is to be thrown out and walked on.
translation_verse: “Yesu apani si, “Mlalɛ ŋwisi kumwɛ ki kelea pɔ́. Pɔ̀ xegi ɔgyi nɔ eblo ní kɔ mɛ ɛ, batrátani ɔgyi nɔ kudzini ikpɛ ní kɔ mɛ tɔ ɔ. Kúpɛ ɛki kididɔ tɔ ɔ. Nite si biakyi ko bali kɔ banima bɛga ní kɔ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ʋisu kani xe izɛkɔ yɛ kpɛ kasɔʋi ese e. Boŋu i, bizɛkɔ yɛ ɛsu lifune kɔ ekle aki ɔnɔ kakaa ní kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't suppose I came to do away with the Law and the Prophets. I did not come to do away with them, but to give them their full meaning.
translation_verse: “Mlakígyo le edi si maba si míbu Mose yɛ Isã nɛ ni beboedoplɛ ʋa bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ etu eʋe e. Mába si míbu ʋe zanɔ ɔ, boŋu i maba si tɔ ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ biba bɛ mɛ.

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Do not murder” and “A murderer must be brought to trial.”
translation_verse: “Mleenu gi bɛsi kana mɛ banɛ ma si, ‘Wukíye ɔnɔ ɔ. Ɔniliɛ gi aaye ɔnɔ ɛ, biaxwa litsi ni yɛ.’

english_verse: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, mɛɛsi mlɔ si ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aamɔ kusuyeʋi ní yenemi su i, biaxwa litsi ni yɛ. Ɔniliɛ gi aasi yenemi ye si, ‘Wɔ́pɛ ɔki kididɔ ɛ!’ biahe ye ɔtsinɔ ní otsixwaklɔ. Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aakpinì yenemi ye si ɔga ɛ, aaze kuxixa mɛ kí kifu monimoni ye me kutrɛtrɛ.

english_verse: leave your gift there in front of the altar. Make peace with that person, then come back and offer your gift to God.
translation_verse: yurɔ wɔ bidɔ mɛ plɛ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ ɔtɔ nɔ atɔ zɛbitɛ kusuyɔ ni yɛ kyɔŋ. Lɛ kede e, dzi ba atɔ gapɔʋi wɔ bidɔ mɛ ki Aya.

english_verse: Before you are dragged into court, make friends with the person who has accused you of doing wrong. If you don't, you will be handed over to the judge and then to the officer who will put you in jail.
translation_verse: “Xe ɔnutɔ akɔ liboe ní wɔ su ní otsixwakɔ ɔ, xe lipoe le lilɛ ɛ, ye liboe le nì yɛ xe litsixwawe le liana a. Xegi aní nite e, xe mleku otsixwaklɔ ɛ, aakɔ̀ wɔ kukpɛ awla ki otsixwa yɛ, kɔ yɛ kyɛ akɔ wɔ akpɛ awla aki bewukpɛ ʋa ɛ. Niteme si biakpɛ wɔ ligba.

english_verse: I promise you will not get out until you have paid the last cent you owe. Marriage
translation_verse: Sugba sugba ɛ, níklɔ wɔaze kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi wɔatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi bɛnya ɛki wɔ ɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní ádzɛboe nì enyimeboe su u

english_verse: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
translation_verse: “Bɛpani fɛ si, ‘Ɔnɔ kakaa gi aayurɔ yedze e, ɔŋwlimi kadzɛgbeplekpa aki yɛ.’

english_verse: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
translation_verse: “Mlenu gi bepani dzɛ si, ‘Mlakíta mlɔ kedoplɛ ese, pɔ̀ mlabitɛ ní mlɔ bitamu bɛ aʋa kí Ɔkaɛ.’

english_verse: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
translation_verse: Mlɔ kiboedoe ko kize si, ‘Ee’ aloo ‘Oo’. Liboe kakaa gi mlapani lɛ ya kede e, lidɔ ní Ozimizi ye klɔ ɔ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní kuwleʋiʋi su u

english_verse: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
translation_verse: “Mleenu gi bɛpani si, ‘Xegi wɔyaʋi ɔnutɔ kimbi i, linu si bayaʋi wɔ kiniɛ kyɛ.’ Lɛ ɛ, xegi wɔvini ɔnutɔ line e, biavini wɔ lini nɛ kyɛ.

english_verse: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛɛsi mlɔ abla si mlakíʋi kuwle aki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ. Xe ɔnutɔ ábɔ wɔ bidɔ ní ɔŋawla lɔ kutu yɔ mɛ ɛ, ki tɔ ɔbɔ wɔ ní kaʋaa kuni yɔ mɛ kyɛ.

english_verse: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔgũta ɔtɔ emi wɔ aʋa si tu yɛ lidɔtu le ki yɛ ɔtiplɛkɔ tole e, tu le itiplɛkɔ tiʋa.

english_verse: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ eʋi wo kiditɔ ɛ, ki yɛ kɛ. Xe ɔnutɔ apɛ si yidɔ kiditɔ ní wɔ awla ɛ, dɔ kɛ̀ ki yɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ si banima ba dimɛni ni ba ahwafianɛ ma

english_verse: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
translation_verse: “Mleenu gi bɛpani si, ‘Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba atɔ mlekyri mlɔ ahwafianɛ ma.’

english_verse: But I tell you to love your enemies and pray for anyone who mistreats you.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛɛsi mlɔ abla a si: mlalɔ mlɔ ahwafianɛ ma lɛ mlapɛ Aya ligbã aki banama gi bɛyɔ kedu ní mlɔ su i.

english_verse: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
translation_verse: Atɔ mlaadzi mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne yɛ bebi. Lɛ ese linu si yɛ izɛki yɛ kuweo kuzekle ɛki banɔ zimizi ba ni banɔ gbã ba pɔ́. Lɛ ɛ, izɛki dzeni ye izɛdɔ ɛki banɔ gbã ba ni banɔ zimizi ba.

english_verse: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
translation_verse: Xegi banama gi bɛdimɛ ni mlɔ ko mlɔ kyɛ mlɛdimɛ nɔ ɛ, mlakígyo le edi si Aya aatsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɔki mlɔ ɔ. Lɛ ese linu si ligbãzeze lɛ klɔ elu lilɛ babɔgyɔ ba fɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mlɔ baʋia nɛ ba ko mlezeegu kunugu yo nɔ e, kiboebitɛ gbã woli lu mlɛsa kubitɛ ɛ? Banama gi béte Aya ɛ, ani nite ko ba kyɛ bizɛbitɛ ɛ?

english_verse: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Mlaki si mlɔ kizezee me kiflɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne aflɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
translation_verse: “Mledi lɛ aʋa si, mlakíbitɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na ní banima aŋu mɛ tɔ bamɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ xe mlɛbitɛ nite, Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne ɔ́tratsa liʋɛ tɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe wɔpɔni opiplinete e, wukíki tɔ wɔ ɔʋia nɛ kyɛ ote e.

english_verse: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
translation_verse: “Xe mlɛɛxwa Aya ɛ, mlakíbitɛ lɛ ŋwisi banɔ aŋumɛboebitɛ ba kinɛ ɛ, gi bizɛpɛ si bɛyɔ ɛlɛɛ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ ni ablɔ ʋa aʋa tɔ bɛxwa Aya atɔ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔmɔ ba a. Sugba sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si bɛzi ba liʋɛtsatsa lɛ kooko.

english_verse: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe wɔɛxwa Aya ɛ, tiku wɔ ligba lɛ mɛ, da opupo lo tɔ xwa Aya gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋímɔ ɛ. Kɔ wɔ Ɔka gi izɛmɔ kikpòkpo me kiboebitɛ kakaa ɛ, aatsa liʋɛ nɛ ki wɔ.

english_verse: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
translation_verse: “Xe wɔɛxwa Aya ɛ, wukízeege eboe la epio ŋwisi kiliɛ gi babubitɛ ba bizɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si bebu si xegi ba eboe la ɛsrɛ ɛ, kɔ ba Aya enu aki ba ɛ.

english_verse: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
translation_verse: Wukíyi ba, lɛ ese linu si wɔ Ɔka ete wɔ baziazia ʋa kooko xe ableke wɔeʋi ye e.

english_verse: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xe mlɛxwa Aya ɛ, mlapani si: ‘Blɔ Ɔka ní lifunu ne: biakpɛ kubùbù yo ní wɔ linyí dradra lɛ su;

english_verse: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
translation_verse: Kí wɔ Kaʋaŋaa keple ku ní blɔ kusi, kí tɔ babitɛ ní wɔ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa ní kesea aʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi lilɛ ní lifunu ne e.

english_verse: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
translation_verse: Lɛ “Xe mlɛkɔ banima eboe zimizi na ní mlɔ su i, kyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka ní lifunu ne fɛ aakɔ̀ mlɔ ani na kyɛ mlɔ.

english_verse: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
translation_verse: Pɔ xe mlákɔ banima eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba ɛ, mlɔ Ɔka fɛ ɔ́trakɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ mlɔ. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní onugu nyanya su

english_verse: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: atɔ banima bakímɔ egyesi si mlɛɛnya onugu, pɔ̀ mlɔ Ɔka gi baʋímɔ ɛ yɛ tete ko. Atɔ mlɔ Ɔka gi izɛɛmɔ bidɔ gi bɛbitɛ ní kikpokpo me e, aatsa liʋɛ nɛ ki mlɔ. Yesu abasɛ bidɔ ní kupimi kublabla su ní lifunu ne

english_verse: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mlaki tɔ mlɔ bimbi ʋe bizã ní Aya kaʋaŋaa a ni yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa kubitɛɔ su kpɛ ɔtɔ atɔ biakɔ bidɔ bɛ ya petee kukɔpɔnɔ ki mlɔ.

english_verse: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlakíta ɔna ní kivo su u, lɛ ese linu si kivo ɔtutɔ lɔ aakyi awla ní isu ya su. Liwe kakaa liawa axwɛ ní lɛ baxixa ʋa su.

english_verse: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
translation_verse: “Mlakibla kupimi yo ɛki mlɔ isu ya ní kesea aʋa niklɔ gi asu na ni kugyewlegyewleo kwiazizi ʋe xe beze ʋa biayaʋi uku xe biazuru.

english_verse: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
translation_verse: Pɔ mlabla kupimi yo ki isu ya ní lifunu ne, niklɔ gi asu na ni kugyewlegyewle kutrázizi ʋe lɛ beze ʋa kyɛ batrátani kuyaʋi ku xe biazuru u.

english_verse: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
translation_verse: Pɔ xe wɔ bimbi ʋe bìpɛ ɛ, wɔ ɔnɔ zizi yɛ wɔayi ní litumu. Lɛ loso xe kaniɛ ní wɔ mɛ onu litumu i, kɔlɔ litumu zizi lilɛ ní wɔ mɛ ɛ. Yesu abasɛ bidɔ si kukíta ɔna ki kivo

english_verse: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́traze baka tiaʋa ba ɔxwɛdzɛ ní lipoe tile me, aakyi kede kpɛ tole kɔ adimɛ ni ɔʋla ɛ; loo aadra isu mɛ ki tole kɔ ayɔ kedu ní ɔʋlaɛ su i. Wɔtratani Aya ni kibɔ axwɛ kuwa akpɛ likpɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso e, mlakítà ɔna ni mlɔ begyodi ʋa si, ege kwíatà, ege kwiaŋa, ege kwiaŋwɛ loo ege kwiakpɛ ɛ?

english_verse: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso mɛɛsi mlɔ si mlakíta ɔna ní mlɔ ligbã, loo kiŋaŋaɛ loo biŋwɛŋwɛ, aloo mlɔ isu ya su ani kidiliɛ gi mlakpɛ su. Xe wodi i, ligbã líziã ɛ sani kiŋaŋa na a? Lɛ ɔnɔse lo ɔ́ŋa kedzi i sani awu na a?

english_verse: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
translation_verse: Mledi liwɔ lɛ mɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ; kuʋíkpɛ bidɔ loo vini bidɔ kɔbla ní owle me, pɔ fɛ ɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e izeedi kɔ aʋa. Mláŋa kedzi asani kɔ na a?

english_verse: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
translation_verse: “Lɛ ege lɛ loso mlɛɛkpɛ isu kunugu i ki bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ ɛ? Mledi kiliɛ gi otukpomeyaʋi lɔ izefu u, xe izeeyo e. Ɔʋíwa axwɛ loo yo bidɔ ki isu.

english_verse: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ fɛ ɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ si Okusi ye Salomo kura ni yɛ kepimia ni yɛ kukemeo petee me fɛ ɛ ɔ̀ŋwi ŋwisi lɔ kiniɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
translation_verse: Xegi nite Aya izɛlulɔ siwa sɛ ɛ, gi silɛ omono o, xe kivo biaŋwia sɛ ɔkpɛ kifu mɛ ɛ, ɔ́tratani mlɔ kululɔ ni bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ sanɔ na a? Mlɔ kuziŋaa kuli sisami koŋ!

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
translation_verse: Niteme bidɔ mɛ banama gi béte Aya ɛ bizɛɛpɛ. Lɛ mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e, ete si niteme bidɔ mɛ petee biziã mlɔ.

english_verse: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
translation_verse: “Mlakixwa litsi nì ɔnɔ, tɔ Aya kyɛ aʋáxwa litsi nì mlɔ.

english_verse: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, Aya aaxwa litsi ni mlɔ kiliɛ gi mlɛxwa litsi nì banima ɛ. Lɛ isãɛ nɛ gi mlɛkɔ ɛxwa litsi nì banima ɛ, lɛ ké aakɔ̀ xwa litsi ni mlɔ fɛ.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: Ege lɛ loso woevu aŋu ní kumlu uliɔ gi kulí ní wonemi ye kimbi ye aʋa xe wɔyurɔ kanyikpea trɔ ní wɔ tutɔ wɔ kimbi ye aʋa na a?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
translation_verse: Lɛ kítɛmɛ wɔɛsi wonemi ye si, ‘Di, kítɔ mibu simlu se ní wɔ kimbi ye aʋa,’ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi kanyikpea katini ní wɔ tutɔ wɔ kimbi ye aʋa na a?

english_verse: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
translation_verse: Wɔ ɔnɔ ɔŋumɛboebitɛ, ti kanyikpea ní wɔ kimbi ye aʋa kɔ wɔɛmɔ mumɔ kɔ wɔtani simlu se kuti ní wonemi ye yɛ kimbie aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mlɔ gi mlenu banɔ zimizi ba, mlete kiliɛ gi mlaki mlɔ bebi ba bidɔ gbã ɛ; kitɛ xe mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne ɔ́traki banama gi beʋi ye bidɔ gbã ʋɛ na a.

english_verse: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
translation_verse: Obika woli Yebi aaʋi ye lìklã xe aakɔ̀ likla ɔki yɛ na a?

english_verse: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: “Mleʋi bidɔ tɔ biaki mlɔ, mlapɛ bidɔ tɔ mlaamɔ; mlasa opupo lo, tɔ biadra lɔ ɔki mlɔ.

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, ɔnɔ kakaa gi eeʋi bidɔ ɛ, bizɛki yɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛpɛ bidɔ ɛ, aamɔ ʋɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa gi aasa opupo lo e, biadra lɔ ɔki yɛ.

english_verse: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: Aloo xe eʋi ye lìkpa ɛ, aakɔ̀ ɔgblaga ɔki yɛ na a?

english_verse: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso kiliɛ gi mlɛpɛ si babitɛ akí mlɔ ɛ, mlɔ fɛ mlábitɛ nite aki ba a! Lɛ linu Ɔsã nɔ ese kununu yo ni Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ ɛ. Kudɔbasɛ ní kevunu pɔpɔa ni kevunu nènea su u

english_verse: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso e, ɔnɔ kakaa gi izeenu mɛ eboe la xe izɛɛbitɛ ní la aʋa ɛ, ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi oboete, gi awa yɛ ligba lɛ ní liklakpɔ aʋa kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ligbã́ nɛ kevunu ya kɔ ɛ, kɛpɔ; lɛ ka kudeo aʋa kugagaa ɛ, kuli i gagla a, niteme si banɔ sisami atɔ ko bizɛmɔ kɔ. Aŋuzeze ní asia beboedoplɛ ba su u

english_verse: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.
translation_verse: “Mleze ní aŋuplɛplɛ mɛ ní asia na beboedoplɛ ba su, lɛ ese linu si biaba mlɔ klɔ ŋwisi iʋesi kinɛ ní aŋu mɛ. Pɔ̀ ní kimɛ mɛ ɛ, banyraga ɔtutɔ benu u.

english_verse: You can tell what they are by what they do. No one picks grapes or figs from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Mlatè ba gani ba kiboebitɛɛ mɛ. Ɔdɔ̃ nɔ liho le liʋíyo wain yɛ ɛ, nite ke fife e oʋí yo esebi ŋaŋa a.

english_verse: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e ose gbã izeyo esebi gbã, pɔ̀ ose nyani nyani izeyo esebi nyani nyani.

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: Lɛ ose gbã ɔ́tratani esebi nyani nyani kuyo, nite ke ose nyani nyani ɔ́tratani esebi gbã kuyo o.

english_verse: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
translation_verse: Lɛ ose kakaa gi óʋiyo esebi gbã ɛ, biatsa lɔ kukɔ ŋwia kpɛ́ ní kifu mɛ.

english_verse: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke
translation_verse: Nite si mlaate ba gani ba kiboebitɛɛ mɛ ɛ. Litsixwawe le su bidɔ basɛbasɛ

english_verse: Anyone who hears my teachings and doesn't obey them is like a foolish person who built a house on sand.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa gi izeenu mɛ eboe la xe ɔ́ʋibitɛ ní la aʋa ɛ, asrɔ ni ɔga gi awa yɛ ligba lɛ atrɔ ní siklami se aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and the winds blew and beat against that house. Finally, it fell with a crash.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi dzeni ye adɔ xe inipe le iyi i igbigba a, xe liwɔ gagla lɛ lifu i ihali ligba lɛ ɛ, liki si ligba ɛlɛ klɔ ʋa likpa gbloo.” Yesu abasi bidɔ ni kigagla a

english_verse: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
translation_verse: “Aní ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛxwa mɛ si, ‘Ɔka, Ɔka’ ɛ, yɛ aati uku ní lifunu ne Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ banama gi bɛɛbitɛ ní mɛ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa ɛ, ba ko biati uku.

english_verse: On the day of judgment many will call me their Lord. They will say, “We preached in your name, and in your name we forced out demons and worked many miracles.”
translation_verse: Ní litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, banɔ glegle biaŋwlɛ isu ya litukpo si, ‘Ɔka, Ɔka! Wɔ línyi ne me kwipani wɔ Liboe le e, lɛ wɔ linyi ne me kwikpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi glegle e dono o, lɛ wɔ linyi ne me kwibitɛ enugutseboe glegle la!’

english_verse: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
translation_verse: Nite si maasi ba wee si, “Méte mlo liwewe e, Mlasɛ ní mɛ su, mlɔ banɔ dovo ba!” Bagbawa tiaʋa ba a

english_verse: Rain poured down, rivers flooded, and winds beat against that house. But it was built on solid rock, and so it did not fall.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi dzeni ye adɔ, xe inipe le iyi igbigba a, xe liwɔ gagla lɛ lifu ihali ligba lɛ ɛ, líkpa a, lɛ ese linu si bɛwa lɛ ɛtrɔ ní liklakpɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu agu yɛ eboe la ɛ, anɔkpi la inugu itse ní yɛ kudɔbasɛ yɔ su u.

english_verse: He taught them like someone with authority, and not like their teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si abasi ba bidɔ ŋwisi ɔniliɛ gi kigagla kilí yɛ awla kinɛ ɛ, aní ŋwisi ba Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba kiniɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banama gi ba kaŋase kenu Kaʋaŋaa xe e, Aya aaŋwia ba ɛkpɛ ní litumu ne me. Niklɔ biabemi xe biatà ba ene na.”

english_verse: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Agi ɔlagɔ lɔ ɛna ɛ, banima bɛmani banɔ glegle gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne izɛkpɛ ba kunugu yo ní Yesu klɔ ɔ. Lɛ Yesu akpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne nì liboe tile, lɛ ayɔni banyanya ba petee e.

english_verse: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Lɛ onyime ɔtɔ gi azɛɛnya kunya kyikyĩ yɔ ɛ, aba yɛ klɔ. Edu yɛ aklamasi na kesea, lɛ aki yɛ litukpo si, “Ɔka, xe linu wɔ kupɛpɛ ɛ, ki mɛ isu ya idra a.”

english_verse: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu anina yɛ ɔwla lɔ xe agbɔni onyimee e, lɛ asi yɛ si, “Èe, linu mɛ kupɛpɛ. Wɔ isu ya ìdra a!”

english_verse: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Maatrɛ tɔ miiyɔni yɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
translation_verse: Agi Yesu enu baguta ba ɔkaɛ yɛ liboe lɛ ya ɛ, litu ye kugyɔ, lɛ asi banama gi bali ni yɛ kude si, “Mɛɛsi mlɔ ní anukwarɛ mɛ si, mámɔ ɔnɔnɔ ní Israel nì kuʋaziŋa ʋidi uliɔ ya elu oʋídi i.

english_verse: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
translation_verse: “Ɔka, mɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ eetse gi ɔfini kesea ní kepea mɛ ɛ. Ɔ́ʋitani kuhwa a lɛ eepe nyani nyani.”

english_verse: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, banima biadɔ kewetu ya ni keweku ya gi biaze ní ɔdɔŋakplɔ nɔ su nì Abraham, Isak ani Yakob ní lifunu ne Kaʋaŋaa mɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi baguta ba ɔkaɛ si, “Ga trɛ! Kidiliɛ gi kɛ aʋa wɔziŋa ɛ, kiaba kɛ mɛ ɔki wɔ.” Nite si baguta ba ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ayɔ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ ke me. Yesu ayɔ ni banɔ glegle

english_verse: Suddenly they shouted, “Jesus, Son of God, what do you want with us? Have you come to punish us before our time?”
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, bɛkpɛ ozi taataataa si, “liboe woli wɔɛpɛ ní blɔ su, wɔ́ Aya Yebie? Kiba wɔba si wuwa blɔ nyɔnyɔɛ xe ableke lipoe trɔtrɔ lɛ liana na a?”

english_verse: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Nite si banima pɔ́ gi bɛdɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, bedo gi bɛzɛtsani Yesu. Agi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, bɛ bɛki yɛ litukpo si ɔsɛ ní ba kesea mɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu aba eku Petro kepea mɛ ɛ, amɔ si kuwe kwivu Petro yɛnadzee plɛ ní ɔtɔsi aʋa.

english_verse: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
translation_verse: Nite si Yesu agbɔni yɛ ɔwla lɔ; lɛ kuweo kwiyurɔ yɛ, lɛ ayɔ xe ekpese yɛ aʋa kudi i.

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: Abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ yiki liboe lɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya apani i liba lɛ mɛ si, “Akɔ blɔ kubotsobotsoo atrɔ isu ya aʋa lɛ etu blɔ bànya ʋa.” Banɔ tiaʋa bɛpɛ si biakuni Yesu kude

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔsã nɔ ɔdɔbasɛ ɔtɔ aba Yesu klɔ lɛ asi yɛ si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, maze ni wɔ kude trɛ ní ɔkɔ kakaa gi wɔatrɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Yesu yɛ ɔdɔkpasi dzedze apani si, “Ɔka, kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, kí tɔ midzitrɛ tɔ mizeevive mɔka.”

english_verse: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtrɛ xe beze nyime ye xe bɛsi yɛ si, “Bu blo Ɔka, kwitranyrɔ!”

english_verse: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
translation_verse: Yesu emu eku ní katapokpaa mɛ lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bekuni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa yɛ onugu ètse, lɛ beʋi si, “Onyime woli lu liɛ ya a, gi liwɔ lɛ ni kuni yo akpɔ la bizee nu yɛ igbè le e!” Yesu ayɔni benyime tiaʋa mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ikpasi

english_verse: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu aba Gadara banɛ ma kesea me ní limu ne kapɛ ʋla ɛ, benyime tiaʋa gi bedo edɔni bekpo ʋa idrɔ lɛ kusi i, bɛtsani yɛ. Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ize ní benyime ba mɛ, lɛ bizɛwa anyra na nite me si ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋíkeni idi si yiga kudeo liɔ klɔ ʋa.

english_verse: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Iha kisrɛsrɛ ɛtɔ iizɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ní niklɔ ɔza lɔ.

english_verse: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
translation_verse: Nite si ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne iiki Yesu litukpo si, “Xe wɔtra kpikpini blɔ edono e, kɔ lɔɔ ki tɔ kutiku ní iha lɛ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mledo,” nite si, ido lɛ izeetiku ní iha lɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ iha lɛ pɔ́ iise eple ní otoganɔ kapɛa eku ní limu ne me lɛ inyrɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
translation_verse: Niteme si banɔ ba klɔ gi bɛzeedi iha lɛ aʋa ɛ, bese etrɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ. Niklɔ bedo liboe kakaa gi liba iha lɛ aʋa a, ni kiliɛ gi lize kí benyime ba klɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ize e.

english_verse: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.
translation_verse: Yesu emu eku ní katapokpaa mɛ, lɛ edzi atrɛ ní limu ne kedea ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus heard them and answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu enu ba liboe le, lɛ ebu le onu si, “Banama gi balɛ mumɔ ɛ, bázia ɔwabitɛ, pɔ̀ banama gi bɛɛnya ɛ.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. Both the skins and the wine will then be safe. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ nite ke ɔnɔnɔ óʋinye kuda vuvu ukpɛ ní kagaplekpa kuʋe koko me e. Lɛ ese linu si kagaplekpaa kuʋeo kwiayaʋi, gi biaki si kudaa kwiabali, kɔ kagaplekpaa kuʋeo kwizizi i. Boŋu i, bizenye kuda vuvu i, kpɛ ní kagaplekpa kuʋe vuvu mɛ, niteme si bɛtuʋa biaze ní kizeze me gbã mɛ.” Yesu azɛ Yudanɛ ma ɔnɔtsitsi ye yebidzɛ ligbã lɛ ayɔ ni ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ ɛ

english_verse: News about this spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya líhle eku ní kesea kapɛ kakaa. Yesu ayɔni benyime kuŋupo Tuʋa

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses said to themselves, “Jesus must think he is God!”
translation_verse: Lɛ niklɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ bɛsi ba ɔtutɔ ba isu ya si, “Onyime liɛ ya ɛ, ɛɛpani ebusioboe!”

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their minds, and he said, “Why are you thinking such evil things?
translation_verse: Agi ba begyodi ʋa bezee kutete me ɛki Yesu loso e, eʋi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mleegyo eboe zimizi na edi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: But I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ tɔ mlete si kigagla kilɛ ní Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye awla ní kesea ʋa si aakɔ̀ eboe zimizi na kukyɛ ɛ.” Lɛ asi onyime liɛ gi yɛ kapɛ tiele ketse e, si “Yɔ́ kɔ wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ tɔ trɛ ní kepea mɛ!”

english_verse: The man got up and went home.
translation_verse: Lɛ onyimee ayɔ lɛ atrɛ ní kepea mɛ.

english_verse: Go and learn what the Scriptures mean when they say, ‘Instead of offering sacrifices to me, I want you to be merciful to others.’ I didn't come to invite good people to be my followers. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Mlatrɛ tɔ mlazɛ pami liboe lɛ gi bɛpani plɛ mɛ si, ‘Asigagadidi mapɛ, aní baga kuyeye ki Aya.’ Mába si matraxwa banɔ gbã ba boŋu i, banɔ zimizi ba.” Yesu abasi bidɔ ní Onugu nyanya su

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Le kede gi Yesu asɛ ní klɔ ɛ, benyime kuŋupo tuʋa bezeni yɛ kude e, gi bɛzɛɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Di blɔ asigaga, Dawid Yebi ye!”

english_verse: As Jesus and his disciples were on their way, some people brought to him a man who could not talk because a demon was in him.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi benyime ba klɔ bɛzɛ sɛ ɛ, banɔatɔ bɛmani onyime ɔtɔ gi oʋí gu kunugu u, lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi, ɛzɛkpɛ yɛ kunugu ni Yesu klɔ.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving, he saw a tax collector named Matthew sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.” Matthew got up and went with him.
translation_verse: Yesu asɛ ní niklɔ, lɛ gi azɛɛga aza ɛ, amɔ ɔbɔgyɔ gi yɛ linyi linu Mateo gi odi ní yɛ lixwɛwagba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ asi yɛ si, “Kuni mɛ kude e.” Mateo ayɔ lɛ ekuni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, gi Yesu azɛɛŋa bidɔ ní Mateo kepea mɛ ɛ, babɔgyɔ glegle atɔ ni “eboe zimizi na babitɛbitɛ atɔ” bɛba eku ní Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba kusi lɛ ba pɔ́ bɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus' disciples, “Why does your teacher eat with tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Agi Farisianɛ ma bɛmɔ ba ɛ, beʋi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɔ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ izɛɛŋa bidɔ mɛ nì babɔgyɔ ba ni banama gi bizɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na na a?”

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, an official came and knelt in front of him. The man said, “My daughter has just now died! Please come and place your hand on her. Then she will live again.”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ɛɛpani eboe ala ɛ, ɔka ɔtɔ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ aba yɛ klɔ, edu yɛ aklamasi na kesea lɛ asi Yesu si, “Mɛ obidzɛɛ etse ablahate; pɔ̀ gatrɔ awla ni yɛ aʋa tɔ odzi ligbã.”

english_verse: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
translation_verse: Yesu ekyi lɛ gi amɔ yɛ ɛ, asi yɛ si, “Mɛ obidzɛɛ, mɔ ɔna! Wɔ kuziŋaa kwiyɔ ni wɔ ɔ.” Dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ko e, ika lɛ imɔni kudo ní yɛ su u.

english_verse: After Jesus had forced the demon out, the man started talking. The crowds were so amazed they began saying, “Nothing like this has ever happened in Israel!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi akpikpini Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no edono ko e, omúmu no ekpese kunugu yo kugu gi linɔkpi le lɛ inugu itse. Lɛ bɛpani si, “Kuʋí mɔ bidɔ bɛ ya elue ʋídi ní Israel kesea mɛ!”

english_verse: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba Yesu klɔ lɛ beʋi yɛ si, “Ege lɛ loso blɔ ni Farisianɛ ba kuzɛɛnya onugu lipoe me lipoe me, pɔ̀ wɔ badɔkpasi ba kɔɔ baʋínya onugu liwewe na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom aren't sad while he is still with them. But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Kigyo mlegyo edi si banama gi bɛba kudzɛgbanɔ liwe le me, biazɛɛwa awowoe la ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi ɔdzɛnyee ɔlɛ ní ba su na a? Sugba ɛ, litráze nite e! Pɔ̀ liwe le litrába gi biagbani ɔdzɛnyee sɛnɔ ní ba su u, lɛ niklɔ bianya onugu lo o.

english_verse: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ oʋíkɔ lisiapɛ vuvu u, kɔ lulɔ kusa koko gi kwikyi i, lɛ ese linu si lisiapɛ vuvu nɛ liapɔ ɔ kɔ lipa kusa kokoo, gi liwu le lia keme sanɔ tsã a.

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si asi yɛ isu ya si, “Xe màtani yɛ liwu le ɔgyurɔ lɔ tètè kugbɔnɔ ko e, maayɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples got up and went with the man.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Yesu ayɔ lɛ ekuni yɛ kude ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba ɛ.

english_verse: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ ʋa mɛ ɛ, ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi ika lɛ izɛɛba yɛ su agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa zizi i, aga Yesu yɛ kedea lɛ agbɔni yɛ liwu le ɔgyurɔ lɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus went into the home of the official and saw the musicians and the crowd of mourners,
translation_verse: Agi Yesu eti eku ní Yudanɛ ma ɔkaɛ yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ. Amɔ bebemidzikpɛ ba ní banama gi bebemi okpo ye e,

english_verse: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.
translation_verse: lɛ asi ba si “Mledo mlɔ petee! Ɔdzɛʋi yɛ ótse, sra ko ɛɛdɔ!” Pɔ̀ ba petee bɛmwɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But after the crowd had been sent out of the house, Jesus went to the girl's bedside. He took her by the hand and helped her up.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bebu linɔkpi le edono e, Yesu eti eku ní ɔdzɛʋi yɛ ligba lɛ mɛ lɛ evu yɛ ɔwla lɔ ɔ ko lɛ ayɔ ɛ.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone indoors, the two blind men came up to him. He asked them, “Do you believe I can make you well?” “Yes, Lord,” they answered.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu eti eku ní kepea mɛ ɛ, benyime tiaʋa ba klɔ gi ba bimbi ʋe bini i bɛba yɛ klɔ, lɛ eʋi ba si, “Mlɛzi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa si maatani mlɔ bimbi ʋe kudra na?” Bebu le onu si, “Ee, Ɔkaɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus touched their eyes and said, “Because of your faith, you will be healed.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agbɔni ba bimbi ʋe lɛ apani si. “Liba lɛ mɛ kimlɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɛzi i ŋa ɛ!”

english_verse: They were able to see, and Jesus strictly warned them not to tell anyone about him.
translation_verse: lɛ ba bimbi ʋe bidra ɛ. Yesu akpɛ ɔsã gagla aki ba si, “Mlakí pani ní lɛ su i, si ɔnɔnɔ!”

english_verse: But they left and talked about him to everyone in that part of the country. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Talk
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bɛsɛ lɛ beze fuflu Yesu su liboe le e, klanɔ ní kesea kapɛ a klɔ petee aʋa ɛ. Yesu ayɔ ni onyime omúmu

english_verse: But the Pharisees said, “The leader of the demons gives him the power to force out demons.” Jesus Has Pity on People
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Farisianɛ ma bɛpani si, “Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ɔkaɛ yɛ izɛɛki yɛ kigaglɛ xe izɛkɔ ɛkpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne kudo no o.” Asigaga ɛga Yesu nì banima

english_verse: Jesus went to every town and village. He taught in their synagogues and preached the good news about God's kingdom. Jesus also healed every kind of disease and sickness.
translation_verse: Yesu aklanɔ gi azaadzɛ ima nɛ ni ima sisami nɛ petee me. Abasi bidɔ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ, lɛ apani Aya yɛ liboe Gbã nɛ ní Kaʋaŋaa su u, lɛ ayɔ ni banɔ gi kunya kakaa elu kwize ní ba su i.

english_verse: When he saw the crowds, he felt sorry for them. They were confused and helpless, like sheep without a shepherd.
translation_verse: Agi amɔ anɔkpi la ɛ, ba liboe liga yɛ asigaga, lɛ ese linu si bɛpisa lɛ beze banɔ gi kupɔnɔ kúma ɛkɔ ɛ, ŋwisi iʋesi gi ɔgbanɔgbanɔ ɔ́ma ɔki lɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He said to his disciples, “A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ bidɔ mɛ bisrɛ, pɔ̀ baxwɛwa gi biakpɛ ʋɛ likpɔ básrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlaki ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee litukpo tɔ ɔtsisi baxwɛwa ba ní yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Philip, Bartholomew, Thomas, Matthew the tax collector, James the son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles.
translation_verse: Filipo ni Bartolomeo; Toma ni Mateo gi onu ɔbɔgyɔ ɛ; Yakobo gi onu Alfeo Yebi ni Tadeo;

english_verse: But when someone arrests you, don't worry about what you will say or how you will say it. At that time you will be given the words to say.
translation_verse: Xegi bɛmani mlɔ ní litsi le me e, mlɔ begyodi ʋa bakípisa ní liboe lɛ gi mlapani su u, aloo ní kiliɛ gi mlaapani lɛ su u. Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, biaki mlɔ eboa la gi mlapani i.

english_verse: Go only to the people of Israel, because they are like a flock of lost sheep.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, mlatrɛ ní Israel bebi ba gi balɛ ŋwisi iʋesi gi ilila kinɛ ɛ klɔ.

english_verse: I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves. So be as wise as snakes and as innocent as doves.
translation_verse: “Mlata kutu! Mɛɛkpɛ mlɔ ŋwisi iʋesi kinɛ ní kuŋwafudroʋi yo kusi. Lɛ loso e, mlete liboe ŋwisi bagblaga kinɛ pɔ̀ tɔ mlayɔ ŋwisi abronuma kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The others were Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Instructions for the Twelve Apostles (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ bagɔgɔ ba benu Simon, gi ádɔ ní Kanaa ɛ, ani Yuda Iskariot gi yɛ akpɛ Yesu awla ɛ. Yesu asi yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba kidiliɛ gi biabitɛ ɛ

english_verse: Jesus sent out the twelve apostles with these instructions: Stay away from the Gentiles and don't go to any Samaritan town.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba a, lɛ asi ba si, “Mlakítrɛ ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma klɔ ɔ, lo Samaria ɔma ɔtɔtɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And don't carry a traveling bag or an extra shirt or sandals or a walking stick. Workers deserve their food.
translation_verse: mlekívu igudo, iiki kude gaga loo liwu ʋla, afɔkpa ʋla loo aloo ose vuga vuga a. Lɛ ese linu si, ɔxwɛwa kakaa ɛ, aaŋa bidɔ ɔ dɔnɔ ní yɛ ohihe tata lɔ me e.

english_verse: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
translation_verse: “Xe mleku ɔma ɔtɔ lo ɔma sisami ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ, mlapɛ́ ɔnɔ gbã gi adimɛ si yizɛ mlɔ ɛ, atɔ mlatí yɛ klɔ kɔkpɛni mlɔ lisɛpoe le e.

english_verse: I promise you the day of judgment will be easier for the towns of Sodom and Gomorrah than for that town. Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Sugba, sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si, ní litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, Aya aadi banama gi beze ní Sodom ni Gomora asigaga a sani ɔma lɔ klɔ mɛ benete ʋa a. Yesu apani ní keduyɔyɔ alia klɔ gi kiaba su u

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray one another and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: Beneminyime biakpɛ ba beneminyime ní awla tɔ beye; lɛ bebika batɔ kyɛ biabitɛ nite ke tsini ba bebi ba su u, bebi biayɔ kedu ní ba bapɔnɛ ba su gi biaki biaye ba a.

english_verse: Watch out for people who will take you to court and have you beaten in their synagogues.
translation_verse: Mledi isu ya aʋa mɔmɔmɔ, lɛ ese linu si banɔatɔ biavu mlo tsinɔ ní otsixwaklɔ ɔ, lɛ biafiaʋi mlɔ bidɔ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Because of me, you will be dragged before rulers and kings to tell them and the Gentiles about your faith.
translation_verse: Kɔ kpɛ mɛ loso e, biaxwa litsi ni mlo ní baʋaŋa ba ani bekusi ʋidi ba aŋu mɛ; lɛ gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, mlaapani liboe Gbã nɛ kí bekusi ba ni banama gi bénu Yadanɛ ma kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mlátrapani ní mlɔ isuwla mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ eboe la aadɔ ní mlɔ Ɔka yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ gi ɛɛpani gani mlɔ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa aavu mlo kpɛ ní ofu me kɔ kpɛ mɛ loso. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi ɔwla ótse ye kɔna ni ɔgũklɔ ɛ, biazɛ yɛ ligbã a.

english_verse: When people mistreat you in one town, hurry to another one. I promise you before you have gone to all the towns of Israel, the Son of Man will come.
translation_verse: Xegi bɛyɔ kedu ní mlɔ su ní ɔma ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ mlese atrɛ ní odzedze me. Sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si mlatragù mlɔ axwɛ na ní Israel ima nɛ pɔ́ mɛ xe Ɔnɔɛ Ye Bi ye aaba a.

english_verse: Students are not better than their teacher, and slaves are not better than their master.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔkpasibi ɔtɔtɔ óʋikeme ɔsani yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ; lɛ ɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ɔtɔtɔ óʋimu ɔsani yɛ ɔkaɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It is enough for students to be like their teacher and for slaves to be like their master. If people call the head of the family Satan, what will they say about the rest of the family? The One To Fear (Luke
translation_verse: Niteme si isami iku ɔdɔkpasibi ye xe bɛbitɛ tsini yɛ su ŋwisi yɛ kiliɛ gi bɛbitɛ tsini yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ su kinɛ ɛ; tɔ isami iku ɔnɔdzidzi ye xe edzì ŋwisi yɛ kaɛ kinɛ ɛ. Xegi bɛki kepea ɔkaɛ linyi si Beezebul i, kitɛ bátraxwa kepea me benete ba enyi dovo e sanɔ ɛ? Ɔniliɛ gi lilɛ si kwiase e

english_verse: Don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but they cannot harm your soul. Instead, you should fear God who can destroy both your body and your soul in hell.
translation_verse: Mlakíse banama gi biaye kisugyaɛ pɔ̀ báma esobo la kuye me e; pɔ̀ mlese Aya gi aatani kisugyaɛ ni esobo la kuzizi ní kifu mobloe me.

english_verse: Aren't two sparrows sold for only a penny? But your Father knows when any one of them falls to the ground.
translation_verse: Tiasɛ kura abia taʋa izɛɛzɛ ɛ? Pɔ̀ libia ɛtɔtɔ litrawɔli dɔ kesea xegi mlɔ ɔka Aya ɔ́ki kudeonu u.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
translation_verse: Mlɔ kɔ ɛ, mlɔ litukpo le siya sɛ kyɛ ɛ, Aya ete sɛ kiklaklaɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Luke
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlakíse lifu u. Lɛ ese linu si, mlɛŋa kedzi isani abia likpi. Yesu yɛ su kudradra ni yɛ kugbegbe

english_verse: If you tell others you belong to me, I will tell my Father in heaven you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Xe ɔnutɔ aanada si yete me ní banɔ aŋu mɛ, mɛ fɛ maanada si mete ye ní mɛ Ɔka aŋu mɛ ní lifunu ne.

english_verse: But if you reject me, I will tell my Father in heaven you don't belong to me. Not Peace, but Trouble (Luke
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ aagbe me ní banɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, mɛ fɛ maagbe ye ní mɛ Ɔka aŋu mɛ ní lifunu ne. Yesu amani kulɛlɛ ní kelea mɛ

english_verse: Don't think I came to bring peace to the earth! I came to bring trouble, not peace.
translation_verse: “Mlakígyo le edi si kusuyɔ mamanɔ ní kelea mɛ ɛ. Owo, mámani kusuyɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ kusi lɛlɛ kwitraze e.

english_verse: I came to turn sons against their fathers, daughters against their mothers, and daughters-in-law against their mothers-in-law.
translation_verse: Maba si tɔ bebinyime bayɔ kedu ní ba baka su u; tɔ bebidzɛ bayɔ kedu ní ba bene ba su; atɔ beblidze ba bayɔ kedu ní ba banadze ba su;

english_verse: Your worst enemies will be in your own family.
translation_verse: nite si ɔnɔ yɛ ahwafianɛ ba biadɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kepea mɛ.

english_verse: If you love your father or mother or even your sons and daughters more than me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: “Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi adimɛ ni yɛka lo yene a sani mɛ ɛ; ɔ́ma gi aaze mɛ ɔdɔkpasi i; lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi adimɛ ni yebinyime lo yebidzɛ a sani mɛ ɛ, ɔ́ma gi aaze mɛ ɔdɔkpasi i.

english_verse: And unless you are willing to take up your cross and follow me, you are not fit to be my disciples.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔ́pɛ si yimɔ ɔzia mɛ kiniɛ ɛ, ɔ́ma gi aaze mɛ ɔdɔkpasi i.

english_verse: If you try to save your life, you will lose it. But if you give it up for me, you will surely find it. Rewards (Mark
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aalila yɛ ligbã nɛ kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, aabu le. Liʋɛtsatsa ní Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi kuzɛɛzɛ loso

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes a prophet, just because that person is a prophet, will be given the same reward as a prophet. Anyone who welcomes a good person, just because that person is good, will be given the same reward as a good person.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ ɛ, ní kiliɛ gi onu Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ onu i, aamɔ liʋɛtsatsa ŋwisi oboedoplɛ kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aazi ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa kɔ kpɛ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa loso e, aamɔ liʋɛtsatsa ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ linɛ ligyɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who welcomes you welcomes me. And anyone who welcomes me also welcomes the one who sent me.
translation_verse: “Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aazi mlɔ ɛ, mɛ azɛ, lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi azɛ mɛ ɛ, azɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ akpɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And anyone who gives one of my most humble followers a cup of cool water, just because that person is my follower, will be rewarded.
translation_verse: Lɛ sugba sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si, ɔniliɛ gi aki mɛ badɔkpasi ba mɛ ɔgɔgɔɛ kuni ŋrini kepliʋi tiele e, ní kiliɛ gi onu mɛ ɔdɔkpasi onu i, aazi yɛ liʋɛtsatsa lɛ kikaa elu.”

english_verse: As John's followers were going away, Jesus spoke to the crowds about John: What sort of person did you go out into the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Agi Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛsɛ̀ ɛ, Yesu ekpese kupani ní Yohanes su u, kí linɔkpi le si, “Gi mlɛdzi Yohanes klɔ ní ɔyaya klɔ ɛ, ege mlegyo edi si mlɛtrɛ ɛmɔ ɔ? Kize eze ŋwisi oxugbe ní liwɔ onu loo o?

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Agi Yohanes aba ɛ azaanya onugu lo lɛ ɔ́ŋwɛ wain kyɛ ɛ; lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa apani si, ‘Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔkpasi ní yɛ mɛ!’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished instructing his twelve disciples, he left and began teaching and preaching in the towns.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu abasi yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba ikli odukɔ pɔ́ ɛ, asɛ niklɔ lɛ azɛ basɛ bidɔ, lɛ apani liboe gbã nɛ a klanɔ ní Galilea ima nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: John was in prison when he heard what Christ was doing. So John sent some of his followers
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ eze ligba lɛ. Enu Kristo yɛ axwɛwawa la. Nite si akpɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba ní Kristo klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: tɔ beʋi ye si, “Wɔ wonu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ su Yohanes apani aplɛ si atrába, loo kudi kudeonu ɔki ɔnɔ dzedze na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Go and tell John what you have heard and seen.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mledzi a trɛ tɔ mlasi Yohanes eboa la gi mlenu, ani eboa la aŋu mɛ gi mlɛmɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The blind are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People with leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Kuŋupoo kwimɔ, lɛ bafa ba bɛɛga a; kunya kyikyĩ yɔ banyanya ba ɛ, ba isu iidra a, lɛ itoku le kyɛ iinu i. Bekpo ʋa beedzi ligbã, lɛ bɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ɛsi bepiplinete ba a.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Aya aakrira ɔnɔ kakaa gi ógbe me ní eboa la gi mɛɛbitɛ loso e!”

english_verse: What kind of man did you go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who dress like that live in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Ege mledo etrɛ si mlɛtrɛ ɛmɔ ɔ? Onyime gi ɔkpasi liwu gbã mɛ loo o? Banama gi bizɛkpɛ ewu gbã na ɛ, kekusipe me bizeeze e.

english_verse: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: Mlasi mɛ, ege mledo etrɛ si mlɛzeedi i? Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ loo o? Nite ɖii; pɔ ɔniliɛ gi mlɛmɔ ɛ, ekeme a sani oboedoplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I tell you no one ever born on this earth is greater than John the Baptist. But whoever is least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than John.
translation_verse: Sugba ɛ, mɛɛsi mlɔ si Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ ekeme a sani ɔnɔ kakaa gi aga ɔdzɛ kupɔpɔ mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi onu ɔsisami yɛ ní Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, ekeme a sani yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: From the time of John the Baptist until now, violent people have been trying to take over the kingdom of heaven by force.
translation_verse: Dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Yohanes apani yɛ eboe la, gakɔ nani ablahate e, Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa kɛtrɛ ɔtɔ ni kigagla, lɛ banɔ aŋu mɛ gagla benete bɛpɛ si bɛzi ka ní kigagla a.

english_verse: And if you believe them, John is Elijah, the prophet you are waiting for.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe mlɛpɛ si mlaazi ba eboe la aʋa ŋa ɛ, kɔlɔ, Yohanes yɛ onu Eliya gi yɛ kubabaa su u bɛpani i plɛ ɛ.

english_verse: “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not mourn!”
translation_verse: “ ‘kwikpɛ kudzɛgbanɔ idzi iki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mleyo o. Kwikpɛ ibemidzi i ki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mlébemi i.’

english_verse: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
translation_verse: Agi Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aba ɛ, azaaŋa bidɔ, lɛ azaaŋwɛ wain yɛ kyɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa apani si, ‘Mledi onyime liɛ ya a! onu ɔŋazi nɛ ni ɔdaŋwɛmu, babɔgyɔ ba ni “banɔ zimizi ba” ɔʋia nɛ.’ Kuboeteo kɔ kiboebitɛ kɛ kizebu isu ya ɛbasɛ si kwina a.” Imaɛ lɛ klɔ gi izi Yesu yɛ liboe le aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ

english_verse: In the towns where Jesus had worked most of his miracles, the people refused to turn to God. So Jesus was upset with them and said:
translation_verse: Agi banama gi bali ní imaɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ gi Yesu abitɛ yɛ enugutseboe la begbe xe békyi ní ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, Yesu atɛni ba a.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? You will go down to hell! If the miracles that took place in your town had happened in Sodom, it would still be standing.
translation_verse: Lɛ wɔ Kapernaum kɔ ɛ! Kipɛ́ wɔpɛ si wumuni wɔ isu ya kɔna ni lifunu ne na a? Aya aawɔli wɔ kɔŋwia kpɛ ní kifu monimoni ye me! Atɛsi enugutseboe ala gi bɛbitɛ ní wɔ mɛ ɛ, xe bɛbitɛ la ní Sodom i, nya Sodom aaze kɔ bana ni omono o!

english_verse: So I tell you on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than you. Come to Me and Rest (Luke
translation_verse: Mɛɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛ, kimlɔ si, ní litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, Aya aadi Sodom asigaga a sani wɔ ɔ!” Mlaba mɔklɔ tɔ mleze amɔni

english_verse: At that moment Jesus said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yesu apani si, “Medo wo ɖase Ɔka, Lifunu ne ni kesea Ɔkaɛ! Lɛ ese linu si wobu ɔbasɛ banama gi béte keplekpa ɛ, kidiliɛ gi wɔkɔ okpo beboete ba ni beplekpate ba ɛ.

english_verse: Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Ee, Ɔka, nite wɔpɛ si liba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who truly knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: “Mɛ ɔka akɔ kidɔ kakaa aki mɛ. Ɔnɔnɔ óte Obi ye sani Ɔka yɛ ko, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ fɛ óte Ɔkaɛ, sani Obi ye ko, ani banama gi Obi ye apɛ si yibu ye e basɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you are tired from carrying heavy burdens, come to me and I will give you rest.

english_verse: It was taken on a serving plate to the girl, and she gave it to her mother.

translation_verse: Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba efeke okpo ye, lɛ bezeevive ye e; lɛ kede e, bɛzɛɛsi Yesu liboe le. Yesu agani banɔ akpe tietsu ni bidɔŋaŋa

translation_verse: Agi Yesu enu Yohanes liboe le e, asɛ ni klɔ ní katapokpa mɛ, lɛ atrɛ ní ɔkutɔ gi ebu lidze dzaa yɛ tete e. Banima benu si asɛ lɛ bɛsɛ ní ba ima nɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛga ikli lɛ lɛ bɛzɛtsani yɛ.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “They don't have to leave. Why don't you give them something to eat?”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Líma si basɛ, mlɔ tutɔ mlaki ba kiditɔ tɔ baŋa!”

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples to bring the food to him,
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, “Kɔlɔ ɛ, mlakɔ la amani mɛ ní ya.”

english_verse: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.

english_verse: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.

translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɛɛta Aya isã nɛ ese xe mlevu mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlɔ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ awla na a?

translation_verse: Libi i tɔtɔ líma lɛ mɛ si besumu me e, lɛ ese linu si bɛ basɛ banɔ batrɔtrɔ ŋwisi bɛ binu mɛ ba isã nɛ na a.’ ” Kidiliɛ gi kize lulu ɔnɔ ɛ

translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́yaka yɛ ɛ. Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba beti apɔni yɛ lɛ bɛki yɛ litukpo si, “Kítɔ ɔsɛ! Ɔlɛ ni blɔ kude gi ɛkpɛ blɔ kutoku u!”

english_verse: The food you put into your mouth doesn't make you unclean and unfit to worship God. The bad words that come out of your mouth are what make you unclean.”
translation_verse: Aní kidiliɛ gi kize eku ɔnɔ onugu lo me e, kɛ kize lulu ye ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu u, boŋu i eboa la gi izedo edɔní yɛ onu i, la izelulu ye.”

english_verse: Then his disciples came over to him and asked, “Do you know you insulted the Pharisees by what you said?”
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba yɛ klɔ lɛ beʋi ye si, “Wote si eboa la gi wɔpani i eeyeʋi Farisianɛ ma na a?”

english_verse: Peter replied, “What did you mean when you talked about the things that make people unclean?”
translation_verse: Petro azɛ liboe le yɛ onu si, “Dra abee liɛ ya su ki blɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus then said: Don't any of you know by now what I am talking about?
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Kɔ mlenu mɛ badɔkpasi pɔ̀ mlenu liboe lɛ ya ese ŋwi banɔ gɔgɔ ba lo o?”

english_verse: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?

english_verse: But the words that come out of your mouth come from your heart. And they are what make you unfit to worship God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ eboa la gi izeedo ega onugu lo me e, edɔ yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ, lɛ la enu kidiliɛ gi kizelulu ɔnɔ ɔ.

translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, dɔni yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, niklɔ begyodi nyani nyani ʋa, kí kunɔye, kihasiwawa, ɔnɔ su kuzizizizi; adzɛ ni enyimeboe, kuzé, ni asia kusɛsɛ, kalɔa ani ligbãzeze zimizi kakaa elu bizɛdɔ.

english_verse: These are what make you unclean. Eating without washing your hands will not make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Mark
translation_verse: Kiboebitɛ liɛ ya elu i, kɛ kizelulu ɔnɔ. Pɔ̀ tɔ ŋa bidɔ awla moplo e, biʋílulu ɔnɔ.” Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ yɛ kuziŋa

english_verse: Jesus said, “I was sent only to the people of Israel! They are like a flock of lost sheep.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Israel ɔlɛ ŋwisi iʋesi lɛ klɔ gi ilila ɛ, lɛ bakɔ ko bɛkpɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
translation_verse: Anɔkpi ʋidi atɔ ɛba yɛ klɔ, gi ɛmani bafa ba, kuŋupoo, banɔ klɔʋiklɔʋi ba, imumu ne ani bànya ede ede banyanya ba ní Yesu klɔ, nite si ayɔ ni ba pɔ́.

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the territory near the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu asɛ ní niklɔ ɛ, atrɛ ní kesea klɔ mɛ gi Tiro ni Sidɔn balɛ ɛ.
