translation_verse: Lɛ Kanandze ɔtɔ gi ádɔ ní kusio klɔ ɛ, aba Yesu klɔ lɛ akpɛ ozi si, “Ɔkaɛ Dawid yɛ lipo me obi ye! Di me asigaga! Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔtɔ ɔkpasi mobidzɛ mɛ, lɛ ɛkpɛ yɛ kunugu yo o.”

english_verse: The woman came closer. Then she knelt down and begged, “Please help me, Lord!”
translation_verse: Niklɔ ɔdzɛɛ aba dɔ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa ní Yesu ketu ya lɛ asi yɛ si, “Ɔkaɛ, pɔni mɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “lima si bakɔ banuvɔ ba bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ aŋwia aplɛ akí kudroʋio.”

english_verse: “Lord, this is true,” the woman said, “but even puppies get the crumbs that fall from their owner's table.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛɛ ebu le onu si, “Nite ɖii lilɛ, Ɔkaɛ, pɔ̀ kudroʋio fɛ kuzɛɛŋa bɛ klɔ gi biwɔli dɔnɔ kɔ ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔdɔŋakplɔ nɔ aʋa ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Dear woman, you really do have a lot of faith, and you will be given what you want.” At that moment her daughter was healed. Jesus Heals Many People
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ɔdzɛ wɔ kuziŋaa kwikeme koŋ! Kidiliɛ gi wɔɛpɛ ɛ kiaba kɛ mɛ ɔki wɔ.” Lɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɖi i yebidzɛ ayɔ. Yesu ayɔ nì banyanya glegle

english_verse: From there, Jesus went along Lake Galilee. Then he climbed a hill and sat down.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu asɛ niklɔ ɛ, edzi aba Galilea limu ne kutu. Emu ketokpaa lɛ eze ní kesea ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at what they saw and heard. People who had never spoken could now speak. The lame were healed, the paralyzed could walk, and the blind were able to see. Everyone was praising the God of Israel. Jesus Feeds Four Thousand (Mark
translation_verse: Banima inugu itse gi bɛmɔ imumu ne gi iigu kunugu yo, banɔ klɔʋiklɔʋi ba gi bɛɛga, bafa ba bɛyɔ ɛga, lɛ kuŋupoo fɛ kwimɔ ɛ. Nite si bɛkpɔ̀ Israel Aya. Yesu aga ni banɔ akpe tiene

english_verse: Jesus called his disciples together and told them, “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat. I don't want to send them away hungry. They might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Yesu axwa yɛ badɔkpasi ba amanɔ ní yɛ isu ya, lɛ asi ba si, “Banɔaya ba liboe liiga mɛ asigaga, lɛ ese linu si balɛ ní mɛ su ewe tata lɛ abla ɛ, kididɔ fɛ kíma gi biaŋa a. Mápɛ si basɛ nì lipo me pluplu u, xegi ani nite e, biatɔ ɔpiti ní kudeo me e.”

english_verse: His disciples said, “This place is like a desert. Where can we find enough food to feed such a crowd?”
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba beʋi ye si, “Nifɔ kwitramɔ kiŋaŋa ɛki linɔkpii lɛ ní ɔyaya klɔ ya na a?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them how much food they had. They replied, “Seven small loaves of bread and a few little fish.”
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi si, “Aklã tasɛ alɛ ní mlɔ awla a?” Lɛ bebu le onu si, “Aklã tegloele ni akpa sisami atɔ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had told the people to sit down,
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu aki si linɔkpi le lize ní kesea ɛ.

english_verse: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
translation_verse: Lɛ akɔ aklã tegloele la ni akpa la, lɛ gi edo Aya ɖase e, ayaʋi la mɛ, lɛ akɔ la aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba ɛ; lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛkɔ la ɛki banima ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone ate all they wanted, and the leftovers filled seven large baskets.
translation_verse: Ba pɔ́ bɛŋa gi beke e. Lɛ kede e, biŋaŋa gɔgɔɛ klɔ gi badɔkpasi ba bɛhali i, biyi kusɔʋi tugloele.

english_verse: After Jesus had sent the crowds away, he got into a boat and sailed across the lake. He came to shore near the town of Magadan.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu akpɛ ba kude pɔ ɛ, eku katapokpaa trɛ ní Magadan kesea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't you know by now that I am not talking to you about bread? Watch out for the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees!
translation_verse: Lɛ ege lɛ loso mlénu le ese si aní aklã su mazeegu kunugu yo aki mlɔ ɛ? Mlakpɛ isu lixaŋu ní Farisianɛ ma ni Zadukianɛ ma ba binyasi dɔ mɛ su!”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were near the town of Caesarea Philippi, he asked them, “What do people say about the Son of Man?”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu atrɛ ní Kaisarea Filipi kesea mɛ ɛ, eʋi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Nyaŋwɛ banima bizedo si Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye onu na a?”

english_verse: Finally, the disciples understood that Jesus wasn't talking about the yeast used to make bread, but about the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees. Who Is Jesus? (Mark
translation_verse: Able ko yɛ badɔkpasi ba benu le ese si ɔzɛsi ba si bezeni aŋu ní aklã mɛ binyasi dɔ mɛ su, boŋu i azɛɛpani ní Farisianɛ ma ni Zadukianɛ ma bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ su. Petro adra Yesu su

english_verse: He told them: If the sky is red in the evening, you say the weather will be good.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Xegi liwo le liiku u, xe kana mɛ kɛ zã ɛ, kɔ mlɔ si, ‘Kana mɛ kɛtrapɛ keletitia mɛ.’

english_verse: But if the sky is red and gloomy in the morning, you say it is going to rain. You can tell what the weather will be like by looking at the sky. But you don't understand what is happening now.
translation_verse: Lɛ livlɛ lɛ ɛ, mlɔ si ‘Dzeni ye atrádɔ lɛ ese linu si ayɛɛ mɛ azã lɛ aplɛ la ɛzi kana mɛ. ‘Xegi mlɛzɛtani kana mɛ kumɔ gyesi i, kitɛ xe mlátratani eboa la gi ɛɛtrɛ la aʋa abla ɛ, kumɔ ogyesi i?”

english_verse: The disciples answered, “Some people say you are John the Baptist or maybe Elijah or Jeremiah or some other prophet.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bebu le onu si, “Banɔatɔ si Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ; lɛ batɔ fɛ si Eliya, banɔatɔ fɛ si Yeremya, loo Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba mɛ ɔtɔ.”

english_verse: From then on, Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “I must go to Jerusalem. There the nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make me suffer terribly. I will be killed, but three days later I will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Dɔnɔ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ, Yesu ekpese lɛ mɛ kudra aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Linu si mitrɛ ní Yerusalem tɔ mizɛɛmɔ kini gagla ní kusumu yo baka ba ni Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba iwla mɛ. Biaye me, pɔ̀ ní liwe tanɛ nɛ aʋa ɛ, Aya aayɔ ni mɛ gi maadzi ligbã a.

english_verse: Jesus then warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mledi mumɔ; mleze ni aŋu ní Farisianɛ ma ni Zadukianɛ ma kunyasi yɔ su u.”

english_verse: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bekpese yɛ eboe la kuta odi ní ba tsali kusi i, “Apani eboa la klɔ lɛ ese linu si kúmani aklã a tɔtɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: The Son of Man will soon come in the glory of his Father and with his angels to reward all people for what they have done.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye e, adziba ní yɛka kusudraa mɛ nì Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba, lɛ atrátsa liʋɛ ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa ní yɛ axwɛwawa la onu.

english_verse: So I will call you Peter, which means “a rock.” On this rock I will build my church, and death itself will not have any power over it.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e mɛɛsi wɔ Petro si wɔ linyine ese linu si liklakpɔ, lɛ liklakpɔɛ lɛ ya aʋa maadu mɛ ɔsɔli ha lɔ ɔ, lɛ kutseo fɛ kútratani lɔ aʋa kuŋa a.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ba si, “Nyaŋwɛ mlɔ pɔ̀ mlɛɛsɛ si menu na a?”

english_verse: Simon Peter spoke up, “You are the Messiah, the Son of the living God.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Simon Petro ebu le onu si, “Wɔ wonu Kristo ye, Aya gi ɔlɛ ligbã nɛ Yebinyime e!”

english_verse: Jesus told him: Simon, son of Jonah, you are blessed! You didn't discover this on your own. It was shown to you by my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu apani si “Isami iku wo, Simon Yona yɛ obinyimee! Anukwarɛɛ liɛ klɔ ɔ́dɔ ɔnɔ gbagbe ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ mɔka ní lifunu ne e, yɛ ebu le abasi wɔ.

english_verse: I will give you the keys to the kingdom of heaven, and God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth. But he will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: Maakɔ lifunu ne Kaʋaŋaa safi ʋa aki wɔ. Liboe kakaa gi wɔanya ní kesea aʋa ɛ, bianya nɛ ní lifunu ne, lɛ kidɔ kakaa gi wɔable ni kesea aʋa ɛ, biable ke ní lifunu ne.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples not to tell anyone he was the Messiah. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, bakísi ɔnɔnɔ si yɛ yenu Kristo ye. Yesu apani ní yɛ kinimɔmɔ ɛ ni yɛ kutseo su u

english_verse: Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that. He said, “God would never let this happen to you, Lord!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ebu Yesu kuka xe atɛni yɛ si, “Liwewe Ɔkaɛ, Aya otini le ɔzanɔ ni wɔ su”

english_verse: Jesus turned to Peter and said, “Satan, get away from me! You're in my way because you think like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ekyi kyɔŋ lɛ aka aŋu mɛ akí Petro si, “Wɔ Satana! Sɛ ní mɛ su! Woetsi kude ɛki mɛ; lɛ ese linu si wɔ begyodi ba ya ɛ, bádɔ Aya kɔ ɔ; keleme banɔ begyodi benu.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to his disciples: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Xe ɔnutɔ apɛ si yizeni mɛ kude e, lilɛ si odze yɛ isu ya, tɔ otu yɛ osetsase lo, atɔ okuni mɛ kude.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will find it.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi apɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aalila yɛ ligbã nɛ kɔ kpɛ mɛ loso e, aamɔ nɛ.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself? What would you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: Libi woli liaze lɛ mɛ ki ɔnɔ xe kelea mɛ kedzì yɛ kanɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ aalila yɛ esobo la na a? Owo! Ɔ́tramɔ kididɔ kura! Lɛ ege kɛ akɔ atsrɛ yɛ ligbã nɛ na a?

english_verse: I promise you some of those standing here will not die before they see the Son of Man coming with his kingdom.
translation_verse: Matrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa aki mlɔ si banɔatɔ bali blɔ kusi niya gi bátratse e, kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi biamɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye yɛ kibabaɛ mɛ ŋwisi Okusi kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: There in front of the disciples, Jesus was completely changed. His face was shining like the sun, and his clothes became white as light.
translation_verse: Agi bezeedi ye e, bɛmɔ si yɛ kiŋwiŋwiɛ mɛ kikyí, yɛ aŋu na mɛ ɛzekle ŋwisi liwo le kinɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ kyɛ ɛ, bixwa pititi gi bizeŋle e.

english_verse: Then the disciples understood Jesus was talking to them about John the Baptist. Jesus Heals a Boy (Mark
translation_verse: Niklɔ badɔkpasi ba benu lɛ ese si Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ yɛ su azɛɛpani. Yesu ayɔ ni ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ mɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi eze e

english_verse: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Ewe teglo kede e, Yesu agbani Petro ni beneminyime tiaʋa ba Yakobo ani Yohanes evu, lɛ bemu lito gyɔgyɔ ɛtɔ ɛ; nite si ba tete bebu lidze ní niklɔ.

english_verse: All at once Moses and Elijah were there talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ ko e, lɛ badɔkpasi tiata ba bɛmɔ Mose ni Eliya gi bɛzɛpani ní Yesu i.

english_verse: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ebu le edo asi Yesu si, “Ɔkaɛ, lipɛ koŋ si kwiba ní ya! Xe wɔpɛ ɛ, kwiawa akatsa tata ní niya; tile ki wɔ, tile kí Mose ani tile kí Eliya.”

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Óbu onugu ní lɛ mɛ xe aplɛ klekle atɔ ewe ba a. Ko lɛ ole ɔtɔ eyi dɔni aplɛ la mɛ si, “Ɔniliɛ ya onu mɛ ɔtutɔ mobinyime gi malɔ ɛ, gi eku mɛ liŋu u; mlata yɛ kutu!”

english_verse: When the disciples heard the voice, they were so afraid they fell flat on the ground.
translation_verse: Agi badɔkpasi ba benu ole lo e, lifu ne liku ba ɛsanɔ nite si bɛkɔ ba aŋu na mɛ ewe kesea.

english_verse: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu aba ba klɔ; lɛ agbɔni ba a; xe asi ba si i, “Mlayɔ! Mlakíse lifu u!”

english_verse: When they opened their eyes, they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛyɔ ɔ xe bedi klanɔ ɛ, bámɔ ɔnɔnɔ sani Yesu ko.

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Lɛ badɔkpasi ba beʋi Yesu si, “Ege lɛ loso Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛpani si linu si Eliya yɛ aatu kuba na a?”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Elijah certainly will come and get everything ready.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Eliya yɛ aatu kuba, kɔ akpɛ kidɔ kakaa likpɔ aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛɛsi mlɔ si; Eliya aba kooko pɔ̀ lɛ banima bámɔ yɛ egyesi, lɛ bebu i tu ye ní ba kupɛpɛɔ onu i. Nite ke biawa Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye fɛ nyɔnyɔɛ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to the crowd. A man knelt in front of him
translation_verse: Agi Yesu nì yɛ badɔkpasi ba bedziba ɛtsani linɔkpi le e, onyime ɔtɔ aba Yesu klɔ, lɛ adɔ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa ni yɛ ketu ya a,

english_verse: and said, “Lord, have pity on my son! He has a bad case of epilepsy and often falls into a fire or into water.
translation_verse: lɛ apani si, “Ɔkaɛ, di mobinyimee asigaga! Kukpakpanya kuli ní yɛ su u, gi izeegleʋe eku kifu nì kuni me e, nite si izɛɛmɔ ɔzia glegle.

english_verse: I brought him to your disciples, but none of them could heal him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso magbani yɛ amani wɔ badɔkpasi ba, pɔ̀ bátani yɛ kuyɔnɔ nɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people are too stubborn to have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy here.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu azɛ liboe le si, “mlɔ omono kizeze me kyrikyri bebi ba ya, kitɛ xe kuʋaziŋa kúma ni mlɔ awla na a? Lɛ kɔzɛna ni lipoe woli i onu maaze ni mlɔ su du u? Lɛ kɔna ni lipoe woli onu mazɛɛzi mlɔ eboe la na? Mlagbani onyimemi ye amani mɛ ní ya!”

english_verse: Then Jesus spoke sternly to the demon. It went out of the boy, and right then he was healed.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no lɛ edo ní onyimemi ye me kyɔŋ ko lɛ onyimemi ye ayɔ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Later the disciples went to Jesus in private and asked him, “Why couldn't we force out the demon?”
translation_verse: Lɛ kede gi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bebu lidze e, beʋi ye si, “Ege lɛ loso blɔ kɔ ɔ kútani Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no kukpikpini dono na a?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: It is because you don't have enough faith! But I can promise you this. If you had faith no larger than a mustard seed, you could tell this mountain to move from here to there. And it would. Everything would be possible for you. Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi lɔ ya elu i, gani Aya kuxwaxwa ni onugu nyanya mɛ ko mlatani lɔ kukpikpini dono o. Kidɔ dzedze kakaa elu kítratanɔ ɔ.” Yesu apani ní yɛ kutseo su dzɛ ɛ

english_verse: While Jesus and his disciples were going from place to place in Galilee, he told them, “The Son of Man will be handed over to people
translation_verse: Agi badɔkpasi ba pɔ́ bɛkpɛ likpɔ ní Galilea ɛ, Yesu asi ba si, “Bíha Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kukpɛ awla ki banima

english_verse: who will kill him. But three days later he will rise to life.” All of this made the disciples very sad. Paying the Temple Tax
translation_verse: niteme si biaye ye; pɔ̀ ní liwe tanɛ we le aʋa ɛ, Aya aayɔ ni yɛ gi aadzi ligbã a.” Liki si badɔkpasi ba pɔ́ beze ní asigaga mɛ ɛ. Yesu atsa kusumu yo ligba lɛ kagyɔa

english_verse: When Jesus and the others arrived in Capernaum, the collectors for the temple tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your teacher pay the temple tax?”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba Kapernaum i, Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ babɔgyɔ ba beʋi Petro si, “Dani mlɔ ɔdɔbasɛɛ izɛtsa Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ kagyɔa na a?

english_verse: “Yes, he does,” Peter answered. After they had returned home, Jesus went up to Peter and asked him, “Simon, what do you think? Do the kings of this earth collect taxes and fees from their own people or from foreigners?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ebu le onu si, “Ee, izɛtsa a.” Lɛ gi Petro atrɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ, Yesu etu ye kuʋi si, “Petro, kitɛ wogyo liboe le odi i? Nyaŋwɛ izɛtsa kagyɔ ɔki kelea ya mɛ bekusi ba na a? Kesea mɛ bebi ba loo bababa ba na a?

english_verse: But we don't want to cause trouble. So go cast a line into the lake and pull out the first fish you hook. Open its mouth, and you will find a coin. Use it to pay your taxes and mine.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ tɔ kukíta ɔnɔnɔ ese e, trɛ ní limu ne kutu yɔ, tɔ kpɛ wɔ dadiwa ɛ. Likpaa tɔpu inɛ gi wɔabu i, yaka lɛ onugu lo me. Wɔamɔ kibɔ gi kiana ki mɛ ni wɔ lisumugba lɛ kagyɔa ɛ. Kɔ kɛ zɛɛtsa mɛ ní wɔ onu.”

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, poke it out and get rid of it. You would be better off to go into life with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fires of hell. The Lost Sheep (Luke
translation_verse: Lɛ xe wɔ kimbi ye kiaki si wɔata kese e, kpaʋi kɛ tuʋe. Liapɛ ɔki wɔ si tiku ní ligbã moblo le me ni kimbi tile sanɔ si tɔ bimbi tuʋa bize wo pɔ̀ tɔ bakɔ wɔ aŋwia akpɛ ní kifumoni ye me e! Yesu ebu abee ní oʋesi gi ɛlila su u

english_verse: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
translation_verse: wo gyeni wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ asigaga kudi ŋwisi kiliɛ gi medi wɔ kyɛ asigaga la kinɛ ɛ?’

english_verse: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ ʋidi ye ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, yɛ onu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi eple ni isu ya xe edzì ŋwisi ɔnuvɔ liɛ ya kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi azɛ banuvɔ ba ya elu ba mɛ tole ní mɛ linyi ne me e, mɛ azɛ. Aŋu mɛ kukaka ki ɔniliɛ gi aaki si ɔnutɔ aabitɛ liboe zimizi i

english_verse: If the follower refuses to listen to them, report the matter to the church. Anyone who refuses to listen to the church must be treated like an unbeliever or a tax collector. Allowing and Not Allowing
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔ́zɛ ba liboe le e, zɛ kɔ wɔ liboe le plɛ ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ aŋu mɛ. Niteme si, xegi ónu kutu ki ɔhalɔ kyɛ ɛ, kɔ yɛ kudeo kí yɛ tɔ oze ɔki wɔ ŋwisi ɔbubitɛ loo ɔtumonu kinɛ ɛ. Badɔkpasi ba bɛmɔ kigagla si biatsi kude loo biaki kude ni eboe su

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the deepest part of the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks!
translation_verse: “Xegi ɔnutɔ aaki si banuvɔ ba ya gi bɛzi mɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa biakyi kede kpɛ mɛ ɛ, liapɛ ɔki ɔniliɛ klɔ sanɔ xe bɛka kuhɔ ʋidi ní yɛ ole lo xe bɛkɔ yɛ ɛŋwia kpɛ ní axu la odzudzu klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: If your hand or foot causes you to sin, chop it off and throw it away! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed or lame than to have two hands or two feet and be thrown into the fire that never goes out.
translation_verse: Xe wɔ kawlakpɔa loo wɔ ɔkli aaki si wɔata kese e, tsa lɔ tuʋe. Liapɛ ɔki wɔ si tiku ní ligbã moblo le me ni kawlakpɔ tiele, loo ɔkli tole sani si tɔ iwla loo ikli tiʋa ize wo, pɔ tɔ bakɔ wɔ aŋwia akpɛ ní kifumoni ye me e.

english_verse: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
translation_verse: “Xe wonemi abitɛ liboe zimizi amani wɔ su i, wɔ tètè tipɔni yɛ tɔ bu le kubasɛ yɛ ní mlɔ tiaʋa kusi. Xe enu kutu i, tɔ mleku likpɔ.

english_verse: Don't be cruel to any of these little ones! I promise you their angels are always with my Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye e, kiba aba si yibu banama gi bɛlila ɛ.

english_verse: I am sure that finding it would make you happier than having the
translation_verse: Kikaka elu i, xegi amɔ lɔ ɛ, onyimee aamɔ isami glegle ní lɔ su sani iʋesi avi ɔgɔtole ni ɔgɔtole le gi ílila su i.

english_verse: That's how it is with your Father in heaven. He doesn't want any of these little ones to be lost. When Someone Sins (Luke
translation_verse: Nite ke línu mlɔ Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne kupɛpɛ si banuvɔ sisami ba ya mɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔlila a. Kiliɛ gi biabitɛ onemi ɔʋaziŋa liɛ gi ata kese e

english_verse: But if that one refuses to listen, take along one or two others. The Scriptures teach that every complaint must be proven true by two or more witnesses.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi ónu kutu i, gbani ɔnɔ tole, tiaʋa pɔni isu ya ‘tɔ ɔʋatrɔ oze ní wɔ awla ní liboe kakaa gi mlaapani su u ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya liboe le libasɛ kinɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: I promise you God in heaven will allow whatever you allow on earth, but God will not allow anything you don't allow.
translation_verse: “Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ pɔ́ si: xe mletsi kude ɛki kiditɔ ní kesea aʋa ɛ, biatsi kɛ onu ní lifunu ne, lɛ xe mlɛki kude ní kiditɔ su ní kesea aʋa ɛ, biaki kɛ kude ní lifunu ne.

english_verse: I promise that when any two of you on earth agree about something you are praying for, my Father in heaven will do it for you.
translation_verse: “Lɛ mɛ ɛɛpani ipɔnɔ si, xegi mlɔ mɛ banɔ tiaʋa mlaakpɛ mlɔ begyodi ʋa likpɔ ní kidɔ kakaa su ní kesea aʋa xe mleʋi ke e, mɔka ní lifunu ne aabitɛ kɛ ɔki mlɔ.

english_verse: Whenever two or three of you come together in my name, I am there with you. An Official Who Refused To Forgive
translation_verse: Lɛ niklɔ gi banɔ tiaʋa loo tiata biakpɛ likpɔ ní mɛ linyi ne me e, malɛ ní ba kusi.” Abee Ní ɔnɔsumu ye gi ɔ́kɔkyɛ ɛ yɛ su

english_verse: Peter came up to the Lord and asked, “How many times should I forgive someone who does something wrong to me? Is seven times enough?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro aba Yesu klɔ xe eʋi ye si, “Ɔkaɛ, tasɛ mɛ ɔhanɛ ɔʋaziŋa aabitɛ liboe zimizi mani mɛ su xe maakɔ kyɛ yɛ na a? Tegloele na a?”

english_verse: This story will show you what the kingdom of heaven is like: One day a king decided to call in his officials and ask them to give an account of what they owed him.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si lifunu ne kaʋaŋaa kalɛ nite e: ɔʋaŋa ɔtɔ eze e; lɛ liwe etɔ lɛ ɛ, atrɔ si yɛ aga kibɔɛ eboe la mɛ ni yɛ banɔsumu ba.

english_verse: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi kibɔ kíze ní ɔnɔsumu ye awla xe akɔ tsa liʋɛ nɛ gi adɔ onu i, lɛ ɔʋaŋaɛ asi si badà yɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ dzidzi kinɛ ɛ, yɛ nì yedze, yɛ bebi ba kɔpɔni kidɔ kakaa elu gi kiliɛ yɛ awla ɛ, tɔ bakɔ atsa liʋɛɛ nɛ gi adɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔsumu ye adɔ́ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa aki ɔʋaŋaɛ si “Maki wɔ litukpo, plɛ litɔ kese ki mɛ; tɔ maatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi madɔ ní wɔ su i!’

english_verse: The king felt sorry for him and let him go free. He even told the official that he did not have to pay back the money.
translation_verse: Yɛ liboe le liga ɔʋaŋaɛ asigaga, nite si akɔ liʋɛɛ nɛ gi adɔ ní yɛ su i, akɔ lɛ akyɛ yɛ, lɛ ayurɔ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The man got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you back.”
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ ɔhanuglɔ ɔnɔsumu ye adɔ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa, xe aki yɛ litukpo si i, ‘Plɛ litɔ kese ki mɛ ɛ; maatsa liʋɛɛ nɛ gi madɔ ɛ!’

english_verse: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ ɔ́zɛ lɛ ɔki yɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, aki bɛkpɛ yɛ ligba kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi aatsa liʋɛ nɛ ɔki yɛ pɔ́ ɛ.

english_verse: When some other officials found out what had happened, they felt sorry for the man who had been put in jail. Then they told the king what had happened.
translation_verse: Agi banɔsumu dzedze ba bɛmɔ eboa la gi ɛtrɛ la ʋa nite e, limi ba aʋa koŋ. Nite si bɛtrɛ ní ɔʋaŋaɛ klɔ, lɛ bɛsi yɛ liboe kakaa gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
translation_verse: Liki si ɔʋaŋaɛ axwa ɔnɔsumu ye amani yɛ klɔ. Lɛ atɛni yɛ si, ‘Wɔ ɔnɔsumu litukpo me gagla onete wonu. Makɔ wɔ aʋɛ na petee akyɛ wɔ lɛ ese linu si wɔki mɛ litukpo si mikɔ la kyɛ wɔ,

english_verse: The king was so angry that he ordered the official to be tortured until he could pay back everything he owed.
translation_verse: Nite si ɔʋaŋaɛ amɔ kusuyeʋi nyani nyani, lɛ aki bɛkpɛ yɛ ligba tɔ bawa yɛ nyɔnyɔɛ kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi aatsa liʋɛ kakaa gi adɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: That is how my Father in heaven will treat you, if you don't forgive each of my followers with all your heart.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ liboe le si, “Nite ɖii mɔka ní lifunu ne aabitɛ mlɔ mɛ kakaa liɛ gi ɔ́kɔ kyɛ yɛ ɔhanuglɔ ɔʋaziŋa dɔnɔ ní yɛ ɔna mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus finished teaching, he left Galilee and went to the part of Judea east of the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu agù yɛ kupaniɔ ɛ, asɛ ní Galilea lɛ atrɛ ní Yudea kesea ní Yordan onipe lo kede e.

english_verse: Some people are unable to marry because of birth defects or because of what someone has done to their bodies. Others stay single in order to serve God better. Anyone who can accept this teaching should do so.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Mark
translation_verse: Mlenu lɛ ese si, eboe glegle alɛ gi la loso onyime ɔ́tratani ɔdzɛ kugbanɔ ɔ: benyimeatɔ ɛ, bɛpɔ ba si bawɔ ɔdzɛ mɔgbanɔ ɔ; batɔ ɛ banɔ bɛbitɛ ba nite; lɛ benyimeatɔ fɛ bágbani badzɛ kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa loso o. Ɔniliɛ gi aatani lɛ ese kunu i tɔ ɔzɛ lɛ.” Yesu akrira banuvɔ ba

english_verse: Large crowds followed him, and he healed their sick.
