english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. They came up to him and asked, “Is it right for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
translation_verse: Farisianɛatɔ bɛba yɛ klɔ lɛ bɛpɛ si bɛɛpunu yɛ ni kuʋíʋi si, “Dani blɔ Ɔsã nɔ ɛki kude si wɔagbe wodze ní kude kakaa onu gi wɔpɛ na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Don't you know in the beginning the Creator made a man and a woman?
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Kikla mlákla ní Aya liboe le me si ní okpese klɔ ɛ, Aya ebu ba onyime ni ɔdzɛ na a?

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married. He becomes like one person with his wife.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya apani si, ‘lɛ loso onyime aayurɔ yɛka ni yene tɔ yɛ nì yedze biakpɛ likpɔ ɔ, lɛ ba tiaʋa biadzi kisugya tile na a.’

english_verse: Then they are no longer two people, but one. And no one should separate a couple God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bénu banɔ tiaʋa tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ kisugya tile. “nite si kidiliɛ gi Aya akpɛ likpɔ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ki lɛ kɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why did Moses say a man could write out divorce papers and send his wife away?”
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi Yesu si, “ege lɛ loso, Mose aki kude si onyime ɔki yedze kegbegbeplekpa tɔ ɔkpɛ yɛ kude na a?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You are so heartless! That's why Moses allowed you to divorce your wife. But from the beginning God did not intend it to be that way.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mose aki mlɔ kude si mlegbe mlɔ bedze ba lɛ ese linu si mlɔ batu ba mɛ bali i gagla. Pɔ̀ lize nite ní kelea okpese klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I say if your wife has not committed some terrible sexual sin, you must not divorce her to marry someone else. If you do, you are unfaithful.”
translation_verse: Abla ɖii, mɛɛsi mlɔ si, xe onyime ɔtɔ egbe yedze ni kude dzedze onu gi aní enyimeboe alɔ ɛ, xe agbani ɔdzɛ dzedze e, kɔ onyimee liɛ klɔ alɔ ádzɛboe e.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “Only those people who have been given the gift of staying single can accept this teaching.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu azɛ liboe le si, “Ani ɔnɔ kakaa yɛ aanu liboe lɛ ya ese e, pɔ̀ banama gi Aya aki kesenunu i, ba linɛ.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus, so he could place his hands on them and pray for them. His disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Banɔatɔ bɛgbani banuvɔ ba ɛ mani Yesu klɔ tɔ ɔtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa ni tɔ ɔxwa Aya ní ba litukpo, pɔ̀ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtɛni banɔaklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: A man came to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Lipoetɔ kede e, onyime ɔtɔ ɛba Yesu klɔ lɛ eʋi ye si, “Ɔdɔbasɛɛ, kidɔ gbã woli lu lilɛ si mibitɛ xe maamɔ ligbã moblo le e?”

english_verse: “Which ones?” the man asked. Jesus answered, “Do not murder. Be faithful in marriage. Do not steal. Do not tell lies about others.
translation_verse: Lɛ onyimee eʋi si, “Isã woli lɛ klɔ ɔ?” Yesu ebu le onu si, “Wukíye ɔnɔ ɔ; wukílɔ enyimeboe ni ádzɛboe; wukízuru; wukísɛ asia akpɛ ɔnɔ awla a;

english_verse: After Jesus had placed his hands on the children, he left. A Rich Young Man (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ atrɔ awla ní ba aʋa a, lɛ asɛ. Lilii gagla ɛki bexonete ba si beti eku ni Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ebú le onu si, Ege lɛ loso wɔeʋi me liboe ní kidɔ gbã su u? Koŋu i, ɔnɔ tole ko ɔlɛ gi yɛ apɛ ɛ. Ga ní isã nɛ aʋa xe wɔpɛ si wutiku ní ligbã́ moblo le me e.”

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me, and don't try to stop them! People who are like these children belong to God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu egbe liboe aki ba si, “Mlaki tɔ banuvɔ ba baba mɔ klɔ; mlakítsi kude aki ba, lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, ba elu kanɛ kenu u.”

english_verse: But many who are now first will be last, and many who are last will be first.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ glegle gi benu batɔpu i, biaze bagɔgɔ, lɛ banɔ glegle gi benu bagɔgɔ abla ɛ, biadzi batɔpu u.

english_verse: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Bù wɔka ni wone; tɔ dimɛni wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ tutɔ lɔ wɔ isu ya kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The young man said, “I have obeyed all of these. What else must I do?”
translation_verse: Lɛ onyimee ebu isu ya onu si, “Maga ní isãɛ nɛ klɔ pɔ́ aʋa. Ege kigɔ dzɛ gi linu si mibitɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “If you want to be perfect, go sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Xe wɔpɛ si wuflɔ ɛki ligbã moblo le e, trɛ tɔ zɛɛda wɔ bidɔ mɛ petee atɔ kɔ kibɔɛ kí bepiplinete ba. Liaki si exo aaze ní wɔ awla ní lifunu ne; Atɔ wɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, ga kuni mɛ kude.”

english_verse: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Agi onyimemi ye enu liboe le e, asɛ ní asigaga mɛ, lɛ ese linu si oxonete ʋidi onu u.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Mɛɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si, liaze gagla ki bexonete ba si beti eku Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ.

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Meedzini le epani si: liaze gagla ki oxonete si oti oku Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ sani si kposɔɛ ɔga trɔ̀ni yɛ liwu le me odo.”

english_verse: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Agi badɔkpasi ba benu liboe le e, ba inugu itse mɔmɔmɔ, lɛ beʋi si, “Kɔ abla ɛ, nyaŋwɛ koŋ aamɔ kububu i?”

english_verse: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu edu aŋu na ní ba aʋa a, lɛ ebu le onu si, “Ɔnɔ gbagbe ɔ́tratani ɔnɔnɔ kubu u, pɔ̀ Aya kɔ ɛ, kidɔ kakaa kiatani kɛ mɛ kuba ki yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ebu le edo si, “Dike, ɔka! kwiyurɔ kidɔ kakaa, lɛ kwikuni wɔ kude e. Ege kwiamɔ obu ní lɛ mɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Yes, all of you have become my followers. And so in the future world, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, I promise you will sit on twelve thrones to judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Sugba mɛsi mlɔ si, xe Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaze ní yɛ likusigbo le aʋa ní isami mɛ ní kele vuvu ya mɛ gi Aya aabitɛ ɛ, kɔ́ mlɔ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba gi mlalɛ ni mɛ kude e, mlɔ fɛ mlaze ní ekusigbo aʋa, gi mlaaŋa kukusi ní Israel akpɔ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa la aʋa a.

english_verse: All who have given up home or brothers and sisters or father and mother or children or land for me will be given
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi ayurɔ kupe loo, beneminyime, loo benemidzɛ, loo ɔka, loo one, loo bebi, loo inyɔɔ kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, aamɔ bɛ elu ede alafa kɔ sanɔ, lɛ Aya aaki ɔniɛ ligbã mobloe le e.

english_verse: As Jesus was telling what the kingdom of heaven would be like, he said: Early one morning a man went out to hire some workers for his vineyard.
translation_verse: Yesu atrɛ lɛ aʋa si “Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kalɛ nite e. Livlɛ ɛtɔ lɛ onyime ɔtɔ edo atrɛ gi azɛɛkɔ apaa benete tɔ bawa axwɛ aki yɛ ni yɛ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ mɛ

english_verse: After he had agreed to pay them the usual amount for a day's work, he sent them off to his vineyard.
translation_verse: Adimɛ si yɛ atsa liwe le apaa yɛ kibɔɛ aki ba; lɛ atsisi ba ní yɛ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Edzi atrɛ ní ablɔ yɛ mɛ ní ɔnyrɔ ɔgɔtole me, lɛ azɛɛmɔ benyimeatɔ gi balɛɛ kididɔ mɔbitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, asi ba si, ‘Mlɔ fɛ mlazɛwa axwɛ na ní mɛ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ, atɔ maatsa liʋɛɛ nɛ gi ligyeno e ɛki mlɔ ɔ.’

english_verse: So they went. At noon and again about three in the afternoon he returned to the market. And each time he made the same agreement with others who were loafing around with nothing to do.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛtrɛ ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ. Nite ke atsisi banɔatɔ ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ, ní inyɔ liɔfɔ ni tiʋa ani inyɔ tita mɛ dzɛ.

english_verse: Finally, about five in the afternoon the man went back and found some others standing there. He asked them, “Why have you been standing here all day long doing nothing?”
translation_verse: Agi inyɔ titsu iha kusa ɛ, edzì atrɛ ní ablɔ ʋa aʋa dzɛ lɛ azɛɛtsani benyime dzedzeatɔ gi balɛɛ ni niklɔ ɔ, lɛ eʋi ba si, ‘Ege lɛ loso mleezizi liwe zizi nɛ kidiidɔ mɔbitɛ na a?’

english_verse: “Because no one has hired us,” they answered. Then he told them to go work in his vineyard.
translation_verse: Lɛ bebu isu ya onu si, ‘Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ki blɔ lixwɛxwɛ ɛ.’ Lɛ asi ba si, ‘Lipɛ, nite kɔ mlɔ fɛ mlazɛwa axwɛ na ni mɛ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ mɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: That evening the owner of the vineyard told the man in charge of the workers to call them in and give them their money. He also told the man to begin with the ones who were hired last.
translation_verse: “Agi ɔlagɔ lɔ inyɔ tiglo isã ɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ, onetee asi yɛ axwɛ na aʋa odidi ye si, ‘Xwa baxwɛwa ba tɔ tsa ba aʋɛ na kí ba. Kpese ní baxwɛwa gɔgɔ ba aʋa zɛkɔna ni baxwɛwa tɔpu ba aʋa a.

english_verse: When the workers arrived, the ones who had been hired at five in the afternoon were given a full day's pay.
translation_verse: Benyime ba klɔ gi bɛtrɛ ní axwɛ na mɛ, ní inyɔ titsu le me e, bɛtsa liwe le liʋɛ nɛ ɛki ba mɛ ɔkakaa.

english_verse: they began complaining to the owner of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Agi bɛzi ba kibɔɛ ɛ, beku kutɛ mɛ.

english_verse: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
translation_verse: Ba liboe le linu si, ‘Benyime ba ya gi bɛba kudegba ɛ, ɔnyɔsasa tole ko bɛwa axwɛ na a; lɛ blɔ kɔ ɔ kwize kuweo me e, wa axwɛ na liwe zizi nɛ petee. Pɔ̀ liʋɛ lide tile ko wɔtsa aki blɔ petee!”

english_verse: The owner answered one of them, “Friend, I didn't cheat you. I paid you exactly what we agreed on.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ onete ebu le onu ki ba mɛ tole si, ‘Ta kutu, ɔʋiaa. Mába wɔ kura. Wɔtutɔ, wɔdimɛ si, wɔawa liwe tile axwɛ ɛ ki liwe le liʋɛ nɛ.

english_verse: Take your money now and go! What business is it of yours if I want to pay them the same that I paid you?
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, zi wɔ liʋɛ nɛ tɔ sɛ. Mɛ mapɛ si miki ɔxwɛwa gɔgɔɛ nite gi maki wɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Don't I have the right to do what I want with my own money? Why should you be jealous, if I want to be generous?”
translation_verse: Aloo mámɔ kude gi maabitɛ kidiliɛ gi mapɛ ni mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ kibɔɛ na a? Kɔ ege lɛ loso wɔzã kimbi ní mɛ lipomepɛpɛ lɛ su i?’ ”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “So it is. Everyone who is now last will be first, and everyone who is first will be last.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ liboe le si, “Lɛ loso e, banɔ gɔgɔ ba biadzi batɔpu ba, lɛ banɔ tɔpu ba biadzi banɔ gɔgɔ ɔ.” Yesu apani yɛ kutseo su kutanɛ ɛ

english_verse: When Jesus heard them, he stopped and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”
translation_verse: Yesu alɛɛ, lɛ axwa ba xe eʋi ba si, “Ege mlɛɛpɛ si mibitɛ ki mlɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for them and touched their eyes. At once they could see, and they became his followers.
translation_verse: Yesu edi ba asigaga lɛ agbɔni ba bimbi ʋe e; kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, ba bimbi ʋe bidra, lɛ bekuni Yesu kude e.

english_verse: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Linɔkpi le litɛni ba lɛ lisi ba si bagɔ tsriɖi. Pɔ̀, bɛkpɛ ozi gaglaa ɛsanɔ dzɛ si, “Ɔkaɛ Dawid yɛ lipo me obi ye e! Dí blɔ asigaga!”

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
translation_verse: Agi Yesu azeemu atrɛ ní Yerusalem i, ebu yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba kuka ní kudeo aʋa, lɛ apani aki ba ɛ.

english_verse: and then they will hand him over to foreigners who will make fun of him. They will beat him and nail him to a cross. But on the third day he will rise from death. A Mother's Request (Mark
translation_verse: Kɔ bɛkɔ yɛ kpɛ́ awla ɛki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ. Ba kyɛ ɛ, biakpɛ yɛ kakpaa kɔ bekusi ye e; gɔgɔ ɛ, kɔ beku ye ní ose lo su i. Pɔ̀ liwe tanɛ we le aʋa ɛ, Aya aayɔ ni yɛ ɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ.” Yakobo ni Yohanes ba onee eʋi kupɔnɔ aki yɛ bebi tiaʋa ba

english_verse: The mother of James and John came to Jesus with her two sons. She knelt down and started begging him to do something for her.
translation_verse: Lɛ Zebedeo yedze aba Yesu klɔ ni yɛ bebinyime tiaʋa ba ɛ. Edu yɛ aklamasi na kesea akí yɛ, lɛ eʋi si ɔpɛ lipo me ɔki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked her what she wanted, and she said, “When you come into your kingdom, please let one of my sons sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi ye si, “Ege wɔɛpɛ si mibitɛ ki wɔ ɔ?” Lɛ ebu le onu si, “Kaplɛ ki mɛ si, mɛ bebínyime tiaʋa ba ya ɛ, biaze ni wɔ kaŋaa ni wɔ kabaa mɛ xegi woku wɔ kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Not one of you knows what you are asking. Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from?” James and John said, “Yes, we are!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu aki bebinyime ba si, “Mléte kidiliɛ gi mleeʋi i, mlaatani ɔzia nɔ lidɔtu ilɛ gi matratu i, kutu na?” Bebu le onu si, “Ee, kwiatanɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlaatani mɛ lidɔtu le kutu baa; Pɔ̀ kigagla kíma ní mɛ awla si mikyá ɔniliɛ gi yɛ aaze ní mɛ kaŋaa loo kabaa mɛ ɛ. Banɔaklɔ gi Mɔka abla izekɔɛ lɛ klɔ akɔ ɛ, ba ko biatani niklɔ kuze e.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with the two brothers.
translation_verse: Agi badɔkpasi liɔfɔ gɔgɔ ba benu liboe le e, bɛmɔ kusuyeʋi ní beneminyime tiaʋa ba su.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Yesu axwa badɔkpasi ba akpɛ likpɔ, lɛ asi ba si, “Mlete si banama gi bizɛ ŋa banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma aʋa ɛ, bizɛɛŋa kuka ní ba aʋa, lɛ ba onu bezeze ba ɛ, bɛmɔ kigagla ní ba aʋa a.

english_verse: But don't act like them. If you want to be great, you must be the servant of all the others.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ líma si lize ní mlɔ kusi nite e. Lɛ loso xe mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔ apɛ si yize ɔnɔ ʋidi i, lilɛ si oze ɔnɔsumu ɔki mlɔ bagɔgɔ ba;

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be the slave of the rest.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe mlɔmɛ tole apɛ si yize ɔtɔpu i, lilɛ si oze ŋwisi ɔnɔ dzidzi kí mlɔ bagɔgɔ ba a.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
translation_verse: Lilɛ si mlabitɛ nite lɛ ese linu si Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye ɔ́ba si besumu ye e, boŋu i, aba si tɔ yisumu atɔ yikɔ yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛkɔ ɛki banɔ glegle ba ligbã kuzɛɛzɛ.” Yesu ayɔ ni kuŋupo tuʋa

english_verse: Jesus was followed by a large crowd as he and his disciples were leaving Jericho.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛɛ sɛ ní Yerixo e linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lize ni Yesu kude.

english_verse: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: Lɛ benyime tiaʋa gi beze kuŋupo e, beze ní kudeo su, lɛ gi benu si Yesu azɛɛza ɛ, bekpese ozí kukpɛ si, “Ɔkaɛ Dawid yɛ lipo me obi ye, di blɔ asigaga.”

english_verse: They answered, “Lord, we want to see!”
translation_verse: Bebu le onu si, “Ɔkaɛ, kwipɛ si kí tɔ kumɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus said: I will tell you a story about a man who had two sons. Then you can tell me what you think. The father went to the older son and said, “Go work in the vineyard today!”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu abee si, “Abla ɛ kitɛ mlegyo edi ni liboe lɛ ya su u? Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi bebinyime tiaʋa beze ni yɛ awla a. Adzɛ otsitsi ye klɔ lɛ asi yɛ si, ‘Mobi ye, trɛ tɔ zɛɛwa axwɛ na ní wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ omono.’

english_verse: But when they saw the man's son, they said, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bɛmɔ obinyime e, bɛpani ni bahanuglɔ ba kusi sì, ‘Ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee Yebi ye iliɛ ya, yɛ atráŋa ɔnyrɔ nɔ. Mlaki tɔ kuye ye, atɔ kuzi yɛ ɔŋa klɔ!’

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs them.’ He will at once let you have the donkeys.”
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔnutɔ eʋi mlo liboetɔ ɛ, mlasi yɛ si, ‘Ɔkaɛ yɛ aziã ʋa a,’ nite si aayurɔ ba ɔki mlɔ kyɔŋ.”

english_verse: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ nite atɔ Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ yɛ eboe la aba la mɛ si:

english_verse: “Announce to the people of Jerusalem: ‘Your king is coming to you! He is humble and rides on a donkey. He comes on the colt of a donkey.’ ”
translation_verse: “Mlasi Yerusalem ɔma ʋidi no me bezeze ba si, Mledi, mlɔ okusi ye atrába ní mlɔ klɔ! Epleni isu ya, lɛ ɔtini ní tedzi ni yebi ye aʋa.”

english_verse: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
translation_verse: Nite si badɔkpasi tiaʋa ba bɛzɛ bitɛ kiliɛ gi Yesu asi ba ɛ:

english_verse: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Begbani tedzi ye ni yebi ye manɔ, bɛkɔ ba bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ ɛtrɔ ní ba aʋa lɛ Yesu emu atrɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu eti eku Yerusalem i, ɔma nɔ pɔ́ ɛkpa ge. Lɛ banima beʋi si, “Nyaŋwɛ iliɛ ya lee e?”

english_verse: But the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses were angry when they saw his miracles and heard the children shouting praises to the Son of David.
translation_verse: Kusɔliɔ baka ba ni Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛmɔ kusuyeʋi gi bɛmɔ enugutseboe ala gi azɛɛbitɛ ɛ, ni banuvɔ ba gi bɛzɛkpɛ ozi ní Kasɔlipea mɛ si, “Hosana kí Dawid yɛ lipo me Obi ye e!”

english_verse: When the chief priests and the Pharisees heard these stories, they knew Jesus was talking about them.
translation_verse: Agi Kusɔliɔ baka ʋidi ba ni Farisianɛ ma benu Yesu yɛ abee liɛ ɛ, betè si ba su azeegu kunugu yo,

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship.’ But you have turned it into a place where robbers hide.”
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ni Aya yɛ liboe le me sì Aya apani sì, ‘Mɛ Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, biaxwa lɛ si, ligba gi biazesumu me ní lɛ mɛ ɛ. ‘Pɔ̀ mlɛ kɔ lɛ ɛbitɛ okpokɔ ki beze ba!”

english_verse: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
translation_verse: Xegi mlɛziŋa ɛ, kidɔ kakaaɛ liɛ gi mlaʋi i xe mlɛxwa Aya ɛ, mlamɔ kɛ.” Kuʋiʋi ní Yesu yɛ kigaglaɛ su

english_verse: The men said to Jesus, “Don't you hear what those children are saying?” “Yes, I do!” Jesus answered. “Don't you know that the Scriptures say, ‘Children and infants will sing praises’?”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso beʋi Yesu si, “Dani woenu eboa la gi banuvɔ ba ya bɛɛpani i?” Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ee menu.” “Mlaʋíkla Aya liboe le oʋídi si, ‘Mlɛ basɛ banuvɔ ba nì bebi ba sì biaki kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ liɔ gi kwiflɔ na a?’ ”

english_verse: Then Jesus left the city and went out to the village of Bethany, where he spent the night. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asɛ ní banima su ní Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ mɛ xe edo ní Yerusalem atrɛ ní Betania ɔma nɔ mɛ, niklɔ azɛɛfɛ. Yesu exo ifi akpɛ ogbose lo

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Maʋi mlo liboe tile ko, lɛ xe mlebu le onu i kí mɛ ɛ, maasi mlɔ kigaglaɛ liɛ klɔ gi kilɛ mɛ awla xe mɛkɔ ɛbitɛ eboa la ya a.

english_verse: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
translation_verse: Agi Yesu edziitrɛ Yerusalem keletitia livlɛ lɛ ɛ, kuʋe kwizeeku ye.

english_verse: But Jesus said to them, “If you have faith and don't doubt, I promise you can do what I did to this tree. And you will be able to do even more. You can tell this mountain to get up and jump into the sea, and it will.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Matrɔ likla lɛ aʋa aki mlɔ si, xe mlɛziŋa a xe mlɛ́keɖi i, mlaatani kidiliɛ gi mabitɛ aki ogbose lo e, kubitɛ. Aní lɛ tete, pɔ̀ mlaatani ketokpa lia ya kyɛ kusɛ si, ‘Kpaʋi tɔ zɛɛŋwia isu ya akpɛ ní axu la mɛ,’ lɛ lia ba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus said, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” A Story about Two Sons
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, bebu le onu ki Yesu si, “Kúte.” Lɛ asi ba si, “Lɛ kɔ mɛ fɛ, mátrasi mlɔ, kigaglaɛ liɛ gi mɛkɔ ɛbitɛ eboa la ya a. Abee ní bebinyime tiaʋa ba su

english_verse: Jesus told the chief priests and leaders to listen to this story: A land owner once planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it and dug a pit to crush the grapes in. He also built a lookout tower. Then he rented out his vineyard and left the country.
translation_verse: Yesu apani si, “Mlata kutu tɔ mlenu abee dzedze. Kese onete ɔtɔ eze, lɛ awà wain ɔnyrɔ ɔ, akpɛ kuka aklà ni lɔ̀, lɛ etu lizokpo akí wain yɛ kukakà ɛ, lɛ awa ɔnyrɔ nɔ bepepe ba ligba gyɔgyɔ ɛtɔ ɛ. Lɛ ahayi wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ akí ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba, lɛ atrɛ ní kude me e.

english_verse: Jesus had gone into the temple and was teaching when the chief priests and the leaders of the people came up to him. They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Yesu edzi aba Yerusalem Kasɔlipea mɛ; lɛ gi azɛɛbasɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛ, kusɔliɔ baka ba ni banɔ tsitsi ba bɛba yɛ klɔ lɛ beʋi ye si, “Kigagla woli wɔmɔ xe wɔɛbitɛ eboa la ya elu u? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aki wɔ kudeo?”

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Nifɔ Yohanes yɛ kigaglɛ ki dɔ gi kɛ azɛɛkɔ akpɛ kuni yo aki banima: Aya klɔ loo banima klɔ na?” Lɛ bekpese eboe la kuŋwlɛ ní ba atsali kusi si, “Liboe woli lɛ kwiapani i? Xe kwibu le onu si, ‘Dɔni Aya kɔ ɛ,’ aaʋi blo si, ‘Kɔ ege lɛ loso mlázi Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ?’

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think John was a prophet, and we are afraid of what they might do to us. That's why we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe kwipani si, ‘Dɔni banɔ kɔ ɛ,’ kwise lifune kidiliɛ gi banima biabitɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ba petee bɛzi lɛ si Yohanes onu Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ.”

english_verse: His son told him he would not do it, but later he changed his mind and went.
translation_verse: Ebu le onu si, ‘Mápɛ si miwa axwɛ,’ pɔ̀ lɛ kede e, ekyi yɛ kugyodi yo lɛ atrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛka atrɛ ní obinyime ʋlaɛ klɔ, lɛ asi yɛ nite me liboe le ké. Ebu le onu si, ‘yoo, mɔka maadzɛ,’ pɔ̀ ɔ́dzɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Which one of the sons obeyed his father? “The older one,” the chief priests and leaders answered. Then Jesus told them: You can be sure tax collectors and prostitutes will get into the kingdom of God before you ever will!
translation_verse: Ba tiaʋa ɛ, bamɛ owoli abitɛ ní yɛka kupɛpɛɔ aʋa na a?” Bebú le onu si, “Obinyime tsitsi ye.” Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Sugba, mɛsi mlɔ si: babɔgyɔ ba ni bawawadze ba betu ɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ lɛ beti eku ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ.

english_verse: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si gi Yohanes kuni yo okpɛkpɛɛ aba mlɔ klɔ xe azaabasɛ mlɔ kude gbã gi mlaga ɛ, mlázi yɛ aʋa ŋa; pɔ babɔgyɔ ba ni bawawadze ba bɛzi yɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa. Lɛ gi mlɛmɔ nite fɛ ɛ, mlékyi mlɔ begyodi ʋa xe mlazi yɛ aʋa ŋa a. Abee ní wain ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba su

english_verse: When it was harvest time, the owner sent some servants to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Agi lipoe le lina kí wain yɛ kugugu yɔ ɛ, akpɛ yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba atsisi yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba klɔ atɔ bazɛ yɛ ɔgyɔ lɔ amani yɛ.

english_verse: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bevu yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba a, bekusi tole, beye ɔtɔ, lɛ bɛsa ɔtɔ fɛ akla na ɛ.

english_verse: He then sent more servants than he did the first time. But the renters treated them in the same way.
translation_verse: Onyimee edzi akpɛ yɛ baxwɛdzɛ dzedze batɔ gi bɛsrɛ sani batɔpu ba, lɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bɛ bitɛ ba nite me ke.

english_verse: Finally, the owner sent his own son to the renters, because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Gɔgɔɛ, atsisi yebinyime ní baklɔ. Maka lɛ aʋa si, ‘biakpɛ kubùbù yo ní mobi ye su.’

english_verse: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
translation_verse: Nite si bɛ ʋã yɛ gbli, bu ye dono ní wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ beye ye e.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “When the owner of that vineyard comes, what do you suppose he will do to those renters?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ba si, “Abla xe wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee aba ɛ, ege aabitɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ bepepe ba na a?”

english_verse: The chief priests and leaders answered, “He will kill them in some horrible way. Then he will rent out his vineyard to people who will give him his share of grapes at harvest time.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bebu le onu si, Lɛ mɛ lidra si aaye benyime zimizi ba klɔ ʋa, kɔ akɔ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aki ɔnyrɔ aʋa bedidi dzedze, gi bakɔ biakɔ yɛ ɔgyɔ lɔ mani yɛ xe wain yɛ ligúpoe le lina ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You surely know that the Scriptures say, ‘The stone the builders tossed aside is now the most important stone of all. This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, “Mlaʋíkla e ʋidi ní Aya yɛ liboe le me si, ‘Likla ɛnɛ klɔ gi beglitrɔ ba begbe si líma ní liboeboe me e, lɛ lidzi likla veve le koŋ ní la petee kusi. Ɔkaɛ yɛ abitɛ lɛ nite; linu liboe gi litu blo kugyɔ ɔ!’

english_verse: I tell you God's kingdom will be taken from you and given to people who will do what he demands.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso mɛɛsi mlɔ si, Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ biazɛ ka ní mlɔ awla kɔ kí banama gi biayo esebi gbã na ɛ.”

english_verse: Anyone who stumbles over this stone will be crushed, and anyone it falls on will be smashed to pieces.”
translation_verse: “Ɔniliɛ gi aagleʋe dɔ ní likla ɛlɛ ya aʋa ɛ, avini kukpe kukpe. Pɔ̀ xe likla ɛ lɛ ya lia wɔli dɔ ɔnutɔ aʋa ɛ, liahɔ yɛ pliplipli.”

english_verse: So they looked for a way to arrest Jesus. But they were afraid to, because the people thought he was a prophet.
translation_verse: lɛ loso bɛ pɛ si beevu ye. Pɔ̀ bezese linɔkpi le, gi lizi lɛ si Yesu onu Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ liboe le si, “Bɛxwa banɔ glegle pɔ̀ basisamiatɔ ko bebú ba mɛ ɛ.” Yesu apani ní igyɔ kutsatsa su

english_verse: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Lipoe tɔ kede, Farisianɛ ma bɛsɛ lɛ bɛzɛbitɛ kunyanya tɔ bɛkɔ ɛ punu Yesu i vu gani beʋiʋi me.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, Mose asi blɔ sì xe onyime ɔtɔ agbani ɔdzɛ pɔ̀ bápɔ bebi xe etse e, lilɛ si yɛ oneminyime ɔgbani ɔtrɛtrɛ yedze tɔ bapɔ bebi aki yɛ ɔtrɛtrɛɛ.

english_verse: Once again Jesus used stories to teach the people:
translation_verse: Yesu egu kunugu yo aki ba dzɛ ní abee ʋa mɛ si,

english_verse: The kingdom of heaven is like what happened when a king gave a wedding banquet for his son.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa kalɛ ŋwisi nite gi okusi ɔtɔ eze e xe azɛɛgbani ɔdzɛ aki yebinyime e. Lɛ edu ɔdɔŋakplɔ aki banima a.

english_verse: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
translation_verse: Akpɛ yɛ banɔsumu ba sì bazɛ si banɔxwaxwa ba tɔ baba kudzɛgbanɔɔ ɔdɔŋa klɔ, pɔ̀ bádimɛ ní lɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: He sent other servants to say to the guests, “The banquet is ready! My cattle and prize calves have all been prepared. Everything is ready. Come to the banquet!”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso akpɛ banɔsumu dzedze nì ole kí banɔxwaxwa ba si: ‘Mɛ ɔdɔŋakplɔ nɔ ni kidɔ kakaa kiflɔ; meyé mɛ egumenyime na nì ádzɛ la gi eku kumu yɔ ɛ, lɛ kidɔ kakaa kina a, mlaga ba ní kudzɛgbanɔɔ liwe le me.’

english_verse: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔxwaxwa ba báfuɛ ɛ, ba axwɛ na ɔza ko bezee di: batɔ bɛtrɛ ní ba inyrɔ nɛ mɛ, batɔ kyɛ bɛtrɛ ní ba axwɛ na mɛ,

english_verse: Others grabbed the servants, then beat them up and killed them.
translation_verse: lɛ banɔ gɔgɔ ba bevu yɛ banɔsumu ba, bebu kinie ɛki ba lɛ beye ba ɛ.

english_verse: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
translation_verse: Isu iyeʋi okusi ye nyani nyani; niteme si akpɛ yɛ baguta ba lɛ beze ye banɔye ba klɔ ɔ xe bɛkani ba ɔma nɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
translation_verse: Lɛ axwa yɛ banɔsumu ba lɛ asi ba si, ‘Mɛ obi ye kudzɛgbanɔɔ liwe le lina, pɔ̀ banama gi maxwa ɛ bégyeno aki lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Go out to the street corners and tell everyone you meet to come to the banquet.”
translation_verse: Le loso e, mlatsanì ɔma nɔ bede ʋa tɔ mlaxwa banima kisrɛsrɛ liɛ ko gi mlamɔ ɛ amani kudzɛgbanɔɔ liwe le me.

english_verse: When the king went in to meet the guests, he found that one of them wasn't wearing the right kind of clothes for the wedding.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Okusi ye etiku tɔ yidi bababa ba ɛ, amɔ onyime ɔtɔ gi ɔ́kpɛ kudzɛgbanɔɔ liwu le e.

english_verse: Tell us what you think! Should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Si blɔ, kitɛ wogyodi? Liyɔ kedu ní blɔ ɔsã nɔ su si kutsa ɔgyɔ ɔki Roma banɛ ma ɔʋaŋaɛ loo kukí tsa na a?”

english_verse: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
translation_verse: Nite ke liba lɛ mɛ ɛki yeneminyime ʋla ɛ ní ɔtanɛ aʋa a, riɖiɖi kɔzɛna ogloele aʋa, ba tiegloele ba petee bɛgbani ɔdzɛɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́pɔ obi xe etse e.

english_verse: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkpɛ ba badɔkpasi ba ni banɔatɔ gi bɛdɔ Herodes yɛ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ mɛ ní Yesu klɔ ɔ, lɛ bɛpani si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, kwite sì wónu ɔsia nɛ ɛ. Lɛ wɔ kudɔbasɛ yɔ kuli ní Aya yɛ kudeo aʋa ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ, wɔʋíkpɛ isu kunugu ní ɔnɔ gbagbe yɛ begyodi ba su u, lɛ ese linu si wɔkɔ litɔtɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki ba ozekɔ

english_verse: Let me see one of the coins used for paying taxes.” They brought him a silver coin,
translation_verse: Mlabasɛ mɛ kibɔ ɛ liɛ klɔ gi bizɛ kɔ ɛtsa ɔgyɔ lɔ ɛ!” Agi bɛkɔ kibɔɛ mani yɛ ɛ,

english_verse: “The Emperor's,” they answered. Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.”
translation_verse: Bebu le onu si, “Kaisaro binɛ.” Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Nite kɔ ɛ, mlakɔ kidiliɛ gi kinu Kaisaro kiniɛ akí Kaisaro, tɔ mlakɔ Aya kiniɛ akí Aya.”

english_verse: His answer surprised them so much that they walked away. Life in the Future World (Mark
translation_verse: Agi benu liboe lɛ ya ɛ, litu ba kugyɔ, lɛ bɛyurɔ yɛ xe bɛsɛ ɛ. Yesu apani ní bekpo ʋa kuyɔyɔ su

english_verse: At last the woman died.
translation_verse: Gɔgɔ ɛ, ɔdzɛɛ kyɛ etse.

english_verse: All the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets are based on these two commandments. About David's Son (Mark
translation_verse: Mose yɛ Isã nɛ pɔ́ nì Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ bɛ like ne lɛ ya.” Nyaŋwɛ Yebi onu Kristo ye

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe people would rise to life after death. So that same day some of the Sadducees came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Ní liwe lɛ klɔ ke aʋa ɛ, Zadukianɛ batɔ gi bizedo si bekpo ʋa batrádzi ligbã́ tɔ ɛ, bɛba Yesu klɔ nì kuʋíʋi sì

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki ba si, “Mlɛ ta kese koŋ! Lɛ ese linu si mléte Aya yɛ liboe le loo Aya yɛ kigagla ɛ.

english_verse: When God raises people to life, they won't marry. They will be like the angels in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xe bekpo ʋa bɛyɔ xe bedzi ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, biaze ŋwisi Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba kinɛ ní lifunu ne lɛ batrá gbani badzɛ loo zeni benyime.

english_verse: And as for people being raised to life, God was speaking to you when he said,
translation_verse: Ableke ní bekpo ʋa kuyɔyɔɔ su i, mlaʋíkla ʋídi liboe lɛ gi Aya asi mlɔ na a? Apani si,

english_verse: “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living.
translation_verse: ‘Mɛ menu Aya akí Abraham, Aya akí Isak, ni Aya akí Yakob.’ Yɛ onu Aya akí banɔ gbagbe ba, aní kí bekpo ʋa.”

english_verse: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
translation_verse: Agi linɔkpi le linu liboe le e, ba inugu itse ní yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ su. Yesu abasi bidɔ ní ɔsaa nɔ gi ɛsa kedzi kuzɛ ɛ su

english_verse: After Jesus had made the Sadducees look foolish, the Pharisees heard about it and got together.
translation_verse: Agi Farisianɛ ma benu si Yesu amwa ene na akpɛ Zadukianɛ ma onu i, bɛ kpɛ likpɔ,

english_verse: “Teacher, what is the most important commandment in the Law?”
translation_verse: Eʋi ye si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, Ɔsã woli ɛsa kedzi kuzɛ na a?”

english_verse: This is the first and most important commandment.
translation_verse: Lɔ ya onu ɔtɔpu nɔ gi ɛsa kedzi kuzɛ ní isã nɛ kusi i.

english_verse: The second most important commandment is like this one. And it is, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Ɔʋla lɔ gi eyi lo o lɔ onu si: ‘Lɔ wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ ɔtutɔ wɔ isu ya.’

english_verse: While the Pharisees were still there, Jesus asked them,
translation_verse: Agi Farisianɛatɔ bɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛ, Yesu eʋi ba si,

english_verse: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
translation_verse: “Ɔka Aya asi mɛ Ɔkaɛ si: ‘Ze niya ní mɛ kaŋa mɛ kɔkpɛ ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi makɔ wɔ ahwafianɛ ma kukpɛ ní wɔ kukliseo.’

english_verse: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
translation_verse: “Kitɛ mlebu ní Kristo su u? Nyaŋwɛ Yebi onu u?” Bebu le onu si, “Dawid yɛ lipo me obi onu.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “How then could the Spirit lead David to call the Messiah his Lord? David said,
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi ba si, “Kɔlɔ ege lɛ loso Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛki si Dawid ɔxwa yɛ si, ‘Ɔkaɛ’? Lɛ ese linu si Dawid apani si,

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be a son of King David?”
translation_verse: Xe nite Dawid axwa yɛ si, ‘Ɔkaɛ’, kɔ kitɛ mɛ Kristo aaze Dawid yɛ lipo me obi i?”

english_verse: No one was able to give Jesus an answer, and from that day on, no one dared ask him any more questions.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani le onu kubu kí Yesu u, lɛ dɔnɔ liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa kɔtsinɔ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani yɛ liboeboe kuʋi tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi aabitɛ isu ya ɔnɔ ʋidi i, Aya aaple ni ye lɛ ɔniliɛ gi aaple ni isu ya ɛ, Aya aamu ni ye. Yesu ebu kusɔliɔ baka ba gi benu aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba lipɔ ɔ

english_verse: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
translation_verse: Lilɛ si, ɔnɔ ʋidi ye ní mlɔ kusi i, yɛ oze mlɔ ɔnɔsumu ye.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Yerusalem, Yerusalem! Wɔ gi wòye Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba, xe wɔ̀sa Aya yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba gi atsisi wɔ ɛ, akla na! Tasɛ mapɛ si mikpɛ wɔ bebi ba likpɔ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔkukɔne lɔ izeewe lɔ bebi ʋa ni siwlasasa sɛ, pɔ̀ wɔ́ki mɛ kude e!

english_verse: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
translation_verse: Niteme si, xe ɔnutɔ aka kitamu nì ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ ɛ, aka kitamu ni ɔsuʋɔ lɔ ni kidɔ kakaa gi bɛki ní lɔ aʋa ɛ;

english_verse: Jesus said to the crowds and to his disciples:
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, Yesu apani aki anɔkpi la ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba si,

english_verse: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law are experts in the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: “Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma ba bɛki kigaglɛ tɔ bebu Mose isã nɛ ese.

english_verse: So obey everything they teach you, but don't do as they do. After all, they say one thing and do something else.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, lilɛ si mlabitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi biasi mlɔ si mlabitɛ ɛ aʋa, tɔ mlezeni liboe kakaa gi biasi mlɔ si mlabitɛ kude e; pɔ̀ mlakikpasi ba kiboebitɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si baʋíga ní ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
translation_verse: “Bizɛbitɛ liboe kakaa tɔ banima bamɔ ba. Mledi igɛmi nɛ gi bɛŋwlimi Aya liboe le ni lɛ aʋa a gi bizɛkɔ ɛ nya ba kutu yo, ni ba iwla lɛ, tɔ mlevu aŋu ní kiliɛ gi bikeme su i! Able mlevu aŋu dzɛ tɔ mledi kiliɛ gi ba igɛmi nɛ idze ní ba ewu la su i!

english_verse: They love the best seats at banquets and the front seats in the synagogues.
translation_verse: Bɛdimɛ ni izekɔ gyɔgyɔ lɛ ní bidɔ mɛ iŋaklɔ nì egbo gbã na ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ ɔ;

english_verse: And when they are in the market, they like to have people greet them as their teachers.
translation_verse: Bízɛpɛ si ba pani ba nì kubùbù ní ene na mɛ nì tɔ baxwa ba si, ‘Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ.’ Líma si baxwa mlɔ si.

english_verse: But none of you should be called a teacher. You have only one teacher, and all of you are like brothers and sisters.
translation_verse: “ ‘Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ’ lɛ ese linu si mlɔ pɔ́ ɛ, benemi ko mlenu lɛ ɔdɔbasɛ tole ko ɔlɛ ní mlɔ awla.

english_verse: Don't call anyone on earth your father. All of you have the same Father in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlakíxwa ɔnɔnɔ ní kesea aʋa ya si, ‘Ɔka a’, lɛ ese linu si Ɔka tole ko ɔlɛ ní mlɔ awla ní lifunu ne.

english_verse: None of you should be called the leader. The Messiah is your only leader.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlakíki si baxwa mlɔ si, ‘Ɔnɔgbanɔ’, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ɔnɔgbanɔ tolee ko onu Kristo ye.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba. Mlɛtrɛ ní batrɛtrɛ ba bedze ba klɔ si mlɛɛpɔni ba, pɔ̀ kɔ ɛ, mlemeni ba kɔ mlɛzi ba kudɔkpa ɛ. Kɔ lɛ kede kɔ mlalɛ ni banima aŋu na mɛ, kɔ mlɛɛxwa Aya lipoe gblamadza tɔ mlɛkɔ emeni ba. Mlɔ kutuhehe o kɔ kwianyani sanɔ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You travel over land and sea to win one follower. And when you have done so, you make that person twice as fit for hell as you are.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba! Mlɛdzi kaale ni kaʋa tɔ mleekyi ɔnɔ, pɔ̀ xe mlemɔ ɔnɔ ɛki Aya ɛ, kɔ mlɛbitɛ yɛ ɔnɔ zimizi gi aasɔ̀ ni kifumoni ye me sani mlɔ tutɔ lɔ ɛ!

english_verse: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ kuŋupoo, gi mlɛɛgbani banima ɛ! Mlɛɛbasɛ si, ‘Xe ɔnutɔ aka kitamu ni Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, kitamu yɛ kínya yɛ; pɔ̀ xe aka kitamu ni Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ sika ɛ, kɔlɔ kitamu yɛ kinya yɛ.’

english_verse: You blind fools! Which is greater, the gold or the temple that makes the gold sacred?
translation_verse: Kuŋupo ni baga! Liwoli lisa ɛveve e, sika yɛ loo Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ gi lɛ liki si sika yɛ su adra na a?

english_verse: You also teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the altar. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gift on the altar.
translation_verse: Mlɛɛbasɛ dzɛ si, ‘Xe ɔnutɔ aka kitamu ni ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ ɛ, ɔsã nɔ óvu ye e; pɔ̀ xe aka kitamu ni bidɔ kɔkɔ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa ɛ, kitamu yɛ kivu ye.’

english_verse: Are you blind? Which is more important, the gift or the altar that makes the gift sacred?
translation_verse: Kitɛ mlɔ bimbi ʋe bini te! Biwoli bísaɛ veve e, bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ loo ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ gi lɔ ɛki bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ su bidra na a?

english_verse: And anyone who swears by the temple also swears by God, who lives there.
translation_verse: lɛ xe aka kitamu ni Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, aka kitamu yɛ ni lɛ ni Aya, gi yɛ yɛ̀ ozekɔ ɛ;

english_verse: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
translation_verse: lɛ xe ɔnutɔ aka kitamu ni lifunu ne e, aka kitamu ni Aya yɛ likusigbo le ni Aya gi yɛ odi ní lɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ, Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ! Mlɛzɛɛki Aya oni me bikpɛdɔ flafla dɔni mlɔ inyɔ nɛ mɛ bɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa, pɔ̀ mlɛyurɔ Ɔsã nɔ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ bɛ likenè ŋwisi, eboe me kutsatsa gbã, asigagadidi ni kuʋaziŋa. Mlabitɛ ní la ya aʋa pɔ̀ mlakiyurɔ agɔgɔ la a.

english_verse: You blind leaders! You strain out a small fly but swallow a camel.
translation_verse: Kuŋupo banɔgbanɔ ba! Mlɛyurɔ eboe nyipu nyipu na lɛ mleeti eboe fiteete la xunyɔ ɛ!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba! Mleplo mlɔ kupliʋi yo ni kuzi yo kedea, pɔ̀ kɔmɛ kwiyi ni bidɔ bɛ klɔ gi mlɛzɛ ni kigagla ni kuŋazi i.

english_verse: You blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of a cup, and then the outside will also be clean.
translation_verse: Wɔ Farisiɔnɛ! kaŋupo! Plo kepliʋia ni kezia mɛ gbã, kɔ ka kedea kyɛ kɛdra ɛ!

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba! Mlalɛ ŋwisi idrɔ lɛ gi bedzà akalo ekɔ kinɛ, gi iipedi ní oge no o pɔ̀ ni kimɛ mɛ kɔ ɛ, iyí ni bekpo ba axwa ni bidɔ fumufumu.

english_verse: You Pharisees and teachers are nothing but show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You build monuments for the prophets and decorate the tombs of good people.
translation_verse: “Litraze gagla ɛki mlɔ, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba, Mlɛwa Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba idrɔ lɛ, lɛ mlɛlulɔ banɔ gbã ba idrɔ lɛ kyɛ ɛ;

english_verse: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlɛsi isu ya si, atɛsi kwize blɔ baka ba lipoe le e, nya kúkpɛ inugu ni Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba kuyeye me.

english_verse: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
translation_verse: Nite si mlɛzi lɛ si mlɔ mlenu besoebi ba aki banama gi bá beye Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba a!

english_verse: You are nothing but snakes and the children of snakes! How can you escape going to hell?
translation_verse: Mlɔ bagblaga ʋa ni bagblaga bebi ʋa! Kitɛ mlegyo edi si mlaase ní lipɔ bubu le gi liakpɛ mlɔ ní kifumoni ye me na a?

english_verse: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ ní lɛ mɛ si: sugba ɛ, kutuhehe kí kunɔye uliɔ pɔ́ kwiba mlɔ abla bebi ba pɔ́ aʋa a! Yesu yɛ kulɔlɔɔ ki Yerusalem

english_verse: You won't see me again until you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Dɔnɔ abla kɔtsinɔ ɛ, mɛsi mlɔ si, mlatrámɔ mɛ tɔɔ kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi mlaapani si, ‘Aya ɔkrira ɔniliɛ gi atrába ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me e.’ ”

english_verse: After Jesus left the temple, his disciples came over and said, “Look at all these buildings!”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu edo ní Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ mɛ xe azaasɛ ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba beti i pɔni yɛ si tɔ odi ligba lɛ

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Ee, pɔ̀ mlɛɛmɔ agba la ya petee e, mɛɛsi mlɔ si, sugba ɛ, akla ana petee biatsra la, lɛ litɔtɔ lítrawɔ ní litɔtɔ aʋa a.” Yesu apani ní kunugu kpɛkpɛ ni keduyɔyɔ su

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will say they are the Messiah, and they will fool many people.
translation_verse: Banɔ glegle biaba ní mɛ linyi ne me si, ba benu Kristo ye gi biameni banɔ glegle e.

english_verse: You will soon hear about wars and threats of wars, but don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Mlaanu bigu ʋɛ ni agutaboe la onu ní idzage ne, pɔ̀ mledi lɛ aʋa si lifu likíku mlo, eboa la elu aaba la mɛ, pɔ̀ kelea lidupoe le liʋína a.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, his disciples came to him in private and asked, “When will this happen? What will be the sign of your coming and of the end of the world?”
translation_verse: Lɛ kede agi Yesu odi ni olif ye ise le lito le aʋa ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba beti apɔni yɛ, lɛ beʋi ye si “Si blɔ lipoe lɛ gi eboa la petee aaba la mɛ ɛ. Lɛ kidiliɛ gi kiabu wɔ kubabaa ni kelea lidu le kubasɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
translation_verse: Kese kiayɔ kedu ní kese su, lɛ kukusi kwiata kigũ ni kukusi, kuʋɛ kwiadɔ lɛ kese kiahwa ni ikɔ glegle e.

english_verse: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Liaze gagla ɛki bamɛdzɛ ba nì bebi ne ba ní ewe ala klɔ mɛ!

english_verse: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Bɛya elue biaze ŋwisi kuyeʋiyeʋi ní kubipɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You will be arrested, punished, and even killed. Because of me, you will be hated by people of all nations.
translation_verse: Lɛ biabu mlo kɔ si tɔ behe kutu aki mlɔ nì tɔ beye mlo, lɛ kelea pɔ kiatrɔ mlɔ ahwafi kɔkpɛ mɛ loso o.

english_verse: Many will give up and will betray and hate each other.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, banɔ glegle biayurɔ ba kuziŋaa. Lɛ biatrɔ ba ahanuglɔ ba ahwafi, biabu ba ahanuglɔ ba kukɔ.

english_verse: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
translation_verse: Niteme si asia na beboedoplɛ ba biaŋwi gi biameni banɔ glegle e.

english_verse: Someone may say, “Here is the Messiah!” or “There he is!” But don't believe it.
translation_verse: “Niteme si, xe ɔnutɔ asi wɔ si, ‘Di, imɔ Kristo ye ní ya!’ loo imɔ yɛ ní kɔlɔ ɛ!’ wukízɛ yɛ aʋa ŋa a.

english_verse: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Mleʋi Aya tɔ mlɔ kuseseo kukídɔ ní kublɔ lipoe le loo ní isu mɛ liyɔwe le aʋa!

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” in the holy place, just as the prophet Daniel said. Everyone who reads this must try to understand!
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, “Mletramɔ, ‘Litugu ne ní Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ’ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi oboedoplɛɛ Daniel apani i. Litralɛɛ ní ɔdradra klɔ.” ɔdɔkla yɛ ovu aŋu tɔ onu liboe lɛ ya ese.

english_verse: If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: “Kɔlɔ ɛ, banama gi bali ní Yudea kesea mɛ ɛ, bese atrɛ ní eto la mɛ.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔlɛ ní yɛ ɔnyrɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔkidzi itrɛ kepea si yatrɛɛkɔ yɛ ewu la a.

english_verse: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya atí ní ewe la aʋa kooko; xe ɔ́bitɛ nite e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́traze ligbã. Kɔkpɛ yɛ banɔxwaxwa ba, loso e Aya atráti ní ewe la aʋa.

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Niteme ke xe mlɛɛmɔ eboa la gi ɛɛba la mɛ ɛ, mlete si lipoe le lina, lɛ lina kevunu ya a.

english_verse: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kubaba ɛ, kwiaze ko ŋwisi nite gi lize ní Noa lipoe le me e.

english_verse: No one knows the day or hour. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ ote, liwe le loo ɔnyɔsasa nɔ, Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ní lifunu ne loo Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kyɛ béte e, Ɔka Aya tete ko ete.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
translation_verse: Kpɛ ɔtɔ kí kuni yiyio e, banima bɛzɛŋa, lɛ bɛzɛŋwɛ kyɛ, benyime nì badzɛ bɛzɛgbani baglɔ ʋa, kɔzɛna nani liwe gɔgɔ lɛ aʋa gi Noa emu eku ní onitsahu lo me e.

english_verse: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bamɔ liboe lɛ gi lizɛba egyesi kɔzɛna nani lipoe lɛ gi kuni yiyio kwiba ɛgba ba ɛsɛ nɔ ɛ. Nite ké litraze xe Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaba a.

english_verse: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, benyime tiaʋa biazɛwa axwɛ ní ɔnyrɔ mɛ, biasɛ ni tole, lɛ ɔʋla ɛ, biayurɔ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding grain, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Lɛ badzɛ tiaʋa biaze ní lihɔkla nɛ aʋa a; biasɛ ni tole, pɔ̀ ɔʋla ɛ, biayurɔ yɛ.

english_verse: So be on your guard! You don't know when your Lord will come.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso e, mleze ní aŋu plɛplɛ mɛ, lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe lɛ klɔ gi mlɔ Ɔkaɛ aaba a.

english_verse: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
translation_verse: Xe kepea ɔkaɛ ete elesi la lipoe lɛ klɔ gi oze ye aaba ɛ, wɔaka ní lɛ aʋa si aaze ni aŋu, lɛ ɔ́traki kude xe oze ye aatiku ní yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Always be ready! You don't know when the Son of Man will come. Faithful and Unfaithful Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlɔ fɛ mlabla aplɛ, lɛ ese linu si Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaba ní ɔnyɔsasa lɔ klɔ aʋa gi mlaʋídi yɛ kudeo onu i. Ɔnɔsumu ye osumu ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe onu ɔnɔsumu nyani nyani i, aasi isu ya sì, yɛ ɔkaɛ ɔ́tratsɔ odzi i,

english_verse: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: “Nite kɔ ɛ, nyaŋwɛ onu ɔnɔsumu gbã nì oboete e? Yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ɔkaɛ akɔ edu ní banɔsumu dzedze ba onu u, tɔ aaki ba bá kiŋaŋaɛ ni lipoe le aʋa.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Liaku ɔnɔsumu liɛ klɔ isami xe yɛ ɔkaɛ adziba tsani yɛ gi eedi kepea aʋa mumɔ ɛ!

english_verse: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Sugba mɛsi mlɔ si, yɛ ɔkaɛ aakɔ̀ ɔnɔsumu liɛ klɔ du ní yɛ bidɔ mɛ petee onu.

english_verse: Suppose this evil servant starts beating the other servants and eats and drinks with people who are drunk.
translation_verse: kɔ ekpese yɛ bahanuglɔ banɔsumu ba kukusi, lɛ aaŋa kɔ aŋwɛ ni badaŋwɛmu ba ɛ.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
translation_verse: Xe ɔnɔsumu liɛ klɔ yɛ ɔkaɛ aadzi i liwe lɛ gi odi yɛ kudeo onu i, ní ɔnyɔsasa lɔ klɔ gi óte aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
translation_verse: Yɛ ɔkaɛ aatsà yɛ kukpe kukpe lɛ aaki si yɛ nì banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ ba biaze ní kakpá tiele me. Niklɔ aabemi xe aata yɛ ene na a.

english_verse: The foolish ones took their lamps, but no extra oil.
translation_verse: Idisami kunukpadzeo kwikɔ lɛ kani ʋa pɔ̀ kúvu kumu ipɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The ones who were wise took along extra oil for their lamps.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ idisami beboete ba ɛ, bevu kumu ní kuʋeo me epɔni ba kani ʋa.

english_verse: Then in the middle of the night someone shouted, “Here's the groom! Come to meet him!”
translation_verse: “Elesi la tetsu i, ozi lo eyi si, ‘ɔdzɛnyee aba loo. Mledo aba tsani yɛ!’

english_verse: When the women got up and started getting their lamps ready,
translation_verse: Lɛ idisami liɔfɔ lɛ inyime e, lɛ imuni lɛ kani ʋa kuklekleo e.

english_verse: the foolish ones said to the others, “Let us have some of your oil! Our lamps are going out.”
translation_verse: Lɛ idisami kunukpadzeo kwisi beboete ba si, ‘Mlapɔli mlɔ kumu kutɔ aki blɔ ɔ; blɔ kani ʋa bíha kuni.’

english_verse: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
translation_verse: Idisami beboete ba bebu le onu si ‘Owo!’ ‘kumu liɔ gi kuli ní blɔ awla ɛ, kútrana blɔ nì mlɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso e, mlaze ebu aki isu ya ní kɔ badàdà ba klɔ ɔ.’

english_verse: But the groom replied, “I don't even know you!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔdzɛnyee ebu le onu si mɛɛsi mlɔ si, méte mlo.”

english_verse: So, my disciples, always be ready! You don't know the day or the time when all this will happen. A Story about Three Servants (Luke
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ abee si lɛ loso e, mleze ni aŋu lɛ ese linu si mléte liwe le e loo ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɔ, gi maadziba ɛ. Yesu ebu abee ni banɔsumu tiata ba su

english_verse: Some time later the master of those servants returned. He called them in and asked what they had done with his money.
translation_verse: “Lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ kede e, besumubi ba ɔkaɛ edzì niteme si eʋi ba kiliɛ gi bɛwa kibɔɛ su axwɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You could have at least put my money in the bank, so I could have earned interest on it.”
translation_verse: Agi wote si nite malɛ ɛ, ege lɛ loso wɔ́kɔ mɛ kibɔɛ ɔzɛɛkɔ ɔkpɛ ní kibɔɛ ɔbla klɔ ɔ, xe kiapɔ trɔ kímɛ, xe ableke madzi na a.’

english_verse: The kingdom is also like what happened when a man went away and put his three servants in charge of all he owned.
translation_verse: “Yesu atrɛ ni yɛ abee ba aʋa si dzɛ, lifunu ne kaʋaŋaa kiaze ŋwisi kiliɛ gi onyime ɔtɔ azɛɛsɛ atrɛ ni kude me e xe axwa yɛ besumu bi ʋa a, lɛ akɔ yɛ bidɔ mɛ akpɛ ni ba awla kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ ɔkaɛ apani si, ‘Wɔbitɛ mumɔ, wɔpɛ, wonu osumubi gbã, wodo anukwarɛ nì kibɔ sisami liɛ ya, madu wo ní eboe ʋidi onu. Ba tɔ gamɔ isami ni mɛ!’

english_verse: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
translation_verse: “Yɛ ɔkaɛ edo si ‘Wɔbitɛ mumɔ, wonu osumubi gbã, wodo anukwarɛ nì kibɔ sisami liɛ ya. Madu wo ní eboe glegle onu. Ba tɔ gamɔ isami nì mɛ!’

english_verse: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
translation_verse: Nite si mese lifu lɛ mazeekpo wɔ kibɔ ɛ ni sise se me. Ɔka di imɔ wɔ kibɔɛ, zikɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: The master of the servant told him, “You are lazy and good-for-nothing! You know I harvest what I don't plant and gather crops where I haven't scattered seed.
translation_verse: “Lɛ ɔkaɛ apani si, ‘Wɔ ɛ, ɔ́flɔ ni litukpo me gagla osumubi wonu, kɔ wote si mizeebu bidɔ niklɔ gi mákpɛ kididɔ ɛ, lɛ mizɛɛŋwlɛ bidɔ eŋa ní níklɔ gi mehle kididɔ ɛ

english_verse: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
translation_verse: “ ‘Abla ɛ, mlazi kibɔ okotoku tole lo ní yɛ awla tɔ mlakɔ kɛ aki kibɔ ikotoku liɔfɔ lɛ onete e.

english_verse: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa liɛ awla gi kidɔ kilɛ ɛ, biapɔnɔ ɔki yɛ, tɔ aamɔ sanɔ; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ awla gi kididɔ kíma ɛ, kisisami liɛ gi kiliɛ yɛ awla ɛ, biazɛ kɛ.

english_verse: You are a worthless servant, and you will be thrown out into the dark where people will cry and grit their teeth in pain.” The Final Judgment
translation_verse: Osumubi ɔ́flɔ liɛ ya xunyɔ ɛ mlebu yɛ aŋwia kpɛ oŋwe no ní litumu ne me. Niklɔ aabemi xe aazeɔta yɛ ene na.’ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaxwa litsi nì ɔnɔ kakaa

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes in his glory with all his angels, he will sit on his royal throne.
translation_verse: “Xegi Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aba ni sikusi me ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba petee, aaze ní yɛ likusigbo le aʋa,

english_verse: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
translation_verse: nite si kelea mɛ banima petee biaxogo ba ni yɛ aŋu na mɛ. Kɔ alɛ bamɛ ní kupɛ tuʋa ɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ ko gi oʋesi gbanɔ yɛ izeebu iʋesi le lidze ni bamu ʋa su kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He will place the sheep on his right and the goats on his left.
translation_verse: Aadu banama gi bɛbitɛ ní yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa ɛ, ni yɛ kaŋaa mɛ, kɔ atsini bagɔgɔ ba ni yɛ kaba mɛ.

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
translation_verse: Kɔ Okusi ye asi yɛ kaŋaa mɛ bezeze ba si, ‘Mlaba, mlɔ Mɔka akrira, mlagazɛ mlɔ ɔŋa klɔ ni kaʋaŋaa gi abla aplɛ kimlɔ dɔni kelea likpese le e.

english_verse: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
translation_verse: Gi kuʋɛɔ kwize kume e, mlɛki mɛ kiŋaŋa, olile ezeku me lɛ mlɛki mɛ kuni; Meze ɔbaba lɛ mlɛzi mɛ kpɛ mlɔ kupeo me.

english_verse: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
translation_verse: Meze ni liya lɛ mlɛkpɛ kusa ní mɛ su i; Mazɛɛnya lɛ mledi mɛ aʋa ɛ, gi meze ligba fɛ ɛ, mlɛba edi me.’

english_verse: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
translation_verse: Lɛ lipoe woli kwimɔ wɔ gi woze ɔbaba xe kwizi wɔ ɛkpɛ blɔ kupeo me e loo woze ni liya xe kwikpɛ kusa ni wɔ su u?

english_verse: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Lɛ lipoe woli kwimɔ si wɔ isu mɛ igɔ ɔ, loo woze ligba, xe kwiba edi wo o?’

english_verse: The king will answer, “Whenever you did it for any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you did it for me.”
translation_verse: Nite si Okusi ye aabu le onu si, ‘Mɛsi mlɔ anukwarɛɛ si, lipoe kakaa gi mlɛbitɛ nite me eboa la elu ɛki mɛ benemi ba ya mɛ tole e gi ɔnɔnɔ oʋíbù i, mɛ mlɛbitɛ lɛ ɛkɔ.’

english_verse: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
translation_verse: “Kɔ asi banama gi bali ní yɛ kabãa mɛ si, ‘Mlaseza ní mɛ su, mlɔ Aya exo ifi le akpɛ. Mlazatrɛ ní kifumoni ye gi bɛkpɛ plɛ kí Ozimizi ye ni yɛ bapɔnɔpɔnɔ ba mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
translation_verse: Kuʋɛ kwiku me pɔ̀ mlɛda aŋu na mláki mɛ kiŋaŋa a, olile eku me mláki mɛ kuni i;

english_verse: I was a stranger, but you did not welcome me, and I was naked, but you did not give me any clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail, but you did not take care of me.”
translation_verse: Meze ɔbaba pɔ̀ mlazi mɛ akpɛ mlɔ kupeo me e. Meze ni liya mlegbe mlákpɛ kusa mɛ su u. Gi mazɛɛnya ɛ xe meze ligba kyɛ ɛ, mlákɔ litɔtɔ ní le mɛ aki mɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
translation_verse: Kɔ banima bebu le onu ki yɛ si, ‘Ɔkaɛ, lipoe woli koŋ kwimɔ wɔ gi kuʋɛ kwiku wo, loo olile ezeku wo, loo woze ɔbaba, woze ni liya, wɔ isu ya mɛ igɔ ɔ, loo woze ní ligba xe kwikyí kede kpɛ wɔ na a?’

english_verse: The king will say to them, “Whenever you failed to help any of my people, no matter how unimportant they seemed, you failed to do it for me.”
translation_verse: Niteme si Okusi ye aasi ba si, ‘Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, lipoe kakaa ɛ lɛ gi mlegbe xe mlapɔni mɛ benemi ba ya gi ɔnɔnɔ óʋibu i, mɛ mlapɔnɔ.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Those people will be punished forever. But the ones who pleased God will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso biatsisi ba ní kutuhehe moblomoblo me e. Kɔ bɛtsisi banama gi bɛbitɛ ni Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa a, ní ligbã moblo le me e.”

english_verse: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
translation_verse: “Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlete plɛ kinɛ ɛ, betráŋa kutseo lidɔzawe le ní ewe taʋa kede. Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ biakɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kpɛ awla tɔ beku ye ní ose lo su.”

english_verse: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Lɛ edzì atrɛ ní badɔkpasi ba klɔ ɛ, xe eʋi ba si “Sra sɛ ko mlafini idɔ xe mlɛyɔ isu ya mɛ na a? Mledi ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɛna! Bɛtra bu Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kpɛ awla ɛki banɔ zimizi ba.

english_verse: They planned how they could sneak around and have Jesus arrested and put to death.
translation_verse: lɛ bɛbitɛ batrɔtrɔ níklɔ si biavu Yesu ní kikpokpo me tɔ biaye ye.

english_verse: But they said, “We must not do it during Passover, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Mark
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bɛpani si “lima si kwiabitɛ lɛ ní liweŋaŋa lɛ mɛ” “xe aníte e banima biayɔ kedu.” Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ asra kumu akí Yesu ní Betania

english_verse: We could have sold this perfume for a lot of money and given it to the poor.”
translation_verse: “Nya biatani kumu liɔ kudà kedzi ʋidi gi biakɔ kibɔɛ ɔki bepiplinete ba xe e!”

english_verse: But when his disciples saw this, they became angry and complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi ba beyeʋi isu gi bɛmɔ nite e, lɛ beʋi si “Ege lɛ loso ezizi kumu yɔ nite e?”

english_verse: Then they sang a hymn and went out to the Mount of Olives. Peter's Promise (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛza ɔdzi tole e, bedo etrɛ ní Olif ye ise le ketokpa aʋa a. Yesu edo aplɛ si Petro aanada yɛ

english_verse: and from then on he started looking for a good chance to betray Jesus. Jesus Eats the Passover Meal with His Disciples (Mark
translation_verse: Dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ, Yuda azaapɛ kudeonu gi akɔ Yesu kukpɛ awla ki ba ɛ. Yesu aŋa Kutseo Lidɔzawe le bidɔ mɛ nì yɛ Badɔkpasi ba

english_verse: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
translation_verse: Gi bɛzɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, Yesu apani si “mɛsi mlɔ anukwarɛɛ si, mlɔ mɛ tole atrákpɛ mɛ awla.”

english_verse: On the first day of the Festival of Thin Bread, Jesus' disciples came to him and asked, “Where do you want us to prepare the Passover meal?”
translation_verse: Ni Akla mɔnyasi na liwe tɔpu nɛ aʋa ɛ, badɔkpasi ba bɛba Yesu klɔ lɛ beʋi ye si, “Nifɔ wɔ̀pɛ si kululɔ plɛ ki kutse lidɔzawe le bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ kí wɔ na a?”

english_verse: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
translation_verse: Agi bɛzɛɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, Yesu akɔ lìklã nɛ, lɛ edo Aya ɖase e, xe ayaʋi lɛ ɛ. Lɛ akɔ lɛ aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba ɛ, lɛ asi ba si, “Mlazɛ atà; lɛ lìnu mɛ kisugyaɛ.”

english_verse: This is my blood, and with it God makes his agreement with you. It will be poured out, so that many people will have their sins forgiven.
translation_verse: “Bɛ ya binu mɛ ika lɛ gi bɛkɔ e du kunyanya vuvu yɔ ní Aya ni yɛ banima kusi, mɛ ika lɛ gi bɛbali ní banɔ glegle ba eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ litukpo o.

english_verse: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Mɛsi mlɔ si, Mátraŋwɛ wain liɛ tɔɔ kɔzɛna nì liwe lɛ gi maaŋwɛ wain yɛ dzɛ nì mlɔ ní Mɔka Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples, “During this very night, all of you will reject me, as the Scriptures say, ‘I will strike down the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.’
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu apani asi ba si, “Omono elesi ala ya ɛ, mlɔ pɔ́ mlaase yurɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya liboe le lipani si: “ ‘Aya aaye oʋesi gbanɔɛ, kɔ iʋesi le isehle e.’

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I promise you before a rooster crows tonight, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Yesu asi Petro si, “Sugba ɛ, katsaa gi ɔkukɔ lɔ si loeme elesi la ɛ, wɔanada tatá si wote me.”

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtrɛ ní ɔkutɔ gi bizɛxwa si Getsemane e, lɛ asi ba si, “Mleze ní ya tɔ mitrɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ kɔlɔ tɔ mizɛɛxwa Aya.”

english_verse: and he said to them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: lɛ asi ba si, “mɛ esobo la ɛxixa gbɛgbɛ nite si míhatse. Mleze nì aŋu nì mɛ ní ya.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ebu le onu aki Yesu si, “Xe banima pɔ́ bese yurɔ wɔ kura kyɛ ɛ, mátradzi kede ní wɔ su liwewe e!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you.” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ebu le onu si, “Xe liaze si beye me apɔni wɔ kyɛ ɛ, mátranada si mete wo!” Lɛ badɔkpasi gɔgɔ ba kyɛ bɛpani nite ke e. Yesu axwa Aya ní Getsemane

english_verse: Again Jesus went to pray and said, “My Father, if there is no other way, and I must suffer, I will still do what you want.”
translation_verse: Yesu azɛɛxwa Aya kuʋla si, “Mɔka, xe lítrasɔnɔ si wɔati ɔzia nɔ kepliʋia ní mɛ aʋa a, sani si maŋwɛ ka ɛ, lɛ kɔ ɛ bitɛ ní wɔ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa a.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt with his face to the ground and prayed, “My Father, if it is possible, don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Agi atrɛ ni ɔtɔ nɔ taʋɛ ɛ, adɔ nì yɛ aŋu na mɛ ní kesea. Lɛ aki Aya litukpo si, “Mɔka, xe liapɛ ɛ, tɔ ti ɔzia nɔ kaŋwɛpliʋia lia ní mɛ aʋa! Pɔ̀ aní kiliɛ gi mapɛ pɔ̀ bitɛ ní wɔ kupɛ́pɛɔ onu u.”

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Mark
translation_verse: Mlayɔ! Mlaki tɔ kusɛ! Imɔ ɔniliɛ gi eebu me kɔ ɛ.” Kusɔliɔ baka ba bevu Yesu

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Mlezè ni aŋu tɔ mlaxwa Aya tɔ mlakíku ní kufekedi me. Nya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛdimɛ xe e pɔ̀ kisugyaɛ kibotso o.”

english_verse: He left them and prayed the same prayer once more.
translation_verse: Dzɛ ɛ, Yesu ayurɔ ba, lɛ asɛ atrɛ xe azɛɛxwa Aya kutanɛ nì eboa la klɔ ke e.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “My friend, do what you came for.” The men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Kɔsi ni lɛ su, ɔʋia!” Lɛ linɔkpi le liga xe liba evu Yesu gagla ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu aze gu kunugu yo e, Yuda, badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba mɛ tole e, aba eku ni ba kusi i. Linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ gi kusumu yo baka ba ni banɔtsitsi ba bɛkpɛ ɛ, xe livu ihɛ piɔpiɔ ʋa ni kusekpeo, lize ni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
translation_verse: Yuda gi yɛ ɛkpɛ Yesu awla ɛ, abitɛ kutrɔtrɔ ni linɔkpi le si; “Ɔniliɛ gi yɛ lidzɔ lɛ maamu i, yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi mlɛpɛ ɛ, mlevu ye!”

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yuda eti apɔni Yesu kyɔŋ, lɛ apani yɛ si, “Kusuyɔ ki wɔ, Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ.” Lɛ amu yɛ lidzɔ lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Banama gi beze nì Yesu kude e, bamɛ tole etso yɛ ihɛ piɔpiɔ yɛ lɛ ati kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye yɛ kutukpa ɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus told him, “Put your sword away. Anyone who lives by fighting will die by fighting.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ɔdɔkpasi liɛ klɔ si, “Dzini wɔ ihɛ piɔpiɔ yɛ kpɛ ní yɛ ozeklɔ,” “Banama petee gi bizeetso ihɛ ɛ, biatse ní ihɛ onu.

english_verse: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
translation_verse: Lɛ kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye akyì yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ liwu le tɔ yikɔ ɛbasɛ yɛ kusuyeʋio, lɛ apani si, “Libusioboe! kúdzi uzia ɔnɔ gi eze liboe le katsalia tɔɔ. Mlenu yɛ libusioboe le gi apani i.

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔfuɛ, nì yɛ ɛ. Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye asi Yesu dzɛ si “Maka kitamu akí wɔ ní Aya linyi ne me, si blɔ xe wɔ wonu Kristo ye gi onu Aya Yebi ye e?”

english_verse: When Peter had gone out to the gate, another servant girl saw him and said to some people there, “This man was with Jesus from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Lɛ edo atrɛ ní kepea kevunu ya, níklɔ ɔnɔsumu ɔdisami dzedze ɛmɔ yɛ lɛ ɛsi banɔaklɔ gi beze níklɔ si i, “onyime liɛ eze ni Yesu gi ádɔ Nazaret kude e.”

english_verse: After Jesus had been arrested, he was led off to the house of Caiaphas the high priest. The nation's leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses were meeting there.
translation_verse: Banama gi bevu Yesu i, bɛtsini yɛ ní Kayafa gi onu Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye kepea mɛ, níklɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛtsa.

english_verse: But Peter followed along at a distance and came to the courtyard of the high priest's palace. He went in and sat down with the guards to see what was going to happen.
translation_verse: Petro fɛ eze ní ba kedea ke. Agà riɖiɖi eti eku ní kusumu yo ɔkaɛ kepea mɛ. Eze kesea ní kepea mɛ kɔpɔni kepea bepepe ba tɔ yimɔ kiliɛ gi eboe la aatrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
translation_verse: Kusumu yo baka ba ni Yudanɛ ma betsixwa ba petee bɛpɛ asia tɔ bɛkɔ ɛbu Yesu lipɔ atɔ biaye ye;

translation_verse: pɔ̀ bátani litɔtɔ kumɔ ɔ, linyaze lɛ mɛ si, banɔ glegle bedo bɛba ɔtɔ nɔ lɛ bɛsɛ asia na ɛkpɛ yɛ awla a, gɔgɔɛ benyime tiaʋa bedo bɛba ɔtɔ nɔ,

english_verse: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”

english_verse: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu akí yɛ si, “lilɛ ní lɛ mɛ kiliɛ gi wɔpani i.” Pɔ̀ mɛsi mlɔ si: Lipoe le litrába gi mlɛtramɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye gi odi i ní Aya kigagla ɛ petee onetee yɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ, xe atrába dɔni lifunu ne ní aplɛ la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
translation_verse: Banɔatɔ bɛɛtsí sita sɛ kpɛ Yesu aŋu na mɛ, batɔ bekusi ye xe batɔ kyɛ bɛbɔ yɛ isumu ne e

translation_verse: lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Do liboe plɛ ki blɔ Kristo! Nyaŋwɛ asa wɔ bidɔ mɛ na a?” Petro Anada si yéte Yesu

english_verse: Again Peter denied it, and this time he swore, “I don't even know that man!”
translation_verse: Petro anada dzɛ si, “Maka kitamu si méte onyime liɛ klɔ!”

english_verse: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Petro eze ní kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ kepea kuka mɛ ɛ, ɔdzɛ osumubi ɔtɔ eti apɔni Petro lɛ asi yɛ si, “Wɔfɛ woze ni Yesu Galilea ɔnɛ kude.”

english_verse: But in front of everyone Peter said, “That isn't so! I don't know what you are talking about!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro anada ní ba petee aŋu na mɛ si, “Méte liboe lɛ su gi wɔɛpani i.”

english_verse: A little while later some people standing there walked over to Peter and said, “We know you are one of them. We can tell it because you talk like someone from Galilee.”
translation_verse: Lidze xe banama a gi bɛlɛɛ niklɔ ʋa ɛ, bɛsi Petro si, “Lilɛ lɛ mɛ si bamɛ tole wɔfɛ wonu, wɔ kipaniɛ kibu wɔ ɛkɔ!”

english_verse: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro aplɛ aŋu ní liboe lɛ klɔ gi Yesu asi yɛ aʋa a si: “Xe ableke ɔkukɔ lɔ aame e, wɔanada tatá si wote me.” Edo lɛ aze bemi tiitiiti i.

english_verse: Pilate knew the leaders had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Pilato etè mɔmɔɔmɔ si Yudanɛ ma baka ba ɛ, bɛkɔ Yesu i mani yɛ lɛ ese linu si bɛkɔ kaŋuza nì yɛ.

english_verse: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi bedo ɔma nɔ mɛ xe bɛzɛtrɛ ɛ, bɛtsani onyime ɔtɔ gi ádɔ Kirene gi bizɛxwa si Simon i, baguta ba bemi yɛ aʋa si ɔzɛ Yesu osetsase lo otu.

english_verse: and said, “I have sinned by betraying a man who has never done anything wrong.” “So what? That's your problem,” they replied.
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si i, “Mabitɛ liboe zimizi gi makɔ onyime e gi ɔ́ŋa lipɔ ɛ akpɛ awla kí kutse.” Lɛ bebu le onu kí yɛ si i, “Kitɛ líyi blo e? Wɔ liboe linu!”

english_verse: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bizɛxwa kesea lia klɔ si ìka kese kɔkpɛ nì omono o.

translation_verse: Niteme si liboe lɛ klɔ gi Aya apani i agani oboedoplɛ Yeremya aʋa liba lɛ mɛ si, “Bɛkɔ kibɔ pititi avitata la gi Israelbanɛ ma bɛdimɛ si biatsa ní yɛ litukpo e,

english_verse: They paid it for a potter's field, as the Lord had commanded me.” Pilate Questions Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: bekɔ kɛ edzi ɔdɔʋɔɛ kesea ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔkaɛ aki mɛ ɔsã nɔ kinɛ ɛ.” Pilato eʋi Yesu beʋíʋi

english_verse: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
translation_verse: Yesu alɛɛ ní Roma ɔnɛɛ kesea ɔʋadi ye aŋu mɛ, lɛ eʋi Yesu si, “Wɔ wonu Yudanɛ ma okusi ye na a?” Yesu ebu le onu aki yɛ si, “Nite wɔ̀pani.”

english_verse: And when the chief priests and leaders brought their charges against him, he did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi kusumu yo baka ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛkɔ eboe la ní yɛ su i, óbu litɔtɔ onu u.

english_verse: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu obu liboeboe onu tɔɔ gi lítu kesea ɔʋadi ye kugyɔ ɔ. Pilato atsa kutsépia akí Yesu

translation_verse: Lɛ Pilato ayurɔ Baraba aki ba ɛ; xe aki sì bɛfiaʋi Yesu atam la ɛ. Lɛ kede e, ayurɔ yɛ aki ba tɔ bazeekú ye ní ose lo su. Baguta ba bɛkpɛ Yesu kakpaa

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛzɛga ɛza ɛ besugu ba etukpo la, lɛ bekpini Yesu si:

english_verse: But the chief priests and the leaders convinced the crowds to ask for Barabbas to be set free and for Jesus to be killed.
translation_verse: Kusumu yo baka ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛliɔʋi linɔkpi le tɔ liʋi Pilato tɔ ɔyurɔ Baraba ɔki ba tɔ beye Yesu boŋu.

english_verse: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
