translation_verse: Agi Pilato amɔ si litrabu libiitɔtɔ xe yɛ atrɛ ní lɛ aʋa a, pɔ̀ anyra ko aaga lɛ mɛ kudo e, akɔ kuni yo lɛ eplo yɛ awla la ní linɔkpi le aŋu mɛ, lɛ apani si, “Mɛ ɔwla ɔma ní onyime liɛ ya kutseo me! Mlɔ kiboebitɛ kɛ ya.”

english_verse: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
translation_verse: Lɛ linɔkpi le pɔ́ libu le onu si, “Ki yɛ kutseo kɔ kutuheheo kuba blɔ ni blɔ bebi ba aʋa a!”

english_verse: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: Lɛ beyo bibu ʋɛ likuto le xe bɛkɔ lɛ ekume ki yɛ lɛ bɛkpɛ kiseʋi ní yɛ kaŋawla mɛ; xe bɛdɔ ní ba aklamasi na aʋa ni yɛ ketu ya, lɛ bɛkpɛ yɛ kakpaa ɛ nì eboa la si, “Oheye lo! Yudanɛ ma okusi ye ozè ligbã gblamadzaa!”

english_verse: He trusted God, so let God save him, if he wants to. He even said he was God's Son.”
translation_verse: Aká Aya aʋa lɛ apani si, Aya Yebie yenu u. Xe nite e, mlaki tɔ kudi sì Aya aapɛ si yibu ye abla na!”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Agi bɛkpɛ yɛ kakpaa pɔ́ ɛ, bebu liwu kyíkyĩ nɛ ní yɛ su lɛ bedzini yɛ ɔtutɔ ewu la ɛkpɛ ki yɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛgbani yɛ edono si bɛzekú ye ní ose lo su i. Baguta ba bekú Yesu ní ose lo su

english_verse: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
translation_verse: Ni klɔ bɛki Yesu kuda gi bɛtsa ni bidɔ tiitii bitɔ; pɔ̀ gi akpɛ kɔ onu edi i ɔŋwɛ kɔ.

english_verse: The soldiers nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bekú ye ní osetsase lo su i, bɛlɛ yɛ ewu la gani exoeʋi ʋi kutata mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then they sat down to guard him.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, beze kesea xe bezeepe ye e.

english_verse: Above his head they put a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is Jesus, the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Ní yɛ etukpo la onu i, bekú bidɔ ŋwlimiŋwlimi gi bipani si, “Yesu, Yudanɛ ma Okusi ye liɛ ya.”

english_verse: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
translation_verse: “Wɔpani si wɔayaʋi Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ, lɛ wɔadzini lɛ kuwa ní ewe tata mɛ ɛ! Bú isu ya xe Aya Yebi wonu i, tɔ ple ní osetsase lo su!”

english_verse: Others said, “Wait! Let's see if Elijah will come and save him.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ gɔgɔ ba bɛpani si, “Lɛɛ mlaki tɔ kudi sì Eliya atra bu ye na a!”

english_verse: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
translation_verse: Gi kemizea inyɔ liɔfɔ ni tiʋa isã ɛ, litumu ne lìdɔ ní kesea pɔ́ aʋa inyɔ sasa tita.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Gi inyɔ tita isã ɛ, Yesu akpɛ ozi lo gaglaa si, “Eli, eli, lama sabaxtani?” gi lɛ ese linu si, “Mɛ Aya, mɛ Aya, ege lɛ loso wokyi kede ɔkpɛ mɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
translation_verse: Bamɛ tole ese kyɔŋ, lɛ azɛɛkɔ ɔsapo ɔtɔ, akɔ lɔ akpɛ ní kuda nyagba nyagba mɛ, lɛ ete lo ní ose ɔtɔ onu i, xe akɔ lɔ atɔ si aki yɛ si ɔŋwɛ.

english_verse: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
translation_verse: Ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ kusa gi bɛkɔ ɛtsa lɛ mɛ ɛ, kwikyi idɔnɔ lifune banani kesea. Lɛ kesea kesugu u, xe aklakpɔ la ɛyaʋi i.

english_verse: Graves opened, and many of God's people were raised to life.
translation_verse: Idrɔ lɛ idra lɛ banɔ dradra glegle gi betse e, bɛyɔni ba gi bedzi ligbã.

english_verse: They left their graves, and after Jesus had risen to life, they went into the holy city, where they were seen by many people.
translation_verse: Bedo ní ba idrɔ lɛ mɛ, le gi Yesu ayɔ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, bɛtrɛ ni ɔma ʋidi dradra lɔ mɛ, lɛ banɔ glegle bɛmɔ ba a.

english_verse: The officer and the soldiers guarding Jesus felt the earthquake and saw everything else that happened. They were frightened and said, “This man really was God's Son!”
translation_verse: Agi baguta ba ɔkaɛ ni baguta ba gi beze ni yɛ su u, xe bɛzeepe Yesu u bɛmɔ kesea kusugu yo ni eboa la gi ɛba lɛ mɛ ɛ, lifu liku ba lɛ bɛpani si, “Sugba ɛ, Aya Yebi iliɛ ya!”

english_verse: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
translation_verse: Badzɛ glegle gi beze nì yɛ kude e dɔni Galilea xe bezee di yɛ baziazia ba aʋa ɛ, bezee di bidɔ ɛdɔni ɔdzage no.

english_verse: went and asked for Jesus' body. Pilate gave orders for it to be given to Joseph,
translation_verse: Atrɛ ní Pilato aŋu mɛ lɛ eʋi si ɔkɔ Yesu yɛ okpo ye ɔki yɛ ɛ. Lɛ Pilato aki ɔsã si bakɔ okpo ye akí Yosef.

english_verse: who took the body and wrapped it in a clean linen cloth.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yosef akɔ okpo ye, xe axla kusa pititi vuvu ní yɛ su i,

english_verse: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
translation_verse: lɛ azɛɛkɔ Yesu okpo ye akpɛ ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ɔdrɔ vuvu gi ebu akpɛ ní liklakpɔ ɛtɔ mɛ. Lɛ emli likla ʋidi itɔ etsi ɔdrɔ lɔ kevunu ya xe asɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
translation_verse: Maria Magdaladzee ni Maria ʋlaɛ, bezè niklɔ gi bebu aŋu na mɛ kpɛ ɔdrɔ lɔ ɛ. Bedu ɔnɔ si oze pɛ ɔdrɔ lɔ

english_verse: On the next day, which was a Sabbath, the chief priests and the Pharisees went together to Pilate.
translation_verse: Keletitia gi keze Lizemɔniwe le e, kusumu yo baka ba ni Farisianɛ ma bɛzɛtsani Pilato,

english_verse: They said, “Sir, we remember what this liar said while he was still alive. He claimed in three days he would come back from death.
translation_verse: lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Ɔka, kwiplɛ aŋu si lipoɛ lɛ gi ɔsia nɛ liɛ klɔ ezè ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, apani aplɛ si, ‘Mayɔ ní ewe tata kede’

english_verse: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
translation_verse: Ki ɔsã nɔ tɔ bazeepe yɛ ɔdrɔ lɔ kɔzɛna nì liwetanɛ we le tɔ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bakítani yɛ okpo ye kuzuru tɔ biasi banima si, bɛyɔ ni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ. Asia gɔgɔa la ya anyani sani atɔpu na.”

english_verse: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Pilato asi ba si, “Mlatsini ɔnɔpe; tɔ mlazeetsi ɔdrɔ lɔ onu niteme gi mlaatanɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: So they sealed it tight and placed soldiers there to guard it.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsɛ xe bɛzɛtsi ɔdrɔ lɔ onu i, lɛ bedu ɔnɔpe ye si ozeope ɔdrɔ lɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus' eleven disciples went to a mountain in Galilee, where Jesus had told them to meet him.
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tole ba ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní ketokpaa aʋa ní Galilea ní niklɔ gi Yesu asi ba si batrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
translation_verse: Gi kusumu yo baka ba nì banɔtsitsi ba bɛtsá ɛ, bɛbitɛ kutrɔtrɔ lɛ bɛki baguta ba kibɔ gi kidzi aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and teach them to do everything I have told you. I will be with you always, even until the end of the world.
translation_verse: Tɔ mlabasɛ ba sì babitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi kɛsu makpɛ ɔsã aki mlɔ ɛ. Lɛ mlaplɛ aŋu si, malɛ nì mlɔ lipoe kakaa kɔzɛna nì kelea ligù nɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlakíse lifu, mlazɛ si mɛ benemi ba si batrɛ ní Galilea, lɛ niklɔ biamɔ mɛ ɛ.” Banɔpe ba kunutsatsaa

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake struck, and the Lord's angel came down from heaven. He rolled away the stone and sat on it.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, kesea kɛhwa gagla; lɛ Ɔka Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ eple adɔ ni lifunu ne, lɛ emli likla nɛ gi bɛkɔ etsi ɔdrɔ lɔ onu i azanɔ ɛ, lɛ ezè ní lɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: The angel looked as bright as lightning, and his clothes were white as snow.
translation_verse: Yɛ kiŋwiŋwi yɛ mɛ, kize ŋwisi dzeni kifububu kinɛ, lɛ yɛ ewu la ɛxwa pititi ŋwisi aklala kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The guards shook from fear and fell down, as though they were dead.
translation_verse: Lifu liku banɔpe ba nyani nyani sì besugu nite si bɛfɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi betse na a.

english_verse: The angel said to the women, “Don't be afraid! I know you are looking for Jesus, who was nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ apani aki badzɛ ba sì, “Mlakíse lifu, mete sì Yesu gi beku ní osetsase lo su i, yɛ mlɛpɛ.”

english_verse: Now hurry! Tell his disciples he has been raised to life and is on his way to Galilee. Go there, and you will see him. This is what I came to tell you.”
translation_verse: Mlatrɛ kyɔŋ, tɔ mlasi yɛ badɔkpasi ba sì, ‘Bɛyɔni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ. Eku mlɔ ɔtɔ ɛɛtrɛ ní Galilea, niklɔ mlamɔ yɛ!’ Mlaplɛ aŋu ní liboe lɛ gi masi mlɔ aʋa ɛ.”

english_verse: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, Yesu atsani ba lɛ apani ba si “Kusuyɔ ki mlɔ.” Beti ipɔni Yesu, bevu yɛ kuklikpɔɔ lɛ besumu ye e.

english_verse: While the women were on their way, some soldiers who had been guarding the tomb went into the city. They told the chief priests everything that had happened.
translation_verse: Agi badzɛ ba bɛsɛ ɛtrɛ ɛ, baguta ba ba me batɔ bɛtrɛ ní kusumu yo baka ba klɔ ní ɔma ʋidi lo me. Lɛ bɛsi ba liboe kakaa gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi ba si mlapani si yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba ezuru yɛ okpo ye elesi la gi kwidɔ sra sɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The soldiers took the money and did what they were told. The people of Judea still tell each other this story. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Mark
translation_verse: Banɔpe ba bɛzi kibɔɛ lɛ bɛzɛbitɛ ní liboe lɛ klɔ gi bɛsi ba aʋa ɛ. Niteme si lɛ lize liboe lɛ gi lizɛklanɔ ní Yudanɛ ma kiboedoe onu ga kɔna ni omono o. Yesu ebú isu ya abasi yɛ badɔkpasi ba

english_verse: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eti apɔni ba lɛ asi ba sì, “Aya akɔ kigaglaɛ pɔ́ ní lifunu ne ni kesea aʋa akpɛ ní mɛ awla a.

english_verse: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlatrɛ, ní ɔkɔ kakaa ni banima pɔ́ klɔ tɔ mlabitɛ ba mɛ badɔkpasi: mlakpɛ kuni aki ba ní Ɔka yɛ ni Obi ye ani Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ linyi ne me.

english_verse: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
translation_verse: Agi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, besumu ye, pɔ̀ ba mɛ batɔ bazɛ lɛ aʋa ŋa a.

english_verse: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
translation_verse: Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, bɛyurɔ ba dzikle ʋa ko lɛ bekuni ni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: Jesus walked on and soon saw James and John, the sons of Zebedee. They were in a boat, mending their nets.
translation_verse: Aga atrɛ ɔtɔ nɔ taʋɛ ko lɛ amɔ beneminyime tiaʋa dzedze e, Yakobo nì Yohanes. Beze Zebedeo yɛ bebi. Beze ba katapokpaa mɛ gi bɛzɛɛlulɔ ba dzikle ʋa a.

english_verse: News about Jesus quickly spread all over Galilee. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Niteme si Yesu yɛ linyi ne lihle eku ní ɔkɔ kakaa ní Galilea bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀. Yesu ayɔ ni banyanya glegle

english_verse: It began just as God had said in the book written by Isaiah the prophet, “I am sending my messenger to get the way ready for you.
translation_verse: Likpese ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ oboedoplɛɛ Yesaya aŋwlimi nɛ kinɛ si: “Aya apani si, ‘Maakpɛ mɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ní wɔ ɔtɔ nɔ tɔ aadra kudeo ki wɔ.’

english_verse: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
translation_verse: Yohanes yɛ bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ bize kposɔ sipi liwu; anya yɛ kasɛa mɛ ni kagaplekpa ɔgɛmi, lɛ yɛ bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ bize akikli la nì liŋwafu mɛ kibú.

english_verse: John also told the people, “Someone more powerful is going to come. And I am not good enough even to stoop down and untie his sandals.
translation_verse: Apani asi banima sì, “Ɔniliɛ gi aaba mɛ kede e, ekeme kura sani mɛ. Lɛ mágbɛ gi maaze yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ní kapɛa tɔtɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to the town of Capernaum. Then on the next Sabbath he went into the synagogue and started teaching.
translation_verse: Yesu nì yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtrɛ ní Kapernaum ɔma nɔ mɛ. Gi Lizemɔniwe le lina ɛ, Yesu atrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsa klɔ; lɛ ekpese bidɔ mɛ kubasɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛta yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ kutu i, ba inugu itse, lɛ ese linu si yɛ kiniɛ kiga lidze ní Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ su. Yesu xunyɔ ɛ, abasi bidɔ mɛ ni kigagla.

english_verse: About that time Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
translation_verse: Ni ewe ala klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yesu ádɔ Nazaret ní Galilea kesea mɛ aba a, lɛ Yohanes akpɛ kuni aki yɛ ní Yordan Onipe lo me e.

english_verse: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi Yesu eedo eemu dɔni kuni yo me ko e, amɔ si lifunu ne lizɛɛdra, gi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ eeple dɔ yɛ aʋa ŋwisi abronuma kinɛ na a.

english_verse: A man with leprosy came to Jesus and knelt down. He begged, “You have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”
translation_verse: Onyime ɔtɔ azɛɛnya kunya kyikyĩ yɔ. Lɛ aba Yesu klɔ ɛ. Edu aklamasi kesea lɛ aki yɛ litukpo si ɔpɔni yɛ ɛ. Asi Yesu si, “Xegi linu wɔ kupɛpɛ ɛ, wɔatani kubitɛ gi mɛ isu ya iadra a.”

english_verse: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu yɛ liboe le linu si, “Lipoe le lina ablɔ kɔ; lɛ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ketipɔnɔ ɔ! Mlekyi kede akpɛ mlɔ eboe zimizi na, tɔ mlazi Liboe Gbã nɛ aʋa ŋa!”

english_verse: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi amɔ ba nite ko e, axwa ba. Bɛyurɔ ba ɔkaɛ Zebedeo ni apaa yɛ bebi ʋa ní katapokpaa me ko lɛ ba kɔɔ bɛsɛ xe bekuni Yesu kude e. Yesu akpikpini Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi idono ní onyime ɔtɔ mɛ

english_verse: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
translation_verse: Eti eku ní ɔdzɛɛ aʋa, lɛ evu yɛ kawlakpɔa lɛ apɔni yɛ xe ayɔ ɛ. Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, kuweo kwití yɛ aʋa; lɛ ayɔ xe ekpese ba aʋa kudi i.

english_verse: And when they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you!”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, bɛsi yɛ si, “Ɔnɔ kakaa ɛɛpɛ wɔ.”

english_verse: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, onyime ɔtɔ gi ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi eze yɛ mɛ ɛ, akpɛ ozi tatata ní kutsatsaa mɛ si,

english_verse: “Jesus from Nazareth, what do you want with us? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are! You are God's Holy One.”
translation_verse: “Wɔ Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ, liboe woli wɔɛpɛ ní blɔ su u? Blɔ ɔkyurɔ kɔ wɔba niya putɔ ɔ? Mete ɔniliɛ gi wonu i. Wɔ wonu Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ dradra yɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
translation_verse: Yesu akpɛ ozi ní ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ aʋa si, “Gɔ tsriɖi! Do báza ní ɔnɔɛ mɛ!”

english_verse: The spirit shook him. Then it gave a loud shout and left.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ esugu onyimee gaglaa, ɛkpɛ ozi tatata, lɛ edo eza ní ɔnɔɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone was completely surprised and kept saying to each other, “What is this? It must be some new kind of powerful teaching! Even the evil spirits obey him.”
translation_verse: Banima pɔ́ ba inugu itse, lɛ bekpese ba ahanuglɔ ba kuʋi si, “Liboe woli lɛ ya a? Bidɔbasɛbasɛ vuvu woli lu bɛ ya a? Onyime liɛ kɔ kigagla kilɛ yɛ awla gi izɛɛki ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔsã a, lɛ izeenu kutu yɔ fɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “We must go to the nearby towns, so that I can tell the good news to those people. This is why I have come.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ebu lɛ onu si, “Lilɛ si kutrɛ ní kumami uliɔ klɔ gi kwiklani blɔ mɛ ɛ. Liziã si mipani liboe le ní niklɔ fɛ, lɛ ese linu si lɛ ya loso maba a.”

english_verse: Jesus felt sorry for the man. So he put his hand on him and said, “I want to! Now you are well.”
translation_verse: Asigaga la ɛga Yesu nì onyimee; anina yɛ ɔwla lɔ, lɛ agbɔni onyimee e. Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Linu mɛ kupɛpɛ; wɔ isu ya idra a!”

english_verse: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi Yesu nì yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛsɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní Simon ni Andrea kepea mɛ laa; Yakobo ni Yohanes fɛ beze ba kusi.

english_verse: When they got there, Jesus was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.
translation_verse: Simon yɛnadzee, kuwe kwivu ye plɛ ɔtɔsi aʋa, lɛ gi Yesu eku kepea mɛ ko e, bɛki yɛ kutète kyɔŋ.

english_verse: That evening after sunset, all who were sick or had demons in them were brought to Jesus.
translation_verse: Agi liwe le liku xe ɔlagɔ lɔ ɛna ɛ, banima bɛkɔ ba banyanya ba petee ni banama mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ize e, manɔ ní Yesu klɔ.

english_verse: In fact, the whole town gathered around the door of the house.
translation_verse: Ɔma nɔ pɔ́ ɛkpɛ likpɔ ní kepea kevunu ya.

english_verse: Jesus healed all kinds of terrible diseases and forced out a lot of demons. But the demons knew who he was, and he did not let them speak.
translation_verse: Yesu ayɔni banyanya ba dɔni ba bànya ede ede na mɛ, lɛ akpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi glegle edo no ní banima mɛ ɛ. Pɔ ɔ́ki lɛ kudeo si ipani i, lɛ ese linu si ifuɛfuɛ nɛ ite ɔniliɛ gi Yesu onu i. Yesu apani liboe le ní Galilea

english_verse: Very early the next morning before daylight, Jesus got up and went to a place where he could be alone and pray.
translation_verse: Liwe ʋla lɛ ɔlezatɔ tsitsi no e, Yesu ayɔ, lɛ edo kepea mɛ ɛ. Edo ní ɔma nɔ mɛ lɛ atrɛ ní okpokpo kɔ ɔ, niklɔ axwa Aya ɛ.

english_verse: Simon and the others started looking for him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Simon nì yɛ ɔha lɔ bɛzɛɛklanɔ gi bɛzɛɛpɛ́ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus went to their synagogues everywhere in Galilee, where he preached and forced out demons. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, aga Galilea kesea petee me, lɛ apani liboe le ní ba itsaklɔ ɛ. Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, akpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ebu ní banima mɛ. Yesu ayɔ ni onyime ɔnyanya ɔtɔ

english_verse: At once the man's leprosy disappeared, and he was well.
translation_verse: Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, yɛ isu ya ilulɔ; kunyaa fɛ kwilila ní yɛ su.

english_verse: After Jesus strictly warned the man, he sent him on his way.
translation_verse: Niklɔ Yesu akpɛ onyimee kude ni ɔsã gagla si,

english_verse: He said, “Don't tell anyone about this. Just go and show the priest that you are well. Then take a gift to the temple as Moses commanded, and everyone will know that you have been healed.”
translation_verse: “Ta kutu, di lɛ aʋa si wódo liboeboe si ɔnɔnɔ ní kibitɛɛ liɛ ya su u. Boŋu i, ga laa tɔ trɛ ní Yudanɛ ma kusumu yo ɔkaɛ klɔ; ki tɔ ɔtapo wo odi. Lɛ kede e, tɔ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔmɔ si wɔnya ahaya ɛ, zeeyi ligoʋi lɛ klɔ gi Mose ɔsã nɔ eʋi i.”

english_verse: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi onyimee asɛ ɛ, azeekpese liboe le kufuflu ní ɔkɔ kakaa a. Onyimee yɛ onugu haha lɔ ɛki si Yesu ɔ́zɛɛtani kudo ní ɔha kusi ní ɔma tɔtɔ mɛ ɛ. Kɔ ɛ, ebu isu ya lidze dzaa ní banima su, pɔ̀ banima bɛzɛɛba yɛ klɔ dɔni ɔkɔ kakaa a.

english_verse: It was during the time of Abiathar the high priest. David went into the house of God and ate the sacred loaves of bread that only priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his followers.”
translation_verse: Nite si atrɛ Aya kepea mɛ gi azɛɛta liklã dradra lɛ gi bɛbla plɛ ki Aya ɛ. Liba lɛ mɛ nite lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Abiata eze Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye e. Ni ɔsã nɔ onu i, kusumu yo baka ba ko biata liklã ɛnɛ klɔ xe e, pɔ̀ Dawid atà nɛ lɛ aki yɛ banima kyɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Then so many of them came to the house that there wasn't even standing room left in front of the door. Jesus was still teaching
translation_verse: Banɔ glegle bɛba ɛkpɛ likpɔ níklɔ lɛ loso e, ɔkli ɔkpɛkɔ óze ligba lɛ mɛ kɔmanɔ ní ligba lɛ kevunu ya a. Yesu azɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ asi ba.

english_verse: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
translation_verse: Niklɔ benyime tiene bɛba eku i. Befeke onyime ɔtɔ gi yɛ kapɛ̀ tiele ketse e manɔ ní Yesu klɔ.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they made a hole in the roof above him and let the man down in front of everyone.
translation_verse: Linɔkpi le lɛ loso e, bátani ɔnɔɛ kukɔtsini yɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso bepu liwui ní ligba lɛ ada la mɛ niklɔ gi Yesu eze e. Gi bepu liwui le pɔ́ ɛ, bepleni ɔnɔɛ nì yɛ ɔtɔsi lɔ ɛkpɛ.

english_verse: “Why would he say such a thing? He must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: “Kitɛ mɛ edo igyi le xe apani te e?” Libusioboe lɛ ya! Aya ko onu ɔniliɛ gi aatani eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I will show you that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” So Jesus said to the man,
translation_verse: Mapɛ si mlete si kigagla kiliɛ ní Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye awla ní kesea aʋa si aakɔ̀ eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ ɛ.” Lɛ loso edo asi ɔnyanyaɛ sì,

english_verse: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
translation_verse: “Mɛsì yɔ! Feke wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ tɔ sɛ trɛ kepea mɛ.”

english_verse: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní Galilea Limu ne kutu dzɛ ɛ. Linɔkpiʋidi itɔ liba yɛ klɔ lɛ ekpese le bidɔ kubasɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus finished by saying, “People were not made for the good of the Sabbath. The Sabbath was made for the good of people.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agù yɛ liboe le si, “Lizemɔniwe le ɛ, bɛbitɛ lɛ ɛki banɔ kupɛ̀pɛ, bábitɛ banima aki Lizemɔniwe le e.

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Levi's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners had become followers of Jesus, and they were also guests at the dinner.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, gi Yesu azɛɛŋa bidɔ ní Lewi kepea mɛ ɛ. Babɔgyɔ glegle atɔ ni banama gi bɛta Yudanɛ ma isã nɛ ese e, ba mɛ batɔ bekuni Yesu kude e. Niteme si ba mɛ beglegle bɛkpɛ awla ní kezi me e pɔni Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba.

english_verse: Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees, and they saw Jesus eating with sinners and tax collectors. So they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with tax collectors and sinners?”
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ gi beze Farisianɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ si Yesu azɛɛŋa bidɔ ni banama gi bɛta Yudanɛ ma isã nɛ ese e, ni babɔgyɔ ba lɛ loso beʋi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso Yesu izɛŋa bidɔ nì banɔ ba ya elu na a?”

english_verse: The followers of John the Baptist and the Pharisees often went without eating. Some people came and asked Jesus, “Why do the followers of John and those of the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
translation_verse: Yohanes Kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba ni Farisianɛ ma bɛzɛɛnya onugu. Banɔ batɔ bɛba Yesu klɔ lɛ beʋi ye si, “Ege lɛ loso Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi ba ni Farisianɛ ma ba badɔkpasi ba bizɛɛnya onugu, xe wɔ banɛ ma pɔ̀ baʋínya onugu na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu abee aki ba si, “Mlɛpɛ si banama gi bɛba ɔdzɛɛ ɔgbanɔ klɔ ɛ, baza kuʋɛ na a?” Litrába lɛ mɛ ɛ. Xegi ɔdzɛnyee ɔlɛ basu i, bátranya onugu u.

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ liwe etɔ litraba gi biagbani ɔdzɛnyee sɛnɔ ní basu u. Lipoe lɛ klɔ bianya onugu lo.

english_verse: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋíkɔ lisaba vuvu kukɔlulɔ kusa koko o, lɛ ese linu si kusa kokoo kuapá vu lisaba vuvu nɛ kɔ liwu le likeme esani kiliɛ gi lize tsã ɛ.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined. New wine must be put into new wineskins. A Question about the Sabbath (Matthew
translation_verse: Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ óʋinye kuda vuvu kukɔkpɛ ní kagaplekpa keʋe koko me e, lɛ ese linu si kuda vuvu yɔ kwiaki sì kagaplekpa keʋe kokoa kiata. Boŋu i, lilɛ si benye kuda vuvu yɔ akpɛ ní kagaplekpa keʋe vuvu mɛ.” Kuʋiʋi ní Lizemɔniwe le su

english_verse: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
translation_verse: Yesu azɛɛga aza ní idoli inyrɔ ɛtɔ mɛ ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa. Gi yɛ ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛza ɛ, beku idoli le kuyasi mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus, “Why are your disciples picking grain on the Sabbath? They are not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Nite si Farisianɛ ma bɛsi yɛ si, “Di! Wɔ badɔkpasi ba bɛta blɔ ɔsã nɔ ese, gi bɛɛbitɛ nite ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa ɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Haven't you read what David did when he and his followers were hungry and in need?
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si “Mlákla kidiliɛ gi Dawid abitɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa gi aziã kidiitɔ si yiŋa na a? Kuʋɛ kwizeeku ye nì yɛ banima.

english_verse: So the Son of Man is Lord over the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye onu Ɔkaɛ, gi aŋa Lizemɔniwe le kyɛ aʋa a.”

english_verse: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
translation_verse: Linɔkpi le likeme lɛ loso e, Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si bapɛ́ katapokpa atɔ aki yɛ tɔ banima bakípɔ yɛ akpɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Pharisees wanted to accuse Jesus of doing something wrong, and they kept watching to see if Jesus would heal him on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Banɔatɔ beze niklɔ gi bɛpɛ si beebu Yesu lipɔ ɛ. Lɛ loso e bedu aŋu na ní yɛ aʋa gaglaa tɔ beedi si aayɔni ɔnyanyaɛ ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa na a.

english_verse: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
translation_verse: Yesu asi onyimee si, “Ga lɛɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ ya ní banima aŋu na mɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus was angry as he looked around at the people. Yet he felt sorry for them because they were so stubborn. Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He did, and his bad hand was healed.
translation_verse: Yesu edi ba aklanɔ ní kusuyeʋi me pɔ̀ fɛ ɛ, ba liboe liga yɛ asigaga, lɛ ese linu si ba ina nɛ mɛ izee gagla lɛ ba linɛ lígyɔ ɔ. Lɛ asi onyimee sì: “Nina wɔ ɔwla lɔ!” Anina lɔ ko lɛ edzì iku lɔ kiku ye e.

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples down to the shore of the lake. Large crowds followed him from Galilee, Judea,
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛsɛ trɛ ní Galilea Limu ne kutu lɛ linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ likuni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: Simon was one of the twelve, and Jesus named him Peter.
translation_verse: Banɔ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba klɔ gi akya ɛ, ba benu: Simon .

english_verse: Jesus decided to ask some of his disciples to go up on a mountain with him, and they went.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu asɛ ní klɔ ɛ, emu ketokpa katɔ lɛ axwa banama gi apɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
translation_verse: Yakobo ni yenemi ye Yohanes gi benu Zebedeo yɛ bebi ba a .

english_verse: Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, and Thaddaeus were also apostles. The others were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: Bagɔgɔ ba benu Andrea, Filipo, Bartolomeo, Mateo, Toma, Yakobo gi onu Alfeo Yebi, Tadeo, Simon Kanaa ɔnɛ gi adimɛ nì yɛ kakàsea ɛ

english_verse: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ gi bɛdɔ Yerusalem i, bɛzeepani si, “Beelzebul yɛ ɔkpasi yɛ mɛ! Lɛ yɛ onu ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ɔkaɛ, lɛ yɛ ɛɛki Yesu kigaglaɛ xe ɛɛkpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono o.”

english_verse: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔha ɔtɔ eze Yesu su lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Dike! Wone ni wɔ beneminyime ba ani wɔ benemidzɛ ba balɛɛ ní oŋwe no gi bɛɛpɛ wɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu axwa ba manɔ ɔ, xe egu kunugu yo aki ba ní abee ʋa me si, “Kitɛ Abosam yɛ aatani abosam yɛ kukpikpinɔ dono e?

english_verse: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yesu atrɛ ní kepea mɛ. Dzɛ ɛ, linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ liba gi liki si Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba a, bámɔ lipoe xe biaŋa bidɔ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
translation_verse: Xegi keduyɔyɔ kɛba keseatɔ mɛ gani kifúgɔnigɔni mɛ ɛ, kesea lia klɔ kiayaʋi.

english_verse: So if Satan fights against himself, that will be the end of him.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xegi Abosam yɛ kaʋaŋaa kɛlɛ kluʋikluʋi i, katrátani kuze lipoe gblamadza, kiayaʋi kɔ kedu i.

english_verse: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
translation_verse: “Ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kutiku ní ɔnɔ gagla yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ xe aahali yɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛ, sani si anya ɔnɔ gagla yɛ akpɛ ɔtɔ ɔ, xe ableke aatani kiditɔ kuzuru u.

english_verse: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
translation_verse: “Mɛɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si Aya aakɔ̀ banima eboe zimizi kakaa ni ebusioboe kakaa gi biado e, kukɔkyɛ ba.

english_verse: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aapani liboe zimizi kuyurɔ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛ, batrákɔ ɛkyɛ yɛ liwewe e, lɛ ese linu si abitɛ liboe zimizi gi batrákɔ ɛkyɛ yɛ liwewe.”

english_verse: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu apani nite lɛ ese linu si banɔatɔ bɛpani si, “Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔkpasi ní yɛ mɛ.” Yesu yene ní yɛ benemi ba

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu yene nì yɛ benemi ba bɛba eku u. Bɛlɛɛ ní oŋwe no lɛ bɛkpɛ odu itsisi yɛ si ɔba a.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Nyaŋwɛ onu mone e? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ ba benu mɛ benemi ba a?”

english_verse: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
translation_verse: Lɛ edi banama gi bedi iklani yɛ ɛ, lɛ apani si, “Mledi! mone liɛ ya, lɛ mɛ benemi ba ba ya a!

english_verse: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi izɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi Aya apɛ ɛ, yɛ onu mɛ oneminyime e, nì mɛ onemidzɛɛ ani mone e.”

english_verse: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
translation_verse: Yesu ekpese yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ ní Galilea Limu ne kutu. Linɔkpii lɛ gi lize yɛ ní su i, likeme niteme si, Yesu emu eku ní katapokpa atɔ mɛ lɛ eze ní ka mɛ ɛ. Katapokpaa kɛtrɔ ní kuni yo aʋa lɛ linɔkpi le lize ní limu ne kutu yɔ ɛ.

english_verse: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
translation_verse: Gi azeefuflu amu na ní kayaa mɛ ɛ, átɔ ɛdɔ kudeo me, lɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ kwita la ɛmlɔ ɛ.

english_verse: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
translation_verse: Ebú eboe glegle me aki ba epoe glegle gani abee ʋa mɛ si:

english_verse: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
translation_verse: “Mlata kutu! Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi atrɛ amu na oglu kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
translation_verse: Atɔ ɛdɔ ní akla na mɛ niklɔ gi sise sè sísrɛ ɛ, Lídze ko lɛ amubi la ɛyɛ ɛ lɛ ese linu si sise sè sísrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi liwo le litu u xe kuweo kwiyi i, amuyɛyɛ la ɛyrɔ lɛ etse e. Lɛ ese linu si igũ̀ ne ísrɛ ní la ese e.

english_verse: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
translation_verse: Amubiatɔ fɛ ɛwɔli eku ní bibu ʋe me. Gi bibu ʋe bitsì i, biwà ɛda ní amubi yɛyɛ la aʋa, niteme si amu na ébu libii tɔtɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
translation_verse: Yesu apani dzɛ si, “Ege kwiakɔ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kusrɔnɔ ɔ? Loo abee woli kwiakɔ obu yɛ kaʋaŋaa ese e?

english_verse: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ liboe le liʋívu kese e, lɛ baʋíze gagla ní liboe le me e. Lɛ loso xe kuxixa loo keduyɔyɔ kɛba agani liboe le me e, kɔ bɛɛdzi kede kyɔŋ.

english_verse: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: tɔ ɛ, ‘Biatapo odi dzini kutapo odi, pɔ̀ batrámɔ egyesi i; biata kutu dzini kuta, pɔ̀ batránu kididɔ ese e. Lɛ ese linu si xegi bɛmɔ bidɔ egyesi i, loo benu eboe ese e, biakyi ní ba ina mɛ vu Aya kɔ akɔ akyɛ ba ɛ.’ ” Yesu ebu amugluglu la abee ese

english_verse: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ balɛ ŋwisi amu ana klɔ gi ɛdɔ kudeo su kinɛ ɛ. Xegi benu liboe le ko e, kɔ Satana aba kɔ lɛ asɛ nɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
translation_verse: Yesu atrɛ ní yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ aʋa si, “Nyaŋwɛ izɛsú kani xe izɛkɔ yɛ kpɛ agba yɛ ese loo abati ese e? Aní kani yɛ ose lo aʋa bizɛtrɔ kani yɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
translation_verse: Yesu atrɛ lɛ aʋa sì, “Mlata eboa la gi mlenu kutu mɔmɔmɔ! Kikpɛɛ liɛ gi mlɛkpɛ ɛki banima ɛ, nite ke biakpɛ ɔki mlɔ. Pɔ̀ mlɔ kiniɛ kiasanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní yɛ eboe la aʋa si, “Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kalɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi onyime ɔtɔ azɛɛkpɛ esebi la ní yɛ kayaa mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
translation_verse: Kesea ɔtutɔ lɔ kizɛki sì amu na izeetsi xe izɛɛpɔ ɔ. Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, amu na izedu. Lɛ kede e, kɔ awlakpa la, kɔ ɔmuya lɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
translation_verse: Xegi amu na ɛzã ɛ, kɔ ɔnyrɔmɛdzɛɛ ekpese la kupa ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si la lipa nɛ le lina a.” Abee ní lisebi sisami nɛ su u

english_verse: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
translation_verse: Yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kalɛ ŋwisi lisebi sisami gɔgɔ lɛ gi onyime ɔtɔ azɛɛkɔ akpɛ ní yɛ kesea mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
translation_verse: Lipoetɔ kede e, lisebi le liyɛ gi lidzi ose ʋidi i gi ekeme sani ise gɔgɔ lɛ petee, lɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ kwiwa kɔ evu na ní lɔ aya la mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu ɔ́pɛ si yipani iiki ba a, sani si gani abee ʋa mɛ. Pɔ̀ xegi yɛ tete ɔlɛ pɔni yɛ badɔkpasi ba ɛ, kɔ ebú liboe kakaa ese aki ba ɛ. Yesu ayɔ liwɔ lɛ onu

english_verse: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
translation_verse: Liwe lɛ klɔ ke gi kulagɔ kwina ɛ, Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Mlaki tɔ kutsa limu ne atrɛ ní oge lɔ kɔlɔ”

english_verse: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
translation_verse: Yesu apani yɛ liboe le asi banima ní abee ʋa mɛ ŋwisi ba yà kinɛ ɛ. Apani aki ba kiliɛ onu gi bianu eboe la ese e.

english_verse: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsɛ ní ɔnɔha lɔ su xe badɔkpasi ba bemu iku ní katapokpaa mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ Yesu xunyɔ ɛ, eze ní katapokpaa mɛ koko; ko lɛ ba petee bɛsɛ ɛ. Kutapokpa dzedzeo fɛ kwipɔni yɛ.

english_verse: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
translation_verse: Lɛ lisɛ nɛ ɛ, liwɔ gagla ɛtɔ likpese kufu niteme si kuni yo akpɔ la ɛgbigba eku katapokpaa mɛ gi keyi pɔ́ ɔ.

english_verse: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
translation_verse: Yesu afɛ ní katapokpaa mɛ kedea ɔza lɔ gi adɔ sra ní sumu ye aʋa ɛ. Yɛ badɔkpasi ba benyime ye lɛ beʋi ye si, “Ɔdɔbasɛɛ, wɔ́kɔ lɛ mɛ ɔki blɔ gi kuhatse na a?”

english_verse: Jesus got up and ordered the wind and the waves to be quiet. The wind stopped, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Yesu ayɔ ɔlɛɛ kedu ya. Lɛ akpɛ ozi lo ní liwɔ lɛ aʋa si, “Gɔ tsriɖi!” Lɛ ekyi eku kuni yo akpɔ la aʋa lɛ apani si, “Dɔ zĩi!” Liwɔ lɛ onu liyɔ kyɔŋ lɛ niklɔ petee ɛgɔ kranaa a.

english_verse: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Ege nì lifu mleese e? Kuziŋaa tɔtɔ kúma ní mlɔ awla kɔʋana nì abla na a?”

english_verse: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ akaŋu yɛ atsa ba, lɛ ba mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa eʋi yɛ ɔhanuglɔɛ si, “Onyime woli lu iliɛ ya? Gi liwɔ lɛ ni kuni yo akpɔ la fɛ binu kutu iiki yɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
translation_verse: Lɛ asi yɛ isu ya sì, “Xegi matani Yesu awu la kugbɔnɔ ko e, maahaya.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
translation_verse: Niklɔ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Mɛ obidzɛɛ, wɔ kuziŋaa kwiki si wɔ haya. Ga trɛ ní kusuyɔ mɛ tɔ wɔ kunyaa kuyurɔ wɔ.”

english_verse: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
translation_verse: Bawabitɛ glegle bekeni idi pɔ̀ liboeboe lísɛ ɛ. Ezizi yɛ exo la petee tɔ nya yɛ isu ya mɛ idzi iku lɛ kiku ye, pɔ̀ lɛ́ kinyani boŋ lizɛɛnyani itrɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Gi eti eku ni ba aʋa ɛ, eʋi si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɛpisa nite e? Liboe woli lɛ loso mleebemi nite e? Ɔnuvɔ yɛ oʋítse, sra ko ɛɛdɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yesu nì yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzeku Galilea Limù ne oge lɔ kɔlɔ ní Gerasanɛ ma kesea aʋa.

english_verse: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, idrɔ lɛ mɛ ize yɛ ozeklɔ ɔ. Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́zɛtani yɛ bibɔ ʋɛ kukpɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
translation_verse: Bɛzɛkpɛ yɛ bibɔ ʋɛ ní ikli lɛ ni iwla lɛ epoe glegle, pɔ̀ lipoe kakaa ɛ, azɛɛpa egboo na, kɔ avini bala ʋa ní yɛ ikli lɛ ɛ. Eze gagla niteme si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́zɛtani yɛ aʋa kuŋa a.

english_verse: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
translation_verse: Azɛɛklanɔ ní idrɔ lɛ kusi ní eto la aʋa elesi ni kemize gi azɛɛkpɛ ozi táataa, lɛ azɛɛkɔ akla na akpɛ isu ya ebi la a.

english_verse: When the man saw Jesus in the distance, he ran up to him and knelt down.
translation_verse: Eze ɔdzage no xe amɔ Yesu atsisi i. Lɛ loso e, ese lɛ adɔ́ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa ní Yesu ketu ya ɛ.

english_verse: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
translation_verse: Lɛ akpɛ ozi ni ole gagla sì, “Wɔ Yesu, Aya Ʋidi ye Bi ye! Liboe woli wɔɛpɛ ní mɛ su u? Aya loso e, maki wɔ litukpo si wukíhe kutu iiki mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
translation_verse: Niteme si azɛɛki Yesu litukpo si ɔkikpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono ní kusio klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si ihà likpi itɔ lizɛɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ní lito etɔ su ní niklɔ ʋa ɔza lɔ.

english_verse: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne iiki Yesu litukpo si, “Tsisi blɔ ní ihà lɛ klɔ ʋa mɛ, tɔ ki tɔ kuti iku lɛ mɛ!”

english_verse: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
translation_verse: Banama gi bezeedi ihà lɛ aʋa ɛ, bese lɛ bɛzeeefuflu liboe le kpɛ ɔma nɔ mɛ ni inyrɔ nɛ aʋa a. Banima bedo eba tɔ bedi liboe le kibabaɛ mɛ

english_verse: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
translation_verse: niteme si gi bɛba Yesu klɔ ɛ, bɛmɔ onyime liɛ klɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi likpi le lize bázanɔ ɛ. Bemɔ yɛ gi odi i, akpɛ awu la mɔmɔmɔ gi eze ní sínɔ mɛ, nite si lifu ne liku banima pɔ́.

english_verse: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso banima bɛsi Yesu sì ɔsɛ ní ba kesea mɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́dimɛ ɔ́ki yɛ, lɛ asi yɛ boŋ si, “Dzi trɛ ní kepea mɛ ní wɔ benete ba klɔ tɔ zɛɛsi ba kidiliɛ gi Aya abitɛ akí wɔ nì asigaga glegle ala gi edi wo e.”

english_verse: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi onyimee asɛ nite e, aklanɔ ní Ima Liɔfɔ lɛ pɔ́ mɛ. Asi banima petee kidiliɛ gi Yesu abitɛ aki yɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso banama petee gi benu yɛ kupaniɔ ɛ, ba inugu itse. Yesu ayɔ ni Yairo yɛ obidzɛɛ nì ɔdzɛɛ liɛ su gi ika lɛ kunyaa kwize e

english_verse: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀, banɔ baklɔ gi beze niklɔ ɛ, bɛmwɛ yɛ; lɛ loso, akpikpini ba petee edono ní ligba lɛ mɛ. Banɔ baklɔ gi beti iku ɔnuvɔ yɛ aʋa a pɔni yɛ ɛ, ba ko beze obika yɛ, obinee ni yɛ badɔkpasi tiata ba.

english_verse: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
translation_verse: Yesu edzì atsà limu ne atrɛ ní oge lɔ kɔlɔ aʋa. Gi eku níklɔ ɛ, linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lixogo ní yɛ su.

english_verse: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
translation_verse: Lɛ Yairo gi eze Yudanɛ ma ɔnɔtsitsi ní ba ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, yɛ fɛ aba eku i. Gi amɔ Yesu i, adɔ ní Yesu ketu ya

english_verse: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
translation_verse: lɛ eble yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ kupɛpɛɔ aki Yesu si, “Blɔ Ɔkaɛ! Mɛ obidzɛʋi yɛ eetse gaglaa. Maki wɔ litukpo, ba tɔ gatrɔ awla ní yɛ aʋa atɔ ɔyɔ.”

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ eze ní banima kusi, gi azɛɛnya ɛ. Ika lɛ izedo ní yɛ su agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa nyɔŋ.

english_verse: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
translation_verse: Enu Yesu onu lɛ loso yɛ fɛ ɛ, eti eku ní linɔkpi le me gani kedea.

english_verse: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi anya gbɔni Yesu awu la ko e, ika lɛ gi ííba yɛ su i, itsa kyɔŋ. Enu le ní yɛ isu ya mɛ ké si yɛ ahaya.

english_verse: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
translation_verse: Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, Yesu amɔ egyesi sì kigagla kido ní yɛ mɛ. Ekyi ní ɔnɔha lɔ gi eze ní yɛ su kusi yo lɛ eʋi si, “Nyaŋwɛ agbɔni mɛ awu la ɛ?”

english_verse: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
translation_verse: Yɛ badɔkpasi ba bebu le onu si, “Wɔ tutɔ wɔɛmɔ kiliɛ gi banima bɛsa likpakpa lɛ ní wɔ su i, lɛ woeʋi dzɛ si nyaŋwɛ agbɔni wɔ liwu le na a?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu azeedi aklanɔ si yimɔ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ agbɔni yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
translation_verse: Gi ɔdzɛɛ amɔ kidiliɛ gi kiba kɛ mɛ ɛki yɛ onu i, edo ní banima kusi aba ɔtɔ nɔ. Lifu ne likɔ si azeesugu. Edu yɛ aklamasi na kesea ní Yesu ketu ya lɛ asi yɛ liboe zizi nɛ lɛ kizezee me ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi Yesu azɛɛpani eboa la klɔ ko e, banɔ batɔ bɛdɔ ni Yairo kepea mɛ nì odu iiki Yairo si “Wɔ obidzɛʋi yɛ etse.” Lɛ loso e, “kúkpɛ Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ kunugu tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́kpɛ ba liboe le linɔ ɔ. Lɛ asi Yairo si, “Kúse lifu u; wɔ kɔ zi ŋa ko!”

english_verse: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔ́ki ɔnɔnɔ kude si okuni ba kude sani Petro, Yakobo ni yenemi ye Yohanes ko o.

english_verse: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi beku Yairo kepea mɛ ɛ, Yesu amɔ kiliɛ gi ozi lo ni kubemio kwize ní niklɔ ɛ.

english_verse: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
translation_verse: Nite si ɔdzɛʋi yɛ ayɔ kyɔŋ gi ekpese kuklanɔ ɛ. Azɛ agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa a. Gi liboe le liba lɛ mɛ nite e, banima pɔ́ ba inugu itse e.

english_verse: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
translation_verse: Yesu xunyɔ akpɛ ɔsã aki ba si bakífeke onugu ní liboe le aʋa aki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ. Lɛ asi ba si, “Mlaki ɔnuvɔ ɛ kiditɔ tɔ ɔŋa.”

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
translation_verse: Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtsa limu ne lɛ beple eku ní Genesaret kesea mɛ; lɛ bɛka katapokpaa ní limu ne kutu yɔ ɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
translation_verse: Nite si Yesu asi ba si, “Bizɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba su ní ɔkɔ kakaa. Pɔ̀ ní ba kepea ɛ, ba akɔanɛ ma nì ba benete ba kusi kɔ ɛ, beboedoplɛ ba béyi kididɔ ní ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Herodes enu liboe le e, yɛ kɔɔ asi sì, “Yɛ onu Yohanes Kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ! Maki bɛti yɛ litukpo le, pɔ̀ edzi ligbã a!”

english_verse: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
translation_verse: Yesu yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛtrɛ, bedzi; lɛ bebatsani yɛ ɛ. Bɛsi yɛ eboa la gi etrɛ lɛ aʋa ani bidɔ bɛklɔ gi bɛbasɛ petee.

english_verse: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
translation_verse: Ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa ɛ, Yesu ekpese bidɔ kubasɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ. Banɔ glegle beze níklɔ, lɛ gi benu yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ ɛ, bɛwɔ yaa. Lɛ beʋi si, “Nifɔ yɛ kɔ adɔ ni eboa la ya petee? Kuboeteo liɔ gi bɛki yɛ ɛ, nifɔ kwidɔ? Kitɛ xe izɛɛtani enugutseboe la kubitɛ nite e?

english_verse: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
translation_verse: Aní osetsi yɛ iliɛ ya lò o? Ani Maria yɛ obinyimee, gi yɛ benemi ba benu Yakobo, Yosef ni Yuda ani Simon lò o? Lɛ aní yɛ benemidzɛ ba bali blɔ kusi lò o?” Lɛ loso e, bázɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
translation_verse: Lɛ akpɛ ɔsã aki ba si, “Xe mleku kudeo me e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔkivu kididɔ ɛsani ose vuga vuga. Ɔnɔnɔ ɔkikɔ liklã̀ loo kudɔʋí kepimi loo kibɔ ɛtɔtɔ kpɛ isu kura a.

english_verse: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
translation_verse: Xe mleku ɔma ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ, xe bázɛ mlɔ ɛ, loo bápɛ si bɛzi mlɔ liboe le e, mlasɛ ní ɔmaa lɔ klɔ mɛ. Xegi mlɛɛsɛ ɛ, mlekple ovugbo lo mlɔ kukliseo aplɛ. Bɛ biaze kiseʋi gi mledu ní ba su i.”

english_verse: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
translation_verse: Lɛ Okusi ye Herodes pɔ ɛ, azeese Yohanes, lɛ ese linu si etè si Yohanes eze ɔnɔ gbã, lɛ yɛ isu fɛ idra ní Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ. Lɛ loso e, edi le aʋa si kididɔ kísa Yohanes kura ní ligba lɛ ɛ. Yohanes yɛ eboe pani pani la ezetu Okusi ye Herodes lipoe kakaa gi enu la ɛ; pɔ̀ fɛ azɛɛpɛ si yinu la.

english_verse: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
translation_verse: Niteme si aka bitamu glegle aki ɔnuvɔɛ, lɛ atrɛ lɛ aʋa si, “Matàni kidɔ kakaa si, kidɔ kakaa gi wɔaʋi i, vaa xegi mɛ kaʋaŋaa kapɛ tiele fɛ ɛ, maaki wɔ kɛ.”

english_verse: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Niteme si, ɔdzɛʋi yɛ edo atrɛ ní yene klɔ lɛ azeeʋi ye si, “Mone, ege maaʋi i?” Niklɔ yene asi yɛ si, “Ʋi Yohanes Kuni yo Ɔkpɛkpɛ yɛ litukpo le!”

english_verse: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛʋi yɛ akɔsi edzi atrɛ ní Okusi ye Herodes klɔ, lɛ asi yɛ si, “Mapɛ si kɔ Yohanes Kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛ yɛ litukpo le trɔ ní kitɛɛ aʋa ki mɛ tɔ mizɛ ablahate!”

english_verse: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
translation_verse: Liboe le likɔ si Okusi ye Herodes awɔ ni asigaga mɛ. Pɔ̀ yɛ bitamu ʋɛ gi aka ní yɛ banɔxwaxwa ba aŋu mɛ loso e, ɔ́pɛ si yigbe ɛki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The guard put the head on a serving plate and took it to the girl. Then she gave it to her mother.
translation_verse: Lɛ akɔ lɛ atrɔ ní kitɛɛ aʋa a xe akɔ amanɔ ɛ. Akɔ linɔtukpo le aki ɔdzɛʋi yɛ, lɛ yɛ fɛ akɔ lɛ aki yene e.

english_verse: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi ba benu liboe le e, bɛtrɛ, lɛ bɛzɛkɔ Yohanes okpo ye zevive e. Yesu aki banɔ akpe tietsu bidɔŋaŋa

english_verse: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi banɔ glegle bɛzɛtrɛ xe batɔ fɛ bɛzɛba ɛ, Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bámɔ kudeonu tete xe biaŋa bidɔ fɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso asi ba si, “Mlaki tɔ kubu lidze dzaa atɔ mlazeze mɔni taʋɛ.”

english_verse: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Bemu iku katapokpa atɔ mɛ lɛ beku kudeo me etrɛ ní ɔkutɔ gi ɔnɔnɔ óze e.

english_verse: That evening the disciples came to Jesus and said, “This place is like a desert, and it's already late.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi kelea kekpese kublo e, yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Niya ebu lidze” lɛ “Kelea keeblo nì blo.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “You give them something to eat.” But they asked him, “Don't you know it would take almost a year's wages to buy all of these people something to eat?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlɔ tutɔ mlaki ba bidɔ tɔ baŋa.” Lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Kibɔɛ liɛ gi kwiazizi ní ba aklã na litukpo e, kiana ɔnɔ tole yɛ badzidzi ɔgɔtiaʋa liʋɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ba si, “Aklã tasɛ alɛ mlɔ awla a? Mlazedi.” Gi bedzi itrɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Aklã tetsu ani akpa taʋa.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to tell the people to sit down on the green grass.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si baki tɔ linɔkpi le lize kesea ní ahakpɔ mɛ ní siwa sɛ aʋa.

english_verse: All of them saw him and were terrified. But at this same time he said, “Don't worry! I am Jesus. Don't be afraid.”
translation_verse: Gi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, belulu pɔ̀ Yesu asi ba ni le onu klɔ si, “Mlamɔ ɔna! Mɛ iliɛ ya! Mlakíse lifu u!”

english_verse: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akɔ aklã tetsu la ni akpa taʋa la ɛ. Efeke yɛ aŋu na edi lifunu ne, lɛ edo Aya ɖase e. Ayaʋi aklã na lɛ avini akpa la fɛ mɛ xe akɔ aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba si balɛ aki linɔkpi le petee e.

english_verse: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa aŋa, gi eke e.

english_verse: Jesus' disciples picked up twelve large baskets of leftover bread and fish.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi bɛŋa pɔ ɛ, badɔkpasi ba bɛkpɛ bidɔ mɛ gi bɛŋa ɛgɔ likpɔ, gi biyi kusɔʋi liɔfɔ ni tuʋa a.

english_verse: At once, Jesus made his disciples get into the boat and start back across to Bethsaida. But he stayed until he had sent the crowds away.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba ní katapokpa me tɔ bekú yɛ ɔtɔ nɔ atrɛ ní Betsaida ní limu ne kapɛ ʋla. Yesu apɛ si yikpɛ linɔkpi le kude e

english_verse: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Gi akpɛ banima kude pɔ́ ɛ, emu atrɛ ní lito etɔ aʋa si yixwa Aya.

english_verse: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
translation_verse: Gi kelea keblo e, katapokpaa keze ní limu ne kusi yo; pɔ̀ Yesu tole eze ní oge no o.

english_verse: He could see that the disciples were struggling hard, because they were rowing against the wind. Not long before morning, Jesus came toward them. He was walking on the water and was about to pass the boat.
translation_verse: Amɔ si yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzetutru ni katapokpaa ní limu ne aʋa lɛ ese linu si bɛtsani liwɔ lɛ gi lifu idɔni niklɔ gi bɛzɛtrɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso olezatɔ nɔ ŋwisi ɔkukɔ tɔpu nɔ kimemee me kinɛ ɛ, Yesu aga kuni yo aʋa aba tsani ba. Nya apɛ si yiza ba aʋa xe e.

english_verse: When the disciples saw Jesus walking on the water, they thought he was a ghost, and they started screaming.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ gi azɛɛga kuni yo aʋa ɛ, begyodi si okpo bɛmɔ, niteme si bese lifu ne. Lɛ bɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Okpo ye!”

english_verse: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
translation_verse: Lɛ emu eku apɔni ba ní katapokpaa mɛ ɛ. Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, liwɔ lɛ onu líyɔ miɔɔ. Badɔkpasi ba onugu etse

english_verse: Their minds were closed, and they could not understand the true meaning of the loaves of bread. Jesus Heals Sick People in Gennesaret (Matthew
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si bénu liboe lɛ gi likpasi ní banɔ akpe tietsu ba ba kaʋadi ya mɛ ese e. Ba begyodi ʋa bátani liboe le kuvu kura. Yesu ayɔ ni banyanya ba ní Genesaret

english_verse: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
translation_verse: Agi beple ní katapokpaa mɛ pɔ́ ko e, banima bɛmɔ Yesu igyesi kyɔŋ.

english_verse: So they ran all over that part of the country to bring their sick people to him on mats. They brought them each time they heard where he was.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, banima bese eklani kesea mɛ petee niteme si ɔkɔ́ kakaa gi benu si Yesu ɔlɛ ko e, befeke ba banyanya ba ɛmani yɛ ní ba inyanyatɔsi lɛ aʋa.

english_verse: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔkɔ kakaa gi Yesu atrɛ ɛ, ní kumami yɔ mɛ, ima ʋidi ne me ani inyrɔ nɛ aʋa ko e, banima bɛkɔ ba banyanya ba ɛtsinɔ ní ablɔ ʋa aʋa. Kɔ bɛki Yesu litukpo si ɔki tɔ bagbɔni yɛ awu la kutu yɔ. Lɛ banama petee gi bɛgbɔni yɛ ɛ, ba isu ya mɛ iku igagla a.

english_verse: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
translation_verse: Gi Farisianɛ ma ni Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ bɛdɔ ní Yerusalem xe bɛba ɛ, bexogo ní Yesu su.

english_verse: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ yɛ liboe le aʋa si, “Ni mlɔ kuboeteo me e, mlɛyurɔ Aya isã nɛ ɛplɛ lɛ mlevu isu i kɔ ni mlɔ tutɔ mlɔ isã nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
translation_verse: Farisianɛ ma ni Yudanɛ ma pɔ́, bekuni ba bakatsi ba bidɔbasɛbasɛ bɛ kude, nite si xegi béplo awla la ní ligoʋi le onu i, bátraŋa bidɔ ɔ.

english_verse: None of them will eat anything they buy in the market until it is washed. They also follow a lot of other teachings, such as washing cups, pitchers, and bowls.
translation_verse: Lɛ kidɔ kakaa gi bedzi idɔnɔ kedzia mɛ ɛ, linu si beplo ke ni tɔ beplo ba awla la ní ɔtɔpu kɔ xe ableke biaŋa bidɔ ɔ. Isã dzedze itɔ gi ba bakatsi ba bɛkpɛ ɛplɛ, ŋwisi kupliʋi yo, kudɔtɔfukpoo, ni kudɔkpaa liɔ mɛ bizɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, bɛ kiploploe ní ba egoʋi la onu petee, bɛzɛbitɛ ní lɛ aʋa.

english_verse: The Pharisees and teachers asked Jesus, “Why don't your disciples obey what our ancestors taught us to do? Why do they eat without washing their hands?”
translation_verse: Nite si Farisianɛ ma ni Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba beʋi Yesu si, “Ege lɛ loso xe wɔ badɔkpasi ba kɔ bévu blɔ bakatsi ba ba egoʋi la gi bɛkpɛ ɛplɛ ɔzɔ mɛ na a, xe ba kɔ bizɛɛŋa bidɔ ni awla gi la su ádra na a?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu lɛ onu si, “Yesaya liboe le ligyɔ koŋ gi amɔ liboe edo aplɛ ní mlɔ su i! Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ beboebitɛ mlenu, kiliɛ gi aŋwlimi aplɛ si i: Aya si, “ ‘Banɔ ba ya ɛ, enugumeboe ko bɛkɔ ɛlɔ mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ ba ina nɛ kɔ ívu me kura.

english_verse: It is useless for you to worship me, when you teach rules made up by humans.”
translation_verse: Líziã si besumu me e. Lɛ ese linu si banima isã nɛ aʋa ko bizɛbitɛ ŋwisi lɛ inu Aya yɛ isã nɛ na a!’

english_verse: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
translation_verse: “Mlɛyurɔ Aya yɛ isã nɛ, lɛ mlevu banima isã nɛ ɔzɔ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mlɔ si, xegi kidiitɔ kilɛ ɔnutɔ awla gi aatani kɛ kukɔ odi yɛka loo yene aʋa a, pɔ̀ xe asi ba si kinu Aya kinɛ ɛ,

english_verse: You won't let those people help their parents.
translation_verse: kɔ libasɛ si mlɛki yɛ kudeo si ɔkipɔni yɛka loo yene e.

english_verse: And you ignore God's commands in order to follow your own teaching. You do a lot of other things just as bad. What Really Makes People Unclean (Matthew
translation_verse: Nite mlɛbitɛ, gi mlevu mlɔ bakatsi ba egoʋi la awla a, xe mlɛki si Aya isã nɛ íyi kididɔ tɔɔ ɔ. Lɛ ɛ, eboe glegle dzedze alɛ ŋwisi la ya kinɛ gi mlɛzɛbitɛ ɛ.” Yesu apani ní kidiliɛ gi kizelulu ɔnɔ su i

english_verse: Then Jesus said: What comes from your heart is what makes you unclean.
translation_verse: Lɛ atrɛ ní yɛ liboe le aʋa si, “Kidiliɛ gi kido edɔní ɔnɔse lo me e, kɛ kizelulu ɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Jesus called the crowd together again and said, “Pay attention and try to understand what I mean.
translation_verse: Dzɛ ɛ, Yesu axwa linɔkpi le amani isu ya lɛ asi ba si, “Liboe lɛ gi matrapani i, mlɔ petee mlata kutu tɔ mlenu lɛ ese.

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples had left the crowd and gone into the house, they asked him what these sayings meant.
translation_verse: Gi asɛ ní linɔkpi le su xe atrɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba beʋi ye si obu abee liɛ ya ese ɔki ba.

english_verse: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi ba si, “Kɔ mlɔ fɛ mlénu eboa la ya ese na a? Kidiliɛ gi kidɔ oge no e, kitrátani ɔnɔ kululu u.

english_verse: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ klɔ ɛ, kitiiku ní yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ kiza eku ní yɛ lipo le me kɔ kido eza ní yɛ ɔnɔse lo me e.” Liboe lɛ gi Yesu apani nite e, libasɛ si kidɔŋaŋa kakaa ɛ, kɛ su kidra gi biaŋa fãa a.

english_verse: unfaithfulness in marriage, greed, meanness, deceit, indecency, envy, insults, pride, and foolishness.
translation_verse: kuŋazi, litukpomegagla, kunɔmeno, onugududu, kunɔmobu, kaŋuzã, banɔ enyi kuxwaxwa, kusufeke ani kugadɔdɔ.

english_verse: All of these come from your heart, and they are what make you unfit to worship God. A Woman's Faith (Matthew
translation_verse: Eboe dovo la ya petee ɛdɔ ní ɔnɔɛ kimɛ ɛmɛ lɛ la izelulu ɔnɔ ɔ.” Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ yɛ kuziŋaa ní Yesu me

english_verse: Jesus left and went to the region near the town of Tyre, where he stayed in someone's home. He did not want people to know he was there, but they found out anyway.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yesu asɛ klɔ lɛ atrɛ ní Tiro bidɔ mɛ. Eti eku kepe katɔ mɛ. Ɔ́pɛ si ɔnɔnɔ ote si yɛ ɔlɛ ní klɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ ɛ, ɔ́tani isu ya kukpo o.

english_verse: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi eze ní yɛ obidzɛʋi ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ, enu si Yesu aba ni klɔ, lɛ aba Yesu klɔ kyɔŋ lɛ edu aklamasi na kese ní yɛ ketu ya ɛ.

english_verse: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ eze Heladze gi bɛpɔ ní Foenike bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀ ní Siria kesea mɛ ɛ. Aki Yesu litukpo si ɔkpikpini ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no dono ní yɛ obidzɛʋi yɛ mɛ ɔki yɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “The children must first be fed! It isn't right to take away their food and feed it to dogs.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu asi ɔdzɛɛ si, “Ki tɔ baki banuvɔ ba bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ kpɛ ɔtɔ. Líma si bakɔ banuvɔ ba bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ tɔ bakɔ aŋwia plɛ aki kudroʋio o!”

english_verse: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
translation_verse: Gi ɔdzɛɛ eku yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ, amɔ Yebi ye gi ɔfini yɛ ɔtɔsi lɔ aʋa a, gi ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no edo eza ní yɛ mɛ ɛ. Yesu ayɔ ni otoku omúmu ɔtɔ

english_verse: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yesu asɛ ní Tiro bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀, lɛ aga Sidon xe aba Galilea Limu ne kutu xe azaatrɛ ní Ima Liɔfɔ lɛ bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ.

english_verse: Some people brought to him a man who was deaf and could hardly talk. They begged Jesus just to touch him.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ bɛgbani onyime ɔtɔ gi ɔ́zenu i, lɛ ɔ́zɛtani kunugu yo kugu fɛ ɛmani Yesu klɔ ɔ; lɛ bɛki Yesu litukpo si ɔtrɔ awla ní yɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: After Jesus had taken him aside from the crowd, he stuck his fingers in the man's ears. Then he spit and put it on the man's tongue.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu onyimee lidze dzaa ní banima kusi i. Yesu akɔ yɛ biwlaʋi ʋɛ akpɛ ní onyimee batu ʋa mɛ, lɛ ebu sita sɛ; lɛ agbɔni onyimee kinemi ye.

english_verse: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu efeke aŋu na edu lifunu ne, egu hũu, lɛ asi onyimee ní si Hebri sɛ mɛ si, “Hefata!” Lɛ ese linu si, “Dra a!”

english_verse: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
translation_verse: Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki banima si bakísi ɔnɔnɔ. Pɔ̀ lɛ kiliɛ gi azaakpɛ ɔsã nɔ aki ba si bakífeke onugu ní liboe le aʋa ɛ, nite ke banima fɛ bezehle liboe le.

english_verse: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
translation_verse: Yesu agba ba si, “Mleze ni aŋu tɔ mledi mumɔ ní Farisianɛ ma ni Okusi ye Herodes ba kunyagbãa su.”

english_verse: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
translation_verse: Adra liboe le me asi ba. Lɛ loso Petro ebu ye kuka lɛ ekpese ye kutɛnɔ ɛ.

english_verse: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
translation_verse: “Banɔ ba ya ba liboe liga mɛ asigaga, lɛ ese linu si ewe tatá la ya gi balɛ ní mɛ su u, lɛ ba bidɔŋaŋa bɛ fɛ biblo o.

english_verse: Some of them live a long way from here. If I send them away hungry, they might faint on their way home.”
translation_verse: Xegi médi ba aʋa a, xe makpɛ ba kudeo e, biatɔ ɔpiti ní kudeo aʋa, lɛ ese linu si batɔ ba idɔ klɔ idze e.”

english_verse: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi linɔkpi le si beze keseatsalia. Lɛ akɔ aklã tegloele la a, xe edo Aya ɖase ní la aʋa a. Lɛ ayaʋi la mɛ xe akɔ aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba si bakɔ alɛ̀ aki linɔkpi le e. Niteme si badɔkpasi ba bɛlɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛki ba petee e.

english_verse: They also had a few little fish, and after Jesus had blessed these, he told the disciples to pass them around.
translation_verse: Akpa wli wli klakla ʋi atɔ eze ní badɔkpasi ba awla. Yesu edo ɖase ní la aʋa, lɛ akɔ la aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba si bakɔ la fɛ alɛ aki linɔkpi le e.

english_verse: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi Yesu akpɛ banima ma kude pɔ ɛ, emu eku ní katapokpaa mɛ lɛ atrɛ ní Dalmanuta bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ. Farisianɛ ma bɛpɛ linugutseboe dɔni Yesu iwla mɛ

english_verse: The Pharisees came out and started an argument with Jesus. They wanted to test him by asking for a sign from heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ Farisianɛ batɔ bɛba Yesu klɔ xe bekpese eboe la kuhe ni yɛ ɛ. Bezeekeni ye edi lɛ loso e, beʋi idɔni yɛ awla si ɔbitɛ linugutseboe litɔ ɔki ba tɔ beedi gi liabasɛ si Aya yɛ anya kpɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
translation_verse: Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi ba bedze bákɔ aklã atɔtɔ evu sani tile gi lizee ba awla ní katapokpaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking and asked, “Why are you talking about not having any bread? Don't you understand? Are your minds still closed?
translation_verse: Agi Yesu kɔ ete liboe lɛ gi bɛzeedo onu i, eʋi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɔ kugyodi yo kwitrɛ ní liklã su u? Kɔ mleʋínu eboe la ese fukɔ na a? Kitɛ xe mlɔ begyodi ʋa bɛdɔ sra sɛ nite e?

english_verse: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
translation_verse: Yesu evu kaŋupoa ɔwla lɔ lɛ agbani yɛ edono ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ Yesu ebu sita sɛ atrɔ ní kaŋupoa bimbi ʋe aʋa a. Lɛ kede e, Yesu atrɔ awla ní yɛ aʋa lɛ eʋi ye si, “Wɔmɔ kiditɔ na a?”

english_verse: The man looked up and said, “I see people, but they look like trees walking around.”
translation_verse: Onyimee efeke yɛ aŋu na lɛ ebú isu ya onu si, “Ee, mɛɛmɔ banima; pɔ̀ balɛ ŋwisi ise ko gi iga ɛza kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɔ awla la ní kaŋupoa bimbi ʋe aʋa dzɛ ɛ. Able kɔ onyimee edu yɛ aŋu na ke, lɛ yɛ bimbi ʋe bidra a, nite me si amɔ kidɔ kakaa tsrà a.

english_verse: Jesus said to him, “You may return home now, but don't go into the village.” Who Is Jesus? (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ yɛ kude si ɔtrɛ kepea ni ɔsã si “Kúdzitrɛ ní kamamia mɛ ɛ!” Petro adra Yesu su

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “But who do you say I am?” “You are the Messiah!” Peter replied.
translation_verse: Lɛ eʋi ba dzɛ si, “Mlɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, nyaŋwɛ mlɛɛsɛ si menu u?” Petro ebu le onu si, “Wɔ wonu Kristo ye.”

english_verse: Jesus warned the disciples not to tell anyone about him. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki ba si, “Mlakípani liboe lɛ ya ní mɛ su isi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ!” Yesu apani ní yɛ kutseo su u

english_verse: Jesus began telling his disciples what would happen to him. He said, “The nation's leaders, the chief priests, and the teachers of the Law of Moses will make the Son of Man suffer terribly. He will be rejected and killed, but three days later he will rise to life.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ekpese yɛ badɔkpasi ba bidɔ kubasɛ si, “Lilɛ si, Ɔnɔɛ Ye Bi ye, ɔmɔ ɔziã glegle. Lɛ banɔtsitsi ba, Kusumu yo baka ba ni Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba biadzi kede yɛ su u. Lɛ biaye ye, pɔ̀ ní ewe tata kede e, ayɔ gi aadzi ligbã a.”

english_verse: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ekyi edi yɛ badɔkpasi ba, lɛ aka aŋu mɛ aki Petro si, “Satana, titrɛ ní mɛ su! Wɔ begyodi ʋa bádɔ Aya kɔ ɔ; kisugya mɛ begyodi ba klɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu axwa ɔnɔha lɔ ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba amanɔ ní yɛ isu ya su lɛ asi ba si, “Xegi ɔnutɔ apɛ si yiku ni mɛ kude e, lilɛ si odze yɛ isu ya liboe, otu yɛ osetsase lo tɔ ɔga aze ni mɛ kude e.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ligbã nɛ lialila ɛ, ní mɛ litukpo loo ní Liboe Gbã nɛ litukpo e, aaze ligbã.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself?
translation_verse: Libi woli liaze lɛ mɛ ɛki ɔnɔ, xegi kelea mɛ bidɔ mɛ petee biadzi yɛ binɛ ɛ, pɔ aalila yɛ Esobo la na a?

english_verse: What could you give to get back your soul?
translation_verse: loo ege ɔnɔ aakɔ̀ atsrɛ yɛ Esobo la na?

english_verse: Don't be ashamed of me and my message among these unfaithful and sinful people! If you are, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in the glory of his Father with the holy angels.
translation_verse: Xegi ɔnutɔ ese kuye ní mɛ su aloo ní mɛ liboe le su ní kele zimizia lia ya mɛ gi béte Aya ɛ, kɔ Ɔnɔɛ Ye Bi ye fɛ, aase kuye ní yɛ su xegi aba ní yɛ Ka kusudra mɛ ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ dradra ba ɛ.”

english_verse: I can assure you that Elijah has already come. And people treated him just as they wanted to, as the Scriptures say they would. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ si, Eliya aba kooko, pɔ̀ banima bɛbitɛ ba kupɛpɛɔ aʋa ɛyurɔ yɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi iplɛ ní yɛ su ní Aya yɛ liboe le me kinɛ ɛ.” Yesu ayɔni ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ mɛ gi ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi eze e

english_verse: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
translation_verse: Lɛ kede agi Yesu edzi atrɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba beʋi ye ní kikpokpo me si, “Ege lɛ loso kútani ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kukpikpini dono na a?”

english_verse: Then Jesus asked a child to stand near him. He put his arm around the child and said,
translation_verse: Lɛ akɔ ɔnuvɔ ɔtɔ amanɔ ní ba aŋu na mɛ ɛ. Agi akpɛ awla yɛ ole lo e, agba ba si,

english_verse: And his clothes became much whiter than any bleach on earth could make them.
translation_verse: Yɛ awu la edzi pititi gi eze ŋle; nite si kesea aʋa ɔsaplo ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́tratani kididɔ kuplo xe kiaxwa nite e.

english_verse: Then Elijah and Moses appeared and were talking with Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi tiata ba bɛmɔ Eliya ni Mose gi bɛzɛɛpani ni Yesu i.

english_verse: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Lɛ liplɛ ɛtɔ lido xe liwe ba aʋa ni lɛ kuyeo e. Ko lɛ benu ole ɔtɔ ní liplɛ lɛ mɛ si, “Mobi ye gi malɔ ɛ, yɛ iliɛ ya. Mlata yɛ kutu.”

english_verse: At once the disciples looked around, but they saw only Jesus.
translation_verse: Bedi iklanɔ kyɔŋ, pɔ̀ bámɔ ɔnɔnɔ tɔ ɔ sani Yesu ni ba tete ko.

english_verse: So they kept it to themselves. But they wondered what he meant by the words “raised from death.”
translation_verse: Badɔkpasi ba bɛbitɛ ní Yesu ɔsã nɔ aʋa. Pɔ̀ ní ba tete kusi i, bezegyo eboe la kudi si, “Kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, lɛ pɔ̀ lɛ ese si kitɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: The disciples asked Jesus, “Don't the teachers of the Law of Moses say that Elijah must come before the Messiah does?”
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi Yesu si, “Ege lɛ loso Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛpani si, Eliya lilɛ si otu kuba na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: Elijah certainly will come to get everything ready. But don't the Scriptures also say that the Son of Man must suffer terribly and be rejected?
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Li lɛ mɛ si Eliya yɛ aatu kuba tɔ aalulɔ kidɔ kakaa plɛ. Pɔ̀ fɛ ɛ, ege lɛ loso bɛŋwlimi iplɛ si, Ɔnɔɛ Ye Bi ye aamɔ ɔziã glegle lɛ batrázɛ yɛ na a?

english_verse: When Jesus and his three disciples came back down, they saw a large crowd around the other disciples. The teachers of the Law of Moses were arguing with them.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ni badɔkpasi tiata ba beple edɔ ɔ xe bɛba ɛtsani badɔkpasi gɔgɔ ba ɛ, bɛmɔ si linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ liklani ba. Niteme si Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ bɛzɛŋwlɛ eboe ni badɔkpasi ba.

english_verse: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛgbani yɛ ɛmani Yesu i. Gi ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛmɔ Yesu ko e, ɛŋwia ɔnuvɔɛ sa kesea. Niteme si emli aklanɔ ní kesea, lɛ ɔfɔ lɔ fɛ ɛzedo ní yɛ onu i.

english_verse: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔ kakaa liɛ gi aazɛ banuvɔ ba ya elu ba mɛ tole ní mɛ linyi ne me e, mɛ azɛ. Lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa liɛ gi azɛ mɛ ɛ, aní mɛ tete azɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ azɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ akpɛ mɛ fɛ ɛ.” Ɔniɛ klɔ gi ɔ́yɔ kedu ní blɔ su i, onu blɔ ɔnɛ

english_verse: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi linɔkpi le pɔ́ limɔ Yesu ko e, libitɛ lɛ onugutsetse; lɛ bese trɛ ní yɛ klɔ xe bɛpani yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
