translation_verse: “Oo! Kitɛ xe kumɔziŋaa kulɛ mlɔ omono kelea lia ya te e?” “Lɛ kɔzɛna ni lipoe woli unu mlɛpɛ si mize ní mlɔ su u? Lɛ kɔzɛna ni lipoe woli te onu maabasɛ mlɔ bidɔ kudu u? Mlagbani ɔnuvɔɛ amani mɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ɔnuvɔɛ yɛka si, “Lipoe woli onu kizezee liɛ mɛ kiku ní yɛ su u?” Ebu le onu si, “Dɔni yɛ kunuvɔɔ mɛ ke.”

english_verse: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
translation_verse: Lɛ obika yɛ akpɛ ozi kyɔŋ si, “Kuziŋa kulɛ me awla, pɔ̀ kúsrɛ ɛ. Pɔni mɛ tɔ kusrɛ!”

english_verse: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu amɔ egyesi si linɔkpi le lizeetipɔni ba ɛ, akpɛ ɔsã aki ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no si, “Wɔ kutoku ni omúmu ɔfuɛfuɛ, makpɛ ɔsã aki wɔ si, doza ní ɔnuvɔɛ mɛ lɛ wukídzi batiku ní yɛ mɛ liwewe tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛkpɛ ozi gagla lɛ edugu ɔnuvɔɛ, xe edo eza yɛ mɛ ɛ. Ɔnuvɔɛ afɛ ŋwisi okpo kinɛ. Niteme si ɔnɔ kakaa azeedo si, “Ɔnuvɔɛ etse!”

english_verse: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu evu ɔnuvɔɛ yɛ kawlakpɔa lɛ apɔni yɛ xe ayɔ ɔlɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ɔfuɛfuɛ lɔ ya elu i, Aya kuxwaxwa ko kwiata ni lɔ kukpikpini dono o. Kidɔ dzedze kakaa elu kítratanɔ ɔ.” Yesu apani ní yɛ kutseo su dzɛ

english_verse: Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it,
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛsɛ níklɔ ɛ, bɛga Galilea ɛza. Yesu ɔ́pɛ si ɔnɔnɔ ote niklɔ gi yɛ ɔkpasi i;

english_verse: because he was teaching the disciples that the Son of Man would be handed over to people who would kill him. But three days later he would rise to life.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si azɛɛbasɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bidɔ si: “Ɔnɔɛ Ye Bi ye e, biabu ye kukɔ kí banama gi biaye ye e. Pɔ̀ ewe tata kede e, aayɔ gi aadzi ligbã a.”

english_verse: The disciples did not understand what Jesus meant, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ badɔkpasi ba bénu bidɔ mɛ gi abasɛ ba ese e, lɛ lifu ne fɛ lize ba mɛ ní kiliɛ gi biaʋi ye le ese e. Ɔniliɛ gi yɛ asa keme e

english_verse: If your foot causes you to sin, chop it off. You would be better off to go into life lame than to have two feet and be thrown into hell.
translation_verse: Níklɔ asu ana klɔ gi izɛta ba ɛ, eʋitsé liwewe e, lɛ kifui liɛ klɔ gi kizɛkani ba ɛ, kiʋini liwewe e.

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, bɛba Kapernaum; lɛ gi beti eku kepea mɛ ɛ, Yesu eʋi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Liboe woli mlɛzɛŋwlɛ ní kudeo me e?”

english_verse: They had been arguing about which one of them was the greatest, and so they did not answer.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bébu le onu u, lɛ ese linu si bɛzɛŋwlɛ eboe ní ba tsali kusi ní kudeo me tɔ béte bamɛ iliɛ gi yɛ asa kukeme e.

english_verse: After Jesus sat down and told the twelve disciples to gather around him, he said, “If you want the place of honor, you must become a slave and serve others!”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu eze kesea ɛ, axwa yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba; lɛ asi ba si, “Mlɔ mɛ iliɛ gi apɛ si yize ɔtɔpu yɛ ɛ, ɔkɔ isu ya kpɛ ní ɔgɔgɔ klɔ, tɔ oze mlɔ pɔ́ mlɔ osumusumu ye e.”

english_verse: Salt is good. But if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made salty again? Have salt among you and live at peace with each other.
translation_verse: “Kumɔɔ ɛ, kidɔ gbã kùnu u. Pɔ̀ xegi kɔ ɔgyi nɔ eblo e, kitɛ mlabitɛ xe ɔgyi nɔ aaku kɔ su dzɛ ɛ? “Lɛ loso e, mlaki tɔ kulɔlɔ kuze ní mlɔ kusi ŋwisi kumɔɔ ɔgyi nɔ kinɛ ɛ; atɔ mleze ní kusuyɔ mɛ ni mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba a.”

english_verse: John said, “Teacher, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But he wasn't one of us, and we told him to stop.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yohanes asi Yesu si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, kwimɔ onyime ɔtɔ gi azɛɛkpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono ní wɔ linyi ne me e, lɛ kwisi yɛ si ɔmɔni, lɛ ese linu si ónu blɔ mɛ tole e.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: Don't stop him! No one who works miracles in my name will soon turn and say something bad about me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlakitsi kudeo onu akí yɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani enugutseboe kubitɛ ní mɛ linyi ne me xe lɛ kede e, aapani eboe zimizi kuyurɔ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Anyone who isn't against us is for us.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔ́yɔ kedu ní blɔ su i, onu blɔ ɔnɔ.

english_verse: And anyone who gives you a cup of water in my name, just because you belong to me, will surely be rewarded. Temptations To Sin (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɛɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si, ɔnɔ kakaa gi aaki mlɔ kuni kepliʋi tiele si mlaŋwɛ gi mlenu mɛ banɔ loso e, aazi yɛ liʋɛtsatsa lɛ kikaka elu ye. Kufekedi ki Eboe zimizi

english_verse: It will be terrible for people who cause even one of my little followers to sin. Those people would be better off thrown into the ocean with a heavy stone tied around their necks.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu apani si, “Xegi ɔnutɔ aaki si banuvɔ ba ya ba mɛ ɔtɔ aalila yɛ kuziŋaa ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, xe aabitɛ eboe zimizi i, liapɛ kí ɔniliɛ klɔ sanɔ xegi bɛka kuhɔ ʋidi ní yɛ ole e, xe bɛkɔ yɛ ŋwia ɛkpɛ ní axu la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So if your hand causes you to sin, cut it off! You would be better off to go into life paralyzed than to have two hands and be thrown into the fires of hell that never go out.
translation_verse: Niklɔ asu ana klɔ gi izɛta ba ɛ, eʋitsé liwewe e, lɛ kifui liɛ klɔ gi kizɛkani ba ɛ, kiʋíni liwewe e.

english_verse: If your eye causes you to sin, get rid of it. You would be better off to go into God's kingdom with only one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into hell.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi wɔ kimbi ye kiaki si wɔabitɛ eboe zimizi i, kpaʋi kɛ tuʋe! Liapɛ ɛki wɔ si wɔatiku Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ni kimbi tile sani si bimbi tuʋa bize wɔ awla pɔ̀ tɔ baŋwia wɔ akpɛ ní kifumoni ye me e.

english_verse: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu asɛ ní klɔ ɛ, atrɛ ní Yudea kesea mɛ, lɛ atsa Yordan onipe lo e. Anɔkpi la ɛkpɛ likpɔ ní yɛ su dzɛ, lɛ abasi ba bidɔ mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi azɛɛbitɛ lipoe kakaa kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: A woman who divorces her husband and marries again is also unfaithful.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ niteme ke ɔdzɛ liɛ klɔ gi aagbe yenye e xe aazeni onyime dzedze e, awa kihasi ayurɔ yenye.” Yesu akrira banuvɔ ba

english_verse: Some Pharisees wanted to test Jesus. So they came up to him and asked if it was right for a man to divorce his wife.
translation_verse: Farisianɛ batɔ bɛba yɛ klɔ gi bɛzɛpɛ si bɛ́punu yɛ ɛ. Beʋi Yesu si, “Si blɔ, dani ɔsã nɔ ɛki kude si wɔatani wodze kugbe na a?”

english_verse: Jesus asked them, “What does the Law of Moses say about this?”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu ni kuʋíʋi si, “Ɔsã woli Mose aki mlɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: They answered, “Moses allows a man to write out divorce papers and send his wife away.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ba fɛ bebu le onu si, “Mose aki kude si onyime ɔŋwlimi kegbegbeplekpa tɔ ɔkikɔ kutete si yɛyurɔ ye dze, tɔ ɔyurɔ yɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Moses gave you this law because you are so heartless.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mose aki mlɔ ɔsãa nɔ ya lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ilɛ gagla.

english_verse: But in the beginning God made a man and a woman.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní okpese klɔ ní kelea ɔbitɛ klɔ ɛ, ‘Aya abitɛ ba onyime ni ɔdzɛ.’

english_verse: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and gets married.
translation_verse: ‘Lɛ klɔ ʋa loso e, onyime aasɛ ní yɛka ni yene su lɛ aakpɛ likpɔ ni ye dze e,

english_verse: He becomes like one person with his wife. Then they are no longer two people, but one.
translation_verse: kɔ ba tiaʋa bedzi kidɔ tile!’ Lɛ loso e, bénu banɔ tiaʋa tɔɔ ɔ, bedzi kisugya tile.

english_verse: And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.”
translation_verse: Niteme si kidiliɛ gi Aya akpɛ likpɔ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔkilɛ kɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said.
translation_verse: Agi bedzitrɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ, Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi ba beʋi ye liboe le ese.

english_verse: He told them, “A man who divorces his wife and marries someone else is unfaithful to his wife.
translation_verse: Ebu le onu akí ba si, “Onyime liɛ klɔ gi aagbe yedze e, xe aagbani ɔdzɛ dzedze e, awa kihasi ayurɔ yedze.

english_verse: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, banɔ batɔ bɛmani banuvɔ ba ní Yesu klɔ si ɔtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa; pɔ̀ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛnyra ni ba.

english_verse: I promise you that you cannot get into God's kingdom, unless you accept it the way a child does.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛtrɔ likla lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si, ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́zɛ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ŋwisi ɔnuvɔ kinɛ ɛ, ɔ́tratiku ka mɛ liwewe e.”

english_verse: When Jesus saw this, he became angry and said, “Let the children come to me! Don't try to stop them. People who are like these little children belong to the kingdom of God.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu amɔ ba kibitɛ ɛ, amɔ kusuyeʋi ni ba, lɛ asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Mlaki tɔ banuvɔ ba baba mɔ klɔ; lɛ mlakítsi kudeo nu aki ba a. Lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, banama gi balɛ ŋwisi banuvɔ ba ya elu i, ba kanɛ kenu.

english_verse: Jesus looked closely at the man. He liked him and said, “There's one thing you still need to do. Go sell everything you own. Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come with me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu edu aŋu na ní yɛ aʋa ni kulɔlɔ, lɛ apani si, “Kidɔ tile ko kiziã si bitɛ ɛ. Trɛ zɛɛda kidɔ kakaa gi kilɛ ní wɔ awla tɔ kɔ kibɔɛ kí bazianete ba, tɔ wɔamɔ exo la ní lifunu ne; lɛ kede e, ba tɔ gaze ni mɛ kude.”

english_verse: When the man heard Jesus say this, he went away gloomy and sad because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Agi onyimee enu liboe le e, ayrɔ lɛ asɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si oxonete ʋidi onu u.

english_verse: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu edi aklanɔ ɛ, asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Liaze e gagla kí bexonete ba si biati uku ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Liboe le litu badɔkpasi ba kugyɔ, pɔ̀ Yesu atrɛ ní liboe le aʋa si, “Mɛ bebi ba! Liaze gagla xe ɔnɔ aatani kutiku ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Edo apɔnɔ si, “liaze gagla ki oxonete si oti oku ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ sani ki kposɔɛ si ɔga abui ye liwu le me kudo o!”

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were even more amazed. They asked each other, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Liboe le gi apani i, liwa axwɛ ní yɛ badɔkpasi ba aʋa, lɛ beʋi bahanuglɔ ba si, “Kɔ nyaŋwɛ koŋ aatani kubúbú kumɔ ɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yesu edu aŋu na ní ba aʋa lɛ ebu le onu si, “Lítratani lɛ mɛ kuba ní banima klɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ liboe kakaa lilɛ plɔplɔ ɛki Aya xunyɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: The disciples were confused as Jesus led them toward Jerusalem, and his other followers were afraid. Once again, Jesus took the twelve disciples aside and told them what was going to happen to him. He said:
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba beze ní kudeo aʋa a, xe bɛzɛtrɛ ní Yerusalem i, Yesu etu ɔtɔ aki ba. Liboe le litu yɛ badɔkpasi ba kugyɔ, lɛ banama gi beze ní ba kedea fɛ ɛ, lifu ne lizeku ba. Yesu ebu yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba kuka dzɛ ní kudeo aʋa. Lɛ asi ba eboa la gi ɛtraba yɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: We are now on our way to Jerusalem where the Son of Man will be handed over to the chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses. They will sentence him to death and hand him over to foreigners,
translation_verse: Asi ba si, “Mlata kutu! Kwitrɛ ní Yerusalem. Niklɔ biabu Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kukɔ kí kusɔliɔ baka ba ni Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba. Biatsa kutse pia ɔki yɛ; kɔ bɛkɔ yɛ kpɛ awla ɛki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked them what they wanted,
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ba si, “Ege mlɛpɛ si mibitɛ ki mlɔ ɛ?”

english_verse: and they answered, “When you come into your glory, please let one of us sit at your right side and the other at your left.”
translation_verse: Bebú le onu si, “Xegi wodi ní wɔ likusigbo le aʋa ní wɔ kukusi dradra mɛ ɛ, kwipɛ si ki kuze ní wɔ su: blɔ mɛ tole oze ní wɔ kaŋaa mɛ, tɔ ɔnɔ ʋlaɛ oze ní wɔ kabaa mɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mléte kidi liɛ gi kɛ mleeʋi i. Kuyeʋiyeʋi yo kepliʋia lia gi matraŋwɛ ɛ, mlaatani ka kuŋwɛ na a? Lɛ kuni yo gi lilɛ si bakpɛ aki mɛ ɛ, mlaatani kɔ kukpɛ na a?”

english_verse: “Yes, we are!” James and John answered. Then Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from the cup from which I must drink. And you will be baptized just as I must!
translation_verse: Lɛ bebu le onu si, “Kwiatanɔ.” Yesu asi ba si, “Kepliʋia lia gi matraŋwɛ ɛ, mlaaŋwɛ ka baa, lɛ kiliɛ gi biakpɛ kuni yo ki mɛ fɛ ɛ, mlaakpɛ kɔ.

english_verse: But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for God to decide.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kudeo onu kúma si mɛ mibu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aaze ní mɛ kaŋaa mɛ loo ní mɛ kabaa mɛ ɛ. Aya abla izekɔɛ lɛ klɔ aki banɔ baklɔ gi akya ɛ.”

english_verse: When the ten other disciples heard this, they were angry with James and John.
translation_verse: Agi yɛ badɔkpasi ba mɛ liɔfɔ gɔgɔ ba benu liboe le e, bɛmɔ kusuyeʋio ní Yakobo ni Yohanes su u.

english_verse: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know that those foreigners who call themselves kings like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over the people they rule.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu axwa ba pɔ́ akpɛ likpɔ ní isu ya a, lɛ asi ba si, “Mlete si banɔ baklɔ gi bizeebù si ba benu baʋaŋa ba akí babubitɛ ba ɛ, kigagla kilɛ ba awla ní ba aʋa, nite si baʋaŋa ba bizemi babubitɛ ba ɛkpɛ kesea ni kusufeke e.

english_verse: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔ apɛ si yize ɔtɔpu yɛ ɛ, lilɛ si oze mlɔ petee mlɔ ɔnɔ sumu ye.

english_verse: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Blind Bartimaeus (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye ɔ́ba si tɔ besumu ye e. Boŋu i, kiba aba si yisumu tɔ yikɔ yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛkɔ tɔ yibu banɔ glegle.” Yesu ayɔ ni Bartimeo Kaŋupoa

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples went to Jericho. And as they were leaving, they were followed by a large crowd. A blind beggar by the name of Bartimaeus son of Timaeus was sitting beside the road.
translation_verse: Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛba Yerixo. Agi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba ni linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ beze do ni ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, kaŋupo ɔdɔʋi Timeo Yebi ye Batimeo azeeʋi bidɔ ní kudeo su u.

english_verse: When he heard that it was Jesus from Nazareth, he shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Agi enu si Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ azɛɛza ɛ, ekpese ozi kukpɛ si, “Yesu! Dawid lipo me obi ye, di mɛ asigaga!”

english_verse: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Banɔ glegle bɛka yɛ aŋu mɛ si ɔgɔ tsriɖi. Pɔ̀ akpɛ ozi lo gaglaa sani kutɔpu yɔ si, “Dawid lipo me obi ye, di mɛ asigaga!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and said, “Call him over!” They called out to the blind man and said, “Don't be afraid! Come on! He is calling for you.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atɔ, lɛ asi ba si, “Mlaxwa yɛ aki mɛ!” Lɛ loso bɛxwa kaŋupoa, lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Mɔ ɔna! Yɔ lɛ ɛ! Yesu yɛ ɛɛxwa wɔ ɔ!”

english_verse: The man threw off his coat as he jumped up and ran to Jesus.
translation_verse: Nite si Batimeo ebu yɛ liwu le etu eʋe, ayɔ lixu le, lɛ aba Yesu klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you want me to do for you?” The blind man answered, “Master, I want to see!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ye si, “Ege wɔɛpɛ si mibitɛ ki wɔ ɛ?” Kaŋupoa kebu le onu si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, mapɛ si mimɔ dzɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Ga trɛ, wɔ kuziŋaa kwiyɔ nì wɔ ɔ!” Lɛ onu klɔ koe ekpese bidɔ kumɔ. Nite si ekuni Yesu kude ní kudeo aʋa.

english_verse: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi Yesu eku Yerusalem i, atrɛ ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, lɛ edi kidɔ kakaa aklanɔ ɛ. Pɔ̀, agi kelea keeblo onu i, asɛ atrɛ ní Betania ni yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba. Yesu exo ifi akpɛ ogbose lo

english_verse: That evening, Jesus and the disciples went outside the city. A Lesson from the Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi ɔlagɔ lɔ ɛna ɛ, Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bedo ní ɔma nɔ mɛ. Kudɔkpasi ní ogbose tsetse lo ni kuziŋa su

english_verse: They asked, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi Yesu si, “Kigagla woli wɔmɔ ɔ, xe wɔɛbitɛ eboa la ya elu u? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aki wɔ kudeo o?”

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔnutɔ eʋi mlo si ege lɛ loso mleeble ye na ɛ, mlasi ɔniliɛ klɔ si, ‘Ɔkaɛ yɛ aziã yɛ; lɛ aadzi ni ye kumanɔ kpekpemi i.’”

english_verse: The disciples left and found the donkey tied near a door that faced the street. While they were untying it,
translation_verse: Agi bɛtrɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ tedzi obi ye ní ablɔ yɛ su kepe atɔ kevunu ya. Lɛ gi bezeeble ye e,

english_verse: some of the people standing there asked, “Why are you untying the donkey?”
translation_verse: banɔ batɔ gi bɛlɛɛ níklɔ ɛ, beʋi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mleeble tedzi ye e?”

english_verse: They told them what Jesus had said, and the people let them take it.
translation_verse: Bebu le onu ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Yesu asi ba kinɛ ɛ. Niteme si banɔ baklɔ ba bɛki ba kudeo.

english_verse: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
translation_verse: Agi bɛmani tedzi obi ye e, ní Yesu klɔ ɛ, bebu ba ewu la ɛtrɔ ní ɔgaɛ aʋa, lɛ Yesu emu atrɔ ɛ.

english_verse: In front of Jesus and behind him, people went along shouting, “Hooray! God bless the one who comes in the name of the Lord!
translation_verse: Banɔ ba klɔ gi beze ní ɔtɔ nɔ nì kedea pɔ́ bɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Hosana! Aya ɔkrira ɔniliɛ klɔ gi atrába ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ linyine me e!

english_verse: God bless the coming kingdom of our ancestor David. Hooray for God in heaven above!”
translation_verse: Aya ɔkrira blɔ Ɔkatsitsi ye Dawid yɛ kaʋaŋaa gi kɛtraba ɛ. Hosana ní lifunu ne hwii.”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses heard what Jesus said, and they started looking for a way to kill him. They were afraid of him, because the crowds were completely amazed at his teaching.
translation_verse: Agi kusɔliɔ baka ba ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba benu liboe le e, bekpese kugyodi kiliɛ gi biamɔ kude kutɔ onu u, xe biaye Yesu i. Pɔ̀ bezeese ye e, lɛ ese linu si linɔkpi le pɔ́ lɛ inugu itse ní yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ su u.

english_verse: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ogbose lo si, “Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́traŋa lisebi dɔni wɔ mɛ liwewe tɔ ɔ!” Niteme si yɛ badɔkpasi ba benu liboe lɛ gi apani i. Yesu akpikpini badɔda ba ebu ní lisumugba lɛ mɛ

english_verse: Then he taught the people and said, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be called a place of worship for all nations.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: Lɛ abasɛ banima bidɔ si, “Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ Liboe le me si, ‘Biaxwa Mɛ kepea mɛ si kelea mɛ banima petee ba Aya ɔxwakɔ.’ Pɔ̀ lɛ mlekyi niklɔ edzì beze ba okpokɔ ɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples: Have faith in God!
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mlazi Aya aʋa ŋa.”

english_verse: After Jesus and his disciples reached Jerusalem, he went into the temple and began chasing out everyone who was selling and buying. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and the benches of those who were selling doves.
translation_verse: Agi bezeeku Yerusalem pɔ́ ɛ, Yesu eti eku ní lisumugba lɛ mɛ; lɛ ekpese badɔda ba ni badɔdzi ba pɔ́ kukpikpini dono e. Lɛ etutru babɔtsrɛ ba ba ikplɔ nɛ ni abronuma ʋa badàdà ba egbo la eʋe e.

english_verse: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔ́ki kude si ɔnɔnɔ ɔkɔ bidɔdada tɔtɔ gani Kesumupea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Whenever you stand up to pray, you must forgive what others have done to you. Then your Father in heaven will forgive your sins. A Question about Jesus' Authority (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi mlákɔ akyɛ ɛ, mlɔ Ɔkaɛ ní lifunu ne fɛ ɛ, ɔ́trakɔ mlɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ mlɔ ɔ.” Eboe Kuŋwlɛŋwlɛ ní Yesu yɛ kigaglaɛ kɛ ɔdɔkɔ su

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples returned to Jerusalem. And as he was walking through the temple, the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses came over to him.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, bedzi ba Yerusalem. Gi Yesu azaaklanɔ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, kusɔliɔ baka ba, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni banɔtsitsi ba bɛba yɛ klɔ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “I have just one question to ask you. If you answer it, I will tell you where I got the right to do these things.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Matraʋi mlo liboe tile. Xegi mlebu le onu i, mɛ fɛ maasi mlɔ niklɔ gi mɛ kigaglaɛ kidɔ ɔ, xe mɛɛkɔ ɛbitɛ eboa la ya elu i.

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Mlasi mɛ, nifɔ Yohanes amɔ kigaglaɛ adɔ nɔ xe azɛakpɛ kuni yo aki banima na a? Aya klɔ loo, banima klɔ na a?”

english_verse: They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say that God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Niklɔ kyɔŋ ɛ, kuboehe kwiku ní ba kusi. Lɛ bɛpani si, “Xe kwibu le onu si kigaglaɛ kidɔ ní Aya klɔ ɛ, aaʋi blo si kɔ lɔ, ege lɛ loso kúzi Yohanes aʋa ɛɛŋa na a?

english_verse: On the other hand, these people think that John was a prophet. So we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize.” They were afraid of the crowd
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe kwibu le onu si Kigaglaɛ liɛ klɔ kidɔ ní banima kɔ ɛ, bezeese banima.” Lɛ ese linu si bɛzeese banima ní kiliɛ mɛ gi ɔnɔ kakaa azɛ lɛ si Yohanes anya eze Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ onu i.

english_verse: and told Jesus, “We don't know.” Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.”
translation_verse: Niteme si bebú le onu ki Yesu si, “Kúte e.” Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mɛ fɛ matrasi mlɔ niklɔ gi mɛ kigaglaɛ kidɔ ɔ, xe mɛɛkɔ ɛbitɛ eboa la ya elu u.”

english_verse: It is also true that we must love God with all our heart, mind, and strength, and that we must love others as much as we love ourselves. These commandments are more important than all the sacrifices and offerings that we could possibly make.”
translation_verse: Lɛ tɔ lɔ Aya nì wɔ ɔna nɔ petee e, ni wɔ kugyodi yo petee, ni wɔ isu ya mɛ kigaglaɛ petee, ani tɔ lɔ wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ tutɔ wɔ isu ya kinɛ ɛ, bilɛ veve sanɔ si tɔ kɔ baga, bidɔkɔkɔ ni bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ʋɛ kí Aya.”

english_verse: Jesus asked, “What do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.
translation_verse: “Ege mlegyo edi si ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee aabitɛ ɛ? Ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee aaba ye ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba klɔ aaye ba, kɔ akɔ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ akpɛ ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi dzedze awla ɛ.

english_verse: Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and has no children, his brother should marry the widow. Their first son would then be thought of as the son of the dead brother.
translation_verse: “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, Mose aŋwlimi ɔsã nɔ ya aki blɔ si, xegi onyime etse ní yedze su u pɔ̀ bápɔ gi obi ɔtɔtɔ ɛ, onyime liɛ klɔ yɛ oneminyime ɔgbani ɔtrɛtrɛɛ yedze tɔ bapɔ bebi ba ní okpo ye yɛ linyi ne me.

english_verse: When it was harvest time, he sent a servant to get his share of the grapes.
translation_verse: Agi wain yɛ ligúpoe le lina ɛ, atsisi yɛ ɔnɔsumu ɔtɔ ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba klɔ si ɔzɛ yɛ ɔŋa klɔ ɔmani yɛ.

english_verse: The renters grabbed the servant, beat him up, and sent him away without a thing.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi ɔnɔsumu ye adzɛ ɛ, ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bevu ye kusi, lɛ bɛkpikpini yɛ ɛtsisi ni iwla pluplu i.

english_verse: Then the man sent another servant, and they killed him. He kept sending servant after servant. They beat some of them and killed some.
translation_verse: Ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee atsisi onɔsumu dzedze dzɛ, lɛ beye ye e. Nite ke bɛbitɛ banɔ glegle atɔ gi akpɛ ɛ, bekusi batɔ, lɛ beye batɔ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The owner had a son he loved very much. Finally, he sent his son to the renters because he thought they would respect him.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ tole kpoo gi agɔ ɔ gi biakpɛ dzɛ ɛ, yɛ eze ɔkaɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ yebinyime gi alɔ ɛ. Gɔgɔɛ atsisi yebinyimee ní ba klɔ ni kugyodi si, ‘biabu yebinyimee.’

english_verse: But they said to themselves, “Someday he will own this vineyard. Let's kill him! That way we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bɛsi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, ‘Ɔnyrɔ nɔ onetee Yebi ye iliɛ. Mlaki tɔ kuye ye tɔ yɛ kaŋasea kiadzi blɔ kanɛ.’

english_verse: So they grabbed the owner's son, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.
translation_verse: Nite si bɛsɔ́li obinyimee, lɛ beye ye e, lɛ bɛŋwia yɛ ɛkpɛ ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ kedea ɛ.

english_verse: This is something the Lord has done, and it is amazing to us.’ ”
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ Aya yɛ abitɛ lɛ ɛ. Lɛ linu linugutseboe ʋidi ní blɔ aŋu mɛ!’ ”

english_verse: The leaders knew that Jesus was really talking about them, and they wanted to arrest him. But because they were afraid of the crowd, they let him alone and left. Paying Taxes (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ ma baka ba bɛpɛ si bévu Yesu u, lɛ ese linu si betè si basu ebu abee liɛ klɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ gi bezese linɔkpi le onu i, bɛyurɔ yɛ, lɛ bɛsɛ ɛ. Beʋi Yesu eboe ní Kagyɔa Kutsatsa su u

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were up to, and he said, “Why are you trying to test me? Show me a coin!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu amɔ ba ɔdaye lo, lɛ ebu le onu si, “Ege lɛ loso mleekeni idi si mlɛpunu mɛ ɛ? Mlamani mɛ kibɔ kitɔ tɔ midi.”

english_verse: You surely know about people being raised to life. You know that in the story about Moses and the burning bush, God said, “I am the God worshiped by Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.”
translation_verse: Abla ní bekpo ʋa kuyɔyɔɔ su i, kɔ mlákla liboeboe ní Mose keplekpaa mɛ ní ose lo gi ɛɛsɔ lɔ su iʋidi na a? Niklɔ Mose aŋwlimi aplɛ si, Aya asi yɛ si, ‘Mɛ menu Abraham yɛ Aya, Isak yɛ Aya ni Yakob yɛ Aya.’

english_verse: The Pharisees got together with Herod's followers. Then they sent some men to trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e bɛkpɛ Farisianɛ batɔ ni Okusi ye Herodes baʋatrɔ batɔ ní Yesu klɔ tɔ bapunu yɛ gani beʋiʋi me.

english_verse: They went to him and said, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are. And you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. Tell us, should we pay taxes to the Emperor or not?”
translation_verse: Agi bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ, bɛsi yɛ si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, kwite si wuzɛɛpani anukwarɛɛ, lɛ wɔʋíkɔ banima begyodi ʋa kɔwa lixwɛxwɛ ɛ. Woʋidi ɔnɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, lɛ woʋigyo ba izekɔ odi xe wuzɛɛpani i. Pɔ̀ wuzɛɛbasɛ banima anukwarɛ liɛ gi onu Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ ɛ. Lɛ loso e, kwipɛ si kúte xe liyɔ kedu ní blɔ Ɔsã nɔ su si, kutsá kagyɔ ɔki Kaisaro o? Ligyeno si kutsa kagyɔa loo kukítsa na a?”

english_verse: They brought him a silver coin, and he asked, “Whose picture and name are on it?” “The Emperor's,” they answered.
translation_verse: Agi bɛkɔ kibɔɛ ɛki yɛ ɛ, eʋi ba si, “Nyaŋwɛ litukpo ni linyi bitini ní kibɔɛ aʋa a?” Bebu le onu si, “Kaisaro.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them, “Give the Emperor what belongs to him and give God what belongs to God.” The men were amazed at Jesus. Life in the Future World (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Kɔlɔ mlakɔ kidiliɛ gi kinu Kaisaro kiniɛ mlakɔ kɛ aki Kaisaro, tɔ kidiliɛ gi kinu Aya kiniɛ ɛ, mlakɔ kɛ aki Aya.” Niteme si litu ba kugyɔ. Yesu abasɛ bidɔ ní Kuyɔyɔ dɔni Bekpo ʋa mɛ su

english_verse: He isn't the God of the dead, but of the living. You Sadducees are all wrong. The Most Important Commandment (Matthew
translation_verse: Yɛ onu Aya ki banama gi balɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, lɛ aní ki bekpo ʋa a. Mlɛta kese koŋ!” Yesu abasɛ bidɔ ní ɔsã ʋidi no su u

english_verse: But they cheat widows out of their homes and pray long prayers just to show off. They will be punished most of all. A Widow's Offering (Luke
translation_verse: Bízɛŋa kamla ní badzɛ aba gi ba benye ba betse e aʋa kɔ bɛzi ba bidɔ mɛ petee ní ba awla ɛ. Lɛ bizɛbasɛ isu ya nì Aya kuxwaxwa gblamadza mɛ ɛ. Ba kutuheheo kwianyani usanɔ!” Ɔdzɛɛ gi yenye etse e yɛ kagyɔa

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus and said:
translation_verse: Lɛ Zadukianɛ ma gi bázɛ kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ aʋa aŋa ɛ, bɛba Yesu klɔ, lɛ bɛsi yɛ si,

english_verse: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki ba si, “Mlɛta kese kura! Mlete kidiliɛ loso o? Lɛ ese linu si, mlénu Aya yɛ Liboe le ni Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ ese e.

english_verse: When Jesus saw that the man had given a sensible answer, he told him, “You are not far from God's kingdom.” After this, no one dared ask Jesus any more questions. About David's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Agi Yesu amɔ kuboeteo uliɔ gi kulɛ ní yɛ liboe le onu kubúbú yo me e, asi yɛ si, “Woti ɔpɔni Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa.” Lɛ ya kede e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani Yesu liboeboe kuʋi tɔ ɔ. Nyaŋwɛ onu Mesiya yɛ

english_verse: One of the teachers of the Law of Moses came up while Jesus and the Sadducees were arguing. When he heard Jesus give a good answer, he asked him, “What is the most important commandment?”
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ ɔdɔbasɛ ɔtɔ eze ní niklɔ lɛ enu eboa la gi bezevu edi i. Gi amɔ si Yesu ebu eboe la onu mɔmɔmɔ aki Zadukianɛ ma ɛ, eti apɔni yɛ lɛ eʋi ye si, “Ɔsã woli ɛsaɛ veve ní isã nɛ kusi na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “The most important one says: ‘People of Israel, you have only one Lord and God.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ɔsãa nɔ gi ɛsaɛ veve petee, lɔ onu si, ‘Israel, ta kutu! Ɔkaɛ gi onu blɔ Aya ɛ, Ɔka tole ko onu.

english_verse: The second most important commandment says: ‘Love others as much as you love yourself.’ No other commandment is more important than these.”
translation_verse: Ɔsã ʋla lɔ gi ɛgbani lɔ ɛkpɛ ɛ, lɔ onu si: ‘Lɔ wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ tutɔ wɔ isu ya kinɛ ɛ.’ Ɔsã ɔtɔtɔ ɔma kunyipu isani isã tiʋa ɛlɛ ya a.”

english_verse: The man replied, “Teacher, you are certainly right to say there is only one God.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔsã nɔ Ɔdabasɛ yɛ asi Yesu si, “Wɔpani mumɔ, Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ! Nite ɖii lilɛ si Ɔkaɛ ko onu Aya, lɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́ma sani yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was sitting in the temple near the offering box and watching people put in their gifts. He noticed that many rich people were giving a lot of money.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu eze ní niklɔ gi bizɛɛgyɔ kibɔɛ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, evu aŋu ní ba bibɔgyɔgyɔ ʋɛ su. Amɔ si bexonete gegle bɛzɛgyɔ kibɔɛ ní ba exomɔmɔ la onu.

english_verse: Everyone else gave what they didn't need. But she is very poor and gave everything she had. Now she doesn't have a cent to live on.
translation_verse: Apani si, banɔ gɔgɔ ba kɔ ɛ, bɛgyɔ kibɔɛ ní ba exomɔmɔ la onu. Pɔ opiplinetee kɔ ɛ, akɔ yɛ ligbã nɛ mɛ exo la petee akɔ.”

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the teachers of the Law of Moses say the Messiah will come from the family of King David?
translation_verse: Agi Yesu azɛɛbasɛ bidɔ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, eʋi si, “Kitɛ mɛ Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba biapani si, Kristo ye onu Dawid Yebi i?

english_verse: If David called the Messiah his Lord, how can the Messiah be his son?” The large crowd enjoyed listening to Jesus teach. Jesus Condemns the Pharisees and the Teachers of the Law of Moses (Matthew
translation_verse: “Agi Dawid ɔtutɔ lɔ axwa yɛ si ‘Ɔka’ ɛ, lɛ kitɛ Kristo ye aaze Dawid yɛ obi i?” Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ba kusufekeo ni kɔ su kutuheheo Linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lizɛta Yesu kutu ni isami.

english_verse: As Jesus was teaching, he said: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes and be greeted in the market.
translation_verse: Agi azɛɛbasɛ ba bidɔ mɛ ɛ, apani si, “Mlevu aŋu ní Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba su. Bizɛɛga ɛklanɔ ni ba ewu gblamadzaa na mɛ lɛ bizɛpɛ si banima bapani ba nì kubùbù ní enè na mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They like the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: Bizɛpɛ izekɔ gyɔgyɔ lɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ba itsaklɔ ni izekɔ ʋidi ne ní idɔŋakplɔ̃ nɛ su u.

english_verse: Finally, a poor widow came up and put in two coins worth only a few pennies.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛ opiplinete ɔtɔ gi yenye etse e, aba lɛ agyɔ kɔba tiaʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples to gather around him. Then he said: I tell you that this poor widow has put in more than all the others.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu axwa yɛ badɔkpasi ba akpɛ likpɔ lɛ asi ba si, “Mɛɛsi mlɔ ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ, si ɔdzɛ opiplinete liɛ ya yɛ kagyɔa kɛsrɛ sani banɔ gɔgɔ ba petee ba kanìa.

english_verse: As Jesus was leaving the temple, one of his disciples said to him, “Teacher, look at these beautiful stones and wonderful buildings!”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu azeedò ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba mɛ tole apani si, “Di ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ! Akla manímaní woli lu la ya te gi bɛkɔ ɛwa agba ʋidi ala ya xe eepedi te e!”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Do you see these huge buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Dani mlɛɛmɔ agba ʋidi gaglaa la ya ɛ? Likla ɛtɔtɔ litráwɔ ní lɛ ɔtrɔ klɔ ɔ. Bɛtrátsra la petee.” Yesu aki kugbágba ní keduyɔyɔ su

english_verse: Jesus answered: Watch out and don't let anyone fool you!
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agba bà si, “Mleze nì aŋu tɔ mlakíki tɔ ɔnɔnɔ omeni mlo o.

english_verse: Many will come and claim to be me. They will use my name and fool many people.
translation_verse: Banɔ glegle biayɔ ɔlɛɛ ní mɛ linyi ne me, gi biabu isu ya onu si, ‘Mɛ menu ye e!’ Nite me si biameni banɔ glegle.

english_verse: Later, as Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives across from the temple, Peter, James, John, and Andrew came to him in private.
translation_verse: Yesu eze ní Olif ye lito le aʋa, gi libu aŋu mɛ ɛkpɛ Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ. Lɛ Petro nì Yakobo nì Yohanes anì Andrea, ba tete bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Someday you will see that “Horrible Thing” where it should not be. Everyone who reads this must try to understand! If you are living in Judea at that time, run to the mountains.
translation_verse: “Xegi mlɛmɔ ebusioboe zimizi na gi izeezizi bidɔ ɔ, gi alɛɛ niklɔ ɔ gi líma si eze e, Lɛ kɔ banama gi balɛ ní Yudea ɛ, bese atrɛ ní eto la mɛ.

english_verse: When you hear about wars and threats of wars, don't be afraid. These things will have to happen first, but that isn't the end.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bikíhwani mlɔ ɔ, xe mlenu bigu ʋɛ izi le gi itipɔni mlɔ ɔ, ni agutaboe la ní idzage ne e; lilɛ si eboa la ya elu aba la mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ líbasɛ si kelea mɛ liblo le lina a.

english_verse: This will be the worst time of suffering since God created the world, and nothing this terrible will ever happen again.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ewe a la klɔ mɛ baxixa ʋa ɛ, bianyani sani báklɔ gi beze edi dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Aya ebu kelea gakɔna ni omono e. Lɛ baxixa ʋa klɔ elu bátraba tɔɔ ɔ.

english_verse: They asked, “When will these things happen? What will be the sign that they are about to take place?”
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi Yesu si, “Si blɔ lipoe lɛ klɔ gi eboa la ya aaba la mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ege kiabasɛ si lipoe le lina ki eboa la ya la mɛ kubaba a?”

english_verse: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. There will be earthquakes in many places, and people will starve to death. But this is just the beginning of troubles.
translation_verse: Kuse kwiata bigu nì kuse, kaʋaŋaa kiayɔ kedu ní kaʋaŋaa su; kese kiasugu ní ɔkɔ kakaa, lɛ kuʋɛ kwiadɔ ɔ. Eboa la ya alɛ ŋwisi limɛ kutàta kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Be on your guard! You will be taken to courts and beaten with whips in their synagogues. And because of me, you will have to stand before rulers and kings to tell about your faith.
translation_verse: “Mlɔ ɔtutɔ mleze ni aŋu. Biavu mlo kɔtsinɔ ní otsixwaklɔ ɔ. Biakusi mlo ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ; kɔkpɛ mɛ loso e, mlɛtralɛɛ ní baʋaŋa ba ni bekusi ba aŋu mɛ tɔ mlaapani liboe gbã nɛ ɔsi ba.

english_verse: But before the end comes, the good news must be preached to all nations.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe able kelea lidu le liana ɛ, lilɛ si basɔli Liboe Gbã nɛ asi kelea petee.

english_verse: When you are arrested, don't worry about what you will say. You will be given the right words when the time comes. But you will not really be the ones speaking. Your words will come from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi bevu mlo ɛtsinɔ ní otsixwaklɔ ɛ, eboa la gi mlaapani ní niklɔ ɛ, mlakíbu litukpo me ní la su ikpɛ ɔtɔ ɔ. Xegi lipoe le lina ɛ, mlɔ kɔ mlapani liboe kakaa gi liaba mlɔ onu i. Lɛ ese linu si eboa la gi mlɛtrapani i, atrádɔ mlɔ klɔ ɔ; ɛɛtradɔ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Brothers and sisters will betray each other and have each other put to death. Parents will betray their own children, and children will turn against their parents and have them killed.
translation_verse: Oneminyime aakpɛ oneminyime awla tɔ beye ye; lɛ Ɔka fɛ aabitɛ nite tsinɔ Yebi su u. Bebi ba biayɔ kedu ní ba baka ba ni ba bene ba su tɔ beye ba.

english_verse: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you keep on being faithful right to the end, you will be saved. The Horrible Thing (Matthew
translation_verse: Banima petee biayɔ kedu ní mlɔ su kɔkpɛ mɛ loso. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aatani kulɛɛ gaglaa kɔna ni lipoe gɔgɔ lɛ ɛ, aamɔ kubúbú. Baxixa zimizi ba su Kuboedoplɛ

english_verse: If you are on the roof of your house, don't go inside to get anything.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔtini ní yɛ ligba lɛ aʋa ɛ, ɔkizizi lipoe si yiple eku yɛ kepea mɛ tɔ yikɔ kididɔ edono o.

english_verse: If the Lord doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of his chosen and special ones, he will make the time shorter.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya aatí ewe ala klɔ aʋa; atɛsi ɔ́bitɛ lɛ nite e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tragɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ yɛ banɔ banɔbúbú ba loso e aati ní ewe a la klɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: If you are out in the field, don't go back for your coat.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔlɛ ní yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ, okídzi itrɛ kepea mɛ si yizɛɛkɔ yɛ liwu le e.

english_verse: It will be an awful time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
translation_verse: Lɛ liaze gagla ní ewe ala klɔ mɛ kí bamɛdzɛ ba ni bebinea ba gi ba bebi ba bɛɛmwa ɛ.

english_verse: False messiahs and false prophets will come and work miracles and signs. They will even try to fool God's chosen ones.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si asia na Mesiya ba ni asia na Beboedoplɛ ba biaba. Biabitɛ enugutseboe la ni eboe mamɔ edi atɔ gi biakɔ meni Aya yɛ banɔ kyakya ba fɛ ɛ, xegi biatanɔ ɛ.

english_verse: But be on your guard! That's why I am telling you these things now. When the Son of Man Appears (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mleze ní aŋuplɛplɛ mɛ! Masi mlɔ liboe kakaa xe ableke lipoe le liana a. Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kubaba ʋlaa

english_verse: So when you see all these things happening, you will know that the time has almost come.
translation_verse: Nite ke xegi mlɛmɔ eboa la ya gi ɛɛba la mɛ ɛ, tɔ mlete si lipoe le lina a.

english_verse: The sky and the earth will not last forever, but my words will. No One Knows the Day or Time (Matthew
translation_verse: Lifunu ne ni kesea biaza, pɔ̀ mɛ eboe la onu atráza liwewe e. Ɔnɔnɔ óte liwe le loo lipoe lɛ gi ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaba a

english_verse: The stars will fall, and the powers in the sky will be shaken.”
translation_verse: Badzidziebi ʋa biawɔli dɔni lifunu ne, lɛ lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ ɛ biaŋwia ní ʋɛ izeklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Learn a lesson from a fig tree. When its branches sprout and start putting out leaves, you know summer is near.
translation_verse: “Mlaki ogbose lo ɔbasɛ mlɔ bidɔ. Xegi lɔ aya la ɛtuma ɛ, kɔ mlete si kwiku ní kedzenilea mɛ.

english_verse: Then the Son of Man will be seen coming in the clouds with great power and glory.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, banima biamɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye gi atrába ní aplɛ la mɛ ni kigagla ʋidi ni yɛ kuklekleo.

english_verse: He will send his angels to gather his chosen ones from all over the earth. A Lesson from a Fig Tree (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ aakpɛ yɛ lifunu ne baxwɛdzɛ ba kutsisi kesea akɔtɔ tene na mɛ tɔ bakpɛ Aya yɛ banɔbúbú ba likpɔ ɔdɔni kelea mɛ kapɛ tiele kɔtsini kapɛ ʋlaa, ni lifunu ne ɔduklɔ. Kudɔkpasi ní ogbose lo su

english_verse: No one knows the day or the time. The angels in heaven don't know, and the Son himself doesn't know. Only the Father knows.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ óte liwe lɛ klɔ, ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi eboa la aaba la mɛ ɛ. Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ní lifunu ne fɛ béte e; lɛ Yebi e fɛ óte e; Ɔka Aya ko ete e.

english_verse: So be alert! You don't know when the master of the house will come back. It could be in the evening or at midnight or before dawn or in the morning.
translation_verse: Mleze ni aŋu lɛ ese linu si mléte lipoe lɛ klɔ gi kepea ɔkaɛ aadzi i liatani ɔlagɔ loo olezatɔ loo livlɛ kuze.

english_verse: So watch out and be ready! You don't know when the time will come.
translation_verse: “Mleze ní aŋuplɛplɛ mɛ tɔ mleze ni aŋu, lɛ ese linu si mléte lipoe lɛ klɔ gi lɛ liaze ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɔ.

english_verse: You can be sure that some of the people of this generation will still be alive when all this happens.
translation_verse: Ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ eboa la ya pɔ́ aaba la mɛ xe ableke banama petee gi balɛ ligbã́ nɛ abla ɛ, biatse e.

english_verse: But if he comes suddenly, don't let him find you asleep.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi aado ní mlɔ aʋa kpoyi i, ɔ́kiba atsani mlɔ ní sra mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It is like what happens when a man goes away for a while and places his servants in charge of everything. He tells each of them what to do, and he orders the guard to keep alert.
translation_verse: Liaze ko ŋwisi kiliɛ gi onyime ɔtɔ asɛ yɛ kepea mɛ xe atrɛ ní kude me kinɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ xe aasɛ ɛ, akɔ yɛ besumubi ba edu ní eboe la onu, lɛ aki ɔkakaa yɛ lixwɛ nɛ ɛ; lɛ asi kevunu ya ɔʋadi ye si oze ni aŋu.

english_verse: I tell everyone just what I have told you. Be alert!
translation_verse: Eboa la gi masi mlɔ ɛ, la ke mɛɛsi ɔnɔ kakaa si: ‘Mleze ni aŋu!’ ”

english_verse: They were saying, “We must not do it during the festival, because the people will riot.” At Bethany (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lɛ bɛsi isu ya si, “Kudi lɛ aʋa si kukíbitɛ lɛ ní liwe le me e. Xe aní nite e, keduyɔyɔ kiaba, kɔ ozi eku i.” Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ abali kumu flafla ní Yesu aʋa ní Betania

english_verse: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Teacher!” Then Judas kissed him,
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi Yuda eku ní klɔ ko e, atrɛ Yesu klɔ lɛ axwa yɛ si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ!” Lɛ amu yɛ lidzɔ lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was led off to the high priest. Then the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses all met together.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, bɛtsini Yesu ní kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ kepea mɛ. Niklɔ kusumu yo baka ba, ɔma nɔ baka ba ni banɔ tsitsi ba, ani ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛzɛtsa.

english_verse: She has done all she could by pouring perfume on my body to prepare it for burial.
translation_verse: Abitɛ kidiliɛ gi aatani aki mɛ ɛ; abali kumu flaflaa ní mɛ aʋa gi akɔ alulɔ mɛ kisugyaɛ kpɛ ɔtɔ akí mɛ kuviveo.

english_verse: This made some of the guests angry, and they complained, “Why such a waste?
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ bezè niklɔ gi bɛmɔ kusuyeʋi i; lɛ bɛsi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Ege lɛ loso ɔdzɛ liɛ ezizi kumu flafla liɔ te e?

english_verse: You may be sure that wherever the good news is told all over the world, people will remember what she has done. And they will tell others. Judas and the Chief Priests (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ anukwarɛɛ si, ɔkɔ kakaa gi biapani Liboe Gbã nɛ ní kelea petee me e, biabu ɔdzɛ liɛ yɛ kiboebitɛɛ kubasɛ gi biakɔ zɛplɛ aŋu ní yɛ aʋa a.” Yuda adimɛ si yɛ akpɛ Yesu awla

english_verse: The two disciples went into the city and found everything just as Jesus had told them. So they prepared the Passover meal.
translation_verse: Badɔkpasi ba bɛsɛ ɛtrɛ ní ɔma ʋidi no me e, lɛ bɛzɛmɔ kidɔ kakaa ŋwisi kiliɛ ɖii gi Yesu asi ba kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ bekyi awla ní Kutseolidɔzawe le bidɔ ŋaŋa ʋɛ su i.

english_verse: Judas Iscariot was one of the twelve disciples. He went to the chief priests and offered to help them arrest Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yuda Iskariot, gi onu Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba mɛ tole e, atrɛ ní kusumu yo baka ʋidi ba klɔ si yizɛɛkpɛ Yesu awla ɛki ba a.

english_verse: The Son of Man will die, just as the Scriptures say. But it is going to be terrible for the one who betrays me. That man would be better off if he had never been born.” The Lord's Supper (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye atrátse ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ liboe le lipani kinɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ lianyani kí ɔniliɛ gi aakpɛ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye awla ɛ! Liapɛ ɔki ɔniliɛ klɔ sanɔ xegi báxa yɛ apɔ kura ɛ!” Ɔkaɛ yɛ kuŋuplɛ bidɔŋaŋa

english_verse: Jesus picked up a cup of wine and gave thanks to God. He gave it to his disciples, and they all drank some.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, akɔ kaŋwɛpliʋia lɛ edo Aya ɖase e, xe akɔ ka akpɛ ba awla ɛ. Lɛ ba pɔ́ bɛŋwɛ ní ka onu i.

english_verse: Jesus replied, “This very night before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.”
translation_verse: Yesu asi Petro si, “Mɛɛsi wɔ anukwarɛɛ si, xe ableke ɔkukɔ lɔ aamè kuʋlaa olezatɔ nɔ̀ ɛ, wɔanada tatá si wote me e.”

english_verse: He answered, “It is one of you twelve men who is eating from this dish with me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu lɛ onu si, “Litraze mlɔ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba mɛ tole gi ɛɛkpɛ awla ní kezia mɛ ni mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But Peter was so sure of himself that he said, “Even if I have to die with you, I will never say I don't know you!” All the others said the same thing. Jesus Prays (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro aŋwlɛ isu ya gaglaa sanɔ si, “Xegi lizĩa si mɛ ni wɔ kwiatse kyɛ ɛ, mátranada wɔ liwewe e.” Badɔkpasi dzedze ba pɔ́ bɛpani liboe lɛklɔ ke e. Yesu axwa Aya ní Getsemane abɔɔ yɛ mɛ

english_verse: told them, “I am so sad that I feel as if I am dying. Stay here and keep awake with me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Mɛ ɔna nɔ ɛɛmɔ kuyeʋiyeʋi glegle gi míhatse e. Mleze ní ya tɔ mleze ni aŋu.”

english_verse: Jesus walked on a little way. Then he knelt down on the ground and prayed, “Father, if it is possible, don't let this happen to me! Father, you can do anything. Don't make me suffer by drinking from this cup. But do what you want, and not what I want.”
translation_verse: Yesu axwa si, “Abba, Mɔka! Kidɔ kakaa kilí plɔplɔ ɛki wɔ. Ki tɔ kuyeʋiyeʋi yo kaŋwɛpliʋia lia kàdɔ mɛ ɔza. Pɔ̀ aní ní mɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu u, boŋu ní wɔ kupɛpɛɔ onu.”

english_verse: Stay awake and pray that you won't be tested. You want to do what is right, but you are weak.”
translation_verse: Lɛ agba ba si, “Mleze ni aŋu atɔ mlaxwa Aya tɔ mlakíwɔli ku ní kufekedi me e. Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛdimɛ, pɔ̀ kisugyaɛ kiibotsò o.”

english_verse: But after I am raised to life, I will go ahead of you to Galilee.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe mayɔ adɔ ní bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, maaku mlɔ ɔtɔ trɛ ní Galilea.”

english_verse: Peter spoke up, “Even if all the others reject you, I never will!”
translation_verse: Petro ebu le onu si, “Xegi banɔ gɔgɔ ba biase ní wɔ su fɛ ɛ, mɛ Petro kɔ ɛ, mátrase yurɔ wɔ liwewe e.”

english_verse: and the men grabbed Jesus and arrested him.
translation_verse: Banima bevu Yesu lɛ bɛnya yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went back and prayed the same prayer.
translation_verse: Asɛ dzɛ, lɛ azɛɛxwa Aya ni eboa la klɔ ké e.

english_verse: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane, and he told them, “Sit here while I pray.”
translation_verse: Bɛtrɛ ní abɔɔtɔ mɛ gi bizɛxwa si Getsemane e, lɛ Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Mleze ní ya tɔ mɛ pɔ ɛ, mizɛɛxwa Aya.”

english_verse: Jesus took along Peter, James, and John. He was sad and troubled and
translation_verse: Agbani Petro, Yakobo ni Yohanes apɔni isu ya. Axixa ní yɛ isu ya mɛ, lɛ ese linu si eku ní lipoe gagla mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus came back and found the disciples sleeping, he said to Simon Peter, “Are you asleep? Can't you stay awake for just one hour?
translation_verse: Lɛ edzi aba yɛ badɔkpasi tiata ba klɔ, pɔ̀ amɔ si bɛdɔ sra a. Lɛ eʋi Petro si, “Simon, sra wɔɛdɔ lò o? Kɔ wɔ́tani aŋu kuze ɔnyɔsasa tole kpoo na a?”

english_verse: But when he returned to the disciples, he found them sleeping again. They simply could not keep their eyes open, and they did not know what to say.
translation_verse: Agi edzi aba ní badɔkpasi ba klɔ ɛ, amɔ si bɛzɛdɔ sra dzɛ. Lɛ bátani isu ya onu kubu lɛ ese linu si sra sɛ sivu ba bimbi ʋe.

english_verse: When Jesus returned to the disciples the third time, he said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? Enough of this! The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
translation_verse: Agi edzi aba kutanɛɔ ɛ, eʋi ba si, “Sra mlɛdɔ dzɛ ɛ, xe mlɛyɔ isu mɛ lo o? Lisrɛ! Ɔnyɔsasa nɔ ɛna! Mledi bɛkɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kpɛ awla ɛki banɔ zimizi ba.

english_verse: Get up! Let's go. The one who will betray me is already here.” Jesus Is Arrested (Matthew
translation_verse: Mlayɔ tɔ kusɛ. Ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛkpɛ mɛ awla ɛ, imɔ yɛ!” Bevu Yesu

english_verse: Someone standing there pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banama gi bɛlɛɛ níklɔ ba mɛ tole etso yɛ ihɛ gblamadza yɛ lɛ aŋwia yɛ ɛ. Nite si ihɛ azɛɛtsí kusumu yo Ɔka Ʋidi ye yɛ osumubi ye yɛ kutu yɔ ɛzanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a mob of men armed with swords and clubs were with him. They had been sent by the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Yesu azɛɛpani xe Yuda a, gi onu badɔkpasi ba mɛ tole e, aba edo o. Linɔkpi itɔ gi livu ihɛ gblamadza ʋa, ni kusekpeo e, lize ni Yuda kude. Banɔ baklɔ beze banɔ gi kusumu yo baka ba, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni banɔtsitsi ba bɛkpɛ si bevu Yesu u.

english_verse: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss. Tie him up tight and lead him away.”
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi atrakpɛ Yesu awla ɛ, asi linɔkpi le kibitɛ kitɔ aplɛ si, “Ɔnyime liɛ klɔ gi yɛ lidzɔ lɛ matramu i, yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi mlɛpɛ ɛ. Mlevu ye tɔ mlagbani yɛ asɛ nɔ ni sigagla.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the mob, “Why do you come with swords and clubs to arrest me like a criminal?
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi ba si, “Kizia liziã si mlamani ihɛ gblamadza ba ni kusekpeo tɔ mlaba evu me ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi ɔ́ʋibitɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa na a?

english_verse: Day after day I was with you and taught in the temple, and you didn't arrest me. But what the Scriptures say must come true.”
translation_verse: Liwe kakaa meze ní mlɔ kusi gi mabasɛ bidɔ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ mlévu me e. Pɔ̀ lilɛ si Aya yɛ liboe le liba lɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: All of Jesus' disciples ran off and left him.
translation_verse: Niklɔ badɔkpasi ba petee bese esɛ̀ ní yɛ su i.

english_verse: One of them was a young man who was wearing only a linen cloth. And when the men grabbed him,
translation_verse: Ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ, gi enyime kusa tete ko e, eze ní Yesu kude. Bekeni edi si bévu ye,

english_verse: he left the cloth behind and ran away naked. Jesus Is Questioned by the Council (Matthew
translation_verse: pɔ̀ ayurɔ kusaa akpɛ ba awla a, lɛ ese asɛ nì liya lɛ ɛ. Yudanɛ ma Otsixwakɔ Ʋidi no ɛxwa litsi ni Yesu

english_verse: Finally, some men stood up and lied about him. They said,
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔ batɔ bɛyɔ ɛlɛɛ, lɛ bɛsɛ asia na ɛkpɛ ní Yesu awla si i,

english_verse: Peter replied, “That isn't true! I don't know what you're talking about. I don't have any idea what you mean.” He went out to the gate, and a rooster crowed.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro anada lɛ si, “Méte liboe lɛ gi wɔɛpani i, lɛ menu lɛ ese fɛ ɛ.” Lɛ edo atrɛ ní kevunu ya ɛ. Niklɔ ko lɛ ɔkukɔ lɔ eme e.

english_verse: Peter had followed at a distance, and when he reached the courtyard of the high priest's house, he sat down with the guards to warm himself beside a fire.
translation_verse: Petro ezeni ba kude ní ɔdzage no ke, xe eti eku ní kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ. Niklɔ azɛɛŋani kifu yɛ ni Lisumugba lɛ bepepe ba.

english_verse: The chief priests and the whole council tried to find someone to accuse Jesus of a crime, so they could put him to death. But they could not find anyone to accuse him.
translation_verse: Kusumu yo baka ba ni Yudanɛ ma betsixwa ʋidi ba bekeni edi si biamɔ kunutsatsa kutɔ ní Yesu su tɔ biaye ye; pɔ̀ bátani kutɔtɔ kumɔ ɔ.

english_verse: At once the high priest ripped his robe apart and shouted, “Why do we need more witnesses?
translation_verse: Kusumu yo Ɔka Ʋidi ye akyì yɛ ewu la lɛ apani si i, “Katsali woli lu koŋ kwipɛ ní liboe lɛ ya mɛ dzɛ ɛ!

english_verse: This time Peter began to curse and swear, “I don't even know the man you're talking about!”
translation_verse: Niklɔ Petro apani si, “Matàni bidɔ si anukwarɛɛ mɛɛpani! Aya ohe kutu ki mɛ xegi asia mɛɛsɛ ɛ! Méte onyime liɛ su gi mlɛpani i!”

english_verse: “We heard him say he would tear down this temple that we built. He also claimed that in three days he would build another one without any help.”
translation_verse: “Kwinu gi apani si, ‘Maayaʋi lisɔligba ɛlɛ ya gi banima bɛwa ni awla la ɛ; lɛ ní ewe tata kede e, maawa lidzedze gi aní banɔ bɛwa lɛ ɛ.’ ”

english_verse: But even then they did not agree on what they said.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ba kyɛ ɛ, ba katsalia kásɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The high priest stood up in the council and asked Jesus, “Why don't you say something in your own defense? Don't you hear the charges they are making against you?”
translation_verse: Niklɔ Kusumu yo Ɔka Ʋidi ye ayɔ ní ba pɔ́ aŋu na mɛ xe eʋi Yesu si i, “Kunububu kutɔtɔ kúma ní wɔ awla ní eboa la gi bɛkɔ ní wɔ su kura na a?”

english_verse: But Jesus kept quiet and did not say a word. The high priest asked him another question, “Are you the Messiah, the Son of the glorious God?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́pani i, xe aabu liboeboe onu u. Lɛ Kusumu yo Ɔka Ʋidi ye eʋi ye dzɛ si, “Wɔ wonu Mesiya yɛ gi yɛ onu Aya Dradra yɛ Yebi ye na a?”

english_verse: You heard him claim to be God! What is your decision?” They all agreed he should be put to death.
translation_verse: Mlenu yɛ libusioboe lɛ gi apani i. Ege kìnu mlɔ kugyodi o?” Ba pɔ́ bɛzi lɛ si, aŋa lipɔ! Lilɛ si beye ye e!

english_verse: Some of the people started spitting on Jesus. They blindfolded him, hit him with their fists, and said, “Tell us who hit you!” Then the guards took charge of Jesus and beat him. Peter Says He Doesn't Know Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔatɔ bekpese sita sɛ kubu ubali Yesu aʋa ɛ. Bɛnya yɛ aŋu na mɛ, lɛ bɛsa yɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ besi yɛ si i, “Basɛ blɔ ɔniliɛ gi asa wɔ bidɔ mɛ ɛ!” Lɛ Lisumugba lɛ bepepe ba bɛgbani yɛ ɛsɛnɔ ɛ, lɛ bezeekusi ye e. Petro anada si yetè Yesu u

english_verse: The servant girl saw Peter again and said to the people standing there, “This man is one of them!”
translation_verse: Gi ɔdzɛʋi yɛ amɔ yɛ ní niklɔ ɛ, ekpese kudo si banama gi bɛlɛɛ níklɔ dzɛ si, “Onu bamɛ tole.”

english_verse: While Peter was still in the courtyard, a servant girl of the high priest came up
translation_verse: Agi Petro eze ní kepea kukaa mɛ ɛ Kusumu yo Ɔka Ʋidi ye yɛ ɔdzɛʋi ɔnɔsumu tole aba a.

english_verse: and saw Peter warming himself by the fire. She stared at him and said, “You were with Jesus from Nazareth!”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi amɔ Petro gi azɛɛŋani kifu yɛ ɛ, edi ye haã lɛ asi yɛ si, “Wɔ kyɛ, woze nì Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ kude e.”

english_verse: “No, I'm not!” Peter replied. A little while later some of the people said to Peter, “You certainly are one of them. You're a Galilean!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro anada lɛ dzɛ. Lidze xe banɔ baklɔ gi balɛɛ ní klɔ bebu le etu Petro dzɛ si, “Wɔtratani lɛ kúnada si wonu bamɛ tole e; lɛ ese linu si wɔ kyɛ ɛ, Galilea ɔnɛ wonu u.”

english_verse: At once the rooster crowed a second time. Then Peter remembered that Jesus had told him, “Before a rooster crows twice, you will say three times that you don't know me.” So Peter started crying.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, ɔkukɔ lɔ eme kuʋlaa, lɛ Petro aplɛ aŋu liboe lɛ gi Yesu asi yɛ aʋa a si, “Xe ableke ɔkukɔ lɔ aamè kuʋla ɛ, wɔanada tatá si wote me e.” nite si amɔ isu ya egyesi lɛ eku kubemi me e.

english_verse: Early the next morning the chief priests, the nation's leaders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met together with the whole Jewish council. They tied up Jesus and led him off to Pilate.
translation_verse: Keletitia livlɛ lɛ fififĩ i, kusumu yo baka ba, Yudanɛ ma banɔtsitsi ba, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba, ani Yudanɛ ma betsixwa Ʋidi ba pɔ́ bɛkpɛ likpɔ kise ise niteme si bɛtrɔ likla ní liboe tile aʋa. Bɛkpɛ Yesu bibɔ ʋɛ, lɛ bɛgbani yɛ ɛsɛnɔ; lɛ bɛkɔ yɛ ɛkpɛ awla ɛki Pilato e.

english_verse: But the chief priests told the crowd to ask Pilate to free Barabbas.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kusumu yo baka ba bɛfuɛ ɛkpɛ linɔkpi le me si, beʋi si, Pilato tɔ ɔyurɔ Baraba boŋu ɔki ba a.

english_verse: Pilate asked, “But what crime has he done?” “Nail him to a cross!” they yelled even louder.
translation_verse: Lɛ Pilato eʋi dzɛ si, “Liboe zimizi woli abitɛ ɛ?” Bɛkpɛ ozi lo gaglaa ɛsanɔ si, “Ku ye ní ose lo su!”

english_verse: But Jesus did not answer, and Pilate was amazed. The Death Sentence (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́pani liboeboe, nite si litu Pilato kugyɔ ɔ. Pilato atsa kutsépia akí Yesu

english_verse: They made fun of Jesus and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
translation_verse: Lɛ bekpese yɛ kukpɔ̀ si: “Yudanɛ ma Okusi Ʋidi ye e, oze ligbã gblamadza!”

english_verse: Then Pilate questioned him again, “Don't you have anything to say? Don't you hear what crimes they say you have done?”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, Pilato eʋi ye dzɛ si, “Wɔtrabu isu ya onu lò o. Dani wɔenu kiliɛ gi bɛɛtsa wɔ onu i?”

english_verse: Pilate wanted to please the crowd, so he set Barabbas free. Then he ordered his soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip and nail him to a cross. Soldiers Make Fun of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi Pilato apɛ si yibitɛ ní linɔkpi le kupɛpɛɔ aʋa loso e, ayurɔ Baraba aki ba a. Aki si bekusi Yesu, lɛ akɔ yɛ akpɛ awla si bazekú ye ní ose lo su i. Baguta ba bɛkpɛ Yesu kakpaa

english_verse: On it was a sign that told why he was nailed there. It read, “This is the King of the Jews.”
translation_verse: Lɛ kunutsatsaa liɔ klɔ gi bɛŋwlimi iku ní yɛ litukpo le onu i, kwipani si: “Yudanɛ ma Okusi Ʋidi ye.”

english_verse: They yelled, “Nail him to a cross!”
translation_verse: Bɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Ku ye ní ose lo su!”

english_verse: It was about nine o'clock in the morning when they nailed him to the cross.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi bekú Yesu ní ose lo su i, lize livlɛ lɛ inyɔ ɔgɔtole.

english_verse: A Roman army officer was standing in front of Jesus. When the officer saw how Jesus died, he said, “This man really was the Son of God!”
translation_verse: Baguta ba ɔka ɔtɔ gi eze ní osetsase lo ɔtɔ nɔ ɔ, gi amɔ kiliɛ mɛ gi Yesu akɔ etse e, lɛ apani si, “Sugba sugba ɛ, Onyime liɛ, Aya Yebi ɔnyaa nu u!”

english_verse: The soldiers led Jesus inside the courtyard of the fortress and called together the rest of the troops.
translation_verse: Baguta ba bɛgbani Yesu itsinɔ ní Roma banɛ ma ɔʋaŋaɛ yɛ kepea mɛ, lɛ bɛxwa baguta ba lihakpɔ gɔgɔ lɛ ɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛ.

english_verse: And at that time there was a prisoner named Barabbas. He and some others had been arrested for murder during a riot.
translation_verse: Ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, onyime ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Baraba ɛ, eze ní banɔatɔ gi bɛyɔ kedu u, xe beye banɔ ɔ, xe lɛ loso bɛkpɛ ba ligba ɛ, ba kusi.

english_verse: The crowd now came and asked Pilate to set a prisoner free, just as he usually did.
translation_verse: Linɔkpi le litsa lɛ banima beʋi idɔnɔ Pilato awla si ɔbitɛ kiliɛ gi izɛbitɛ ɛki ba a.

english_verse: Pilate asked them, “Do you want me to free the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Lɛ eʋi ba si, “Mlɛpɛ si miyurɔ Yudanɛ ma okusi ye ki mlɔ na a?”

english_verse: Pilate knew that the chief priests had brought Jesus to him because they were jealous.
translation_verse: Etè mɔmɔmɔ si kusumu yo baka ba bɛkɔ Yesu ikpɛ yɛ awla kɔkpɛ gi bɛkɔ kaŋuzã ní Yesu su loso o.

english_verse: Then Pilate asked the crowd, “What do you want me to do with this man you say is the king of the Jews?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Pilato eʋi linɔkpi le dzɛ si, “Kɔ ege mlɛpɛ si mibitɛ ni ɔniliɛ gi mlɛɛxwa si Yudanɛ ma okusi ye e?”

english_verse: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the purple robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Agi bɛkpɛ yɛ kakpaa pɔ́ ɛ, bebu liwu kyíkyĩ nɛ ní yɛ su, lɛ bɛkɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ liwu le dzini kpɛ ɛki yɛ ɛ. Lɛ bɛgbani yɛ dono etsinɔ si beezeku ye ní ose lo su i. Bekú Yesu ní ose lo su u

english_verse: Simon from Cyrene happened to be coming in from a farm, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross. Simon was the father of Alexander and Rufus. Jesus Is Nailed to a Cross (Matthew
translation_verse: Agi bɛzɛtrɛ ɛ, bɛtsani onyime ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Simon i. Simon aga ádɔ ní kumami yɔ mɛ xe azɛatrɛ ní Yerusalem ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ gi baguta ba bɛtsani yɛ ɛ, bemi yɛ aʋa si ɔzɛ Yesu yɛ ose lo kutu. Simon ɔtutɔ lɔ ádɔ ní Kirene; lɛ yɛ bebi ba beze Alɛksanda ni Rufus i.

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses also made fun of Jesus. They said to each other, “He saved others, but he can't save himself.
translation_verse: Niteme ke kusumu yo baka ba ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba fɛ bɛkpɛ Yesu kakpaa ní ba tsali kusi si, “Ebu banɔ dzedze ba, pɔ̀ ɔ́tani isu ya kubu u!

english_verse: Pilate was surprised to hear that Jesus was already dead, and he called in the army officer to find out if Jesus had been dead very long.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ litu Pilato kugyɔ gi enu si Yesu etse kyikya te e. Axwa baguta ba ɔkaɛ, lɛ eʋi ye si Yesu anya etse na a.

english_verse: A man named Joseph from Arimathea was brave enough to ask Pilate for the body of Jesus. Joseph was a highly respected member of the Jewish council, and he was also waiting for God's kingdom to come.
translation_verse: Yosef gi ádɔ Arimatia ɛ, aba eku. Eze banɔtsitsi ba likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ mɛ ɔnɔ gi kubùbù kwize ní yɛ su u. Lɛ yɛ fɛ azeepe Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kubabaa a. Edo igyi le atrɛ ní Pilato aŋu mɛ lɛ eʋi si bakɔ Yesu okpo ye aki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The soldiers took Jesus to Golgotha, which means “Place of a Skull.”
translation_verse: Bɛgbani Yesu itsinɔ ní ɔkutɔ gi bizɛxwa si Golgota ketokpaa aʋa a. Golgota lɛ ese linu si, “Litukpoxwa ozekɔ.”

english_verse: They nailed Jesus to a cross and gambled to see who would get his clothes.
translation_verse: Lɛ bekú ye ní ose lo su xe bɛta exoeʋi la ɛki yɛ bidɔ kpɛkpɛ ʋɛ ɛ. Nite si bɛlɛ yɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: People who passed by said terrible things about Jesus. They shook their heads and shouted, “Ha! So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛzɛɛga ɛza ɛ, besugu ba etukpo la, lɛ bezeebu onugu me ní Yesu su si i, “Ɛhɛ̃ɛ! Aní wɔ wɔ si, wɔtrayaʋi Lisɔligba lɛ, tɔ wɔadzini le kuwa ní ewe tata mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: If he is the Messiah, the king of Israel, let him come down from the cross! Then we will see and believe.” The two criminals also said cruel things to Jesus. The Death of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Xegi yɛ onu Mesiya yɛ ɛ, gi onu Israel Okusi Ʋidi ye e, kɔlɔ ople ní ose lo su ablahate tɔ kwiazi yɛ aʋa ŋa a!” Lɛ banɔ tiaʋa ba klɔ gi beku ní ise le su ipɔni Yesu kyɛ ɛ, bekpini ye e. Yesu yɛ Kutseo

english_verse: After the officer told him, Pilate let Joseph have Jesus' body.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi enu baguta ba ɔkaɛ yɛ kunutsatsaa si Yesu anya etse e, Pilato ayurɔ okpo ye aki Yosef.

english_verse: About noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until around three o'clock.
translation_verse: Agi inyɔ liɔfɔ ni tiʋa isa ɛ, litumu ne lidɔ ní kesea petee aʋa inyɔsasa tita.

english_verse: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eloi, Eloi, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
translation_verse: Agi inyɔ tita isa ɛ, Yesu akpe ozi tatata si, “Eloi, Eloi, lama sabaxtani!” Gi lɛ ese linu si, “Mɔka, Mɔka, ege lɛ loso wɔyurɔ mɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Some of the people standing there heard Jesus and said, “He is calling for Elijah.”
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ gi beeze ní klɔ ɛ, benu yɛ ozi lo, lɛ bɛpani si, “Mlata kutu, ɛɛxwa Eliya!”

english_verse: One of them ran and grabbed a sponge. After he had soaked it in wine, he put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus. He said, “Let's wait and see if Elijah will come and take him down!”
translation_verse: Banɔ baklɔ ba mɛ tole ese, lɛ azɛɛkɔ ɔsapo lo o, lɛ akpɛ lɔ ní kuda nyagba nyagba mɛ ɛ, lɛ ete lo ní ose gblamadza ɔtɔ onu, lɛ akɔ kɔ aki Yesu si ɔŋwɛ ɛ. Lɛ apani si, “Mlaplɛ litɔ kese tɔ kudi xe Eliya aaba pleni ye ní ose lo su i!”

english_verse: Some women were looking on from a distance. They and many others had come with Jesus to Jerusalem. But even before this they had been his followers and had helped him while he was in Galilee. Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of the younger James and of Joseph were two of these women. Salome was also one of them. Jesus Is Buried (Matthew
translation_verse: Lipoɛ lɛ klɔ gi Yesu eze ní Galilea ɛ, badzɛ ba klɔ beze ni yɛ kude, xe bɛzɛpɔni yɛ ɛ. Badzɛ dzedze gi bezeni yɛ kude eba Yerusalem kyɛ beze níklɔ. Bevive Yesu

english_verse: It was now the evening before the Sabbath, and the Jewish people were getting ready for that sacred day.
translation_verse: Lize Lisɔpa kulagɔ, liwe lɛ gi bizɛbla ɛplɛ ɛki Lizemɔniwe le e,

english_verse: Joseph bought a linen cloth and took the body down from the cross. He had it wrapped in the cloth, and he put it in a tomb that had been cut into solid rock. Then he rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb.
translation_verse: Yosef edzi aklala evu, lɛ gi eple nì okpo ye ní osetsase lo su i, axla yɛ akpɛ ní aklala yɛ mɛ lɛ akɔ yɛ akpɛ ní ɔdrɔ ɔtɔ gi aki si bebu ní likla nɛ mɛ̀ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ kede e, aki si bemli likla ʋidi itɔ etsi ɔdrɔ lɔ onu.

english_verse: Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Joseph were watching and saw where the body was placed.
translation_verse: Maria Magdalene ni Maria Yose yene bɛmɔ ní niklɔ gi bɛplɛ Yesu yɛ okpo ye e.

english_verse: Later, Jesus appeared in another form to two disciples, as they were on their way out of the city.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, Yesu ebu isu ya ní kizeze me dzedze me abasɛ yɛ badɔkpasi tiaʋa gi beze ní kudeo aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Then the disciples left and preached everywhere. The Lord was with them, and the miracles they worked proved that their message was true. ANOTHER OLD ENDING TO MARK'S GOSPEL
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛpani liboe le ní ɔkɔ kakaa a; lɛ Ɔkaɛ eze nì ba gi atrɔ likla lɛ aʋa aki ba si ba kiboedoe kinu anukwarɛ gani enugutseboe ala gi bɛbitɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: She left and told his friends, who were crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Yɛ azɛɛsi yɛ bagaglɔnɛ ba. Bɛzɛɛwa awowoe la xe bezee bemi ye e;

english_verse: Very early on Sunday morning, just as the sun was coming up, they went to the tomb.
translation_verse: Lipadzruwe le livlɛ lɛ fififĩ gi liwo le liitu i, bɛtrɛ ní ɔdrɔ lɔ su.

english_verse: But when they looked, they saw that the stone had already been rolled away. And it was a huge stone!
translation_verse: Beʋi bahanuglɔ ba ní kudeo me si, “Nyaŋwɛ atrámli likla nɛ ní ɔdrɔ lɔ onu iiki blɔ abla ɛ?” . Pɔ̀ gi bedi ikpɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ si bemli likla nɛ ɛzanɔ kooko.

english_verse: The women went into the tomb, and on the right side they saw a young man in a white robe sitting there. They were alarmed.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso beti eku ní ɔdrɔ lɔ mɛ ɛ; níklɔ bɛmɔ ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ gi ɛkpɛ liwu pititi itɔ ɔ xe odi ní kaŋaa mɛ ɛ. Liki si lifu liku ba.

english_verse: Now go and tell his disciples, and especially Peter, that he will go ahead of you to Galilee. You will see him there, just as he told you.”
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mlatrɛ tɔ mlazɛɛsi yɛ badɔkpasi ba, ni Petro si: ‘Eku ɔtɔ aki mlɔ atrɛ ní Galilea; níklɔ mlamɔ yɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi asi mlɔ kinɛ ɛ.’ ”

english_verse: They will handle snakes and will drink poison and not be hurt. They will also heal sick people by placing their hands on them. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Luke
translation_verse: Xe bevu bagblaga lo bɛŋwɛ aɖi kakaa ɛ, batrámɔ kutse; biatrɔ awla ní banyanya ba aʋa, gi biahaya.” Befeke Yesu itsini lifunu ne

english_verse: But when these disciples told what had happened, the others would not believe either. What Jesus' Followers Must Do (Matthew
translation_verse: Bedziba edo liboe le ɛsi badɔkpasi gɔgɔ ba, pɔ̀ bázɛ lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa a. Yesu ebu isu ya abasɛ yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tole ba

english_verse: Anyone who believes me and is baptized will be saved. But anyone who refuses to believe me will be condemned.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi azɛ aŋa a xe bɛkpɛ kuni ɛki yɛ ɛ, biazɛ yɛ ligbã́; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔ́zɛ ŋa ɛ, biabu ye lipɔ.

english_verse: After the Lord Jesus had said these things to the disciples, he was taken back up to heaven where he sat down at the right side of God.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu apani aki ba pɔ ɛ, befeke ye tsini lifunu ne lɛ eze ní Aya yɛ kaŋaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Afterwards, Jesus appeared to his eleven disciples as they were eating. He scolded them because they were too stubborn to believe the ones who had seen him after he had been raised to life.
translation_verse: Gɔgɔɛ, Yesu ebu isu ya abasɛ yɛ badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tole ba gi bɛzɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ. Aka aŋu mɛ aki ba lɛ ese linu si kuziŋa kúze ba awla a; lɛ bɛkɔ ina gagla si bátrazɛ banama gi bɛmɔ yɛ si ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, aʋa ɛɛŋa a.

english_verse: Then he told them: Go and preach the good news to everyone in the world.
translation_verse: Asi ba si, “Mlatrɛ ní kelea petee me tɔ mlapani Liboe Gbã nɛ aki ɔnɔ kakaa elu.

english_verse: Everyone who believes me will be able to do wonderful things. By using my name they will force out demons, and they will speak new languages.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛziŋa ɛ, biaki ba kigagla tɔ biabitɛ enugutseboe la: biakpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne kudono ní mɛ linyi ne me; biagu kunugu yo ni binemi vuvu ʋɛ.

english_verse: Many people have tried to tell the story of what God has done among us.
translation_verse: Tiofili dimɛnɔɛ: Banɔ glegle bekeni edi ŋwlimi eboa la gi ɛba la mɛ ní blɔ kusi i plɛ.

english_verse: They wrote what we had been told by the ones who were there in the beginning and saw what happened.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi banama gi beze la katsalia a, xe bɛmɔ la kibabaɛ mɛ ni ba bimbi ʋe e, dɔni la okpese klɔ, gi benu Aya yɛ banɔsumu u, xe bɛpani liboe le kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So I made a careful study of everything and then decided to write and tell you exactly what took place. Honorable Theophilus,
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Obùbùnɛ ɛ, gi mapami eboe la mɛ tsiitsiitsii i, dɔni la okpese kɔ ɛ, mamɔ si liziã si miŋwlimi la ní la kibabaɛ onu ki wɔ.

english_verse: I have done this to let you know the truth about what you have heard. An Angel Tells about the Birth of John
translation_verse: Liaki si wɔate anukwarɛ liɛ petee gi ɔlɛ bidɔ bɛklɔ gi bɛbasɛ wɔ mɛ ɛ. Bɛpani Yohanes Kuni yo Ɔkpɛkpɛ Kupɔpɔɔ ɛplɛ

english_verse: When Herod was king of Judea, there was a priest by the name of Zechariah from the priestly group of Abijah. His wife Elizabeth was from the family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi Herodes eze okusi xe azɛɛŋa Yudea kesea aʋa ɛ. Aya kusumu yo ɔka ɔtɔ eze gi bɛzɛxwa si Zakaria ɛ. Adɔ ní Abiya kusumu yo baka ba lihakpɔ lɛ mɛ. Yedze linyi lize Elisabet, lɛ yɛ kyɛ eze Aron yɛ osobi i.

english_verse: Both of them were good people and pleased the Lord God by obeying all that he had commanded.
translation_verse: Ba tiaʋa beze agbã́ gbã ní Aya aŋu mɛ, lɛ bɛbitɛ Ɔkaɛ isã nɛ petee aʋa lipɔ mobu u.

english_verse: But they did not have children. Elizabeth could not have any, and both Zechariah and Elizabeth were already old.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ obi ɔtɔtɔ óze ní ba awla a, lɛ ese linu si Elisabet eze okonɔ, lɛ yɛ ni yenye Zakaria betsi i.

english_verse: One day Zechariah's group of priests were on duty, and he was serving God as a priest.
translation_verse: Liwetɔ lɛɛ, gi lina yɛ okú no aʋa a, xe Zakaria azɛɛwa esumuxwɛ na ní Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: According to the custom of the priests, he had been chosen to go into the Lord's temple that day and to burn incense,
translation_verse: Ní kusumu yo egoʋi la onu i, lidɔ yɛ aʋa si, ɔzɛɛkpɛ lifi ye kifu ní lifi ye ɔsuʋɔ lɔ aʋa. Lɛ loso eti eku ní Aya yɛ lisumugba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: while the people stood outside praying.
translation_verse: Lɛ banima petee bɛlɛɛ ní oŋwe no xe bɛzɛxwa Aya ɛ.

english_verse: All at once an angel from the Lord appeared to Zechariah at the right side of the altar.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ɔtɔ alɛ ní lifi ye ɔsuʋɔ lɔ kaŋawla mɛ.

english_verse: Zechariah was confused and afraid when he saw the angel.
translation_verse: Gi Zakaria amɔ yɛ ɛ, elulu lɛ lifu liku ye e.

english_verse: But the angel told him: Don't be afraid, Zechariah! God has heard your prayers. Your wife Elizabeth will have a son, and you must name him John.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi yɛ si, “Zakaria, kíse lifu! Aya enu wɔ yɛ kuxwaxwa, lɛ wodze Elisabet aapɔ obinyime ɔki wɔ. Wɔaki yɛ linyi si Yohanes.

english_verse: His birth will make you very happy, and many people will be glad.
translation_verse: Liaze isami glegle kiwɔ, lɛ yɛ kupɔpɔɔ kwiakpɛ banɔ glegle kyɛ isami i.

english_verse: Your son will be a great servant of the Lord. He must never drink wine or beer, and the power of the Holy Spirit will be with him from the time he is born.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, aaze ɔnɔ ʋidi ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ. Líma si ɔŋwɛ wain loo kuda gagla a. Dɔni yɛ kupɔpɔɔ mɛ ɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ aayi ye.

english_verse: John will lead many people in Israel to turn back to the Lord their God.
translation_verse: Lɛ aadzi ni Israelbanɛ glegle manɔ ní Ɔkaɛ gi onu ba Aya yɛ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: He will go ahead of the Lord with the same power and spirit that Elijah had. And because of John, parents will be more thoughtful of their children. And people who now disobey God will begin to think as they ought to. This is how John will get people ready for the Lord.
translation_verse: Yɛ aaku ɔtɔ ki Ɔkaɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ ni kigagla mɛ ŋwisi oboedoplɛɛ Eliya kinɛ. Lɛ aakpɛ baka ni bebi likpɔ, lɛ aakyi banama gi bénu batu ba kyɛ, aadzi ni ba manɔ ní Aya yɛ kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa a. Tɔ aabla banɔ plɛ ki Ɔkaɛ.”

english_verse: Zechariah said to the angel, “How will I know this is going to happen? My wife and I are both very old.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Zakaria eʋi ɔxwɛdzɛɛ si, “Kitɛ maate si, liboe lɛ gi wɔɛpani i, lilɛ lɛ mɛ ɛ? Lɛ ese linu si mɛ metsi i; lɛ modze kyɛ etsi i!”

english_verse: The angel answered, “I am Gabriel, God's servant, and I was sent to tell you this good news.
translation_verse: Ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi yɛ si, “Mɛ menu Gabriɛl. Mizɛɛlɛɛ ní Aya gi yɛ akpɛ mɛ si migasi wɔ liboe gbã ɛlɛ ya ɛ, yɛ aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: You have not believed what I have said. So you will not be able to say a thing until all this happens. But everything will take place when it is supposed to.”
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi mamanɔ ɛ, wɔ́zɛ lɛ aʋa ŋa a. Pɔ̀ litraba lɛ mɛ ní lipoe trɔtrɔ lɛ aʋa. Agi wɔ́zɛ lɛ aʋa ŋa onu i, wɔadzi omúmu zɛkɔna ni liwe lɛ gi mɛ kakaplɛa kiaba ka mɛ ki wɔ ɛ!”

english_verse: The crowd was waiting for Zechariah and kept wondering why he was staying in the temple so long.
translation_verse: Ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, banima bezeepe Zakaria lɛ béte kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso awɔ lisumugba lɛ mɛ te e.

english_verse: When he did come out, he could not speak, and they knew he had seen a vision. He motioned to them with his hands, but did not say a thing.
translation_verse: Gi edo aba ɛ, ɔ́tani kupani i, lɛ loso banima bɛmɔ egyesi si Aya ebu kiditɔ abasi yɛ ní lisumugba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Gi ɔ́tani kupani onu i, awla ko abasi banima.

english_verse: When Zechariah's time of service in the temple was over, he went home.
translation_verse: Agi yɛ kusumu yo axwɛwawa la eblo e, Zakaria edzi atrɛ ní yɛ kepea.

english_verse: Soon after this, his wife was expecting a baby, and for five months she did not leave the house. She said to herself,
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, yedze Elisabet akɔ limɛ. Lɛ ekpo isu ya badzidzi tietsu nyɔŋ ɔ.

english_verse: “What the Lord has done for me will keep people from looking down on me.” An Angel Tells about the Birth of Jesus
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si, “Gɔgɔɛ, Ɔkaɛ apɔni mɛ gi ebu mɛ kuyeo azanɔ ní banima aŋu mɛ ɛ.” Bedo Yesu kupɔpɔɔ ɛplɛ

english_verse: One month later God sent the angel Gabriel to the town of Nazareth in Galilee
translation_verse: Ní Elisabet yɛ limɛ nɛ kukɔkɔ lɛ ɔdzidzi glonɛɛ mɛ ɛ, Aya akpɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ Gabriɛl ní Nazaret ɔma nɔ mɛ ní Galilea kesea mɛ.
