english_verse: with a message for a virgin named Mary. She was engaged to Joseph from the family of King David.
translation_verse: Atsini odu lo ní ɔdisami ɔtɔ gi óte onyime e, gi bɛkpɛ kuwlawla ɛki onyime ɔtɔ ɔ, gi bɛzɛxwa si Yosef i. Yosef, onu okusi ye Dawid yɛ osobi. Lɛ ɔdisami nɔ linyi linu Maria a.

english_verse: The angel greeted Mary and said, “You are truly blessed! The Lord is with you.”
translation_verse: Ɔxwɛdzɛɛ atrɛ ní yɛ klɔ lɛ apani yɛ si, “Kusuyɔ ki wɔ. Ɔkaɛ ɔlɛ ni wɔ, lɛ akrira wɔ kpaŋu u.”

english_verse: Mary was confused by the angel's words and wondered what they meant.
translation_verse: Ɔxwɛdzɛɛ yɛ eboe la ɛki si Maria apisa lɛ eʋi isu ya si kunɔpanɔ liɔ ya kɔ ese si kitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then the angel told Mary, “Don't be afraid! God is pleased with you,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi yɛ si, “Maria, wukíse lifu u. Aya apɛ lipo me aki wɔ.

english_verse: and you will have a son. His name will be Jesus.
translation_verse: Wɔakɔ limɛ, gi wɔapɔ obinyime e, lɛ wɔaki yɛ linyi si Yesu u.

english_verse: He will be great and will be called the Son of God Most High. The Lord God will make him king, as his ancestor David was.
translation_verse: Aaze ɔnɔ ʋidi gi biaxwa yɛ si Aya kigaglaɛ petee Ɔkaɛ Yebi. Ɔkaɛ Aya aaki si yɛ aaze ní Yɛkatsitsie Dawid yɛ likusigbo le aʋa.

english_verse: He will rule the people of Israel forever, and his kingdom will never end.”
translation_verse: Lɛ aaŋa Yakob yɛ kepea aʋa kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ, lɛ yɛ kaʋaŋaa katrádu liwewe e!

english_verse: Mary asked the angel, “How can this happen? I am not even married!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Maria eʋi ɔxwɛdzɛɛ si, “Ɔdisami gi óte onyime menu u. Lɛ eboa la gi wɔpani i, kitɛ aatani la mɛ kuba a?”

english_verse: The angel answered, “The Holy Spirit will come down to you, and God's power will come over you. So your child will be called the holy Son of God.
translation_verse: Ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu le onu si, “Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ aaba wɔ aʋa, gi Aya kigaglaɛ kiawe wo o. Lɛ loso obi dradra liɛ gi wɔtrapɔ̀ ɛ, biaxwa yɛ si Aya Yebi ye.

english_verse: Your relative Elizabeth is also going to have a son, even though she is old. No one thought she could ever have a baby, but in three months she will have a son.
translation_verse: Plɛ aŋu ní wɔ ɔŋanɛɛ Elisabet aʋa. Besi si ɔ́tratani obi kupɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ ní yɛ kutsitsi uliɔ mɛ ɛ, lipo lilɛ ní yɛ su badzidzi tieglo ba ya.

english_verse: Nothing is impossible for God!”
translation_verse: Libasɛ si kididɔ kíma gi Aya ɔ́tratani kubitɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Mary said, “I am the Lord's servant! Let it happen as you have said.” And the angel left her. Mary Visits Elizabeth
translation_verse: Lɛ Maria apani si, “Ɔkaɛ yɛ osumubi menu. Lɛ loso e, kiliɛ gi wɔpani lɛ ɛ, tɔ liba lɛ mɛ ɛki mɛ nite e.” Lɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asɛ ní yɛ su i. Maria azeedi Elisabet

english_verse: A short time later Mary hurried to a town in the hill country of Judea.
translation_verse: Ni lipoe kpekpemi itɔ kede e, Maria abla lɛ akɔsi atrɛ ní Yudea eto la ɔma ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: She went into Zechariah's home, where she greeted Elizabeth.
translation_verse: Atrɛ ní Zakaria kepea mɛ lɛ azɛɛpani Elisabet i.

english_verse: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, her baby moved within her. The Holy Spirit came upon Elizabeth.
translation_verse: Agi Maria kunɔpanɔɔ kwiza eku Elisabet kutu yɔ mɛ ko, lɛ obi ye ahwa ní yɛ lipo le me. Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ eyi Elisabet

english_verse: Then in a loud voice she said to Mary: God has blessed you more than any other woman! He has also blessed the child you will have.
translation_verse: lɛ emuni yɛ ole lo si, “Wɔ́ Aya asa krira ní badzɛ ba petee kusi; lɛ obi liɛ gi wɔtrapɔ ɛ, biakrira yɛ!

english_verse: Why should the mother of my Lord come to me?
translation_verse: Ege lɛ loso meku Aya liŋu te si mɛ Ɔkaɛ yene aaba si yipani mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: As soon as I heard your greeting, my baby became happy and moved within me.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kiliɛ gi wɔ kunɔpaniɔ kwiza eku mɛ kutu yɔ mɛ ko e, obi ye ní mɛ lipo le me, ahwa ni isami.

english_verse: The Lord has blessed you because you believed that he will keep his promise. Mary's Song of Praise
translation_verse: Lisamiboe linu si wɔzi lɛ aʋa ŋa si Ɔkaɛ eboe la gi asi wɔ ɛ, aaba la mɛ!” Maria akpɔ̀ Aya

english_verse: Mary said: With all my heart I praise the Lord,
translation_verse: Lɛ Maria apani si, “Mɛ ɔna nɔ ɛɛkpɔ Ɔkaɛ;

english_verse: and I am glad because of God my Savior.
translation_verse: Lɛ isami iku mɛ esobo la ní Aya, yɛ onu mɛ ɔnɔbu ye;

english_verse: God cares for me, his humble servant. From now on, all people will say God has blessed me.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si aplɛ aŋu ni mɛ yɛ osumubi gi oyi kididɔ aʋa a. Dɔni abla ɛ, banima petee biaxwa mɛ si ɔsamì nɛ

english_verse: God All-Powerful has done great things for me, and his name is holy.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ eboe ʋidi ala klɔ gi kigaglaɛ petee onetee abitɛ aki mɛ ɛ. Yɛ linyi ne linu Kudradra!

english_verse: He always shows mercy to everyone who worships him.
translation_verse: Banama petee gi bizenu kutu iiki yɛ ɛ izedi ba asigaga dɔni kipɔpɔ mɛ tsinɔ kipɔpɔ mɛ.

english_verse: The Lord has used his powerful arm to scatter those who are proud.
translation_verse: Abitɛ eboe kigagla nì yɛ ɔwla lɔ, lɛ ehle basufeke ba ni ba batrɔtrɔ ʋa petee e,

english_verse: God drags strong rulers from their thrones and puts humble people in places of power.
translation_verse: Lɛ bekusi ʋidi ba ɛ, ebu ba asa dɔni ba ekusigbo la aʋa, lɛ banama gi bepleni isu ya ɛ, emuni ba.

english_verse: God gives the hungry good things to eat, and sends the rich away with nothing.
translation_verse: Banama gi kuʋɔɔ kwiku i, aki beke ni bidɔ gbã mɛ, lɛ akpɛ bexonete ba pɔ̀ kude ni iwla pluplu i.

english_verse: God helps his servant Israel and is always merciful to his people.
translation_verse: Eboa la gi atrɔ aplɛ aki blɔ bakatsi ba ɛ abitɛ la aʋa lɛ apɔni Israel, yɛ besumubi ba.

english_verse: The Lord made this promise to our ancestors, to Abraham and his family forever!
translation_verse: Edi Abraham ni yɛ besoebi ba petee, asigaga kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.”

english_verse: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months. Then she went back home. The Birth of John the Baptist
translation_verse: Maria eze ní Elisabet su ŋwisi badzidzi tiata xe edzi atrɛ kepea a. Elisabet apɔ Yohanes, kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ

english_verse: her neighbors and relatives heard how kind the Lord had been to her, and they too were glad.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi yɛ axolika ba ni yɛ benete ba benu yɛ kubipɔ ɛ, bɛmɔ isami pɔni yɛ ní kiliɛ gi Aya apɛ lipo me aki Elisabet loso.

english_verse: Eight days later they did for the child what the Law of Moses commands. They were going to name him Zechariah, after his father.
translation_verse: Ni liwe ɔgɔtɔʋa yɛ aʋa ɛ, bɛba si bɛtsa ɔyaʋi tɔ bɛki obi ye linyi. Bɛpɛ si bɛkɔ yɛka linyi ne Zakaria ɛki yɛ.

english_verse: But Elizabeth said, “No! His name is John.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ yene ɔ́dimɛ lɛ aʋa a; lɛ asi ba si “yɛ linyi ne linu si Yohanes”.

english_verse: The people argued, “No one in your family has ever been named John.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bɛsi yɛ si “wɔ ɔŋanɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔkɔ linyii lɛ klɔ.”

english_verse: So they motioned to Zechariah to find out what he wanted to name his son.
translation_verse: Lɛ, bɛbasɛ Zakaria awla a, xe beʋi ye linyi nɛ klɔ ɔ, gi apɛ si biaxwa obi ye e.

english_verse: Zechariah asked for a writing tablet. Then he wrote, “His name is John.” Everyone was amazed.
translation_verse: Zakaria eʋi si baki yɛ lidɔŋwlimikla lɛ aŋwlimi si, “Yɛ linyi si Yohanes.” Litu ɔnɔ kakaa kugyɔ.

english_verse: All the neighbors were frightened because of what had happened, and everywhere in the hill country people kept talking about these things.
translation_verse: Liki si lifu liku axolika ba petee, lɛ liboe le lihle eku ní Yudea eto la ima nɛ petee me.

english_verse: Everyone who heard about this wondered what this child would grow up to be. They knew the Lord was with him. Zechariah Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Nite si ɔnɔ kakaa gi enu liboe le e, egyo le edi. Lɛ beʋi si, “Obi liɛ, ege atrádzi i?” Lɛ mɛ lidra si, Aya yɛ ɔwla ɔlɛ ní yɛ aʋa. Zakaria akpɔ Aya

english_verse: The Holy Spirit came upon Zechariah, and he began to speak:
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ eyí Zakaria gi onu Yohanes yɛka ɛ, niteme si apani aplɛ si,

english_verse: Praise the Lord, the God of Israel! He has come to save his people.
translation_verse: “Mlaki tɔ kukpɔ̀ Ɔkaɛ, gi onu Israel Aya ɛ! Aba apɔni yɛ banima, lɛ ebu ba ní bibɔ ʋɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Our God has given us a mighty Savior from the family of David his servant.
translation_verse: Aki blɔ Ɔnɔbu gagla dɔni Dawid gi eze yɛ osumubi yɛ lipo le me e.

english_verse: Long ago the Lord promised by the words of his holy prophets
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi edo aplɛ gani yɛ beboedoplɛ dradra ba aʋa lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ lɛ ya kinɛ ɛ

english_verse: God said he would be kind to our people and keep his sacred promise.
translation_verse: Asi yɛ adi blɔ bakatsi ba asigaga, lɛ yɛ aplɛ aŋu ní yɛ kunyanya dradra ni ba aʋa a.

english_verse: that he would rescue us from our enemies. Then we could serve him without fear,
translation_verse: si yɛ aabu blo ní blɔ ahwafianɛ ma ba iwla mɛ tɔ kwiamɔ kudeonu gi kwiasumu ye lifumose e,

english_verse: You, my son, will be called the prophet of God Most High. You will go ahead of the Lord to get everything ready for him.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso mobi ye, biaxwa wɔ si kigaglaɛ petee onetee yɛ oboedoplɛ. Wɔatu ɔtɔ ki Ɔkaɛ tɔ wɔalulɔ kudeo plɛ ki yɛ,

english_verse: You will tell his people that they can be saved when their sins are forgiven.
translation_verse: lɛ wɔ́ wɔtrabasɛ Aya yɛ banima si, Aya aabu ba kɔ akɔ ba eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba ɛ.

english_verse: God's love and kindness will shine upon us like the sun that rises in the sky.
translation_verse: Blɔ Aya onu ɔnɔ gi izeedi asigaga a lɛ yɛ ɔna ɛyɔ ɔ. Lɛ aaki ligbã nɛ liwo le liatu ki blɔ dɔni lifunu ne,

english_verse: On us who live in the dark shadow of death this light will shine to guide us into a life of peace.
translation_verse: gi líakle bali banama gi bákpasi litumu ni kutseo lifu kusese le me aʋa; gi kuklekleo liɔ klɔ kwiagbani blɔ kukpɛ ní kusuyɔ lideke le me e.”

english_verse: As John grew up, God's Spirit gave him great power. John lived in the desert until the time he was sent to the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Lɛ obi ye etsi gi eze gagla ní kisugya ni Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ. Yɛ ozeklɔ eze ɔyayakɔ kɔkpɛ ni liwe lɛ gi ebu isu ya abasɛ Israelbanɛ ma ɛ.

english_verse: About that time Emperor Augustus gave orders for the names of all the people to be listed in record books.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ si Roma Ɔʋaŋaɛ Kaisaro Augusto akpɛ ɔsã si bakla banama petee gi beze ní Roma Kaʋaŋaa ese e, tɔ baŋwlimi aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: At this time a man named Simeon was living in Jerusalem. Simeon was a good man. He loved God and was waiting for him to save the people of Israel. God's Spirit came to him
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, onyime ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Simeon i, eze ní Yerusalem. Eze ɔnɔ gbã lɛ azɛɛbitɛ ní Aya yɛ isã nɛ aʋa a. Azeepe lipoe lɛ gi Aya aabu Israel i. Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ eze ní yɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: These first records were made when Quirinius was governor of Syria.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya lize banɔ kuklakla tɔpu ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi Kirenio eze Roma banɛ ma ɔʋaŋaɛ ní Siria a.

english_verse: Everyone had to go to their own hometown to be listed.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ɔnɔ kakaa atrɛ ní yɛ kepea si tɔ bakla yɛ.

english_verse: So Joseph had to leave Nazareth in Galilee and go to Bethlehem in Judea. Long ago Bethlehem had been King David's hometown, and Joseph went there because he was from David's family.
translation_verse: Liki si Yosef asɛ Nazaret ɔma nɔ mɛ ní Galilea kesea mɛ atrɛ ní Betlehem ɔma nɔ mɛ gi ɔlɛ Yudea kesea mɛ ɛ. Niklɔ onu Okusi ye Dawid yɛ ɔpɔklɔ. Lɛ ese linu si Yosef ádɔ Okusi ye Dawid yɛ okú no me.

english_verse: Mary was engaged to Joseph and traveled with him to Bethlehem. She was soon going to have a baby,
translation_verse: Yɛ ni Maria gi onu ɔdzɛɛ ɛ gi bɛkpɛ kuwlawla ɛki yɛ ɛ, ba bɛtrɛ ní enyi na ɔŋwlimi klɔ. Pɔ̀ lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɖi i, lipo lize ní Maria su.

english_verse: she gave birth to her first-born son. She dressed him in baby clothes and laid him on a bed of hay, because there was no room for them in the inn. The Shepherds
translation_verse: Nite si apɔ Yebi tɔpu yɛ gi eze obinyime e. Lɛ axla basa ní yɛ su, xe akɔ yɛ aplɛ ní baga ʋa kadɔŋazi ya mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si bámɔ ozekɔ ní bababa ba ɔya klɔ ɔ. Aya baxwɛdzɛ ba bedo Yesu kupɔpɔ ɛsi beʋesigbanɔ ba

english_verse: That night in the fields near Bethlehem some shepherds were guarding their sheep.
translation_verse: Beʋesigbanɔ ba bezeepe ba iʋesi le ní ɔyayakɔ ní kusio klɔ elesi la.

english_verse: All at once an angel came down to them from the Lord, and the brightness of the Lord's glory flashed around them. The shepherds were frightened.
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ɔtɔ ebu isu ya abasi ba, lɛ Aya yɛ kudradraa kwikle ebali ní ba aʋa a gi lifu liku ba a.

english_verse: But the angel said, “Don't be afraid! I have good news for you, which will make everyone happy.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi ba si i, “Mlekíse lifu! Mamani mlɔ liboe gbã, lɛ liboe lɛ klɔ linu lisamiboe ki banima petee e.

english_verse: This very day in King David's hometown a Savior was born for you. He is Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si omono liwe le e, bɛpɔ Ɔnɔbu ye ki mlɔ ní Dawid ɔma nɔ mɛ. Yɛ onu Kristo Ɔkaɛ.

english_verse: You will know who he is, because you will find him dressed in baby clothes and lying on a bed of hay.”
translation_verse: Nite si kidiliɛ gi mlakɔ mɔ yɛ gyesi i, kɛ kinu si, mlaamɔ obi ɔtɔ gi bɛxla basa ní yɛ su u, xe bɛkɔ yɛ ɛplɛ ní baga ʋa kadɔŋazia me e.”

english_verse: Suddenly many other angels came down from heaven and joined in praising God. They said:
translation_verse: Lɛ lisɛ́ɛ nɛ ɛ, lifunu ne baxwɛdzɛ ba likpiʋiidi itɔ liple eku iklani ɔxwɛdzɛ tɔpu yɛ; bɛzɛkpɛ idzi nɛ lɛ bɛzɛkpɔ̀ Aya si:

english_verse: “Praise God in heaven! Peace on earth to everyone who pleases God.”
translation_verse: “Kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ kunu Aya yɛ kunɛ ní lifunu ne hwii! tɔ kusuyɔ kuba Kesea aʋa ki banama gi beku yɛ liŋu i.”

english_verse: After the angels had left and gone back to heaven, the shepherds said to each other, “Let's go to Bethlehem and see what the Lord has told us about.”
translation_verse: Gi Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba bedziimu lifunu ne ní ba su i, beʋesigbanɔ ba bɛpani ɛsi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Mlaki tɔ kutrɛ ní Betlehem tɔ kuzeedi liboe lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛ, gi lɛ Ɔkaɛ ebu abasɛ blɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: They hurried off and found Mary and Joseph, and they saw the baby lying on a bed of hay.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛkɔsi itrɛ lɛ bɛzɛmɔ Maria ni Yosef ani obi ye gi ɔfini ní baga ʋa kadɔŋazia mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When the shepherds saw Jesus, they told his parents what the angel had said about him.
translation_verse: Gi beʋesigbanɔ ba bɛmɔ obi ye e, behle liboe lɛ gi Aya ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi ba ní ɔnuvɔɛ su i.

english_verse: But Mary kept thinking about all this and wondering what it meant.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Maria aplɛ aŋu ní eboe la petee aʋa, lɛ egyo la edi fɔ̃ɔ ni yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: As the shepherds returned to their sheep, they were praising God and saying wonderful things about him. Everything they had seen and heard was just as the angel had said.
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋesigbanɔ ba bɛsɛ ɛ. Bɛzɛkpɛ idzi nɛ zɛɛkpɔ Aya lɛ ese linu si eboa la gi bɛmɔ ɔ xe benu i, eze kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi ba ɛ. Bɛki Yesu linyi

english_verse: Eight days later Jesus' parents did for him what the Law of Moses commands. And they named him Jesus, just as the angel had told Mary when he promised she would have a baby. Simeon Praises the Lord
translation_verse: Liwe ɔgɔtɔʋa yɛ gi lipoe le lina si batsà ɔyaʋi aki obi ye e, bɛki yɛ linyi si Yesu. Linyi lɛ klɔ gi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ edo aplɛ akpɛ ɔtɔ si bakɔ aki yɛ ɛ, xe ableke bɛkɔ yɛ limɛ nɛ ɛ. Bɛtsini Yesu ní Yerusalem lisɔligba lɛ mɛ

english_verse: The time came for Mary and Joseph to do what the Law of Moses says a mother is supposed to do after her baby is born. They took Jesus to the temple in Jerusalem and presented him to the Lord,
translation_verse: Gi lipoe le lina si Yosef ni Maria bazeplo isu ya ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ onu i. Bɛtsini ɔnuvɔɛ ní Yerusalem, tɔ bɛkɔ yɛ basɛ Ɔkaɛ.

english_verse: just as the Law of the Lord says, “Each first-born baby boy belongs to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Ɔkaɛ Ɔsã nɔ mɛ si, “Obinyime tɔpu kakaa ɛ, biakɔ yɛ ɔki Ɔkaɛ.”

english_verse: The Law of the Lord also says parents have to offer a sacrifice, giving at least a pair of doves or two young pigeons. So that is what Mary and Joseph did.
translation_verse: Bɛdzɛ fɛ si tɔ biaki Aya bisumudɔ, gi binu abronuma tiaʋa loo akpakpo nuvɔ taʋa, ní Ɔkaɛ Ɔsã nɔ onu u.

english_verse: and told him that he would not die until he had seen Christ the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ lɔ̀ ɛtrɔ likla lɛ aʋa ɛki yɛ si ɔ́tratse e, kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi aamɔ Ɔkaɛ yɛ Kristo ye e, gi aka aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When Mary and Joseph brought Jesus to the temple to do what the Law of Moses says should be done for a new baby, the Spirit told Simeon to go into the temple.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ́ ɛgbani Simeon tsini Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi bapɔnɛ ba fɛ bɛkɔ obi ye Yesu i manɔ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ní ligoʋi le oxo klɔ ní Ɔsã nɔ onu.

english_verse: Simeon took the baby Jesus in his arms and praised God,
translation_verse: Simeon azɛ obi ye evu lɛ edo Aya ɖase e si:

english_verse: “Lord, I am your servant, and now I can die in peace, because you have kept your promise to me.
translation_verse: “Abla ɛ, Ɔkaɛ Aya, liboe lɛ gi wɔka aplɛ ɛ, wɔbitɛ ní lɛ aʋa. Lɛ loso e, ki tɔ wɔ osumubi ye, ɔsɛ ní kusuyɔ mɛ.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen what you have done to save your people,
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mɛ ɔtutɔ mamɔ wɔ kubúbú yo ni mɛ bimbi ʋe.

english_verse: and foreign nations will also see this.
translation_verse: Gi lɛ wɔbla aplɛ ní banima petee aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Your mighty power is a light for all nations, and it will bring honor to your people Israel.”
translation_verse: Kuklekle gi kiabu wɔ kupɛ́pɛ ɔ ɔbasɛ banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ. Lɛ kwiamani kuklekleo ki wɔ banima Israel.”

english_verse: Jesus' parents were surprised at what Simeon had said.
translation_verse: Obika yɛ ni obinee ba inugu itse ní eboa la gi Simeon apani ní ɔnuvɔɛ su i.

english_verse: Then he blessed them and told Mary, “This child of yours will cause many people in Israel to fall and others to stand. The child will be like a warning sign. Many people will reject him,
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Simeon akrira ba ɛ, asi Maria, Yesu yene si, “Obi liɛ, Aya ɔtutɔ akya yɛ si gani yɛ aʋa ɛ, banɔ glegle ní Israel biagleʋe. Pɔ̀ gani yɛ ke aʋa ɛ, banɔ glegle biamɔ kubúbú ní Israel. Yɛ aaze lixaŋu kikpɛdɔ dɔni Aya klɔ, gi kɛsu banɔ glegle biayɔ kedu u.

english_verse: and you, Mary, will suffer as though you had been stabbed by a dagger. But all this will show what people are really thinking.” Anna Speaks about the Child Jesus
translation_verse: Lɛ banima begyodi kpòkpò ʋa biaŋwi. Pɔ̀ wɔ tutɔ ɛ, kuyeʋiyeʋi kwiaze ŋwisi ihɛ piɔpiɔ, gi aatiku wɔ ɔna nɔ mɛ kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The prophet Anna was also there in the temple. She was the daughter of Phanuel from the tribe of Asher, and she was very old. In her youth she had been married for seven years, but her husband died.
translation_verse: Lipoɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔdzɛ oboedoplɛ tsitsi ɔtɔ eze ní Yerusalem. Yɛ linyi si Hana eze Fanuɛl gi ádɔ ní Asa yɛ okú no me e, Yebi. Agɔ tegloele ko eze ní kenyime pe xe yenye etse e.

english_verse: At this time Anna came in and praised God. She spoke about the child Jesus to everyone who hoped for Jerusalem to be set free. The Return to Nazareth
translation_verse: Yɛ fɛ eze ní kesumupea mɛ ní ɔnyɔsasa lɔ klɔ aʋa. Lɛ gi eti apɔni ba ɛ, edo Aya ɖase. Lɛ banama petee gi bɛzeepe Aya si aazi ki Yerusalem i, Hana apani liboe ní obi ye su asi ba. Yosef ni Maria bedzitrɛ ní Nazaret

english_verse: After Joseph and Mary had done everything that the Law of the Lord commands, they returned home to Nazareth in Galilee.
translation_verse: Gi Yosef ni Maria bexo egoʋi la pɔ́ ni Ɔkaɛ Ɔsã nɔ onu i; bedzitrɛ ní Nazaret, ba kepea ní Galilea kesea mɛ.

english_verse: The child Jesus grew. He became strong and wise, and God blessed him. The Boy Jesus in the Temple
translation_verse: Ɔnuvɔɛ etsi gi kigagla kize ní yɛ su u. Kuboeteo fɛ kwize ní yɛ awla, lɛ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lize ní yɛ aʋa a. Yesu ní Lisumugba lɛ mɛ

english_verse: Every year Jesus' parents went to Jerusalem for Passover.
translation_verse: Ligɔ kakaa Yesu yɛ bapɔnɛ ba bɛzɛɛdzɛ Yerusalem Kutselidɔzawe le ɔŋakɔ.

english_verse: And when Jesus was twelve years old, they all went there as usual for the celebration.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu azɛ agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní liwe le ɔŋakɔ dzɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛzɛbitɛ lɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After Passover his parents left, but they did not know that Jesus had stayed on in the city.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi bɛŋa liwe le pɔ́ ɛ, yɛ bapɔnɛ ba beku kudeo me dzitrɛ ní Kepea. Pɔ̀ obi ye Yesu xunyɔ awɔ Yerusalem ní yɛ bapɔnɛ ba kumotemote me.

english_verse: They thought he was traveling with some other people, and they went a whole day before they started looking for him.
translation_verse: Begyodi si ba obi ye eze ní ɔnɔha lɔ kusi xe bɛzɛɛtrɛ ɛ. Bɛga kudeo liwe zizi, xe ableke bekpese ba obi ye kupɛ́ ní ba benete ba ni ba baʋianɛ ba kusi i.

english_verse: When they could not find him with their relatives and friends, they went back to Jerusalem and started looking for him there.
translation_verse: Agi bámɔ yɛ ɛ, bedzitrɛ ní Yerusalem gi bɛɛzɛpɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Three days later they found Jesus sitting in the temple, listening to the teachers and asking them questions.
translation_verse: Liwe tanɛ we le e, bɛtsani yɛ ní Lisumugba lɛ mɛ. Eple eze ní Yudanɛ ma badɔbasɛ ba kusi gi azɛɛta ba kutu u, xe azeeʋi ba beʋiʋi ʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone who heard him was surprised at how much he knew and at the answers he gave.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa gi enu yɛ eboe la ɛ, yɛ onugu etse ní yɛ kuboesenu yo nì eboe la onu kubúbú mɔmɔmɔ su.

english_verse: When his parents found him, they were amazed. His mother said, “Son, why have you done this to us? Your father and I have been very worried, and we have been searching for you!”
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi yɛ bapɔnɛ ba bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, litu ba kugyɔ. Lɛ yene eʋi ye si, “Mobi ye, ege lɛ loso wɔbitɛ nite ɔmanɔ ní blɔ su u? Mɛ ni wɔka kwikyigyi gi kwipɛ wɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Why did you have to look for me? Didn't you know that I would be in my Father's house?”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɛpɛ́ mɛ ɛ? Kɔ kite mléte si, lilɛ si mize ní mɔka kepea mɛ na a?”

english_verse: But they did not understand what he meant.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bénu liboe lɛ gi apani ese e.

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth with his parents and obeyed them. His mother kept on thinking about all that had happened.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu edzi atrɛ ní Nazaret nì ba ɛ. Lɛ Yesu enu kutu aki ba a. Pɔ̀ yene evu eboa la petee akpɛ yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus became wise, and he grew strong. God was pleased with him and so were the people.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu, etsi ní kisugya ni kuboeteo me, gi yɛ liboe liku Aya ni banima aŋu u.

english_verse: When tax collectors came to be baptized, they asked John, “Teacher, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Babɔgyɔ batɔ kyɛ bɛba si ɔkpɛ kuni ɔki ba lɛ beʋi ye si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ”, “ege lilɛ si kubitɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Do something to show that you really have given up your sins. Don't start saying you belong to Abraham's family. God can turn these stones into children for Abraham.
translation_verse: Mlabitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, tɔ mlamɔni isu ya kusɛ si Abraham yɛ lipo me bebi kunu. Mɛɛka lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si Aya aatani besoebi kubu dɔni akla la ya mɛ ki Abraham!

english_verse: But to Herod the ruler, he said, “It was wrong for you to take Herodias, your brother's wife.” John also said Herod had done many other bad things.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yohanes aka aŋu mɛ aki Herodes gi eze kesea ɔʋadi ye e, ní kiliɛ gi azɛ yenemi ye yedze Herodia agbanɔ ni eboe dovo dzedze ala la petee gi abitɛ ní lɛ ya kede e.

english_verse: Everyone became excited and wondered, “Could John be the Messiah?”
translation_verse: Banima bezee di kudeonu lɛ ba petee bezegyo edi si putɔ Yohanes yɛ onu Kristo ye na a?

english_verse: Annas and Caiaphas were the Jewish high priests. At that time God spoke to Zechariah's son John, who was living in the desert.
translation_verse: lɛ Anas ni Kayafa ba kyɛ beze kusumu yo baka ba ɛ. Lipoɛ klɔ mɛ Aya yɛ ole lo ɛba ɛki Yohanes, Zakaria ye bi ye ní ɔyaya klɔ.

english_verse: Isaiah the prophet wrote about John when he said, “In the desert someone is shouting, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord! Make a straight path for him.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ni Yesaya oboedoplɛ yɛ keplekpaa mɛ kinɛ si, “Ɔnutɔ ɛɛkla ní ɔyaya klɔ si, ‘Mlalulɔ kudeo aplɛ akí Ɔkaɛ, Mlebu kude gyɔgyɔ aplɛ aki yɛ tɔ aaga!

english_verse: Fill up every valley and level every mountain and hill. Straighten the crooked paths and smooth out the rough roads.
translation_verse: Mlayaʋi eto la tɔ mlakɔ etsi bàpɛ ʋa, tɔ mlepleni eto la ni ketokpa kakaa tɔ bidzi kadadase. Bede klɔʋiklɔʋi ʋa petee bàgyɔ̀ tɔ bede aʋa gi bázrɔ ɛ, ba petee aʋa tɔ bazrɔ.

english_verse: Then everyone will see the saving power of God.’ ”
translation_verse: Tɔ ɔnɔ gbagbe yɛ kupɔpɔɔ pɔ́ kwiamɔ Aya yɛ kubúbú yo.’ ”

english_verse: Crowds of people came out to be baptized, but John said to them, “You bunch of snakes! Who warned you to run from the coming judgment?
translation_verse: Yohanes eʋi anɔkpi ala gi edo etrɛ si ɔkpɛ kuni ɔki ba a si, “Mlɔ bagblaga kipɔpɔ mɛ bebi ba a, nyaŋwɛ asi mlɔ si mlaatani kuse ní Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio gi kwitraba su u

english_verse: The crowds asked John, “What should we do?”
translation_verse: Linɔkpi le liʋi ye si, “Kɔlɔ ege kwiabitɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Some soldiers asked him, “And what about us? What do we have to do?” John told them, “Don't force people to pay money to make you leave them alone. Be satisfied with your pay.”
translation_verse: Bagutaatɔ kyɛ beʋi ye si, “Blɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, ege lilɛ si kubitɛ ɛ?” Lɛ asi ba si, “Mlakíkɔ gigagla ɛzi kibɔ ní ɔnɔnɔ awla a; lɛ mlakísɛ asia ɛkpɛ ɔnɔnɔ awla a. Mlɔ liʋɛ nɛ liku mlɔ aŋu.”

english_verse: John said, “I am just baptizing with water. But someone more powerful is going to come, and I am not good enough even to untie his sandals. He will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yohanes asi ba petee si, “Mɛ kɔ ɛ mɛɛkpɛ kuni iiki mlɔ; pɔ̀ ɔnutɔ atrába ní mɛ kede gi ekeme asani mɛ ɛ. Lɛ mégyeno gi maable yɛ afɔkpa ʋa igbé ne e. Yɛ aakpɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ nì kifu ki mlɔ.

english_verse: Finally, Herod put John in jail, and this was the worst thing he had done. The Baptism of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, Herodes abitɛ liboe zimizi litɔ sanɔ gi lɛ lize si evu Yohanes akpɛ ligba a. Yohanes akpɛ kuni aki Yesu

english_verse: While everyone else was being baptized, Jesus himself was baptized. Then as he prayed, the sky opened up,
translation_verse: Agi akpɛ kuni aki banima petee pɔ ɛ, akpɛ kuni aki Yesu fɛ. Lipoe lɛ gi Yesu azɛɛxwa Aya, ɛ, lifunu ne lidra

english_verse: and the Holy Spirit came down upon him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, “You are my own dear Son, and I am pleased with you.” The Ancestors of Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ eple edɔ yɛ aʋa ŋwisi abronuma kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ ole ɔtɔ eyi dɔni lifunu ne si, “Wɔ wonu mɛ obinyimee gi madimɛnɔ ɔ. Wɔ liboe liku mɛ liŋu.” Yesu yɛ Kigyɔgyɔɛ mɛ

english_verse: Enosh, and Seth. The family of Jesus went all the way back to Adam and then to God.
translation_verse: lɛ Kenan fɛ ɛ, eze Enos yɛ obinyime e, gi Enos eze Set yɛ obinyime e, lɛ Set fɛ eze Adam yɛ obinyime e; niteme si Adam kɔ eze Aya Yebi i.

english_verse: After the sun had set, people with all kinds of diseases were brought to Jesus. He put his hands on each one of them and healed them.
translation_verse: Gi liwo le liku i, banɔ baklɔ petee gi ba benete ba bɛzɛnyà bànyà ede ede na ɛ, bɛgbani banyanyaa ba petee manɔ ní Yesu klɔ. Yesu atrɔ awla ni ba mɛ ɔkakaa aʋa lɛ ayɔ ni ba ɛ.

english_verse: The devil said to Jesus, “If you are God's Son, tell this stone to turn into bread.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Ozimizi ye asi yɛ si i, “Xe Aya Yebi wonu i, ki tɔ likla lɛ ya lidzì lìklã.”

english_verse: Then the devil led Jesus up to a high place and quickly showed him all the nations on earth.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ozimizi ye atsini yɛ ní ɔkɔ gyɔgyɔ ɔtɔ, lɛ ebu kelea mɛ baʋaŋa ʋa petee abasi Yesu ni lipoe kpekpemi itɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Just worship me, and you can have it all.”
translation_verse: Xegi wɔasumu me e, bɛ petee biadzi wɔ binɛ.”

english_verse: Finally, the devil took Jesus to Jerusalem and had him stand on top of the temple. The devil said, “If you are God's Son, jump off.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ozimizi ye agbani Yesu atsinɔ ní Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ ɔgyɔgyɔ klɔ. Lɛ asi yɛ si, “Xegi Aya Yebi wonu i, lɛ kɔ yɔ lixu le dɔni ya tɔ zɛdɔ kesea.

english_verse: He taught in the Jewish synagogues, and everyone praised him. The People of Nazareth Turn against Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Abasɛ bidɔ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ, gi banima petee bɛkpɔ̀ yɛ ɛ. Bekyi kede ekpɛ Yesu ní Nazaret

english_verse: Jesus went back to Nazareth, where he had been brought up, and as usual he went to the synagogue on the Sabbath. When he stood up to read from the Scriptures,
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní Nazaret niklɔ gi bɛgani yɛ ɛ. Lɛ ní Sabat liwe le aʋa ɛ, atrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi izɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ. Ayɔ si yikla Aya yɛ Liboe le.

english_verse: “The Lord's Spirit has come to me, because he has chosen me to tell the good news to the poor. The Lord has sent me to announce freedom for prisoners, to give sight to the blind, to free everyone who suffers,
translation_verse: “Ɔkaɛ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔtini mɛ aʋa, lɛ ese linu si akya mɛ si mikɔ liboe gbã nɛ tsini bepiplinete ba. Akpɛ mɛ si, mible banama gi balɛ ní kunyánya mɛ ɛ, tɔ kuŋupoo fɛ kumɔ, tɔ banama gi bemi kpɛ kese e, fɛ bamɔ obufuɛkɔ.

english_verse: and to say, ‘This is the year the Lord has chosen.’ ”
translation_verse: Atɔ miki kelea petee ketè si, lipoe le lina gi Aya aabu yɛ banima ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus closed the book, then handed it back to the man in charge and sat down. Everyone in the synagogue looked straight at Jesus.
translation_verse: Yesu ada keplekpaa lɛ edzini ka aki ɔnɔpɔnɔ ɛ, xe eze kesea ɛ. Banama petee gi beze ní ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, bezeedi ye

english_verse: All the people started talking about Jesus and were amazed at the wonderful things he said. They kept on asking, “Isn't he Joseph's son?”
translation_verse: Yɛ liboe le liku ba petee aŋu, lɛ yɛ kinemi ye kɛ kubebeo, kwibitɛ ba yaa. Niteme si beʋi isu ya si, “Aní yɛ onu Yosef yebinyime lo o?”

english_verse: Jesus answered: You will certainly want to tell me this saying, “Doctor, first make yourself well.” You will tell me to do the same things here in my own hometown that you heard I did in Capernaum.
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si: Maka lɛ aʋa si mlɛtrabu abee liɛ ɛki mɛ si, “Ɔwabitɛɛ, bitɛ kuwa ki isu” mlɛtrapani si mɛ fɛ si, ‘eboa la klɔ gi mlenu si bɛbitɛ ní Kapernaum i, mibitɛ la ní mɛ kepea ní ya fɛ.’

english_verse: But you can be sure that no prophets are liked by the people of their own hometown.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛɛsi mlɔ si, “Oboedoplɛ tɔtɔ liboe liʋiku aŋu ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kepea.”

english_verse: Once during the time of Elijah there was no rain for three and a half years, and people everywhere were starving. There were many widows in Israel,
translation_verse: Atrɛ lɛ aʋa si, “Mlata kutu: anukwarɛ linu si, lipoɛ lɛ klɔ gi dzeni ɔ́dɔ ɔ ní Israel kesea aʋa agɔ tata nì kapɛ ní Eliya lizepoe le e, xe kuʋɛ gagla atɔ kwidɔ ní kesea mɛ ɛ, badzɛ glegle gi ba benye ba betse e, beze ni Israel.

english_verse: but Elijah was sent only to a widow in the town of Zarephath near the city of Sidon.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya ɔ́kpɛ Eliya ní ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ɔ, sani ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi yenye etse ní Zarefat ní Sidon kesea mɛ kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: During the time of the prophet Elisha, many men in Israel had leprosy. But no one was healed, except Naaman who lived in Syria.
translation_verse: Lɛ kunya kyikyi yɔ kwize ní banɔ glegle su ní Israel ní Oboedoplɛɛ Elisa lizepoe le e. Pɔ̀ Aya ɔ́yɔni ɔtɔtɔ sani Naaman, Siria ɔnɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: But Jesus slipped through the crowd and got away. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Mark
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu aga linɔkpi le kusi i, lɛ asɛ ní ba su i. Yesu akpikpini Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi idono ní Onyime ɔtɔ mɛ

english_verse: Jesus went to the town of Capernaum in Galilee and taught the people on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní Kapernaum ɔma ɔtɔ ní Galilea kesea mɛ, lɛ abasɛ banima bidɔ ní Sabat liwe le aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: There in the synagogue was a man with an evil spirit. He yelled out,
translation_verse: Onyime ɔtɔ eze ní ɔtsaklɔ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi eze ní yɛ aʋa a. Akpɛ ozi tatata si,

english_verse: Jesus ordered the evil spirit to be quiet and come out. The demon threw the man to the ground in front of everyone and left without harming him.
translation_verse: Yesu akpɛ ozi ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no aʋa si, “Gɔ tsriɖi! Do za ní onyimee mɛ!” Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no ɛŋwia onyimee plɛ kesea ní banima aŋu mɛ pɔ̀ ɔ́kpɛ yɛ libi i, lɛ edo ní yɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They all were amazed and kept saying to each other, “What kind of teaching is this? He has power to order evil spirits out of people!”
translation_verse: Banima petee inugu itse lɛ bekyi xe beʋi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “E! Eboe woli lu la ya a? Amɔ kudeonu ni kigagla gi ɛɛkɔ ɛpani iiki ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne lɛ iido e.”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over that part of the country. Jesus Heals Many People (Matthew
translation_verse: Niteme si Yesu yɛ linyi ne liyii klanɔ ní kusio klɔ petee. Yesu ayɔ ni banyanya glegle

english_verse: Jesus left the synagogue and went to Simon's home. When Jesus got there, he was told that Simon's mother-in-law was sick with a high fever.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu asɛ ní Ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, atrɛ ní Simon kepea mɛ. Kuwe gagla kutɔ kwivu Simon yɛ nadze; nite si bɛki Yesu kutete ní yɛ su.

english_verse: So Jesus went over to her and ordered the fever to go away. Right then she was able to get up and serve them a meal.
translation_verse: Adzɛ alɛɛ ní yɛ ɔtɔsi lɔ su lɛ akpɛ ɔsã aki kunyaa si kudo ní yɛ mɛ. Kuweo kwido ní yɛ mɛ nite si ayɔ kyɔŋ lɛ edi ba aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Demons went out of many people and shouted, “You are the Son of God!” But Jesus ordered the demons not to speak because they knew he was the Messiah.
translation_verse: Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne gi izɛkpɛ ozi tatata si “wɔ wonu Aya Yebi ye” kyɛ ido ní banɔ glegle me gi Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki lɛ si ikípani yɛ su i. Lɛ ese linu si ite si yɛ onu Kristo ye. Yesu edo Liboe Gbã nɛ aklanɔ ni Yudanɛ ma Itsaklɔ

english_verse: The next morning Jesus went out to a place where he could be alone, and crowds came looking for him. When they found him, they tried to stop him from leaving.
translation_verse: Keletitia livlɛ lɛ ɛ, Yesu edo ní ɔma nɔ mɛ, lɛ atrɛ ni ɔkutɔ gi ebu lidze e. Banima bɛzɛpɛ́ yɛ klanɔ. Lɛ gi bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, bekeni edi si betsí kudeo ɛki yɛ kusɛ̀sɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “People in other towns must hear the good news about God's kingdom. This is why I was sent.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ asi ba si, “Lilɛ si mipani Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa su Liboe Gbã nɛ klanɔ ní ima dzedze le kyɛ mɛ. Lixwɛ lɛ klɔ loso Aya akpɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: So he kept on preaching in the synagogues in Judea.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Yesu apani Liboe Gbã nɛ aklanɔ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ petee ní kesea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus would often go to some place where he could be alone and pray. Jesus Heals a Man Who Could Not Walk (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu azeebu isu ya lidze e, lɛ azɛɛxwa Aya ní ɔyaya kɔ ɔ. Yesu ayɔni Onyime ɔtɔ gi yɛ Kapɛ Tiele Ketse e

english_verse: When Jesus saw how much faith they had, he said to the man, “My friend, your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu amɔ ba kuziŋaa ɛ, asi onyimee si, “Ɔʋia, bɛkɔ wɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ wɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus got into the boat that belonged to Simon and asked him to row it out a little way from the shore. Then Jesus sat down in the boat to teach the crowd.
translation_verse: Yesu emu eku ní tole me. Katapokpa lia klɔ keze Simon kanɛ. Nite si Yesu asi yɛ si otutru ka tsinɔ kuni yo aʋa taʋɛ. Yesu eple eze ní katapokpaa mɛ lɛ abasɛ banima bidɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: “Master,” Simon answered, “we have worked hard all night long and have not caught a thing. But if you tell me to, I will let the nets down.”
translation_verse: Simon ebu le onu si, “Ɔkaɛ, kwikeni idi elesi zizi na petee pɔ̀ kúbu kididɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ ní wɔ liboe le onu i, maapleni blɔ dzikle ʋa.”

english_verse: They did this and caught so many fish that their nets began ripping apart.
translation_verse: Gi epleni be ɛ, bebu akpa la gbí gi ɛki si ba dzikle ʋa bekpese kukyì i

english_verse: Then they signaled for their partners in the other boat to come and help them. The men came, and together they filled the two boats so full that they both began to sink.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛbasɛ ba benemi ba awla ní katapokpa ʋla mɛ si ba gapɔni ba. Bɛba ɛpɔni ba lɛ akpa la gi bebu i, eyí kutapokpa tuʋa gi ligɔ sisami tɔ kunyrɔ ɔ.

english_verse: His partners James and John, the sons of Zebedee, were surprised too. Jesus told Simon, “Don't be afraid! From now on you will bring in people instead of fish.”
translation_verse: Nite ke Simon yɛ ahanuglɔ ba gi bɛzɛxwa si Yakobo ni Yohanes gi benu Zebedeo yɛ biba fɛ ɛ, libitɛ ba yaa. Lɛ Yesu asi Simon si, “Wukíse lifu u, dɔnɔ abla ɛ, banɔ wɔtrabu!”

english_verse: The men pulled their boats up on the shore. Then they left everything and went with Jesus. Jesus Heals a Man (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi betini ba kutapokpaa ɛmanɔ ní oge no e, bɛyurɔ kidɔ kakaa lɛ bekuni Yesu kude e. Yesu ayɔ ni Kunya Kyikyĩ yɔ Ɔnyanya ɔtɔ

english_verse: News about Jesus kept spreading. Large crowds came to listen to him teach and to be healed of their diseases.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu su eboe la eyi klanɔ ɛsanɔ. Lɛ anɔkpi la ɛba si lɛ enu yɛ eboe la tɔ biamɔ kuyɔyɔ dɔni ba bànya ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But because of the crowd, they could not get him to Jesus. So they went up on the roof, where they removed some tiles and let the mat down in the middle of the room.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔnɔha lɔ loso e, bátani kudeo kumɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso befeke ye emu ní ligba lɛ aʋa a. Lɛ bebu liwu le ní ɔvitɔ lɔ ɔ, xe bepleni ɔnyanyaɛ ní yɛ ɔtɔsi lɔ aʋa gani lɛ mɛ kpɛ ligba lɛ mɛ, ní banima kusio ní Yesu ketu ya a.

english_verse: The Pharisees and the experts began arguing, “Jesus must think he is God! Only God can forgive sins.”
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma bekpese kupani ní batsali kusi si, “Nyaŋwɛ iliɛ ya gi ɛɛpani ebusioboe ala la te e? Aya ko onu ɔniliɛ gi aatani eboe zimizi kukɔ kyɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Is it easier for me to tell this man that his sins are forgiven or to tell him to get up and walk?
translation_verse: Liwoli lisa ɛplɔplɔ si biapani i, ‘Bɛkɔ wɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ wɔ’ loo, ‘Yɔ tɔ ga na a’?

english_verse: But now you will see that the Son of Man has the right to forgive sins here on earth.” Jesus then said to the man, “Get up! Pick up your mat and walk home.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ tɔ mlete si Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye amɔ kude ní kesea aʋa si aakɔ̀ eboe zimizi na kukyɛ ɛ.” asi onyime liɛ gi yɛ kapɛ tiele ketse e si, “Mɛɛsi wɔ si, yɔ; feke wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ tɔ trɛ kepea mɛ!”

english_verse: At once the man stood up in front of everyone. He picked up his mat and went home, giving thanks to God.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ ní ba pɔ́ aŋu mɛ ɛ, onyimee ayɔ, efeke yɛ ɔtɔsi ɔlɔ aʋa gi afɛ ɛ, lɛ atrɛ kepea mɛ nì Aya kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɔ.

english_verse: Everyone was amazed and praised God. What they saw surprised them, and they said, “We have seen a great miracle today!” Jesus Chooses Levi (Matthew
translation_verse: Litu ba pɔ́ kugyɔ. Lifu liku ba; lɛ bɛkpɔ̀ Aya xe bɛpani si, “Enugutseboe woli aŋu mɛ kwimɔ nite omono o?” Yesu axwa Lewi

english_verse: Later, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the place for paying taxes. Jesus said to him, “Follow me.”
translation_verse: Eboa la ya kede e, Yesu edo; lɛ azɛɛmɔ ɔbɔgyɔ ɔtɔ ɔ, gi yɛ linyi linu Lewi i, gi eze ní yɛ lixwɛwagba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Kuni mɛ kude e.”

english_verse: Levi left everything and went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ Lewi ayɔ, ayurɔ kidɔ kakaa lɛ eku ni yɛ kude e.

english_verse: In his home Levi gave a big dinner for Jesus. Many tax collectors and other guests were also there.
translation_verse: Lɛ Lewi atrɔ ɔdɔŋakplɔ ʋidi si yikpɛ kubùbù ní Yesu su ní yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ. Babɔgyɔ glegle ni banɔ dzedze batɔ beze ní ɔkplɔ nɔ su.

english_verse: The Pharisees and some of their teachers of the Law of Moses grumbled to Jesus' disciples, “Why do you eat and drink with these tax collectors and other sinners?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ líze Farisianɛ batɔ ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ gi bafɛ beze farisianɛ kule e. Lɛ loso beʋi Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɛɛkpɛ awla ní kezi me xe mlɛɛŋwɛ bidɔ ni babɔgyɔ ba ni banama a gi benu banɔ zimizi na a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Healthy people don't need a doctor, but sick people do.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu akí ba si, “Banama gi balɛ mumɔ ɛ, báziã ɔwabitɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ banama gi bɛɛnya ɛ, ba bɛziã ɔwabitɛ.

english_verse: I didn't come to invite good people to turn to God. I came to invite sinners.” People Ask about Going without Eating (Matthew
translation_verse: Mába si mixwa banɔ gbã, pɔ̀ banɔ zimzi ba maba si mixwa tɔ bekyi ní ba ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.” Yesu apani ní Onugu nyanya su

english_verse: Some people said to Jesus, “John's followers often pray and go without eating, and so do the followers of the Pharisees. But your disciples never go without eating or drinking.”
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ bɛsi Yesu si, “Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi ba bizɛnya onugu, lɛ bizɛxwa Aya lipoe kakaa; lɛ nite ke Farisianɛ ma badɔkpasi ba fɛ bizɛbitɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ wɔ badɔkpasi ba xunyɔ ɛ kuŋaa ni kuŋwɛŋwɛɔ aʋa ko balɛ́.”

english_verse: Jesus told them, “The friends of a bridegroom don't go without eating while he is still with them.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu akí ba si, “Xegi ɔdzɛnyee ɔlɛ ɔkplɔ nɔ su i, kitɛ yɛ banɔxwaxwa ba biaze ni kuʋɛ ɛ? Lítraba lɛ mɛ liwewe e!

english_verse: But the time will come when he will be taken from them. Then they will go without eating.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lipoe le litrába gi biagbani ɔdzɛnyee sɛnɔ ɔ. Kɔ banɔxwaxwa ba bɛnya onugu i.”

english_verse: Jesus then told them these sayings: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and make the hole even bigger.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu abee aki ba dzɛ si: “Ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋíkyi lisiapɛ dɔni liwu vuvu su kukɔlulɔ liwu koko ɔpami kɔ. Xe liba lɛ mɛ nite e, libasɛ si ezizi liwu vuvu nɛ, lɛ lisiapɛ nɛ ɛ, lítrasɔ nɔ liwu koko le e.

english_verse: No one pours new wine into old wineskins. The new wine would swell and burst the old skins. Then the wine would be lost, and the skins would be ruined.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ʋikɔ kuda vuvu kukpɛ ní kagaplekpa keʋe koko me e. Xe aní te e, keʋe kokoa kiata kɔ kuda kwibali kɔ keʋea kezizi.

english_verse: New wine must be put only into new wineskins.
translation_verse: Boŋu i lilɛ si bakɔ kuda vuvu yɔ akpɛ ní Kagaplekpa keʋe vuvu me.

english_verse: No one wants new wine after drinking old wine. They say, “The old wine is better.”
translation_verse: Nite ké ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋípɛ si yiŋwɛ kuda vuvu yɔ xegi aŋwɛ kukokoo pɔ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si aapani si, ‘Kuda kókoo kwipɛ ɛsani kuvuvũ yɔ!’ ”

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he told the man to stand up where everyone could see him. And the man stood up.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu kɔ ete ba begyodi ʋa lɛ loso asi onyimee si, “Yɔ! Ga ba ɔtɔ nɔ yà.” Nite si onyimee ayɔ ɔlɛɛ.

english_verse: Some Pharisees said, “Why are you picking grain on the Sabbath? You're not supposed to do that!”
translation_verse: Niteme si Farisianɛ atɔ beʋi si, “Ege loso kidiliɛ liɛ gi blɔ ɔsã nɔ ɛsi si mlatrátani kubitɛ ní sabat yɛ aʋa ɛ, xe mlɔ kɔ mlɛbitɛ kɛ na a?”

english_verse: Long ago your own people did these same things to the prophets. So when this happens to you, be happy and jump for joy! You will have a great reward in heaven.
translation_verse: “Mleyo ni isami xe liba lɛ mɛ nite e, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ liʋɛtsatsa lɛ lilɛ ní lifunu ne e. Likípè mlo o, lɛ ese linu si nite ké ba bakatsitsi ba bɛbitɛ tsinɔ ní Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba su ní kanɔa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Ask God to bless anyone who curses you, and pray for everyone who is cruel to you.
translation_verse: Mlakrira banama gi beexo ifi le ekpɛ mlɔ ɛ, lɛ banama gi beebu kini ɛki mlɔ ɛ, mlaxwa Aya ní ba litukpo.

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “On the Sabbath should we do good deeds or evil deeds? Should we save someone's life or destroy it?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Miʋi mlo o: ege blɔ Ɔsã nɔ ɛsi si kubitɛ ní Sabat yɛ aʋa na a? Kubitɛ liboe gbã loo kubitɛ liboe nyani nyani i? Kubu ɔnɔ ligbã loo kudi ye tɔ otse e?”

english_verse: One was Simon, and Jesus named him Peter. Another was Andrew, Peter's brother. There were also James, John, Philip, Bartholomew,
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ ba beze Simon ni yenemie Andrea, Yakobo ni Yohanes i, Filipo ani Batolomeo.

english_verse: On another Sabbath Jesus was teaching in a synagogue, and a man with a paralyzed right hand was there.
translation_verse: Ni sabat dzedze aʋa ɛ, Yesu atrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ gi azɛɛbasɛ bidɔ ɔ. Onyime ɔtɔ gi yɛ ɔŋawla lɔ etse e, eze klɔ.

english_verse: The teachers and the Pharisees were furious and started saying to one another, “What can we do about Jesus?” Jesus Chooses His Twelve Apostles (Matthew
translation_verse: Isu ya iyeʋi Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma nyani nyani nite si begyodi ni ba kusi kidiliɛ gi biatani Yesu kubitɛ ɛ. Yesu akya yɛ Banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba

english_verse: The next morning he called his disciples together and chose twelve of them to be his apostles.
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, axwa yɛ badɔkpasi ba akpɛ likpɔ. Lɛ ebu ba mɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa gi aki ba linyi si banɔkpɛkpɛ ba.

english_verse: Everyone was trying to touch Jesus, because power was going out from him and healing them all. Blessings and Troubles (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa ekeni edi si yigbɔni yɛ, lɛ ese linu si kigagla kizɛdɔ ní yɛ mɛ gi kizɛyɔni banyanya ba petee e. Isami ni asigaga mɛ kuzeze

english_verse: Matthew, Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus. The rest of the apostles were Simon, known as the Eager One,
translation_verse: Batɔ fɛ beze Mateo ni Toma, Yakobo, Alfeo yebie ni Simon gi adimɛ ni yɛ kesea ɛ.

english_verse: Jude, who was the son of James, and Judas Iscariot, who later betrayed Jesus. Jesus Teaches, Preaches, and Heals (Matthew
translation_verse: Bagɔgɔ ba beze Yuda, Yakobo yebie, ani Yuda Iscariot gi yɛ abakpɛ Yesu ní awla ɛ. Yesu abasi bidɔ, lɛ ayɔni banyanya ba ɛ

english_verse: You well-fed people are in for trouble. You will go hungry! You people who are laughing now are in for trouble. You are going to cry and weep!
translation_verse: Babaa kimlɔ banama gi mlekè abla ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ kuʋɛ kwitraku u! Babaa ki mlɔ banama gi mlɛmwɛ abla ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ mlɛtraze ní asigaga mɛ ɛ, xe mlaabemi i!

english_verse: These people had come to listen to Jesus and to be healed of their diseases. All who were troubled by evil spirits were also healed.
translation_verse: Banɔ baklɔ ba petee bɛba si benu yɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ ni tɔ ɔyɔ ni ba dɔni ba bànya ʋa mɛ. Banɔ baklɔ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne izɛkpɛ kunugu yo fɛ bɛba gi Yesu ayɔni ba a.

english_verse: God will bless you hungry people. You will have plenty to eat! God will bless you people who are now crying. You will laugh!
translation_verse: Mlamɔ isami mlɔ banama gi kuʋɛ kwiku abla ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlɛtrake e. “Mlamɔ isami mlɔ banama gi mlebemi abla ɛ lɛ ese linu si mlɛtramwɛ ɛ

english_verse: God will bless you when others hate you and won't have anything to do with you. God will bless you when people insult you and say cruel things about you, all because you are a follower of the Son of Man.
translation_verse: Mlamɔ isami xe banima bevu mlo ekpɛ lipo me e, xe begbe mlo e, xe bekpini mlo o, xe bedo eboe nyani nyani na ɛyurɔ mlɔ ɔ kɔkpɛ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye loso.

english_verse: You are in for trouble when everyone says good things about you. That is what your own people said about those prophets who told lies. Love for Enemies (Matthew
translation_verse: “Babaa ki mlɔ xe banima pɔ́ bɛkpɔ̀ mlɔ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si nite ke ba bakatsitsi ba bɛpani ní asia na beboedoplɛ ba su.” Mlalɔ mlɔ Ahwafianɛ ma

english_verse: If you give to others, you will be given a full amount in return. It will be packed down, shaken together, and spilling over into your lap. The way you treat others is the way you will be treated.
translation_verse: Mlaki banɔ bidɔ tɔ Aya fɛ aaki mlɔ. Mlaamɔ bidɔ gi biayi kekpoa biami ʋe kpɛ lɛ biagbigba xe biakɔ kpɛ mlɔ awla. Lɛ ese linu si kália mɛ gi wɔkpɛ ɔki wonemi i, kamɛ ké Aya fɛ aakpɛ ɔki wɔ.”

english_verse: is like someone who dug down deep and built a house on solid rock. When a flood came and the river rushed against the house, it was built so well that it didn't even shake.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔlɛ ŋwisi onyime liɛ gi azɛɛwa ligba kinɛ ɛ. Ebu ide ne gi iidzu u xe ina liklakpɔ lɛ ní sise se me; lɛ akpɛ ɔklaxɔ gagla ní ide ne me e. Onipe lo eyi gbigba gi ɛyaʋi iku ligba lɛ klɔ ese e; pɔ̀ kididɔ kísa ligba lɛ, lɛ ese linu si bɛwa lɛ mumɔ ɔ.

english_verse: If someone slaps you on one cheek, don't stop that person from slapping you on the other cheek. If someone wants to take your coat, don't try to keep back your shirt.
translation_verse: Xe gi ɔnutɔ ábɔ wɔ osumu ní ɔtuʋa tole e, tɔ kyi ɔʋla lɔ ki yɛ tɔ ɔbɔ, lɛ xegi ɔnutɔ akɔ wɔ liwu le aʋa lini nɛ ɛ, tɔ bu lɛ ese liwu le fɛ ki yɛ.

english_verse: Give to everyone who asks and don't ask people to return what they have taken from you.
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ eʋi wo kiditɔ ɛ, kɔ kɛ ki yɛ. Lɛ xegi ɔnutɔ akɔ wɔ kidɔ ɛ, wukíti ke e.

english_verse: Treat others just as you want to be treated.
translation_verse: Lɛ kidiliɛ gi wɔ̀pɛ si babitɛ aki wɔ ɛ, bitɛ nite ke ki banima.

english_verse: If you love only someone who loves you, will God praise you for that? Even sinners love people who love them.
translation_verse: “Xegi mlɛdimɛ ni banama ko gi bɛdimɛ ni mlɔ ɛ, kitɛ Aya aakrira mlɔ ɛ? Lɛ ese linu si banɔ zimizi ba kura fɛ ɛ bizɛdimɛ ni banama gi bɛdimɛ ni ba ɛ.

english_verse: If you are kind only to someone who is kind to you, will God be pleased with you for that? Even sinners are kind to people who are kind to them.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mlɛbitɛ eboe gbã ɛki banama ko gi bizɛbitɛ eboe gbã ɛki mlɔ ɛ, ege lɛ loso biakpɔ mlɔ ɛ? Lɛ ese linu si nite ke banɔ zimizi ba fɛ bizɛbitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Have pity on others, just as your Father has pity on you. Judging Others (Matthew
translation_verse: Mlazedi banɔ asigaga ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ Ɔkaɛ Aya fɛ izedi asigaga la kinɛ ɛ. Ní etsi kuxwaxwa nì banɔ su

english_verse: Jesus said: Don't judge others, and God won't judge you. Don't be hard on others, and God won't be hard on you. Forgive others, and God will forgive you.
translation_verse: “Mlakíxwa litsi ni mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba tɔ Aya fɛ ɔ́kixwa litsi ni mlo o. Mlakíbu mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba lipɔ tɔ Aya fɛ aabu mlo lipɔ ɔ. Mlakɔ eboe akyɛ tɔ Aya fɛ aakɔ̀ kyɛ mlɔ.

english_verse: Jesus also used some sayings as he spoke to the people. He said: Can one blind person lead another blind person? Won't they both fall into a ditch?
translation_verse: Yesu ebu abee dzedze aki ba si, “Kaŋupo kátratani kaŋupo dzedze kugbanɔ ɔ. Xe ani nite e, ba tiaʋa pɔ́ biawɔli ku ní lizokpo me e.

english_verse: Are students better than their teacher? But when they are fully trained, they will be like their teacher.
translation_verse: Ableke e, ɔdɔkpasibi ɔ́tɔtɔ óʋikeme ɔsani yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ xegi ɔdɔkpasibi ye akpasi bidɔ mɛ pɔ́ ɛ, kɔ edzì ŋwisi yɛ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
translation_verse: Ege lɛ loso wɔedi ɔflimi nɔ ní wɔ onemi ye kimbie me e, xe wodze kanyikpea lia gi kalɛ ní wɔ ɔtutɔ wɔ kimbi ye me na a?

english_verse: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye? You show-offs! First, get the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye. A Tree and Its Fruit (Matthew
translation_verse: Kitɛ wɔasi wɔ onemi ye si, ‘Ki tɔ mibu ɔflimi nɔ ní wɔ kimbi ye me’ gi wɔʋítani kanyikpea lia gi kalɛ ní wɔ kimbi ye me kumɔ na a? Wɔ ɔnɔ aŋumɛboebitɛ, bú kanyikpea ní wɔ kimbi ye me kpɛ ɔtɔ, kɔ wɔmɔ mumɔ kɔ wobu ɔflimi nɔ ní wɔ onemi ye kimbi ye me. Lisebi le bizɛkɔ tè ose lo

english_verse: A good tree cannot produce bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot produce good fruit.
translation_verse: “Kiliɛ gi ose gbã oʋí yo esebi nyani nyani i, nite ké ose nyani nyani kyɛ ɔ́tratani esebi gbã kuyo o.

english_verse: You can tell what a tree is like by the fruit it produces. You cannot pick figs or grapes from thornbushes.
translation_verse: Ose kakaa ɛ, lɔ esebi la bizɛkɔ te lo. Ɔ́nɔnɔ ɔ́ʋigu mangɔ ní ɔdɔ̃ mɛ ɛ; lɛ ɔ́nɔnɔ fɛ ɔ́ʋigu imumɔɛ ní fife me e.

english_verse: Good people do good things because of the good in their hearts, but bad people do bad things because of the evil in their hearts. Your words show what is in your heart. Two Builders (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ɔnɔ gbã ɛ, izebu bidɔ gbã ɛdɔnɔ ní bidɔ gbã ɛ mɛ gi bilɛ ní yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ. Nite ké ɔnɔ zimizi ye kyɛ izebu eboe zimizi dɔnɔ ní bidɔ zimizi bɛ klɔ gi bilɛ ní yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ̀ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si eboa la gi eyi ɔna nɔ ɛ, la onugu lo izɛpani. Bagbawa tiaʋa

english_verse: Why do you keep on saying that I am your Lord, when you refuse to do what I say?
translation_verse: “Lɛ Yesu eʋi ba dzɛ si, “Ege lɛ loso mlɛxwa mɛ si, ‘Ɔka, Ɔka’, xe mláʋibitɛ ní eboa la gi mɛɛsi mlɔ aʋa a!

english_verse: But anyone who hears what I say and doesn't obey me is like someone whose house wasn't built on solid rock. As soon as the river rushed against that house, it was smashed to pieces!
translation_verse: Pɔ̀, ɔnɔ kakaa gi izenu mɛ eboe la a, xe ɔ́ʋibitɛ ni la aʋa ɛ, ɔlɛ ŋwisi onyime ɔtɔ gi awa yɛ ligba lɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔ́kpɛ ɔklaxɔ ní ligba lɛ ese kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso lipoe lɛ gi kuni yo kwiyaʋi iku ligba lɛ ese e, likpà kyɔŋ gi kibitɛ tɔtɔ kize ní lɛ su u.”

english_verse: I have officers who give orders to me, and I have soldiers who take orders from me. I can say to one of them, ‘Go!’ and he goes. I can say to another, ‘Come!’ and he comes. I can say to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he will do it.”
translation_verse: Mɛ fɛ makpasi ɔnutɔ yɛ kigagla ese, lɛ baguta bákpasi mɛ ese e. Xe masi tole si, ‘Trɛ ɛ!’ aatrɛ. Xe masi ɔʋlaɛ si, ‘Ba a!’ aaba. Lɛ xe maka mɛ osumubi ye si, ‘Bitɛ kidiliɛ ɛ,’ izɛɛbitɛ kɛ.”

english_verse: He loves our nation and even built us a synagogue.”
translation_verse: Alɔ blɔakɔanɛ ma, lɛ yɛ, awa blɔ ɔtsaklɔ ligba lɛ aki blɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: When the Lord saw the woman, he felt sorry for her and said, “Don't cry!”
translation_verse: Agi Ɔkaɛ amɔ ɔtrɛtrɛ yedze e, yɛ liboe liga yɛ asigaga. Lɛ asi yɛ si, “Wukíbemi i.”

english_verse: The leaders went to Jesus and begged him to do something. They said, “This man deserves your help!
translation_verse: Gi bɛba Yesu klɔ ɛ, bɛki yɛ litukpo glegle si, “Liziã si wɔapɔni baguta ba ɔka liɛ.

english_verse: Simon answered, “I suppose it would be the one who had owed more and didn't have to pay it back.” “You are right,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Simon ebu le onu si? “Megyo edi si ba mɛ iliɛ gi yɛ liʋɛ nɛ lisa keme e, xe bɛkɔ ɛkyɛ yɛ ɛ.” Lɛ Yesu atrɛ lɛ aʋa si, “Nite ɖii lilɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: In this town an army officer's servant was sick and about to die. The officer liked his servant very much.
translation_verse: Roma banɛ ma baguta ba ɔka ɔtɔ eze gi osumubi gbã ɔtɔ eze yɛ awla. Yɛ ɔkaɛ ɔ́dimɛ ní yɛ su, osumubi ye yɛ isu ya mɛ izizi gi ihatse e.

english_verse: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent some Jewish leaders to ask him to come and heal the servant.
translation_verse: Agi baguta ba ɔkaɛ enu Yesu unu i, ebu Yudanɛ ma banɔtsitsi batɔ akpɛ ní Yesu klɔ si basɛ yɛ si ɔga ayɔni yɛ osumubi ye ɔki yɛ.

english_verse: So Jesus went with them. When Jesus wasn't far from the house, the officer sent some friends to tell him, “Lord, don't go to any trouble for me! I am not good enough for you to come into my house.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e Yesu atrɛ ni ba. Kiliɛ gi bíha kepea mɛ okú i, baguta ba ɔkaɛ atsisi yɛ ole si, “Ɔkaɛ, wukíkpɛ isu kunugu u, mégyeno si wɔatiku mɛ kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And I am certainly not worthy to come to you. Just say the word, and my servant will get well.
translation_verse: Lɛ mébu isu ya si megyeno gi maaba wɔ kɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso e, wɔ kɔ pani liboe ko mɛ osumubi ye aayɔ.

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “In all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu enu yɛ eboe la ɛ, yɛ onugu etse lɛ ekyi edi linɔkpii lɛ gi lize ni yɛ kude e; lɛ asi ba si, “sugba ɛ, mámɔ kuziŋa ʋidi uliɔ elu edi ní Israel fɛ.”

english_verse: The officer's friends returned and found the servant well. A Widow's Son
translation_verse: Lɛ gi baxwɛdzɛ ba bedzitrɛ ní baguta ba ɔkaɛ kepea mɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ si osumubi ye ayɔ. Yesu ayɔni ɔtrɛtrɛ ɔtɔ yedze yebinyime

english_verse: As they came near the gate of the town, they saw people carrying out the body of a widow's only son. Many people from the town were walking along with her.
translation_verse: Gi ana ɔma nɔ kevunu ya ɛ, atsani ɔnɔha gi ɛtrɛɛvive okpo ye e. Okpo ye eze ɔtrɛtrɛ ɔtɔ yedze yebinyime tole. Lɛ linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lize ni ɔtrɛtrɛ yedzee kude dɔni ɔma nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Jesus went over and touched the stretcher on which the people were carrying the dead boy. They stopped, and Jesus said, “Young man, get up!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eti apɔni okpo ye yɛ aɖaka yɛ lɛ agbɔni yɛ ɛ. Lɛ banama gi bevu aɖaka yɛ ɛ, bedzialɛ. Lɛ Yesu apani si, “Ɔyasɔʋi, mɛɛsi wɔ si, yɔ!”

english_verse: The boy sat up and began to speak. Jesus then gave him back to his mother.
translation_verse: Ko lɛ okpo ye ayɔ eze, xe ekpese kunugu yo kugu i. Lɛ Yesu edzini yɛ aki yene e.

english_verse: Everyone was frightened and praised God. They said, “A great prophet is here with us! God has come to his people.”
translation_verse: Liki si lifu liku ba petee, lɛ bɛkpɔ̀ Aya a, lɛ bɛpani si, “Oboedoplɛ ʋidi ɔtɔ aba blɔ kusi i. Aya aba gi atrázɛ ikí yɛ banima a!”

english_verse: News about Jesus spread all over Judea and everywhere else in that part of the country. John the Baptist (Matthew
translation_verse: Nite si Yesu yɛ linyi ne liyi gi litsa Yudea kesea ni besi ba klɔ petee. Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛ yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛba Yesu klɔ ɔ

english_verse: John's followers told John everything that was being said about Jesus. So he sent two of them to ask the Lord, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
translation_verse: Lɛ akpɛ ba si bazeeʋi Ɔkaɛ si, “Wɔ wonu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi bɛsi si aaba a, putɔ kudi ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔ kudeonu na a?”

english_verse: When these messengers came to Jesus, they said, “John the Baptist sent us to ask, ‘Are you the one we should be looking for? Or are we supposed to wait for someone else?’ ”
translation_verse: Agi bɛba Yesu klɔ ɛ, bɛsi yɛ si, “Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ yɛ akpɛ blɔ si kuʋi wo si wɔ wonu ɔniliɛ su gi yɛ edo aplɛ si aaba na, putɔ kudi ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔ kudeonu na?”

english_verse: At that time Jesus was healing many people who were sick or in pain or were troubled by evil spirits, and he was giving sight to a lot of blind people.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɖii, Yesu ayɔni banyanya glegle adɔ ní ba bànya ʋa mɛ, lɛ akpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne adɔ nɔ batɔ mɛ ɛ; xe aki kuŋupo glegle bimbi ʋe bidra a.

english_verse: Jesus said to the messengers sent by John, “Go and tell John what you have seen and heard. Blind people are now able to see, and the lame can walk. People who have leprosy are being healed, and the deaf can now hear. The dead are raised to life, and the poor are hearing the good news.
translation_verse: Lɛ ebu le onu aki Yohanes yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba si i, “Mledzi atrɛ tɔ eboa la gi mlɛmɔ ɔ, xe mlenu i, mlakɔ la abasɛ Yohanes, si kuŋupoo kwimɔ, lɛ gbafa ba bɛyɔ ɛga a. Lɛ banama gi bɛnya kunya kyikyi yɔ ɛ, ba isu ya iidra. Lɛ itoku le fɛ ɛ, iinu, lɛ bekpo ʋa fɛ ɛ, bɛɛyɔ edzì ligbã. Lɛ bɛɛpani Liboe Gbã nɛ ɛsi bepiplinete ba.

english_verse: God will bless everyone who doesn't reject me because of what I do.”
translation_verse: Isami iku banama gi bálila ba kuziŋa ni mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi mɛɛbitɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: After John's messengers had gone, Jesus began speaking to the crowds about John: What kind of person did you go out to the desert to see? Was he like tall grass blown about by the wind?
translation_verse: Agi Yohanes yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba bɛsɛ ɛ, Yesu ekpese kupani ki linɔkpi le ní Yohanes su si, “gi mledo etrɛ ní ɔyaya klɔ ɛ, ege mlɛdi kudeonu si mlamɔ ɔ? Aplɔ̀plɔ̀ na gi liwɔ lɛ lizeefu putɔ ɔ?

english_verse: What kind of man did you really go out to see? Was he someone dressed in fine clothes? People who wear expensive clothes and live in luxury are in the king's palace.
translation_verse: Ege mledo etrɛ si mlɛzeedi i? Ɔnɔ gi eze ní awu gbã na mɛ putɔ ɔ? Banama gi bizɛkpɛ awu la nite e, xe bizɛŋa ligbã nɛ ɛ, kukusipe me bizɛmɔ ba.

english_verse: What then did you go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was! I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
translation_verse: Mlasi mɛ; ege mledo etrɛ xe mlɛzɛɛmɔ ɔ? Oboedoplɛ? Ee, nite ɖii. Pɔ̀ kidiliɛ gi mlɛmɔ ɛ, kisani oboedoplɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures, God calls John his messenger and says, “I am sending my messenger ahead of you to get things ready for you.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Yohanes yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ sú Aya yɛ liboe le lipani plɛ si, “ ‘Aya si, maakpɛ mɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ wɔ ɔtɔ nɔ tɔ aadra kudeo ɔki wɔ.’

english_verse: No one ever born on this earth is greater than John. But whoever is least important in God's kingdom is greater than John.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu edo apɔnɔ si, “Mɛɛsi mlɔ si Yohanes ekeme asani ɔnɔ kakaa gi eze edi i. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi onu ɔsisami yɛ ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, ekeme asani Yohanes.”

english_verse: Everyone had been listening to John. Even the tax collectors had obeyed God and had done what was right by letting John baptize them.
translation_verse: Banama pɔ́ gi benu liboe lɛ gi Yesu apani i, ni babɔgyɔ ba kyɛ ɛ, bɛzi lɛ si Aya yɛ kudeo kwigyɔ, lɛ loso ba Yohanes akpɛ kuni yo akɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But the Pharisees and the experts in the Law of Moses refused to obey God and be baptized by John.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ farisianɛ ma ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba kɔ ɛ, begbe bázɛ Aya kudeo gi ata aplɛ akí ba ligbã nɛ kupɛ̀pɛɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso bádimɛ si Yohanes ɔkpɛ kuni ɔki ba a.

english_verse: Jesus went on to say: What are you people like? What kind of people are you?
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ yɛ liboe le aʋa si, “Ege maakɔ abla kelea mɛ banima srɔnɔ ɛ? Ege beyi i?

english_verse: You are like children sitting in the market and shouting to each other, “We played the flute, but you would not dance! We sang a funeral song, but you would not cry!”
translation_verse: Balɛ ko ŋwisi banuvɔ gi bɛɛwaʋi ní line ne me kinɛ ɛ. Ba ɔha tole ɛɛkpɛ ozi lo exwa ɔha ʋla lɔ si, “ ‘Kwiʋe isamidzi iiki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mléyo o; kwikpɛ ibemidzi iiki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mlébemi i.’

english_verse: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “John has a demon in him!”
translation_verse: Agi Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ aba ɛ, anya onugu lɛ ɔ́ŋwɛ kuda fɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ lɛ mlɛpani ní yɛ su si, ‘Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ɔkpasi ní yɛ mɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: But because the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, you say, “Jesus eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aba ɛ. Yɛ kɔ ɛ, aŋa bidɔ, lɛ aŋwɛ bidɔ fɛ ɛ. Lɛ mlɛsi si, ‘Mledi onyime liɛ! Ɔŋazinete ni ɔdaŋwɛmu onu u. Ɔnɔ gi onu babɔgyɔ ba ni “banɔ zimizi ba ba ɔʋia!” ’

english_verse: Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what its followers do. Simon the Pharisee
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banama gi bɛzi Aya yɛ Kuboeteo e, ba bizɛbasɛ si Aya kuboeteo kɔ kunu kuboeteo.” Yesu atrɛ ní Simon Farisiɔnɛ yɛ Kepea mɛ

english_verse: A Pharisee invited Jesus to have dinner with him. So Jesus went to the Pharisee's home and got ready to eat.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si farisiɔnɛ ɔtɔ axwa Yesu si ɔga aŋa bidɔ ni yɛ. Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní yɛ kepea mɛ lɛ eple ze ní ɔkplɔ nɔ su si yiŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When a sinful woman in that town found out that Jesus was there, she bought an expensive bottle of perfume.
translation_verse: Ni ɔma nɔ klɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi eze ɔwawadze e, enu si Yesu eze ní ɔkplɔ nɔ su ní farisiɔnɛ yɛ kepea mɛ, lɛ akɔ kumu flafla alabasta keʋe tiele,

english_verse: Then she came and stood behind Jesus. She cried and started washing his feet with her tears and drying them with her hair. The woman kissed his feet and poured the perfume on them.
translation_verse: lɛ atrɛ ɛ xe ɔzɛ ɔlɛ ní Yesu kedea ní yɛ kuklikpɔ onu i. Niklɔ ekpese kubeo kubemi balɛ yɛ kuklikpɔɔ aʋa a. xe ɛkɔ yɛ siya sɛ etsoli kubeo ní yɛ kuklikpɔ ɛ. Lɛ amu kɔ ɛ, xe akɔ kumu flaflaa abali kɔ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: The Pharisee who had invited Jesus saw this and said to himself, “If this man really were a prophet, he would know what kind of woman is touching him! He would know that she is a sinner.”
translation_verse: Gi farisiɔnɛ amɔ nite e, egyo le edi ni yɛ isu ya mɛ si, “Atɛsi oboedoplɛ onyime liɛ ɔnyanu i, nya aatè ɔniliɛ elu gi ɔdzɛliɛ onu u xe ɛɛgbɔni yɛ ɛ; lɛ aatè ligbã nyaninyanii lɛ gi ɔdzɛliɛ ɔlɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to the Pharisee, “Simon, I have something to say to you.” “Teacher, what is it?” Simon replied.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu apani aki yɛ si, “Simon, mapɛ si misi wɔ liboetɔ!” Simon adimɛ aki yɛ si, “Yoo, Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, mɛɛta wɔ kutu.”

english_verse: But Jesus told the woman, “Because of your faith, you are now saved. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lɛ Yesu asi ɔdzɛɛ si, “Wɔ kuziŋaa kwibu wo o. Sɛ̀trɛ ní kusuyɔ mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: Since neither of them could pay him back, the moneylender said that they didn't have to pay him anything. Which one of them will like him more?”
translation_verse: Agi bamɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́tani yɛ liʋɛ nɛ kutsa onu i, ɔbɔdaɛ akɔ ba aʋɛ na akyɛ ba a. Baʋɛnete tiaʋa ya ɛ, ba mɛ owoli wogyodi si asa ɔbɔdaɛ kudimɛ nɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: He turned toward the woman and said to Simon, “Have you noticed this woman? When I came into your home, you didn't give me any water so I could wash my feet. But she has washed my feet with her tears and dried them with her hair.
translation_verse: Lɛ ekyi aŋu na mɛ akpɛ ɔdzɛɛ, lɛ eʋi Simon si, “Dani wɔɛmɔ ɔdzɛ liɛ ɛ? Agi meti eku wɔ kepea mɛ ɛ, wɔ́ki mɛ kuni si miplo mɛ kuklikpɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ yɛ kɔ ɛ, eplo mɛ kuklikpɔɔ ni yɛ kubeo, lɛ etsoli ko ni yɛ siya sɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You didn't greet me with a kiss, but from the time I came in, she has not stopped kissing my feet.
translation_verse: Lɛ wɔ́kpɛ mɛ wolaa a. Pɔ̀, dɔni lipoe lɛ gi maba kɔna ni abla ɛ, ɔ́mɔni mɛ kuklikpɔɔ kumu u.

english_verse: You didn't even pour olive oil on my head, but she has poured expensive perfume on my feet.
translation_verse: Ableke gi maba ɛ, wɔ́sra kumu kí mɛ litukpo le e. Pɔ̀ yɛ xunyɔ ɛ, abali kumu flafla ní mɛ kuklikpɔɔ.

english_verse: So I tell you that all her sins are forgiven, and that is why she has shown great love. But anyone who has been forgiven for only a little will show only a little love.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛɛsi wɔ si kulɔlɔ glegle kɔklɔ gi abasɛ ɛ, kwibu ibasɛ si bɛkɔ yɛ eboe zimizi glegle la ɛkyɛ yɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔnɔ kakaa elu gi bɛkɔ sisami ɛkyɛ ɛ, yɛ kudimɛnɔɔ kyɛ kuzeeze sisami.”

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the woman, “Your sins are forgiven.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ɔdzɛɛ si, “Bɛkɔ wɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ wɔ!”

english_verse: Some other guests started saying to one another, “Who is this who dares to forgive sins?”
translation_verse: Ɔkplɔ nɔ su bezeze gɔgɔ ba beʋi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Nyaŋwɛ liɛ, gi yɛ kɔ ɔ izɛkɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: and so were some women who had been healed of evil spirits and all sorts of diseases. One of the women was Mary Magdalene, who once had seven demons in her.
translation_verse: Badzɛ batɔ mɛ gi Yesu akpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne dono o, xe ayɔni ba ní ba bànya ba mɛ fɛ beze ni ba kude e. Ba mɛ ɔtɔ eze Maria gi bizɛxwa fɛ si Magdalene e, gi yɛ mɛ Yesu akpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi tigloele le dono e.

english_verse: The seeds that fell on rocky ground are the people who gladly hear the message and accept it. But they don't have deep roots, and they believe only for a little while. As soon as life gets hard, they give up.
translation_verse: Amubi ala la klɔ gi edɔ ní kese kpakpla aʋa ɛ, la enu banɔ baklɔ gi bizeenu liboe le e gi bizɛzɛ lɛ ni isami fɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ liboe le liʋita igu u. Lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko bizɛzɛ lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa. Pɔ̀ xegi baxixa ʋa bɛba ko e, kɔ bɛɛdzi kede e.

english_verse: Other seeds fell on rocky ground and started growing. But the plants did not have enough water and soon dried up.
translation_verse: Atɔ ɛwɔli ɛdɔ kese kpakpla aʋa. Ɛyɛ, pɔ̀ gi sise se sísrɛ ki la loso e, amɔ kuni, lɛ loso ɛyurɔ ɔ lɛ etse e.

english_verse: So he answered: I have explained the secrets about God's kingdom to you. But for others I use stories, so they will look, but not see, and they will hear, but not understand. Jesus Explains the Story about a Farmer (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ ebu le onu aki ba si i, “Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa su eboe kpokpo la su kuboeteo e, bɛkɔ kɔ ɛki mlɔ. Pɔ̀ ki banɔ gɔgɔ ba ɛ, mizɛpani ní abee ʋa mɛ, “ ‘tɔ xegi bedi fɛ ɛ, bátramɔ ɔ; lɛ xegi bɛta kutu fɛ ɛ, bátranu liboeboe ese e.’ Yesu ebu amu na ogluglu ye yɛ abee ese

english_verse: There is nothing hidden that will not be found. There is no secret that will not be well known.
translation_verse: “Lɛ kidɔ kakaa liɛ gi bekpo e, kiaŋwi. Lɛ kidiliɛ aʋa gi bewe bidɔ ɛ, biamɔ kɛ kɔ bebu ke basɛ ní oŋwe no e.

english_verse: and while they were sailing across, he went to sleep. Suddenly a storm struck the lake, and the boat started sinking. They were in danger.
translation_verse: Agi beze kuni yo aʋa ɛ, Yesu adɔ sra. Kyɔŋ ko, liwɔ gagla ɛtɔ likpese kufu ní limu ne aʋa gi liki si kuni yo kwiyaʋi iku katapokpaa mɛ ɛ. Nite si ba petee ba agbã́ na, eku kuxixa mɛ.

english_verse: This is what the story means: The seed is God's message,
translation_verse: “Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, abee yɛ ese onu si: amubi la enu Aya yɛ liboe le.

english_verse: The seeds that fell among the thornbushes are also people who hear the message. But they are so eager for riches and pleasures that they never produce anything.
translation_verse: Amubi ala la klɔ gi ɛwɔli eku bibu ʋe me e, alɛ aki banama gi bizenu liboe le e. Pɔ̀ ligbã nɛ mɛ kusuŋwasiɔ, exo la ni ligbã́ŋaŋa nɛ, bikpɛ likpɔ kɔ bimi liboe le kpɛ ba mɛ, gi líʋibu libi tɔ́tɔ liwewe e.

english_verse: Those seeds that fell on good ground are the people who listen to the message and keep it in good and honest hearts. They last and produce a harvest. Light (Mark
translation_verse: Amubi ala la klɔ gi ɛwɔli ɛdɔ kese gbã aʋa ɛ, la alɛ aki banɔ baklɔ gi xe benu liboe le e, bizevu lɛ ɔzɔ mɛ ni kutununu ɔna gbã a. Nite si bizɛkpɛ ikũ nɛ ní liboe le kɔkpɛ nì lipoe lɛ klɔ gi liabu libi i. Kani yɛ izɛ ki kuklekle

english_verse: Then Jesus asked the disciples, “Don't you have any faith?” But they were frightened and amazed. They said to each other, “Who is this? He can give orders to the wind and the waves, and they obey him!” A Man with Demons in Him (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Nifɔ mlɔ kuziŋaa kulɛ ɛ?” Pɔ̀ liboe le litu ba kugyɔ ɔ, lɛ lifu liku ba a. Lɛ beʋi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Nyaŋwɛ iliɛ? gi Yɛ kɔ izɛki liwɔ lɛ ni kuni yo ɔsã xe bizenu kutu iiki yɛ ɛ!” Yesu ayɔni onyime ɔtɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ize e

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a bowl or under a bed. A lamp is always put on a lampstand, so people who come into a house will see the light.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ʋisu kani i, xe izɛkɔ yɛ ɛkpɛ agba yɛ ese e loo kpo ye ní abati ese. Boŋu i, bizɛkɔ yɛ trɔ ní kani yɛ ose lo aʋa tɔ okle ki banama gi beetiku kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “Someone touched me, because I felt power going out from me.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu apani si, “Ɔnutɔ agbɔni mɛ lɛ ese linu si mete si kigagla kido ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he told Jairus, “Don't worry! Have faith, and your daughter will get well.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu enu liboe le, lɛ asi Yairo si, “Wukíse lifu u; wɔ kɔ zi lɛ aʋa ŋa ko. Wobi ye aayɔ!”

english_verse: They begged Jesus not to send them to the deep pit, where they would be punished.
translation_verse: Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ikí Yesu litukpo si ɔki kpikpini nɛ zɛɛkpɛ ní lizokpo gluglui lɛ gi odukɔ ɔma ɔki lɛ ɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The people went out to see what had happened, and when they came to Jesus, they also found the man. The demons had gone out of him, and he was sitting there at the feet of Jesus. He had clothes on and was in his right mind. But the people were terrified.
translation_verse: Lɛ banima bedoetrɛ si bezeedi liboe lɛ liba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Gi bɛba Yesu klɔ ɛ, bɛmɔ onyime liɛ klɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ido e, gi odi ní Yesu ikli lɛ onu u; akpɛ awu, lɛ yɛ begyodi ʋa bɛflɔ ɔ, lɛ lifu liku ba a.

english_verse: Jesus' mother and brothers went to see him, but because of the crowd they could not get near him.
translation_verse: Yesu yene ni yɛ beneminyime ba bɛba si bɛɛmɔ Yesu. Pɔ̀ bátani yɛ kuti pɔnɔ ɔ, linɔkpi le kisrɛsrɛ loso.

english_verse: As Jesus was getting out of the boat, he was met by a man from this town. The man had demons in him. He had gone naked for a long time and no longer lived in a house, but in the graveyard.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi Yesu eple ní katapokpaa mɛ ko e, onyime ɔtɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ize e, ádɔ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ aba atsani yɛ ɛ. Onyimee eze liya lipoe gblamadza lɛ óze kepe me fɛ ɛ. Ikpodrɔ lɛ me ize yɛ ozeklɔ.

english_verse: Just then the man in charge of the synagogue came and knelt down in front of Jesus. His name was Jairus, and he begged Jesus to come to his home
translation_verse: Lɛ onyime ɔtɔ gi bizɛɛxwa si Yairo aba a, eze ɔka ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ. Adɔ ní Yesu yɛ ketu ya, lɛ aki yɛ litukpo si ɔba yɛ Yairo kepea mɛ ɛ

english_verse: One day, Jesus and his disciples got into a boat, and he said, “Let's cross the lake.” They started out,
translation_verse: Liwe etɔ lɛ ɛ, Yesu nì yɛ badɔkpasi ba bemu iku ní katapokpa atɔ mɛ. Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlaki tɔ kutsa limu ne tɔ kutrɛ ní oge lɔ kɔlɔ aʋa.” Lɛ beku kudeo me e.

english_verse: So they went to Jesus and woke him up, “Master, Master! We are about to drown!” Jesus got up and ordered the wind and waves to stop. They obeyed, and everything was calm.
translation_verse: Lɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛhwani Yesu u, lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Ɔkaɛ, Ɔkaɛ, kuhanyrɔ ɔ!” Yesu ayɔ, lɛ aki liwɔ lɛ ni kuni nyra nyra ɔsã ɛ. Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, liwɔ lɛ limɔni kufu, lɛ kuni yo kwidɔ zii gi niklɔ ɛgɔ kranaa a.

english_verse: The man saw Jesus and screamed. He knelt down in front of him and shouted, “Jesus, Son of God Most High, what do you want with me? I beg you not to torture me!”
translation_verse: Gi amɔ Yesu i, akpɛ ozi gaglaa, lɛ adɔ ní Yesu ketu ya ɛ. Lɛ apani ni ozi si, “Wɔ Yesu, Ɔka Ʋidi ye Yebi ye! Liboe woli wɔɛpɛ ní mɛ su u? Maki wɔ litukpo wukihe kutu iiki mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: When the men taking care of the pigs saw this, they ran to spread the news in the town and on the farms.
translation_verse: Baxwɛwaa ba gi bezeedi iha lɛ aʋa ɛ, bɛmɔ liboe lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛ; nite si bese etrɛ lɛ bezehle liboe le kpɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ni inyrɔɔ nɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: He said this because Jesus had already told the evil spirit to go out of him. The man had often been attacked by the demon. And even though he had been bound with chains and leg irons and kept under guard, he smashed whatever bound him. Then the demon would force him out into lonely places.
translation_verse: Apani nite lɛ ese linu si, Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no si “do ní yɛ mɛ.” Epoe glegle e, xe ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛdɔ ní yɛ aʋa a, xe bɛkpɛ yɛ bibɔ ʋɛ, ikli ni iwla a, xe beepe ye fɛ ɛ, ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no izɛki si onyimee izɛpa bala ʋa kɔ ɛkpikpini yɛ ɛtsisi ɔyaya klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked the man, “What is your name?” He answered, “My name is Lots.” He said this because there were “lots” of demons in him.
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi ye si, “Kitɛ bizɛxwa wɔ ɔ?” Onyimee ebu isu ya onu si, Mɛ linyi si “Likpi.” Lɛ ese linu si ifuɛfuɛ zimizi glegle ize ní yɛ mɛ.

english_verse: A large herd of pigs was feeding there on the hillside. So the demons begged Jesus to let them go into the pigs, and Jesus let them go.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, iha likpɔkpɛkpɛ ʋidi itɔ lizɛɛŋa bidɔ ní ketokpaa su ní niklɔ. Lɛ loso ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne iiki Yesu litukpo, si ɔki tɔ izetiku ní iha lɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ Yesu aki lɛ kudeo onu i.

english_verse: Then the demons left the man and went into the pigs. The whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
translation_verse: Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ido ní onyimee me lɛ izetiku ní iha lɛ mɛ ɛ. Nite si iha lɛ likpi le petee liseeple ketokpaa lɛ izɛdɔ limu ne, iŋwɛ kuni yo xe itse e.

english_verse: Everyone from around Gerasa begged Jesus to leave, because they were so frightened. When Jesus got into the boat to start back,
translation_verse: Lɛ Gerasese banɛ ma petee bɛsi Yesu si, ɔsɛ ní ba kesea mɛ lɛ ese linu si lifu liku ba nyani nyani. Lɛ loso e, Yesu emu eku katapokpaa mɛ lɛ asɛ ɛ.

english_verse: the man who had been healed begged to go with him. But Jesus sent him off and said,
translation_verse: Onyime liɛ klɔ mɛ gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ido e, aki Yesu litukpo si, “Ki tɔ mize ni wɔ kude.” Pɔ̀ Yesu akpɛ yɛ kude si,

english_verse: “Go back home and tell everyone how much God has done for you.” The man then went all over town, telling everything that Jesus had done for him. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
translation_verse: “Dzi trɛ ní kepea mɛ tɔ zɛɛsi banima kidiliɛ gi Aya abitɛ akí wɔ ɛ.” Niteme si onyimee atrɛ lɛ azɛɛpani aklanɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ kidiliɛ gi Yesu abitɛ aki yɛ ɛ. Yairo yɛ obidzɛɛ yɛ kuyɔnɔɔ Ni Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi agbɔni Yesu yɛ liwu le e

english_verse: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years. She had spent everything she had on doctors, but none of them could make her well.
