translation_verse: Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi ika izedo ní yɛ su agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa nyɔŋ ɛ, eze ní linɔkpi le kusi. Pɔ̀ ɔdzɛ liɛ ɛ, ezizi yɛ kibɔɛ petee ní bawabitɛ ba su, pɔ̀ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani yɛ kuyɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Everyone had been waiting for Jesus, and when he came back, a crowd was there to welcome him.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu edzì atrɛ ní limù ne oge lɔ kɔlɔ ɛ, banima bɛkpɛ yɛ wolaa, lɛ ese linu si banima petee bezeepe ye.

english_verse: because his twelve-year-old child was dying. She was his only daughter. While Jesus was on his way, people were crowding all around him.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si yɛ obidzɛ tolee gi aŋa agɔ liɔfɔ ni taʋa ihatse e. Agi Yesu eze kudeo me e, linɔkpi le lizɛpɔ yɛ ɛkpɛ ní kupɛɔ petee.

english_verse: As soon as she came up behind Jesus and barely touched his clothes, her bleeding stopped.
translation_verse: Etutru isu ya akpɛ linɔkpi le kusi gani Yesu kedea, lɛ agbɔni yɛ liwu le kutu yɔ ɛ. Lɛ onu klɔ ko e, yɛ ika lɛ kudodoo kwitsa!

english_verse: “Who touched me?” Jesus asked. While everyone was denying it, Peter said, “Master, people are crowding all around and pushing you from every side.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi si, “Nyaŋwɛ agbɔni mɛ ɛ?” Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́dimɛ si yɛ ɛ. Niklɔ Petro asi yɛ si, “Ɔka, banima petee bɛpɔ wɔ ɛkpɛ”,

english_verse: The woman knew that she could not hide, so she came trembling and knelt down in front of Jesus. She told everyone why she had touched him and that she had been healed at once.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ amɔ si kibitɛ kima tɔ ɔ; nite si ekpese kusugu, lɛ adɔ Yesu ní ketu ya ɛ. Lɛ ní banima petee aŋu na mɛ ɛ, asi yɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso yɛ agbɔni yɛ nì kiliɛ gi kunyaa kwiyurɔ yɛ kyɔŋ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Mɛ obidzɛɛ, wɔ kuziŋaa kwiyɔni wɔ. Ga trɛ ní kusuyɔ mɛ!”

english_verse: While Jesus was speaking, someone came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu azaapani i, ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ ádɔ ní Yairo kepea mɛ aba si Yairo ɔtsaklɔ ɔkaɛ si, “Wɔ obidzɛɛ etse,” lɛ loso e, “wukúkpɛ Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ kunugu tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus went into the house, but he did not let anyone else go with him, except Peter, John, James, and the girl's father and mother.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu eku Yairo kepea mɛ ɛ, ɔ́ki ɔnɔnɔ kude onu si oti oku sani Petro, Yohanes ni Yakobo kɔpɔni obika yɛ ni obinee e.

english_verse: Everyone was crying and weeping for the girl. But Jesus said, “The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa eze ni asigaga mɛ lɛ bezeebemi ɔnuvɔɛ. Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlakíbemi i! Ɔnuvɔɛ ótse e; sra ko ɛɛdɔ!”

english_verse: The people laughed at him because they knew she was dead.
translation_verse: Ba petee bɛmwɛ yɛ, lɛ ese linu si bete si ɔdzɛʋi yɛ etse.

english_verse: Jesus took hold of the girl's hand and said, “Child, get up!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu evu ɔdzɛʋi yɛ kawlakpɔa lɛ asi yɛ si, “Mɛ obi ye, yɔ!”

english_verse: She came back to life and got right up. Jesus told them to give her something to eat.
translation_verse: Yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ edzì iku yɛ mɛ, nite si ayɔ ɔlɛɛ kyɔŋ. Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, bapɛ́ kiditɔ aki ɔdzɛʋi yɛ tɔ ɔŋa a.

english_verse: Her parents were surprised, but Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened.
translation_verse: Obidzɛʋi yɛ yɛka ni yene ba inugu itse. Pɔ̀ Yesu akpɛ ɔsã aki ba si bakífeke onugu ní liboe le aʋa si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The apostles came back and told Jesus everything they had done. He then took them with him to the village of Bethsaida, where they could be alone.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bedzi idɔni kudeo me e, bɛba ɛki yɛ kunutsatsa ní eboa la petee gi bɛzɛɛbitɛ su u. Lɛ agbani ba evu lɛ bedoetrɛ ní ɔma ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Bɛtsaida mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They did this, and all the people sat down.
translation_verse: Gi badɔkpasi ba bɛki si banima beze kesea ɛ,

english_verse: Herod the ruler heard about all that was happening, and he was worried. Some people were saying John the Baptist had come back to life.
translation_verse: Gi Herodes gi azɛɛŋa Galilea kesea aʋa a, enu eboa la petee gi Yesu azaabitɛ ɛ, apisa. Lɛ ese linu si banɔ batɔ bɛpani si, Yohanes Kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛɛ yɛ edzì ligbã.

english_verse: He told them, “Don't take anything with you! Don't take a walking stick or a traveling bag or food or money or even a change of clothes.
translation_verse: Asi ba si, “Mlakíkɔ kididɔ evu aki mlɔ kudegaga a: mlakívu osevugavuga, kepimi loo kiŋaŋa, kibɔ loo liwu ʋla a.

english_verse: When you are welcomed into a home, stay there until you leave that town.
translation_verse: Xegi bɛzi mlɔ ní kepe atɔ mɛ ɛ, mleze ni iklɔ kɔkpɛni mlɔ lisɛpoe le ní ɔmaa lɔ klɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Late in the afternoon the twelve apostles came to Jesus and said, “Send the crowd to the villages and farms around here. They need to find a place to stay and something to eat. There is nothing in this place. It's like a desert!”
translation_verse: Agi liwo le lizeeku i, badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba bɛba ɛsi yɛ si, “Ki tɔ banima basɛ tɔ beti iku kumami yɔ ni inyrɔ nɛ me tɔ bazɛpɛ kiŋaŋa ni iyakɔ; lɛ ese linu si niya ebu lidze.”

english_verse: While he was praying, his face changed, and his clothes became shining white.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi Yesu azɛɛxwa Aya ɛ, yɛ aŋu na mɛ kiŋwiŋwi yɛ mɛ kikyi, yɛ bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ bixwa pititi lɛ bize ŋle e.

english_verse: Jesus strictly warned his disciples not to tell anyone about this. Jesus Speaks about His Suffering and Death (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu akpɛ ɔsã gagla aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba si bakífeke onugu ní eboa la aʋa asi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: “Pay close attention to what I am telling you! The Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies.”
translation_verse: “Mlakídze liboe lɛ gi matrasi mlɔ ɛ. Bɛtrákpɛ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye ní awla ɛki banima.”

english_verse: But a lot of people found out about this and followed him. Jesus welcomed them. He spoke about God's kingdom and healed everyone who was sick.
translation_verse: Agi linɔkpi le linu niklɔ gi bɛtrɛ ɛ, bɛgbani yɛ ɛkpɛ. Azɛ ba lɛ apani aki ba ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa su u, lɛ banama gi bɛziã kupɔnɔ ní ba bànya ʋa mɛ ɛ, ayɔni ba a.

english_verse: “Don't stop him!” Jesus said. “Anyone who isn't against you is for you.” A Samaritan Village Refuses To Receive Jesus
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ nì badɔkpasi gɔgɔ ba si, “Mlekítsi kudeo aki yɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́yɔ kedu ní mlɔ su i, ɔtini mlɔ aʋa.” Samaria ɔma ɔtɔ ègbe ɔ́zɛ Yesu u

english_verse: About eight days later Jesus took Peter, John, and James with him and went up on a mountain to pray.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi ewe ɔgɔtaʋa kede gi apani eboa la klɔ ɛ, Yesu agbani Petro, Yohanes ni Yakobo, lɛ bɛtrɛ ní ketokpa katɔ aʋa si tɔ bɛzɛxwa Aya a.

english_verse: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me, you will save it.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔ kakaa gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aakɔ̀ yɛ ligbã nɛ kukɔkyɛ ní mɛ litukpo e, aabu yɛ ligbã nɛ.

english_verse: What will you gain, if you own the whole world but destroy yourself or waste your life?
translation_verse: Libi woli lilɛ ki ɔnutɔ xe aamɔ kelea mɛ bidɔ mɛ petee e pɔ̀ xe aalila yɛ esobo la a?

english_verse: If you are ashamed of me and my message, the Son of Man will be ashamed of you when he comes in his glory and in the glory of his Father and the holy angels.
translation_verse: Xegi ɔnutɔ aase kuye ní mɛ nì mɛ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ su i, kɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye fɛ aase kuye ní yɛ su xegi aba ní yɛ tutɔ yɛ kusudraa, nì Yeka ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ dradra ba petee ba kusudraa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They appeared in heavenly glory and talked about all that Jesus' death in Jerusalem would mean.
translation_verse: bɛŋwi ní lifunu ne kusudraa mɛ; gi bɛzɛpani ni Yesu ní kiliɛ mɛ gi litraze xe aatse ní Yerusalem ní Aya kutrɔtrɔ onu su u.

english_verse: ) While everyone was still amazed at what Jesus was doing, he said to his disciples,
translation_verse: Petro ni yɛ ahanuglɔ ba bɛzɛ ní sra mɛ, pɔ̀ gi ba aŋu mɛ ɛdra ɛ, bɛmɔ Yesu yɛ kusudraa ni banɔ tiaʋa ba gi balɛɛ ni Yesu i.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, a shadow from a cloud passed over, and they were frightened as the cloud covered them.
translation_verse: Gi azaapani i, liplɛ ɛtɔ liŋwi lɛ liba ewe ba a; lɛ gi beti iku liplɛ lɛ mɛ ɛ, lifu liku badɔkpasi ba.

english_verse: From the cloud a voice spoke, “This is my chosen Son. Listen to what he says!”
translation_verse: Ole ɔtɔ eyi dɔnɔ ní liplɛ lɛ mɛ si, “Mɛ Obi ye iliɛ ya, yɛ makya! Mlata yɛ kutu!”

english_verse: After the voice had spoken, Peter, John, and James saw only Jesus. For some time they kept quiet and did not say anything about what they had seen. Jesus Heals a Boy (Matthew
translation_verse: Gi ole lo ɛpani tsa ɛ, bɛmɔ si Yesu tètè ko agɔ. Badɔkpasi ba béfeke onugu ní eboa la aŋu mɛ gi bɛmɔ su i si ɔnɔnɔ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ. Yesu ayɔni ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ mɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi ize e

english_verse: A demon often attacks him and makes him scream. It shakes him until he foams at the mouth, and it won't leave him until it has completely worn the boy out.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ ɔtɔ izɛdɔ yɛ aʋa gi izɛki si izɛkpɛ ozi lo, kɔ ekpese kuhe e. Nite si ɔfɔ lɔ izedo yɛ onu. Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛdugu ye gi sigagla mɛ izɛyurɔ awla ní yɛ su u.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You people are stubborn and don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you?” Then Jesus said to the man, “Bring your son to me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Kitɛ xe kumɔziŋa ni kesetata kalɛ mlɔ mɛ nite e?” “Kɔna ni lipoe woli maaze mlɔ su du u? Lɛ kɔna ni lipoe wolite maplɛ litɔ kese kimlɔ du u? Lɛ asi onyimee si, Gbani wɔ obi ye mani ya a!”

english_verse: While the boy was being brought, the demon attacked him and made him shake all over. Jesus ordered the demon to stop. Then he healed the boy and gave him back to his father.
translation_verse: Agi ɔyasɔʋi lɔ etrába ɛ, ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no ɛsa yɛ kesea gi ekpese kuhe e. Pɔ̀ Yesu akpɛ ozi ní ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ aʋa, lɛ ayɔni ɔyasɔʋi lɔ, lɛ akɔ lɔ aki yɛka ɛ.

english_verse: But the disciples did not know what he meant. The meaning was hidden from them. They could not understand it, and they were afraid to ask. Who Is the Greatest? (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ yɛ badɔkpasi ba bénu liboe le ese e. Bɛ kɔ lɛ ekpo ba tɔ bakítani lɛ ese kunu. Lɛ badɔkpasi ba kyɛ ɛ, bese lifu ne ní kiliɛ gi biaʋi ye liboe le ese e. Nyaŋwɛ asa keme

english_verse: Jesus' disciples were arguing about which one of them was the greatest.
translation_verse: Kuŋwlɛŋwlɛ kwiku badɔkpasi ba kusi ní ɔniliɛ gi yɛ asa keme ní ba kusi su u.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he had a child stand there beside him.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ete ba begyodi ba onu i, agbani ɔnuvɔ ɔtɔ amanɔ lɛ aki si ɔlɛ ni yɛ su i;

english_verse: Then he said to his disciples, “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me. Whichever one of you is the most humble is the greatest.” For or against Jesus (Mark
translation_verse: lɛ asi ba si, “Ɔniliɛ gi aazi kanuvɔʋia lia ní mɛ linyi ne me e, mɛ ɔniɛ azɛ ɛ. Lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ mɛ ɛ, azɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ akpɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi yɛ onu ɔsisami yɛ petee ní mlɔ kusi i, yɛ asa keme e.” Ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́yɔ kedu ní mlɔ su i, mlɔ aʋa ɔtini

english_verse: John said, “Master, we saw a man using your name to force demons out of people. But we told him to stop, because he isn't one of us.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yohanes apani si, “Blɔ Ɔkaɛ, kwimɔ onyime ɔtɔ gi azɛkpikpinì ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono ní wɔ linyi ne me e, nite si kwisi yɛ si odzini kumɔni lɛ ese linu si aní blɔ mɛ tole onu u.”

english_verse: Not long before it was time for Jesus to be taken up to heaven, he made up his mind to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu yɛ lifunu ne kutrɛtrɛɔ lipoe le litipɔnɔ ɛ, atrɔ lɛ si yɛ atrɛ ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: He sent some messengers on ahead to a Samaritan village to get things ready for him.
translation_verse: Niteme si atsisi baxwɛdzɛ ba ní yɛ ɔtɔ nɔ ní Samaria kamami katɔ mɛ si babla kidɔkakaa aplɛ aki yɛ.

english_verse: But he was on his way to Jerusalem, so the people there refused to welcome him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banima ni iklɔ bázɛ yɛ ɛ; lɛ ese linu si lɛ mɛ lidra ɛki ba si Yesu atrága ba klɔ ɛtrɛ ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: When the disciples James and John saw what was happening, they asked, “Lord, do you want us to call down fire from heaven to destroy these people?”
translation_verse: Agi yɛ badɔkpasi ba Yakobo ni Yohanes bɛmɔ lɛ gyesi i, beʋi si, “Blɔ Ɔkaɛ, wɔpɛ si kuxwa kifu idɔni lifunu ne tɔ kibakani banɔ ba ya na a?”

english_verse: But Jesus turned and corrected them for what they had said.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ekyi xe aka aŋu mɛ aki ba ɛ

english_verse: Then they all went on to another village. Three People Who Wanted To Be Followers (Matthew
translation_verse: Nite si Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bekyi itrɛ ní kamami dzedze me. Banama elu gi biaze ni Yesu kude e

english_verse: Along the way someone said to Jesus, “I'll follow you anywhere!”
translation_verse: Agi bɛzɛtrɛ ní kudeo me e, onyime ɔtɔ asi Yesu si, “Ɔkɔ kakaa gi wɔatrɛ ɛ, maze ni wɔ kude.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Foxes have dens, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man doesn't have a place to call his own.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Wayu ʋa bɛmɔ beke ʋa, lɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ kyɛ kwimɔ evu na. Pɔ̀ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye e, ɔ́mɔ ɔkɔkɔ gi aafɛ ɛ, xe aayɔ isu mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told someone else to come with him. But the man said, “Lord, let me wait until I bury my father.”
translation_verse: Yesu asi ɔnɔ dzedze si, “Zenì mɛ kude!” Pɔ̀ onyimee asi yɛ si, “Ɔka kitɔ mizeevive mɔka xe e.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Let the dead take care of the dead, while you go and tell about God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Ki tɔ bekpo ba bevive ba tutɔ ba bekpo ba. Wɔ kɔ trɛ tɔ zepani ni Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa su u.”

english_verse: Then someone said to Jesus, “I want to follow you, Lord, but first let me go back and take care of things at home.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔ dzedze kyɛ asi yɛ si, “Ɔkaɛ, maaku ni wɔ kude; pɔ̀ ki tɔ mizɛɛklá mɛ benete ba xe e.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Anyone who starts plowing and keeps looking back isn't worth a thing to God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛwa lixwɛ xe ɔ́ʋiwa lɛ ni yɛ ɔna nɔ petee, ógyeno gi aasumu ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: He said to them: A large crop is in the fields, but there are only a few workers. Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Amu na lipapoe le lina pɔ̀ la bapapa ba básrɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso mleʋi tɔ ɔnyrɔɔ nɔ onetee ɔtsisi baxwɛwa ba ní yɛ kayaa mɛ tɔ bapa la.

english_verse: Now go, but remember, I am sending you like lambs into a pack of wolves.
translation_verse: Mlatrɛ! Mɛɛkpɛ mlɔ ŋwisi kuʋesibi kinɛ ní kuŋwafudroʋi yo kusi i.

english_verse: Don't take along a moneybag or a traveling bag or sandals. And don't waste time greeting people on the road.
translation_verse: Xe mlɛɛtrɛ ɛ, mlakívu kepimi loo ɔdɔʋi gudo loo afɔkpa lide ʋla, Mlakílɛɛ apani ɔnɔnɔ ní kudeo aʋa a.

english_verse: As soon as you enter a home, say, “God bless this home with peace.”
translation_verse: “Xe mleti iku kepe katɔ mɛ ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, mlapani si, “Kusuyɔ kuze ní kepea lia aʋa a!”

english_verse: If the people living there are peace-loving, your prayer for peace will bless them. But if they are not peace-loving, your prayer will return to you.
translation_verse: Xegi kusuyɔ onete ɔlɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ, mlɔ kusuyɔɔ kwiaze ní yɛ aʋa. Xe aní te pɔ̀ ɛ, mlɔ kusuyɔɔ kwiadzi kuba mlɔ klɔ.

english_verse: Stay with the same family, eating and drinking whatever they give you, because workers are worth what they earn. Don't move around from house to house.
translation_verse: Mlatí ní kepea lia klɔ mɛ. Mlaŋa bidɔ tɔ mlaŋwɛ kidɔ kakaa gi bɛki mlɔ ɛ; lɛ ese linu si ɔxwɛwa yɛ yɛ bizɛɛtsa liʋɛ ɛkɔ ɔ. Mlakítsrɛ kupeo o.

english_verse: If the people of a town welcome you, eat whatever they offer.
translation_verse: Xegi mleti iku ɔma ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ, xe bɛkpɛ mlɔ wolaa ɛ, mlaŋa kidiliɛ gi bɛki mlɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Heal their sick and say, “God's kingdom will soon be here!”
translation_verse: “Mlayɔ ni banyanya ba ní ɔmaa lɔ klɔ mɛ, tɔ mlasi banima ní klɔ si, ‘Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ketipɔni mlɔ.’

english_verse: But if the people of a town refuse to welcome you, go out into the street and say,
translation_verse: Pɔ niklɔ gi mlɛtrɛ ɛ, xe bázɛ mlɔ ɛ, mledo ní lɔ ablɔ ʋa aʋa tɔ mlapani si,

english_verse: I tell you that on the day of judgment the people of Sodom will get off easier than the people of that town! The Unbelieving Towns (Matthew
translation_verse: Matrɔ likla lɛ aʋa aki mlɔ si, Aya aadi Sodom asigaga sani ɔma lɔ klɔ ní Litsixwawe le aʋa. Kutuhehe ki Imaɛ lɛ klɔ gi íziŋa ɛ

english_verse: But don't be happy because evil spirits obey you. Be happy that your names are written in heaven! Jesus Thanks His Father (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ isami ikíku mlo tete si ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne inu kutu iiki mlɔ ɔ; boŋu i, isami iku mlo si bɛŋwlimi mlɔ enyi na ní lifunu ne.” Yesu amɔ isami koŋ

english_verse: Many prophets and kings were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear.” The Good Samaritan
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, eboa la gi mlɛɛmɔ ɛ, beboedoplɛ glegle ni bekusi ba bɛpɛ la aŋu mɛ si bɛɛmɔ pɔ̀ líba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ bɛpɛ si beenu eboa la gi mleenu i, pɔ̀ bénu u.” Abee ní Samaria ɔnɛ gbã yɛ su

english_verse: On the day of judgment the people of Tyre and Sidon will get off easier than you will.
translation_verse: Ni Litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, Aya aadi Tiro ni Sidon asigaga sani mlɔ.

english_verse: People of Capernaum, do you think you will be honored in heaven? Well, you will go down to hell!
translation_verse: Lɛ wɔ Kapernaum ɔma nɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, kitɛ wɔpɛ si wumuni isu ya lifune e? Lizokpo gluglui lɛ gi odukɔ ɔma kilɛ ɛ, lɛ mɛ bɛtráŋwia wɔ ɛkpɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told them: I saw Satan fall from heaven like a flash of lightning.
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, “Mamɔ Satana gi awɔli adɔ nɔ lifunu ne ŋwisi dzeni kifububu kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I have given you the power to trample on snakes and scorpions and to defeat the power of your enemy Satan. Nothing can harm you.
translation_verse: Mlata kutu! Maki mlɔ kigagla si tɔ mlaatani kuga ní bagblaga ʋa ni kutapiadzɔɔ aʋa, lɛ mlaaŋa Ozimizi ye kigaglaɛ petee aʋa gi kididɔ kítrasa mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: An expert in the Law of Moses stood up and asked Jesus a question to see what he would say. “Teacher,” he asked, “what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si ɔsã nɔ ɔdɔbasɛ ɔtɔ aba Yesu klɔ gi apɛ si yipunu yɛ evu u. Lɛ eʋi ye si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ,” “ege maabitɛ xe maamɔ ligbã́ moblo le e?”

english_verse: The man replied, “The Scriptures say, ‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart, soul, strength, and mind.’ They also say, ‘Love your neighbors as much as you love yourself.’ ”
translation_verse: Lɛ onyimee ebu le onu si, “Dimɛni Ɔkaɛ gi yɛ onu wɔ Aya ɛ, nì wɔ ɔna nɔ petee, nì wɔ esobo la petee, nì wɔ kigaglaɛ petee ani wɔ kugyodi yo petee. Lɛ dimɛni wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ isu ya kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: At that same time, Jesus felt the joy that comes from the Holy Spirit, and he said: My Father, Lord of heaven and earth, I am grateful that you hid all this from wise and educated people and showed it to ordinary people. Yes, Father, this is what pleased you.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ ɛki si isami iyi Yesu me. Lɛ apani si, “Mɔka, wɔ wonu lifunu ne ni kesea Ɔkaɛ! Medo wɔ ɖase si wɔkɔ eboa la okpo beboete ba ni beplekpate ba, pɔ̀ woble la ɔbasɛ krutu ba. Ee, Ɔka, nite wɔ pɛ si lize.

english_verse: My Father has given me everything, and he is the only one who knows the Son. The only one who really knows the Father is the Son. But the Son wants to tell others about the Father, so they can know him too.
translation_verse: “Mɔka akɔ bidɔ mɛ petee akpɛ mɛ awla. Ɔnɔnɔ óte ɔniliɛ gi Obi ye onu u sani Ɔkaɛ. Lɛ ɔnɔnɔ óte ɔniliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ onu u sani Obi ye ni banama gi beku Obi ye liŋu si yibu Ɔkaɛ basɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus then turned to his disciples and said to them in private, “You are really blessed to see what you see!
translation_verse: “Lɛ Yesu ekyi edi yɛ badɔkpasi ba xe asi ba tètè dzaa si, “Kunya kulɛ mlɔ su koŋ gi mlɛmɔ eboa la aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is written in the Scriptures? How do you understand them?”
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi ye si, “Kitɛ bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Mose ɔsã nɔ mɛ ɛ? Kitɛ wonu le ese e?”

english_verse: Jesus said, “You have given the right answer. If you do this, you will have eternal life.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Wobu le onu mumɔ, Trɛ tɔ zɛbitɛ kiliɛ gi wɔpani i, tɔ wɔaze ligbã́.”

english_verse: But the man wanted to show that he knew what he was talking about. So he asked Jesus, “Who are my neighbors?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔsã nɔ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ apɛ si yɛ linɛ ligyɔ ɔ; lɛ loso eʋi Yesu dzɛ si, “Nyaŋwɛ onu mɛ ɔhanuglɔɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: As a man was going down from Jerusalem to Jericho, robbers attacked him and grabbed everything he had. They beat him up and ran off, leaving him half dead.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki yɛ si, “Liba lɛ mɛ liwetɔ lɛ si, onyime ɔtɔ ádɔ Yerusalem gi azeeple atrɛ ní Yerixo e. Beze batɔ bɛdɔ yɛ aʋa, bɛzi yɛ bidɔ mɛ petee ní yɛ awla, bekusi ye lɛ bebu ye liya lɛ ɛ. Niteme si bɛyurɔ yɛ ɛplɛ gi etse okpokpo lo e.

english_verse: A priest happened to be going down the same road. But when he saw the man, he walked by on the other side.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si kusɔli yɔ ɔka ɔtɔ azɛɛga kudeo liɔ klɔ ke, pɔ̀ gi amɔ onyimee gi bekusi i, aga kedepɛ ʋla aza a.

english_verse: Later a temple helper came to the same place. But when he saw the man who had been beaten up, he also went by on the other side.
translation_verse: Nite ke Lewiɔnɛ ɔtɔ kyɛ gi aba na níklɔ ɔ xe edi onyime e, yɛ kyɛ aga kedepɛ ʋlaa aza yɛ aʋa.

english_verse: A man from Samaria then came traveling along that road. When he saw the man, he felt sorry for him
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Samaria ɔnɛ ɔtɔ gi yɛ kyɛ azɛɛga kudeo liɔ klɔ ɛ, aklani onyime onu. Gi amɔ yɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔna ekyi vu onyimee.

english_verse: and went over to him. He treated his wounds with olive oil and wine and bandaged them. Then he put him on his own donkey and took him to an inn, where he took care of him.
translation_verse: Eti apɔni yɛ su, lɛ abali wain ni kumu akpɛ ní yɛ ebi la mɛ ɛ, lɛ anya na ɛ. Lɛ efeke ye atrɔ ní yɛ tedzi ye aʋa a, xe akɔ yɛ atsinɔ ní bedega ba ɔyakɔ ɔtɔ ɔ, xe edi yɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: The next morning he gave the innkeeper two silver coins and said, “Please take care of the man. If you spend more than this on him, I will pay you when I return.”
translation_verse: Keletitia mɛ ɛ, ebu bibɔ pititi tuʋa akɔ aki kepea ɔʋadi ye. Lɛ asi yɛ si, ‘Di yɛ aʋa, tɔ kidɔ kakaa elu gi wɔazizi pɔnɔ ní yɛ litukpo e, maatsa kɛ ɔki wɔ xe medzi aba ɛ.’

english_verse: Then Jesus asked, “Which one of these three people was a real neighbor to the man who was beaten up by robbers?”
translation_verse: “Lɛ Yesu agu yɛ liboe le ni kuʋiʋi si, “Ni wɔ kugyodi yo onu i, ni banɔ tiata ba ya mɛ ɛ, owoli onu ɔhanuglɔ ɔki odega liɛ gi awɔli eku ní beze ba iwla mɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: The expert in the Law of Moses answered, “The one who showed pity.” Jesus said, “Go and do the same!” Martha and Mary
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔsã nɔ ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ ebu le onu si, “ɔniliɛ gi edi yɛ asigaga ɛ.” Yesu asi yɛ si, “Wɔ kyɛ trɛ, tɔ zɛbitɛ nite ke e.” Yesu adzɛ Marta ni Maria klɔ

english_verse: The Lord and his disciples were traveling along and came to a village. When they got there, a woman named Martha welcomed him into her home.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba beze ní ba kudeo aʋa ɛ, beku kamamiatɔ mɛ gi ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Marta eze e. Yɛ si azɛ Yesu akpɛ ní yɛ kepea mɛ.

english_verse: She had a sister named Mary, who sat down in front of the Lord and was listening to what he said.
translation_verse: Yɛ onemidzɛ ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Maria ɛ, eze ní Yesu yɛ ikli lɛ onu gi azeenu yɛ kudɔbasɛ yɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Martha was worried about all that had to be done. Finally, she went to Jesus and said, “Lord, doesn't it bother you that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to come and help me!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kaʋadi ya axwɛ na emi ní Marta tole aʋa a. Nite si Marta aba Yesu klɔ, lɛ asi yɛ si, “Ɔka wɔ́kɔ lɛ me si monemi ye ayurɔ axwɛ na petee atrɔ ni mɛ tole aʋa a? Si yɛ tɔ ɔbapɔni mɛ.”

english_verse: The Lord answered, “Martha, Martha! You are worried and upset about so many things,
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki yɛ si, “Marta, Marta wɔepè lɛ wɔxixa ní eboe glegle su u.

english_verse: but only one thing is necessary. Mary has chosen what is best, and it will not be taken away from her.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kidɔ tile ko kizia. Maria kɔ akya kidɔ gbã ɛ gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trazi kɛ ní yɛ awla a.”

english_verse: So I tell you to ask and you will receive, search and you will find, knock and the door will be opened for you.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso mɛɛsi mlɔ si i, mleʋi bidɔ tɔ baki mlɔ; mlapɛ bidɔ tɔ mlamɔ; mla sa opupo lo tɔ badra lɔ aki mlɔ.

english_verse: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give the Holy Spirit to anyone who asks. Jesus and the Ruler of Demons (Matthew
translation_verse: Mlɔ ŋwisi baka kinɛ gi mlɛnyani kyɛ ɛ, mlete kiliɛ gi mlaaki mlɔ bebi ba bidɔ gbã ʋɛ ɛ. Lɛ kitɛ gbɛgbɛ liaze xe mlɔ Ɔka yɛ ní lifunu ne e, ɔ́trayurɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ ɛki banama gi biaʋi ye na a!” Yesu nì Belzebul

english_verse: And suppose your friend answers, “Don't bother me! The door is bolted, and my children and I are in bed. I cannot get up to give you something.”
translation_verse: “Lɛ xegi liba lɛ mɛ si wɔ ɔʋiaɛ aabu le onu dɔni yɛ ligba lɛ mɛ si, ‘Wukíkpɛ mɛ kunugu u! Madà mɛ kevunu ya kooko, lɛ mɛ nì mɛ bebi ba kwiku ligba a. Mátratani kuyɔ xe maadi wɔ ɔza a.’

english_verse: Which one of you fathers would give your hungry child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
translation_verse: “Nyaŋwɛ ɔlɛ ní ya gi onu obika xe Yebi ye aaʋi ye likpa a xe aakɔ̀ ɔgblaga ɔki yɛ ɛ?

english_verse: So Jesus told them, “Pray in this way: ‘Father, help us to honor your name. Come and set up your kingdom.
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, xegi mlɛɛxwa Aya ɛ, mlapani si, “ ‘Ɔka, Kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ki wɔ linyi ne; Ki tɔ wɔ Kaʋaŋaa keple kú ní blɔ kusi,

english_verse: Forgive our sins, as we forgive everyone who has done wrong to us. And keep us from being tempted.’ ”
translation_verse: Kɔ blɔ eboe zimizi na kyɛ blɔ ɔ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi blɔ kyɛ ɛ, kuzɛɛkɔ ɛkyɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛta blɔ ese kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ wukígbani blɔ ɛkpɛ kufekedi me e.’”

english_verse: Everyone who asks will receive, everyone who searches will find, and the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, ɔnɔ kakaa gi izeʋi bidɔ ɛ, bizɛki yɛ; lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛpɛ bidɔ ɛ, izɛmɔ; lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛsa opupo lo e, bizɛdra lɔ ɛki yɛ.

english_verse: Just as Jonah was a sign to the people of Nineveh, the Son of Man will be a sign to the people of today.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Yona oboedoplɛɛ eze liŋwitsɛ aki Ninive banɛ ma ɛ, nite ke Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kyɛ aaze liŋwitsɛ aki abla kelea mɛ benete ba.

english_verse: While Jesus was still talking, a woman in the crowd spoke up, “The woman who gave birth to you and nursed you is blessed!”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu agù yɛ kupaniɔ ɛ, ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ ní ɔnɔha lɔ kusi apani si, “Aya aakrira one liɛ gi apɔ wɔ xe aki wɔ limwa nɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus forced a demon out of a man who could not talk. And after the demon had gone out, the man started speaking, and the crowds were amazed.
translation_verse: Yesu azɛ kpikpini ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi gi ɔʋípani dono ní onyime ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ. Gi ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no edo pɔ́ ɛ, onyimee apani, gi litu linɔkpi le kugyɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Others wanted to put Jesus to the test. So they asked him to show them a sign from God.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ kyɛ bɛpɛ si bɛɛpunu Yesu. Lɛ loso e, beʋi idɔnɔ ní yɛ awla si, ɔbitɛ linugutseboe litɔ gi liabasɛ si Aya anya kpɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus knew what they were thinking, and he said: A kingdom where people fight each other will end up in ruin. And a family that fights will break up.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Yesu etè ba begyodi ʋa onu i, asi ba si, “Kaʋaŋaa kakaa gi ka mɛ benete ba bɛyɔ kedu ní ba ahanuglɔ ba su i, kiayaʋi, lɛ kepe kakaa gi ka mɛ benete ba bɛyɔ kedu ní ba ahanuglɔ ba su i, kiayaʋi.

english_verse: If Satan fights against himself, how can his kingdom last? Yet you say that I force out demons by the power of Beelzebul.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xe Satana kaʋaŋaa kɛlɛ ní paso ʋa mɛ ɛ, xe bɛɛgɔni i, kitɛ liaze xe kiali i? Mlɛɛpani si Belzebul yɛ aki mɛ kigaglaɛ xe mɛɛkpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono o.

english_verse: If I use his power to force out demons, whose power do your own followers use to force them out? They are the ones who will judge you.
translation_verse: Xegi Belzebul aʋa mɛɛkpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono e, kɔ nyaŋwɛ yɛ kigagla mɛ mlɔ kude bezenozeno ba bizɛkpikpini lɛ edono e? Mlɔ tutɔ mlɔ kude bezenozeno ba ɛ, ba beebu mlɔ lipɔ si mlɔ linɛ lígyɔ ɔ!

english_verse: When a strong man arms himself and guards his home, everything he owns is safe.
translation_verse: “Xegi onyimewa eepe yɛ kepea ni yɛ bigutadɔ mɛ ɛ, yɛ kepea kizeze ní kusuyɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You love the front seats in the synagogues, and you like to be greeted with honor in the market.
translation_verse: “Litráze gagla ɛki mlɔ Farisianɛ ma, mlɛzɛɛpɛ izekɔ gyɔgyɔ lɛ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ; lɛ mlɛzɛɛpɛ si bapani mlɔ ni kubùbù ní ablɔ ʋa aʋa.

english_verse: A teacher of the Law of Moses spoke up, “Teacher, you said cruel things about us.”
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba mɛ tole asi yɛ si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, xegi wɔɛɛpani eboa la ɛ, wɔekpini blɔ kyɛ.”

english_verse: When an evil spirit leaves a person, it travels through the desert, looking for a place to rest. But when it doesn't find a place, it says, “I will go back to the home I left.”
translation_verse: “Xegi ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi edo ní ɔnɔ mɛ ɛ, izɛpɛ ozekɔ ɛklanɔ ní ɔyaya kɔ. Xegi ɔ́mɔ ɔkɔ gbã ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ɛ, izɛsi isu ya si, ‘Maadzi trɛ ní mɛ kepe kokoa mɛ.’

english_verse: When it gets there and finds the place clean and fixed up,
translation_verse: Xe edzi atrɛ ɛ, aamɔ si bɛgba kepea mɛ ɛlulɔ,

english_verse: it goes off and finds seven other evil spirits even worse than itself. They all come and make their home there, and that person ends up in worse shape than before. Being Really Blessed
translation_verse: kɔ ɛzɛɛgbani ifuɛfuɛ zimizi tigloele dzedze gi inyani sani lɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛmanɔ kɔ iiba kɔ iizɛ ní kepea mɛ, gɔgɔɛ ɔnɔɛ yɛ kizezee me kudegbaa kianyani sani kiliɛ gi eze tsã ɛ.” Yesu abasi bidɔ ni Isami su

english_verse: Jesus replied, “That's true, but the people who are really blessed are the ones who hear and obey God's message!” A Sign from God (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Aya aakrira banama gi beenu Aya Liboe le xe bɛɛbitɛ ní lɛ aʋa a boŋu!” Yesu Ɔ́bitɛ Linugutseboe

english_verse: The Pharisee was surprised that he did not wash his hands before eating.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ litu Farisiɔnɛ kugyɔ si Yesu óplo awla xe ekpese bidɔ mɛ kuŋa a.

english_verse: You Pharisees are in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your gardens, such as mint and rue. But you cheat people, and you don't love God. You should be fair and kind to others and still give a tenth to God.
translation_verse: “Litráze gagla ɛki mlɔ Farisianɛ ma, lɛ ese linu si mlɛzɛki Aya oni bitɔ́dɔ, ni siwa flafla, kapɛ liɔfɔa, pɔ̀ mlapɛ si mlɛxwa etsi gyɔgyɔ, lɛ mlálɔ Aya kyɛ ɛ. Mlegyeni la ya kubitɛ pɔ̀ mlatrayurɔ atɔpu na kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: As crowds were gathering around Jesus, he said: You people of today are evil! You keep looking for a sign from God. But what happened to Jonah is the only sign you will be given.
translation_verse: Agi anɔkpi la ɛkpɛ likpɔ ní Yesu i, atrɛ ní yɛ kunugu yo aʋa si, “Kelea lia mɛ bebi ba ɛ, bɛnyani koŋu! Bɛɛpɛ linugutseboe si bɛɛmɔ ɔ; pɔ̀ bátramɔ litɔtɔ ɛ sani Yona yɛ linugutseboe le e.

english_verse: When the judgment comes, the Queen of the South will stand there with you and condemn you. She traveled a long way to hear Solomon's wisdom, and yet here is something far greater than Solomon.
translation_verse: Ní Litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, Sheba Okusidzɛɛ, aayɔ alɛɛ gi aabu kiwlaʋi kukpɛ mlɔ abla bebi ba aŋu mɛ. Lɛ ese linu si aga adɔ ní yɛ kepea ke, gi aba ata Okusi ye Salomo yɛ kuboeteo kutu u. Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ si, ɔnutɔ gi ekeme asani Salomo ɔlɛ ní ya a.

english_verse: The people of Nineveh will also stand there with you and condemn you. They turned to God when Jonah preached, and yet here is something far greater than Jonah. Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Ní Litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, Ninive banɛ ma kyɛ biayɔ ɔlɛɛ, gi bíabu mlɔ omono bebi ba lípɔ ɔ. Lɛ ese linu si bekyi ɔna mɛ gi benu Yona yɛ liboe le e. Lɛ mɛɛtrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si ɔnutɔ ɔlɛ ní ya gi ekeme asani Yona ɛ. Kimbi ye kinu kani ɔki ɔnɔse lo

english_verse: No one lights a lamp and then hides it or puts it under a clay pot. A lamp is put on a lampstand, so everyone who comes into the house can see the light.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔnɔ ɔʋísu kani xe izɛkɔ yɛ ɛkpɛ agba yɛ ese loo izekpo ye; boŋu i, izɛkɔ yɛ ɛtrɔ ní kani yɛ ose lo aʋa tɔ okle ɔki banama gi beetiku ní kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Your eyes are the lamp for your body. When your eyes are good, you have all the light you need. But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark.
translation_verse: Mlɔ bimbi ʋe binu kani ɔki mlɔ inɔse le. Xegi mlɔ bimbi ʋe bipɛ ɛ, kɔ mlɔ inɔse le ina ɛ. Pɔ xegi mlɔ bimbi ʋe bípɛ ɛ, kɔ mlɔ inɔse le iwɔ ní litumu ne me e.

english_verse: So be sure your light isn't darkness.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mledi le aʋa si kuklekleo kukídzi litumu ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You fools! Didn't God make both the outside and the inside?
translation_verse: Baga! Aní Aya gi abitɛ kedea ɛ, yɛ ke abitɛ kimɛ mɛ kyɛ na a?

english_verse: Yes, you are really in for trouble. You build monuments to honor the prophets your own people murdered long ago.
translation_verse: “Litráze gagla ɛki mlɔ! Mlɛlulɔ Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba ya gi mlɔ bakatsitsi ba beye e, ba idrɔ lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus finished speaking, a Pharisee invited him home for a meal. Jesus went and sat down to eat.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu agù yɛ kupaniɔ ɛ, Farisiɔnɛ ɔtɔ ekpe ye si ɔga aŋa bidɔ ni yɛ ní yɛ kepea mɛ. Lɛ loso Yesu atrɛ, eple eze lɛ ekpese bidɔ mɛ kuŋa ni yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So the Lord said to him: You Pharisees clean the outside of cups and dishes, but on the inside you are greedy and evil.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi kepea ɔkaɛ si, “Mlɔ Farisianɛ ma mlɛzeplo mlɔ kupliʋi yo ni kudɔŋazi yo kɔ kedea; pɔ̀ mlɔ mɛ eyi ni kuŋazi ni litukpo me gagla a.

english_verse: If you would only give what you have to the poor, everything you do would please God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kidiliɛ gi kikpasi ní mlɔ kupliʋi yo ni kuzi yo me e, mlakɔ kɛ aki bepiplinete ba tɔ kidɔ kakaa su kidra ɛki mlɔ ní mlɔ egoʋi la onu.

english_verse: But you are in for trouble! You are like unmarked graves that people walk on without even knowing it.
translation_verse: “Litráze gagla ɛki mlɔ! Mla lɛ ko ŋwisi idrɔ gi bákpɛ lɛ gyesi i, xe banima bize ga lɛ aʋa ɛza ní motemote me kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied: You teachers are also in for trouble! You load people down with heavy burdens, but you won't lift a finger to help them carry the loads.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Litráze gagla ɛki mlɔ kyɛ, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba! Mlɛtrɔ adɔtu zegbezegbe ní banima aʋa gi bátratani kutu i; pɔ̀ mlaʋíkpɛ awla si mlɛpɔni ba tɔ betu la a.

english_verse: You must think that was the right thing for your people to do, or else you would not have built monuments for the prophets they murdered.
translation_verse: Mlɔ kibitɛɛ kibasɛ si mlɛtrɔ ní mlɔ bakatsitsi ba kiboebitɛɛ aʋa a; lɛ ese linu si beye Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba lɛ mlɔ pɔ̀ mlɛba ɛʋɔ ba idrɔ lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Because of your evil deeds, the Wisdom of God said, “I will send prophets and apostles to you. But you will murder some and mistreat others.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e Aya ní yɛ kuboeteo me e, edo aplɛ si, ‘Maatsisi ba beboedoplɛ ba ni baxwɛdzɛ ba; biaye ba mɛ batɔ, kɔ bɛyɔ kedu ní bedzedze ba su i.’

english_verse: You people living today will be punished for all the prophets who have been murdered since the beginning of the world.
translation_verse: Nite si Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba klɔ petee gi beye e, dɔnɔ ní kelea likpese le gakɔna ni abla ɛ, mlɔ abla bebi ba ɛ, biaʋi ba kuwle dɔni mlɔ awla.

english_verse: This includes every prophet from the time of Abel to the time of Zechariah, who was murdered between the altar and the temple. You people will certainly be punished for all of this.
translation_verse: Kuwleo ki banama gi beye e dɔnɔ ní Abel aʋa gakɔna ni Zakaria gi yɛ beye ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ ni lisumugba lɛ kusi yo e. Sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si, biahe kutu ki mlɔ abla kelea bebi ba ní kunɔyeo liɔ klɔ petee litukpo!

english_verse: You teachers of the Law of Moses are really in for trouble! You carry the keys to the door of knowledge about God. But you never go in, and you keep others from going in.
translation_verse: “Litráze gagla ɛki mlɔ ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba! Mletso safi ye ebu ní kuboeteo ligba lɛ opupo lo onu. Mlɔ tutɔ mléʋiti ku lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ mletsi kudeo onu ɛki banama gi bɛɛpɛ si beetiku i.”

english_verse: Jesus was about to leave, but the teachers and the Pharisees wanted to get even with him. They tried to make him say what he thought about other things,
translation_verse: Agi Yesu asɛ níklɔ ɛ, Farisianɛ ma nì Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛyɔ kedu ní yɛ su lɛ beʋi ye beʋiʋi ní eboe glegle su ní kusuyeʋi me

english_verse: so they could catch him saying something wrong.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si bɛpɛ si ɔpani liboe trama ɛtɔ tɔ bɛɛpunu ye evu.

english_verse: As thousands of people crowded around Jesus and were stepping on each other, he told his disciples: Be sure to guard against the dishonest teaching of the Pharisees! It is their way of fooling people.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si banɔ akpe akpe ba bɛkpɛ likpɔ, gi beze ga ní ba ahanuglɔ ba aʋa kyɛ ɛ. Yesu agba yɛ badɔkpasi ba ní ɔtɔpu kɔ si, “Mlakpɛ lixaŋu ní Farisianɛ ma kunyasi yɔ gi kunu banɔ aŋu mɛ kiboebitɛ su u.

english_verse: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. Won't he do even more for you? You have such little faith!
translation_verse: Xegi nite Aya izɛkpɛ kupedi ni siwa sɛ su gi silɛ omono, kivo pɔ̀ siyrɔ, kɔ sitse kɔ bɛkani sɛ ni kifu i. Kitɛ xe ɔ́trakɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki mlɔ ɛsani siwa sɛ ɛ? Kitɛ xe mlɔ kuziŋaa kulɛ sisami te e?

english_verse: But put God's work first, and these things will be yours as well. Treasures in Heaven (Matthew
translation_verse: Boŋu i, mleti yɛ kaʋaŋaa tɔ yɛ pɔ̀ ɛ aakɔ̀ bidɔ bɛ ya ʋa ɔki mlɔ ɔ. Lifunu ne onu exo la ɔbla klɔ

english_verse: My friends, don't be afraid of people. They can kill you, but after that, there is nothing else they can do.
translation_verse: “Mɛ baʋia ba, mɛɛsi mlɔ si, banama gi ɔnɔse lo ko biata ni kuye e, pɔ̀ xe bátratani kididɔ kubitɛ sanɔ ɛ, mlakíse ba.

english_verse: Even the hairs on your head are counted. So don't be afraid! You are worth much more than many sparrows. Telling Others about Christ (Matthew
translation_verse: Mlɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, mlɔ litukpo le siya sɛ kura kyɛ ɛ, bɛkla sɛ petee. Lɛ loso lifu likíku mlo o. Mlɛŋa kedzi isanɔ ayikpakpa glegle. Yesu yɛ kuzɛ́zɛ ni yɛ kunada

english_verse: My little group of disciples, don't be afraid! Your Father wants to give you the kingdom.
translation_verse: “Mlɔ ɔhá sisami nɔ, lifu likíku mlo o. Lɛ ese linu si liku mlɔ Ɔkaɛ aŋu si ɔkɔ Kaʋaŋaa ɔki mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Sell what you have and give the money to the poor. Make yourselves moneybags that never wear out. Make sure your treasure is safe in heaven, where thieves cannot steal it and moths cannot destroy it.
translation_verse: Mlada mlɔ isu ya bidɔ mɛ tɔ mlakɔ kibɔɛ aki bepiplinete ba. Xe mlɛbitɛ nite e, bibɔ ikotoku gi itrapu i, iaze mlɔ awla ní lifunu ne. Lɛ niklɔ ʋa ɛ, mlɔ exo la atráŋini i, lɛ ese linu si oze ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́ma ni iklɔ ɔ, lɛ asu na kyɛ atrázizi bidɔ ɔ.

english_verse: At that time the Holy Spirit will tell you what to say. A Rich Fool
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ aabasɛ mlɔ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ eboa la gi mlapani i.” Abee ní Oxonete Kanukpaʋi ya su

english_verse: But God said to him, “You fool! Tonight you will die. Then who will get what you have stored up?”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ Aya asi yɛ si, “Wɔ Kanukpaʋi! Kele kátrati nì wɔ ɔ! Kɔ bidɔ mɛ gi wɔbla aplɛ ki wɔ isu ya ɛ, nyaŋwɛ binɛ bitradzì i?”

english_verse: If you tell others that you belong to me, the Son of Man will tell God's angels that you are my followers.
translation_verse: “Sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si, banama gi bɛpani ní banima aŋu mɛ si mɛ benu mɛ banɛ ɛ, Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kyɛ aapani ní Aya baxwɛdzɛ ba aŋu mɛ si, yɛ banɛ benu.

english_verse: But if you reject me, you will be rejected in front of them.
translation_verse: Nite ke banama gi bianada me ní banima aŋu mɛ ɛ, mɛ kyɛ maanada si mete ba ní Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: When you are brought to trial in the synagogues or before rulers or officials, don't worry about how you will defend yourselves or what you will say.
translation_verse: “Xegi bɛtsini mlɔ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ ɔ, loo kesea baʋaŋa ba aloo kesea ʋa bedidi ba aŋu mɛ, si baxwa litsi ni mlɔ ɛ, mlakípè ní kiliɛ gi mlaŋwlɛ isu, nì liboe lɛ gi mlaapani su u.

english_verse: A man in a crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, tell my brother to give me my share of what our father left us when he died.”
translation_verse: Onyime ɔtɔ ní ɔnɔha lɔ kusi asi Yesu si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, si mɛ onemi ye tɔ ɔlɛ kaŋasea gi blɔ ɔkaɛ ayurɔ aplɛ aki blɔɛ nì mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Who gave me the right to settle arguments between you and your brother?”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki yɛ si, “Onyime, nyaŋwɛ edu me otsixwa loo ɔdɔlɛ ní mlɔ tiaʋa kusi i?”

english_verse: and he said to himself, “What can I do? I don't have a place large enough to store everything.”
translation_verse: Egyo le edi si, ‘Ɔdɔblakɔ ʋidi ɔma ní mɛ awla a gi maabla mɛ ɔnyrɔɔ nɔ mɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: Later, he said, “Now I know what I'll do. I'll tear down my barns and build bigger ones, where I can store all my grain and other goods.
translation_verse: “Lɛ asi isu ya si, ‘Maayaʋi mɛ idɔblakɔ koko le tɔ maawa ivuvũ gi ikeme sanɔ ɛ. Niklɔ maayasi mɛ idoli le ni kayaa mɛ bidɔ gɔgɔ ʋɛ kukpɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: I tell you not to worry about your life! Don't worry about having something to eat or wear.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Lɛ loso mɛɛsi mlɔ si mlakíkpɛ isu kunugu ní mlɔ ligbã́ nɛ su, kidiliɛ gi mlaaŋa loo ní mlɔ isu ya kidiliɛ gi mlaakpɛ su u.

english_verse: Life is more than food or clothing.
translation_verse: Ligbã nɛ liziã boo esani kiŋaŋaɛ; lɛ ɔnɔse lo kyɛ ɛzia ɛsani kusa a.

english_verse: Look how the wild flowers grow! They don't work hard to make their clothes. But I tell you Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of these flowers.
translation_verse: “Mlevu aŋu ní otukpomeyaʋi lɔ kitsitsi ye su. Ɔʋíwa lixwɛxwɛ, lɛ ɔʋiyó kusa ki isu u. Pɔ̀ mɛsi mlɔ si Okusi ye Salomo ní yɛ kukusi yo ni yɛ kusudraa petee me kyɛ, yɛ bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ bitipɔni lɔ kufufu yo ní kupedi me e.

english_verse: Don't keep worrying about having something to eat or drink.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlakíkpɛ isu kunugu ní kiŋaŋa ni kiŋwɛŋwɛ su u.

english_verse: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father knows what you need.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si babubitɛ ba ɛ, bidɔ bɛ ya petee elu su bizeekyigyi. Pɔ̀ mlɔ Ɔkaɛ etè si biziã mlɔ.

english_verse: But suppose one of the servants thinks that the master won't return until late. Suppose that servant starts beating all the other servants and eats and drinks and gets drunk.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi osumubi liɛ klɔ aasi isu ya si, liadze xe mɛ kepea ɔkaɛ adzi i, xe aakpese besumubi benyime ni badzɛ ba kukusi i, xe ɛɛŋa xe ɛɛŋwɛ emu i;

english_verse: Do you think that I came to bring peace to earth? No indeed! I came to make people choose sides.
translation_verse: Kigyo mlegyo edi si mɛ kubaba ɛ, kwiamani kusuyɔ ní kelea mɛ lo o? Owo, kútramani kusuyɔ pɔ̀ kulɛlɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master finds them awake and ready when he comes! I promise you he will get ready and let his servants sit down so he can serve them.
translation_verse: Biakrira besumubi ba klɔ gi biaze nì aŋu xe ba Ɔkaɛ aadzi i. Mɛɛsi mlɔ si kepea ɔkaɛ aasama yɛ kusaa kɔ aki si yɛ besumubi ba beze kese kɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ esumu ba ɛ.

english_verse: Those servants are really fortunate if their master finds them ready, even though he comes late at night or early in the morning.
translation_verse: Biakrira besumubi ba klɔ gi bɛbla ɛki ba Ɔkaɛ yɛ kubabaa, elesi tetsu loo olezatɔ ɔ.

english_verse: You would surely not let a thief break into your home, if you knew when the thief was coming.
translation_verse: Mlete le si, xegi kepea ɔkaɛ etè lipoe lɛ gi oze ye aaba ɛ, ɔ́traki yɛ kudeo onu si ɔyaʋi opupo lo ní yɛ onu tɔ oti oku ní yɛ aʋa.

english_verse: Peter asked Jesus, “Did you say this just for us or for everyone?”
translation_verse: Petro eʋi si, “Blɔ Ɔkaɛ, abee liɛ gi wobu i, blɔ wobu ye ɔkɔ putɔ wobu ye ɔki ɔnɔ kakaa na a?”

english_verse: The Lord answered: Who are faithful and wise servants? Who are the ones the master will put in charge of giving the other servants their food supplies at the proper time?
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ ebu le onu si, “Osumubi woli onu ɔgbã ɛ nì oboete e?” Yɛ onu iliɛ gi yɛ kepea ɔkaɛ aakɔ̀ kepea akpɛ awla akɔ ɔ, gi yɛ aaki besumubi dzedze ba ba kiŋaŋaɛ ní lipoe gbã nɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: Servants are fortunate if their master comes and finds them doing their job.
translation_verse: Biakrira osumubi liɛ klɔ, xegi yɛ kepea ɔkaɛ aaba klani yɛ onu gi ɛɛwa nite me axwɛ na ɛ.

english_verse: A servant who is always faithful will surely be put in charge of everything the master owns.
translation_verse: Sugba ɛ, kepea ɔkaɛ aakɔ̀ yɛ exo la petee akɔ kpɛ osumubi liɛ klɔ awla si odi be aʋa.

english_verse: If that happens, the master will come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him. That servant will then be punished and thrown out with the servants who cannot be trusted.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe kepea ɔkaɛ aadzi ní lipoɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ gi osumubi ye ógyo odi, ni liwe lɛ aʋa gi ɔ́plɛ aŋu i, Yɛ, kepea ɔkaɛ aafli ye kukpe kukpe aakɔ̀ yɛ ɔkpɛ batu gagla benete ba kusi i.

english_verse: If servants are not ready or willing to do what their master wants them to do, they will be beaten hard.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, osumubi liɛ klɔ gi ete yɛkaɛ yɛ kupɛpɛɔ, pɔ̀ xe ɔ́blaa ɔki kɔ aʋa kubitɛ ɛ, biafiaʋi yɛ biseʋi glegle ŋwisi kutuhehe kinɛ.

english_verse: But servants who don't know what their master wants them to do will not be beaten so hard for doing wrong. If God has been generous with you, he will expect you to serve him well. But if he has been more than generous, he will expect you to serve him even better. Not Peace, but Trouble (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ osumubi liɛ klɔ gi óte yɛkaɛ kupɛpɛɔ xe ata kese e, biahe kutu ki yɛ taʋɛ. Ɔniliɛ gi bɛki bidɔ glegle e, biaʋi bidɔ glegle dɔni yɛ iwla mɛ. Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi bɛki bidɔ ɛsanɔ ɛ, biaʋi bidɔ glegle sanɔ dɔni yɛ awla. Yesu amani kulɛlɛ ɛ

english_verse: I came to set fire to the earth, and I wish it were already on fire!
translation_verse: Maba si míkpɛ kifu kesea aʋa, gi nya mapɛ si kikpese kusɔ kooko xe e.

english_verse: I am going to be put to a hard test. And I will have to suffer a lot of pain until it is over.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lilɛ si miga baxixa ba mɛ, ŋwisi kuni kpɛkpɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ maxixa isu ya mɛ kɔkpɛ ni lipoe lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: A family of five will be divided, with two of them against the other three.
translation_verse: Dɔnɔ abla kɔtsinɔ ɛ, ní banɔ tietsu ní kepe me e, ba mɛ tiata biayɔ kedu ní tiaʋa su u, lɛ tiaʋa kyɛ biayɔ kedu ní tiata su u.

english_verse: Jesus said to all the people: As soon as you see a cloud coming up in the west, you say, “It's going to rain,” and it does.
translation_verse: Yesu asi banima si, “Xegi mlɛmɔ aplɛ la gi ɛdɔ ní keweku ya ɛ, kɔ mlɔsi si dzeni ye atrádɔ ɔ; lɛ izɛdɔ kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When the south wind blows, you say, “It's going to get hot,” and it does.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi liwɔ lɛ lifu idɔnɔ ni kalea ɔza lɔ ɛ; kɔ mlɛsi si kana mɛ kɛtraze kpikpi, gi lizɛba lɛ mɛ te kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Are you trying to fool someone? You can predict the weather by looking at the earth and sky, but you don't really know what's going on right now.
translation_verse: Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ boe bitɛ ba! Xegi mlɛtani kesea ni liwɔ lɛ mɛ kizezee kudi i, gi mlɔ kiboedoe kizɛba kɛ mɛ ɛ, ege lɛ loso xe mlatrátani abla eboe la ese kunu u?” Kusi kululɔlulɔ

english_verse: Why don't you understand the right thing to do?
translation_verse: “Ege lɛ loso mlatrátrɛ mlɔ isu ya mɛ edi xe mlate kidiliɛ gi mlabitɛ ɛ?

english_verse: When someone accuses you of something, try to settle things before you are taken to court. If you don't, you will be dragged before the judge. Then the judge will hand you over to the jailer, and you will be locked up.
translation_verse: Xegi ɔnutɔ akɔ liboe ní wɔ su u, xe atsini wɔ ní otsixwakɔ ɛ, kenidi tɔ mlalulɔ liboe le ní mlɔ kusi xe ableke mlaaku otsixwaklɔ ɔ. Xe aníte e, aatsini wɔ ní otsixwa yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ, kɔ yɛ kyɛ akɔ wɔ akpɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa odidi ye awla ɛ, kɔ bɛkpɛ wɔ ligba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You won't get out until you have paid the last cent you owe.
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, níklɔ wɔaze ga kɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi wɔtsa liʋɛ kakaa a, gi bɛnya ɛki wɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: About this same time Jesus was told that Pilate had given orders for some people from Galilee to be killed while they were offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, banɔ batɔ beze ní iklɔ gi bɛsi Yesu kiliɛ gi Pilato eye Galileanɛ atɔ ɔ, xe apisa ba ika lɛ ni bidɔ mɛ ɛ gi bɛzɛpɔʋi ɛki Aya a.

english_verse: Jesus replied: Do you think that these people were worse sinners than everyone else in Galilee just because of what happened to them?
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si “Kigyo mlegyo edi si Galileanɛ baklɔ gi beye te e, ba bɛsa eboe zimizi na kubitɛ sani Galileanɛ gɔgɔ ba na a?

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also be killed.
translation_verse: Aníte lilɛ ɛ! Lɛ loso mɛɛsi mlɔ si, xegi mlékyi ní mlɔ eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, mlɔ pɔ́ mlaatse e!

english_verse: Jesus said, “What is God's kingdom like? What can I compare it with?
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eʋi si, “Kitɛ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kalɛ ɛ? Lɛ ege maakɔ ka kusrɔnɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: Not at all! But you can be sure that if you don't turn back to God, every one of you will also die. A Story about a Fig Tree
translation_verse: Kura! Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ si xegi mlékyi ní mlɔ eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, mlɔ pɔ́ mlaatse e.” Abee ní mangɔ gi óbu libiitɔtɔ su u

english_verse: Jesus then told them this story: A man had a fig tree growing in his vineyard. One day he went out to pick some figs, but he didn't find any.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu abee liɛ aki ba si: Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi akpɛ mangɔ ni yɛ wain ɔnyrɔ mɛ ɛ, Adzɛ si yigu esebi ní mangɔ yɛ mɛ, pɔ ɔ́mɔ litɔtɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The gardener answered, “Master, leave it for another year. I'll dig around it and put some manure on it to make it grow.
translation_verse: Pɔ ɔnyrɔ nɔ ɔʋadi ye asi yɛ si, “ ‘Ɔka,’ ‘yurɔ lɔ ligɔ tile dzɛ ɛ. Maatu lɔ su tɔ maakpɛ biŋaŋa lɔ su.

english_verse: Maybe it will have figs on it next year. If it doesn't, you can have it cut down.” Healing a Woman on the Sabbath
translation_verse: Xegi eyo galigɔ ɛ, liapɛ; pɔ xe aníte e, wɔatani kuki si biatsa lɔ fãa.’ ” Yesu ayɔni ɔdzɛ bafa ɔtɔ ní Sabat liwe le aʋa

english_verse: One Sabbath, Jesus was teaching in a synagogue,
translation_verse: Ní Sabat liwe etɔ aʋa ɛ, Yesu azɛɛbasɛ bidɔ ní Yudanɛ ma Ɔtsaklɔ.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the woman, he called her over and said, “You are now well.”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu amɔ yɛ ɛ, axwa yɛ atsisi lɛ asi yɛ si, “Ɔdzɛ, wɔ kunyaa, kwiyurɔ wɔ!”

english_verse: The Lord replied, “Are you trying to fool someone? Won't any one of you untie your ox or donkey and lead it out to drink on a Sabbath?
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ azɛ lɛ yɛ onu si, “Mlɔ banɔ aŋu mɛ boebitɛ ba,! Mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa izebu yɛ tedzi ye loo ligume nyime ne edono ní kuka mɛ gi izɛgbani bɛ zɛki bɛ kuni ní Sabat liwe le aʋa a.

english_verse: Jesus' words made his enemies ashamed. But everyone else in the crowd was happy about the wonderful things he was doing. A Mustard Seed and Yeast (Matthew
translation_verse: Kuye kwiku yɛ ahwafianɛ ma petee pɔ̀ banima kɔ ɛ, isami iku ba ní enugutseboe ala petee gi Yesu azaabitɛ su u. Abee ní lisebi sisami nɛ su

english_verse: At that time some Pharisees came to Jesus and said, “You had better get away from here, because Herod wants to kill you!”
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɖii, Farisianɛ batɔ bɛba Yesu klɔ lɛ bɛsi ye si, “Sɛ ní niya tɔ trɛ ní ɔkɔ dzedze lɛ ese linu si Herodes ɛɛpɛ si yiye wo.”

english_verse: But I am going on my way today and tomorrow and the next day. After all, Jerusalem is the place where prophets are killed. Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ linu si mize kudeo me omono, kivo ni kivodé. Líma si beye oboedoplɛ ní ɔkɔkɔ sani Yerusalem.

english_verse: There the ones who are now least important will be the most important, and those who are now most important will be least important. Jesus and Herod
translation_verse: Nite me si banama gi ba benu banɔ gɔgɔ ba abla ɛ, ba biaze banɔ tɔpu ba, lɛ banama gi benu banɔ tɔpu abla ɛ, ba biaaze banɔ gɔgɔ ba.” Yesu alɔ Yerusalem

english_verse: Jesus said to them: Go tell that fox, “I am going to force out demons and heal people today and tomorrow, and three days later I'll be through.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlatrɛ tɔ mlazɛsi wayu liɛ klɔ si, ‘Matrakpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne e dono, lɛ maayɔni banyanya ba kyɛ omono ni kivo o. Liwetanɛ we le e, maagù mɛ axwɛ na.’

english_verse: It is like what happens when someone plants a mustard seed in a garden. The seed grows as big as a tree, and birds nest in its branches.”
translation_verse: Kaʋaŋaa kalɛ ŋwisi lisebi sisami gi onyime ɔtɔ akpɛ ní yɛ kayaa mɛ kinɛ ɛ. Lisebi le litsi gi lidzi ose ʋidi ɔtɔ gi kudzɔʋi yɔ kwiwa evu na ɛkpɛ ní lɔ ayaa la mɛ ɛ. Abee ní kunyasi su

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “What can I compare God's kingdom with?
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi dzɛ si, ege maakɔ Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa srɔnɔ ɛ?

english_verse: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he taught the people in the towns and villages.
translation_verse: Yesu aga aklanɔ ní ima ni kumami yɔ mɛ azaabasɛ bidɔ gi azaatrɛ ní Yerusalem i.

english_verse: Do all you can to go in by the narrow door! A lot of people will try to get in, but will not be able to.
translation_verse: Mlekeni edi tɔ mleti iku gani kevunu pɔpɔa mɛ; lɛ ese linu si meetrɔ likla lɛ aʋa ɛki mlɔ si; banɔ glegle bɛtráŋwlɛ si beti eku pɔ̀ batrátanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Once the owner of the house gets up and locks the door, you will be left standing outside. You will knock on the door and say, “Sir, open the door for us!” But the owner will answer, “I don't know a thing about you!”
translation_verse: Xegi kepea ɔkaɛ ayɔ xe ada opupo lo e, xe mlalɛ ni oŋwe no o, xe mlɛsa opupo lo o si, “Ɔkaɛ, Kwiki wɔ litukpo o, dra opupo lo ki blɔ ɛ!” Pɔ̀ aasi mlɔ si, “Méte mlo loo niklɔ gi mlɛdɔ ɔ!”

english_verse: Then you will start saying, “We dined with you, and you taught in our streets.”
translation_verse: Niklɔ mlɔ kyɛ mlaabù isu ya onu si, “Blɔ ni wɔ kwiŋa lɛ kwiŋwɛ kyɛ ɛ, lɛ wɔbasɛ bidɔ ní blɔ ima nɛ ablɔ ʋa aʋa a!”

english_verse: But he will say, “I really don't know who you are! Get away from me, you evil people!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ aasi mlɔ dzɛ si, “Méte mlo loo niklɔ gi mlɛdɔ ɔ! Mlasɛ ní mɛ su, mlɔ litukpo me gagla benete ba.

english_verse: Then when you have been thrown outside, you will weep and grit your teeth because you will see Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and all the prophets in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Mlaabemi gi mlata ene na a, xegi mlɛmɔ Abraham, Isak, Yakob ani beboedoplɛ ba petee ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe bɛŋwia mlɔ xunyɔ ɛkpɛ ni oŋwe no e.

english_verse: People will come from all directions and sit down to feast in God's kingdom.
translation_verse: Banɔ biadɔ ní kewetu ya, keweku ya, kaʋa ni kale gi biaŋa lɛ biaŋwɛ bidɔ ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jerusalem, Jerusalem! Your people have killed the prophets and have stoned the messengers who were sent to you. I have often wanted to gather your people, as a hen gathers her chicks under her wings. But you wouldn't let me.
translation_verse: “Wɔ Yerusalem, Woye Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba, lɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba gi Aya atsisi i, wɔsa ba akla na. Tasɛ mapɛ si mikpɛ wɔ bebi ba likpɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔkukɔnee izewe yɛ bebi ba kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ wɔ́ki mɛ kudeo onu u!

english_verse: Now your temple will be deserted. You won't see me again until the time when you say, “Blessed is the one who comes in the name of the Lord.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, wɔ lisumugba lɛ lilɛ kiwɔ pluplu. Sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si, mlatrámɔ mɛ tɔɔ kɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi mlaakpɛ osayee lo si, ‘Aya ɔkrira ɔniliɛ gi atrába ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me e!’ ”

english_verse: All of a sudden a man with swollen legs stood up in front of him.
translation_verse: Gi si yidi i, onyime ɔtɔ gi yɛ ikli ni iwla iye e, aba edo ní yɛ aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: Jesus turned and asked the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses, “Is it right to heal on the Sabbath?”
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ni Farisianɛ ma si, “Blɔ Ɔsã nɔ ɛki kude si bayɔni ɔnyanya ní Sabat liwe le aʋa loo kitɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: But they did not say a word. Jesus took hold of the man. Then he healed him and sent him away.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banima bapani i. Lɛ Yesu ebu onyimee kuka a, ayɔni yɛ, lɛ akpɛ yɛ kude e.

english_verse: There was nothing they could say. How To Be a Guest
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bátani liboe lɛ ya onu kubu kí yɛ ɛ. Kudɔbasɛ ní Kusububɔ ni Kunɔdimɛnɔ su

english_verse: Jesus saw how the guests had tried to take the best seats. So he told them:
translation_verse: Gi Yesu akpɛ lɛ lixaŋu kiliɛ gi banɔkpekpe ba bɛzɛpɛ izekɔ gyɔgyɔ lɛ ki isu ya ɛ, Yesu ebu abee liɛ aki ba petee si,

english_verse: When you are invited to a wedding feast, don't sit in the best place. Someone more important may have been invited.
translation_verse: Xegi ɔnutɔ axwa wɔ amanɔ ní ɔdzɛ ɔgbanɔkplɔ su i, wukíze ní ozekɔ gyɔgyɔ lɔ ɔ; lɛ ese linu si wóte xe bekpe ɔnɔ gbɔgbɔ sani wɔ amanɔ ní ɔdɔŋakplɔ nɔ su u.

english_verse: If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔ kakaa gi emuni isu ya ɛ, biaple ni ye, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi epleni isu ya ɛ, biamuni ye.

english_verse: Then Jesus said to the man who had invited him: When you give a dinner or a banquet, don't invite your friends and family and relatives and rich neighbors. If you do, they will invite you in return, and you will be paid back.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi kepea ɔkaɛ si, xegi wɔ si wutrɔ ɔkplɔ ɛ, kíxwa wɔ baʋia nɛ, wɔ benemi, wɔ baŋanɛ loo banama gi ɔwla mɛ ɔlɛ ba a; lɛ ese linu si ba kyɛ ɛ, biadzini wɔ kuxwa gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, kɔ bɛtsa wɔ kuxwaxwaa liʋɛ nɛ ɛki wɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When the servant returned, he said, “Master, I've done what you told me, and there is still plenty of room for more people.”
translation_verse: “Gi liŋwe ne si lɛdɔ ɛ, ɔnɔsumu ye edzi aba lɛ asi yɛ kepea ɔkaɛ ɛ si, ‘Lixwɛ nɛ gi wɔkpɛ mɛ ɛ, mawa lɛ. Pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, izekɔ lɛ iʋíblo o.’

english_verse: After Jesus had finished speaking, one of the guests said, “The greatest blessing of all is to be at the banquet in God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi banama gi beze ní ɔkplɔ nɔ su ni Yesu ba mɛ tole enu liboe le e, asi yɛ si, “Banama gi biaŋa bidɔ ní ɔdɔŋakplɔ ʋidi lo su ni lifunu ne kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, isami koŋ bɛtra mɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus told him: A man once gave a great banquet and invited a lot of guests.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Onyime ɔtɔ atrɔ ɔkplɔ ʋidi ɔtɔ, lɛ axwa banɔ gbí si bagaŋa bidɔ ni yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When the banquet was ready, he sent a servant to tell the guests, “Everything is ready! Please come.”
translation_verse: Agi bidɔ mɛ liŋapoe le lina ɛ, akpɛ yɛ ɔnɔsumu ye si ɔzɛsi banɔxwaxwa ba si, ‘Mlaga ba, kidɔ kakaa kiflɔ.’

english_verse: One guest after another started making excuses. The first one said, “I bought some land, and I've got to look it over. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ ba petee bekpese ba baxixa ʋa kubu basɛ tole tole e. Ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ asi ɔnɔsumu ye si, ‘Medzi kese katɔ gi lilɛ si mizɛɛtapo kamɛ odi i, lɛ loso e, zi mɛ kuxixaa ki mɛ.’

english_verse: Another guest said, “I bought five teams of oxen, and I need to try them out. Please excuse me.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ dzedze kyɛ si, ‘Medzi egume nyime liɔfɔ tɔ míkɔ ɛsɔ mɛ ɔnyrɔ mɛ, nite si kudeo me malɛ te gi matrɛsɔ ni la edi i, lɛ loso mátratani ku ba a.’

english_verse: The servant told his master what happened, and the master became so angry he said, “Go as fast as you can to every street and alley in town! Bring in everyone who is poor or paralyzed or blind or lame.”
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔsumu ye azɛɛki yɛ ɔkaɛ kunutsatsaa. Ɔkaɛ amɔ kusuyeʋi gagla lɛ asi yɛ ɔnɔsumu ye si, ‘Kɔsi tɔ trɛ ní ɔma nɔ ablɔ ʋa aʋa ni igbade ne me, tɔ zɛɛxwa bepiplinete ba, bafa ba, kuŋupoo ni banama gi kapɛ tiele ketse ki ba ɛ manɔ.’

english_verse: His master then told him, “Go out along the back roads and make people come in, so my house will be full.
translation_verse: Nite si ɔkaɛ asi ɔnɔsumu ye si, ‘Do trɛ ní inyrɔ nɛ bede ʋidi ʋa aʋa ni ikli lɛ bede ʋa me tɔ xwa ɔnɔ kakaa gi wɔamɔ manɔ ɔ, tɔ mɛ kepea keyi.

english_verse: Not one of the guests I first invited will get even a bite of my food!” Being a Disciple (Matthew
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, banama petee gi bɛxwa ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, ba mɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́trakpɛ mɛ ɔkplɔ nɔ aʋa kididɔ ní onugu me e.’ ” Ɔdɔkpasi kuzeze, kúnu kidɔ plɔplɔ ɔ

english_verse: Large crowds were walking along with Jesus, when he turned and said:
translation_verse: Liwe etɔ lɛ gi Yesu ni linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ beze ní kudeo aʋa ɛ, ekyi lɛ asi ba si,

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple, unless you love me more than you love your father and mother, your wife and children, and your brothers and sisters. You cannot follow me unless you love me more than you love your own life.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔ kakaa gi aba mɛ klɔ ɛ, ɔ́tratani mɛ ɔdɔkpasi kudzi xegi ɔ́lɔ mɛ sani ɔka loo one e, odze loo bebi, beneminyime loo benemidzɛ, loo yɛ tutɔ yɛ isu ya kyɛ.

english_verse: You cannot be my disciple unless you carry your own cross and follow me.
translation_verse: Banama gi bátratani ba tutɔ ba isetsase le kutu u, xe biakuni mɛ kude e, bátratani mɛ badɔkpasi kuze e.

english_verse: Suppose one of you wants to build a tower. What is the first thing you will do? Won't you sit down and figure out how much it will cost and if you have enough money to pay for it?
translation_verse: “Xegi mlɔ mɛ tole ɔtɔ atrɔ si yiwa lifunu ligba a, liziã si oze kese ní ɔtɔpu kɔ, tɔ obu akɔnta atɔ odi si tiasɛ ligba lɛ liazɛ ɛ, lɛ kibɔ kisrɛ ní yɛ awla gi aatani ligba lɛ kuwa gù na a.

english_verse: Otherwise, you will start building the tower, but not be able to finish. Then everyone who sees what is happening will laugh at you.
translation_verse: Xegi óbu ɔkliɔ lɔ klɔ ɛ, ɔ́tratani ligba lɛ kuwa gù u, xe akpɛ ɔxɔ lɔ pɔ ɛ. Nite si banama petee gi biamɔ yɛ kibitɛ ɛ, biamwɛ yɛ.

english_verse: They will say, “You started building, but could not finish the job.”
translation_verse: Kɔ bɛpani si, ‘Onyime liɛ ekpese ligba lɛ kuwa pɔ̀ ɔ́tani lɛ kugù u.’

english_verse: If he thinks he won't be able to defend himself, he will send messengers and ask for peace while the other king is still a long way off.
translation_verse: Xegi ete si yɛ ɔ́tratanɔ ɛ, kɔ akpɛ banɔ ní okusi liɛ klɔ kɔ lipoe lɛ gi ba kusi kwidze e, tɔ beʋi kusuyɔ.

english_verse: So then, you cannot be my disciple unless you give away everything you own. Salt and Light (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu agu yɛ liboe le si, Nite ke mɛɛsi mlɔ si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani mɛ ɔdɔkpasi kuze e sanɔ si ayurɔ kidɔ kakaa gi kilɛ yɛ awla ɛ.” Kùmwɛ gi kupɛ ɛ

english_verse: Salt is good, but if it no longer tastes like salt, how can it be made to taste salty again?
translation_verse: “Kùmwɛ kuzeze ɔgyi, pɔ̀ xegi ɔgyi nɔ eblo ní kɔ mɛ ɛ, bátratani ɔgyi kukpɛ ní kɔ mɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: It is no longer good for the soil or even for the manure pile. People simply throw it out. If you have ears, pay attention!
translation_verse: Kúpɛ ɛki kididɔ tɔ ɔ, kiŋwia ko biaŋwia kɔ oʋe. Xegi kutu kuli mlɔ ɛ, tɔ mlenu.”

english_verse: I will go to my father and say to him, ‘Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you.
translation_verse: Maayɔ, maatrɛ ní mɔka klɔ lɛ maasi yɛ si, Mɔka matá Aya ni wɔ ese.

english_verse: The younger son said to his father, “Give me my share of the property.” So the father divided his property between his two sons.
translation_verse: Obinuvɔɛ asi yɛ ka si, ‘Ɔka, kɔ mɛ ɔŋa klɔ kí mɛ.’ Lɛ alɛ yɛ bidɔ mɛ aki ba tiaʋa ɛ.

english_verse: He had spent everything, when a bad famine spread through that whole land. Soon he had nothing to eat.
translation_verse: Gi ezizi yɛ bidɔ mɛ petee e, kuʋɛ gagla kutɔ kwidɔ ní kesea lia klɔ aʋa, nite si eku ɔzia mɛ gi kididɔ kigɔ yɛ awla tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Tax collectors and sinners were all crowding around to listen to Jesus.
translation_verse: Liwetɔ lɛ gi babɔgyɔ glegle atɔ ni banɔ zimizi ba beti ipɔni Yesu si beenu yɛ eboe la ɛ,

english_verse: So the Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses started grumbling, “This man is friendly with sinners. He even eats with them.”
translation_verse: Farisianɛ ma ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛzɛɛta ànyà na si, “Onyime liɛ ya ɛ, ɛɛzi banɔ zimizi ba lɛ izɛɛŋa bidɔ ni ba kyɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Then Jesus told them this story:
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu abee liɛ akí ba si;

english_verse: And when you find it, you will be so glad that you will put it on your shoulder
translation_verse: Lɛ xe amɔ lɔ ɛ, aafeke lo trɔ ní yɛ aŋwladɔ na aʋa nì isami

english_verse: and carry it home. Then you will call in your friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found my lost sheep.”
translation_verse: xe akɔ lɔ amani kepea mɛ ɛ. Kɔ axwa yɛ baʋia nɛ ba ni yɛ axolika ba kpɛ likpɔ kɔ asi ba si, “Mlamɔ isami ni mɛ; mamɔ mɛ oʋesi ɔlɔ gi ɛlila na a.”

english_verse: Jesus told the people another story: What will a woman do if she has ten silver coins and loses one of them? Won't she light a lamp, sweep the floor, and look carefully until she finds it?
translation_verse: Loo ɔdzɛ gi bibɔ pititi liɔfɔ bilɛ ní yɛ awla a, xe tile kilila kɛ su i, ɔ́trasu kani i, xe aagba yɛ kepea mɛ tɔ yipɛ kibɔɛ tsiitsiitsii kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi aamɔ kɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Then she will call in her friends and neighbors and say, “Let's celebrate! I've found the coin I lost.”
translation_verse: Lɛ xe amɔ kɛ ɛ, aaxwa yɛ baʋia nɛ ba ni yɛ axolika ba kpɛ likpɔ xe aasi ba si, ‘Mlamɔ isami ni mɛ; Mamɔ mɛ kibɔɛ liɛ gi kilila ɛ.’

english_verse: Jesus said, “In the same way God's angels are happy when even one person turns to him.” Two Sons
translation_verse: Nite me ké mɛɛsi mlɔ si, Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba biamɔ isami glegle kɔkpɛ ɔnɔ zimizi tole gi ekyi yɛ ɔna mɛ loso o. Abee Ní Obinyime gi alila su u

english_verse: Jesus told them yet another story: Once a man had two sons.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní lɛ aʋa si, “Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi bebinyime tiaʋa beze ní yɛ awla a.

english_verse: Not long after that, the younger son packed up everything he owned and left for a foreign country, where he wasted all his money in wild living.
translation_verse: “Ewe klakla atɔ kede e, ɔnuvɔɛ ada yɛ bidɔ mɛ lɛ akɔ kibɔɛ xe ekú kude me e. Atrɛ ní kese dzedze katɔ aʋa gi niklɔ azeezizi yɛ kibɔɛ gani ligbãŋaŋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He went to work for a man in that country, and the man sent him out to take care of his pigs.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, akɔ isu ya apɔni kesea klɔ aʋa ɔnɔ gi aki yɛ axwɛ ní yɛ ɔnyrɔ mɛ si ozeedi yɛ iha lɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: He would have been glad to eat what the pigs were eating, but no one gave him a thing.
translation_verse: Nya azɛapɛ si yiŋa iha lɛ kiŋaŋaɛ, pɔ́ ɔnɔnɔ ɔki yɛ kitɔtɔ si ɔŋa a.

english_verse: Finally, he came to his senses and said, “My father's workers have plenty to eat, and here I am, starving to death!
translation_verse: “Gɔgɔɛ enyime lɛ asi isu ya si, ‘Mɔka yɛ baxwɛwa ba pɔ́ ɛ, ba kiŋaŋaɛ kisrɛ sani ba kuŋaŋa, lɛ mɛ pɔ̀ kuʋɛ kuha mɛ kuye ni ya a!

english_verse: I am no longer good enough to be called your son. Treat me like one of your workers.’ ”
translation_verse: Mégyeno si wɔaxwa mɛ si wobi tɔ ɔ, bitɛ mɛ ŋwisi wɔ baxwɛwa ba mɛ tole kinɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: The younger son got up and started back to his father. But when he was still a long way off, his father saw him and felt sorry for him. He ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e ayɔ ekú kudeo me gi ɛɛtrɛ ní yɛka klɔɛ ɛ. “Pɔ̀ gi eze ní ɔdzage no e, yɛka amɔ yɛ atsisi; yɛ ɔna nɔ ekyi evu ye, lɛ ese e xe azɛɛkpɛ awla ni yɛ ole lo o, xe amu yɛ adzɔ la ɛ.

english_verse: The son said, “Father, I have sinned against God in heaven and against you. I am no longer good enough to be called your son.”
translation_verse: Lɛ obi ye apani si, ‘Mɔka matá Aya ni wɔ ese. Mégyeno si wɔaxwa mɛ si wobi tɔ ɔ.’

english_verse: But his father said to the servants, “Hurry and bring the best clothes and put them on him. Give him a ring for his finger and sandals for his feet.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ yɛ ka axwa yɛ banɔsumu ba lɛ asi ba si, ‘Mlakɔ si, tɔ mlaakɔ liwu gblamadza gbã nɛ petee amanɔ tɔ mlakɔ lɛ akpɛ aki yɛ. Mlakpɛ asukpɛɛ ní yɛ kíwlaʋi yɛ tɔ mlakpɛ afɔkpa ʋa yɛ kuklikpɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Get the best calf and prepare it, so we can eat and celebrate.
translation_verse: Atɔ mlazevu ligume obi liɛ gi amɔ isu ya mɛ ɛ, mleye ye tɔ kumɔ isami.

english_verse: This son of mine was dead, but has now come back to life. He was lost and has now been found.” And they began to celebrate.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si lize ŋwisi mobinyime liɛ etse e, pɔ̀ abla ɛ, ɔlɛ ligbã́; alila, pɔ̀ abla ɛ, bɛmɔ yɛ. Lɛ bekpese isami nɛ kumɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The older son had been out in the field. But when he came near the house, he heard the music and dancing.
translation_verse: “Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, yebinyime tsitsi ye eze ní ɔnyrɔ mɛ. Gi atrédzi i xe eti apɔni kepea mɛ ɛ, enu idzi ni evu kuyoyo.

english_verse: So he called one of the servants over and asked, “What's going on here?”
translation_verse: Lɛ axwa banɔsumu ba mɛ tole lɛ eʋi ye si, ‘liboe woli liitrɛ lɛ aʋa a?’

english_verse: The servant answered, “Your brother has come home safe and sound, and your father ordered us to kill the best calf.”
translation_verse: Ebu le onu akí yɛ si, ‘Wonemi ye yɛ edzi aba kepea, lɛ wɔka eye ligume obi liɛ gi eku kumu u, lɛ ese linu si amɔ Yebi ye gi alila a xe edzi aba ni isu mɛ pɛ̀pɛ̀ mɛ.’

english_verse: The older brother got so angry that he would not even go into the house. His father came out and begged him to go in.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ obinyime tsitsi ye eyeʋi isu ya gbɛgbɛ si ɔdimɛ si yitiku kepea mɛ ɛ; lɛ yɛka aba a xe aki yɛ litukpo si oti oku kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But he said to his father, “For years I have worked for you like a slave and have always obeyed you. But you have never even given me a little goat, so that I could give a dinner for my friends.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ebu le onu akí yɛka si, ‘Dí, mɔka agɔ ala ya petee gi mawa axwɛ na aki wɔ ŋwisi ɔnɔdzidzi kinɛ ɛ, xe máta wɔ isã nɛ ese edi i. Ege wɔki mɛ ɛ? Ɔmu tete fɛ ɛ wɔ́ki mɛ edi tɔ mɛ ni mɛ baʋia nɛ ba kwiakɔ mɔ isami i!

english_verse: This other son of yours wasted your money on prostitutes. And now that he has come home, you ordered the best calf to be killed for a feast.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ wobi liɛ ya ɛ, azeezizi wɔ bidɔ mɛ pɔ́ nì bawawadze ba pɔ̀, gi edzi aba kepea ɛ, woye ligume obi gi liku kumu aki yɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: His father replied, “My son, you are always with me, and everything I have is yours.
translation_verse: Yɛka ebu le onu si, ‘Mobi ye, wɔlɛ ní mɛ sú lipoe kakaa, lɛ kidɔ kakaa gi kinu mɛ kinɛ ɛ, wɔ kínɛ kinu.

english_verse: But we should be glad and celebrate! Your brother was dead, but he is now alive. He was lost and has now been found.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lilɛ si kuŋa liwe tɔ kumɔ isami, lɛ ese linu si lize si wonemi ye etse, pɔ̀ abla ɛ ɔlɛ ligbã́; lize si alila, pɔ abla ɛ bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ.’ ”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: A rich man once had a manager to take care of his business. But he was told that his manager was wasting money.
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Oxonete ɔtɔ eze gi ɔdɔaʋadi eze yɛ awla a. Pɔ̀ bɛsi yɛ si yɛ ɔdɔaʋadi ye e azeezizi yɛ kibɔɛ.

english_verse: So the rich man called him in and said, “What is this I hear about you? Tell me what you have done! You are no longer going to work for me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso axwa yɛ lɛ asi yɛ si, ‘Liboe woli lɛ ya gi meenu ní wɔsu u? Bu akɔnta kí mɛ ní kiliɛ gi wɔɛkɔ ɛga ní mɛ bidɔ mɛ su u, lɛ ese linu si wɔtratani mɛ bidɔ aʋa kudi tɔ ɔ.’

english_verse: The manager said to himself, “What shall I do now that my master is going to fire me? I can't dig ditches, and I'm ashamed to beg.
translation_verse: “Ɔdɔaʋadi ye asi isu ya si, ‘Mɛ ɔkaɛ atrá kpikpini mɛ ní mɛ lixwɛ nɛ mɛ. Ege maabitɛ abla ɛ? Kigagla kíma ní mɛ mɛ̀ gi maakɔ wa axwɛ gagla a, lɛ kuye kwiaku me si miʋi bidɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I know what I'll do, so that people will welcome me into their homes after I've lost my job.”
translation_verse: Mete kidiliɛ gi maabitɛ ɛ! Tɔ xe bɛyurɔ mɛ ní axwɛ na mɛ fɛ ɛ, Maamɔ baʋianɛ gi biazɛ mɛ kpɛ ní ba kupeo me e.

english_verse: “A hundred barrels of olive oil,” the man answered. So the manager said, “Take your bill and sit down and quickly write ‘
translation_verse: “Ebu le onu si, ‘Olif ye kumu ŋaŋa gago alafa tole.’ “Lɛ ɔdɔaʋadi ye asi yɛ si, ‘Zi wɔ liʋɛ nɛ keplekpaa, ze kesea tɔ ŋwlimi avi tetsu.’

english_verse: The manager asked someone else who was in debt to his master, “How much do you owe?” “A thousand sacks of wheat,” the man replied. The manager said, “Take your bill and write ‘
translation_verse: “Lɛ eʋi ɔnɔ ʋla yɛ si, ‘Wɔ pɔ tiasɛ liʋɛ wɔdɔ ɔ? “Ebu le onu si ‘Kimimi ikotoku akpe tole.’ Lɛ ɔdɔaʋadi ye asi yɛ si, ‘Zi wɔ́ liʋɛ nɛ keplekpea tɔ ŋwlimi alafa ɔgɔtiaʋa.’

english_verse: The master praised his dishonest manager for looking out for himself so well. That's how it is! The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light.
translation_verse: “Ɔkaɛ akpɔ ɔdɔaʋadi nyani nyani liɛ si, ebu ɔdaye ɔkli lɔ ya; lɛ ese linu si kelea lia ya mɛ banima bete liboe ní kiliɛ gi biaga ní ɔɖaye lo me ni ba hanuglɔ ba, sani banama gi benu kuklekle me banɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: My disciples, I tell you to use wicked wealth to make friends for yourselves. Then when it is gone, you will be welcomed into an eternal home.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ ní lɛ aʋa si, “Lɛ loso e, mɛɛsi mlɔ si: mlakɔ kelea mɛ exo la evu baʋia ba tɔ, xe eblo e, tɔ biazɛ mlɔ kpɛ ni kupe moblo me.

english_verse: So Abraham said, “If they won't pay attention to Moses and the prophets, they won't listen even to someone who comes back from the dead.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Abraham apani si, ‘Xe batrá nu Mose ni beboedoplɛ ba eboe la ɛ, batrázɛ lɛ xe ɔnutɔ ayɔ adɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ fɛ.’ ”

english_verse: The Pharisees really loved money. So when they heard what Jesus said, they made fun of him.
translation_verse: Agi Farisianɛ ma benu eboa la petee e, bɛkpɛ Yesu kakpaa lɛ ese linu si bɛdimɛ ni kibɔ ɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus told them: You are always making yourselves look good, but God sees what is in your heart. The things that most people think are important are worthless as far as God is concerned.
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, “Mlɔ mlenu banɔ baklɔ ba gi mlɛbitɛ isu ya tɔ mlaaŋwui banɔ gbã ní banɔ aŋu mɛ, pɔ̀ Aya ete mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ kidiliɛ gi kizɛ kedzi ní banɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, kíyi kididɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: Until the time of John the Baptist, people had to obey the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets. But since God's kingdom has been preached, everyone is trying hard to get in.
translation_verse: “Mose Ɔsã nɔ ni beboedoplɛ ba ba bidɔ ŋwlimiŋwlimi bɛ biizɛwa axwɛ na gakɔ nani Yohanes kuni yo ɔkpɛkpɛ yɛ lipoe le; dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ, bɛɛpani ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ka Liboe Gbã nɛ su, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa eekeni idi si yitiku ni kigagla a.

english_verse: Heaven and earth will disappear before the smallest letter of the Law does.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ linu kidɔ plɔplɔ ɛki lifunu ne ni kesea tɔ bilila sani si ba yurɔ Ɔsã nɔ kapɛmi sisami gɔgɔa.

english_verse: It is a terrible sin for a man to divorce his wife and marry another woman. It is also a terrible sin for a man to marry a divorced woman. Lazarus and the Rich Man
translation_verse: “Onyime kakaa gi aayurɔ yedze e, xe aagbani ɔdzɛ dzedze e, awa kihasi; lɛ onyime liɛ klɔ gi aagbani ɔdzɛ liɛ gi bɛyurɔ ɛ, yɛ fɛ awa kihasi. Onyime Oxonetee nì Lazaro

english_verse: There was once a rich man who wore expensive clothes and every day ate the best food.
translation_verse: “Onyime oxonete ɔtɔ eze gi yɛ bidɔ kpɛkpɛ ʋɛ ɛ, biŋa kedzi glegle lɛ azɛɛŋa ligbã liwe kakaa a.

english_verse: But a poor beggar named Lazarus was brought to the gate of the rich man's house.
translation_verse: Ɔzíanete ɔtɔ fɛ eze gi bɛzɛxwa si Lazaro e, ebi la ɛtsa ɔnɔ zizi yɛ, lɛ bɛzɛɛmani yɛ ní oxonetee yɛ kevunu ya.

english_verse: He was happy just to eat the scraps that fell from the rich man's table. His body was covered with sores, and dogs kept coming up to lick them.
translation_verse: Azeegyo edi si yɛ amɔ kiŋaŋa sisami liɛ gi kizɛɛwɔli dɔnɔ ní oxonetee yɛ ɔkplɔ nɔ aʋa a. Kudroʋi yo kɔ kwizeemini yɛ ebi la mɛ.

english_verse: The poor man died, and angels took him to the place of honor next to Abraham. The rich man also died and was buried.
translation_verse: “Ɔzianetee etse lɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba befeke ye tɔ ozeze ní Abraham su u. Oxonetee kyɛ etse lɛ bevive ye e.

english_verse: He went to hell and was suffering terribly. When he looked up and saw Abraham far off and Lazarus at his side,
translation_verse: lɛ ní kifumoni ye me e, gi eze ní kuyeʋiyeʋi glegle me e, efeke yɛ aŋu na lɛ amɔ Abraham ní ɔdzage no gi Lazaro odi ní yɛ su i.
