english_verse: he said to Abraham, “Have pity on me! Send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and touch my tongue. I'm suffering terribly in this fire.”
translation_verse: Lɛ akpɛ ozi axwa yɛ si, ‘Ɔka Abraham! Di me asigaga, tɔ kpɛ Lazaro tɔ ɔkpɛ yɛ kiwlaʋi yɛ ní kuni me tɔ ɔkɔ yɔ mɛ kinemie, lɛ ese linu si malɛ ní kuyeʋiyeʋi me ní kifui liɛ mɛ.’

english_verse: Abraham answered, “My friend, remember that while you lived, you had everything good, and Lazarus had everything bad. Now he is happy, and you are in pain.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ Abraham apani si, ‘mɛ obi ye plɛ aŋu si, ní wɔ ligbã́ nɛ mɛ ɛ wɔzi wɔ bidɔ gbã mɛ, ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi Lazaro kɔ azɛ bidɔ nyani nyani ʋɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, ɛɛmɔ isami nɛ ní ya, lɛ wɔ́ wɔli ní kuyeʋiyeʋi yo me e.

english_verse: But the rich man said, “Abraham, then please send Lazarus to my father's home.
translation_verse: “Lɛ oxonete apani si, ‘Kɔlɔ ɛ, maki wɔ litukpo ɔka Abraham, kpɛ Lazaro ní mɔka kepea mɛ,

english_verse: Let him warn my five brothers, so they won't come to this horrible place.”
translation_verse: níklɔ gi mɛ beneminyime tietsu balɛ ɛ. Tɔ ɔzɛku le kpɛ ní ba mɛ tɔ bakíba kuyeʋiyeʋi uliɔ ya mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Abraham answered, “Your brothers can read what Moses and the prophets wrote. They should pay attention to that.”
translation_verse: “Lɛ Abraham apani si, ‘Mose ɔsã nɔ ni beboedoplɛ ba bidɔ ŋwlimiŋwlimi bɛ bilɛ, wɔ benemi ba benu liboe lɛ klɔ gi bi basɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: Then the rich man said, “No, that's not enough! If only someone from the dead would go to them, they would listen and turn to God.”
translation_verse: “Oxonetee ebu le onu si, ‘Owo Ɔka Abraham,’ ‘Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ aayɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, xe atrɛ ní baklɔ ɛ, biakyi ní ba eboe zimizi na mɛ.’

english_verse: Why was this foreigner the only one who came back to thank God?”
translation_verse: Ege lɛ loso ɔbaba tole liɛ ko edzi aba do Aya ɖase e?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: If you had faith no bigger than a tiny mustard seed, you could tell this mulberry tree to pull itself up, roots and all, and to plant itself in the ocean. And it would!
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔkaɛ ebu le onu si, “Xe mlɔ kuziŋa kulɛ ŋwisi lisebi sisami nɛ fɛ ɛ, mlaatani kupani si ose ɔlɔ si, ‘Kpaʋi isu ya ni wɔ igu ne tɔ zɛkpɛ isu ya ní axu la mɛ!’ lɛ aanu kutu kí mlɔ ɔ. Ɔnɔsumu gbã ɛ yɛ Axwɛ na

english_verse: If your servant comes in from plowing or from taking care of the sheep, would you say, “Welcome! Come on in and have something to eat”?
translation_verse: “Xegi mlɔ mɛ tole yɛ ɔnɔsumu ye ádɔ ɔnyrɔ mɛ loo iʋesi le aʋa odi klɔ edzì i, aasi yɛ si ɔkɔsi tɔ ɔŋa yɛ bidɔ mɛ na a?

english_verse: There is no use saying, ‘Look! Here it is’ or ‘Look! There it is.’ God's kingdom is here with you.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trapani si, ‘Dí, imɔ ka ní ya a! loo imɔ ka ni ní kɔlɔ ɔ!’ lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, kakpasi ní mlɔ mɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: The time will come when you will long to see one of the days of the Son of Man, but you will not.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi badɔkpasi ba si, “Lipoe le litraba gi mlaapɛ si mlɛɛmɔ Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye yɛ ewe a la klɔ la mɛ tile, pɔ mlatrá mɔ lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Servants don't deserve special thanks for doing what they are supposed to do.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔsumu ye ɔ́ziã ɖase ɛki isã nɛ aʋa gi abitɛ ɛ, loo aziã a?

english_verse: Jesus said to his disciples: There will always be something that causes people to sin. But anyone who causes them to sin is in for trouble. A person who causes even one of my little followers to sin
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ badɔkpasi ba si, “Kidiliɛ gi kiaki si banɔ biabitɛ eboe zimizi i, kitraba ba pɔ̀ litraze gagla a ɛki mlɔ mɛ iliɛ gi yɛ aʋa kiaga a xe kiaba ɛ!

english_verse: So be careful what you do. Correct any followers of mine who sin, and forgive the ones who say they are sorry.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlevu aŋu ní mlɔ kiboebitɛɛ su u! “Xegi wɔ onemi nɛ ata wɔ ese e, tɛni yɛ; xegi ekyi yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, tɔ kɔkyɛ yɛ.

english_verse: Even if one of them mistreats you seven times in one day and says, “I am sorry,” you should still forgive that person.
translation_verse: Xe ata wɔ ese tegloele ní liwe tile me e, xe edzi aba wɔ klɔ tegloele si i, ‘Mekyi ɔna mɛ,’ lilɛ si kɔkyɛ yɛ.” Kuziŋaa

english_verse: The apostles said to the Lord, “Make our faith stronger!”
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛsi ɔkaɛ si, “Kítɔ blɔ kuziŋaa kuze gagla sanɔ.”

english_verse: No, you wouldn't say that. You would say, “Prepare me something to eat. Get ready to serve me, so I can have my meal. Then later on you can eat and drink.”
translation_verse: Aníte! boŋu i, kisi asi yɛ si, ‘kyí awla ní mɛ kiŋaŋaɛ su, tɔ ka kusa ole tɔ pe me gi mɛɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ xe mɛɛŋwɛ kuda ɛ; lɛ klɔ kede e, tɔ wuiŋa wɔ bidɔ mɛ.’

english_verse: And that's how it should be with you. When you've done all you should, then say, “We are merely servants, and we have simply done our duty.” Ten Men with Leprosy
translation_verse: Niteme ke mlɔ fɛ xe mlɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi bɛsi mlɔ si mlabitɛ ɛ, mlapani si, ‘Banɔsumu ko kunu; blɔ axwɛ ko kwiwa.’ ” Yesu Ayɔni Benyime Liɔfɔ

english_verse: On his way to Jerusalem, Jesus went along the border between Samaria and Galilee.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu azaatrɛ ní Yerusalem i, aga Samaria ni Galilea kumɔ aʋa.

english_verse: As he was going into a village, ten men with leprosy came toward him. They stood at a distance
translation_verse: Gi atrátiku ní kamami katɔ mɛ ɛ, benyime liɔfɔ gi bɛɛnyà kunya kyikyi yɔ bɛtsani yɛ ɛ. Bɛlɛɛ ní ɔdzage no

english_verse: and shouted, “Jesus, Master, have pity on us!”
translation_verse: lɛ bɛkpɛ ozi gagla si, “Yesu! Ɔkaɛ! Dí blɔ asigaga a!”

english_verse: Jesus looked at them and said, “Go show yourselves to the priests.” On their way they were healed.
translation_verse: Yesu amɔ ba lɛ asi ba si, “Mlatrɛ tɔ mlaki kusumu yo baka ba batapo mlo edi.” Gi bɛzɛtrɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ si ba isu ya idra.

english_verse: When one of them discovered that he was healed, he came back, shouting praises to God.
translation_verse: Agi ba mɛ tole amɔ si yɛ isu ya idra ɛ, edzi aba, lɛ azɛɛkpɔ̀ Aya ni ole gagla a.

english_verse: He bowed down at the feet of Jesus and thanked him. The man was from the country of Samaria.
translation_verse: Adɔ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa ní Yesu ketù ya lɛ edo yɛ ɖase e. Onyimee eze Samaria ɔnɛ.

english_verse: Jesus asked, “Weren't ten men healed? Where are the other nine?
translation_verse: Yesu eʋi si, “Aní benyime liɔfɔ su ɛdra na a?, Nífɔ ɔgɔtole gɔgɔ ba balɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Then Jesus told the man, “You may get up and go. Your faith has made you well.” God's Kingdom (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu apani asi yɛ si, “Yɔ tɔ ga trɛ; wɔ kuziŋa kwiyɔni wɔ.” Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ka kubabaa

english_verse: The day of the Son of Man will be like lightning flashing across the sky.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi dzeni ye izɛtsi kifu yɛ ɛ, xe kizeekle dɔni ayɛɛ kapɛ tiele tsini kapɛ ʋla ɛ, nite ke Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaze ní yɛ liwe le aʋa.

english_verse: When people say to you, “Look there,” or “Look here,” don't go looking for him.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ biaze gi biasi mlɔ si i, ‘Dí, imɔ yɛ ní kɔlɔ!’ loo, ‘Dí imɔ yɛ niya!’ Pɔ̀ mlakíze ni ba kude e.’

english_verse: When Lot lived, people were also eating and drinking. They were buying, selling, planting, and building.
translation_verse: “Liaze ŋwisi Lot lipoe le. Banima bɛzɛŋa lɛ bɛzɛŋwɛ ɛ, bezee dzi lɛ bɛzɛda bidɔ ɔ, bɛzɛkpɛ bidɔ lɛ bɛzɛɛwa agba a.

english_verse: Then Jesus' disciples spoke up, “But where will this happen, Lord?” Jesus said, “Where there is a corpse, there will always be vultures.”
translation_verse: Badɔkpasi ba beʋi ye si, “Ɔkaɛ, nifɔ ɔ?” Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Níklɔ gi kidɔ tsetse kifini i, níklɔ opéte ʋa bizɛkpɛ likpɔ.”

english_verse: Some Pharisees asked Jesus when God's kingdom would come. He answered, “God's kingdom isn't something you can see.
translation_verse: Farisianɛ batɔ beʋi Yesu lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa kiaba ɛ, Ebu le onu aki ba si, “Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, kátraba ŋwisi bidɔ gi biamɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But first he must suffer terribly and be rejected by the people of today.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, lilɛ si ɔmɔ ɔziã glegle tɔ banama gi balɛ ligbã́ nɛ abla bekyi kede akpɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When the Son of Man comes, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi lize ni Noa lipoe le e, nite ke liaze ní Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye ewe la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day when Noah went into the big boat. Then the flood came and drowned everyone on earth.
translation_verse: Banima bɛzɛŋa lɛ bɛzɛŋwɛ ɛ, benyime ba ni badzɛ ba bɛzɛgbani ba ahanuglɔ ba kɔzɛna ni liwe lɛ gi Noa eti eku ní onitsahu lo me xe kuni yo kwiyi i, xe kwiba ɛgbani ba petee sɛnɔ ɛ.

english_verse: But on the very day Lot left Sodom, fiery flames poured down from the sky and killed everyone.
translation_verse: Liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa gi Lot edo ní Sodom i, lɛ kifu yɛ ni kifu yɛ akla na ɛdɔ dɔni lifunu ne e, xe eye ba petee e.

english_verse: The same will happen on the day when the Son of Man appears.
translation_verse: Nite ke litraze xe Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaŋwi i.

english_verse: At that time no one on a rooftop should go down into the house to get anything. No one in a field should go back to the house for anything.
translation_verse: “Ní liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ, ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔtini ní yɛ ligba lɛ aʋa ɛ, ɔki ple eku ní kepea mɛ si yitrɛkɔ yɛ bidɔ mɛ ɛ; nite ke ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔlɛ ni ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa ɛ, okídzi trɛ kepea mɛ si yizɛkɔ kididɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Remember what happened to Lot's wife.
translation_verse: Mlaplɛ aŋu ní Lot yedze aʋa!

english_verse: People who try to save their lives will lose them, and those who lose their lives will save them.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aapɛ si yibu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, aalila lɛ; lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aalila yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, aabu le.

english_verse: On that night two people will be sleeping in the same bed, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Ní elesi ala klɔ mɛ, mɛɛsi mlɔ si, banɔ tiaʋa biafɛ ní ɔtɔsi tole aʋa; pɔ̀ biasɛ ni tole e, lɛ biayurɔ ɔʋla ɛ.

english_verse: Two women will be together grinding wheat, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
translation_verse: Benyime tiaʋa biazɛwa axwɛ na ní ɔnyrɔ mɛ, biasɛ ni tole, lɛ biayurɔ ɔʋla ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples a story about how they should keep on praying and never give up:
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu abee aki yɛ badɔkpasi ba tɔ yikɔ ɛbasɛ ba si liziã si bazɛxwa Aya lipoe kakaa tɔ ba ina nɛ ikí gbɔ̀ ɔ.

english_verse: In a town there was once a judge who didn't fear God or care about people.
translation_verse: Apani si “Otsixwa ɔtɔ eze ní ɔma ɔtɔ mɛ gi óse Aya a, lɛ ɔ́kɔ lɛ mɛ ɔ́ki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: In that same town there was a widow who kept going to the judge and saying, “Make sure that I get fair treatment in court.”
translation_verse: Ɔtrɛtrɛ ɔtɔ yedze fɛ eze ní ɔma lɔ klɔ mɛ gi azɛɛba yɛ klɔ nì kugyodi si tɔ ɔtsa litsi ɔki yɛ ɛ, Apani si, ‘Tsa litsi kí mɛ ní mɛ nì ɔnɔ gi apɛ si yitafa mɛ kusi i!’

english_verse: For a while the judge refused to do anything. Finally, he said to himself, “Even though I don't fear God or care about people,
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ kɔki lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ ɛ, otsixwa yɛ ɔ́wa lixwɛxwɛ ní lɛ su. Gɔgɔɛ asi isu ya si, ‘Lilɛ lɛ mɛ si meʋíse Aya loo kɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ,

english_verse: I will help this widow because she keeps on bothering me. If I don't help her, she will wear me out.”
translation_verse: pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ kunugu yo gi ɔtrɛtrɛ yedze liɛ ɛɛkpɛ mɛ loso ɛ, maadi lɛ aʋa si matsa litsi gyɔgyɔ aki yɛ ɛ. Xe aníte e, yɛ mɔklɔ kubaba lipoe kakaa ɛ, kwikpɛ mɛ kupepe.’ ”

english_verse: The Lord said: Think about what that crooked judge said.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ atrɛ ní lɛ aʋa si, “Mlenu liboe lɛ gi otsixwa nyani nyani liɛ klɔ apani i.

english_verse: Won't God protect his chosen ones who pray to him day and night? Won't he be concerned for them?
translation_verse: Kɔ Aya ɔ́tratsa litsi kí yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ banima gi beebemi ikí yɛ elesi nì kemize kí kupɔnɔ na a? Ɔ́trakɔsi xe aapɔni ba na a?

english_verse: He will surely hurry and help them. But when the Son of Man comes, will he find on this earth anyone with faith? A Pharisee and a Tax Collector
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, aakɔsi aatsa litsi kiba. Pɔ̀ xe Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aaba kesea aʋa ɛ, aamɔ kuziŋa na?” Abee Ní Farisiɔnɛ Ni Ɔbɔgyɔɛ Su

english_verse: Jesus told a story to some people who thought they were better than others and who looked down on everyone else:
translation_verse: Yesu ebu abee akí banama gi bɛka ní isu ya aʋa si bɛɛpɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe bɛɛplɛ banɔgɔgɔ ba kesea ɛ.

english_verse: The Pharisee stood over by himself and prayed, “God, I thank you that I am not greedy, dishonest, and unfaithful in marriage like other people. And I am really glad that I am not like that tax collector over there.
translation_verse: “Farisiɔnɛ ebu isu ya lidze lɛ ɔlɛ kedu ya xe axwa Aya si, ‘Medo wɔ́ Aya ɖase e, si kuŋazi kúma mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, katafa kama mɛ mɛ̀, lɛ ménu ɔhasinɛ ɛ, ŋwisi banɔ gɔgɔ ba kinɛ ɛ. Medo wɔ ɖase si máma ŋwisi ɔbɔgyɔ liɛ kɔlɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I go without eating for two days a week, and I give you one tenth of all I earn.”
translation_verse: Mizɛɛnya onugu taʋa ní ogle no me, lɛ mizɛɛki wɔ mɛ bidɔ mɔmɔ ʋɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa a.’

english_verse: Then Jesus said, “When the two men went home, it was the tax collector and not the Pharisee who was pleasing to God. If you put yourself above others, you will be put down. But if you humble yourself, you will be honored.” Jesus Blesses Little Children (Matthew
translation_verse: “Lɛ Yesu atrɛ lɛ aʋa si Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, aní Farisiɔnɛ pɔ̀ ɔbɔgyɔɛ yɛ liboe ligyɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ, xe bɛsɛ ɛtrɛ ní kepea mɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso ɔnɔ kakaa gi izeemu ni isu ya ɛ, biapleni ye, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi izeepleni isu ya ɛ biamuni ye.” Yesu Akrira Banuvɔ ba

english_verse: Some people brought their little children for Jesus to bless. But when his disciples saw them doing this, they told the people to stop bothering him.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ bɛmani ba bebi ba ní Yesu klɔ tɔ ɔtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa, tɔ ɔkrira ba. Agi badɔkpasi ba bɛmɔ nite e, bɛtɛni ba,

english_verse: will be given much more in this life. And in the future world they will have eternal life.” Jesus Again Tells about His Death (Matthew
translation_verse: aamɔ bidɔ glegle sanɔ ni abla kelea mɛ kɔpɔni ligbã moblo le ní kelea lia gi kiaba mɛ ɛ.” Yesu Apani Dzɛ Ní Yɛ Kutseo Su

english_verse: Jesus took the twelve apostles aside and said: We are now on our way to Jerusalem. Everything that the prophets wrote about the Son of Man will happen there.
translation_verse: Yesu ebú badɔkpasi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba kuka lɛ asi ba si, “Mlata kutu! Kwitrɛ ni Yerusalem niklɔ kidɔ kakaa gi beboedoplɛ ba bɛŋwlimi ni Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye su i, kiaba kɛ mɛ.

english_verse: An important man asked Jesus, “Good Teacher, what must I do to have eternal life?”
translation_verse: Yuda ɔnɛ ɔka ɔtɔ eʋi Yesu si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ gbã ɛ, ege maabitɛ xe maamɔ ligbã́ moblo le e?”

english_verse: He told Jesus, “I have obeyed all these commandments since I was a young man.”
translation_verse: Onyimee ebu le onu si, “Dɔni mɛ kunuvɔ mɛ ké mɛɛbitɛ ni isã nɛ pɔ́ aʋa a.”

english_verse: When Jesus heard this, he said, “There is one thing you still need to do. Go and sell everything you own! Give the money to the poor, and you will have riches in heaven. Then come and be my follower.”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu enu liboe lɛ ɛ, asi yɛ si, “Kigɔ́ kidɔ tile dzɛ gi lilɛ si bitɛ ɛ. Da wɔ bidɔ mɛ petee tɔ kɔ kibɔe kí bazianete ba, tɔ wɔamɔ exo ní lifunu ne; lɛ kede e, bá tɔ gaze ni mɛ kude e.”

english_verse: When the man heard this, he was sad, because he was very rich.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi onyimee enu liboe le e, Líku ye isami i, lɛ ese linu si oxonete ʋidi onu.

english_verse: Jesus saw how sad the man was. So he said, “It's terribly hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!
translation_verse: Yesu edi ye lɛ apani si, “Liaaze gagla xe bexonete ba biati uku ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: In fact, it's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: Sugba ɛ, liaze plɔplɔ ɛki kposɔɛ si ɔga trɔ̀ni yɛ liwu le me odo sani si oxonetee oti oku Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ.”

english_verse: When the crowd heard this, they asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
translation_verse: Banama gi benu liboe le e, beʋi ye si, “Kɔ, nyaŋwɛ aamɔ kubúbú i?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “There are some things that people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Kidiliɛ gi ɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kubitɛ ɛ, kilɛ plɔplɔ ɛki Aya yɛ kubitɛ.”

english_verse: Peter said, “Remember, we left everything to be your followers!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro apani si, “Dí! Kwiyurɔ kidɔ kakaa xe kwikuni wɔ kude e.”

english_verse: He will be handed over to foreigners, who will make fun of him, mistreat him, and spit on him.
translation_verse: Biakpɛ yɛ awla ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, gi biakpɛ yɛ kakpaa, biakpini ye, lɛ biabu sita bali yɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: They will beat him and kill him, but three days later he will rise to life.
translation_verse: Biafiaʋi yɛ litam lɛ biaye ye e, pɔ̀ ewe tata kede e, aadzi ligbã́.”

english_verse: When Jesus was coming close to Jericho, a blind man sat begging beside the road.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu eti apɔni Yerixo e, kaŋupo atɔ keze ní kudeo su, gi keze eʋi bidɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The man heard the crowd walking by and asked what was happening.
translation_verse: Gi enu linɔkpi le onu gi lizɛza ɛ, eʋi si, “Liboe woli liiba a?”

english_verse: Some people told him that Jesus from Nazareth was passing by.
translation_verse: Bɛsi yɛ si, “Yesu Nazaret Ɔnɛɛ yɛ ɛɛza.”

english_verse: So the blind man shouted, “Jesus, Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Lɛ akpɛ ozi si, “Yesu! Dawid Yebi ye! Di me asigaga!”

english_verse: The people who were going along with Jesus told the man to be quiet. But he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
translation_verse: Banama gi beze ni ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ, bɛtɛni yɛ lɛ bɛsi yɛ si ɔgɔ tsriɖi. Pɔ̀ akpɛ ozi lo gagla asanɔ si, “Dawid Yebi ye! Di me asigaga!”

english_verse: Jesus stopped and told some people to bring the blind man over to him. When the blind man was getting near, Jesus asked,
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu alɛɛ xe aki si bagbani kaŋupoa mani yɛ ɛ. Gi eti apɔni yɛ ɛ, Yesu eʋi ye si,

english_verse: “What do you want me to do for you?” “Lord, I want to see!” he answered.
translation_verse: “Ege wɔpɛ si mibitɛ kiwɔ ɔ?” Ebu le onu si, “Ɔkaɛ, mapɛ si mimɔ dzɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Look and you will see! Your eyes are healed because of your faith.”
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Kɔlɔ mɔ! Wɔ kuziŋaa kwiyɔ ni wɔ.”

english_verse: At once the man could see, and he went with Jesus and started thanking God. When the crowds saw what happened, they praised God.
translation_verse: Onyimee bimbi ʋe bidra kyɔŋ lɛ ekuni Yesu kude e, gi azɛɛkpɔ̀ Aya a. Gi linɔkpi le limɔ nite e, ba pɔ́ bɛkpɔ̀ Aya.

english_verse: Jesus was heading his way, and Zacchaeus wanted to see what he was like. But Zacchaeus was a short man and could not see over the crowd. So he ran ahead and climbed up into a sycamore tree.
translation_verse: Nite si ese ekú ɔtɔ kí linɔkpi le lɛ azeemu ni ose ɔtɔ mɛ si yimɔ Yesu gi atrága niklɔ ɛza ɛ.

english_verse: When Jesus got there, he looked up and said, “Zacchaeus, hurry down! I want to stay with you today.”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu ana niklɔ ɛ, emuni aŋu na lɛ asi Zaxeo si, “Kɔsi tɔ ple, Zaxeo, lɛ ese linu si wɔ kepea mɛ mátraze omono o.”

english_verse: Zacchaeus hurried down and gladly welcomed Jesus.
translation_verse: Zaxeo akɔsi eple ní ose lo me, lɛ azɛ yɛ ni isami i.

english_verse: Everyone who saw this started grumbling, “This man Zacchaeus is a sinner! And Jesus is going home to eat with him.”
translation_verse: Banama pɔ́ gi bɛmɔ lɛ ɛ, bekpese kutɛ si, “Onyime liɛ ya atrɛ edzì ɔbaba ɛki onyime gi onu ɔnɔ zimizi i!”

english_verse: Later that day Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord, “I will give half of my property to the poor. And I will now pay back four times as much to everyone I have ever cheated.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Zaxeo ayɔ ɔlɛɛ, lɛ asi Ɔkaɛ si, “Ta kutu, Ɔkaɛ! maakɔ mɛ bidɔ mɔmɔ ʋɛ mɛ kulɛlɛɔ kapɛ aki bazianete ba, lɛ xe memi ɔnutɔ akpɛ fɛ ɛ, Maadzini kidiliɛ klɔ ede tene kutsa ki yɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said to Zacchaeus, “Today you and your family have been saved, because you are a true son of Abraham.
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Kubúbú kwiba kepea lia mɛ omono, lɛ ese linu si onyime liɛ fɛ ɛ, onu Abraham yɛ osobi.

english_verse: The Son of Man came to look for and to save people who are lost.” A Story about Ten Servants (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aba si yipɛ tɔ yibu banama gi bɛlila ɛ.” Abee Ní Sika Kibɔ Su u

english_verse: But they said, “Sir, he already has ten times as much!”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ bɛsi yɛ si, ‘Ɔka, bibɔ liɔfɔ bilɛ ni yɛ awla kooko!’

english_verse: But the people of his country hated him, and they sent messengers to the foreign country to say, “We don't want this man to be our king.”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ yɛ akɔanɛ ma bápɛ yɛ liboe, lɛ loso e, bɛkpɛ banɔ ní yɛ kude tɔ basɛ okusi ʋidi ye si, ‘Kúpɛ onyime liɛ si oze blɔ okusi.’

english_verse: He told them, “Go into the next village, where you will find a young donkey that has never been ridden. Untie the donkey and bring it here.
translation_verse: “Mlatrɛ ní kamamia ní mlɔ ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ; xe mleti iku i, mlaamɔ tedzi obi gi bɛkpɛ ogbe no me e, gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trɔ ní yɛ aʋa ódi i. Mleble ye amani niya.

english_verse: The first servant came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me I have earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: “Ɔtɔpu yɛ aba lɛ apani si, ‘Ɔkaɛ, mamɔ sika bibɔ liɔfɔ kɔpɔni kiliɛ klɔ gi wɔ kimɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: The second one came and said, “Sir, with the money you gave me, I have earned five times as much.”
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔsumu ʋlaɛ fɛ aba lɛ apani si, ‘Ɔkaɛ, mamɔ sika bibɔ tutsu kɔpɔni kiliɛ klɔ gi wɔ kimɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: Another servant came and said, “Sir, here is your money. I kept it safe in a handkerchief.
translation_verse: “Ɔnɔsumu dzedze aba lɛ apani si, ‘Ɔkaɛ, imɔ wɔ sika yɛ kibɔɛ; mabla kɛ ní kavlɔʋi mɛ.

english_verse: Why didn't you put my money in the bank? On my return, I could have had the money together with interest.”
translation_verse: Agi wote nite e, ege lɛ loso wɔ́kɔ mɛ kibɔɛ ɔkpɛ ní kibɔɛ ɔbla klɔ ɔ; atɔ xe medzi i, maazi kɛ ni kitrɔtrɔ ɛ?’

english_verse: Then he said to some other servants standing there, “Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who earned ten times as much.”
translation_verse: “Lɛ asi banama gi bɛlɛɛ niklɔ si, ‘Mlazi sika yɛ kibɔɛ ní yɛ awla tɔ mlakɔ kɛ aki ɔnɔsumu liɛ gi bibɔ liɔfɔ bɛ bilɛ ní yɛ awla ɛ.’

english_verse: The king replied, “Those who have something will be given more. But everything will be taken away from those who don't have anything.
translation_verse: “Ebu le onu si, ‘Mɛɛsi mlɔ si, ɔnɔ kakaa awla gi kidɔ kilɛ ɛ, biapɔnɔ ɔki yɛ; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ klɔ awla gi kididɔ kima ɛ, kisisami iliɛ gi kilɛ yɛ awla ɛ, biazɛ kɛ.

english_verse: Now bring me the enemies who didn't want me to be their king. Kill them while I watch!” Jesus Enters Jerusalem (Matthew
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛ ahwafianɛ ma gi bápɛ si mize ba okusi i, mlagbani ba amanɔ tɔ mleye ba ní mɛ aŋu mɛ.’ ” Yesu Eti eku ní Yerusalem

english_verse: When Jesus had finished saying all this, he went on toward Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Gi Yesu apani eboa la ya pɔ́ ɛ, eze ní ba ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛtrɛ ní Yerusalem i.

english_verse: As he was getting near Bethphage and Bethany on the Mount of Olives, he sent two of his disciples on ahead.
translation_verse: Agi beti apɔni Betfage ni Betania níklɔ gi bizɛxwa si Kumu yɔ ise le ketokpaa su i, akpɛ yɛ badɔkpasi ba mɛ tiaʋa ní yɛ ɔtɔ nɔ, lɛ asi ba si,

english_verse: If anyone asks why you are doing this, just say, ‘The Lord needs it.’ ”
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ aaʋi mlo si ege lɛ loso mleeble ye na ɛ, mlasi yɛ si Ɔkaɛ yɛ aziã yɛ.”

english_verse: They went off and found everything just as Jesus had said.
translation_verse: Bekú kudeo me lɛ bɛmɔ kidɔ kakaa ŋwisi kiliɛ ɖii gi Yesu asi ba kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: While they were untying the donkey, its owners asked, “Why are you doing that?”
translation_verse: Agi bezeeble tedzi obi ye e, yɛ benete ba beʋi ba si, “Ege lɛ loso mleeble ye e?”

english_verse: They answered, “The Lord needs it.”
translation_verse: Bebú le onu si, “Ɔkaɛ yɛ aziã yɛ.”

english_verse: Then they led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back and helped Jesus get on.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛgbani tedzi obi ye mani Yesu i, lɛ bɛkɔ ba ewu la ɛtrɔ ní ɔgaɛ aʋa lɛ bɛpɔni Yesu xe emu atrɔ ɛ.

english_verse: And as he rode along, the people spread clothes on the road in front of him.
translation_verse: Agi azɛɛkpɛ yɛ atsinɔ ɛ, banima bene bá ewu la ɛplɛ ní kudeo me.

english_verse: When Jesus started down the Mount of Olives, his large crowd of disciples were happy and praised God because of all the miracles they had seen.
translation_verse: Agi eti apɔni Yerusalem i, ni niklɔ gi kudeo kwikyi ple ní Olif ye ise le ketokpaa su i, yɛ badɔkpasi ba likpi le likpese Aya ɖase kudo ni kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ni ozi gagla ní eboe ʋidi glegle ala la gi bɛmɔ su i si,

english_verse: They shouted, “Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord! Peace in heaven and glory to God.”
translation_verse: “Aya ɔkrira okusi ye klɔ gi atrába ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me e! Kusuyɔ ní lifunu ne ni kusudra kí Aya!”

english_verse: Some Pharisees in the crowd said to Jesus, “Teacher, make your disciples stop shouting!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Farisianɛ batɔ ní ɔha lɔ̀ kusi i, bɛpani kí Yesu si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, kpɛ ɔsã kí wɔ badɔkpasi ba tɔ bagɔ tsriɖi!”

english_verse: But Jesus answered, “If they keep quiet, these stones will start shouting.”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mɛɛsi mlɔ si xegi bɛgɔ tsriɖi i, akla na ɔtutɔ aakpɛ ozi lo.” Yesu Ebemi ní Yerusalem Litukpo

english_verse: When Jesus came closer and could see Jerusalem, he cried
translation_verse: Agi eti apɔnɔ xe amɔ ɔma ʋidi no e, ebemi ní lɔ litukpo.

english_verse: and said: It is too bad that today your people don't know what will bring them peace! Now it is hidden from them.
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si, “Atɛsi mlete omono kidiliɛ gi kízia ɛki mlɔ kusuyɔ ɛ! Pɔ̀ abla ɛ bekpo ke ní mlɔ su.

english_verse: Jerusalem, the time will come when your enemies will build walls around you to attack you. Armies will surround you and close in on you from every side.
translation_verse: Lipoe le litraba gi mlɔ ahwafianɛ ma biaklani mlɔ lɛ biatsí mlɔ bede ʋa, lɛ biapɔ́ mlɔ ɔkpɛ ní kapɛ kakaa.

english_verse: They will level you to the ground and kill your people. Not one stone in your buildings will be left on top of another. This will happen because you did not see that God had come to save you. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
translation_verse: Biayaʋi wɔ petee lɛ biaye banama gi balɛ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, batráyurɔ likla ɛtɔtɔ ní lɛ ozeklɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlamɔ lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Aya aba si yibu mlo o!” Yesu Atrɛ ní Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ Mɛ

english_verse: When Jesus entered the temple, he started chasing out the people who were selling things.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu eti eku Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ekpese badɔda ba kukpikpinɔ dono e,

english_verse: He told them, “The Scriptures say, ‘My house should be a place of worship.’ But you have made it a place where robbers hide!”
translation_verse: asi ba si, “Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Liboe le me si, Aya apani si ‘Mɛ Ligba lɛ ɛ, biaxwa lɛ si Aya lixwa Gba.’ Pɔ̀ mlekyi le ɛbitɛ beze ba òkpò kɔ́ ɔ!”

english_verse: Each day, Jesus kept on teaching in the temple. So the chief priests, the teachers of the Law of Moses, and some other important people tried to have him killed.
translation_verse: Liwe kakaa Yesu abasɛ bidɔ ní Yerusalem Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ. Kusumu yo baka ba, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba, ni banɔtsitsi ba bɛpɛ si beeye ye,

english_verse: But they could not find a way to do it, because everyone else was eager to listen to him.
translation_verse: pɔ̀ bámɔ kudeonu xe biaye ye, lɛ ese linu si banima pɔ́ bezeenu yɛ eboe la a, lɛ bápɛ si liboeboe lidɔ ba ɛza a.

english_verse: The spies said to him, “Teacher, we know you teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Banɔpunu ba ya ba ɛ, beʋi Yesu si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, kwite si wɔ eboe la gi wuzɛɛpani i, ni kidiliɛ gi wuzɛɛbasɛ banima ɛ, bina. Kwite si woʋidi ɔnɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ wuzɛɛbasɛ anukwarɛɛ ní Aya yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ ki ɔnɔ onu u.

english_verse: The Sadducees did not believe that people would rise to life after death. So some of them came to Jesus
translation_verse: Lɛ Zadukianɛ batɔ, gi bázɛ lɛ aʋa aŋa si banima biayɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, bɛba Yesu klɔ lɛ bɛpani si,

english_verse: When God raises people from death, whose wife will this woman be? All seven brothers had married her.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, ní liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa gi bekpo ʋa biayɔ ɛ, nyaŋwɛ yedze ɔdzɛɛ aaze e, gi ba tiegloele ba pɔ́ bɛgbani yɛ edi i?”

english_verse: asked him, “What right do you have to do these things? Who gave you this authority?”
translation_verse: lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Si blɔ, kude woli wɔmɔ xe wɔɛbitɛ eboa la ya a? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aki wɔ kude o?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I want to ask you a question.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki ba si, “Abla ɛ mlaki tɔ miʋi mlo liboe. Mlasi mɛ,

english_verse: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?”
translation_verse: Nyaŋwɛ aki Yohanes kigaglaɛ xe azaakpɛ kuni yo aki banima a? Aya kɔ́ lidɔ loo dɔni banima kɔ́ na a?”

english_verse: They talked this over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
translation_verse: Lɛ bekpese eboe la kuvu udi ní ba tsali kusi si, “Liboe woli kwiapani i? Xe kwipani si, ‘Dɔni Aya kɔ ɛ,’ aaʋi blo si, ‘Kɔlɔ, ege lɛ loso, mlaazi Yohanes aʋa aŋa ɛ?’

english_verse: And we can't say it was merely some human who gave John the right to baptize. The crowd will stone us to death, because they think John was a prophet.”
translation_verse: Pɔ xe kwipani si, ‘Dɔni banima klɔ ɛ,’ linɔkpi lɛ ya pɔ́ liasa blɔ akla, lɛ ese linu si bɛzi lɛ si Yohanes eze oboedoplɛ.”

english_verse: So they told Jesus, “We don't know who gave John the right to baptize.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e bebu le onu si, “Kúte niklɔ gi kidɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Then I won't tell you who gave me the right to do what I do.” Renters of a Vineyard (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Kɔlɔ mɛ fɛ, mátrasi mlɔ kude kɔkɔ kuliɔ gi mɛɛkɔ ɛbitɛ eboa la ya a.” Abee ní Ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa Bedidi ba Su

english_verse: Guard against the teachers of the Law of Moses! They love to walk around in long robes, and they like to be greeted in the market. They want the front seats in the synagogues and the best seats at banquets.
translation_verse: “Mleze nì aŋu ní Ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba su; gi bizɛpɛ si biazɛga za ní ba ewu gblamadza la mɛ ɛ, xe bɛzɛpɛ si bazɛpani ba nì kubùbù ní ablɔ ʋa aʋa a; gi bizɛkya ibùbùzekɔ ɛki isu ya a, gi bɛgɔ ɛplɛ ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ ɔ, ani ikɔ gbã nɛ ní iŋaŋakplɔ nɛ su i.

english_verse: Jesus told the people this story: A man once planted a vineyard and rented it out. Then he left the country for a long time.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu abee liɛ aki banima si: “Onyime ɔtɔ eze gi awa wain ɔnyrɔ, lɛ akɔ lɔ aki ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba si biatsa ɔgyɔ ɔki yɛ lɛ asɛ ní kepea lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When it was time to harvest the crop, he sent a servant to ask the renters for his share of the grapes. But they beat up the servant and sent him away without anything.
translation_verse: Agi wain yɛ ligu nɛ lina ɛ, akpɛ yɛ ɔnɔsumu tole atsisi yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba tɔ ɔzɛ yɛ ɔŋa klɔ ɔmani yɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bekusi ɔnɔsumu ye e, lɛ bedzini ye tsisi iwla pluplu i.

english_verse: When the renters saw the owner's son, they said to one another, “Someday he will own the vineyard. Let's kill him! Then we can have it all for ourselves.”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ gi ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi ba bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, bɛpani nì bahanuglɔ ba si, ‘Mledi, onetee Yebi ye iliɛ ya. Mlaki tɔ kuye ye, atɔ yɛ kaŋasea kedzi blɔ kanɛ!’

english_verse: I'll tell you what. He will come and kill those renters and let someone else have his vineyard.” When the people heard this, they said, “This must never happen!”
translation_verse: Aaba, kɔ eye benyime ba klɔ, kɔ akɔ wain yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ akpɛ ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bedidi dzedze awla ɛ.” Agi banima benu le e, bɛ pani si, “Likíba lɛ mɛ!”

english_verse: The chief priests and the teachers of the Law of Moses knew that Jesus was talking about them when he was telling this story. They wanted to arrest him right then, but they were afraid of the people. Paying Taxes (Matthew

english_verse: But the whole crowd shouted, “Kill Jesus! Give us Barabbas!”

translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Kitɛ xe mlɛdɔ kuga nite e, lɛ kitɛ xe mlɔ begyodi ʋa bɛyɔ ní beboedoplɛ ba eboe la aʋa kuziŋaa su u?

english_verse: Didn't you know that the Messiah would have to suffer before he was given his glory?”

english_verse: Jesus then explained everything written about himself in the Scriptures, beginning with the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets.

english_verse: They begged him, “Stay with us! It's already late, and the sun is going down.” So Jesus went into the house to stay with them.

english_verse: At once they knew who he was, but he disappeared.

english_verse: So they got up and returned to Jerusalem. The two disciples found the eleven apostles and the others gathered together.

translation_verse: Lɛ banɔ tiaʋa ya kyɛ ɛ, bɛdra lɛ su si ba kidiliɛ gi kiba kɛ mɛ ní kudeo aʋa ɛ, nì kiliɛ gi bɛmɔ Ɔkaɛ gyesi gi ayaʋi liklã̀ nɛ aki ba ɛ. Yesu Ebu Isu Abasi Yɛ Badɔkpasi ba

english_verse: While Jesus' disciples were talking about what had happened, Jesus appeared and greeted them.
translation_verse: Agi banɔ tiaʋa ba ya bɛzɛɛsi ba eboa la ya ɛ, Ɔkaɛ ɔtutɔ alɛɛ ní ba kusi lɛ asi ba si, “Kusuyɔ kimlɔ.”

english_verse: They were frightened and terrified because they thought they were seeing a ghost.
translation_verse: Besugu, lɛ lifu liku ba lɛ begyodi si bɛɛmɔ okpo.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “Why are you so frightened? Why do you doubt?
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ asi ba si, “Ege nì lifu mlese e? Ege lɛ loso mlaʋízi liboe lɛ ya aʋa ɛɛŋa a?

english_verse: Look at my hands and my feet and see who I am! Touch me and find out for yourselves. Ghosts don't have flesh and bones as you see I have.”
translation_verse: Mledi mɛ kuwla mɛ ni mɛ kuklikpɔ, tɔ mlete si mɛ ɔtutɔ. Mlagbɔni mɛ edi, lɛ ese linu si kigyɛ ni axwa aʋíze okpo su ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɛmɔ si bilɛ mɛ su u.”

english_verse: After Jesus said this, he showed them his hands and his feet.
translation_verse: Gi apani ní te e, abasɛ ba yɛ kuwla mɛ ni yɛ kuklikpɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The disciples were so glad and amazed that they could not believe it. Jesus then asked them, “Do you have something to eat?”
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ gi liboe le litu ba kugyɔ ɔ, xe liku ba isami lɛ loso e, bázɛ lɛ aʋa aŋa dzɛ si yɛ, lɛ eʋi ba si, “kiŋaŋa ɛtɔ kilɛ ní mlɔ awla gi biaŋa na a?”

english_verse: They gave him a piece of broiled fish.
translation_verse: Bɛki yɛ likpaa gi bɛpɔʋi i,

english_verse: He took it and ate it as they watched.
translation_verse: lɛ azɛ lɛ xe ata nɛ ní ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “While I was still with you, I told you that everything written about me in the Law of Moses, the Books of the Prophets, and in the Psalms had to happen.”
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Eboa la ya ɖii masi mlɔ gi meze ní mlɔ kusi i: kidɔ kakaa gi bɛŋwlimi ní mɛ su u ní Mose Isã nɛ mɛ nì, beboedoplɛ ba bidɔ ŋwlimiŋwlimi ʋɛ, anì Psalomo ba kuplekpaa mɛ ɛ, lilɛ si kiba kɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then he helped them understand the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Niklɔ adra ba begyodi ba mɛ tɔ benu kuŋwlimi yɔ ese.

english_verse: He told them: The Scriptures say that the Messiah must suffer, then three days later he will rise from death.
translation_verse: lɛ asi ba si, “Eboa la ya bɛŋwlimi plɛ si: Kristo ye aamɔ kini, lɛ aayɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ní ewe tata kede e,

english_verse: They also say that all people of every nation must be told in my name to turn to God, in order to be forgiven. So beginning in Jerusalem,
translation_verse: lɛ ní yɛ linyi ne me e, ɔna mɛ kukyikyi ni kesetata kukɔkyɛ biasɔ̀li bɛ kí kese kakaa, bia kpese dɔni Yerusalem.

english_verse: you must tell everything that has happened.
translation_verse: Mlɔ mlenu katsalia aki eboa la ya.

english_verse: I will send you the one my Father has promised, but you must stay in the city until you are given power from heaven. Jesus Returns to Heaven (Mark
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛ ɔtutɔ maatsísi mlɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ dradra lɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mɔka aka aplɛ kinɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ lilɛ si mleze ní ɔma ʋidi lo me kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi kigaglaɛ kiadɔ ní lifunu ne xe kiaba mlɔ aʋa ɛ.” Befeke Yesu i tsini Lifunu ne

english_verse: Jesus led his disciples out to Bethany, where he raised his hands and blessed them.
translation_verse: Gi agbani ba edono ní ɔma ʋidi lo me atsini Betania ɛ, emuni yɛ iwla lɛ lɛ akrira ba ɛ.

english_verse: As he was doing this, he left and was taken up to heaven.
translation_verse: Gi azɛɛkrira ba ɛ, asɛ ní ba su lɛ befeke ye tsini lifunu ne e.

english_verse: After his disciples had worshiped him, they returned to Jerusalem and were very happy.
translation_verse: Besumu ye, lɛ bedzitrɛ ní Yerusalem ni isami glegle e.

english_verse: They spent their time in the temple, praising God.
translation_verse: Nite si beze Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ lipoe glegle gi bezee do Aya ɖase e.

english_verse: From the very beginning the Word was with God.
translation_verse: Dɔni kelea okpese kɔ ɛ, eze nì Aya.

english_verse: received its life from him, and his life gave light to everyone.
translation_verse: Ligbã́ nɛ lize yɛ mɛ, lɛ ligbã́ nɛ, lɛ lize kuklekleo ki banima a.

english_verse: The light keeps shining in the dark, and darkness has never put it out.
translation_verse: Kuklekleo kwikle ku ní litumu ne me, lɛ litumu ne lítani kɔ aʋa ku ŋa a.

english_verse: God sent a man named John,
translation_verse: Aya akpɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gi bizɛxwa si Yohanes,

english_verse: who came to tell about the light and to lead all people to have faith.
translation_verse: aba ŋwisi katsalia ɔŋaŋa kinɛ tɔ ɔdra kuklekleo su, tɔ gani yɛ aʋa ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa aanu liboe le tɔ aazi ŋa.

english_verse: The true light that shines on everyone was coming into the world.
translation_verse: Kuklekleo ɔtutɔ lɔ gi kuzekle kí banima petee e, kwitra ba kelea mɛ.

english_verse: The Word was in the world, but no one knew him, though God had made the world with his Word.
translation_verse: Aba kelea mɛ, pɔ gi Aya abitɛ kelea gani yɛ aʋa fɛ ɛ, kelea kámɔ yɛ egyesi i.

english_verse: He came into his own world, but his own nation did not welcome him.
translation_verse: Aba yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ akɔanɛ ma klɔ, pɔ̀ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ akɔanɛ ma bázɛ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They were not God's children by nature or because of any human desires. God himself was the one who made them his children.
translation_verse: Bédzi Aya yɛ bebi gani kipɔpɔ mɛ ɛ, loo onyime ni ɔdzɛ ba kutrɔtrɔ mɛ, loo gani obika yɛ kupɛpɛ mɛ; Aya ɔtutɔ onu ba Ɔka.

english_verse: The Word became a human being and lived here with us. We saw his true glory, the glory of the only Son of the Father. From him the complete gifts of undeserved grace and truth have come down to us.
translation_verse: Liboe le lidzi ɔnɔ gi eyi nì lipomepɛpɛ ni anukwarɛ ɛ, lɛ eze ní blɔ kusi i. Kwimɔ yɛ kuklekleo, kuklekleo liɔ klɔ gi azɛ ŋwisi Yɛka yɛ obinyime tolee kinɛ ɛ,

english_verse: Because of all that the Son is, we have been given one blessing after another.
translation_verse: Lipomepɛpɛ lɛ kusrɛsrɛɔ ní yɛ mɛ loso e, akrira blɔ petee, lɛ aki blɔ kukrira trɔ kukrira aʋa a.

english_verse: No one has ever seen God. The only Son, who is truly God and is closest to the Father, has shown us what God is like. John the Baptist Tells about Jesus (Matthew
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́mɔ Aya ʋídi i, Yebinyime tolee, gi ɔʋífili ní Yɛka su i, yɛ ebu Ɔka yɛ abasi blɔ. Yohanes Kuni yo Ɔkpɛkpɛɛ apani si aní yɛ yenu Kristo ye

english_verse: The religious authorities in Jerusalem sent priests and temple helpers to ask John who he was. He told them plainly, “I am not the Messiah.”
translation_verse: Yohanes ógbe liboe le onu kubúbú, pɔ̀ apani wee lɛ adra lɛ mɛ si, “Aní mɛ menu Kristo ye e.”

english_verse: Then when they asked him if he were Elijah, he said, “No, I am not!” And when they asked if he were the Prophet, he also said “No!”
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi ye si, “Kɔlɔ nyaŋwɛ wònu, wɔ wonu Eliya na a?” Yohanes ebu le onu si, “Owo aní yɛ menu u,” lɛ beʋi si, “Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ wònu na a?” Ebu le onu si, “Owo.”

english_verse: Finally, they said, “Who are you then? We have to give an answer to the ones who sent us. Tell us who you are!”
translation_verse: Gɔgɔɛ beʋi ye si, “Kɔlɔ si blɔ ɔniliɛ gi wonu i, lilɛ si kuzɛ kunububu tsini banɔaklɔ gi bɛkpɛ blɔ ɛ. Liboe woli wɔɛpani ní isu ya su na a?”

english_verse: John answered in the words of the prophet Isaiah, “I am only someone shouting in the desert, ‘Get the road ready for the Lord!’ ”
translation_verse: Yohanes ebu le onu nì Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ Yesaya liboe le si, “Mɛ menu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ole lo eeyì ní ɔyaya klɔ si, ‘Mlebu kude gyɔgyɔ akí Ɔkaɛ tɔ ɔga!’ ”

english_verse: Some Pharisees had also been sent to John.
translation_verse: Farisianɛ bɛ kpɛ banima,

english_verse: They asked him, “Why are you baptizing people, if you are not the Messiah or Elijah or the Prophet?”
translation_verse: lɛ beʋi Yohanes si, “Xe ani wɔ́ wonu Kristo ye e, loo Eliya, loo Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ ɛ, kɔ ege loso wuzɛɛkpɛ kuni iiki banima a?”

english_verse: John told them, “I use water to baptize people. But here with you is someone you don't know.
translation_verse: Yohanes ebu le onu si, “Mɛɛkpɛ kuni iiki banima, pɔ̀ ɔnutɔ ɔlɛ ní mlɔ kusi gi mléte e.

english_verse: Even though I came first, I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Yɛ atrába ní mɛ kede, pɔ̀ mégyeno gi maable yɛ afɔkpa ʋa igbé ne e.”

english_verse: John said this as he was baptizing east of the Jordan River in Bethany. The Lamb of God
translation_verse: Eboa la ya petee ɛba la mɛ ní Betania ní Yordan onipe lo oge lɔ kɔlɔ, niklɔ gi Yohanes azaakpɛ kuni yo aki banima ɛ. Yesu yɛ onu Aya Yɛ keʋesibi ya

english_verse: The next day, John saw Jesus coming toward him and said: Here is the Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world!
translation_verse: Keletitia ɛ, Yohanes amɔ Yesu gi atrába yɛ ɔza lɔ ɛ, lɛ apani si, “Mledi imɔ Aya yɛ Keʋesibi ya gi akɔ̀ akɔ si kiakɔ kelea mɛ eboe zimizi na sɛnɔ ɛ.

english_verse: He is the one I told you about when I said, “Someone else will come, who is greater than I am, because he was alive before I was born.”
translation_verse: Yɛ onu ɔniliɛ klɔ su gi mapani si, ‘Onyime ɔtɔ atrába ní mɛ kede e, gi ekeme asani mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ɔlɛ xe ableke bɛpɔ mɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: I didn't know who he was. But I came to baptize you with water, so that everyone in Israel would see him.
translation_verse: Mɛ ɔtutɔ méte ɔniliɛ klɔ gi onu u, pɔ̀ kidiliɛ loso gi maba si míkpɛ kuni iiki banima ɛ, kɛ kinu si tɔ mikɔ ebu ye basɛ Israel banɛ ma.”

english_verse: I was there and saw the Spirit come down on him like a dove from heaven. And the Spirit stayed on him.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yohanes adra Yesu su si, “Mamɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi eeple ŋwisi abronuma kinɛ ɛ, dɔni lifunu ne lɛ eze yɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Before this I didn't know who he was. But the one who sent me to baptize with water had told me, “You will see the Spirit come down and stay on someone. Then you will know that he is the one who will baptize with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Nya mátrate yɛ, pɔ̀ Aya a, gi yɛ akpɛ mɛ si mikpɛ kuni yo aki banima ɛ, apani asi mɛ si, ‘Wɔtramɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi eeple dɔ onyime ɔtɔ aʋa ɛ; yɛ onu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aakɔ̀ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ kpɛ kuni kí banima a.’

english_verse: I saw this happen, and I tell you that he is the Son of God. The First Disciples of Jesus
translation_verse: Lɛ Yohanes apani si, Mamɔ lɛ, lɛ mɛɛdra lɛ mɛ ɛsi mlɔ si yɛ onu Aya Yebinyime e.” Yesu Yɛ badɔkpasi tɔpu ba

english_verse: The next day, John was there again, and two of his followers were with him.
translation_verse: Agi kelea kɛti i, Yohanes alɛ ní klɔ dzɛ nì yɛ badɔkpasi tiaʋa,

english_verse: When he saw Jesus walking by, he said, “Here is the Lamb of God!”
translation_verse: Gi amɔ Yesu gi azɛɛza ɛ, apani si, “Mledi, Aya yɛ Keʋesibi ya!”

english_verse: John's two followers heard him, and they went with Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi tiaʋa ba benu yɛ liboe le e, bekuni Yesu kude e.

english_verse: When Jesus turned and saw them, he asked, “What do you want?” They answered, “Rabbi, where do you live?” The Hebrew word “Rabbi” means “Teacher.”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu ekyi xe amɔ si bali ni yɛ kude e, eʋi ba si, “Ege mlɛpɛ ɛ?” Bebu le onu si, “Rabi” Lɛ ese linu si Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ “Nifɔ onu wɔ oze klɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Come and see!” It was already about four o'clock in the afternoon when they went with him and saw where he lived. So they stayed on for the rest of the day.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mlaga di,” bize ŋwisi kemize inyɔ tine me kinɛ ɛ, Lɛ yɛ ni ba bɛtrɛ xe bɛzɛ mɔ niklɔ gi Yesu eze e, lɛ beze ní yɛ su kele gɔgɔa petee e.

english_verse: One of the two men who had heard John and had gone with Jesus was Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Ba mɛ tole gi enu Yohanes yɛ liboe le e, xe eze ni Yesu kude e, yɛ eze Andrea Simon Petro yeneminyime.

english_verse: The first thing Andrew did was to find his brother and tell him, “We have found the Messiah!” The Hebrew word “Messiah” means the same as the Greek word “Christ.”
translation_verse: Lɛ onu klɔ ʋa ɛ, apɛ́ yeneminyime Simon amɔ, lɛ asi yɛ si, “Kwimɔ Mesiya yɛ.” Le ese linu si, “Kristo ye.”

english_verse: Andrew brought his brother to Jesus. And when Jesus saw him, he said, “Simon son of John, you will be called Cephas.” This name can be translated as “Peter.” Jesus Chooses Philip and Nathanael
translation_verse: Lɛ Andrea agbani Simon amani Yesu i. Yesu edi ye lɛ apani si, “Wɔ linyí linu si Simon, Yohanes Yebi, pɔ̀ biaxwa wɔ si Kefas.” Gi lɛ ese linu si Petro gi lɛ ese ní si Hela sɛ mɛ linu “liklakpɔ ɔ.” Yesu axwa Filipo ani Natanael

english_verse: Philip then found Nathanael and said, “We have found the one that Moses and the Prophets wrote about. He is Jesus, the son of Joseph from Nazareth.”
translation_verse: Filipo amɔ Natanael lɛ asi yɛ si, “Kwimɔ ɔniliɛ klɔ su gi Mose aŋwlimi akpɛ ní Ɔsã nɔ keplekpaa mɛ ɛ, xe Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba fɛ bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ. Yɛ onu Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ, Yosef yebinyime.”

english_verse: Nathanael asked, “Can anything good come from Nazareth?” Philip answered, “Come and see.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Natanael eʋi si, “Kidɔ gbã ɛtɔ kíatani kudɔ ní Nazaret na a?” Lɛ Filipo ebu le onu si, “Ga ba tɔ ga di,”

english_verse: When Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him, he said, “Here is a true descendant of our ancestor Israel. And he isn't deceitful.”
translation_verse: Agi Yesu amɔ Natanael gi atrába yɛ klɔ ɛ, apani yɛ su si, “Israel obi ɔtutɔ iliɛ ya; asia áma yɛ mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: “How do you know me?” Nathanael asked. Jesus answered, “Before Philip called you, I saw you under the fig tree.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Natanael eʋi Yesu si, “Kitɛ mɛ wote me e?” Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mamɔ wɔ gi woze ní ogbose lo ese e, xe ableke Filipo axwa wɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Nathanael said, “Rabbi, you are the Son of God and the King of Israel!”
translation_verse: Natanael ebu le onu si, “Rabi, wɔ́ wonu Aya Yebi ye! Wɔ́ wonu Israel Okusi ye!”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “Did you believe me just because I said that I saw you under the fig tree? You will see something even greater.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu apani si, “Wɔziŋa gi masi wɔ si mamɔ wɔ ní ogbose lo ese e? Wɔtramɔ eboe gi ekeme sani lɛ ya a!”

english_verse: I tell you for certain you will see heaven open and God's angels going up and coming down on the Son of Man.”
translation_verse: Lɛ atrɛ lɛ aʋa si, “Sugba mɛɛsi mlɔ si, mlɛtramɔ lifunu ne si lidra lɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba gi beemu u, xe beeple dɔ ní Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye aʋa a.”

english_verse: Three days later Mary, the mother of Jesus, was at a wedding feast in the village of Cana in Galilee.
translation_verse: Liwetanɛ we le e, kudzɛgbanɔ kwize ní Kana ɔma nɔ mɛ ní Galilea. Yesu yene eze níklɔ,

english_verse: Jesus and his disciples had also been invited and were there.
translation_verse: lɛ bɛxwa Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba fɛ tsinɔ ní ɔdzɛɛ ɔgbanɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: When the wine was all gone, Mary said to Jesus, “They don't have any more wine.”
translation_verse: Agi kudaa kwiblo e, Yesu yene asi yɛ si, “Kudaa kwiblo.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “Mother, my time hasn't yet come! You must not tell me what to do.”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “One ege lɛ loso wɔɛkpɛ mɛ ní lɛ mɛ ɛ?” “Mɛ lipoe le liʋína sukɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Mary then said to the servants, “Do whatever Jesus tells you to do.”
translation_verse: Yesu yene asi besumubi ba si, “Liboe kakaa gi aasi mlɔ ɛ, mlabitɛ ní lɛ aʋa.”

english_verse: At the feast there were six stone water jars that were used by the people for washing themselves in the way that their religion said they must. Each jar held about
translation_verse: Bedu akla izɔ tiglo níklɔ kí kuni gi kɔsu axwɛ Yudanɛ ma bizɛwa ní ba kusuploo ligoʋi le me e, lɛ kuni galɔn avi taʋa tsini avi tata kuzeku ní ɔzɔ kakaa mɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told the servants to fill them to the top with water. Then after the jars had been filled,
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi besumubi ba si, “Mlakpɛ kuni ní izɔ lɛ mɛ.” Lɛ bɛkpɛ kuni yo ní izɔ lɛ mɛ xe iyi i.

english_verse: he said, “Now take some water and give it to the man in charge of the feast.” The servants did as Jesus told them,
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Abla ɛ, mlesi kutɔ atsini bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ ɔʋadi ye.” Besi ko ɛtsini yɛ,

english_verse: and the man in charge drank some of the water that had now turned into wine. He did not know where the wine had come from, but the servants did. He called the bridegroom over
translation_verse: lɛ gi bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ ɔʋadi ye akpɛ kuni uliɔ gi kwikyi dzi wain yɛ onu edi i. Yɛ óte niklɔ gi wain yɛ ádɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ besumubia ba gi ba besi kuni yo kɔ bete e. Lɛ ebu ɔdzɛnyee kuka,

english_verse: and said, “The best wine is always served first. Then after the guests have had plenty, the other wine is served. But you have kept the best until last!”
translation_verse: lɛ asi yɛ si, “Kuda gbã ɔnɔ kakaa izɛ tu kɔ ní ɔdzɛ ɔgbanɔ kɔ ɔ, kɔ xegi banɔxwaxwa ba bɛŋwɛ ɛ xe kwiimu ba ɛ, kɔ ableke bɛmani kuliɔ gi kúpɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ wɔkɔ kuda gbã ɔbla gakɔna ni abla a!”

english_verse: But Jesus was talking about his body as a temple.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Lisɔligba ɛ lɛ su gi Yesu azɛɛpani i, lɛ lize yɛ ɔnɔ se lo.

english_verse: Not long before the Jewish festival of Passover, Jesus went to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Agi Yudanɛ ma Kutseolidɔzawe ŋaŋa nɛ lihana ɛ, Yesu atrɛ ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: So he took some rope and made a whip. Then he chased everyone out of the temple, together with their sheep and cattle. He turned over the tables of the moneychangers and scattered their coins.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Yesu aaʋì igbé lɛ akɔ lɛ akpikpini baga ʋa petee dono ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, iʋesi ni egume; ehle babɔtsrɛ ba bibɔ ʋɛ lɛ ekyi ba ikplɔ nɛ ewe e;

english_verse: The disciples then remembered that the Scriptures say, “My love for your house burns in me like a fire.”
translation_verse: Yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛplɛ aŋu si bɛŋwlimi kpɛ kuŋwlimi dradraa mɛ si, “Aya, isu kukɔkyɛ kí wɔ kepea ɛ, kwiisɔ ní mɛ mɛ̀ ŋwisi kifu kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: “Destroy this temple,” Jesus answered, “and in three days I will build it again!”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mlayaʋi Lisɔligba ɛ lɛ ya, maadzini le kuwa ní ewe tata mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: There he found people selling cattle, sheep, and doves in the temple. He also saw moneychangers sitting at their tables.
translation_verse: Ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, amɔ banima gi bɛzɛda egume, iʋesi ni abronuma ʋa ani banɔatɔ gi bɛzɛtsrɛ kibɔ ɛ, gi bedi ní ba ikplɔ nɛ su i.

english_verse: Jesus said to the people who had been selling doves, “Get those doves out of here! Don't make my Father's house a marketplace.”
translation_verse: lɛ akpɛ ɔsã akí banama gi bɛɛda abronuma ʋa si, “Mlebu bidɔ bɛ ya dono ni ya! Mlakíkɔ Mɔka kepea mɛ akpɛ kedzí me e!”

english_verse: The Jewish leaders asked Jesus, “What miracle will you work to show us why you have done this?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ ma bebu le onu ni kuʋiʋi si, “Linugutseboe woli wɔabitɛ gi liabasɛ blɔ si wɔmɔ kigagla si wɔabitɛ eboa la ya na a?”

english_verse: And when he was raised from death, his disciples remembered what he had told them. Then they believed the Scriptures and the words of Jesus. Jesus Knows What People Are Like
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Aya ayɔni yɛ adɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛplɛ aŋu si Yesu apani liboe lɛ ya aplɛ lɛ bɛzi kuŋwlimi dradra ni liboe lɛ klɔ gi Yesu apani aplɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ. Yesu ete Ɔnɔ gbagbe yɛ Kizeze me

english_verse: In Jerusalem during Passover many people put their faith in Jesus, because they saw him work miracles.
translation_verse: Agi Yesu eze ní Yerusalem ní Kutseolidɔzawe ŋaŋa nɛ mɛ ɛ, banɔ glegle bɛzi yɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa lɛ ese linu si bɛmɔ enugutseboe ala gi abitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But Jesus knew what was in their hearts, and he would not let them have power over him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́yurɔ isu ɔki ba a, lɛ ese linu si ete ɔnɔ gbagbe kakaa.

english_verse: No one had to tell him what people were like. He already knew.
translation_verse: Lɛ líziã si ɔnɔnɔ ɔpani ní basu ɔki yɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔtutɔ ete kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: There was a man named Nicodemus who was a Pharisee and a Jewish leader.
translation_verse: Ɔka ɔtɔ eze gi yɛ linyí linu Nikodemo o, ádɔ Farisianɛ ma lihakpɔ lɛ mɛ lɛ eze Yudanɛ ɔka tole.

english_verse: One night he went to Jesus and said, “Rabbi, we know that God has sent you to teach us. You could not work these miracles, unless God were with you.”
translation_verse: Ní elesi atɔ mɛ ɛ, Nikodemo atrɛ ní Yesu klɔ lɛ asi yɛ sì, “Rabi, kwite si wonu ɔdɔbasɛ gi ádɔ ní Aya kɔ ɛ. Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ma enugutseboe ala gi wɔɛbitɛ ɛ, kubitɛ mɛ xe Aya ɔ́ma ni yɛ kude e.”

english_verse: Jesus replied, “I tell you for certain that you must be born from above before you can see God's kingdom!”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mɛɛsi wɔ anukwarɛɛ: ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ kuze sani si bedzini ye kupɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Nicodemus asked, “How can a grown man ever be born a second time?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Nikodemo eʋi Yesu si, “Kitɛ mɛ biadzini ɔnɔtsitsi kupɔ na a? Ɔ́tratani kudzi iku yene lipo le me kuʋla a, xe biadzini ye kupɔ ɔ!”

english_verse: Jesus answered: I tell you for certain that before you can get into God's kingdom, you must be born not only by water, but by the Spirit.
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mɛɛsi wɔ anukwarɛɛ, Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kutiku Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ, xe bápɔ yɛ gani kuni ni Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Humans give life to their children. Yet only God's Spirit can change you into a child of God.
translation_verse: Bapɔnɛ ba bizɛpɔ ɔnɔ ní kisugya mɛ, pɔ̀ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛpɔ ɔnɔ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't be surprised when I say that you must be born from above.
translation_verse: Likítu wo kugyɔ gi masi wɔ si lilɛ si bedzini wɔ apɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Only God's Spirit gives new life. The Spirit is like the wind that blows wherever it wants to. You can hear the wind, but you don't know where it comes from or where it is going.
translation_verse: Liwɔ lɛ lizeefu itrɛ niklɔ gi lipɛ ɛ; wɔanu le onu pɔ̀ wɔtrate lɛ ɔdɔ kɔ loo lɛ ɔtrɛ kɔ ɔ. Nite ke lilɛ kí ɔnɔ kakaa gi bɛpɔ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: “How can this be?” Nicodemus asked.
translation_verse: Lɛ Nikodemo eʋi Yesu si, “Kitɛ liaba lɛ mɛ nite e?”

english_verse: Jesus replied: How can you be a teacher of Israel and not know these things?
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Wònu ɔdɔbasɛ ʋidi ní Israel lɛ wónu la ya ese a?”

english_verse: I tell you for certain we know what we are talking about because we have seen it ourselves. But none of you will accept what we say.
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi wɔ anukwarɛɛ; kuzɛɛpani ní kidiliɛ gi kwite su u, lɛ kuzɛɛpani ní kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ su u, pɔ̀ mlɔ mɛ ɔ́tɔtɔ ɔ́pɛ si yizi blɔ liboe me dradra lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you don't believe when I talk to you about things on earth, how can you possibly believe if I talk to you about things in heaven?
translation_verse: Mlázi mɛ aʋa ŋa a, gi mɛɛsi mlɔ kelea lia mɛ eboe la a; kitɛ mlaazi mɛ aʋa ŋa a, xe masi mlɔ eboe la gi ɛɛtrɛ la aʋa ní lifunu ne e?

english_verse: No one has gone up to heaven except the Son of Man, who came down from there.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ómu ɔ́trɛ ní lifunu ne oʋídi i, sani Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye gi yɛ ádɔ lifunu ne aba ɛ.

english_verse: And the Son of Man must be lifted up, just as the metal snake was lifted up by Moses in the desert.
translation_verse: Lɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Mose emuni ɔlaɛ ɔgblaga yɛ, gi bɛxla ní ose lo aʋa ní ɔyaya klɔ kinɛ ɛ, nite ké lilɛ si bemuni Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye,

english_verse: Then everyone who has faith in the Son of Man will have eternal life.
translation_verse: tɔ ɔnɔ kakaa gi aazi yɛ aʋa ŋa ɛ, aamɔ ligbã́ moblo le.

english_verse: God loved the people of this world so much that he gave his only Son, so that everyone who has faith in him will have eternal life and never really die.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya alɔ ɔnɔ gbagbe ní kesea aʋa gbɛgbɛ nite si akɔ yebinyime tolee akɔ si ɔnɔ kakaa gi aazi yɛ aʋa ŋa ɛ, ɔ́tratse e, pɔ̀ aamɔ ligbã́ moblo le e.

english_verse: God did not send his Son into the world to condemn its people. He sent him to save them!
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya ɔ́tsisi yebinyime ní kelea mɛ banima kɔ si obu ba lipɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ tɔ yibu ba gani yɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: No one who has faith in God's Son will be condemned. But everyone who doesn't have faith in him has already been condemned for not having faith in God's only Son.
translation_verse: Banɔaklɔ gi bɛzi Obi ye aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ, Aya ɔ́traxwa litsi ni ba a, xe aabu ba lipɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ banama gi bázɛ ŋa ɛ, Aya ebu ba lipɔ koko lɛ ese linu si bázɛ Aya yebinyime tolee linyi ne aʋa ɛɛŋa a.

english_verse: The light has come into the world, and people who do evil things are judged guilty because they love the dark more than the light.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ litsixwaxwa lɛ lɛ ya: kuklekleo kwiba kelea mɛ dɔni lifunu ne, pɔ̀ banima bɛdimɛ ni litumu ne sani kuklekleo, lɛ ese linu si ba kiboebitɛɛ kinu kuzimizi i.

english_verse: People who do evil hate the light and won't come to the light, because it clearly shows what they have done.
translation_verse: Banama petee gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ, baʋídimɛ ni kuklekle lɛ bátraba kuklekleo onu u, lɛ ese linu si bátrapɛ si ba eboe zimizi na edo oŋwe e.

english_verse: But everyone who lives by the truth will come to the light, because they want others to know that God is really the one doing what they do. Jesus and John the Baptist
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banama gi bizɛɛga ní anukwarɛɛ aʋa ɛ, bizɛba kuklekleo onu tɔ kuklekleo kubasɛ si kidiliɛ gi bɛbitɛ ɛ, bɛbitɛ kɛ ní Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu. Yohanes Kuni yo Ɔkpɛkpɛɛ adra Yesu su

english_verse: Later, Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, where he stayed with them for a while and was baptizing people.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, Yesu ni yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛtrɛ ní Yudea kesea mɛ, eze ní basu niklɔ lipoetɔ onu lɛ akpɛ kuni aki banima ɛ.

english_verse: John's followers got into an argument with a Jewish man about a ceremony of washing.
translation_verse: Lɛ kuboeŋwlɛ kwiku ní Yohanes yɛ badɔkpasi batɔ ni Yudanɛ ɔtɔ kusi ní kusuplo ligoʋi le su u.

english_verse: They went to John and said, “Rabbi, you spoke about a man when you were with him east of the Jordan. He is now baptizing people, and everyone is going to him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛtrɛ ní Yohanes klɔ lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Rabi, dani wɔplɛ aŋu ní onyime liɛ klɔ gi eze ní wɔ su ní Yordan kedea aʋa ɛ, iliɛ klɔ su gi wɔpani i? Ɛɛkpɛ kuni iiki banima abla, lɛ banima pɔ́ bɛɛtrɛ ní yɛ klɔ ɔ!”

english_verse: John replied: No one can do anything unless God in heaven allows it.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yohanes ebu le onu si, “Kididɔ kítratani ɔnɔ awla kuze e, xe aní Aya aki yɛ kɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You surely remember how I told you that I am not the Messiah. I am only the one sent ahead of him.
translation_verse: Mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlɔ aŋu mɛ mapani si, ‘Aní mɛ menu Kristo ye e, pɔ̀ kikpɛ Aya akpɛ mɛ ɔtɔ aki yɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: At a wedding the groom is the one who gets married. The best man is glad just to be there and to hear the groom's voice. That's why I am so glad.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ onu ɔdzɛgbanɔɛ yɛ ɔnɛ; pɔ̀ ɔdzɛgbanɔɛ yɛʋiɛ gi izeepe ye e, xe izɛɛta yɛ kutu i, isami izeku ye xe enu ɔdzɛgbanɔɛ yɛ ole lo e. Isami nɛ klɔ inu mɛ inɛ, lɛ abla ɛ, iflɔ.

english_verse: Jesus must become more important, while I become less important. The One Who Comes from Heaven
translation_verse: Li lɛ si yɛ omu ɔsani mɛ tɔ mɛ pɔ̀ ɛ, miple.” Yesu onu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ádɔ lifunu ne

english_verse: God's Son comes from heaven and is above all others. Everyone who comes from the earth belongs to the earth and speaks about earthly things. The one who comes from heaven is above all others.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ádɔ lifunu ne e, ekeme asani ɔnɔ kakaa. Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ádɔ kesea ɛ, yɛ onu kesea ɔnɛ ɛ, lɛ izeegu kunugu yo ní kesea eboe la su u, pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ádɔ lifunu ne e, asani ɔnɔ kakaa.

english_verse: He speaks about what he has seen and heard, and yet no one believes him.
translation_verse: Izɛɛpani ní kidiliɛ gi amɔ ɔ, xe enu su u, pɔ̀ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ʋizi yɛ liboe le e.

english_verse: But everyone who does believe him has shown that God is truthful.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi azɛ yɛ liboe le e, atrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si Aya onu anukwarɛɛ onete.

english_verse: The Son was sent to speak God's message, and he has been given the full power of God's Spirit.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi Aya akpɛ ɛ, izɛɛpani Aya eboe la, lɛ ese linu si Aya akɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ zizi nɔ aki yɛ.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has given him everything.
translation_verse: Kakaa alɔ Obi ye lɛ akɔ kidɔ kakaa akpɛ ní yɛ awla a.

english_verse: Everyone who has faith in the Son has eternal life. But no one who rejects him will ever share in that life, and God will be angry with them forever.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi azɛ Obi ye aʋa aŋa ɛ, aamɔ ligbã́ moblo le; ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔ́tranu kutu iiki Obi ye e, ɔ́tramɔ ligbã́ a; pɔ̀ aaze ní Aya kutuheheo ese e.

english_verse: But Jesus' disciples were really the ones doing the baptizing, and not Jesus himself. Jesus and the Samaritan Woman
translation_verse: Ni anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ, Yesu ɔtutɔ ɔ́kpɛ kuni ɔ́ki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ; boŋu i, yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛɛkpɛ kuni yo.

english_verse: This time he had to go through Samaria,
translation_verse: Lize si ɔga Samaria kesea mɛ ɔza.

english_verse: “You are a Jew,” she replied, “and I am a Samaritan woman. How can you ask me for a drink of water when Jews and Samaritans won't have anything to do with each other?”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛɛ ebu le onu si, “Wonu Yuda ɔnɛ, lɛ menu Samariadze kítɛmɛ woeʋi me kuni ŋwɛŋwɛ ɛ?” Agi Yudanɛ ma ni Samaria banɛ ma báʋiga eku ba ahanuglɔ ba mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: “Sir,” the woman said, “you don't even have a bucket, and the well is deep. Where are you going to get this life-giving water?
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ apani si, “Ɔka,” “kididɔ kíma wɔ awla gi wɔakɔsi kuni i, lɛ vudo ye edzu u. Nifɔ wɔtradɔni kuni uliɔ gi kuzɛɛkɔ ligbã na a?

english_verse: Our ancestor Jacob dug this well for us, and his family and animals got water from it. Are you greater than Jacob?”
translation_verse: “Blɔ ɔkatsitsi ye Yakob gi yɛ aki blɔ vudo liɛ ya ɛ; yɛ ni yɛ bebinyime ba ani ba baga ʋa pɔ́ bɛŋwɛ kuni dɔni vudo liɛ ya mɛ ɛ. Kido wɔedo si wɔsani Yakob na a?”

english_verse: But no one who drinks the water I give will ever be thirsty again. The water I give will become in that person a flowing fountain that gives eternal life.”
translation_verse: pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi aaŋwɛ kuni uliɔ gi maaki yɛ ɛ, olile ɔ́traku ye tɔ ɔ. Sugba, kuni uliɔ gi maaki yɛ ɛ, kwiadzi linitɔ ní yɛ mɛ gi liaki yɛ ligbã moblo o.”

english_verse: The woman replied, “Sir, please give me a drink of that water! Then I won't get thirsty and have to come to this well again.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛɛ apani si, “Ɔka, kimɛ kuni uliɔ klɔ! Atɔ olile okíku me tɔ ɔ, tɔ mikíba kuni osi kɔ ní ya tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus told her, “Go and bring your husband.”
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Trɛ tɔ zɛɛxwa wonye tɔ mlaba ni ya.”

english_verse: The woman said, “Sir, I can see that you are a prophet.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛɛ apani si, “Ɔka mamɔ si Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ wonu.”

english_verse: My ancestors worshiped on this mountain, but you Jews say Jerusalem is the only place to worship.”
translation_verse: “Blɔ bakatsi Samaria banɛ ma bezeesumu ní litoe lɛ ya aʋa, pɔ̀ mlɔ Yudanɛ ma kɔ mlɔ si Yerusalem onu niklɔ gi kwiasumu u.”

english_verse: Jesus said to her: Believe me, the time is coming when you won't worship the Father either on this mountain or in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Ɔdzɛ, zi mɛ liboe le aʋa ŋa, lipoe le litrába gi banima bátrasumu Aya ní litoe lɛ ya aʋa a, loo ní Yerusalem tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: You Samaritans don't really know the one you worship. But we Jews do know the God we worship, and by using us, God will save the world.
translation_verse: Mlɔ Samaria banɛ ma ɛ, mléte ɔniliɛ tututu gi yɛ mleesumu u; pɔ̀ blɔ Yudanɛ ma kɔ kwite ɔniliɛ gi kwisumu i, lɛ ese linu si Yudanɛ ma klɔ kubúbú yo kwidɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But a time is coming, and it is already here! Even now the true worshipers are being led by the Spirit to worship the Father according to the truth. These are the ones the Father is seeking to worship him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lipoe le litraba lɛ liba koko o gi banama a gi besumu Aya ní yɛ kudeo onu tututu i, biasumu ye ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ ni anukwarɛɛ mɛ, lɛ ese linu si banɔsumu ba ya elu Ɔkaɛ ɛɛpɛ.

english_verse: God is Spirit, and those who worship God must be led by the Spirit to worship him according to the truth.
translation_verse: Aya onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ, lɛ gani Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigaglaɛ mɛ ko banima biatani yɛ kusumu ní yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ onu u.”

english_verse: The woman said, “I know that the Messiah will come. He is the one we call Christ. When he comes, he will explain everything to us.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛɛ asi yɛ si, “Mete si Mesiya yɛ gi bizɛxwa si Kristo ye e, atrába, lɛ xe aba ɛ, aabu liboe kakaa mɛ ɔki blɔ.”

english_verse: “I am that one,” Jesus told her, “and I am speaking to you now.”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Mɛ gi mɛ ni wɔ kwigu kunugu yo e, mɛ menu ye.” Yesu badɔkpasi ba bedziba

english_verse: The disciples returned about this time and were surprised to find Jesus talking with a woman. But none of them asked him what he wanted or why he was talking with her.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi ba bedziba, lɛ litu ba kugyɔ gi bɛmɔ si eegu kunugu yo ni ɔdzɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ bamɛ ɔtɔtɔ óʋi si, “Ege wɔ ɛ pɛ?” loo ʋi ye si “Ege lɛ loso woegu kunugu yo ni ɔdzɛɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: The woman left her water jar and ran back into town, where she said to the people,
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔdzɛɛ ayurɔ yɛ kenifukpoa edu i, xe edzi atrɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ lɛ asi banima si,

english_verse: “Come and see a man who told me everything I have ever done! Could he be the Messiah?”
translation_verse: “Mlagadi ɔnyime ɔtɔ gi asi mɛ kidɔ kakaa ɛ liɛ gi mabitɛ edi i. Yɛ aaze Kristo ye na a?”

english_verse: Everyone in town went out to see Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛsɛ ɔma nɔ mɛ lɛ bɛba Yesu klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: While this was happening, Jesus' disciples were saying to him, “Teacher, please eat something.”
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ ya te me e, yɛ badɔkpasi ba bɛki yɛ litukpo si, “Ɔdɔbasɛ yɛ, ŋa kiditɔ!”

english_verse: But Jesus told them, “I have food you don't know anything about.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ebu le onu si, “Kiŋaŋa kiliɛ mɛ awla gi mlete liboeboe kɛ su u.”

english_verse: His disciples started asking each other, “Has someone brought him something to eat?”
translation_verse: Liki si badɔkpasi ba beʋi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Putɔ ɔnutɔ amani yɛ kiŋaŋa na a?”

english_verse: Jesus said: My food is to do what God wants! He is the one who sent me, and I must finish the work that he gave me to do.
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, “Mɛ kiŋaŋaɛ kɛ kinu si mibitɛ ní ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa; atɔ migù axwɛ ana gi aki mɛ si miwa ɛ.

english_verse: You may say there are still four months until harvest time. But I tell you to look, and you will see that the fields are ripe and ready to harvest.
translation_verse: Dani mlɛzɛpani si, ‘Bigɔ badzidzi tiene kɔ kiŋaŋaɛ kiku kepe e?’ Pɔ̀ mɛɛsi mlɔ si, mlevu aŋu ní inyrɔ nɛ su mɔmɔmɔ, mlaamɔ si bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ bitsì gi biamani kepe e!

english_verse: Even now the harvest workers are receiving their reward by gathering a harvest that brings eternal life. Then everyone who planted the seed and everyone who harvests the crop will celebrate together.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛmani kiŋaŋaɛ kepe abla ɛ, bɛɛtsa liʋɛ ki yɛ, lɛ eexogo ɔnyrɔ nɔ aʋa bidɔ mɛ ɛki ligbã́ moblo le e; lɛ loso ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ bidɔ mɛ ni ɔniliɛ gi eexogo bidɔ mɛ ɛ, ba pɔ́ biamɔ isami i.

english_verse: So the saying proves true, “Some plant the seed, and others harvest the crop.”
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi bizɛpani i, lilɛ lɛ mɛ si, ‘Ɔnutɔ izɛɛkpɛ, kɔ ɔnɔ dzedze exogo e.’

english_verse: I am sending you to harvest crops in fields where others have done all the hard work.
translation_verse: Makpɛ mlɔ si mlexogo bidɔ ní niklɔ gi mlépe e; banɔ dzedze ba bɛwa axwɛ gagla la, lɛ mlɛmɔ libi ibú ní ba axwɛwawa lɛ mɛ ɛ.” Samaria banɛ glegle bɛzi iŋa

english_verse: A lot of Samaritans in that town put their faith in Jesus because the woman had said, “This man told me everything I have ever done.”
translation_verse: Samaria banɛ glegle ni ɔma lɔ klɔ mɛ ɛ, bɛzi Yesu aʋa ɛɛŋa lɛ ese linu si ɔdzɛɛ adra isu mɛ si, “Asi mɛ kidɔ kakaa gi mabitɛ edi i.”

english_verse: They came and asked him to stay in their town, and he stayed on for two days.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso gi Samaria banɛ ma bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ, bɛki yɛ litukpo si oze ní ba su, lɛ Yesu eze ni ba su ewe taʋa a.

english_verse: Many more Samaritans put their faith in Jesus because of what they heard him say.
translation_verse: Ɔkpɛ yɛ eboe la loso e, banɔ glegle sanɔ bɛzi ŋa.

english_verse: They told the woman, “We no longer have faith in Jesus just because of what you told us. We have heard him ourselves, and we are certain that he is the Savior of the world!” Jesus Heals an Official's Son (Matthew
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi ɔdzɛɛ si, “Aní liboe lɛ gi wɔpani i, lɛ liki si kwizi ŋa tɔ ɔ, blɔ ɔtutɔ kwinu yɛ liboe le, lɛ kwite si ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, onyime liɛ onu Ɔnɔbu ye kí kelea a.” Yesu Ayɔni Kekusipe me Ɔka ɔtɔ Yebinyime

english_verse: Jesus had said, “Prophets are honored everywhere, except in their own country.” Then two days later he left
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔtutɔ apani si, “Kubùbù kuʋíze ní Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ su u, ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kepea.”

english_verse: and went to Galilee. The people there welcomed him, because they had gone to the festival in Jerusalem and had seen everything he had done.
translation_verse: Agi eku Galilea ɛ, Galileanɛ ma bɛkpɛ yɛ wolaa lɛ ese linu si bɛdzɛ Kutseolidɔzawe le ɔŋa kɔ ní Yerusalem lɛ bɛmɔ kidɔ kakaa gi abitɛ ní liwe le me e.

english_verse: While Jesus was in Galilee, he returned to the village of Cana, where he had turned the water into wine. There was an official in Capernaum whose son was sick.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu edzi atrɛ ní Kanaa ní Galilea dzɛ ɛ, niklɔ gi ekyi kuni yo xe kwidzi kuda ɛ. Kekusipea me ɔka ɔtɔ eze ní klɔ gi yebinyime azeetse e ní Kapernaum.

english_verse: And when the man heard that Jesus had come from Judea, he went and begged him to keep his son from dying.
translation_verse: Gi enu si Yesu ádɔ Yudea aba Galilea ɛ, atrɛ yɛ klɔ lɛ aki yɛ litukpo si ɔba tɔ ɔga yɔni yebinyime, gi ihatse e.

english_verse: Jesus told the official, “You won't have faith unless you see miracles and wonders!”
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Xe mlámɔ enugutseboe ni eboe gi etu mlo kugyɔ ɛ, mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́trazi iŋa liwewe.”

english_verse: The man replied, “Lord, please come before my son dies!”
translation_verse: Kekusipea mɛ ɔkaɛ ebu le onu si, “Ɔka, bá tɔ kutrɛ xe mɛ obi ye aatse e.”

english_verse: Jesus then said, “Your son will live. Go on home to him.” The man believed Jesus and started back home.
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Trɛ, wobinyimee aaze ligbã́!” Onyimee azɛ Yesu liboe le aʋa ŋa lɛ asɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some of the official's servants met him along the road and told him, “Your son is better!”
translation_verse: Gi eze kudeo aʋa ɛ, yɛ besumubi ba bɛtsani yɛ ni liboe gbã ɛ nɛ si, “Wobinyimee ɔlɛ ligbã́!”

english_verse: He asked them when the boy got better, and they answered, “The fever left him yesterday at one o'clock.”
translation_verse: Gi eʋi ba lipoe lɛ klɔ gi yebinyime ayɔ ɛ, bebu le onu si, “Kuweo kwiyurɔ yɛ kivoe kemizea ɔnyɔ tole e.”

english_verse: The boy's father realized that at one o'clock the day before, Jesus had told him, “Your son will live!” So the man and everyone in his family put their faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔkaɛ aplɛ aŋu si, lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɖii Yesu asi yɛ si, “Wobi ye aaze ligbã.” Lɛ loso e, yɛ ni yɛ kepea pɔ́ bɛzi ŋa.

english_verse: This was the second miracle that Jesus worked after he left Judea and went to Galilee.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya lize linugutseboe ʋla gi Yesu abitɛ gi ádɔ Yudea a xe aba Galilea a.

english_verse: Later, Jesus went to Jerusalem for another Jewish festival.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, Yesu atrɛ Yerusalem kí Yudanɛ ma liwe ŋaŋa ɛtɔ.

english_verse: In the city near the sheep gate was a pool with five porches, and its name in Hebrew was Bethzatha.
translation_verse: Kevunu katɔ kalɛ ní Yerusalem gi bizɛxwa si Iʋesi kevunu u. Kuni limu itɔ gi bizɛxwa si Betesda ní si Hebri se me e, litipɔni kevunu ya lia klɔ, lɛ bɛwa akatsa tetsu i klani limu ne e.

english_verse: Beside the pool was a man who had been sick for
translation_verse: Onyime ɔtɔ eze níklɔ gi azɛɛnya agɔ avitata ni ɔgɔtaʋa ɛ.

english_verse: You have not believed his message, because you refused to have faith in the one he sent.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlákpɛ yɛ liboe le ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlázi ɔniliɛ gi yɛ akpɛ ɛ, aʋa ɛɛŋa.

english_verse: When Jesus saw the man and realized that he had been crippled for a long time, he asked him, “Do you want to be healed?”
translation_verse: Gi Yesu amɔ yɛ gi ɔfini klɔ ɛ, xe bɛsi yɛ si onyimee ɔfini níklɔ lipoe gblamadza lɛ ya ɛ; eʋi ye si, “Wɔpɛ si wuyɔ na a?”

english_verse: The man answered, “Sir, I don't have anyone to put me in the pool when the water is stirred up. I try to get in, but someone else always gets there first.”
translation_verse: Ɔnyanyaɛ ebu le onu si, “Ɔka, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ma gi aapɔni mɛ xe maapleku ní limu ne me e, xe bɛpisa kuni yo o; lipoe lɛ klɔ gi meekeni idi si miple eku i, kɔ ɔnutɔ atsã mɛ onu i.”

english_verse: Jesus told him, “Pick up your mat and walk!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi yɛ si, “Yɔ, Kɔ wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ tɔ ga!”

english_verse: Right then the man was healed. He picked up his mat and started walking around. The day on which this happened was a Sabbath.
translation_verse: Onyimee ayɔ kyɔŋ, akɔ yɛ ɔtɔsi lɔ lɛ ekpese kuga ɛ. “Liwe lɛ gi kibitɛ liɛ ya kiba kɛ mɛ ɛ, lize Lizemɔniwe.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders saw the man carrying his mat, they said to him, “This is the Sabbath! No one is allowed to carry a mat on the Sabbath.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ baka ba bɛsi onyimee gi bɛyɔnɔ ɛ si, Omono onu Lizemɔniwe, lɛ liyɔ kedu ní blɔ Ɔsã nɔ su si tu wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ.”

english_verse: But he replied, “The man who healed me told me to pick up my mat and walk.”
translation_verse: Ebu le onu si, “Ɔniliɛ gi ayɔni mɛ ɛ, yɛ asi mɛ si, ‘Kɔ wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ tɔ ga.’ ”

english_verse: They asked him, “Who is this man that told you to pick up your mat and walk?”
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi ye si, “Nyaŋwɛ onu onyimee gi yɛ asi wɔ si, kɔ lɔ tɔ ga a?”

english_verse: But he did not know who Jesus was, and Jesus had left because of the crowd.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ onyimee gi bɛyɔnɔ ɛ, óte ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ayɔni yɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si, Yesu alila eku linɔkpi le gi lize niklɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Later, Jesus met the man in the temple and told him, “You are now well. But don't sin anymore or something worse might happen to you.”
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yesu amɔ yɛ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ lɛ asi yɛ si, “Dike, wɔyɔ abla, lɛ loso dzini wɔ eboe zimizi na kumɔni i, xe aní te e, liboe nyani nyani sanɔ liaba wɔ aʋa.”

english_verse: The man left and told the leaders that Jesus was the one who had healed him.
translation_verse: Lɛ onyimee asɛ ɛ xe azɛɛsi Yudanɛ baka ba si, Yesu yɛ ayɔni yɛ ɛ. Obi ye yɛ izɛki ligbã́

english_verse: They started making a lot of trouble for Jesus because he did things like this on the Sabbath.
translation_verse: Lɛ bekpese kedu kuyɔ ní Yesu su i, lɛ ese linu si ayɔni ɔnɔ ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa a.

english_verse: But Jesus said, “My Father has never stopped working, and this is why I keep on working.”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu aki ba si, “Mɔka ɛɛwa axwɛ na lipoe kakaa, lɛ loso mɛfɛ mɛɛwa axwɛ na a.”

english_verse: Now the leaders wanted to kill Jesus for two reasons. First, he had broken the law of the Sabbath. But even worse, he had said God was his Father, which made him equal with God. The Son's Authority
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya loso e, Yudanɛ baka ba bɛtrɔ lɛ si biaye ye kikakaa elu e; aní si ezizi Lizemɔniwe le ɔsã nɔ tete e, pɔ̀ apani si Aya onu yɛ ɔtutɔ Yɛka a, lɛ ɛɛkɔ isu i srɔni Aya si ba ni yɛ bɛsɔ.

english_verse: Jesus told the people: I tell you for certain the Son cannot do anything on his own. He can do only what he sees the Father doing, and he does exactly what he sees the Father do.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu ebu le onu aki ba si, “Mɛɛsi mlɔ anukwarɛɛ; Obi ye ɔ́tratani kididɔ kubitɛ ní yɛ isuwla mɛ ɛ; izɛɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi amɔ si Yɛka ɛɛbitɛ ɛ. Kidiliɛ gi Ɔka abitɛ ɛ, kɛ Obi ye fɛ izɛɛbitɛ.

english_verse: The Father loves the Son and has shown him everything he does. The Father will show him even greater things, and you will be amazed.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔka yɛ adimɛ ni Obi ye lɛ abasi yɛ kidɔ kakaa gi yɛ ɔtutɔ ɛɛbitɛ ɛ. Aabasɛ yɛ eboe gi ekeme sani lɛ ya, lɛ mlɔ pɔ́ mlɔ inugu aatse e.

english_verse: Just as the Father raises the dead and gives life, so the Son gives life to anyone he wants to.
