translation_verse: Lɛ badɔkpasi gɔgɔ ba bɛsi yɛ si, “Kwimɔ Ɔkaɛ!” Toma asi ba si, “Xegi mámɔ kutakpagyeʋio bebikpo ʋa ní yɛ kuwla mɛ ɛ, xe mákpɛ mɛ kiwlaʋiɛ ní bamɛ ɛ, xe mákpɛ mɛ awla la ní yɛ opoga nɔ mɛ ɛ, mátrazi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa a.”

english_verse: A week later the disciples were together again. This time, Thomas was with them. Jesus came in while the doors were still locked and stood in the middle of the group. He greeted his disciples
translation_verse: Ogle tole kede, gi badɔkpasi ba bɛkpɛ likpɔ dzɛ ní ligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, xe Toma kyɛ eze ba kusi i. Gi bɛda ipupo le kyɛ ɛ, Yesu aba ɔlɛɛ ní ba kusi lɛ apani ba si, “Kusuyɔ ki mlɔ ɔ!”

english_verse: and said to Thomas, “Put your finger here and look at my hands! Put your hand into my side. Stop doubting and have faith!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi Toma si, “Kɔ wɔ kiwlaʋiɛ kpɛ ní ya, tɔ di mɛ kuwlaa mɛ; tɔ nina wɔ awla la kpɛ ní mɛ opoga nɔ mɛ. Atɔ mɔni wɔ eɖikeke la tɔ ziŋa!”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Thomas, do you have faith because you have seen me? The people who have faith in me without seeing me are the ones who are really blessed!” Why John Wrote His Book
translation_verse: Yesu asi yɛ si, “Gi wɔmɔ mɛ ɛ, dani wɔzi ŋa ɛ? Biakrira banama gi bámɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ xe bɛzi ŋa ɛ!”

english_verse: Jesus worked many other miracles for his disciples, and not all of them are written in this book.
translation_verse: Ní yɛ badɔkpasi ba aŋu mɛ ɛ, Yesu abitɛ enugutseboe glegle dzedze gi báŋwlimi akpɛ ní keplekpaa lia ya mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But these are written so that you will put your faith in Jesus as the Messiah and the Son of God. If you have faith in him, you will have true life.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bɛŋwlimi la ya tɔ mlaakɔ la kuzi ŋa si Yesu yɛ onu Kristo ye, Aya Yebi ye, lɛ gani mlɔ kuziŋa ní yɛ linyi ne me e, mlamɔ ligbã́.

english_verse: Jesus later appeared to his disciples along the shore of Lake Tiberias.
translation_verse: Ewe atɔ kede e, Yesu ebu isu abasi yɛ badɔkpasi ba dzɛ ní Tiberia limu ne su. Liba lɛ mɛ nite:

english_verse: Simon Peter, Thomas the Twin, Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, and the brothers James and John, were there, together with two other disciples.
translation_verse: Simon Petro, ni Toma gi bizɛɛxwa si tiaʋa obi ye e, ni Natanael gi ádɔ Kanaa ní Galilea ɛ, ni Zebedeo yɛ bebinyime ba ani Yesu yɛ badɔkpasi tiaʋa dzedze beze ní ɔkɔ tole.

english_verse: Simon Peter said, “I'm going fishing!” The others said, “We will go with you.” They went out in their boat. But they didn't catch a thing that night.
translation_verse: Lɛ Simon Petro asi bagɔgɔ ba si, “Mɛɛtrɛ ní akpa obu kɔ.” Lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Kwiakuni wɔ kude.” Lɛ bezeemu iku ní katapokpaa mɛ, pɔ̀ elesi zizi na petee bébu kididɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Early the next morning Jesus stood on the shore, but the disciples did not realize who he was.
translation_verse: Agi liwo le liitu i, Yesu ɔlɛɛ ní limu ne kutu yɔ, pɔ̀ badɔkpasi ba bámɔ egyesi si Yesu yɛ ɔlɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus shouted, “Friends, have you caught anything?” “No!” they answered.
translation_verse: Lɛ eʋi ba si, “sɔhɛ ba, mlébu kiditɔ lo o?” Bebu le onu si, “Owo kúbu kididɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: So he told them, “Let your net down on the right side of your boat, and you will catch some fish.” They did, and the net was so full of fish that they could not drag it up into the boat.
translation_verse: Asi ba si, “Mlakpɛ mlɔ dzikle ye ní katapokpaa kaŋaa mɛ, mlaabù atɔ.” Lɛ gi bɛŋwia dzikle ye kpɛ ní klɔ ʋa ɛ, bátani yɛ kuhe obu u, lɛ ese linu si ebu akpa la kpaŋu.

english_verse: Jesus' favorite disciple told Peter, “It's the Lord!” When Simon heard it was the Lord, he put on the clothes he had taken off while he was working. Then he jumped into the water.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdɔkpasi liɛ klɔ gi Yesu alɔ ɛ, asi Petro si, “Ɔkaɛ yɛ liɛ klɔ!” Agi Petro enu si Ɔkaɛ liɛ klɔ ɛ, edzini yɛ lifune lìwu le axla isu ya lɛ ese linu si ebu lìwu i lɛ klɔ aplɛ lɛ ayɔ eku ní kuni yo me e.

english_verse: When the disciples got out of the boat, they saw some bread and a charcoal fire with fish on it.
translation_verse: Agi beple ní katapokpaa mɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ si axwa kifu kiisɔ gi akpa ni aklã atɔ atini kɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus told his disciples, “Bring some of the fish you just caught.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlamani akpa la gi mlebu abla ɛ la mɛ atɔ.”

english_verse: Jesus said, “Come and eat!” But none of the disciples dared ask who he was. They knew he was the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu asi ba si, “Mlagaŋa bidɔ mɛ.” Yɛ badɔkpasi ba mɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́tani yɛ kuʋi si, “Nyaŋwɛ wonu u?” lɛ ese linu si bete si yɛ onu Ɔkaɛ.

english_verse: Jesus took the bread in his hands and gave some of it to his disciples. He did the same with the fish.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesu atrɛ xe azɛɛkɔ àklã na a, xe akɔ la aki ba ɛ; niteme ké abitɛ ni akpa la fɛ.

english_verse: This was the third time Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death. Jesus and Peter
translation_verse: Lɛ ya lize kutánɛ gi Yesu ebu isu abasi yɛ badɔkpasi ba gi ayɔ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ kede e. Yesu edzini Petro edu

english_verse: When Jesus and his disciples had finished eating, he asked, “Simon son of John, do you love me more than the others do?” Simon Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I do!” “Then feed my lambs,” Jesus said.
translation_verse: Agi bɛŋa bidɔ mɛ pɔ́ ɛ, Yesu eʋi Petro si, “Simon, Yohanes Yebi ye, wɔlɔ́ mɛ sani kiliɛ gi banɔ gɔgɔa ya bɛlɔ mɛ na a?” Ebu le onu si, “Ee, Ɔkaɛ, wote si malɔ wɔ.” Yesu asi yɛ si, “Di mɛ beʋesibi ʋa aʋa,”

english_verse: Jesus asked a second time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter answered, “Yes, Lord, you know I love you!” “Then take care of my sheep,” Jesus told him.
translation_verse: Kuʋla Yesu eʋi ye si, “Simon, Yohanes Yebi ye, wɔlɔ́ mɛ na a?” Ebu le onu si, “Ee Ɔkaɛ wote si malɔ wɔ.” Yesu asi yɛ si, “Di mɛ iʋesi le aʋa.”

english_verse: Jesus asked a third time, “Simon son of John, do you love me?” Peter was hurt because Jesus had asked him three times if he loved him. So he told Jesus, “Lord, you know everything. You know I love you.” Jesus replied, “Feed my sheep.
translation_verse: Kutanɛ Yesu eʋi ye si, “Simon, Yohanes Yebi ye, wɔlɔ́ mɛ na a?” Asigaga ɛga Petro nì yɛ isu ya lɛ ese linu si Yesu eʋi ye kutanɛ si, “Wɔlɔ́ mɛ na a?” lɛ asi Yesu si, “Ɔkaɛ wote kidɔ kakaa; wote si malɔ wɔ!” Yesu asi yɛ si, “Di mɛ iʋesi le aʋa.

english_verse: I tell you for certain that when you were a young man, you dressed yourself and went wherever you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands. Then others will wrap your belt around you and lead you where you don't want to go.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛsi wɔ anukwarɛɛ: agi woze ɔyasɔʋi i, wɔzɛɛbla kɔ wɔtrɛ niklɔ gi wɔpɛ si wutrɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ xegi wotsi i, wɔanina wɔ iwla lɛ lɛ ɔnɔ dzedze aalulɔ wɔ ɔ, xe aatsini wɔ niklɔ ɔ gi wɔ́pɛ si wutrɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Jesus said this to tell how Peter would die and bring honor to God. Then he said to Peter, “Follow me!” Jesus and His Favorite Disciple
translation_verse: Gi apani te e, Yesu azeebu kudeo liɔ onu gi Petro aga a xe aatse e abasɛ, gi kwiaki Aya kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɔ. Lɛ klɔ kede e, Yesu asi yɛ si, “Zeni mɛ kude e!” Yesu ni ɔdɔkpasi dzedze e

english_verse: Peter turned and saw Jesus' favorite disciple following them. He was the same one who had sat next to Jesus at the meal and had asked, “Lord, who is going to betray you?”
translation_verse: Petro ekyi lɛ amɔ ɔdɔkpasi liɛ gi Yesu alɔ ɛ, ni ba kedea ɔniliɛ gi yɛ aziɔni Yesu ní ɔdɔŋakplɔ nɔ su u, gi bɛzɛŋa bidɔ gɔgɔ ʋɛ kpɛ ɔtɔ ki yɛ kutseo o, xe eʋi ye si, “Ɔkaɛ nyaŋwɛ atrábu wo ekɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: When Peter saw this disciple, he asked Jesus, “Lord, what about him?”
translation_verse: Agi Petro amɔ yɛ ɛ, eʋi Yesu si, “Ɔkaɛ liboe woli litrába onyime liɛ ya pɔ̀ aʋa a?”

english_verse: Jesus answered, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return? You must follow me.”
translation_verse: Yesu ebu le onu si, “Xe mapɛ si oze ligbã́ kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi maadziba ɛ, nifɔ lipa wɔ ɔ? Wɔ kɔ zeni mɛ kude!”

english_verse: So the rumor spread among the other disciples that this disciple would not die. But Jesus did not say he would not die. He simply said, “What is it to you, if I want him to live until I return?”
translation_verse: Lɛ liyi klanɔ ni banama gi beze ni Yesu kude e, kusi si, ɔdɔkpasi liɛ ya ɔ́tratse e. Pɔ̀ Yesu ɔ́pani si ɔ́tratse e; kipani ko apani si, “Xe mapɛ si oze kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ gi maadziba ɛ, nifɔ lipa wɔ ɔ?”

english_verse: This disciple is the one who told all of this. He wrote it, and we know he is telling the truth.
translation_verse: Yɛ onu ɔdɔkpasi liɛ gi apani ní eboa la ya su u, yɛ ké aŋwlimi na aplɛ; lɛ kwite si eboa la klɔ gi apani i, enu anukwarɛɛ.

english_verse: Jesus did many other things. If they were all written in books, I don't suppose there would be room enough in the whole world for all the books.
translation_verse: Yesu abitɛ eboe glegle gi xe si ba ŋwlimi na petee tile tile aplɛ ɛ, megyo edi si kelea petee katrátani ozekɔ kumɔ ki kuplekpaa liɔ klɔ gi biaŋwlimi i.

english_verse: Theophilus, I first wrote to you about all that Jesus did and taught from the very first
translation_verse: Onemi Teofil, ní mɛ keplekpa tɔpu ya mɛ ɛ, maŋwlimi ní kidɔ kakaa gi Yesu abitɛ ɛ, xe abasɛ ɛ dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi ekpese yɛ axwɛ na ɛ,

english_verse: until he was taken up to heaven. But before he was taken up, he gave orders to the apostles he had chosen with the help of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: kɔzɛna ni liwe lɛ klɔ gi Aya emuni ye atsini lifunu ne e. Xe biaseni yɛ omuno e, Yesu gani Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ kigaglaɛ mɛ, asi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba gi akya a si babitɛ eboa la ɔsã gi yɛ akpɛ aki ba ɛ.

english_verse: While he was still with them, he said: Don't leave Jerusalem yet. Wait here for the Father to give you the Holy Spirit, just as I told you he has promised to do.
translation_verse: Lɛ lipoe tɔ lɛ gi azaaŋa bidɔ ni ba ɛ, akpɛ ɔsã lɔ ya aki ba si: “Mlakísɛ ní Yerusalem boŋu i, mlepe bidɔkɔkɔɛ klɔ su gi mapani aki mlɔ ɛ. Bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ su gi Mɔka aka aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: John baptized with water, but in a few days you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit. Jesus Is Taken to Heaven
translation_verse: Yohanes akpɛ kuni aki banima, pɔ̀ mlɔ kɔɔ Aya aakpɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra nɔ ki mlɔ ní ewe klakla atɔ kede e.”

english_verse: While the apostles were still with Jesus, they asked him, “Lord, are you now going to give Israel its own king again?”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba beze ní kutsatsa mɛ ni Yesu dzɛ ɛ, beʋi ye si, Ɔkaɛ, Wɔtradzini kaʋaŋaa ɛki Israel ní lipoe lɛ ya mɛ na a?

english_verse: Jesus said to them, “You don't need to know the time of those events that only the Father controls.
translation_verse: Yesu asi ba si, “Aní mlɔ liboe linu si mlete lipoe le, ni bekyikyi ʋa a, ani kutrɔtrɔ liɔ gi Mɔka abitɛ ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But the Holy Spirit will come upon you and give you power. Then you will tell everyone about me in Jerusalem, in all Judea, in Samaria, and everywhere in the world.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ ɛba mlɔ aʋa ɛ, mlaazi kigagla, lɛ mlaaze mɛ katsalia ní Yerusalem, Yudea petee, ni Samaria ani kesea ɔgɔgɔ klɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: After Jesus had said this and while they were watching, he was taken up into a cloud. They could not see him,
translation_verse: Gi apani nite pɔ́ ɛ, Aya emuni ye atsini lifunu ne, lɛ gi bezeedi ye kpɛ ɛ, liplɛ ɛtɔ liba kpo ye lɛ bámɔ yɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: but as he went up, they kept looking up into the sky. Suddenly two men dressed in white clothes were standing there beside them.
translation_verse: Gi azaasɛ ní basu u, xe bezeedi lifune kpɛ ɛ, kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, benyime tiaʋa gi beze bidɔ pititi mɛ ɛ, bɛba lɛɛ ní basu,

english_verse: They said, “Why are you men from Galilee standing here and looking up into the sky? Jesus has been taken to heaven. But he will come back in the same way you have seen him go.” Someone To Take the Place of Judas
translation_verse: lɛ bɛpani si i, “Galileanɛ ma, ege loso mlalɛɛ ní ya xe mleedi lifune kpɛ te e? Yesu liɛ gi Aya asɛ ní mlɔ su atsini lifunu ne e, aadzi ba nite ke gi mlɛmɔ yɛ ɛ gi ɛɛtrɛ lifunu ne e.” Bɛkya Matias Si Oze ní Yuda ozeklɔ

english_verse: The Mount of Olives was about a kilometer from Jerusalem. The apostles who had gone there were Peter, John, James, Andrew, Philip, Thomas, Bartholomew, Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon, known as the Eager One, and Judas the son of James. After the apostles returned to the city, they went upstairs to the room where they had been staying.
translation_verse: Gi beku ɔma ʋidi no me e, bemu itrɛ ní ligba ɛlɛ klɔ mɛ gi beze e. Petro, Yohanes, Yakobo ni Andrea, Filipo ni Toma, Bartolomeo ni Mateo, Yakobo, Alfeo Yebi ni Simon gi bizɛxwa si Zelote ani Yuda Yakobo Yebi ye.

english_verse: The apostles often met together and prayed with a single purpose in mind. The women and Mary the mother of Jesus would meet with them, and so would his brothers.
translation_verse: Banɔ ba ya petee bɛzɛɛkpɛ likpɔ, ba ni badzɛ ba, Maria, Yesu yene ni Yesu yɛ beneminyime ba lɛ beze ní Aya kuxwaxwa aʋa lipoe me ni lipoe me e.

english_verse: Then Judas bought some land with the money he was given for doing that evil thing. He fell headfirst into the field. His body burst open, and all his insides came out.
translation_verse: Kibɔɛ liɛ gi Yuda amɔ adɔ ni yɛ kiboebitɛ zimizi ye me e, kidzi kese ní níklɔ gi etse e, xe awɔli adɔ ni ɔgyɔgyɔ kɔ ɔ, gi ata a, xe yɛ lipo le me iyaʋi lɛ ido ebali i.

english_verse: When the people of Jerusalem found out about this, they called the place Akeldama, which in the local language means “Field of Blood.”
translation_verse: Banama petee gi balɛ ní Yerusalem i, benu liboe le, lɛ ní ba tutɔ ba siya sɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛxwa kesea lia klɔ si Akeldama, gi lɛ ese linu si ‘Ika kese e.’

english_verse: In the book of Psalms it says, “Leave his house empty, and don't let anyone live there.” It also says, “Let someone else have his job.”
translation_verse: Petro atrɛ lɛ aʋa si, “bɛŋwlimi kpɛ Psalomo ʋa keplekpaa mɛ si,” ‘Yɛ ozeklɔ odzi kepe yaʋiyaʋi, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ okíze ní ka mɛ ɛ.’ Bɛŋwlimi kyɛ si, ‘Lɛ yɛ axwɛ na fɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ dzedze ɔzɛ la.’

english_verse: So we need someone else to help us tell others that Jesus has been raised from death. He must also be one of the men who was with us from the very beginning. He must have been with us from the time the Lord Jesus was baptized by John until the day he was taken to heaven.
translation_verse: dɔni Yohanes kuni kpɛkpɛɔ aʋa zɛkɔna ni liwe lɛ klɔ gi bɛzi Yesu itsini lifunu ne ní blɔ su i, ba mɛ tole odzi Yɛ kuyɔyɔɔ katsalia pɔni blɔ.”

english_verse: Two men were suggested: One of them was Joseph Barsabbas, known as Justus, and the other was Matthias.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkɔ banɔ tiaʋa, Yosef gi bɛxwa si Barsaba ɔniliɛ klɔ ke onu Yusto, ni Matias.

english_verse: Then they all prayed, “Lord, you know what everyone is like! Show us the one you have chosen
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛxwa Aya a, xe bɛpani si i, “Ɔkaɛ, wote banima petee ba ina nɛ mɛ, basɛ blɔ ní banɔ tiaʋa ya kusi, ba mɛ iliɛ gi wɔkya a,

english_verse: to be an apostle and to serve in place of Judas, who got what he deserved.”
translation_verse: si ɔwa axwɛ na ŋwisi ɔnɔxwaxwa kinɛ ɛ, ní Yuda gi adzi kede ní blɔ su u, tɔ yitrɛ ní klɔ gi egyeni ye e, linyi ne me.”

english_verse: They drew names, and Matthias was chosen to join the group of the eleven apostles.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛta exoeʋi la ɛ, ɛdɔ ní Matias aʋa, lɛ bebu ye pɔni banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tole ba ɛ.

english_verse: On the day of Pentecost all the Lord's followers were together in one place.
translation_verse: Gi Pentekoste liwe le lina ɛ, baʋaziŋa ba petee bexogo ní ɔkɔ tole.

english_verse: Suddenly there was a noise from heaven like the sound of a mighty wind! It filled the house where they were meeting.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, ozi ɔtɔ eyi dɔni lifunu ne ŋwisi liwɔ gagla liifu kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ozi lo ɛzi kepea klɔ mɛ gi beze petee e.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit took control of everyone, and they began speaking whatever languages the Spirit let them speak.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra nɔ ɛdɔ ba aʋa a, xe bekpese kunugu yo kugu ní siya ede ede me e, kiliɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛki ba si bapani i.

english_verse: Many religious Jews from every country in the world were living in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ atɔ beze ní Yerusalem, banama gi bizese Aya a, gi bɛdɔ kelea mɛ ɔma kakaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And when they heard this noise, a crowd gathered. But they were surprised, because they were hearing everything in their own languages.
translation_verse: Gi benu ozi lo e, linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ lizɛkpɛ likpɔ níklɔ, ba pɔ́ ba inugu itse, lɛ ese linu si bamɛ ɔnɔ kakaa enu gi bɛzɛɛpani yɛ tutɔ yɛ siya sɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They were excited and amazed, and said: Don't all these who are speaking come from Galilee?
translation_verse: Ba inugu itse lɛ bɛpisa, lɛ beʋi si “Banɔ ba ya gi bɛɛpani te e, aní Galilea banɛ benu na a?

english_verse: Then why do we hear them speaking our very own languages?
translation_verse: Kitɛ lize xe blɔ mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa eenu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ɔma nɔ mɛ siya sɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Some of us are from Parthia, Media, and Elam. Others are from Mesopotamia, Judea, Cappadocia, Pontus, Asia,
translation_verse: Blɔ banama gi kwidɔ Partia ni Media, Elam ni banama gi beze ní Mesopotamia, Yudea ni Kapadokia, Ponto ni Asia,

english_verse: Crete, and Arabia. Some of us were born Jews, and others of us have chosen to be Jews. Yet we all hear them using our own languages to tell the wonderful things God has done.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ma ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ pɔ̀ bɛzi Yudanɛ ma kusumu yo, blɔ bamɛ batɔ gi kwidɔ Kreta ni Arabia, blɔ petee kwinu gi bɛɛpani ní blɔ tutɔ blɔ siya sɛ mɛ axwɛ ʋidi a la gi Aya awa ɛ!”

english_verse: Everyone was excited and confused. Some of them even kept asking each other, “What does all this mean?”
translation_verse: Nì inugutsetse ni kupisa ɛ, bekpese ba ahanuglɔ ba kuʋi si, “Lɛ ya pɔ̀ lɛ ese si kitɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Others made fun of the Lord's followers and said, “They are drunk.” Peter Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ batɔ bɛkpɛ baʋaziŋa ba kakpaa si, “Banɔ ba ya ɛ, kuda bemu!” Petro yɛ Kupaniɔ

english_verse: Peter stood with the eleven apostles and spoke in a loud and clear voice to the crowd: Friends and everyone else living in Jerusalem, listen carefully to what I have to say!
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ayɔ ɔlɛɛ ni banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tole gɔgɔ ba lɛ apani aki linɔkpi le ni ole gagla si i, “Blɔakɔanɛ Yudanɛ ma ni mlɔ banama gi mlalɛ ní Yerusalem i, mlata mɛ kutu tɔ mlaki tɔ mibu lɛ ese ki mlɔ,

english_verse: You are wrong to think that these people are drunk. After all, it is only nine o'clock in the morning.
translation_verse: aní kuda banɔ ba ya bemu kiliɛ gi mleegyo edi i, lɛ ese linu si livlɛ inyɔ ɔgɔtole ko ìsa.

english_verse: But this is what God told the prophet Joel to say,
translation_verse: Boŋu i, liboe lɛ gi oboedoplɛ Yoel apani aplɛ ɛ si,

english_verse: “When the last days come, I will give my Spirit to everyone. Your sons and daughters will prophesy. Your young men will see visions, and your old men will have dreams.
translation_verse: “ ‘Aya apani si Liboe lɛ ya liaba lɛ mɛ ní ewe gɔgɔ la mɛ si, maakɔ mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔki ɔnɔ kakaa, lɛ mlɔ bebinyime ba ni mlɔ bebidzɛ ba biapani eboe plɛ ɛ, lɛ mlɔ iyasɔʋi lɛ iamɔ kububasɛ ɛ, lɛ mlɔ bakatsi ba biaza balila a.

english_verse: In those days I will give my Spirit to my servants, both men and women, and they will prophesy.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní mɛ banɔsumu ba, benyime ni badzɛ ba aʋa kura kyɛ ɛ, maakɔ mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔki ba ní ewe ala mɛ, lɛ biapani mɛ liboe le plɛ ɛ.

english_verse: “I will work miracles in the sky above and wonders on the earth below. There will be blood and fire and clouds of smoke.
translation_verse: Maabitɛ enugutseboe ní lifunu ne ni eboe gi baʋímɔɛ ʋidi ní kesea aʋa. Lɛ biamɔ ika, kifu ni kunyɔ gbudu u;

english_verse: The sun will turn dark, and the moon will be as red as blood before the great and wonderful day of the Lord appears.
translation_verse: liwo le liadzi litumu lɛ ɔdzidziɛ aazã ŋwisi ika kinɛ ɛ, xe Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe ʋidi le e gi Ɔkaɛ aabitɛ ɛ kidiliɛ gi apɛ si yibitɛ ɛ liana.

english_verse: Then the Lord will save everyone who asks for his help.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa a gi aaxwa Ɔkaɛ linyi ne kí kupɔnɔ ɛ, aamɔ kubúbú.’

english_verse: Now, listen to what I have to say about Jesus from Nazareth. God proved he sent Jesus to you by having him work miracles, wonders, and signs. All of you know this.
translation_verse: “Mlata eboa la ya kutu, Israelbanɛ ma! Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ, yɛ onu onyime liɛ gi yɛ Aya akya a gi yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kigaglaɛ Aya ebu abasi mlɔ gani eboe gi etu mlo kugyɔ ɔ ni enugutseboe gi Aya abitɛ ɛ gani yɛ mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ tutɔ mlete kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si, liba lɛ mɛ ní mlɔ kusi i.

english_verse: God had already planned and decided that Jesus would be handed over to you. So you took him and had evil men put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Ni yɛ ɔtutɔ batrɔtrɔ ʋa onu i, Aya atrɔ lɛ kooko si biabu Yesu kí mlɔ; lɛ mleye ye e, gi mlɛki banɔ zimizi ba beku ye ní ose lo su u.

english_verse: But God set him free from death and raised him to life. Death could not hold him in its power.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya ayɔni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, lɛ aki yɛ ɔwlamɔmɔ adɔ ni kutse kigagla ese e, lɛ ese linu si lítratani lɛ mɛ kuba si kutse kwiaŋa yɛ aʋa tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: What David said are really the words of Jesus, “I always see the Lord near me, and I will not be afraid with him at my right side.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, Dawid apani ní yɛ su si; “ ‘Mamɔ Ɔkaɛ lipoe kakaa ní mɛ ɔtɔ nɔ; gi ɔlɛ ní mɛ kaŋawla mɛ loso e, kididɔ kitrakpɛ mɛ lifu.

english_verse: Because of this, my heart will be glad, my words will be joyful, and I will live in hope.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ ɔna nɔ ɛɛmɔ isami, lɛ mɛ kinemi ye kiikpɔ Aya lipoe kakaa a. Kɔ maaze ligbã́ ni kudeonudidi,

english_verse: The Lord won't leave me in the grave. I am his holy one, and he won't let my body decay.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si wɔtrayurɔ mɛ ki ɔdrɔ lɔ ɔ, wɔtraki si wɔ obi dradra yɛ kisugyaɛ kiafumu ní ɔdrɔ lɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He has shown me the path to life, and he makes me glad by being near me.”
translation_verse: Wɔ basɛ mɛ ligbã nɛ kudeo lɛ wɔ aŋu mɛ kuzeze kwiaki si isami iayi me e.

english_verse: My friends, it is right for me to speak to you about our ancestor David. He died and was buried, and his tomb is still here.
translation_verse: “Mɛ benemi ba, lɛ ya loso linu si midra liboe me ki mlɔ ní blɔ ɔkatsi zi linyi Okusi ye Dawid su. Etse, bevive ye, lɛ yɛ ɔdrɔ lɔ ɔlɛ ní blɔ kusi ya kɔna ni omono o.

english_verse: But David was a prophet, and he knew that God had made a promise he would not break. He had told David someone from his own family would someday be king.
translation_verse: Eze oboedoplɛ lɛ ete si Aya aka aplɛ aki yɛ ɛ. Aya aka plɛ si yɛ abitɛ Dawid yɛ osobi tole okusi ŋwisi kiliɛ gi yɛ Dawid eze kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: David knew this would happen, and so he told us Christ would be raised to life. He said God would not leave him in the grave or let his body decay.
translation_verse: Gi Dawid amɔ kidiliɛ ɛ, gi kɛ Aya atrábitɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ, apani ní Kristo ye kuyɔyɔ su si, Békyi kedea kpɛ yɛ ní bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, si yɛ kisugyaɛ kífumu ní ɔdrɔ lɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All of us can tell you that God has raised Jesus to life!
translation_verse: Aya ayɔni Yesu liɛ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ, lɛ loso kwiŋa katsalia ní lɛ su iiki mlɔ.

english_verse: Jesus was taken up to sit at the right side of God, and he was given the Holy Spirit, just as the Father had promised. Jesus is also the one who has given the Spirit to us, and this is what you are now seeing and hearing.
translation_verse: Bɛyɔni yɛ edu yɛka Aya ɔŋawla lɔ mɛ, lɛ azɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ adɔ ni yɛka klɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi aka aplɛ ɛ kinɛ. Lɛ loso kidiliɛ gi mlɛɛmɔ ɔ, xe mleenu i, kɛ kinu bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi akɔ aki blɔ ɔ.

english_verse: David didn't go up to heaven. So he wasn't talking about himself when he said, “The Lord told my Lord to sit at his right side,
translation_verse: Lɛ ɛ, aní Dawid yɛ emu atrɛ ní lifunu ne e, pɔ̀ yɛ apani si “ ‘Ɔkaɛ asi mɛ ɔkaɛ si, ze ní mɛ kaŋaa mɛ ní ya

english_verse: until he made my Lord's enemies into a footstool for him.”
translation_verse: zɛkɔna ni ni lipoe lɛ gi maakpɛ wɔ ahwafianɛ ma ní wɔ kukliseo o.” ’

english_verse: Everyone in Israel should then know for certain that God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, even though you put him to death on a cross.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso lilɛ si mlɔ Israɛlanɛ ma petee mlete le mɔmɔmɔ si Yesu liɛ gi mleku ní ose lo su i, yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi Aya abitɛ Ɔkaɛ ni Mesiya yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When the people heard this, they were very upset. They asked Peter and the other apostles, “Friends, what should we do?”
translation_verse: Agi banima benu le e, likpɛ ba begyodi ʋa kunugu glegle lɛ beʋi Petro ni banɔkpɛkpɛ dzedze ba si i “Ege linu si kubitɛ benemi ba a?”

english_verse: Peter said to them, “Turn to God and be baptized, every one of you, in the name of Jesus Christ for the forgiveness of your sins, and you will receive the gift of the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Petro asi ba si, “Mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa ɔyurɔ yɛ eboe zimizi na, tɔ bakpɛ kuni aki yɛ ní Yesu Kristo linyi ne me, tɔ biakɔ yɛ eboe zimizi na kyɛ yɛ, atɔ mlaazi Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ gi binu Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ ɛ.

english_verse: This promise is for you and your children. It is for everyone our Lord God will choose, no matter where they live.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kalɛ kí mlɔ ni mlɔ bebi ba, ani banama gi bali ní ɔdzage no e, ba benu banama petee gi Ɔkaɛ blɔ Aya aaxwa mani isu ya su u.”

english_verse: Peter told them many other things as well. Then he said, “I beg you to save yourselves from what will happen to all these evil people.”
translation_verse: Petro eʋi adɔ ni ba iwla mɛ, ni eboe glegle dzedze lɛ agba ba si, “Mlebu isu ya dɔni kutuheheo liɔ gi kwiaba etukpo me gagla benete ba ya aʋa a.”

english_verse: They spent their time learning from the apostles, and they were like family to each other. They also broke bread and prayed together. Life among the Lord's Followers
translation_verse: Bɛkɔ ba lipoe le kpasi bidɔ dɔni banɔkpɛkpɛ ba klɔ, lɛ beze ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ní Aya yɛ kiŋaŋaɛ kulɛlɛ ni Aya kuxwaxwa mɛ ɛ. Ligbã́zeze ne ní Baʋaziŋa ba Kusi

english_verse: Everyone was amazed by the many miracles and wonders that the apostles worked.
translation_verse: Eboe mamɔ edi glegle ni enugutseboe ɛba la mɛ gani banɔkpɛkpɛ ba aʋa, lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa akɔ kubùbù ni lifu kusese aki Kaka Aya a.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers often met together, and they shared everything they had.
translation_verse: Baʋaziŋa ba bɛzɛtsa lipoe me ni lipoe me lɛ bɛzɛɛlɛ ba bidɔ mɛ ni ba ahanuglɔ ba a.

english_verse: They would sell their property and possessions and give the money to whoever was in need.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi bɛda ba isu ya bidɔ mɛ ni ba kepimia ɛ, kɔ bɛlɛ kibɔɛ kpɛ ní ba kusi kiliɛ gi ɔnɔ kakaa aziã ɛ.

english_verse: Day after day they met together in the temple. They broke bread together in different homes and shared their food happily and freely,
translation_verse: Liwe kakaa liwe e, bɛzɛɛtsa ŋwisi ɔha kinɛ ɛ, ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, lɛ bɛzɛɛŋa bidɔ ɛkpɛ likpɔ ní ba kupeo me ni isami ní kusububɔ mɛ.

english_verse: while praising God. Everyone liked them, and each day the Lord added to their group others who were being saved.
translation_verse: Bɛzɛɛkpɔ̀ Aya lɛ beze isami mɔmɔ gbã mɛ ni ɔnɔ kakaa. Lɛ liwe kakaa ɛ, Ɔkaɛ azaakɔ banama gi Aya azeebu i, apɔni ɔha lɔ.

english_verse: The time of prayer was about three o'clock in the afternoon, and Peter and John were going into the temple.
translation_verse: Liwe etɔ lɛ ɛ, Petro ni Yohanes bɛzɛtrɛ ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ní kemize inyɔ tita mɛ, Aya lixwapoe le.

english_verse: A man who had been born lame was being carried to the temple door. Each day he was placed beside this door, known as the Beautiful Gate. He sat there and begged from the people who were going in.
translation_verse: Onyime ɔtɔ gi bɛpɔ bafa a, xe ɔ́zɛtani kuga yɛ ligbã́ nɛ mɛ petee e, eze ní kevunu ya lia gi bizɛɛxwa si kevunu u gi Kepeedi i onu. Bezeefeke ye manɔ ní kevunu ya liwe kakaa si tɔ ɔzeeʋi kibɔ ní banama gi beetiku ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ awla.

english_verse: The man saw Peter and John entering the temple, and he asked them for money.
translation_verse: Agi amɔ Petro ni Yohanes gi beetiku i, eʋi ba si baki yɛ kibɔ.

english_verse: But they looked straight at him and said, “Look up at us!”
translation_verse: Bedu aŋu ní yɛ aʋa, lɛ Petro apani si, “Di blo!”

english_verse: The man stared at them and thought he was going to get something.
translation_verse: Lɛ edi ba ɛ, ni kugyodi si bɛtráki yɛ kiditɔ.

english_verse: But Peter said, “I don't have any silver or gold! But I will give you what I do have. In the name of Jesus Christ from Nazareth, get up and start walking.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lɛ Petro asi yɛ si, “Kibɔ ɛ tɔtɔ kíma ní mɛ awla a, pɔ̀ maaki wɔ kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní mɛ awla ɛ; ní Yesu Kristo Nazaret ɔnɛɛ linyi ne me e, mɛɛsi wɔ si yɔ tɔ ga!”

english_verse: Peter then took him by the right hand and helped him up. At once the man's feet and ankles became strong,
translation_verse: Lɛ petro evu yɛ ɔŋawla lɔ kawlakpɔa lɛ apɔni yɛ xe ayɔ ɛ. Ní lɛ onu klɔ ko e, kigagla kiku onyimee yɛ kuklikpɔ ni yɛ aklamasi na mɛ.

english_verse: and he jumped up and started walking. He went with Peter and John into the temple, walking and jumping and praising God.
translation_verse: Lɛ ayɔ lixu le e, xe ɔlɛɛ ní yɛ ikli lɛ aʋa ɛ, ko lɛ ekpese kuga klanɔ ɛ. Lɛ eti eku ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ni ba a, gi azaaga a, xe azaayɔ exu la a, xe azakpɔ Aya ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone saw him walking around and praising God.
translation_verse: Gi banima petee niklɔ bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, gi ɛga a, xe ɛɛkpɔ Aya ɛ,

english_verse: They knew he was the beggar who had been lying beside the Beautiful Gate, and they were completely surprised. They could not imagine what had happened to the man. Peter Speaks in the Temple
translation_verse: bɛmɔ yɛ egyesi si yɛ eze ɔdɔʋi ye gi eze lisɔligba lɛ kevunu ya a, gi bizɛxwa si kepeedi onu u, litu ba kugyɔ lɛ ba petee inugu itse ní liboe lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛki yɛ su u. Liboe lɛ gi Petro asi banima ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ

english_verse: While the man kept holding on to Peter and John, the whole crowd ran to them in amazement at the place known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Gi onyimee asu ní Petro ni Yohanes su ní Salomo ɔviva nɔ ɛ, lize linugutseboe kí banima lɛ bese ba exogo ní basu i.

english_verse: Peter saw that a crowd had gathered, and he said: Friends, why are you surprised at what has happened? Why are you staring at us? Do you think we have some power of our own? Do you think we were able to make this man walk because we are so religious?
translation_verse: Gi Petro amɔ banima ɛ, asi ba si “Israelbanɛ ma, ege loso mlɔ inugu itse ní liboe lɛ ya su u, lɛ ege loso mledu aŋu blɔ aʋa a? Kigyo mlegyo edi si gani blɔ kigagla mɛ loo gani blɔ Aya kuseseo me kwiki onyime liɛ ya ɛɛga na a?

english_verse: The God that Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and our other ancestors worshiped has brought honor to his Servant Jesus. He is the one you betrayed. You turned against him when he was being tried by Pilate, even though Pilate wanted to set him free.
translation_verse: Abraham, Isak ni Yakob Aya, blɔ bakatsi ba Aya yɛ emuni yɛ osumubi Yesu. Pɔ̀ mlebu ye kí Yudanɛ baka ba, lɛ mlɛdzi kede ní yɛ su ní Pilato aŋu mɛ ɛ, gi nya apɛ si yiyurɔ yɛ xe e.

english_verse: You rejected Jesus, who was holy and good. You asked for a murderer to be set free,
translation_verse: Eze ɔnɔ dradra ni ɔnɔ gbã pɔ̀ mlɛdzi kede ní yɛ su u, boŋu i, mleʋi Pilato si tɔ ɔyurɔ ɔnɔyee ɔki mlɔ.

english_verse: and you killed the one who leads people to life. But God raised him from death, and all of us can tell you what he has done.
translation_verse: Mlɛki beye ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛki banima ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ Aya ayɔ ni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, lɛ kunu katsalia aki lɛ.

english_verse: You see this man, and you know him. He put his faith in the name of Jesus and was made strong. Faith in Jesus made this man completely well while everyone was watching.
translation_verse: Yɛ linyi ne kigaglaɛ kɛ kiki bafa liɛ ya kigagla. Kidiliɛ gi mlɛmɔ ɔ, xe mlete e, kɛ kiba kɛ mɛ gani kuziŋaa ní yɛ linyi ne me aʋa; kuziŋaa ní Yesu me e, kɔ kwiyɔni yɛ, kiliɛ gi mlɔ pɔ́ mlɛɛmɔ ɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure you and your leaders didn't know what you were doing.
translation_verse: “Lɛ abla ɛ, mɛ benemi ba, Mete si kidiliɛ gi mlɔ ni mlɔ baka ba mlɛbitɛ mani Yesu su i, mlɛbitɛ kɛ ní kumotemote me.

english_verse: But God had his prophets tell that his Messiah would suffer, and now he has kept this promise.
translation_verse: Aya edo le aplɛ kooko gani beboedoplɛ ba aʋa si, yɛ Kristo ye aamɔ kini, lɛ aki liba lɛ mɛ ní kudeo liɔ klɔ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: So turn to God! Give up your sins, and you will be forgiven.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlekyi ɔna mɛ tɔ mlekyi evu Aya tɔ aakɔ̀ mlɔ eboe zimizi na kyɛ mlɔ. Xegi mlékyi i,

english_verse: Then the time will come when the Lord will give you fresh strength. He will send you Jesus, his chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kigaglaɛ lipoe le litrába dɔni Ɔkaɛ klɔ, lɛ aakpɛ Yesu gi onu Kristo ye e, gi ebu aki mlɔ koko e.

english_verse: But Jesus must stay in heaven until God makes all things new, just as his holy prophets promised long ago.
translation_verse: Linu si oze ní lifunu ne zɛkɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi Aya aabitɛ kidɔ kakaa vuvu i, kiliɛ gi Aya apani gani yɛ beboedoplɛ dradra ba a, gi bezee zanɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: Moses said, “The Lord your God will choose one of your own people to be a prophet, just as he chose me. Listen to everything he tells you.
translation_verse: Mose apani si, ‘Mlɔ Ɔka Aya aatsisi mlɔ oboedoplɛ kiliɛ gi akpɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ aaze mlɔ tutɔ mlɔ ɔnɔ ɔ. Linu si mlabitɛ ní kidɔ kakaa gi asi mlɔ si mlabitɛ ɛ, aʋa.

english_verse: No one who disobeys this prophet will be one of God's people any longer.”
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɔ́zɛ oboedoplɛ yɛ liboe le e, biabu ye zanɔ ní Aya banima kusi lɛ biakyrɔ yɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: Samuel and all the other prophets who came later also spoke about what is now happening.
translation_verse: “Sugba Aya beboedoplɛ ba petee dɔni Samuel aʋa gi bɛpani liboe plɛ ɛ, bɛpani ní kidiliɛ gi kiba kɛ mɛ ní ewe ala ya mɛ ɛ su.

english_verse: You are really the ones God told his prophets to speak to. And you were given the promise God made to your ancestors. He said to Abraham, “All nations on earth will be blessed because of someone from your family.”
translation_verse: Aya yɛ kakaplɛa gani yɛ beboedoplɛ ba aʋa ɛ, kenu mlɔ kanɛ, lɛ mlɛmɔ ɔŋakɔ ní kunyanya gi Aya abitɛ aplɛ ní mlɔ bakatsi ba mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ gi apani aki Abraham si i, ‘gani wɔ besoebi ba aʋa ɛ, maakrira banima petee ní kesea aʋa.’

english_verse: God sent his chosen Son to you first, because God wanted to bless you and make each one of you turn away from your sins.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Aya akya Yebi ye, xe atsisi yɛ mlɔ klɔ kpɛ ɔtɔ ɔ, tɔ aakrira mlɔ tɔ aabitɛ mlɔmɛ ɔkakaa tɔ aamɔni yɛ eboe zimizi na.”

english_verse: The apostles were still talking to the people, when some priests, the captain of the temple guard, and some Sadducees arrived.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Petro ni Yohanes bɛlɛɛ zegu kunugu yo ɛki banima ɛ, kusumu yo baka ba, lisɔligba lɛ bepepe ba ɔkaɛ, ni Zadukianɛ ma bɛba ɛtsani ba.

english_verse: These men were angry because the apostles were teaching the people that the dead would be raised from death, just as Jesus had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Isu iyeʋi ba koŋu, lɛ ese linu si banɔkpɛkpɛ tiaʋa ba ɛ, bɛzɛɛbasɛ banima si Yesu ayɔ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ, gi libasɛ si bekpo ʋa ɛ biayɔ dzi ligbã́.

english_verse: It was already late in the afternoon, and they arrested Peter and John and put them in jail for the night.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, bevu ba lɛ bɛkpɛ ba ligba kɔzɛna ni kele titia mɛ, gi kelea keeblo lɛ loso o.

english_verse: But a lot of people who had heard the message believed it. So by now there were about
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ glegle gi benu liboe le e, bɛzi ŋa; lɛ benyime ba kisrɛsrɛ mɛ kiaze banɔ akpe tietsu u.

english_verse: The next morning the leaders, the elders, and the teachers of the Law of Moses met in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si gi kelea kɛti i, Yudanɛ baka ba; banɔtsitsi ba ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba bɛkpɛ likpɔ ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: The high priest Annas was there, as well as Caiaphas, John, Alexander, and other members of the high priest's family.
translation_verse: Ba ni kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye Hana ni Kayafa, Yohanes, Aleksandro ani banɔ gɔgɔa ba gi bɛdɔ kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye yɛ oku no me e, ba petee bɛba ɛtsa.

english_verse: They brought in Peter and John and made them stand in the middle while they questioned them. They asked, “By what power and in whose name have you done this?”
translation_verse: Bɛmani banɔkpɛkpɛ ba lɛ bɛki si balɛɛ ní ba aŋu mɛ lɛ beʋi si, “Kitɛ mɛ mlɛɛbitɛ nite e? Kigagla woli kiliɛ ní mlɔ awla loo nyaŋwɛ linyi me mlɛwa axwɛ a la ya a?”

english_verse: Peter was filled with the Holy Spirit and told the nation's leaders and the elders:
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ayɔ ɔlɛɛ ní Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigaglaɛ mɛ lɛ ebu ba onu si, “Blɔ baka ba ni banɔtsitsi ba,

english_verse: You are questioning us today about a kind deed in which a man who could not walk was healed.
translation_verse: Xe mleeʋi blo si kuki akɔnta omono o, ní kidɔ gbã ɛ liɛ gi kwibitɛ ki bafa liɛ ɛ, ni kiliɛ gi kwiyɔni yɛ su i,

english_verse: But there is something we must tell you and everyone else in Israel. This man is standing here completely well because of the power of Jesus Christ from Nazareth. You put Jesus to death on a cross, but God raised him to life.
translation_verse: kɔlɔ mlɔ ni Israelbanɛ ma petee mlete si, onyime liɛ ya gi ɔlɛɛ ni mlɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, Yesu Kristo gi ádɔ Nazaret i, gi mleku ní ose lo su u, xe Aya ayɔni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, yɛ linyi ne me bɛyɔ ni yɛ.

english_verse: He is the stone you builders thought was worthless, and now he is the most important stone of all.
translation_verse: “Yesu yɛ su Aya keplekpaa kɛpani si, ‘Likla ɛnɛ gi mlɔ bagbawa ba mlɛyurɔ ɛplɛ ɛ, lɛ lidzi limwa nɛ aʋa likla nɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: Only Jesus has the power to save! His name is the only one in all the world that can save anyone.
translation_verse: Yɛ mɛ ko biamɔ kubúbú ní kelea petee me, linyi tɔtɔ líma gi Aya akɔ ɔ, gi liabu blɔ agbã́ na a.”

english_verse: The officials were amazed to see how brave Peter and John were, and they knew that these two apostles were only ordinary men and not well educated. The officials were certain these men had been with Jesus.
translation_verse: Gi betsixwa ba bɛmɔ kiliɛ gi ɔna eze ní Petro ni Yohanes lipo e, xe benu si banɔ pluplu ko beze e, gi bádzɛ suku ɔtɔtɔ ɛ, ba inugu itse. Lɛ bɛmɔ egyesi si beze ni Yesu kude e.

english_verse: But they could not deny what had happened. The man who had been healed was standing there with the apostles.
translation_verse: Lɛ liboeboe líze gi biapani i, lɛ ese linu si bɛmɔ onyimee gi bɛyɔnɔ ɛ, gi ɔlɛɛ ní Petro ni Yohanes su i.

english_verse: The officials commanded them to leave the council room. Then the officials said to each other,
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi ba si, bedo ní Kutsatsaa mɛ, lɛ bekpese eboe la kugyodi ní batsali kusi i.

english_verse: “What can we do with these men? Everyone in Jerusalem knows about this miracle, and we cannot say it didn't happen.
translation_verse: Lɛ beʋi si ege kwiabitɛ benyime ba ya a? Ɔnɔ kakaa ní Yerusalem ete si linugutseboe ʋidi lɛ ya gi liba lɛ mɛ gani ba aʋa a, lɛ kútratani lɛ kunada a.

english_verse: But to keep this thing from spreading, we will warn them never again to speak to anyone about the name of Jesus.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ tɔ kutsi kude tɔ likíhle eku ní banima mɛ ɛ, mlaki tɔ kukpɛ ɔsã aki banɔ ba ya tɔ bakígu kunugu ikí ɔnɔnɔ ní Yesu linyi ne me tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: So they called the two apostles back in and told them they must never, for any reason, teach anything about the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ bedzini ba ɛxwa ɛmanɔ ɔ, lɛ bɛkpɛ ɔsã ɛki ba si, bakípani loo basɛ bidɔ liwewe ní Yesu linyi ne me tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Peter and John answered, “Do you think God wants us to obey you or to obey him?
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro ni Yohanes bebu le onu ɛki ba si, “Mlɔ tutɔ mlaxwa litsii lɛ klɔ xegi lipɛ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ si, kunu mlɔ igbè loo tɔ kunu Aya yɛ igbè.

english_verse: We cannot keep quiet about what we have seen and heard.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kútratani kunugu yo kumɔni ní kidiliɛ gi blɔ ɔtutɔ kwimɔ ɔ, xe kwinu u su.”

english_verse: The officials could not find any reason to punish Peter and John. So they threatened them and let them go. The man who was healed by this miracle was more than
translation_verse: Onyime liɛ gi yɛ bɛyɔnɔ ɛ, azɛ asani agɔ avitene. Baʋaziŋa ba bɛxwa Aya lɛ beʋi si ɔki ba kigagla

english_verse: As soon as Peter and John had been set free, they went back and told the others everything the chief priests and the leaders had said to them.
translation_verse: Gi bɛ yurɔ Petro ni Yohanes ko e, bedzitrɛ ní ɔha lɔ klɔ lɛ bɛsi ba a liboe lɛ gi kusumu yo baka ba ni banɔtsitsi ba bɛsi ba ɛ.

english_verse: When the rest of the Lord's followers heard this, they prayed together and said: Master, you created heaven and earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: Agi baʋaziŋa ba benu liboe le e, ba petee bɛkpɛ likpɔ ni ole tole lɛ bɛxwa Aya si, “Ɔka wɔ wɔbitɛ lifunu ne, kelea, axu la ni kidɔ kakaa gi kikpasi ní bɛ mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: And by the Holy Spirit you spoke to our ancestor David. He was your servant, and you told him to say: “Why are all the Gentiles so furious? Why do people make foolish plans?
translation_verse: Gani wɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, wɔpani gani blɔ ɔkatsitsie Dawid aʋa gi onu wɔ ɔnɔsumu i, gi apani si, ‘Ege lɛ loso banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ɛ, bɛmɔ kusuyeʋi, ege lɛ loso banima bizɛbitɛ banyánya pluplu ʋa a?

english_verse: The kings of earth prepare for war, and the rulers join together against the Lord and his Messiah.”
translation_verse: Kelea mɛ bekusi ba bɛbla ɛplɛ, lɛ baʋaŋa ba bɛkpɛ likpɔ si bɛta kigũ ni Ɔkaɛ ni yɛ Kristo ye.’

english_verse: Here in Jerusalem, Herod and Pontius Pilate got together with the Gentiles and the people of Israel. Then they turned against your holy Servant Jesus, your chosen Messiah.
translation_verse: Lilɛ lɛ mɛ si Herodes ni Pontio Pilato bɛtsa ní ɔma ʋidi ɔ lɔ ya mɛ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ ani Israelbanɛ ma ní Yesu su, wɔ ɔnɔsumu dradra yɛ gi yɛ wɔkya ɛ.

english_verse: They did what you in your power and wisdom had already decided would happen.
translation_verse: Bɛkpɛ likpɔ tɔ biabitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi gani wɔ kigaglaɛ ni kupɛ́pɛɔ onu i, wɔtrɔ si kiba kɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Lord, listen to their threats! We are your servants. So make us brave enough to speak your message.
translation_verse: Lɛ abla ɛ, Ɔka vu aŋu ní ba adaa la gi bɛfia su u, tɔ ki tɔ blɔ wɔ besumubi ba kupani wɔ liboe le ni aŋu mɛ gagla.

english_verse: Show your mighty power, as we heal people and work miracles and wonders in the name of your holy Servant Jesus.
translation_verse: Ki banima bamɔ wɔ kigaglaɛ tɔ ki tɔ banyanya ba bayɔ, kitɔ kubitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe gi aʋíba la mɛ edi i, gani wɔ Ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ dradra yɛ Yesu linyi ne me.”

english_verse: After they had prayed, the meeting place shook. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and bravely spoke God's message. Sharing Possessions
translation_verse: Gi bɛxwa Aya pɔ ɛ, niklɔ gi bɛtsa ɛ, niklɔ esugu. Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛba ba petee aʋa, lɛ bekpese Aya liboe le kudo ni aŋu mɛ gagla a. Baʋaziŋa ba bɛlɛ ba bidɔmɔmɔ ʋɛ

english_verse: The group of followers all felt the same way about everything. None of them claimed that their possessions were their own, and they shared everything they had with each other.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛziŋa ɛ, ba likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ lize ni kugyodi tule ani ɔna tole. Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́pani si yɛ bidɔmɔmɔ ʋɛ ɛ, yɛ tete binɛ, pɔ̀ ba petee bɛlɛ kidɔ kakaa gi bɛmɔ ɛ, ni ba ahanuglɔ ba.

english_verse: In a powerful way the apostles told everyone that the Lord Jesus was now alive. God greatly blessed his followers,
translation_verse: Lɛ ni kigagla glegle e, banɔkpɛkpɛ ba beze lɛ aʋa bɛzɛŋa katsalia ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu kuyɔyɔ su, lɛ Aya apɛ lipo me glegle aki ba petee.

english_verse: and no one went in need of anything. Everyone who owned land or houses would sell them and bring the money
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ óze ní ɔha lɔ mɛ ɛ, gi bidɔ biziã yɛ ɛ. Banama awla gi kuse loo kupé kwize e, bɛda ʋɛ lɛ bibɔɛ ʋɛ gi bɛmɔ ɛdɔni bɛ kudada mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: to the apostles. Then they would give the money to anyone in need.
translation_verse: bɛkɔ ʋɛ kpɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba awla tɔ balɛ aki ba mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa kiliɛ gi aziã ɛ.

english_verse: Ananias and his wife Sapphira also sold a piece of property.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ onyime ɔtɔ eze gi bɛzɛxwa si Anania a, yɛ ni yedze Safira bɛda ba bidɔ mɛ.

english_verse: But they agreed to cheat and keep some of the money for themselves. So when Ananias took the rest of the money to the apostles,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní yedze kutete me e, abla kibɔɛ kapɛatɔ ki isu lɛ akɔ kigɔgɔɛ akpɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba awla ɛ.

english_verse: Peter said, “Why has Satan made you keep back some of the money from the sale of the property? Why have you lied to the Holy Spirit?
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro apani si, “Anania ege loso wɔki si Satana aŋa wɔ aʋa te e, xe aki si woemeni Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, xe wɔkɔ kibɔɛ kapɛatɔ ɔ, gi wɔzɛ ní wɔ kesea litukpo ɔbla ki isu u?

english_verse: The property was yours before you sold it, and even after you sold it, the money was still yours. What made you do such a thing? You didn't lie to people. You lied to God!”
translation_verse: Xe wɔada kesea ɛ, aní wɔ kanɛ keze e? Lɛ gi wɔda ka ɛ, kibɔɛ aní wɔ kinɛ kíze e? Ege loso wɔbasɛ asia na a? Aní banɔ woemeno o, boŋu i Aya woemeno!”

english_verse: As soon as Ananias heard this, he dropped dead, and everyone who heard about it was frightened.
translation_verse: Gi Anania enu liboe le ko e, egleʋe lɛ etse e, niteme si banama gi benu liboe le e, lifu liku ba.

english_verse: Some young men came in and wrapped up his body. Then they took it out and buried it.
translation_verse: Iyasɔʋi lɛ iba ɛxla okpo ye ni kusa mɛ lɛ befeke ye dono eze vive e.

english_verse: Three hours later Sapphira came in, but she did not know what had happened to her husband.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi inyɔsasa tita kede kinɛ ɛ, yedze gi óte liboe lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ ɛ, eti eku kepea mɛ.

english_verse: Peter asked her, “Tell me, did you sell the property for this amount?” “Yes,” she answered, “that's the amount.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro eʋi ye si, “Si mɛ xe kibɔɛ liɛ kinu kibɔɛ petee gi wɔni wonye mlɛzɛ ní mlɔ kesea litukpo o. Ebu le onu si Ee kibɔɛ petee kɛ klɔ.”

english_verse: Then Peter said, “Why did the two of you agree to test the Lord's Spirit? The men who buried Ananias are by the door, and they will carry you out!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro asi yɛ si, “Ege loso wɔ ni wonye mlɛtrɔ si mlɛkeni Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ edi i? Benyime baklɔ gi bevive wonye e, bali kevunu ya, lɛ biafeke wɔfɛ kudono o!”

english_verse: At once she fell at Peter's feet and died. When the young men came back in, they found Sapphira lying there dead. So they carried her out and buried her beside her husband.
translation_verse: Egleʋe adɔ kesea ní Petro aŋu mɛ kyɔŋ lɛ etse e. Gi iyasɔʋi lɛ itiku i, imɔ si etse, lɛ befeke ye dono xe bɛzeevive ye ní yenye su i.

english_verse: The church members were afraid, and so was everyone else who heard what had happened. Peter's Unusual Power
translation_verse: Lɛ loso lifu liku ɔsɔli ha zizi nɔ ni banɔ gɔgɔa ba petee gi benu eboa la ya ɛ. Banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛbitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe gi etu banɔ kugyɔ ɛ

english_verse: The apostles worked many miracles and wonders among the people. All of the Lord's followers often met in the part of the temple known as Solomon's Porch.
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛzɛbitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe glegle gi ába eʋidi i ní banima kusi. Baʋaziŋa ba petee bɛzɛtsa ní Salomo ɔviva nɔ.

english_verse: No one outside their group dared join them, even though everyone liked them very much.
translation_verse: Gani lɛ mɛ si banima bezegu kunugu gbã ní basu kyɛ, banama gi báma ní ba mɛ ɛ, bamɛ ɔtɔtɔ òkeni odi si yiti ipɔni ba a.

english_verse: Many men and women started having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ glegle bɛba ɛpɔni ba gi benu benyime ni badzɛ linɔkpi i, gi lizi Ɔkaɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Then sick people were brought out to the road and placed on mats. It was hoped that Peter would walk by, and his shadow would fall on them and heal them.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛzɛbitɛ loso e, banima bezefeke banyanya ba tsini ablɔ ʋa aʋa lɛ bɛzɛplɛ ba ní abati loo itɔsi lɛ aʋa a, tɔ xe Petro ɛga ɛza ɛ, tɔ yɛ kuyeo kwiadɔ ní ba mɛ batɔ aʋa.

english_verse: A lot of people living in the towns near Jerusalem brought those who were sick or troubled by evil spirits, and they were all healed. Trouble for the Apostles
translation_verse: Lɛ anɔkpi la ɛdɔ imaɛ nɛ gi iklani Yerusalem mɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛmani banama gi ba isu mɛ igɔ loo banama gi ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne iikpɛ ba kunugu yo e; lɛ ba petee bɛmɔ kuyɔyɔ ɛ. Bɛyɔ kedu ní Banɔkpɛkpɛ ba su

english_verse: They arrested the apostles and put them in the city jail.
translation_verse: Bevu banɔkpɛkpɛ ba lɛ bɛkɔ ba ɛkpɛ ní ɔma nɔ ligba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But that night an angel from the Lord opened the doors of the jail and led the apostles out. The angel said,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ elesi la ɛ, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ adra ligba lɛ ipupo le e, lɛ etu ɔtɔ ni banɔkpɛkpɛ ba edono e, lɛ asi ba si,

english_verse: “Go to the temple and tell the people everything about this new life.”
translation_verse: “Mlatrɛ tɔ mlazɛlɛ ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, tɔ mlapani asi banima ní ligbã́zeze vuvu nɛ su.”

english_verse: So they went into the temple before sunrise and started teaching. The high priest and his men called together their council, which included all of Israel's leaders. Then they ordered the apostles to be brought to them from the jail.
translation_verse: Banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛbitɛ ní lɛ aʋa lɛ olezatɔ nɔ ɛ, beti iku ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ lɛ bekpese bidɔ kubasɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso Kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye ni yɛ baʋatrɔ ba bɛxwa Yudanɛ banɔtsitsi ba kí ɔha lɔ kutsatsa; lɛ bɛkpɛ ɔsã tsisi ligba lɛ bepepe ba tɔ bamani banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ní ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The temple police who were sent to the jail did not find the apostles. They returned and said,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi baka ba beku i, bámɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ní ligba lɛ mɛ, lɛ bedziba kutsatsaa mɛ lɛ bedo si,

english_verse: “We found the jail locked tight and the guards standing at the doors. But when we opened the doors and went in, we didn't find anyone there.”
translation_verse: “Gi kwiku ni klɔ ɛ, kwimɔ si ipupo le ìda lɛ banɔpe ba fɛ balɛɛ xe bepe kevunu ya a, pɔ̀ gi kwidra ipupo le e, kúmɔ ɔnɔnɔ ní ligba lɛ mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: The captain of the temple police and the chief priests listened to their report, but they did not know what to think about it.
translation_verse: Agi kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye ni ɔkaɛ ní lisɔligba lɛ bepepe ba onu benu liboe le e, bɛpisa ní kidiliɛ gi kiba banɔkpɛkpɛ ba aʋa su u.

english_verse: Just then someone came in and said, “Now those men you put in jail are in the temple, teaching the people!”
translation_verse: Lɛ onyime ɔtɔ aba asi ba si, “Mlata kutu! Benyime ba ya gi mlɛkpɛ ligba lɛ ɛ, bali ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ gi bɛbasɛ banima bidɔ mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: The captain went with some of the temple police and brought the apostles back. But they did not use force. They were afraid that the people might start throwing stones at them.
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔpe ba ɔkaɛ ni yɛ banima bɛzɛgbani banɔkpɛkpɛ ba dzini manɔ ɛ. Bákɔ kigagla ni ba, lɛ ese linu si bezese lifu si banima biasa ba akla.

english_verse: When the apostles were brought before the council, the high priest said to them,
translation_verse: Gi bɛgbani banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ɛmanɔ ɛ, bɛtsini ba ní Yudanɛ ma betsixwa ba aŋu mɛ, lɛ kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye eʋi ba si,

english_verse: “We told you plainly not to teach in the name of Jesus. But look what you have done! You have been teaching all over Jerusalem, and you are trying to blame us for his death.”
translation_verse: “Kwikpɛ ɔsã gagla ɛki mlɔ si mlakíbasɛ bidɔ ní onyime liɛ ya linyi ne me tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ mledi kidiliɛ gi kɛ mlɛbitɛ ɛ! Mlene mlɔ bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ ní Yerusalem petee, lɛ mlɛpɛ si mlebu blo lipɔ si blɔ kwiye ye e!”

english_verse: Peter and the apostles replied: We don't obey people. We obey God.
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ni banɔkpɛkpɛ gɔgɔ ba bebu le onu si, “Linu si kunu kutu kí Aya sani kí banima.

english_verse: You killed Jesus by nailing him to a cross. But the God our ancestors worshiped raised him to life
translation_verse: Blɔ bakatsi ba Aya yɛ ayɔni Yesu adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ gi mleku ye ní ose lo su u, xe etse e.

english_verse: and made him our Leader and Savior. Then God gave him a place at his right side, so that the people of Israel would turn back to him and be forgiven.
translation_verse: Aya emuni ye atsinɔ yɛ kaŋaa mɛ tɔ oze Okusi obi ni Ɔnɔbu, tɔ ɔki Israelanɛ ma kudeonu tɔ bekyi tɔ Aya aakɔ̀ ba eboe zimizi na kɔ kyɛ ba.

english_verse: We are here to tell you about all this, and so is the Holy Spirit, who is God's gift to everyone who obeys God.
translation_verse: Kunu katsali aki eboa la ya, blɔ ni Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, gi Aya aki blɔ banama gi kuzeenu kutu ikí yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When the council members heard this, they became so angry they wanted to kill the apostles.
translation_verse: Gi banama a gi bɛtsa xe benu liboe le e, bɛmɔ kusuyeʋi gbɛgbɛ nite si bɛpɛ si beeye banɔkpɛkpɛ ba xe e.

english_verse: But one of the members was the Pharisee Gamaliel, a highly respected teacher. He ordered the apostles to be taken out of the room for a little while.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ betsixwa ba mɛ tole e, Farisi ɔnɛ gi bizɛxwa si Gamaliɛl i, gi onu Mose ɔsã nɔ ɔdɔbasɛ ɛ, xe edzì onu ɔnɔ ɔ gi banima pɔ́ bɛkpɛ kubùbù yɛ su i, ayɔ alɛɛ ní kutsatsaa mɛ. Aki si bebu banɔkpɛkpɛ ba,

english_verse: Then he said to the council: Men of Israel, be careful what you do with these men.
translation_verse: lɛ asi kutsatsaa si, “Israelbanɛ ma, mledi mumɔ ní kidiliɛ gi mlɛpɛ si mlɛbitɛ mani banɔ ba ya su u.

english_verse: Later, when the people of our nation were being counted, Judas from Galilee showed up. A lot of people followed him, but he was killed, and all his followers were scattered.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, Yuda Galilea ɔnɛɛ aŋwi ní banima lìklapoe le, gi ehe linɔkpi apɔni isu ya a, pɔ̀ beye yɛ kyɛ, lɛ banama gi bezeni yɛ kude e, ba petee behle.

english_verse: So I advise you to stay away from these men. Leave them alone. If what they are planning is something of their own doing, it will fail.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ni liboe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, mɛɛgba mlɔ si, mlakíkɔ liboe ní benyime ba ya su u. Mlayurɔ ba! Xe ba batrɔtrɔ ʋa ni kiboebitɛɛ kidɔ banima kɔ ɛ, biadu,

english_verse: But if God is behind it, you cannot stop it anyway, unless you want to fight against God. The council members agreed with what he said,
translation_verse: pɔ̀ xe bidɔ Aya kɔ ɛ, mlatrátani ba aʋa kuŋa a. Mlaamɔ isu ya si mlɛɛta kigũ ni Aya!” Kutsatsaa kwizi Gamaliɛl yɛ kugbagba.

english_verse: and they called the apostles back in. They had them beaten with a whip and warned them not to speak in the name of Jesus. Then they let them go.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛxwa banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ɛmanɔ ɛ, bekusi ba, lɛ bɛkpɛ ɔsã ɛki ba si bakípani ní Yesu linyi ne me liwewe tɔ ɔ; lɛ bɛyurɔ ba ɛ.

english_verse: The apostles left the council and were happy, because God had considered them worthy to suffer for the sake of Jesus.
translation_verse: Gi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛsɛ ní kutsatsaa mɛ ɛ, isami iku ba, lɛ ese linu si Aya ebù ba si begyeno si bakyɛ biaŋa kuye kɔkpɛ Yesu.

english_verse: Every day they spent time in the temple and in one home after another. They never stopped teaching and telling the good news that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Lɛ liwe kakaa a, ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, ní banima kupeo me, bɛzɛɛbasɛ bidɔ lɛ bezeedo Aya yɛ liboe Gbã nɛ ní Yesu gi onu Kristo ye su u.

english_verse: A lot of people were now becoming followers of the Lord. But some of the ones who spoke Greek started complaining about the ones who spoke Aramaic. They complained that the Greek-speaking widows were not given their share when the food supplies were handed out each day.
translation_verse: Ni lipoetɔ kede gi badɔkpasi ba bɛzɛɛsrɛ pɔnɔ ɛ, kuboeŋwlɛ kwiku ní Yudanɛ ama gi bizɛpani Sihela sɛ ni Yudanɛ ama gi bɛpɔ ní ba tutɔ ba kesea mɛ kusi i. Yudanɛ ama gi bizɛpani Sihela sɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ si bizɛdɔ ba badzɛaba gi ba benye ba betse e, za ní liwe le kaʋadi ya mɛ.

english_verse: The twelve apostles called the whole group of followers together and said, “We should not give up preaching God's message in order to serve at tables.
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba bɛxwa baʋaziŋa ba ɔha lɔ ɛkpɛ likpɔ lɛ bɛpani si, “Aní liboe gbã linu si kuyurɔ Aya liboe le kubasɛɔ tɔ kuvu kibɔ kulɛlɛ loo tɔ kuzedi badzɛaba gi ba benye ba betse ba su i aʋa.

english_verse: My friends, choose seven men who are respected and wise and filled with God's Spirit. We will put them in charge of these things.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, benemi ba, mlebu benyime tiegloele dɔni mlɔ mɛ, gi mlete si benu anukwarɛ benete e, gi beyi nì Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, nì kuboeteo, tɔ kuki tɔ beze ní eboa la ya onu u.

english_verse: We can spend our time praying and serving God by preaching.”
translation_verse: Tɔ blɔ tutɔ kukɔ blɔ lipoe le petee kukɔkpɛ ní Aya kuxwaxwa ni Aya yɛ liboe le kubasɛɔ axwɛ na mɛ.”

english_verse: This suggestion pleased everyone, and they began by choosing Stephen. He had great faith and was filled with the Holy Spirit. Then they chose Philip, Prochorus, Nicanor, Timon, Parmenas, and also Nicolaus, who worshiped with the Jewish people in Antioch.
translation_verse: Likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ petee limɔ isami ní banɔkpɛkpɛ ba batrɔtrɔ ʋa su, lɛ loso bebu Stefano, ɔnɔ gi eyi ni kuziŋa ni Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kɔpɔni Filipo, Prokoro, Nikanora, Timona, Pamena ni Nikolao gi ádɔ Antioxia xe ekyi eku Yudanɛ ma kusɔliɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: These men were brought to the apostles. Then the apostles prayed and placed their hands on the men to show they had been chosen to do this work.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔha lɔ ɛgbani ba ɛmani banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ɛ, lɛ bɛxwa Aya ní ba litukpo o, xe bɛtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: God's message spread, and many more people in Jerusalem became followers. Even a large number of priests put their faith in the Lord. Stephen Is Arrested
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Aya liboe le liizene onugu i pɔnɔ. Badɔkpasi ba ní Yerusalem bɛzɛɛsrɛ pɔnɔ lɛ kusumu yo baka glegle kyɛ bɛziŋa a. Stefano Kuvuvu yo

english_verse: God gave Stephen the power to work great miracles and wonders among the people.
translation_verse: Stefano, onyimee gi Aya akrira glegle e, xe Aya kigaglaɛ kiyi yɛ mɛ ɛ, abitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe gi aʋíba ʋidi ní banima kusi i.

english_verse: But some men from Cyrene and Alexandria were members of a group who called themselves “Free Men.” They started arguing with Stephen. Some others from Cilicia and Asia also argued with him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔatɔ gi bɛdɔ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsa kɔ́ lɔ gi ɔlɛ ní Kirene e , bɛyɔ kedu ni yɛ su. Banɔ ba ya benu Yudanɛ gi bɛdɔ ní Kirene ni Aleksandria. Yudanɛ gɔgɔa ba gi bɛdɔ Kilikia kuseo aʋa ani Asia ɛ, bekpese eboe kuŋwlɛ ni Stefano.

english_verse: But they were no match for Stephen, who spoke with the great wisdom that the Spirit gave him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛki Stefano kuboete nite me si gi apani i, bátani yɛ eboe la onu kulɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So they talked some men into saying, “We heard Stephen say terrible things against Moses and God!”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛbitɛ kunyanya ni banɔatɔ ní kikpokpo me tɔ bapani si, “Kwinu gi ɛɛpani ebusioboe mani Mose ni Aya su u!”

english_verse: They turned the people and their leaders and the teachers of the Law of Moses against Stephen. Then they all grabbed him and dragged him in front of the council.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bezizi banima, banɔtsitsi ba ni ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ ba ní Stefano su, lɛ bevu ye e, xe bɛtsini yɛ ní Yudanɛ betsixwa ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some men agreed to tell lies about Stephen, and they said, “This man keeps on saying terrible things about this holy temple and the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛzɛmani banɔatɔ si basɛ asia akpɛ yɛ awla si, “Onyime liɛ ya ɛ, ɛɛpani ebusioboe lipoe kakaa ní blɔ Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ gi kuzeesumu Aya a, ni Mose ɔsã nɔ su.

english_verse: We have heard him claim that Jesus from Nazareth will destroy this place and change the customs Moses gave us.”
translation_verse: Kwinu gi apani si Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛ ɛ, aakpa Lisɔligba lɛ, lɛ aatsrɛ ikɔ̀ɛ lɛ gi idɔ Mose klɔ ɛki blɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Then all the council members stared at Stephen. They saw that his face looked like the face of an angel.
translation_verse: Banama petee gi beze ní kutsatsa ʋidi yo me e, bedu aŋu ní Stefano aʋa lɛ bɛmɔ si yɛ aŋu na mɛ alɛ ŋwisi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ anɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The high priest asked Stephen, “Are they telling the truth about you?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye eʋi Stefano si, “Eboa la gi bɛkɔ ní wɔ su i, alɛ la mɛ na a?”

english_verse: Stephen answered: Friends, listen to me. Our glorious God appeared to our ancestor Abraham while he was still in Mesopotamia, before he had moved to Haran.
translation_verse: Lɛ Stefano ebu le onu si, “Benemi ba ni baka ba, mlata mɛ kutu u! Xe ableke blɔ ɔkatsi, Abraham iize ní Haran i, Aya gi ekeme e, ebu isu abasi yɛ ní Mesopotamia,

english_verse: God told him, “Leave your country and your relatives and go to a land that I will show you.”
translation_verse: lɛ asi yɛ si, ‘Sɛ ní wɔ benete ba ni wɔ kesea mɛ, tɔ trɛ ní kesea lia gi maabasɛ wɔ aʋa a!’

english_verse: Then Abraham left the land of the Chaldeans and settled in Haran. After his father died, Abraham came and settled in this land where you now live.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso e, asɛ ní Kaldeanɛ ma kesea aʋa lɛ azeeze ní Haran i. Ní Abraham yɛka kutseo kede e, Aya aki si ɔba kesea lia aʋa gi mlalɛ abla ɛ.

english_verse: God didn't give him any part of it, not even a square meter. But God did promise to give it to him and his family forever, even though Abraham didn't have any children.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Aya ɔki Abraham kesea kapɛmiatɔtɔ ŋwisi yɛ ɔŋakɔ kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ Aya aka aplɛ akí yɛ si, yɛ akɔ kesea kí yɛ lɛ kiaze yɛ ni yɛ besoebi bà kanɛ ɛ. Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ nite e, obi ɔtɔtɔ óze ní Abraham awla a.

english_verse: God said Abraham's descendants would live for a while in a foreign land. There they would be slaves and would be mistreated
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya Aya apani aki yɛ, ‘Wɔ besoebi ba biaze ní kese dzedze gi kénu ba kanɛ aʋa a, niklɔ biaze banɔdzidzi lɛ biagbani ba ni litukpo me gagla a, lɛ biabu kini ki ba agɔ alafatiene e.

english_verse: But he also said, “I will punish the nation that makes them slaves. Then later they will come and worship me in this place.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ maahe kutu kí banama gi ba biasumu i, ŋwisi banɔ dzidzi kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kede e, biasɛ kesea lia klɔ aʋa tɔ biaba sumu me ní ya.’

english_verse: God said to Abraham, “Every son in each family must be circumcised to show you have kept your agreement with me.” So when Isaac was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him. Later, Isaac circumcised his son Jacob, and Jacob circumcised his twelve sons.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya aki si Abraham oxo ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ ligoʋi le tɔ lize gyesi kí kunyanya ní Aya ni Abraham kusi. Lɛ loso gi bɛpɔ Isak ogle tole kede e, Abraham atsa ɔyaʋi aki yɛ, Isak atsa ɔyaʋi aki Yebi Yakob, lɛ Yakob fɛ atsa iyaʋi aki yɛ bebi liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba gi ba benu blɔ amade na baka ba a.

english_verse: These men were our ancestors. Joseph was also one of our famous ancestors. His brothers were jealous of him and sold him as a slave to be taken to Egypt. But God was with him
translation_verse: “Gi Yakob yɛ bebi ba bɛkɔ kaŋuza ní ba onemi ye Yosef su onu i, bevu yeda tɔ oze ɔnɔdzidzi ní Egipte. Pɔ̀ Aya eze ni yɛ kude e,

english_verse: and rescued him from all his troubles. God made him so wise that the Egyptian king Pharaoh thought highly of him. The king even made Joseph governor over Egypt and put him in charge of everything he owned.
translation_verse: lɛ ebu ye ní yɛ kini mɔmɔɛ mɛ ɛ. Aya aki Yosef kuboete, lɛ aki si Yosef eku Egipte okusi ye Farao aŋu u, lɛ okusi ye abitɛ Yosef Egipte kesea ni kekusipea ɔʋadi i.

english_verse: Everywhere in Egypt and Canaan the grain crops failed. There was terrible suffering, and our ancestors could not find enough to eat.
translation_verse: “Lɛ kuʋɛ kwidɔ ní Egipte petee ni Kanaa i, gi banima beze ní kuxixa glegle me e. Blɔ bakatsi ba bámɔ kiŋaŋa ɛtɔtɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But when Jacob heard there was grain in Egypt, he sent our ancestors there for the first time.
translation_verse: Gi Yakob enu si adeʋi alɛ ni Egipte e, akpɛ yɛ bebi ba gi benu blɔ bakatsitsi ba atsisi ní klɔ, ba kutɔpu yɔ ɔ.

english_verse: It was on their second trip that Joseph told his brothers who he was, and the king learned about Joseph's family.
translation_verse: Kuʋlaa gi bɛdzɛ ɛ, Yosef ebu isu abasi yɛ benemi ba lɛ ebu ba abasi Farao kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Joseph sent for his father and his relatives. In all, there were
translation_verse: Lɛ Yosef atsisi yɛka Yakob ni yɛ baŋanɛ ba petee gi beze banɔ avitegloele ni tietsu i, ole si ba ba Egipte.

english_verse: His father went to Egypt and died there, just as our ancestors did.
translation_verse: Nite si Yakob atrɛ ní Egipte, eze niklɔ kɔzɛna ni yɛ litsewe le, niklɔ yɛ bebi ba fɛ betse.

english_verse: Later their bodies were taken back to Shechem and placed in the tomb that Abraham had bought from the sons of Hamor.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, bɛmani ba bekpo ba ní Sixem, niklɔ bevive ba ní ɔdrɔ lɔ gi Abraham edzi ni kibɔ ní Xemor yɛ bebi ba awla a.

english_verse: Finally, the time came for God to do what he had promised Abraham. By then the number of our people in Egypt had greatly increased.
translation_verse: “Gi lipoe le liti pɔnɔ si Aya ɔbitɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa kí Abraham i, blɔ akɔanɛ ma ní Egipte bɛdɔ̀ ɛpɔnɔ.

english_verse: Another king was ruling Egypt, and he didn't know anything about Joseph.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede Okusi dzedze gi óte liboeboe ní Yosef su i, ekpese kukusi kuŋa ní Egipte.

english_verse: He tricked our ancestors and was cruel to them. He even made them leave their babies outside, so they would die.
translation_verse: Lɛ okusi liɛ ekyi blɔ baka ba axla a, lɛ abitɛ litukpo me gagla ní ba su i, emi ba tɔ bebu ba bebivigye ba zanɔ ní ba kupeo me tɔ betse.

english_verse: During this time Moses was born. He was a very beautiful child, and for three months his parents took care of him in their home.
translation_verse: “Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ bɛpɔ Mose, eze obi gi eyi kuyasɔ ɔ. Yɛ bapɔnɛ ba bɛgani yɛ ní yɛka kepea mɛ badzidzi tiata,

english_verse: Then when they were forced to leave him outside, the king's daughter found him and raised him as her own son.
translation_verse: lɛ gi bátani yɛ kukpo ní kepea mɛ tɔ ɛ, bebú ye ní kepea mɛ, okusi ye yebidzɛɛ amɔ yɛ lɛ azɛ yɛ aganɔ ŋwisi yɛ ɔtutɔ yebinyime kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Moses was given the best education in Egypt. He was a strong man and a powerful speaker.
translation_verse: Bɛbasɛ yɛ Egipte banɛ ma kuboete kakaa, lɛ edzì ɔnɔ ʋidi ní kiboepani ni kiboebitɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: One day he saw an Egyptian mistreating one of them. So he rescued the man and killed the Egyptian.
translation_verse: Amɔ ba mɛ tole gi Egipteɔnɛɔtɔ azeebu kini akɔ ɛ, azɛɛpɔni yɛ lɛ beye Egipteɔnɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Moses thought the rest of his people would realize God was going to use him to set them free. But they didn't understand.
translation_verse: Akɔ lɛ si yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ benete ba bianu lɛ ese si Aya yɛ akpɛ yɛ tɔ yibu ba, pɔ̀ bénu lɛ ese e.

english_verse: The next day Moses saw two of his own people fighting, and he tried to make them stop. He said, “Men, you are both Israelites. Why are you so cruel to each other?”
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, Mose edzi atrɛ ní báklɔ lɛ amɔ Israel bebi tiaʋa gi bɛzɛgɔni i, ekeni edi si yikpɛ ba likpɔ. Lɛ loso apani si, Benyime ba, mlata kutu, benemi Israel bebi mlenu; ege lɛ loso mlɛpɛ si mlɛkpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba ebi i?

english_verse: But the man who had started the fight pushed Moses aside and asked, “Who made you our ruler and judge?
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ɛɛbu kinie ki yenemie e, etutru Mose atsini kapɛ lɛ eʋi ye si, ‘Nyaŋwɛ edu wo ɔʋaŋa ni otsixwa ní blɔ onu u?’

english_verse: Are you going to kill me, just as you killed that Egyptian yesterday?”
translation_verse: Kipɛ wɔpɛ si, ‘Wuye me kilɛ gi woye Egipteɔnɛɛ kivoe na a?’

english_verse: When Moses heard this, he ran away to live in the country of Midian. His two sons were born there.
translation_verse: Gi Mose enu liboe le e, ese ní Egipte lɛ azeeze ní Midia kesea aʋa ŋwisi ɔbaba kinɛ ɛ. Niklɔ apɔ bebinyime tiaʋa.

english_verse: Forty years later, an angel appeared to Moses from a burning bush in the desert near Mount Sinai.
translation_verse: “Agɔ avitene kede e, Aya ɔxwɛdzɛ ebu isu abasi Mose ní lifuha gi liisɔ ní bibu ose sisami ɔtɔ mɛ ɛ, ní ɔyaya kɔ ní Sinai lito le su.

english_verse: Moses was surprised by what he saw. He went closer to get a better look, and the Lord said,
translation_verse: Mose yɛ onugu etse ní kidiliɛ gi amɔ su u, lɛ eti apɔni lifuha lɛ si yidi le mɔmɔmɔ. Pɔ̀ enu Ɔkaɛ yɛ ole lo si,

english_verse: “I am the God who was worshiped by your ancestors, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.” Moses started shaking all over and didn't dare to look at the bush.
translation_verse: ‘Mɛ menu mlɔ bakatsi ba ba Aya, Mɛ menu Abraham, Isak ni Yakob ba Aya.’ Lifu liku Mose lɛ ókeni odi si yidi tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Take off your sandals, because the place where you are standing is holy.
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ asi yɛ si, ‘Bu afɔkpa ʋa ní wɔ kuklikpɔɔ lɛ ese linu si niklɔ gi wɔlɛɛ ɛ, ɔdradra kɔ níklɔ onu.

english_verse: With my own eyes I have seen the suffering of my people in Egypt. I have heard their groans and have come down to rescue them. Now I am sending you back to Egypt.”
translation_verse: Manyamɔ kini glegle liɛ mɛ gi mɛ benete ba balɛ ní Egipte e. Menu ba kutani yɔ lɛ meple aba tɔ míbu ba a. Abla ɛ ba, maadzini wɔ kukpɛ ní Egipte.’

english_verse: This was the same Moses that the people rejected by saying, “Who made you our leader and judge?” God's angel had spoken to Moses from the bush. And God had even sent the angel to help Moses rescue the people and be their leader.
translation_verse: “Mose liɛ ke Israelbanɛ ma begbe, gi beʋi ye si, ‘Nyaŋwɛ edu wo ɔʋaŋa ni otsixwa ní blɔ onu u?’ Yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi Aya akpɛ si ɔŋa banima aʋa tɔ obu ba gani Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi ebu isu abasi yɛ ní lifuha lɛ mɛ ɛ aʋa.

english_verse: In Egypt and at the Red Sea and in the desert, Moses rescued the people by working miracles and wonders for
translation_verse: Yɛ etu ɔtɔ ni banima edono ní Egipte, xe abitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe mamɔ edi la ní Egipte e, ní axu kyíkyĩ la kutu, ani agɔ avitene na mɛ gi beze ní ɔyaya klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Moses is the one who told the people of Israel, “God will choose one of your people to be a prophet, just as he chose me.”
translation_verse: “Mose liɛ ke asi Israelbanɛ ma si, ‘Aya aatsisi mlɔ oboedoplɛ, kiliɛ gi atsisi mɛ ɛ, lɛ aaze mlɔ tutɔ mlɔ mɛ tole e.’

english_verse: Moses brought our people together in the desert, and the angel spoke to him on Mount Sinai. There he was given these life-giving words to pass on to us.
translation_verse: Yɛ akpɛ blɔ baka ba likpɔ ŋwisi Israel banɛ ní ɔyaya klɔ lɛ yɛ Aya ɔxwɛdzɛɛ apani akɔ ní Sinai lito le aʋa a, lɛ yɛ azɛ Aya eboe la gi izɛki ligbã aki blɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But our ancestors refused to obey Moses. They rejected him and wanted to go back to Egypt.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ blɔ bakatsi ba begbe bénu kutu aki yɛ ɛ, boŋu i, beku ye ɔwlaku lɛ begyodi ní ba ina nɛ mɛ si biadzitrɛ ní Egipte e.

english_verse: Then they made an idol in the shape of a calf. They offered sacrifices to the idol and were pleased with what they had done.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, bɛʋɔ litugu ní ligume kilɛlɛɛ mɛ, lɛ bɛmani lɛ bidɔ kɔkɔ gi bɛkɔ eyi busu iiki lɛ, lɛ bɛŋa liwe ni isami gi bɛkɔ ɛkpɛ kubùbù ní kidiliɛ gi ba tutɔ bɛʋɔ su u.

english_verse: God turned his back on his people and left them. Then they worshiped the stars in the sky, just as it says in the Book of the Prophets, “People of Israel, you didn't offer sacrifices and offerings to me during those
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya adzi kede ní ba su u, lɛ ayurɔ ba tɔ besumu lifunu ne Liwo le, ɔdzidzi yɛ ni badzidziebi ʋa a, kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní beboedoplɛ ba keplekpaa mɛ ɛ si, “ ‘Israel banɛ ma! Aní mɛ loso mleye baga ʋa xe mlɛki bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi ʋɛ ɛ agɔ avitene na ní ɔyaya klɔ.

english_verse: Instead, you carried the tent where the god Molech is worshiped, and you took along the star of your god Rephan. You made those idols and worshiped them. So now I will have you carried off beyond Babylonia.”
translation_verse: Mlemuni Molɛx yɛ libugba lɛ, ni Romfan ɔbu yɛ yɛ ɔdzidziobi ye; ba beze etugu gi mlɔ tutɔ mlɛʋɔ xe mlezeesumu u. Lɛ loso e maatsisi mlɔ ní aboyo la me ní Babilonia kesea kede.’

english_verse: The tent where our ancestors worshiped God was with them in the desert. This was the same tent that God had commanded Moses to make. And it was made like the model that Moses had seen.
translation_verse: “Aya likatsa lɛ lize ní blɔ bakatsi ba su ní ɔyaya klɔ. Bɛwa lɛ kiliɛ gi Aya asi Mose si ɔwa lɛ ɛ, ní kilɛlɛ liɛ klɔ mɛ gi Aya abasi Mose onu u.

english_verse: Later it was given to our ancestors, and they took it with them when they went with Joshua. They carried the tent along as they took over the land from those people that God had chased out for them. Our ancestors used this tent until the time of King David.
translation_verse: Lɛ klɔ ʋa kede e, gi blɔ bakatsi ba bɛzi likatsa lɛ dɔni ba baka ba klɔ ɛ, betu le gi bezeni Yosua kude e, xe bɛzi kesea ní amade ana na gi Aya akpikpini edono awla a, ní kiliɛ gi bɛzɛtrɛ onu u. Lɛ lize ní kesea mɛ zɛkɔna ni Dawid lipoe le.

english_verse: He pleased God and asked him if he could build a house of worship for the people of Israel.
translation_verse: Gi amɔ lipomepɛpɛ dɔni Aya kɔ lɛ eʋi Aya tɔ ɔki yɛ kude tɔ yiwa ozekɔ ki Yɛ Yakob yɛ Aya.

english_verse: And it was finally King Solomon who built a house for God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Salomo yɛ awa ligba aki Aya.

english_verse: But the Most High God doesn't live in houses made by humans. It is just as the prophet said, when he spoke for the Lord,
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ Kaka Aya Baka ba Ɔkaɛ óʋize ní agba gi ɔnɔ gbagbe awa mɛ ɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi oboedoplɛɛ apani kinɛ ɛ si,

english_verse: “Heaven is my throne, and the earth is my footstool. What kind of house will you build for me? In what place will I rest?
translation_verse: “Ɔkaɛ si, ‘Lifunu ne linu mɛ likusigbo, lɛ kesea kenu mɛ ikli lɛ odukɔ. Ligba woli lu mlaawa kimɛ ɛ? Lɛ nífɔ aaze mɛ ozekɔ ɔ?

english_verse: Is there one prophet that your ancestors didn't mistreat? They killed the prophets who told about the coming of the One Who Obeys God. And now you have turned against him and killed him.
translation_verse: Oboedoplɛ ɔtɔ eze gi mlɔ bakatsi ba báyɔ kedu ní yɛ su edi na a? Beye Aya yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba, gi bedo le eplɛ si ɔniliɛ gi onu Aya yɛ ɔnɔsumu Dradra yɛ ɛ, gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ, atrába. Lɛ abla ɛ, mlebu ye kɔ lɛ mleye ye e.

english_verse: Angels gave you God's Law, but you still don't obey it. Stephen Is Stoned to Death
translation_verse: Mlɔ mlenu banama gi mlɛzi Aya ɔsã nɔ, gi Aya baxwɛdzɛ ba bɛkɔ ɛki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mlaʋíbitɛ ní lɔ aʋa a!” Bɛsa Stefano Akla

english_verse: When the council members heard Stephen's speech, they were angry and furious.
translation_verse: Gi kutsatsa ʋidi yo me banima benu Stefano liboe le ní ba su i, beyeʋi isu nyani nyani nì yɛ.

english_verse: But Stephen was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked toward heaven, where he saw our glorious God and Jesus standing at his right side.
translation_verse: Stefano pɔ̀ ɛ, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛki si edi lifunu ne, lɛ amɔ Aya yɛ kuklekleo ni Yesu gi ɔlɛɛ ní Aya yɛ kaŋaa mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: Then Stephen said, “I see heaven open and the Son of Man standing at the right side of God!”
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si, “Mledi! Mamɔ lifunu ne gi lidra ɛ, lɛ ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye ɔlɛɛ ní Aya yɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: The council members shouted and covered their ears. At once they all attacked Stephen
translation_verse: Banima bɛkpɛ ozi lo gagla, lɛ betsí ba atukpa la ni ba iwla lɛ ɛ. Lɛ ba petee behuiku yɛ aʋa a,

english_verse: and dragged him out of the city. Then they started throwing stones at him. The men who had brought charges against him put their coats at the feet of a young man named Saul.
translation_verse: behe ye dono ní ɔma ʋidi no me lɛ beku yɛ akla kusa mɛ ɛ. Banama gi beze liboe le katsalia ɛ, bɛyurɔ ba ewu la ɛki ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Saulo si ozedi la aʋa a.

english_verse: As Stephen was being stoned to death, he called out, “Lord Jesus, please welcome me!”
translation_verse: Gi bɛzɛsa Stefano akla na ɛ, axwa Aya si, “Ɔkaɛ Yesu, zi mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ!”

english_verse: He knelt down and shouted, “Lord, don't blame them for what they have done.” Then he died.
translation_verse: Lɛ adɔ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa lɛ akpɛ ozi ni ole gagla si, “Ɔkaɛ! wukívu kuzimizi uliɔ ya ɛplɛ kí ba a!” Gi apani nite ko e, ada yɛ onugu lo.

english_verse: Saul started making a lot of trouble for the church. He went from house to house, arresting men and women and putting them in jail. The Good News Is Preached in Samaria
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Saulo atrɔ lɛ si yiyaʋi baʋaziŋa ba likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ; etiku kupeo me, lɛ ehe banama gi bɛziŋa ɛ, badzɛ ni benyime akpɛ ligba. Filipo edo liboe gbã nɛ ní Samaria

english_verse: The Lord's followers who had been scattered went from place to place, telling the good news.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛziŋa ɛ, besehle lɛ bɛzɛpani liboe le ní ɔkɔ kakaa gi bɛtrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Philip went to the city of Samaria and told the people about Christ.
translation_verse: Filipo kɔ ɛ, atrɛ ní ɔma ʋidi ɔtɔ mɛ ní Samaria, lɛ asɔ̀li Kristo aki banima níklɔ ɛ.

english_verse: They crowded around Philip because they were eager to hear what he was saying and to see him work miracles.
translation_verse: Linɔkpi le lita liboe lɛ gi Filipo apani kutu mɔmɔmɔ ɔ, gi bezeenu yɛ kupaniɔ ɛ, lɛ bɛmɔ eboe mamɔ edi ala gi abitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Many people with evil spirits were healed, and the spirits went out of them with a shout. A lot of paralyzed and lame people were also healed.
translation_verse: Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne ido eza ní banɔ glegle me ni izí gagla, lɛ banɔ glegle gi ba kapɛ tiele ketse e, ni banɔ gi bɛklɔʋi i, ba petee bɛyɔ.
