english_verse: Everyone in that city was very glad because of what was happening.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso isami glegle ize ní ɔma ʋidi ɔlɔ klɔ mɛ.

english_verse: For some time a man named Simon had lived there and had amazed the people of Samaria. He practiced witchcraft and claimed to be somebody great.
translation_verse: Onyime ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Simon eze ní klɔ ɔ, lɛ kɔki lipoetɔ ɛ, aki Samaria banɛ ma inugu itse nì yɛ bawa ʋa. Izefeke isu ya si yenu ɔnɔ ʋidi,

english_verse: Everyone, rich and poor, crowded around him. They said, “This man is the power of God called ‘The Great Power.’ ”
translation_verse: lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ní ɔma ʋidi no me dɔni kilɛlɛ kakaa mɛ ɛ, izɛta yɛ kutu, lɛ izɛpani si, “yɛ onu Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ kapɛa lia klɔ gi bizɛxwa si ‘kigagla ʋidi ye.’ ”

english_verse: For a long time, Simon had used witchcraft to amaze the people, and they kept crowding around him.
translation_verse: Bizɛta yɛ kutu lɛ ese linu si kí lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ ɛ, aki ba inugu itse ni yɛ bawa ʋa.

english_verse: But when they believed what Philip was saying about God's kingdom and about the name of Jesus Christ, they were all baptized.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi bɛzi Filipo liboe le aʋa ɛɛŋa ní Aya kukusi ŋaŋa ni Yesu Kristo liboe gbã nɛ su i, bɛkpɛ kuni kí ba, benyime ni badzɛ.

english_verse: Even Simon believed and was baptized. He stayed close to Philip, because he marveled at all the miracles and wonders.
translation_verse: Simon ɔtutɔ fɛ aziŋa, lɛ gi bɛkpɛ kuni kí yɛ pɔ́ ɛ, amìnì ni Filipo lɛ bize linugutseboe kí yɛ gi amɔ eboe mamɔ edi ʋidi ala gi ɛzɛba la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The apostles in Jerusalem heard that some people in Samaria had accepted God's message, and they sent Peter and John.
translation_verse: Gi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ní Yerusalem benu si banima ní Samaria bɛzi Aya liboe le e, bɛkpɛ Petro ni Yohanes tsisi ba.

english_verse: When the two apostles arrived, they prayed that these people would be given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Agi bezeku i, bɛxwa Aya ní banama gi bɛziŋa litukpo o, tɔ bazɛ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ

english_verse: Before this, the Holy Spirit had not been given to any of them, though some of them had been baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si bamɛ ɔ́tɔtɔ ɔʋízɛ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ sukɔ ɔ, kuni ko bɛkpɛ kí ba ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu linyi ne me.

english_verse: Peter and John then placed their hands on everyone who had faith in the Lord, and they were given the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro ni Yohanes bɛtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa a, lɛ bɛzi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Simon noticed that the Spirit was given only when the apostles placed their hands on the people. So he brought money
translation_verse: Simon amɔ si bɛki banama gi bɛziŋa ɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi banɔkpɛkpɛ ba bɛtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa ɛ. Lɛ azɛɛki Petro ni Yohanes kibɔ ɔ,

english_verse: and said to Peter and John, “Let me have this power too! Then anyone I place my hands on will also be given the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: lɛ apani si, “Mlaki mɛ kyɛ kigaglaɛ liɛ ya, tɔ ɔnɔ kakaa aʋa gi maatrɔ awla ɛ, tɔ ɔzɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra nɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Peter said to him, “You and your money will both end up in hell if you think you can buy God's gift!
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro ebu le onu aki yɛ si, “Atɔ wɔ ni wɔ kibɔɛ mlatrɛ ní kifu moni ye me, gi wogyodi si wɔatani Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ kudzi ni kibɔ ɛ!

english_verse: You don't have any part in this, and God sees that your heart isn't right.
translation_verse: Ɔbitɛkɔ loo ɔŋakɔ ɔma ní wɔ awla ní blɔ axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si wɔ ɔna mɛ ɔ́dra ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I can see that you are jealous and bound by your evil ways.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mamɔ si kaŋuzã tiitii kalɛ ní wɔ mɛ, lɛ wonu ɔnɔdzidzi ki eboe zimizi i.”

english_verse: Simon said, “Please pray to the Lord, so that what you said won't happen to me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Simon asi ba si, “Maki litukpo o, mlaxwa Aya ní mɛ onu tɔ eboa la ya gi wɔpani i, la mɛ litɔtɔ likí ba mɛ aʋa.”

english_verse: After Peter and John had preached about the Lord, they returned to Jerusalem. On their way they told the good news in many villages of Samaria. Philip and an Ethiopian Official
translation_verse: Gi Petro ni Yohanes bɛŋa katsalia ɛki ba pɔ́ ɔ, xe bɛpani Ɔkaɛ liboe le e, bedzitrɛ ní Yerusalem. Ní ba kudeo aʋa ɛ, bɛpani Liboe Gbã nɛ ní Samaria kumami glegle me. Filipo apani aki Ethiopia ɔnɛɛ

english_verse: The Lord's angel said to Philip, “Go south along the desert road that leads from Jerusalem to Gaza.”
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ɔtɔ asi Filipo si, “Bla tɔ trɛ ní kaala kudeo gi kwidɔ Yerusalem i trɛ ní Gaza aʋa,” gi kunu ɔyayakɔ kude e.

english_verse: So Philip left. An important Ethiopian official happened to be going along that road in his chariot. He was the chief treasurer for Candace, the Queen of Ethiopia. The official had gone to Jerusalem to worship
translation_verse: Lɛ abla xe atrɛ ɛ, lɛ ní kudeo aʋa ɛ, atsani Ethiopiaɔnɛ ɔdɔaʋadi ɔtɔ gi bebu ye agùaza ɛ, xe azeedi Ethiopia okusidzɛɛ Kandake yɛ kepimia petee aʋa a, aba Yerusalem Aya ɔxwa kɔ,

english_verse: and was now on his way home. He was sitting in his chariot, reading the book of the prophet Isaiah.
translation_verse: lɛ gi edziisɛ trɛ ní kepea ní yɛ kìke loo tasiaɖam yɛ mɛ ɛ, azɛɛkla oboedoplɛɛ Yesaya yɛ keplekpaa.

english_verse: The Spirit told Philip to catch up with the chariot.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛsi Filipo si, “Tipɔni kìke ye tɔ ze ní yɛ su.”

english_verse: Philip ran up close and heard the man reading aloud from the book of Isaiah. Philip asked him, “Do you understand what you are reading?”
translation_verse: Gi Filipo ese atsani kìke ye e, enu gi onyimee ɛɛkla oboedoplɛɛ Yesaya yɛ keplekpaa; lɛ Filipo eʋi ye si, “Woenu liboe lɛ gi wɔɛkla ese na a?”

english_verse: The official answered, “How can I understand unless someone helps me?” He then invited Philip to come up and sit beside him.
translation_verse: Ɔka ʋidi ye ebu le onu si, “Kitɛ maanu lɛ ese e, xe ɔnɔnɔ óbu lɛ ese ɔ́ki mɛ ɛ?” Lɛ eʋi adɔ ni Filipo kɔ si omu oku ní kìke yɛ mɛ tɔ oze ní yɛ su u.

english_verse: The man was reading the passage that said, “He was led like a sheep on its way to be killed. He was silent as a lamb whose wool is being cut off, and he did not say a word.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi azaakla ní kuŋwlimi dradra mɛ ɛ, lɛ linu si, “Bɛgbani yɛ ŋwisi oʋesi gi bɛtreye e, lɛ kiliɛ gi keʋesibi ya kizɛlɛɛ kpoo ní ɔniliɛ gi izɛha yɛ sipi se aŋu mɛ ɛ, nite ke yɛ fɛ ɔ́yaka onugu u.

english_verse: He was treated like a nobody and did not receive a fair trial. How can he have children, if his life is snatched away?”
translation_verse: Bɛkpɛ yɛ kakpaa, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔma gi azɛ yɛ onu u. Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kupani ní yɛ besoebi ba su u, lɛ ese linu si yɛ kesea aʋa ligbã nɛ lidu.”

english_verse: The official said to Philip, “Tell me, was the prophet talking about himself or about someone else?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔka ʋidi ye eʋi Filipo si, “Si mɛ, nyaŋwɛ su oboedoplɛɛ ɛɛpani eboa la ya a? Yɛ ɔtutɔ su loo ní ɔnɔ dzedzeɔtɔ su na a?”

english_verse: So Philip began at this place in the Scriptures and explained the good news about Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ Filipo ekpese kupani dɔni kuŋwlimi yɔ kapɛ alia ya aʋa a, xe asi yɛ liboe gbã nɛ ní Yesu su í.

english_verse: As they were going along the road, they came to a place where there was some water. The official said, “Look! Here is some water. Why can't I be baptized?”
translation_verse: Filipo asi yɛ si, “Xegi wɔziŋa ni wɔ ɔna nɔ petee e,” Onyimee apani si, “Mazi aŋa si Yesu Kristo Yɛ onu Aya Yebi ye.”

english_verse: He ordered the chariot to stop. Then they both went down into the water, and Philip baptized him.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔka ʋidi ye aki si kìke ye ɔtɔ ɔ. Lɛ ɔka ʋidi ye ni Filipo beple eku ní kuni yo me e, lɛ Filipo akpɛ kuni aki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After they had come out of the water, the Lord's Spirit took Philip away. The official never saw him again, but he was very happy as he went on his way.
translation_verse: Gi bedo ni kuni yo me e, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ efeke Filipo esɛnɔ. Lɛ ɔka ʋidi ye ɔ́mɔ yɛ tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ atrɛ ní yɛ kudegaga aʋa ni isami.

english_verse: Philip later appeared in Azotus. He went from town to town, all the way to Caesarea, telling people about Jesus.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Filipo kɔ azeedo ní Azotus lɛ atrɛ ní Kaisarea ɛ, lɛ ní kudeo aʋa ɛ, edo liboe gbã nɛ ní ɔma kakaa mɛ.

english_verse: Saul preached with such power that he completely confused the Jewish people in Damascus, as he tried to show them that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Saulo liboe le kudodoo kwize kifu, lɛ yɛ likla trɔtrɔ lɛ ní lɛ aʋa si Yesu yɛ onu Kristo ye e, liku aŋu gbɛgbɛ nite si Yudanɛ ba ya gi bali ní Damasko e, bátani yɛ onu kulɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now get up and go into the city, where you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: pɔ̀ yɔ tɔ trɛ ní ɔma ʋidi no me e, níklɔ biasi wɔ kidiliɛ gi lilɛ si bitɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: I will show him how much he must suffer for worshiping in my name.”
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛ ɔtutɔ maabasɛ yɛ kiliɛ gi linu si ɔmɔ kini ní mɛ litukpo e.”

english_verse: When Saul arrived in Jerusalem, he tried to join the followers. But they were all afraid of him, because they did not believe he was a true follower.
translation_verse: Saulo aba Yerusalem lɛ ekeni edi si yize ní baʋaziŋa ba kusi i. Pɔ̀ bázɛ yɛ aʋa aŋa si onu ɔʋaziŋa a, lɛ ba petee bezese ye e.

english_verse: and he has seen a vision. He saw a man named Ananias coming to him and putting his hands on him, so he could see again.”
translation_verse: lɛ amɔ ní kububasɛ mɛ si wɔ Anania wɔbatrɔ awla ní yɛ aʋa a, tɔ ɔtani bidɔ kumɔ dzɛ.”

english_verse: Suddenly something like fish scales fell from Saul's eyes, and he could see. He got up and was baptized.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ ko e, kiditɔ ŋwisi siklui kinɛ kiwɔli ní Saulo yɛ bimbi ʋe aʋa lɛ atani bidɔ kumɔ dzɛ ɛ. Ayɔ lɛ bɛkpɛ kuni kí yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and asked for letters to the leaders of the synagogues in Damascus. He did this because he wanted to arrest and take to Jerusalem any man or woman who had accepted the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Lɛ azeeʋi i, xe bɛŋwlimi kuplekpa gi kwibu ye basɛ kusumu yo baka ba a, ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ní Damasko, si xegi yɛ amɔ banɔatɔ gi bekuni Ɔkaɛ kude niklɔ ɛ, aatani ba kuvu, benyime ni badzɛ dzini manɔ ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: When Saul had almost reached Damascus, a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around him.
translation_verse: Gi Saulo eze ní kudeo aʋa a, xe eti apɔni Damasko e, kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, kuklekle dɔni lifunu ne kwikle klani yɛ.

english_verse: He fell to the ground and heard a voice saying, “Saul! Saul! Why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Egleʋe adɔ kesea lɛ enu ole ɔtɔ gi ɛɛxwa yɛ si, “Saulo, Saulo! Ege lɛ loso wɔɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su u?”

english_verse: “Who are you?” Saul asked. “I am Jesus,” the Lord answered. “I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Lɛ Saulo eʋi si, “Wɔ wonu nyaŋwɛ, Ɔkaɛ ɛ?” Lɛ ole lo ɛpani si, “Mɛ menu Yesu gi yɛ su wɔyɔ kedu ya,

english_verse: The men with Saul stood there speechless. They had heard the voice, but they had not seen anyone.
translation_verse: Benyime ba klɔ gi bani Saulo bɛzɛga ɛ, bɛlɛɛ gi onugu óyi ba a, benu ole lo pɔ̀ bámɔ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Saul got up from the ground, and when he opened his eyes, he could not see a thing. Someone then led him by the hand to Damascus,
translation_verse: Gi Saulo ayɔ ní kesea a, xe adra yɛ bimbi ʋe e, ɔ́tani kididɔ kumɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso kivu bevu yɛ ɔwla lɔ, xe bɛgbani yɛ tsinɔ Damasko ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and for three days he was blind and did not eat or drink.
translation_verse: Kɔki ewe tata ɛ, ɔ́tani kididɔ kumɔ ɔ, lɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔ́ŋa lɛ ɔ́ŋwɛ kididɔ ɔ.

english_verse: A follower named Ananias lived in Damascus, and the Lord spoke to him in a vision. Ananias answered, “Lord, here I am.”
translation_verse: Ɔʋaziŋa ɔtɔ eze ni Damasko gi bɛzɛxwa si Anania, yɛ amɔ kububasɛ gi Ɔkaɛ axwa yɛ si, “Anania!” ebu le onu si, “imɔ mɛ ní ya Ɔkaɛ.”

english_verse: The Lord said to him, “Get up and go to the house of Judas on Straight Street. When you get there, you will find a man named Saul from the city of Tarsus. Saul is praying,
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ asi yɛ si, “Bla tɔ trɛ ní ablɔ liɛ gi bizɛxwa si Ɔgyɔgyɔɛ aʋa a, ní Yuda kepea mɛ tɔ ʋi onyime ɔtɔ gi ádɔ ní Tarso litukpo o, yɛ linyi ne linu Saulo, ɛɛxwa Aya,

english_verse: Ananias replied, “Lord, a lot of people have told me about the terrible things this man has done to your followers in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Lɛ Anania ebu le onu si, “Ɔkaɛ, banɔ glegle bɛpani ní onyime liɛ ya su isi mɛ ní litukpo me gagla lɛ gi abitɛ ní wɔ baʋaziŋa ba su ní Yerusalem i.

english_verse: Now the chief priests have given him the power to come here and arrest anyone who worships in your name.”
translation_verse: Lɛ aba Damasko ni kigagla dɔni kusumu yo baka ʋidi ba kɔ ɔ, si tɔ yivu banama petee gi beesumu wo e.”

english_verse: The Lord said to Ananias, “Go! I have chosen him to tell foreigners, kings, and the people of Israel about me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ asi Anania si, “Trɛ lɛ ese linu si makya yɛ tɔ osumu me, atɔ ɔki tɔ bete mɛ linyi ne ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, ni bekusi ba ani Israelbanɛ ma kusi.

english_verse: Ananias left and went into the house where Saul was staying. Ananias placed his hands on him and said, “Saul, the Lord Jesus has sent me. He is the same one who appeared to you along the road. He wants you to be able to see and to be filled with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Anania atrɛ, eti eku kepea lia mɛ gi Saulo ɔlɛ ɛ, lɛ atrɔ awla yɛ aʋa a, xe apani si, onemi Saulo, Ɔkaɛ yɛ akpɛ mɛ, Yesu ɔtutɔ gi yɛ ebu isu abasi wɔ ní kudeo aʋa a gi wɔtraba ní ya ɛ. Yɛ akpɛ mɛ si tɔ wɔamɔ dzɛ atɔ wɔazɛ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ.

english_verse: Then he ate and felt much better. Saul Preaches in Damascus For several days Saul stayed with the Lord's followers in Damascus.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi aŋa bidɔ pɔ́ ɛ, yɛ kigaglaɛ kidzi kú yɛ mɛ. Saulo asɔli ní Damasko Saulo eze ní baʋaziŋa ba kusi ní Damasko ewe klakla atɔ.

english_verse: Soon he went to the synagogues and started telling people that Jesus is the Son of God.
translation_verse: Eti eku ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ lɛ azaasɔli si Yesu yɛ onu Aya Yebi ye.

english_verse: Everyone who heard Saul was amazed and said, “Isn't this the man who caused so much trouble for those people in Jerusalem who worship in the name of Jesus? Didn't he come here to arrest them and take them to the chief priests?”
translation_verse: Banama petee gi benu yɛ liboe le e, ba inugu itse lɛ beʋi si, “Aní yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi azeeye banama gi bezeesumu onyime liɛ klɔ Yesu ní Yerusalem na a? Lɛ aní kiba aba ní ya niteme ke si yivu banɔaklɔ ba dzini tsini kusumu yo baka ʋidi ba na a?”

english_verse: Later some of them made plans to kill Saul,
translation_verse: Ewe glegle atɔ kede e, Yudanɛ ma bɛtsa lɛ bɛtrɔ si biaye ye e,

english_verse: but he found out about it. He learned that they were guarding the gates of the city day and night in order to kill him.
translation_verse: pɔ̀ bɛga ba batrɔtrɔ ʋa ese si yɛ ɛ. Elesi ni kemize bezeni aŋu ní ɔma ʋidi no kuvunu yo tɔ beye ye.

english_verse: Then one night his followers let him down over the city wall in a large basket. Saul in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ elesiatɔ ɛ, baʋaziŋaa ba klɔ kɔ gi Saulo eze e, bɛgbani yɛ, lɛ bɛkpɛ yɛ kasɔʋi mɛ gani yɛ ní liwuitɔ ní ligli le me pleni kpɛ kesea ɛ. Saulo aba Yerusalem

english_verse: Then Barnabas helped him by taking him to the apostles. He explained how Saul had seen the Lord and how the Lord had spoken to him. Barnabas also said when Saul was in Damascus, he had spoken bravely in the name of Jesus.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Banaba apɔni yɛ ɛ, lɛ agbani yɛ atsini Aya yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba a. Ebu lɛ mɛ aki ba kiliɛ gi Saulo amɔ Ɔkaɛ ní kudeo aʋa a, xe Ɔkaɛ egu kunugu aki yɛ ɛ. Asi ba kiliɛ gi Saulo apani liboe le ní Yesu linyi ne me ní Damasko lifumose e.

english_verse: Saul moved about freely with the followers in Jerusalem and told everyone about the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, Saulo eze ní basu, lɛ aklani Yerusalem petee e, xe azaapani liboe le ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me lifumose e.

english_verse: He was always arguing with the Jews who spoke Greek, and so they tried to kill him.
translation_verse: Ehe eboe ni Yudanɛ ba klɔ gi benu si Hela sɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ bekeni edi si beye ye e.

english_verse: But the followers found out about this and took Saul to Caesarea. From there they sent him to the city of Tarsus.
translation_verse: Gi baʋaziŋa ba benu le e, bɛgbani Saulo tsini Kaisarea lɛ bɛtsisi yɛ ní Tarso e.

english_verse: The church in Judea, Galilee, and Samaria now had a time of peace and kept on worshiping the Lord. The church became stronger, as the Holy Spirit encouraged it and helped it grow. Peter Heals Aeneas
translation_verse: Lɛ liki si baʋaziŋa ba ɔha lɔ ní Yudea, Galilea ni Samaria petee bɛmɔ kusuyɔ ɔ. Gani Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kupɔnɔɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔha lɔ ɛmɔ kigagla lɛ etsi ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛ, gi beze Ɔkaɛ kusese me e. Petro atrɛ ní Lida ni Yopa

english_verse: While Peter was traveling from place to place, he visited the Lord's followers who lived in the town of Lydda.
translation_verse: Agi Petro aklanɔ ní kesea petee me e, adzɛ si yizeedi Aya yɛ banima ní Lida.

english_verse: There he met a man named Aeneas, who for eight years had been sick in bed and could not move.
translation_verse: Niklɔ atsani onyime ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Ainea a, gi aklɔʋi i, xe ɔ́zɛ tani kuyɔ ní yɛ ɔtɔsi lɔ aʋa agɔ ɔgɔtaʋa a.

english_verse: Peter said to Aeneas, “Jesus Christ has healed you! Get up and make up your bed.” At once he stood up.
translation_verse: Petro asi yɛ si, “Ainea, Yesu Kristo ayɔ ni wɔ, yɔ tɔ klɔʋi wɔ ɔtɔsi lɔ.” Kyɔŋ ko lɛ Ainea ayɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Many people in the towns of Lydda and Sharon saw Aeneas and became followers of the Lord. Peter Brings Dorcas Back to Life
translation_verse: Banama petee gi beze ní Lida ni Saron bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, lɛ bekyi vu Ɔkaɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In Joppa there was a follower named Tabitha. Her Greek name was Dorcas, which means “deer.” She was always doing good things for people and had given much to the poor.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ eze ní Yopa gi eze ɔʋaziŋa, yɛ linyi lize Tabita gi lɛ ese linu si Klatsa loo Dɔkas ní sì Hela sɛ mɛ. Akɔ yɛ lipo le me petee apɔni bepiplinete ba gani eboe gbã kubitɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But she got sick and died, and her body was washed and placed in an upstairs room.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, adɔ kunya lɛ etse e. Bewlo ni yɛ okpo ye lɛ bɛplɛ yɛ ní abaa la ligba ɛtɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Joppa wasn't far from Lydda, and the followers heard that Peter was there. They sent two men to say to him, “Please come with us as quickly as you can!”
translation_verse: Gi Yopa ɔ́sadze ní Lida su loso e, gi baʋaziŋa ba ní Yopa benu si Petro aba Lida ɛ, bɛkpɛ benyime tiaʋa tsisi yɛ ni ole si, “Kwiki wɔ litukpo kɔsi tɔ ba blɔ klɔ kyɔŋ.”

english_verse: At once, Peter went with them. The men took Peter upstairs into the room. Many widows were there crying. They showed him the coats and clothes that Dorcas had made while she was still alive.
translation_verse: Petro abla lɛ ekuni ba kude e. Gi aba eku i, bɛtsini yɛ ní abaa la aʋa, niklɔ gi batrɛtrɛ ba bedze ba petee bɛklani Petro ni kube e, xe bɛbasɛ yɛ ewu la ni bidɔ kpɛkpɛ kakaa gi Dɔkas akami aki ba ní yɛ ligbã́ nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After Peter had sent everyone out of the room, he knelt down and prayed. Then he turned to the body of Dorcas and said, “Tabitha, get up!” The woman opened her eyes, and when she saw Peter, she sat up.
translation_verse: Petro ebu ba petee ní ligba lɛ mɛ, lɛ eze ní aklamasi aʋa a, xe axwa Aya ɛ, lɛ gi ekyi edi okpo ye e, apani si, “Tabita, yɔ!” adra yɛ bimbi ʋe, lɛ gi amɔ Petro e, ayɔ eze.

english_verse: He took her by the hand and helped her to her feet. Peter called in the widows and the other followers and showed them that Dorcas had been raised from death.
translation_verse: Petro evu yɛ ɔwla lɔ lɛ apɔni yɛ xe ayɔ ɔlɛ ɛ. Lɛ axwa baʋaziŋa ba petee, kɔpɔni batrɛtrɛ ba bedze ba a, lɛ akɔ yɛ akpɛ ba awla ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone in Joppa heard what had happened, and many of them put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya likaka eku ní Yopa petee lɛ banɔ glegle bɛzi Ɔkaɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Peter stayed on for a while in Joppa in the house of a man named Simon, who made leather.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Petro eze ní Yopa ewe glegle ní Simon kugaplekpa su ɔxwɛwa yɛ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: In Caesarea there was a man named Cornelius, who was the captain of a group of soldiers called “The Italian Unit.”
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Onyime ɔtɔ eze ní Kaisarea gi bɛzɛxwa si Kɔnelio o, eze baguta ba ɔka ní Roma banɛ ma bagutaa ba gi bɛzɛxwa si “Italia baguta ba ɔha lɔ” kusi i.

english_verse: Cornelius was a very religious man. He worshiped God, and so did everyone else who lived in his house. He had given a lot of money to the poor and was always praying to God.
translation_verse: Eze ɔnɔ gbã lɛ yɛ ni yɛ kepea petee bizesumu Aya a lipoe kakaa. azɛɛki bepiplinete ba bidɔ lɛ azɛɛxwa Aya lipoe kakaa a.

english_verse: One afternoon at about three o'clock, Cornelius had a vision. He saw an angel from God coming to him and calling him by name.
translation_verse: Liwe le e, liaze ŋwisi kemize inyɔ tita kinɛ ɛ, amɔ kububasɛ gi amɔ tsra si, Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ aba yɛ kɔ, lɛ axwa yɛ si, “Kɔnelio o.”

english_verse: Cornelius was surprised and stared at the angel. Then he asked, “What is this all about?” The angel answered, “God has heard your prayers and knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Lɛ edu aŋu ni Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ aʋa ní lifu me e, lɛ apani si, “Ɔka, liboe woli liba a?” Ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ebu le onu si, wɔ Aya kuxwaxwa ni wɔ axwɛwawa kí bazianete ba biku Aya aŋu lɛ aplɛ aŋu ní wɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: Now send some men to Joppa for a man named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kpɛ banɔ tsisi ɔka ɔtɔ ní Yopa si ɔba. Yɛ linyi zizi nɛ linu Simon Petro.

english_verse: He is staying with Simon the leather maker, who lives in a house near the sea.”
translation_verse: Onu ɔbaba ní Simon kugaplekpa ɔxwɛwa yɛ kepea mɛ gi ketipɔni axu la ɛ.

english_verse: After saying this, the angel left. Cornelius called in two of his servants and one of his soldiers who worshiped God.
translation_verse: Gi Aya ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asɛ ɛ, Kɔnelio axwa yɛ kepea mɛ banɔsumu tiaʋa, ni ɔguta tole gi izeesumu Aya a, xe onu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ɔpɔnɔpɔnɔɛ.

english_verse: He explained everything to them and sent them off to Joppa.
translation_verse: Asi ba liboe lɛ gi liba ɛ lɛ akpɛ ba tsisi Yopa e.

english_verse: The next day about noon these men were coming near Joppa. Peter went up on the roof of the house to pray
translation_verse: Kele titia mɛ kemizea gi bali ní kudeo aʋa a, xe bíha Yopa kuku i, Petro emu atrɛ ní ligba lɛ aʋa tɔ yixwa Aya.

english_verse: and became very hungry. While the food was being prepared, he fell sound asleep and had a vision.
translation_verse: Kuʋɛ kwizeeku ye lɛ apɛ si yiŋa bidɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ gi bɛzɛtɔ bidɔ mɛ ɛ, atsini sra lɛ amɔ kububasɛ kutɔ ɛ.

english_verse: He saw heaven open, and something came down like a huge sheet held up by its four corners.
translation_verse: Amɔ si lifunu ne lidra, lɛ kiditɔ ŋwisi kusa ʋidi gi bɛka ní igyrɔ tine ne onu kinɛ ɛ, kize ple ba kesea.

english_verse: A voice said to him, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ole ɔtɔ ɛsi yɛ si, “Petro, Yɔ, ye tɔ ta!”

english_verse: But Peter said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never eaten anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro apani si, “Owo lítraze ní te e, Ɔkaɛ! Maʋíŋa kidɔ gi kɛ su kídra loo kilulu ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu oʋídi i.”

english_verse: The voice spoke to him again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: Ole lo egu kunugu yo ɛki yɛ kuʋla si, “Wukíkɔ kididɔ gi Aya adra kɛ su i, si kídra.”

english_verse: This happened three times before the sheet was suddenly taken back to heaven.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ ní te tatá zizi, ko lɛ bedzini kusa emuni tsini lifunu ne e.

english_verse: Peter was still wondering what all this meant, when the men sent by Cornelius came and stood at the gate. They had found their way to Simon's house
translation_verse: Petro apisa lɛ gi azeegyo kububasɛɔ edi si yinu kɔ mɛ ɛ, banama gi Kɔnelio akpɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ niklɔ gi Simon kepea kalɛ ɛ, lɛ balɛ ní kepea kevunu ya ɛ.

english_verse: and were asking if Simon Peter was staying there.
translation_verse: Bɛxwa ɛtsisi lɛ beʋi si i, “Ɔbaba ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Simon Petro ɔlɛ ní klɔ na a?”

english_verse: While Peter was still thinking about the vision, the Holy Spirit said to him, “Three men are here looking for you.
translation_verse: Gi Petro azeegyo kububasɛɔ edi si yinu kɔ ese e, Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛpani si, “Ta kutu! benyime tiata bali ní ya gi bɛɛpɛ wɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Hurry down and go with them. Don't worry, I sent them.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bla, ple tɔ zɛɛtsani ba a. Wukídzi kede ní ba su u, mɛ makpɛ ba.”

english_verse: Peter went down and said to the men, “I am the one you are looking for. Why have you come?”
translation_verse: Petro eple aba lɛ asi benyime ba si, “Mɛ menu ɔniliɛ gi mlɛɛpɛ ɛ, liboe woli loso mlɛba a?”

english_verse: They answered, “Captain Cornelius sent us. He is a good man who worships God and is liked by the Jewish people. One of God's holy angels told Cornelius to send for you, so he could hear what you have to say.”
translation_verse: Bebu le onu si, “Baguta ba ɔka, Kɔnelio yɛ akpɛ blɔ, onu ɔnɔ gbã gi izeesumu Aya a, lɛ Yudanɛ ma petee bizɛki yɛ kubùbù glegle e. Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi yɛ si ɔxwa wɔ mani yɛ kepea mɛ tɔ aanu liboe lɛ gi wɔapani i.”

english_verse: Peter invited them to spend the night. The next morning, Peter and some of the Lord's followers in Joppa left with the men who had come from Cornelius.
translation_verse: Petro agbani ba akpɛ kepea mɛ lɛ azɛ ba ŋwisi yɛ bababa kinɛ ɛ, lɛ aki si bɛza ɛ. Keletitia mɛ ɛ, abla lɛ yɛ ni ba bɛsɛ ɛ, lɛ baʋaziŋaatɔ gi bɛdɔ ní Yopa e, bekuni ba kude.

english_verse: The next day they arrived in Caesarea where Cornelius was waiting for them. He had also invited his relatives and close friends.
translation_verse: Ní keletitia mɛ ɛ, bezeku Kaisarea níklɔ gi Kɔnelio ni yɛ baŋanɛ ba ni yɛ baʋia vevea ba gi axwa ɛ bɛzeepe ba.

english_verse: When Peter arrived, Cornelius greeted him. Then he knelt at Peter's feet and started worshiping him.
translation_verse: Gi Petro eti eku ní kepea mɛ ko e, Kɔnelio atsani yɛ lɛ gi apani yɛ ɛ, adɔ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa ni kubùbù ní Petro ikli lɛ onu.

english_verse: But Peter took hold of him and said, “Stand up! I am nothing more than a human.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro apɔni yɛ si ɔyɔ ɔlɛ ɛ, lɛ apani si i, “Yɔ tɔ lɛɛ ɔnɔ ko mɛ fɛ menu.”

english_verse: As Peter entered the house, he was still talking with Cornelius. Many people were there,
translation_verse: Petro azeegu kunugu yo ni Kɔnelio gi eti eku kepea mɛ ɛ, níklɔ amɔ banɔ kpaŋukpaŋua ba gi bɛtsa a.

english_verse: and Peter said to them, “You know that we Jews are not allowed to have anything to do with other people. But God has shown me that he doesn't think anyone is unclean or unfit.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi ba si, “Mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlete mɔmɔmɔ si liyɔ kedu ní blɔ ɔsã nɔ su si Yudanɛ ɔdzɛ banama gi bénu Yudanɛ kepe me loo ɔkɔ isu ɔpɔni ba a. Pɔ̀ Aya abasi mɛ si líma si mibù ɔnɔnɔ si ɔ́dra loo si elulu u.

english_verse: I agreed to come here, but I want to know why you sent for me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso, gi wɔkpɛ banɔ ní mɛ ese ko e mégbe si maaba a. Abla ɛ, meeʋi wo si ege loso wɔkpɛ banɔ ní mɛ ese e?”

english_verse: Cornelius answered: Four days ago at about three o'clock in the afternoon I was praying at home. Suddenly a man in bright clothes stood in front of me.
translation_verse: Lɛ Kɔnelio apani si, “liaze ŋwisi ome liwe nene ni lipoe lɛ ya ɖii me e, gi mazɛɛxwa Aya ní mɛ kepea mɛ ní kemizea inyɔ tita mɛ ɛ, onyime ɔtɔ gi eze ní liwu klekle me e, alɛɛ ní mɛ aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: He said, “Cornelius, God has heard your prayers, and he knows about your gifts to the poor.
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si, ‘Kɔnelio! Aya enu wɔ kuxwaxwa lɛ aplɛ aŋu ní wɔ kupɔnɔ kí bepiplinete ba aʋa a.

english_verse: Now send to Joppa for Simon Peter. He is visiting in the home of Simon the leather maker, who lives near the sea.”
translation_verse: Kpɛ ɔnɔ tsisi ní Yopa tɔ bagbani onyime ɔtɔ gi yɛ linyi zizi nɛ linu Simon Petro amanɔ ɔ. Onu ɔbaba ní Simon gi izɛwa kugaplekpa su axwɛ ɛ, yɛ kepea mɛ ní axu la kutu yɔ ɔ.’

english_verse: I sent for you at once, and you have been good enough to come. All of us are here in the presence of the Lord God, so that we can hear what he has to say.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso makpɛ banɔ atsisi wɔ kyɔŋ lɛ wɔbitɛ lɛ mumɔ gi wɔba ɛ. Abla ɛ, blɔ petee kuli ní ya ní Aya aŋu mɛ, gi kwipɛ si kunu liboe kakaa gi Ɔkaɛ Aya asi wɔ si pani ki blɔ ɛ.” Petro apani Liboe Gbã nɛ

english_verse: Peter then said: Now I am certain that God treats all people alike.
translation_verse: Lɛ Petro apani si i, “Abla ɛ, mamɔ si linu anukwarɛ si Aya óʋidi ɔnɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God is pleased with everyone who worships him and does right, no matter what nation they come from.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní kese kakaa aʋa ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi izeepleni isu ní Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ, xe izɛɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi kigyɔ ɛ, yɛ izeeku yɛ aŋu.

english_verse: This is the same message that God gave to the people of Israel, when he sent Jesus Christ, the Lord of all, to offer peace to them.
translation_verse: Mlete liboe lɛ gi atsisi Israelbanɛ ma a, xe asi ba kusuyɔɔ Liboe Gbã nɛ gani Yesu Kristo gi onu Ɔkaɛ kí ɔnɔ kakaa aʋa a.

english_verse: You surely know what happened everywhere in Judea. It all began in Galilee after John had told everyone to be baptized.
translation_verse: Mlete liboe ʋidi lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ ní Israel kesea mɛ ɛ, gi likpese ní Galilea a, gi Yohanes asɔli yɛ liboe le ní kunikpɛkpɛɔ kede e.

english_verse: God gave the Holy Spirit and power to Jesus from Nazareth. He was with Jesus, as he went around doing good and healing everyone who was under the power of the devil.
translation_verse: Mlete liboe ní Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ su, ani kiliɛ gi Aya akya yɛ ɛ, xe aki si yɛ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, eze yɛ aʋa a ni kigagla. Ɔkɔ kakaa gi adzɛ ɛ, azɛɛbitɛ eboe gbã, lɛ ayɔ ni banama petee gi bali ní ozimizi ye kigaglaɛ ese e, lɛ ese linu si Aya eze yɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: We all saw what Jesus did both in Israel and in the city of Jerusalem. Jesus was put to death on a cross.
translation_verse: Blɔ kunu katsalia kí kidɔ kakaa gi abitɛ ɛ ní Yerusalem ni Yudanɛ ma kesea petee me. Lɛ beye ye gi beku ye ní ose lo su u.

english_verse: But three days later, God raised him to life and let him be seen.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya ayɔni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ní liwe tanɛ nɛ aʋa a, lɛ aki si ebu isu abasɛ

english_verse: Not everyone saw him. He was seen only by us, who ate and drank with him after he was raised from death. We were the ones God chose to tell others about him.
translation_verse: aní kí ɔnɔ kakaa a, pɔ̀ kí banama gi beze yɛ katsalia a, gi ba Aya ebu aplɛ ɛ, ba benu blɔ gi kwiŋa a, xe kwiŋwɛ ni yɛ ɛ, gi ayɔ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God told us to announce clearly to the people that Jesus is the one he has chosen to judge the living and the dead.
translation_verse: Lɛ akpɛ ɔsã aki blɔ sì kusɔli liboe le aki banima, nì tɔ kuŋa katsalia si yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi Aya akya otsixwa yɛ ki bagbagbe ba ni bekpo ʋa a.

english_verse: While Peter was still speaking, the Holy Spirit took control of everyone who was listening.
translation_verse: Gi Petro azeegu kunugu yo e, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra nɔ eple edɔ ní banama petee gi bɛzɛɛta liboe le kutu aʋa a.

english_verse: Some Jewish followers of the Lord had come with Peter, and they were surprised that the Holy Spirit had been given to Gentiles.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ baʋaziŋa ba gi bezeni Petro kude dɔ ní Yopa e, litu ba kugyɔ si Aya akɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ bidɔ kɔkɔ ʋɛ aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now they were hearing Gentiles speaking unknown languages and praising God. Peter said,
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si benu gi bɛzɛɛpani ní siya ede ede me, lɛ bɛzɛɛkpɔ̀ Aya kukemeo o. Lɛ Petro apani si i,

english_verse: “These Gentiles have been given the Holy Spirit, just as we have! I am certain that no one would dare stop us from baptizing them.”
translation_verse: “Aya aki banɔ ba ya ba Yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ blɔ kiniɛ ɖii. Ɔnutɔ aatani kude kutsi kí ba kunikpɛkpɛɔ na a?”

english_verse: Peter ordered them to be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, and they asked him to stay on for a few days.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso akpɛ ɔsã si bakpɛ kuni aki ba ní Yesu Kristo linyi ne me. Lɛ beʋi dɔni yɛ awla si oze ní ba su ewe atɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The apostles and the followers in Judea heard that Gentiles had accepted God's message.
translation_verse: Lídze xe banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ni baʋaziŋa gɔgɔ ba ní Yudea petee e, benu si banama gi bénu Yudanɛ fɛ bɛzi Aya liboe le e.

english_verse: So when Peter came to Jerusalem, some of the Jewish followers started arguing with him. They wanted Gentile followers to be circumcised, and
translation_verse: Gi Petro atrɛ ní Yerusalem i, Yudanɛ baʋaziŋa ba, bɛtɛni yɛ ni eboa la si,

english_verse: they said, “You stayed in the homes of Gentiles, and you even ate with them!”
translation_verse: “Wòze ɔbaba ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ kepe me e, lɛ wɔŋa bidɔ ni ba kyɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: I was in the town of Joppa and was praying when I fell sound asleep and had a vision. I saw heaven open, and something like a huge sheet held by its four corners came down to me.
translation_verse: “Gi mazɛɛxwa Aya ní Yopa ɔma ʋidi no me e, matsini sra lɛ mamɔ kububasɛ ɛ. Mamɔ kiditɔ gi kiiple ŋwisi kusa ʋidi utɔ ɔ, gi bɛka ní igyrɔ tine ne onu kinɛ ɛ, dɔni lifune lɛ biiple dɔ ní klɔ gi malɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I heard a voice saying to me, “Peter, get up! Kill these and eat them.”
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ ɔ, gi ɛɛsi mɛ si i, ‘Petro! Yɔ lɛɛ, ye tɔ ta!’

english_verse: But I said, “Lord, I can't do that! I've never taken a bite of anything that is unclean and not fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ mapani si, owo o, Ɔkaɛ maʋikpɛ bidɔ gi bɛsu bídra loo biŋaŋa kluklu ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu ní unugu me oʋídi.

english_verse: The voice from heaven spoke to me again, “When God says that something can be used for food, don't say it isn't fit to eat.”
translation_verse: “Ole lo eyi dɔni lifunu ne dzɛ si, Wukíkɔ kidɔ gi Aya adra kɛ su i, si kídra.

english_verse: This happened three times before it was all taken back into heaven.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ nite tatá, lɛ bedzini bidɔ mɛ petee muno ní lifunu ne e.

english_verse: Suddenly three men from Caesarea stood in front of the house where I was staying.
translation_verse: “Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, benyime tiata gi bɛkpɛ dɔni Kaisarea a, bɛba eku kepea lia klɔ mɛ gi meze e.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit told me to go with them and not to worry. Then six of the Lord's followers went with me to the home of a man
translation_verse: “Lɛ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛsi mɛ si mikíkpɛ lidzegaga a, mitrɛ ni ba kyɔŋ. Baʋaziŋa tieglo ba ya gi bɛdɔ Yopa e, kyɛ bekuni mɛ kude xe kwitrɛ ní Kaisarea a, lɛ blɔ petee kwitrɛ ní Kɔnelio kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: who told us that an angel had appeared to him. The angel had ordered him to send to Joppa for someone named Simon Peter.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi blɔ kiliɛ gi amɔ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi ɔlɛɛ ní yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ, xe asi yɛ si i, ‘Kpɛ ɔnutɔ tsisi Yopa tɔ baxwa onyime ɔtɔ gi yɛ linyi zizi nɛ linu Simon Petro amanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Then Peter would tell him how he and everyone in his house could be saved.
translation_verse: Yɛ aapani liboe le kí wɔ lɛ gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, wɔ ni wɔ kepea petee mlamɔ kubúbú.’

english_verse: After I started speaking, the Holy Spirit was given to them, just as the Spirit had been given to us at the beginning.
translation_verse: “Gi mekpese kunugu yo kugu i, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ eple edɔ ba aʋa kiliɛ ɖii gi liba blɔ aʋa ní okpese klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: I remembered that the Lord had said, “John baptized with water, but you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: Lɛ maplɛ aŋu ní liboe lɛ gi Ɔkaɛ apani aʋa a si, ‘Yohanes akpɛ kuni aki banima, pɔ̀ Aya akpɛ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kí mlɔ!’

english_verse: God gave those Gentiles the same gift that he gave us when we put our faith in the Lord Jesus Christ. So how could I have gone against God?
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛ lidra si Aya aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ kidɔkɔkɔ tile liɛ gi aki blɔ ɛ, gi kwizi Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ. Nyaŋwɛ mɛ menu si mitsi kude kí Aya a?”

english_verse: When they heard Peter say this, they stopped arguing and started praising God. They said, “God has now let Gentiles turn to him, and he has given life to them!” The Church in Antioch
translation_verse: Gi benu liboe le e, bɛmɔni ba kutɛtɛɔ, lɛ bɛkpɔ̀ Aya si “Kɔlɔ Aya aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ fɛ kude onu si bekyi ɔna mɛ tɔ biaze ligbã́!” Ɔsɔli ha lɔ ní Antioxia

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers had been scattered because of the terrible trouble that started when Stephen was killed. They went as far as Phoenicia, Cyprus, and Antioch, but they told the message only to the Jews.
translation_verse: Baʋaziŋaatɔ gi bese hle kɔkpɛ keduyɔyɔ a gi kɛ ba ní Stefano kuyeyeo me e, bɛtrɛ ní Foenike, Kipro ni Antioxia ke nì liboe le kí Yudanɛ ma tete.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Cyprus and Cyrene went to Antioch and started telling Gentiles the good news about the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ benyime baʋaziŋaatɔ ɔ, gi bɛdɔ Kipro ni Kirene e, bɛba Antioxia, lɛ bɛpani Aya Liboe gbã nɛ ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu su isi banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Lord's power was with them, and many people turned to the Lord and put their faith in him.
translation_verse: Aya kigaglaɛ kize ni ba, lɛ banɔ glegle bɛzi ŋa a, lɛ bekyi vu Ɔkaɛ ɛ.

english_verse: News of what was happening reached the church in Jerusalem. Then they sent Barnabas to Antioch.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya lizɛna Baʋaziŋa ba ní Yerusalem, nite si bɛkpɛ Barnaba tsisi Antioxia.

english_verse: When Barnabas got there and saw how God had blessed them with undeserved grace, he was very glad. So he begged them to remain faithful to the Lord with all their hearts.
translation_verse: Gi azeeku u, xe amɔ kiliɛ gi Aya akrira banima ɛ, isami iku ye lɛ akpɛ lɛ ní ba petee me si beze kuziŋaa aʋa ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ ni ba ina nɛ petee.

english_verse: Barnabas was a good man of great faith, and he was filled with the Holy Spirit. Many more people turned to the Lord.
translation_verse: Banaba eze ɔnɔ gbã gi eyi ni kuziŋaa ni Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, lɛ aki banɔ glegle bɛzi Ɔkaɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Barnabas went to Tarsus to look for Saul.
translation_verse: Lɛ Banaba atrɛ ní Tarso tɔ yizɛɛpɛ Saulo ɛmɔ ɔ.

english_verse: He found Saul and brought him to Antioch, where they met with the church for a whole year and taught many of its people. There in Antioch the Lord's followers were first called Christians.
translation_verse: Gi amɔ yɛ ɛ, agbani yɛ atsini Antioxia, lɛ kɔkí ligɔ zizi i, ba tiaʋa bɛzɛɛtsa ni ɔsɔli ha lɔ, lɛ bɛ basɛ banɔ glegle bidɔ ɔ. Antioxia bɛ ya baʋaziŋa ba kuxwa si Kristo banɛ.

english_verse: During this time some prophets from Jerusalem came to Antioch.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Aya beboedoplɛatɔ bɛdɔ ní Yerusalem i ba Antioxia.

english_verse: One of them was Agabus. Then with the help of the Spirit, he told that there would be a terrible famine everywhere in the world. And it happened when Claudius was Emperor.
translation_verse: Bamɛ tole gi bɛzɛxwa si Agabo e, ayɔ ɔlɛɛ lɛ gani Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, edo aplɛ si kuʋɛ gagla kutɔ kwitra dɔ ní Roma kesea petee aʋa. Kuʋɛ ɔliɔ ya kwidɔ lipoe lɛ mɛ gi Klaudio azɛɛŋa Roma Kesea aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: The followers in Antioch decided to send whatever help they could to the followers in Judea.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Baʋaziŋa ba ní Antioxia bɛtrɔ lɛ si bamɛ ɔkakaa aatsisi kidiliɛ gi aatani kukɔ ɛ, tɔ biakɔ pɔni ba benemi baʋaziŋa ba ní Yudea.

english_verse: So they appointed Barnabas and Saul to take their gifts to the church leaders in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Bɛbitɛ nite lɛ bɛkɔ ba bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ tsisi ɔha lɔ banɔtsi ba gani Banaba ni Saulo aʋa a.

english_verse: At that time King Herod caused terrible suffering for some members of the church.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Okusi ye Herodes aki si bevu ɔha lɔ banɔatɔ lɛ ese linu si apɛ si yibu kini kí baʋaziŋa ba.

english_verse: He ordered soldiers to cut off the head of James, the brother of John.
translation_verse: Aki si beye Yakobo gi onu Yohanes yenemi nyimee ni ihɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When Herod saw that this pleased the Jewish people, he had Peter arrested during the Festival of Thin Bread.
translation_verse: Gi amɔ si liku Yudanɛ ma liŋu i, atrɛ lɛ aʋa lɛ aki si bevu Petro fɛ ɛ. Liba lɛ mɛ lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi bɛzɛŋa aklãmɔnyasi na liwe le e.

english_verse: He put Peter in jail and ordered four squads of soldiers to guard him. Herod planned to put him on trial in public after the festival.
translation_verse: Gi bevu Petro e, bɛkpɛ yɛ ligba, gi baguta tiene iha tine gi izɛtsrɛ iglɔ lɛ ɛ, izepe ye. Herodes atrɔ si yɛ amani yɛ ní otsixwa kɔ ní ɔha kusi xegi bɛŋa Kutselidɔzawe le pɔ́ ɛ.

english_verse: While Peter was being kept in jail, the church never stopped praying to God for him. Peter Is Rescued
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Petro eze ligba lɛ, pɔ̀ ɔhamɛ banɛ ma bɛzɛxwa Aya ni kimbi kyíkyĩ ní yɛ litukpo o. Aya ebu Petro ní Ligba Lɛ

english_verse: The night before Peter was to be put on trial, he was asleep and bound by two chains. A soldier was guarding him on each side, and two other soldiers were guarding the entrance to the jail.
translation_verse: Liwe lɛ klɔ gi Herodes atrɔ si yimani yɛ ní otsixwaklɔ lɛ elesi la ɛ, Petro afɛ banɔpe tiaʋa kusi. Bɛkpɛ yɛ bibɔ iwla lɛ tiʋa, lɛ banɔpe ba bezeepe ligba lɛ kevunu ya a.

english_verse: Suddenly an angel from the Lord appeared, and light flashed around in the cell. The angel poked Peter in the side and woke him up. Then he said, “Quick! Get up!” The chains fell off his hands,
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ aŋwi, lɛ yɛ kuklekleo kwizi ligba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ agbɔni Petro opoʋa nɔ, lɛ ahwani yɛ ɛ, xe apani si, “Kɔsi tɔ yɔ ɔ!” Bibɔ ʋɛ biwɔli ní Petro kuwlakpɔnu yo ní bɛ ɔtutɔ bɛ isuwla mɛ.

english_verse: and the angel said, “Get dressed and put on your sandals.” Peter did what he was told. Then the angel said, “Now put on your coat and follow me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi yɛ si, “Nya wɔ liwu le aʋa tɔ kpɛ wɔ afɔkpa ʋa a.” Petro abitɛ ní te lɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi yɛ dzɛ si, “Kɔ wɔ liwu le aʋa liwu le xla isu ya tɔ kuni mɛ kude e!”

english_verse: They went past the two groups of soldiers, and when they came to the iron gate to the city, it opened by itself. They went out and were going along the street, when all at once the angel disappeared.
translation_verse: Bɛdɔ banɔpe tɔpu ba ɛza, lɛ bɛza baʋla ba aʋa a, xe bɛba ɛna bala ʋa opupo lo gi ebu aŋu mɛ kpɛ ɔma nɔ ablɔɛ aʋa ɛ. Opupo lo ɛdra ɛki ba ní lɔ tutɔ isuwla mɛ lɛ bɛga kevunu ya edo e. Gi bɛga ablɔ tole aʋa ɛza ko e, lɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ alila ní Petro su i.

english_verse: Peter now realized what had happened, and he said, “I am certain that the Lord sent his angel to rescue me from Herod and from everything the Jewish leaders planned to do to me.”
translation_verse: Gi Petro aŋu na mɛ ɛdra ɛ, apani si ableke mete si etepɔ kanɔgbã mɛ! Aya atsisi yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ si obu me dɔni Herodes kigaglaɛ ese ni dɔni kidiliɛ petee gi Yudanɛ ma begyodi si kiaba kɛ mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ.

english_verse: Then Peter went to the house of Mary the mother of John whose other name was Mark. Many of the Lord's followers had come together there and were praying.
translation_verse: Gi edzi eku sinɔ mɛ ɛ, atrɛ ní Maria gi onu Yohanes Marko yene kepea mɛ niklɔ gi baʋaziŋa glegle bɛtsa xe bɛɛxwa Aya ní yɛ litukpo e.

english_verse: Peter knocked on the gate, and a servant named Rhoda came to answer.
translation_verse: Petro asa ablɔ yɛ su opupo lo, lɛ ɔnɔsumu ye ɔdisami gi bɛxwa si Roda ɛ, yɛ aba si yidi si nyaŋwɛ ɛsa opupo lo na.

english_verse: When she heard Peter's voice, she was too excited to open the gate. She ran back into the house and said Peter was standing there.
translation_verse: Enu Petro ole lo egyesi lɛ isami iku ye gbɛgbɛ nite si edzì ese atrɛ ní ligba lɛ mɛ gi ɔ́dra opupo lo o, lɛ apani si, Petro ɔlɛɛ ní opupo lo onu u.

english_verse: “You are crazy!” everyone told her. But she kept saying it was Peter. Then they said, “It must be his angel.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi yɛ si, “Wɔ aŋu mɛ eezizi!” Pɔ̀ edu ɔkli kese si linu anukwarɛ ɛ. Lɛ bebu le onu si, “Litráze Petro boŋu i, liaze Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi izepe Petro e.”

english_verse: But Peter kept on knocking, until finally they opened the gate. They saw him and were completely amazed.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Petro eze lɛ aʋa azɛɛsa opupo lo. Gɔgɔɛ gi bɛdra opupo lo o, xe bɛmɔ yɛ ɛ, ba inugu itse.

english_verse: Peter motioned for them to keep quiet. Then he told how the Lord had led him out of jail. He also said, “Tell James and the others what has happened.” After that, he left and went somewhere else.
translation_verse: Petro emuni yɛ ɔwla lɔ si badɔ zii, lɛ adra lɛ su aki ba kiliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ ebu ye ní ligba lɛ ɛ. Lɛ asi ba si, “Mlasi Yakobo ni baʋaziŋa gɔgɔ ba kyɛ liboe lɛ gi masi mlɔ ɛ.” Lɛ asɛ xe atrɛ ní ɔkɔ dzedze e.

english_verse: The next morning the soldiers who had been on guard were terribly worried and wondered what had happened to Peter.
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, banɔpe ba bɛpisa dɔ isu ya onu i zanɔ lɛ beʋi si kitɛ Petro aga na a?

english_verse: Herod ordered his own soldiers to search for him, but they could not find him. Then he questioned the guards and had them put to death. After this, Herod left Judea to stay in Caesarea for a while. Herod Dies
translation_verse: Lɛ Herodes aki ɔsã si bapɛ́ yɛ ɛ mɔmɔmɔ, pɔ̀ bátani yɛ kumɔ ɔ. Lɛ gi eʋi banɔpe ba igbè e, aki ɔsã si beye ba a. Lɛ ya kede e, Herodes asɛ Yudea lɛ azeeze ni Kaisarea lipoetɔ onu i. Herodes yɛ Kutseo

english_verse: Herod and the people of Tyre and Sidon were very angry with each other. But their country got its food supply from the region that he ruled. So a group of them went to see Blastus, who was one of Herod's high officials. They convinced Blastus that they wanted to make peace between their cities and Herod,
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Herodes amɔ kusuyeʋi gagla ni Tiro banɛ ma ni Sidonanɛ ma, lɛ gi eze ní Kaisarea ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ si bɛɛmɔ yɛ. Kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, beʋi Blasto gi onu ɔnɔ gi okusi ye azɛ yɛ aʋa aŋa a, xe eze kekusipea ɔʋadi ye si ɔpɔni ba a. Lɛ bɛtrɛ ní Herodes klɔ ɔ, xe beʋi ye si ɔki tɔ babitɛ kusuyɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si okusi ye kesea mɛ ba kesea kizɛmɔ ka kiŋaŋaɛ dɔnɔ.

english_verse: and a day was set for them to meet with him. Herod came dressed in his royal robes. He sat down on his throne and made a speech.
translation_verse: Ní liwe trɔtrɔ lɛ aʋa ɛ, Herodes eze ní sikusi me ní yɛ likusigbo le aʋa xe apani aki banima a.

english_verse: The people shouted, “You speak more like a god than a man!”
translation_verse: Lɛ banima bɛkpɛ ozi lo si i, “Aní ɔnɔ eegu kunugu yo o, pɔ̀ Aya a!”

english_verse: At once an angel from the Lord struck him down because he took the honor that belonged to God. Later, Herod was eaten by worms and died.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, Aya ɔxwɛdzɛɛ aki si Herodes adɔ kunya, lɛ ese linu si ɔ́kɔ kukɔkɔ ɔki Aya a. Ata asu lɛ etse e.

english_verse: And after Barnabas and Saul had done the work they were sent to do, they went back to Jerusalem with John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Banaba ni Saulo bebloni ba lixwɛ nɛ, lɛ bɛgbani Yohanes gi onu Marko epɔni isu ya a, xe bɛsɛ ní Yerusalem i.

english_verse: The church at Antioch had several prophets and teachers. They were Barnabas, Simeon, also called Niger, Lucius from Cyrene, Manaen, who was Herod's close friend, and Saul.
translation_verse: Ní baʋaziŋaa ba gi bizesumu Ɔkaɛ kusi ní Antioxia ɛ, Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba ni badɔbasɛ ba beze, gi ba benu Banaba ni Simeon i, gi bizɛxwa si Ogo ye e, ani Lukio gi ádɔ ní Kirene e, ni Manahem, ɔniliɛ gi bɛganɔ ni Okusi ye Herodes i, ani Saulo.

english_verse: While they were worshiping the Lord and going without eating, the Holy Spirit told them, “Appoint Barnabas and Saul to do the work for which I have chosen them.”
translation_verse: Gi bɛzɛnya onugu u, xe bezesumu Ɔkaɛ ɛ, Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛsi ba si, “Mlebu Banaba ni Saulo lidze aki mɛ tɔ bawa lixwɛ nɛ gi lɛ loso maxwa ba ɛ.”

english_verse: Everyone prayed and went without eating for a while longer. Next, they placed their hands on Barnabas and Saul to show that they had been appointed to do this work. Then everyone sent them on their way. Barnabas and Saul in Cyprus
translation_verse: Gi bɛnya onugu u, xe bɛxwa Aya kede e, bɛtrɔ awla ní ba aʋa, lɛ bɛkpɛ ba kude e. Paulo ni Banaba Bɛtrɛ ní Kipro

english_verse: After Barnabas and Saul had been sent by the Holy Spirit, they went to Seleucia. From there they sailed to the island of Cyprus.
translation_verse: Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛsi ba níklɔ gi biatrɛ ɛ, lɛ loso Banaba ni Saulo bɛtrɛ ní Selukia, lɛ beku onitsahu lo dɔni níklɔ tsini Kipro kunisi yo kesea aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: They arrived at Salamis and began to preach God's message in the synagogues. They also had John as a helper.
translation_verse: Gi beku Salami i, bɛpani Aya liboe le ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ. Lɛ Yohanes Marko fɛ apɔni ba ní axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Barnabas and Saul went all the way to the city of Paphos on the other end of the island, where they met a Jewish man named Bar-Jesus. He practiced witchcraft and was a false prophet.
translation_verse: Gi bɛklani kesea mɛ ɛ, xe beku Pafo e, bɛtsani Yudanɛ ɔtɔ gi eze ɔwanete ni asia oboedoplɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ klɔ yɛ linyi si Bar Yesu.

english_verse: He also worked for Sergius Paulus, who was very smart and was the governor of the island. Sergius Paulus wanted to hear God's message, and he sent for Barnabas and Saul.
translation_verse: Eze ɔʋia nɛ aki ɔʋaŋaɛ Sergio Paulo ní kesea klɔ aʋa. Sergio Paulo eze onyime oboete e. Ɔʋaŋa yɛ axwa Banaba ni Saulo amanɔ lɛ ese linu si apɛ si yinu Aya yɛ Liboe le e.

english_verse: But Bar-Jesus, whose other name was Elymas, was against them. He even tried to keep the governor from having faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Elima yɛ linyi ne ní si Hela sɛ mɛ, gi onu ɔwanete e, ayɔ kedu lɛ apɛ si yitsi kudeonu iki ɔʋaŋa yɛ tɔ ɔki ziŋa a.

english_verse: Then Saul, better known as Paul, was filled with the Holy Spirit. He looked straight at Elymas
translation_verse: Saulo gi bizɛxwa kyɛ si Paulo e, xe Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ní yɛ aʋa ɛ, edu aŋu ní ɔwanetee aʋa a,

english_verse: and said, “You son of the devil! You are a liar, a crook, and an enemy of everything that is right. When will you stop speaking against the true ways of the Lord?
translation_verse: lɛ apani si i, “Wonu ozimizi ye Yebi! Wɔ ahwafiɔnɛ kí kidɔ kakaa gi kipɛ ɛ. Woyi ni kelea mɛ asia ni ìmɔ kakaa, lɛ wuzɛɛpɛ lipoe kakaa si tɔ wúkyi Ɔkaɛ yɛ anukwarɛɛ tɔ odzi asia!

english_verse: The Lord is going to punish you by making you completely blind for a while.” Suddenly the man's eyes were covered by a dark mist, and he went around trying to get someone to lead him by the hand.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, Ɔkaɛ atráhe kutu iiki wɔ, wɔtradzi kaŋupo lɛ wɔtramɔ liwo le kuklekle kɔna ni lipoetɔ ɔ.” Ní lɛ onu klɔ ko e, Elima amɔ si debleku ye ni litumu ne liwe yɛ bimbi ʋe aʋa lɛ azɛɛga klanɔ ni kukenidi si yɛ amɔ ɔnutɔ gi aavu yɛ awla la tsinɔ niklɔ gi apɛ si yi trɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When the governor saw what had happened, he was amazed at this teaching about the Lord. So he put his faith in the Lord. Paul and Barnabas in Antioch of Pisidia
translation_verse: Gi ɔʋaŋa yɛ amɔ kidiliɛ gi kiba kɛ mɛ ɛ, azɛ aŋa lɛ ese linu si bidɔbasɛbasɛ ʋɛ ní Ɔkaɛ su bize linugutseboe kí yɛ. Banaba ni Paulo ní Antioxia ní Pisidia Kesea mɛ

english_verse: Paul and the others left Paphos and sailed to Perga in Pamphylia. But John left them and went back to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, Paulo ni yɛ bagaglɔnɛ ba beku onitsahu i dɔni Pafo lɛ bɛba Perga ɔma ʋidi lo ní Pamfilia kesea mɛ ɛ, niklɔ Yohanes Marko asɛ ní ba su xe edzì aba Yerusalem i.

english_verse: The rest of them went on from Perga to Antioch in Pisidia. Then on the Sabbath they went to the synagogue and sat down.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ba kɔ bɛklani dɔni Pɛrga lɛ bɛba ɛna Antioxia ní Pisidia kesea mɛ ɛ. Lɛ gi bɛtrɛ ni Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa ɛ, beze kesea.

english_verse: After the reading of the Law and the Prophets, the leaders sent someone over to tell Paul and Barnabas, “Friends, if you have anything to say that will help the people, please say it.”
translation_verse: Gi bɛkla bidɔ ɛdɔni Mose ɔsã nɔ ni beboedoplɛ ba bidɔ ŋwlimiŋwlimi ʋɛ mɛ pɔ ɛ, baka ba ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ bɛki ba ole si, “Benemi ba kwipɛ si mlegu kunugu aki banima, xe kugbagba kutɔ kuli ní mlɔ awla kí ba ɛ, tɔ mlapani.”

english_verse: Paul got up. He motioned with his hand and said: People of Israel, and everyone else who worships God, listen!
translation_verse: Paulo ayɔ ɔlɛɛ lɛ abasi banima ɔwla lɔ si badɔ zii, lɛ ekpese kupani si, “Benemi Israelbanɛ ma ni mlɔ banɔaya gi mlénu Yudanɛ pɔ̀ mleesumu Aya a, mlata mɛ kutu!

english_verse: The God of Israel chose our ancestors, and he let our people prosper while they were living in Egypt. Then with his mighty power he led them out,
translation_verse: Israelbanɛ ma, Aya yɛ akya blɔ bakatsi ba lɛ aki si banima bɛsrɛ ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, gi beze ŋwisi bababa ní Egipte kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ Aya ebu ba dɔni Egipte ni yɛ kigagla ʋidi ye e.

english_verse: Make sure what the prophets have said doesn't happen to you. They said,
translation_verse: Abla ɛ mledi mumɔ tɔ, eboa la gi beboedoplɛ ba bɛpani i, akíba mlɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: He destroyed seven nations in the land of Canaan and gave their land to our people.
translation_verse: Aki si bɛkyrɔ amade tegloele ní Kanaa kesea mɛ, lɛ akɔ ba kuseo aki yɛ banima ŋwisi ba kaŋase kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Later, God removed Saul and let David rule in his place. God said about him, “David the son of Jesse is the kind of person who pleases me most! He does everything I want him to do.”
translation_verse: Gi Aya ebu ye azanɔ ɛ, Aya aki si Dawid edzì okusi. Liboe lɛ ya Aya apani ní yɛ su si, ‘Mamɔ Dawid gi onu Isai yebinyime e si onu onyime gi malɔ ɛ, onyime gi aabitɛ kidɔ kakaa a gi mapɛ si ɔbitɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: God promised that someone from David's family would come to save the people of Israel, and this one is Jesus.
translation_verse: “Dɔni Dawid yɛ lipo me bebi ba mɛ ɛ, Aya aki Yesu gi yɛ onu onɔbu ye ki Israɛl banɛ ma a, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi apani aplɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But before Jesus came, John was telling everyone in Israel to turn back to God and be baptized.
translation_verse: Xe ableke Yesu aakpese yɛ axwɛ na ɛ, Yohanes asɔli aki Israɛl banɛ ma petee si bekyi adɔ ni ba eboe zimizi na mɛ, tɔ yikpɛ kuni ɔki ba a.

english_verse: Then, when John's work was almost done, he said, “Who do you people think I am? Do you think I am the Promised One? He will come later, and I am not good enough to untie his sandals.”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Yohanes yɛ axwɛ na ɛɛtrɛ la ɔgũ kɔ ɛ, azeeʋi banima si, ‘Nyaŋwɛ mlegyo edi si menu u? Aní mɛ menu ɔniliɛ gi mlepe e. Pɔ̀ mlata mɛ kutu u! Yɛ atrába ní mɛ kede lɛ mégyeno fɛ si maabu afɔkpa ní yɛ kukliseo o.’

english_verse: Now listen, you descendants of Abraham! Pay attention, all of you Gentiles who are here to worship God! Listen to this message about how to be saved, because it is for everyone.
translation_verse: “Mɛ benemi Israelbanɛ ma, Abraham yɛ besoebi ni mlɔ banama gi mlénu Yudanɛ ɛ pɔ̀ mleese Aya a, blɔ bɛmani kubúbú yo ole ɔ lɔ ya!

english_verse: The people of Jerusalem and their leaders didn't realize who Jesus was. And they didn't understand the words of the prophets they read each Sabbath. So they condemned Jesus just as the prophets had said.
translation_verse: Lɛ banama gi beze ní Yerusalem ni banama gi bali ní ba onu i, bámɔ Yesu si yɛ onu ɔnɔbu ye e, pɔ̀ gi bebu ye lipɔ ɛ, bɛki si beboedoplɛ ba eboa la gi bizɛklà ní Lizemɔniwe kakaa aʋa ɛ, ɛba la mɛ.

english_verse: They did exactly what the Scriptures said they would. Even though they couldn't find any reason to put Jesus to death, they still asked Pilate to have him killed. After Jesus had been put to death, he was taken down from the cross and placed in a tomb.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa a gi kuŋwlimi dradra kwipani plɛ ní yɛ su pɔ́ ɛ, bɛti yɛ ní ose lo su lɛ bɛkɔ yɛ kpɛ ní ɔdrɔ lɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But God raised him from death!
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya aki si edzì ligbã dzɛ ɛ,

english_verse: Then for many days Jesus appeared to his followers who had gone with him from Galilee to Jerusalem. Now they are telling our people about him.
translation_verse: lɛ kɔkí ewe glegle e, ebu isu ya abasi banama gi yɛ ni ba bɛga ɛdɔni Galilea ɛtsini Yerusalem i. Ba benu yɛ katsalia abla kí Israelbanɛ ma.

english_verse: God made a promise to our ancestors. And we are here to tell you the good news
translation_verse: “Lɛ blɔ kwipani liboe gbã nɛ si mlɔ ɔ, kidiliɛ gi Aya aka plɛ ki blɔ baka ba ɛ,

english_verse: that he has kept this promise to us. It is just as the second Psalm says about Jesus, “You are my son because today I have become your Father.”
translation_verse: bɛ Aya abitɛ aki blɔ ba bebi ba gi ayɔni Yesu adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní psalomo ʋlaɛ mɛ kinɛ ɛ si, ‘Mobi wonu; omono kelea medzi wɔka.’

english_verse: God raised Jesus from death and will never let his body decay. It is just as God said, “I will make to you the same holy promises that I made to David.”
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya Aya apani aplɛ ní yɛ kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ su si, Aayɔ dzi ligbã lɛ ɔ́tratse liwewe tɔ ɔ, ‘Maaki mlɔ kukrira gi kɔsu kwidra a ni kumokyimokyi kakaa gi maka aplɛ kí Dawid i.’

english_verse: And in another psalm it says, “God will never let the body of his Holy One decay.”
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛ ke Dawid apani ní yɛ Psalomo dzedze me si, ‘Wɔtraki si wɔ ɔnɔ dradra yɛ aafumu ní ɔdrɔ lɔ mɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: When David was alive, he obeyed God. Then after he died, he was buried in the family grave, and his body decayed.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ gi Dawid esumu Aya ní yɛ kupɛ́pɛ onu ní yɛ lipoe le me e, lɛ etse e, xe bevive ye pɔni yɛ bakatsi ba a, lɛ yɛ kisugyaɛ kifumu ní ɔdrɔ lɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But God raised Jesus from death, and his body did not decay.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lɛ ya líba lɛ mɛ ɛki ɔniliɛ gi Aya ayɔnɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: My friends, the message is that Jesus can forgive your sins! The Law of Moses could not set you free from all your sins.
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso kwipɛ si mlɔ petee mlete, si gani Yesu aʋa ɛ, Aya aaplo mlɔ su, mlaŋwɛ oyi

english_verse: “Look, you people who make fun of God! Be amazed and disappear. I will do something today that you won't believe, even if someone tells you about it!”
translation_verse: “Aya apani si, ‘Mledi, mlɔ banama gi mlɛzɛkpɛ mɛ kakpaa ɛ, litu mlo kugyɔ tɔ mletse, lɛ ese linu si mátrabitɛ kiditɔ ní ewe a la ya mɛ gi xe ɔnutɔ asi mlɔ kura kyɛ ɛ, mlatrázi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa a.’ ”

english_verse: As Paul and Barnabas were leaving the synagogue, the people begged them to say more about these same things on the next Sabbath.
translation_verse: Gi Paulo ni Banaba bɛzɛsɛ̀ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, banima bekpe ba tɔ bedzi aba ní Lizemɔniwe ʋla ɛ aʋa tɔ bagatrɛ ní eboa la su gi Paulo apani i, aʋa aki ba.

english_verse: After the service, many Jews and a lot of Gentiles who worshiped God went with them. Paul and Barnabas begged them all to remain faithful to God, who had treated them with undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Gi banima bɛsɛ ni kutsatsa mɛ pɔ́ ɛ, Yudanɛ glegle ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ bedzi Yudanɛ ɛ, bekuni Paulo ni Banaba kude. Lɛ Paulo ni Banaba beegu kunugu iiki ba a, lɛ bɛkpɛ ba kigagla tɔ beze ligbã́ ní Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The next Sabbath almost everyone in town came to hear the message about the Lord.
translation_verse: Ní Lizemɔniwe lɛ gi ligba ni lɛ ɛkpɛ aʋa ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa kloe ní ɔma nɔ mɛ aba si yinu Aya yɛ liboe le.

english_verse: When the Jewish people saw the crowds, they were very jealous. They insulted Paul and spoke against everything he said.
translation_verse: Gi Yudanɛ ma bɛmɔ linɔkpi le e, kaŋuza keku ba lɛ bɛnada Paulo liboe le e gi azaapani i, lɛ bekpini ye e.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas bravely said: We had to tell God's message to you before we told it to anyone else. But you rejected the message! This proves that you don't deserve eternal life. Now we are going to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo ni Banaba bɛpani ni kigagla ɛ sanɔ ɔ si, “Liziã si bapani Aya liboe le aki mlɔ kpɛ ɔtɔ. Pɔ̀ gi mlázi lɛ ɛ, xe mlebù isu si mlegyeno ɛki ligbã moblo le e, kwiayurɔ mlɔ tɔ kwiatrɛ ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The Lord has given us this command, “I have placed you here as a light for the Gentiles. You are to take the saving power of God to people everywhere on earth.”
translation_verse: Lɛ linu ɔsã nɔ gi Aya aki blɔ ɔ si, ‘Mabitɛ wɔ tɔ ze kuklekle ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, tɔ kelea petee kamɔ kubúbú.’ ”

english_verse: This message made the Gentiles glad, and they praised what they had heard about the Lord. Everyone who had been chosen for eternal life then put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Gi banama gi bénu Yudanɛ benu liboe le e, isami iku ba lɛ bɛkpɔ̀ Ɔkaɛ liboe le e, lɛ banama gi Aya akya aki ligbã moblo le e, bedzi baʋaziŋa ɛ.

english_verse: The message about the Lord spread all over this region.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ liboe le lizeehle eku ɔkɔ kakaa ní kusio klɔ ʋa mɛ.

english_verse: But the Jewish leaders went to some of the important men in the town and to some respected women who were religious. They turned them against Paul and Barnabas and started making trouble for them. They even chased them out of this part of the country.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yudanɛ ma bɛkpɛ ɔpisáse ní banama gi bedi ɔma nɔ eboe la onu ni badzɛ gi bɛɛdzi aŋu mɛ baklɔ ɔ, gi beesumu Aya mɛ ɛ. Bekpese kedu kuyɔ ní Paulo ni Banaba su lɛ bɛkpikpini ba edono ní ba kesea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas shook the dust from that place off their feet and went on to the city of Iconium.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Paulo ni Banaba besugu ovugbo lo ní ba kuklikpɔɔ ɛplɛ ki ba tɔ lize gyesi ki ba si Aya aahe kutu ki ba a, lɛ bɛsɛ ɛtrɛ ní Ikonion e.

english_verse: But the Lord's followers in Antioch were very happy and were filled with the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ baʋaziŋa ba ni Antioxia beyi ni isami ní Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas spoke in the synagogue in Iconium, just as they had done at Antioch, and many Jews and Gentiles put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Nite ke lize ní Ikonion fɛ. Gi Paulo ni Banaba bɛtrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, bɛpani mumɔ nite me si Yudanɛ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ, banɔ glegle bɛzi ŋa.

english_verse: But the Jews who did not have faith in him made the other Gentiles angry and turned them against the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yudanɛ ba ya gi báziŋa ɛ, bɛkpɛ lɛ kpɛ ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ ba mɛ si bakízi ŋa a, lɛ bekyi ba ní Paulo ni Banaba su i.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas stayed there for a while, having faith in the Lord and bravely speaking his message. The Lord gave them the power to work miracles and wonders, and he showed that their message about his gift of undeserved grace was true.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo ni Banaba beze niklɔ lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ ɔ, lɛ bɛpani ni kigagla ní Ɔkaɛ su u, Ɔkaɛ gi yɛ aŋa katsalia aki ba si ba liboe le ní lipomepɛpɛ nɛ su i, linu anukwarɛɛ gi aki ba kigagla a, tɔ babitɛ enugutseboe ni eboe mamɔ edi i.

english_verse: The people of Iconium did not know what to think. Some of them believed the Jewish group, and others believed the apostles.
translation_verse: Banima ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ bɛlɛ kupɛ tuʋa, batɔ beze ni Yudanɛ ma kude, lɛ batɔ fɛ beze ní Paulo ni Banaba kude e.

english_verse: Finally, some Gentiles and Jews, together with their leaders, decided to make trouble for Paul and Barnabas and to stone them to death.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ atɔ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ani ba onu bezeze ba bɛtrɔ si biawa Paulo ni Banaba nyɔnyɔɛ tɔ biasa ba akla.

english_verse: In Lystra there was a man who had been born with paralyzed feet and had never been able to walk.
translation_verse: Ní Listra ɛ, onyime ɔtɔ eze gi agbà adɔ ni yene lipo le me e lɛ ɔ́tani kuga di i.

english_verse: The man was listening to Paul speak, when Paul saw that he had faith in Jesus and could be healed. So he looked straight at the man
translation_verse: Eze niklɔ lɛ enu Paulo eboe la a. Gi Paulo amɔ si aziŋa a, xe biatani yɛ kupɔnɔ ɛ, edu aŋu ní yɛ aʋa,

english_verse: and shouted, “Stand up!” The man jumped up and started walking around.
translation_verse: lɛ nì ole gagla ɛ, apani si, “Yɔ tɔ lɛɛ ní wɔ ikli lɛ aʋa.” Onyimee ayɔ lixu lɛ ekpese kuga klanɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When the crowd saw what Paul had done, they yelled out in the language of Lycaonia, “The gods have turned into humans and have come down to us!”
translation_verse: Gi linɔkpi le limɔ kidiliɛ gi Paulo abitɛ ɛ, bekpese ozi kukpɛ ní ba Likonia siya sɛ mɛ si, “Babu ba bedzi banɔ lɛ beple eku blɔ kusi i!”

english_verse: The people then gave Barnabas the name Zeus, and they gave Paul the name Hermes, because he did the talking.
translation_verse: Bɛki Banaba linyi si Zeus, lɛ bɛki Paulo linyi si Hermes i, lɛ ese linu si Paulo yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi yɛ azɛɛsa kupani i.

english_verse: The temple of Zeus was near the entrance to the city. Its priest and the crowds wanted to offer a sacrifice to Barnabas and Paul. So the priest brought some bulls and flowers to the city gates.
translation_verse: Ɔbunetee gi odi ní Zeus obu yɛ ɛ, gi yɛ lisumugba lɛ lilɛɛ ní ɔma nɔ kutu yɔ ɛ, amani egume gi bɛkpɛ seƒoƒo aga kí la a, ní ɔma nɔ kevunu ya, lɛ ese linu si yɛ ni linɔkpi le bɛpɛ si beeyi busu ikí Paulo ni Banaba.

english_verse: When the two apostles found out about this, they tore their clothes in horror and ran to the crowd, shouting:
translation_verse: Gi Paulo ni Banaba benu kidiliɛ gi bɛpɛ si bɛɛbitɛ ɛ, bɛkyì ba ewu la lɛ bese eku linɔkpi le kusi i ni ozí si,

english_verse: Why are you doing this? We are humans just like you. Please give up all this foolishness. Turn to the living God, who made the sky, the earth, the sea, and everything in them.
translation_verse: “Ege loso mlɛbitɛ nite e? Blɔ ɔtutɔ fɛ banɔ ko kunu mlɔ kiniɛ! Kulí ní ya tɔ kupani Liboe Gbã nɛ kí mlɔ, tɔ mladɔ edzini bidɔ pluplu bɛ ya elu kude, tɔ mlekuni Aya gi yɛ awla ligbã́ nɛ lilɛ ɛ kude e. Yɛ abitɛ lifunu ne ni kesea ni axu la ni kidɔ kakaa gi kikpasi ní bɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In times past, God let each nation go its own way.
translation_verse: Tsã ɛ, aki kude si ɔnɔ kakaa ɔbitɛ ní yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: But he showed he was there by the good things he did. God sends rain from heaven and makes your crops grow. He gives food to you and makes your hearts glad.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ izeki si kute si Yɛ ɔlɛ lipoe kakaa gani bidɔ gbã kubitɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ izɛki dzeni dɔni lifune ní bidɔ likpɛpoe le, izɛki kiŋaŋa ani isami ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Even after Paul and Barnabas had said all this, they could hardly keep the people from offering a sacrifice to them.
translation_verse: Nì eboa la ya kyɛ ɛ, Paulo ni banaba bepe xe ableke bɛtani kude kutsi ki linɔkpi le si likíyi busu aki ba a.

english_verse: Some Jewish leaders from Antioch and Iconium came and turned the crowds against Paul. They hit him with stones and dragged him out of the city, thinking he was dead.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ atɔ gi bɛdɔ Antioxia ní Pisidia ni Ikonion e, bɛba lɛ bekyi linɔkpi le lɛ begyodi ba ɛmani ba kapɛa, lɛ bɛsa Paulo akla, xe behe ye dono ɔma nɔ mɛ ni kugyodi si etse e.

english_verse: But when the Lord's followers gathered around Paul, he stood up and went back into the city. The next day he and Barnabas went to Derbe. Paul and Barnabas Return to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi baʋaziŋa ba bɛba ɛkpɛ likpɔ ní yɛ su i, ayɔ, lɛ edzi atrɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ. Gi kelea kɛti i, yɛ ni Banaba bɛsɛ trɛ ní Derbe. Paulo ni Banaba Bedziba Antioxia ní Siria

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas preached the good news in Derbe and won some people to the Lord. Then they went back to Lystra, Iconium, and Antioch in Pisidia.
translation_verse: Paulo ni Banaba bɛpani Liboe Gbã nɛ ní Derbe ɔma nɔ mɛ, lɛ bɛmɔ baʋaziŋa glegle niklɔ ɔ. Lɛ bedzi trɛ ní Listra ni Ikonion ani Antioxia ní Pisidia ɛ,

english_verse: They encouraged the followers and begged them to remain faithful. They told them, “We have to suffer a lot before we can get into God's kingdom.”
translation_verse: bɛkpɛ ɔna ní baʋaziŋa ba lipo lɛ bɛkpɛ ba kigagla tɔ beze ní anukwarɛ ni kuziŋa mɛ, ani tɔ betè si, “Linu si kuga baxixa glegle me xe kwiatiku Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas chose some leaders for each of the churches. Then they went without eating and prayed that the Lord would take good care of these leaders who had trusted in the Lord.
translation_verse: Ní baʋaziŋa ba ɔha kakaa mɛ ɛ, bedu baka, lɛ gani Aya kuxwaxwa ni onugu nyanya mɛ ɛ, bɛkɔ ba ɛkpɛ Ɔkaɛ ɔniliɛ aʋa gi bɛka ɛ iwla mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas went on through Pisidia to Pamphylia,
translation_verse: Gi bɛga Pisidia bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀ ɛza ɛ, bɛba Pamfilia.

english_verse: where they preached in the town of Perga. Then they went down to Attalia
translation_verse: Niklɔ bɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ní Perga lɛ beple etrɛ ní Atalia ɛ,

english_verse: and sailed to Antioch in Syria. It was there that they had been placed in God's care for the work they had now completed.
translation_verse: dɔni niklɔ ɛ, beku onitsahu lo etrɛ ni Antioxia. Niklɔ gi bɛkɔ ba ɛkpɛ ní Aya iwla mɛ kɔkpɛ axwɛ a na gi bɛgù i.

english_verse: After arriving in Antioch, they called the church together. They told the people what God had helped them do and how he had made it possible for the Gentiles to believe.
translation_verse: Gi bɛba Antioxia ɛ, bɛkpɛ baʋaziŋa ba likpɔ lɛ bɛsi ba kidɔ kakaa a, gi Aya abitɛ gani ba aʋa a, ni kiliɛ gi adra kudeonu aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma fɛ tɔ bazɛ ŋa a.

english_verse: Then they stayed there with the followers for a long time.
translation_verse: Lɛ beze niklɔ lipoe gblamadza ni baʋaziŋa ba a.

english_verse: Some people came from Judea and started teaching the Lord's followers that they could not be saved, unless they were circumcised as Moses had taught.
translation_verse: Banɔatɔ bɛdɔ Yudea gi bɛba Antioxia a, lɛ bɛzɛbasɛ benemi ba bidɔ ɔ si, “Xegi bátsa ɔyaʋi aki mlɔ ní Mose ɔsã nɔ onu i, mlatrámɔ kubúbú u.”

english_verse: This caused trouble, and Paul and Barnabas argued with them about this teaching. So it was decided to send Paul and Barnabas and a few others to Jerusalem to discuss this problem with the apostles and the church leaders. The Church Leaders Meet in Jerusalem
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya limani kuboehe ni eboe me kukyakya glegle ní Paulo ni Banaba ani ba kusi. Lɛ loso baʋaziŋa ba bɛtrɔ lɛ si Paulo, Banaba kɔpɔni bamɛ batɔ batrɛ ní banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ni ɔha lɔ mɛ banɔtsitsi ba gi bali ní Yerusalem i, klɔ tɔ bazɛmɔ ba ní liboe lɛ ya su.

english_verse: The men who were sent by the church went through Phoenicia and Samaria, telling how the Gentiles had turned to God. This news made the Lord's followers very happy.
translation_verse: Baʋaziŋa ba bɛkpɛ ba kude lɛ bɛga Foenike ni Samaria a, gi bɛzɛpani ní kiliɛ ɛ gi banama a gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ bekyi vu Ɔkaɛ su u. Gi lidzi isami ʋidi ki baʋaziŋa ba petee e.

english_verse: When the men arrived in Jerusalem, they were welcomed by the church, including the apostles and the leaders. They told them everything God had helped them do.
translation_verse: Gi beku Yerusalem i, baʋaziŋa ba ni banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ani ɔsɔli ha lɔ banɔtsitsi ba bɛzi ba: lɛ bɛbɔ ba amani yɛ ní kidɔ kakaa ɛliɛ gi Aya abitɛ gani ba aʋa su u.

english_verse: But some Pharisees had become followers of the Lord. They stood up and said, “Gentiles who have faith in the Lord must be circumcised and told to obey the Law of Moses.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔatɔ gi bɛdɔ Farisianɛ ma likpɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ, banama gi bakɔ bɛzi ŋa ɛ, bɛyɔ kedu, lɛ bɛpani si, “Lilɛ si, batsà ɔyaʋi aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, tɔ batrɔ aki ba si, bevu Mose ɔsã nɔ mɛ ní awla.”

english_verse: The apostles and church leaders met to discuss this problem about Gentiles.
translation_verse: Nite si banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ni banɔtsitsi ba bɛtsa tɔ beevu liboe le edi.

english_verse: They had talked it over for a long time, when Peter got up and said: My friends, you know that God decided long ago to let me be the one from your group to preach the good news to the Gentiles. God did this so they would hear and obey him.
translation_verse: Agi bevu liboe le edi kɔna ni lipoetɔ ɛ, Petro ayɔ alɛɛ kedu ya lɛ apani si, “Beneminyime ba, mlete si tsã ɛ Aya akya mɛ adɔ ní mlɔ kusi tɔ banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, bianu liboe Gbã nɛ dɔni mɛ onu, atɔ biazɛ ŋa.

english_verse: He knows what is in everyone's heart. And he showed he had chosen the Gentiles, when he gave them the Holy Spirit, just as he had given his Spirit to us.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya gi yɛ ete ɔnɔ kakaa yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, abasi si yɛ azɛ ba gi akɔ Yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ aki ba kiliɛ gi aki blɔ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God treated them in the same way that he treated us. They put their faith in him, and he made their hearts pure.
translation_verse: Ɔ́kpɛ lidzegaga ní blɔ ni ba kusi i, lɛ akɔ ba eboe zimizi na akyɛ ba kɔkpɛ ba kuziŋa a.

english_verse: Now why are you trying to make God angry by placing a heavy burden on these followers? This burden was too heavy for us or our ancestors.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, ege lɛ loso mleekeni Aya edi i, gi mlɛɛtrɔ adɔtu nyipu ni ba aʋa a, adɔtu ala gi blɔ bakatsi ba loo blɔ kútani kutu u?

english_verse: But we believe that we will be saved by the gift of undeserved grace from our Lord Jesus Christ, just as the Gentiles are.
translation_verse: Owo! Kwizi ŋa lɛ kwimɔ kubúbú gani Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ba kyɛ bɛmɔ kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Everyone kept quiet and listened as Barnabas and Paul told how God had given them the power to work a lot of miracles and wonders for the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Ɔha lɔ petee ɛgɔ tsriɖi gi benu Banaba ni Paulo ba eboe klakla la a, ni enugutseboe la ni eboe mamɔ edi ala pɔ́ gi Aya abitɛ gani ba aʋa ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma kusi i.

english_verse: After they had finished speaking, James said: My friends, listen to me!
translation_verse: Agi bɛgù i, Yakobo ata atrɔ si, “Benemi ba, mlata mɛ kutu!

english_verse: Simon Peter has told how God first came to the Gentiles and made some of them his own people.
translation_verse: Simon asrɔ mlɔ, kiliɛ gi Aya akɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ akí banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, gi akya banɔ adɔ ni ba mɛ tɔ beze yɛ banɛ ɛ.

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will return and rebuild David's fallen house. I will build it from its ruins and set it up again.
translation_verse: ‘Aya apani si, “Lɛ ya kede e, maadziba, tɔ madzini Dawid kukusi yo kudu. Maalulɔ kudɔkpa liɔ gi kwizizi i, lɛ maaki kwiaze gagla dzɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then other nations will turn to me and be my chosen ones. I, the Lord, say this.
translation_verse: Atɔ kelea banɔ gɔgɔ ba pɔ́ biapɛ́ Ɔkaɛ klɔ kudeo, banama petee gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe maxwa si beze mɛ banɛ ɛ. Nite Ɔkaɛ gi izɛbitɛ eboa la ya a,

english_verse: I promised it long ago.”
translation_verse: gi bete dɔni okpese klɔ ke e apani.

english_verse: And so, my friends, I don't think we should place burdens on the Gentiles who are turning to God.
translation_verse: “Mɔkɔ mɛ kugyodi yo kunu si, kukíkpɛ banama a, gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, pɔ xe bekyi vu Aya ɛ, kunugu,

english_verse: We should simply write and tell them not to eat anything that has been offered to idols. They should be told not to eat the meat of any animal that has been strangled or that still has blood in it. They must also not commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: boŋu tɔ ku ŋwlimi ɔki ba si, bekyri kiŋaŋa kakaa gi bɛɛdɔnɔ kabupe me e, ni kihasi, lɛ bakítà baga gi bɛwɔ ika mɛ ɛ, lɛ bakíŋwɛ ba ika lɛ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We must remember that the Law of Moses has been preached in city after city for many years, and every Sabbath it is read in our synagogues. A Letter to Gentiles Who Had Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si dɔnɔ tsã ke e, bizɛklà, lɛ bizɛpani ní Mose ɔsã nɔ su ní ɔma kakaa mɛ ní Lizemɔniwe kakaa aʋa ní Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ.” Keplekpaa lia gi Baka ba Bɛŋwlimi tsisi Baʋaziŋa ba ní Antioxia ɛ

english_verse: The apostles, the leaders, and all the church members decided to send some men to Antioch along with Paul and Barnabas. They chose Silas and Judas Barsabbas, who were two leaders of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ni ɔha lɔ banɔtsitsi ba kɔpɔni ɔsɔli ha lɔ pɔ́ bɛtrɔ lɛ si, biabu benyimeatɔ ní ba kusi tsisi ní Antioxia kɔpɔni Paulo ni Banaba: bá benu Yuda gi bɛzɛxwa si Barsaba ni Sila a, benyime gi benu baka ní benemi ba kusi i.

english_verse: They wrote a letter that said: We apostles and leaders send friendly greetings to all of you Gentiles who are followers of the Lord in Antioch, Syria, and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛŋwlimi keplekpa ɛkpɛ ní ba awla a si, Blɔ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba ni ɔsɔli ha lɔ mɛ banɔtsitsi ba gi kunu mlɔ benemi i, kwitsisi kunɔpanɔ kí benemi ba gi bénu Yudanɛ, gi bali ní Aya kusumu yo me e, gi bali ní Antioxia, Siria ni Kilikia a.

english_verse: We have heard that some people from here have terribly upset you by what they said. But we did not send them!
translation_verse: Kwinu si, banɔatɔ gi bɛdɔ blɔ kusi i, babɛpisa mlɔ begyodi ʋa gani eboa la gi bɛɛpani mɛ ɛ. Aní blɔ kwikpɛ banɔ ba ya a.

english_verse: So we met together and decided to choose some men and to send them to you along with our good friends Barnabas and Paul.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso blɔ pɔ́ kwitrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si kwiakpɛ benyimeatɔ kɔpɔni blɔ baʋia Banaba ni Paulo tsisi mlɔ,

english_verse: These men have risked their lives for our Lord Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: banama gi bɛkɔ ba ligbã́ nɛ kyɛ ɛ, kɔkpɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ linyi ne.

english_verse: We are also sending Judas and Silas, who will tell you in person the same things that we are writing.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kwikpɛ Yuda ni Sila ɛtsisi mlɔ, banama gi ba tutɔ biapani eboa la a, gi kwiŋwlimi si mlɔ ɔ, ni ba tutɔ ba inugu le.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit has shown us that we should not place any extra burden on you.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si liku Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra nɔ ni blɔ aŋu si, kukítrɔ ɔsã nɔ lidɔtu ʋidi itɔtɔ ní mlɔ aʋa a sani eveve a la ya ko,

english_verse: But you should not eat anything offered to idols. You should not eat any meat that still has the blood in it or any meat of any animal that has been strangled.
