You must also not commit any terrible sexual sins. If you follow these instructions, you will do well. We send our best wishes.
translation_verse: mlekyri bidɔ gi bɛki babu ʋa a, ni ika ani baga gi bɛwɔ ika mɛ ɛ ba kigyaɛ, ni kihasi; xe mledi isu ya aʋa mumɔ ní la mɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ mlɔ isu iayɔ. Mleze mumɔ.

english_verse: The four men left Jerusalem and went to Antioch. Then they called the church members together and gave them the letter.
translation_verse: Gi bɛkpɛ ba kude pɔ́ ɛ, bɛba Antioxia, lɛ bɛkpɛ baʋaziŋa ba petee likpɔ ɛ, lɛ bɛkɔ keplekpaa ɛki ba a.

english_verse: When the letter was read, everyone was pleased and greatly encouraged.
translation_verse: Agi bɛkla ka ɛ, kɛkpɛ ɔna ní ba lipo lɛ bɛmɔ isami i.

english_verse: Judas and Silas were prophets, and they spoke a long time, encouraging and helping the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yuda ni Sila gi benu beboedoplɛ ɛ, bɛgba benemi ba ni eboe glegle kɔki lipoe gblamadza: lɛ bɛkpɛ kifu ni kigagla ba mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The men from Jerusalem stayed on in Antioch for a while. And when they left to return to the ones who had sent them, the followers wished them well.
translation_verse: Agi beze niklɔ taʋɛ ɛ, benemi ba bɛkpɛ ba kude ni kusuyɔ lɛ bedzitrɛ ní banama gi bɛkpɛ ba klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: But Paul and Barnabas stayed on in Antioch, where they and many others taught and preached about the Lord. Paul and Barnabas Go Their Separate Ways
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo ni Banaba beze ní Antioxia a, gi ba ni banɔ glegle dzedze e, bɛzɛbasɛ bidɔ lɛ bɛzɛpani Ɔkaɛ yɛ liboe gbã nɛ ɛ. Paulo ni Banaba ba kusi kulɛlɛɔ

english_verse: Sometime later Paul said to Barnabas, “Let's go back and visit the Lord's followers in the cities where we preached his message. Then we will know how they are doing.”
translation_verse: Lipoetɔ kede e, Paulo asi Banaba si, “Abla ɛ, kitɔ kudzi itrɛ tɔ kuzeedi benemi ba ní ɔma kakaa mɛ gi kwipani Ɔkaɛ yɛ liboe le e, si kitɛ balɛ na a.”

english_verse: Barnabas wanted to take along John, whose other name was Mark.
translation_verse: Barnaba apɛ si bagbani Yohanes gi bɛzɛxwa si Marko pɔni ba isu ya.

english_verse: But Paul did not want to, because Mark had left them in Pamphylia and had stopped working with them.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo amɔ si lípɛ si ba gbani ɔniliɛ ya gi adɔ ni ba kude edzino ní Pamfilia a, xe ɔ́wa axwɛ na pɔni ba pɔni isu u.

english_verse: Paul and Barnabas argued, then each of them went his own way. Barnabas took Mark and sailed to Cyprus,
translation_verse: Kuboesemonu gagla kwiku ba kusi, lɛ bɛlɛ ní ba ahanuglɔ ba su i. Banaba agbani Marko apɔni isu ya, lɛ beku onitsahu lo etrɛ ní Kipro e.

english_verse: but Paul took Silas and left after the followers had placed them in God's care.
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo akya Sila pɔni isu ya ɛ, lɛ gi baʋaziŋa ba bɛkɔ ba ɛkpɛ ní Aya iwla mɛ ɛ, bɛsɛ.

english_verse: They traveled through Syria and Cilicia, encouraging the churches.
translation_verse: Lɛ azɛɛga Siria ni Kilikia xe azaakpɛ baʋaziŋa ba iha lɛ kigagla ɛ.

english_verse: Paul and Silas went back to Derbe and Lystra, where there was a follower named Timothy. His mother was also a follower. She was Jewish, and his father was Greek.
translation_verse: Paulo ni Sila bɛtrɛ Derbe lɛ gi bɛsɛ niklɔ ʋa ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní Listra niklɔ gi ɔʋaziŋa ɔtɔ ɔ gi bizɛxwa si Timoteo eze e. Yene eze Yudadze gi azɛ Yesu aʋa aŋa ɛ, pɔ̀ yɛka eze Hela ɔnɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Lord's followers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy,
translation_verse: Benemi ba petee ní Listra ni Ikonion, bɛki kunutsatsa gbã ní Timoteo su.

english_verse: and Paul wanted him to go with them. But Paul first had him circumcised, because all the Jewish people around there knew that Timothy's father was Greek.
translation_verse: Paulo apɛ si yigbani yɛ evu ní yɛ kudegagaa mɛ, lɛ loso aki si batsà ɔyaʋi aki yɛ kɔkpɛ Yudanɛ ma gi bali niklɔ loso o, lɛ ese linu si ba petee bete sì yɛka onu Hela ɔnɛ.

english_verse: As Paul and the others went from city to city, they told the followers what the apostles and leaders in Jerusalem had decided, and they urged them to follow these instructions.
translation_verse: Agi bɛzɛga ima nɛ mɛ ɛza ɛ, bɛsi baʋaziŋa ba batrɔtrɔ ʋa gi baxwɛdzɛ ba ni betsitsi ba, ní Yerusalem bɛtrɔ likla ɛki ba si bevu ní awla su u.

english_verse: The churches became stronger in their faith, and each day more people put their faith in the Lord. Paul's Vision in Troas
translation_verse: Nite si kigagla kiku iha lɛ su ní kuziŋa mɛ, lɛ ba kiklaklaɛ kizɛsrɛ pɔnɔ liwe kakaa a. Paulo Amɔ Kububasɛ ní Troa

english_verse: Paul and his friends went through Phrygia and Galatia, but the Holy Spirit would not let them preach in Asia.
translation_verse: Gi bɛsɛ niklɔ ɛ, Paulo ni yɛ bagaglɔnɛ ba bɛga Frigia ni Galatia kuseo me eza, lɛ ese linu si Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ etsí kude ɛki ba si bakípani liboe gbã nɛ ní Asia,

english_verse: After they arrived in Mysia, they tried to go into Bithynia, but the Spirit of Jesus would not let them.
translation_verse: lɛ gi beku Misia ɛ, bekeni idi si beetiku ní Bitinia, pɔ̀ Yesu yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ́ki ba kude e.

english_verse: So they went on through Mysia until they came to Troas.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛdɔ Misia ɛza lɛ beple eku ní Troa ɛ.

english_verse: During the night, Paul had a vision of someone from Macedonia who was standing there and begging him, “Come over to Macedonia and help us!”
translation_verse: Elesi la ɛ, Paulo amɔ kububasɛ si onyime ɔtɔ gi ádɔ Makedonia ɛ, ɔlɛ kedu ya lɛ ɛɛki yɛ litukpo si, “Tsa axu la ba Makedonia tɔ gapɔni blɔ!”

english_verse: After Paul had seen the vision, we began looking for a way to go to Macedonia. We were sure that God had called us to preach the good news there. Lydia Becomes a Follower of the Lord
translation_verse: Agi Paulo amɔ kububasɛɔ ko e, kwibla kyɔŋ si, kutrɛ ní Makedonia lɛ ese linu si kwimɔ si, Aya axwa blɔ si kuzɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ kí banima niklɔ. Lidia Filipidzee Ekyi yɛ Ɔna mɛ

english_verse: We sailed straight from Troas to Samothrace, and the next day we arrived in Neapolis.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kwiku onitsahu lo dɔni Troa lɛ kwitsa axu la ɛtrɛ ní Samotrake, ní keletitia mɛ ɛ, kwiku Neapoli.

english_verse: From there we went to Philippi, which is a Roman colony in the first district of Macedonia. We spent several days in Philippi.
translation_verse: Lɛ dɔni niklɔ ɛ, kwiba Filipi gi onu Makedonia kesea ɔma ʋidi lo o, eze Roma banɛ ma ɔma lɛ kwize ní ɔma lɔ klɔ mɛ ewe atɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Then on the Sabbath we went outside the city gate to a place by the river, where we thought there would be a Jewish meeting place for prayer. We sat down and talked with the women who came.
translation_verse: Ní Lizemɔniwe le aʋa ɛ, kwido ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛtrɛ ní onipe lo su ní blɔ kugyodi onu si, banima biaxogo ní ɔkutɔ klɔ ʋa zɛɛxwa Aya. Agi kwize kesɛa ɛ, kwipani ki badzɛatɔ gi bexogo niklɔ ɛ.

english_verse: One of them was Lydia, who was from the city of Thyatira and sold expensive purple cloth. She was a worshiper of the Lord God, and he made her willing to accept what Paul was saying.
translation_verse: Badzɛa ya gi beze nu Paulo liboe le e, bamɛ tole eze ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si Lidia a. Eze kusa kyikyi ɔdada ní Tiatira ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ. Eze ɔnɔ gi azeesumu Aya a lɛ Ɔkaɛ adra yɛ ɔna nɔ si onu liboe gbã nɛ, lɛ azɛ eboa la gi Paulo ɛɛpani i.

english_verse: Then after she and her family were baptized, she kept on begging us, “If you think I really do have faith in the Lord, come stay in my home.” Finally, we accepted her invitation. Paul and Silas Are Put in Jail
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛkpɛ kuni kí yɛ ni yɛ kepea petee e, asi blɔ si, “Xe mlɛzi lɛ si medzi Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔʋaziŋa ɛ, kɔlɔ mlagaze ní mɛ kepea mɛ.” Lɛ blɔ fɛ kwizi lɛ ɛ. Bɛkpɛ Paulo ni Sila Ligba ní Filipi

english_verse: One day on our way to the place of prayer, we were met by a slave girl. She had a spirit in her that gave her the power to tell the future. By doing this she made a lot of money for her owners.
translation_verse: Liba lɛ mɛ si, gi kwizɛtrɛ níklɔ gi bizɛxwa Aya ɛ, kadzɛʋi ɔnɔdzidzi ɔtɔ mɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi eze e, xe eze ɔkpakpadze e, atsani blɔ, azɛɛki si yɛ benete ba bɛzɛɛmɔ kibɔ glegle dɔni yɛ kukpakpaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The girl followed Paul and the rest of us, and she kept yelling, “These men are servants of the Most High God! They are telling you how to be saved.”
translation_verse: Ekuni Paulo ni blɔ kude, ni ozi si, “Banɔaya ba ɛ, Aya Ʋidi ye yɛ baxwɛdzɛ benu, bɛɛpani kimlɔ ní kiliɛ gi mlaga a, xe mlamɔ kubúbú su u.”

english_verse: This went on for several days. Finally, Paul got so upset that he turned and said to the spirit, “In the name of Jesus Christ, I order you to leave this girl alone!” At once the evil spirit left her.
translation_verse: Eze ní lɛ aʋa ewe glegle gi liki si Paulo eyeʋi isu u, ekyi lɛ asi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ní ɔdzɛʋi ɛ mɛ si, “Makpɛ ɔsã akí wɔ ní Yesu Kristo linyi ne me si do za ní yɛ mɛ!” Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ edo ní ɔnyɔsasa lɔ klɔ ke aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: When the girl's owners realized they had lost all chances for making more money, they grabbed Paul and Silas and dragged them into court.
translation_verse: Agi ɔdzɛʋi yɛ yɛ benete ba bɛmɔ si, ba ɔŋa klɔ gi lɔ aʋa bɛɛwa igyi le edo eza ɛ, bevu Paulo ni Sila, lɛ behe ba ɛtsini line ne me e, ní betsixwa ba aŋu mɛ,

english_verse: They are telling us to do things we Romans are not allowed to do.”
translation_verse: xe bɛɛbasɛ ikɔɛ lɛ klɔ ɔ, gi iyɔ kedu ní blɔ ɔsã nɔ su u, gi kutrátani lɛ aʋa kubitɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Romaanɛ kunu u.”

english_verse: The crowd joined in the attack on Paul and Silas. Then the officials tore the clothes off the two men and ordered them to be beaten with a whip.
translation_verse: Lɛ linɔkpi le liyɔ kedu ní Paulo ni Sila su u, lɛ betsixwa ba bɛki si bakyì ewu la ní Paulo ni Sila su i, lɛ bɛki kigagla si bekusi ba a.

english_verse: After they had been badly beaten, they were put in jail, and the jailer was told to guard them carefully.
translation_verse: Agi bekusi ba mɔmɔɔmɔ ɛ, bɛkpɛ ba ligba, lɛ bɛki ligba lɛ aʋa odidi ye ɔsã si ɔbla ba mɔmɔɔmɔ.

english_verse: The jailer did as he was told. He put them deep inside the jail and chained their feet to heavy blocks of wood.
translation_verse: Gi yɛ kɔɔ azɛ ɔsã nɔ ɛ, akɔ ba akpɛ ní ligba lɛ odzudzu klɔ ke, lɛ abɔ ba ikli lɛ ègboo na ɛ.

english_verse: About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing praises to God, while the other prisoners listened.
translation_verse: Lɛ elesi la tetsu i, Paulo ni Sila bɛzɛxwa Aya, lɛ bɛzɛzà idzi i zɛkpɔ Aya a, lɛ banama gi beze ligba lɛ ɛ, bɛzɛɛta ba kutu.

english_verse: Suddenly a strong earthquake shook the jail to its foundations. The doors opened, and the chains fell from all the prisoners.
translation_verse: Kyɔŋ klɔ ko e, kesea kɛhwa gagla, niteme si ligba zizi nɛ lisugu, lɛ ipupo le petee idra ní lɛ isuwla mɛ ɛ, lɛ ègboo na gi bɛkpɛ banima ɛ eble e.

english_verse: When the jailer woke up and saw that the doors were open, he thought that the prisoners had escaped. He pulled out his sword and was about to kill himself.
translation_verse: Ko e, ligba lɛ aʋa odidi ye enyime adɔ ni sra mɛ, lɛ gi amɔ si ligba lɛ ipupo le idra edu i, ebu yɛ ihɛ gblamadzaɛ si yiye isu ya, lɛ ese linu si egyoedi si banama petee gi bɛkpɛ ligba lɛ ɛ, bese sɛ.

english_verse: The jailer asked for a torch and went into the jail. He was shaking all over as he knelt down in front of Paul and Silas.
translation_verse: Gi ligba lɛ ɔʋadi ye eʋi i, xe bɛki yɛ kani yɛ ɛ, ese eku ní ligba lɛ mɛ, azeesugu lɛ adɔ ní Paulo ni Sila ikli lɛ onu u.

english_verse: After he had led them out of the jail, he asked, “What must I do to be saved?”
translation_verse: Lɛ agbani ba edono xe eʋi si, “Baka ba, ege kilɛ kí mɛ si mibitɛ xe mamɔ kubúbú na a?”

english_verse: They replied, “Have faith in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved! This is also true for everyone who lives in your home.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bebu le onu si, “Zi Ɔkaɛ Yesu aʋa ŋa a, tɔ mlɔ ni mlɔ kepea mlamɔ kubúbú.”

english_verse: Then Paul and Silas told him and everyone else in his house about the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛzɛpani Ɔkaɛ su eboe la ɛ si yɛ ni banima petee ní yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: While it was still night, the jailer took them to a place where he could wash their cuts and bruises. Then he and everyone in his home were baptized.
translation_verse: Lɛ agbani ba ní elesi la ɔnyɔsasa lɔ klɔ aʋa a, xe abɔ ba ebi la aki ba ɛ, lɛ bɛkpɛ kuni kí yɛ ni yɛ benete ba petee kyɔŋ ɔ.

english_verse: They were very glad they had put their faith in God. After this, the jailer took Paul and Silas to his home and gave them something to eat.
translation_verse: Lɛ ligba lɛ ɔʋadi ye atsini ba ní yɛ kepea mɛ ɛ, xe aki ba kiŋaŋa a, lɛ yɛ ni yɛ kepea pɔ́ bɛmɔ isami si, bedzi Aya yɛ baʋaziŋa a.

english_verse: The next morning the officials sent some police with orders for the jailer to let Paul and Silas go.
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, ɔma nɔ betsixwa ba bɛkpɛ bewukpɛ ba ɛ tsisi ligba lɛ aʋa odidi ye ni ɔsã si, “Yurɔ benyime ba klɔ ba tɔ basɛ.”

english_verse: The jailer told Paul, “The officials have ordered me to set you free. Now you can leave in peace.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ligba lɛ ɔʋadi ye asi Paulo eboa la si, “Ɔma nɔ betsixwa ba bɛtsisi ɔnɔ si, bayurɔ wɔ ni Sila; lɛ loso e, mledo abla tɔ mlatrɛ ní kusuyɔ mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: But Paul told the police, “We are Roman citizens, and the Roman officials had us beaten in public without giving us a trial. They threw us into jail. Now do they think they can secretly send us away? No, they cannot! They will have to come here themselves and let us out.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo asi bewukpɛ ba si, “Gi bekusi blo litsi mɔxwamɔxwa ní ɔma nɔ kusi i, blɔ banama gi kunu Roma banɛ ma ɛ, xe bɛkpɛ blɔ ligba lɛ ɛ, abla kɔ mlábú blo ní kikpokpo me na a? Owo! mlaki tɔ baga ebu blo ba tutɔ.”

english_verse: When the police told the officials that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, the officials were afraid.
translation_verse: Lɛ bewukpɛ ba bɛzɛpani eboa la ya ɛsi betsixwa ba ɛ, agi benu si Roma banɛ Paulo ni Sila benu i lifu liku ba,

english_verse: So they came and apologized. They led them out of the jail and asked them to please leave town.
translation_verse: lɛ bɛba ɛyɔ Paulo ni Sila ba ina nɛ ɛ. Xe bɛ gbani ba edono e, lɛ beʋi dɔni ba iwla mɛ si basɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But Paul and Silas went straight to the home of Lydia, where they saw the Lord's followers and encouraged them. Then they left.
translation_verse: Gi Paulo ni Sila bedo ní ligba lɛ ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní Lidia kepea mɛ, niklɔ bɛtsani baʋaziŋa ba xe bɛkpɛ ɔna ba lipo, lɛ bɛsɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After Paul and his friends had traveled through Amphipolis and Apollonia, they went on to Thessalonica. A synagogue was in that city.
translation_verse: Gi bɛga Amfipoli ni Apolonia ɛza ɛ, bɛba Tesalonika, niklɔ gi Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɔlɛ ɛ,

english_verse: So as usual, Paul went there to worship, and on three Sabbaths he spoke to the people. He used the Scriptures
translation_verse: lɛ kiliɛ gi Paulo izɛbitɛ lɛ ɛ, eti eku ní ɔtsaklɔ; lɛ akɔ Ezemɔniwe tata nyɔŋ akɔ aka sika ni ba dɔni kuŋwlimi dradra mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: to show them that the Messiah had to suffer, but that he would rise from death. Paul also told them that Jesus is the Messiah he was preaching about.
translation_verse: lɛ azeebu lɛ mɛ aki ba si, lilɛ si Kristo ɔmɔ ɔzia, tɔ ɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ, “Ɔniliɛ ya ɛ yɛ onu Kristo Yesu, ɔniliɛ klɔ su liboe gi lɛ mɛɛpani si mlɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Some of them believed what Paul had said, and they became followers with Paul and Silas. Some Gentiles and many important women also believed the message.
translation_verse: Lɛ ba mɛ batɔ bɛziŋa, xe bɛsú Paulo ni Sila su nite ke badzɛ dzi aŋu mɛ glegle ba kyɛ ni Hela nɛ ba ya gi bizeese Aya ɛ, bamɛ beglegle kyɛ bekyi vu Aya.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders were jealous and got some troublemakers who hung around the marketplace to start a riot in the city. They wanted to drag Paul and Silas out to the mob, and so they went straight to Jason's home.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yudanɛ ma bɛkɔ kaŋuza ní ba su u, lɛ loso bɛgbani benyime zimizi batɔ ɛpɔni isu ya dɔni ablɔ ʋa aʋa, lɛ gi bɛki banɔaklɔ ʋa bɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛ, bekpese ozi kukpɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ, lɛ ba balɛ ní Yason kepea onu u, gi bɛzɛpɛ́ Paulo ni Sila si bebu ba ɛki linɔkpi le e.

english_verse: But when they did not find them there, they dragged out Jason and some of the Lord's followers. They took them to the city authorities and shouted, “Paul and Silas have been upsetting things everywhere. Now they have come here,
translation_verse: Gi bámɔ ba ɛ, behe Yason ni baʋaziŋaatɔ ɛtsini ɔma nɔ betsixwa ba klɔ ni ozi si, “Benyime ba ya gi bɛɛkpɛ kunugu ní kelea mɛ ɛ, bɛba ni ya fɛ.

english_verse: and Jason has welcomed them into his home. All of them break the laws of the Roman Emperor by claiming that someone named Jesus is king.”
translation_verse: Ba Yason azɛakpɛ yɛ kepea mɛ; lɛ banɔ ba ya ʋa petee bɛta Kaisaro ɔsã nɔ ese, gi bɛbasɛ si ɔnɔ dzedze e, gi bɛxwa si Yesu i, yɛ onu okusi ye.”

english_verse: The officials and the people were upset when they heard this.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔnɔha lɔ ni ɔma nɔ betsixwa ba bɛpisa a, gi benu eboa la ya ʋa ɛ.

english_verse: So they made Jason and the other followers pay bail before letting them go. People in Berea Welcome the Message
translation_verse: Gi bɛnya bidɔ ɛki Yason ni banɔ gɔgɔ ba a, xe bɛtsa bɛ ɛ, bɛyurɔ awla ní basu. Paulo ni Sila Bɛtrɛ ní Beroia

english_verse: That same night the Lord's followers sent Paul and Silas on to Berea, and after they arrived, they went to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Gi kelea keblo e, baʋaziŋa ba bɛkpɛ Paulo ni Sila kude kyɔŋ ɛtsisi Beroia. Agi beku niklɔ ɛ, bɛtrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ.

english_verse: The people in Berea were much nicer than those in Thessalonica, and they gladly accepted the message. Day after day they studied the Scriptures to see if these things were true.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ ba ya kɔ ɛ, ba begyodi ʋa mɛ bɛdra ɛsani banima ní Tesalonika, ba kɔ ɛ, bɛzi liboe le ni ɔna faã, lɛ bɛzɛpami kuŋwlimi dradra mɛ liwe kakaa si eboa la gi Paulo apani i, alɛ ní la mɛ na a.

english_verse: Many of them put their faith in the Lord, including some important Greek women and several men.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ba mɛ banɔ glegle bɛzi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa, lɛ Heladze dzi aŋu mɛ ni Helanɛ glegle ba fɛ bɛzi ŋa a.

english_verse: When the Jewish leaders in Thessalonica heard that Paul had been preaching God's message in Berea, they went there and caused trouble by turning the crowds against Paul.
translation_verse: Agi Yudanɛ a ma gi bali ní Tesalonika benu si, Paulo apani Aya liboe le ní Beroia fɛ ɛ, bɛba niklɔ, lɛ bɛkpɛ ozi kpɛ ɔnɔha lɔ mɛ tɔ bɛɛpisa ba kyɛ.

english_verse: At once the followers sent Paul down to the coast, but Silas and Timothy stayed in Berea.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ baʋaziŋa ba bɛtsisi Paulo axuta la kyɔŋ, lɛ Sila ni Timoteo bɛwɔ ní Beroia ɛ.

english_verse: Some men went with Paul as far as Athens, and then returned with instructions for Silas and Timothy to join him as soon as possible. Paul in Athens
translation_verse: Lɛ banama gi beze ni Paulo kude e, bɛgbani yɛ zɛnani Atene, lɛ gi Paulo aki ba ole akí Sila ni Timoteo si bagatsani yɛ kyikya ɛ, bedzi ba Beroia. Paulo Abasi Bidɔ ní Atene

english_verse: While Paul was waiting in Athens, he was upset to see all the idols in the city.
translation_verse: Agi Paulo ezeedi ba kudeo onu ní Atene e, likpɛ yɛ kunugu, gi amɔ si etugu na ɛsrɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He went to the synagogue to speak to the Jews and to anyone who worshiped with them. Day after day he also spoke to everyone he met in the market.
translation_verse: Lɛ apani akí Yudanɛ ma ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, gi bizese Aya ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɔ, kɔpɔni banama gi bizɛtsani yɛ liwe kakaa ní kedzia mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some of them were Epicureans and some were Stoics, and they started arguing with him. People were asking, “What is this know-it-all trying to say?” Some even said, “Paul must be preaching about foreign gods! That's what he means when he talks about Jesus and about people rising from death.”
translation_verse: Yɛ ni Epikurio banɛ ma ni Stoa badɔbasɛ batɔ tɔ fɛ bɛka sika. Lɛ banɔatɔ beʋi si, “Liboe woli koŋ motemotee onugugu liɛ ya fɛ ɛpɛ si yido o?” Pɔ̀ batɔ fɛ si, “Libitɛ ŋwisi kese dzedze me babuatɔ su ɛɛpani kinɛ ɛ;” lɛ ese linu si apani Yesu yɛ liboe gbã nɛ ni yɛ kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ su asi ba.

english_verse: They brought Paul before a council called the Areopagus, and said, “Tell us what your new teaching is all about.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛgbani yɛ tsini otsixwa klɔ ɔ, gi bizɛxwa si Areopago o, niklɔ gi ɔma nɔ betsitsi ba bizɛtsa ɛ, ni liboe lɛ ya si, “Kwipɛ si kute kidɔ vuvu iliɛ gi wɔɛbasɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We have heard you say some strange things, and we want to know what you mean.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si eboa la gi wɔɛpani i, edzì eboe vuvu ní blɔ atukpa la mɛ lɛ loso ga bu la ese e.”

english_verse: More than anything else the people of Athens and the foreigners living there loved to hear and to talk about anything new.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ateneanɛ ma petee pɔ́ ni bababa ba níklɔ ɛ, baʋímɔ ɔwla aki kidɔ dzedze tɔtɔ sani si bazenu eboe vuvu tɔ bazɛpani ní la su u.

english_verse: So Paul stood up in front of the council and said: People of Athens, I see that you are very religious.
translation_verse: Gi Paulo ɔlɛɛ ní Areopago ye kutsatsa mɛ ɛ, apani si, “Mlɔ Atene benyime ba, mamɔ si, ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, mleezesumu babu ʋa koŋ.

english_verse: As I was going through your city and looking at the things you worship, I found an altar with the words, “To an Unknown God.” You worship this God, but you don't really know him. So I want to tell you about him.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si gi maklanɔ ní mlɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, xe mamɔ kudɔkpaa liɔ gi mleezesumu i, mamɔ ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ ɔtɔ gi bɛŋwlimi ní lɔ aʋa si, ‘Ki Aya, liɛ gi béte e.’ Abla ɛ, kidiliɛ gi mléte e, pɔ̀ xe mleesumu ke e, kɛ su ko mapɛ si mipani kí mlɔ.

english_verse: This God made the world and everything in it. He is Lord of heaven and earth, and he doesn't live in temples built by human hands.
translation_verse: Aya gi yɛ abitɛ kelea mɛ ni kidɔ kakaa ní kamɛ ɛ, yɛ onu lifunu ne ni kesea Ɔkaɛ, óʋize ní agba gi bɛwa ni iwla mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: He doesn't need help from anyone. He gives life, breath, and everything else to all people.
translation_verse: lɛ aní banɔ iwla fɛ bizɛkɔ esumu ye e, kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, ɔ́ziã kididɔ ɛdɔnɔ ɔnɔnɔ kɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ aki ɔnɔ kakaa ligbã, Ɔfuɛfuɛ ni kidɔ kakaa,

english_verse: From one person God made all nations who live on earth, and he decided when and where every nation would be.
translation_verse: lɛ dɔni ɔnɔ tole me e, ebu kelea mɛ limade kakaa si beze kelea petee me, lɛ yɛ ɔtutɔ atrɔ lipoe ni ozekɔ aplɛ aki ba si beze e.

english_verse: God has done all this, so that we will look for him and reach out and find him. He isn't far from any of us,
translation_verse: Aya abitɛ la ya tɔ banima biapɛ́ yɛ, atɔ nyafɛ biamɔ yɛ, nya yɛ ɔnya dze ni blɔ su xe e.

english_verse: and he gives us the power to live, to move, and to be who we are. “We are his children,” just as some of your poets have said.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔnutɔ apani si, “‘Yɛ ɔlɛ xe kulɛ ɛ, xe kuzɛɛhwa a, xe kunu kidiliɛ gi kunu u.’ “Lɛ mlɔ badzipɔ̀ ba bɛpani si, ‘Blɔ kyɛ yɛ besoebi kunu u.’

english_verse: Since we are God's children, we must not think that he is like an idol made out of gold or silver or stone. He isn't like anything that humans have thought up and made.
translation_verse: “Abla gi kunu Aya yɛ bebi i, líma si kugyo le edi si, Aya liɛ ya ɛ, ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi sika loo klosalo loo likla gi bɛ bitɛ ní ɔnɔ yɛ kibitɛ ani litukpo me kugyodi unu kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In the past, God forgave all this because people did not know what they were doing. But now he says that everyone everywhere must turn to him.
translation_verse: Lɛ tsã ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi mlézete e, Aya ada bimbi ní lɛ aʋa, pɔ̀ abla ɛ, akpɛ ɔsã kí banima ní ɔkɔ kakaa si, bekyi ní ba ina nɛ mɛ.

english_verse: He has set a day when he will judge the world's people with fairness. And he has chosen the man Jesus to do the judging for him. God has given proof of this to all of us by raising Jesus from death.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si atrɔ liwe gi lɛ aʋa atráxwa litsi ni kelea petee ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ, gani onyime ɔtɔ gi akya aʋa, lɛ abasi ɔnɔ kakaa si, linu anukwarɛɛ gi ayɔ ni ɔniliɛ klɔ ʋa dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: As soon as the people heard Paul say a man had been raised from death, some of them started laughing. Others said, “We will hear you talk about this some other time.”
translation_verse: Agi benu gi Paulo ɛɛpani ní bekpo ʋa kuyɔyɔ su i, banɔatɔ bebu onugu me ní yɛ su, pɔ̀ batɔ bɛpani si, “Kwiapɛ si kwidzini eboa la ya unu lipoe dzedze.”

english_verse: When Paul left the council meeting,
translation_verse: Nite lize xe Paulo asɛ ní ba kusi i.

english_verse: some of the men put their faith in the Lord and went with Paul. One of them was a council member named Dionysius. A woman named Damaris and several others also put their faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ benyimeatɔ bɛsú yɛ su, lɛ bɛziŋa ɛ, banɔ ba ya ba mɛ tole onu Dionisio, ɔnɔtsitsi tole e, ani ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Damari kɔpɔni banɔdzedze gi bɛpɔni ba ɛ.

english_verse: Paul left Athens and went to Corinth,
translation_verse: Ní eboa la ya kede e, Paulo asɛ ní Atene, lɛ aba Korinto e.

english_verse: where he met Aquila, a Jewish man from Pontus. Not long before this, Aquila had come from Italy with his wife Priscilla, because Emperor Claudius had ordered the Jewish people to leave Rome. Paul went to see Aquila and Priscilla
translation_verse: Lɛ amɔ Yudanɛ ɔtɔ, yɛ linyi ne linu Akwila, bɛpɔ yɛ ní Ponto, yɛ ni yedze Priskila bɛdɔ Italia ɛ ba liʋí dze, lɛ ese linu si Okusi ye Klaudio akpɛ ɔsã si, Yudanɛ ma petee basɛ ní Roma, lɛ Paulo atrɛ si yizedi ba ɛ,

english_verse: and found out they were tent makers. Paul was also a tent maker, so he stayed with them, and they worked together.
translation_verse: lɛ gi yɛ kyɛ agba la basa ʋa su ɔxwɛwa onu loso e, awɔ baklɔ lɛ bɛzɛɛwa axwɛ a na klɔ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si la izɛwa xe izɛpɛ yɛ kiŋaŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Every Sabbath, Paul went to the synagogue. He spoke to Jews and Gentiles and tried to win them over.
translation_verse: Lɛ azaapani ní ɔtsaklɔ ní Lizemɔniwe kakaa aʋa a, gi azeebu eboe la mɛ aki Yudanɛ ma ni Helanɛ ma tɔ bazɛ aŋa a.

english_verse: But after Silas and Timothy came from Macedonia, he spent all his time preaching to the Jews about Jesus the Messiah.
translation_verse: Gi Sila ni Timoteo bɛdɔ Makedonia ɛba ɛ, Paulo amɔ lipoe le gi azeebu liboe le me akí Yudanɛ ma si Yesu yɛ onu Kristo ye.

english_verse: Finally, they turned against him and insulted him. So he shook the dust from his clothes and told them, “Whatever happens to you will be your own fault! I am not to blame. From now on I am going to preach to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi bɛyɔ kedu ní yɛ su i, xe bɛpani ebusioboe la ɛmani yɛ su i, ekple yɛ iwla lɛ akpɛ ba lɛ apani si, “Xegi mlɛlila ɛ, lídɔ mɔkɔ, mɛ isu idra, dɔni lɛ ya aʋa ɛ miipani liboe gbã nɛ ɛki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Paul then moved into the house of a man named Titius Justus, who worshiped God and lived next door to the synagogue.
translation_verse: Gi asɛ ní ba su i, atrɛ si yizeeze ní onyime ɔtɔ gi onu Yuda ɔnɛ kɔ ɔ, yɛ linyi ne linu Tito Yusto, onu ɔnɔ gi izesumu Aya a, lɛ yɛ kepea ketipɔni Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Crispus was the leader of the synagogue. He and everyone in his family put their faith in the Lord. Many others in Corinth also heard the message, and all the people who had faith in the Lord were baptized.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Krispo ɔtsaklɔ ɔʋadi ye ni yɛ kepea kɔ bɛzi ɔkaɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa, lɛ Korintoanɛ ba mɛ glegle gi bɛta yɛ kutu i, bɛziŋa, lɛ bɛkpɛ kuni kí ba ɛ.

english_verse: One night, Paul had a vision, and in it the Lord said, “Don't be afraid to keep on preaching. Don't stop!
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔkaɛ asi Paulo ní kububasɛ mɛ elesi la si, “Wukíse lifu, pɔ̀ pani liboe le, wukígɔ tsriɖi i,

english_verse: I am with you, and you won't be harmed. Many people in this city belong to me.”
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si malɛ ni wɔ, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trado ní wɔ aʋa si yiwa wɔ nyɔnyɔɛ, lɛ ese linu si banɔ glegle bali ní mɛ awla ní ɔma nɔ ya mɛ.”

english_verse: Paul stayed on in Corinth for a year and a half, teaching God's message to the people.
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo eze niklɔ ligɔ tile ni badzidzi tieglo lɛ azɛɛbasɛ Aya liboe le ní ba kusi i.

english_verse: While Gallio was governor of Achaia, some of the Jewish leaders got together and grabbed Paul. They brought him into court
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi Galio edzì Roma ɔnɛ ɔʋadi ye ní Axaya kesea aʋa ɛ, Yudanɛ atɔ bɛkpɛ isu likpɔ lɛ bɛyɔ kedu ní Paulo su i, xe bevu ye mani otsixwaklɔ ɛ,

english_verse: and said, “This man is trying to make our people worship God in a way that is against our Law!”
translation_verse: lɛ bɛpani si, “Ɔniliɛ eekyi banima begyodi ʋa, ɛɛbasɛ si besumu Aya gani bedeatɔ gi bɛyɔ kedu ní ɔsã nɔ su aʋa a.”

english_verse: Even before Paul could speak, Gallio said, “If you were charging this man with a crime or some other wrong, I would have to listen to you.
translation_verse: Agi Paulo si yidra yɛ onugu lo ko e, Galio asi Yudanɛ ma si, “Xegi liboe zimizi litɔ, loo liboe nyaninyani itɔ abitɛ mlɔ Yudanɛ ma ɛ, nya maaplɛ litɔ kese ta mlɔ kutu,

english_verse: But since this concerns only words, names, and your own law, you will have to take care of it yourselves. I refuse to judge such matters.”
translation_verse: pɔ̀ gi linu eboe hehe, enyi kɔpɔni mlɔ isã nɛ ɛ, mlɔ tutɔ mledi lɛ ɔza, lɛ ese linu si mátraxwa litsi ni eboa la ya elu su u.”

english_verse: Then he sent them out of the court.
translation_verse: Lɛ akpikpini ba dɔni otsixwa klɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The crowd grabbed Sosthenes, the Jewish leader, and beat him up in front of the court. But none of this mattered to Gallio. Paul Returns to Antioch in Syria
translation_verse: Ko ba petee bevu Sostene, Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɔʋadi ye lɛ bekusi ye ní litsixwagbo le ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ. Pɔ̀ Galio ɔ́kɔ litɔtɔ lɛ mɛ koŋu u. Paulo Edzi aba Antioxia

english_verse: After Paul had stayed for a while with the Lord's followers in Corinth, he told them goodbye and sailed on to Syria with Aquila and Priscilla. But before he left, he had his head shaved at Cenchreae because he had made a promise to God.
translation_verse: Agi Paulo eze Korinto ewe glegle e, akla baʋaziŋa ba niklɔ lɛ eku ohu lo atrɛ ní Siria a, Priskila ni Akwila bɛpɔni yɛ, pɔ̀ xe aasɛ ní Kenkrea ɛ, axwa yɛ litukpo le lɛ ese linu si aka kitamu aplɛ aki Aya ní Yudanɛ ma ɔsã nɔ onu u.

english_verse: The three of them arrived in Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. He then went into the synagogue to talk with the people there.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛba Efeso e, lɛ asɛ ní Priskila ni Akwila su niklɔ ɛ, xe yɛ tete atrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ, lɛ yɛ nì Yudanɛ ma bɛkya eboe me e.

english_verse: They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
translation_verse: Agi bɛki yɛ litukpo si oze ní basu tɔ lidze taʋɛ ɛ, ɔ́dimɛ

english_verse: He told them goodbye and said, “If God lets me, I will come back.”
translation_verse: pɔ̀ akla ba lɛ apani si, xe linu Aya yɛ kupɛpɛ ɛ, maadziba mlɔ klɔ. Lɛ eku ohu lo asɛ ní Efeso e.

english_verse: Paul sailed to Caesarea, where he greeted the church. Then he went on to Antioch.
translation_verse: Gi ana Kaisarea ɛ, emu atrɛ ní Yerusalem xe azɛɛpani baʋaziŋa ba a, lɛ eple atre ní Antioxia ɛ.

english_verse: After staying there for a while, he left and visited several places in Galatia and Phrygia. He helped the followers there to become stronger in their faith. Apollos in Ephesus
translation_verse: Agi eze ní niklɔ taʋɛ ɛ, asɛ lɛ azɛɛga Galatia ni Frigia bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, xe azaakpɛ baʋaziŋa ba pɔ́ kigagla ɛ. Apolo Atrɛ Efeso ni Korinto

english_verse: A Jewish man named Apollos came to Ephesus. Apollos had been born in the city of Alexandria. He was a very good speaker and knew a lot about the Scriptures.
translation_verse: Ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, Yudanɛ ɔtɔ gi yɛ linyi ne linu Apolo o, gi bɛpɔ ní Aleksandria ɛ, aba Efeso. Eze onyime, gi ete kunugu kìgu u, lɛ ete kuŋwlimi dradra mɛ eboe la fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He also knew much about the Lord's Way, and he spoke about it with great excitement. What he taught about Jesus was right, but all he knew was John's message about baptism.
translation_verse: Bɛbasɛ yɛ Ɔkaɛ kudeo, lɛ ekpese Ɔkaɛ Yesu su eboe la kubasɛ mɔmɔmɔ ni kifu i, pɔ̀ Yohanes kuni kpɛkpɛ ki banima su liboe ko ete.

english_verse: Apollos started speaking bravely in the synagogue. But when Priscilla and Aquila heard him, they took him to their home and helped him understand God's Way even better.
translation_verse: Lɛ Apolo ekpese kunugu yo kugu lifumose ní ɔtsaklɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ gi Akwila ni Priskila bɛta yɛ kutu i, bɛgbani yɛ tsini ba kepea mɛ lɛ bebu Aya liboe le ese kí yɛ mɔmɔɔmɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Apollos decided to travel through Achaia. So the Lord's followers wrote letters, encouraging the followers there to welcome him. After Apollos arrived in Achaia, he was a great help to everyone who had put their faith in the Lord Jesus because of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Gi Apolo apɛ si yitrɛ Axaya ɛ, baʋaziŋa ba bɛkpɛ ɔna yɛ lipo, lɛ bɛŋwlimi keplekpa ɛki baʋaziŋa ba ní Axaya tɔ bazɛ yɛ. Agi eku niklɔ ɛ, apɔni banɔ glegle si baziŋa gani Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He got into fierce arguments with the Jewish people, and in public he used the Scriptures to prove that Jesus is the Messiah.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ ma bátani yɛ onu kulɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si gani kuŋwlimi dradraa mɛ ɛ, abasi ɔnɔ kakaa si Yesu yɛ onu Kristo ye.

english_verse: While Apollos was in Corinth, Paul traveled across the hill country to Ephesus, where he met some of the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Gi Apolo eze ní Korinto e, liba lɛ mɛ si Paulo aga kaʋetu ya ikli lɛ kudeo lɛ aba Efeso e, niklɔ atsani baʋaziŋaatɔ,

english_verse: He asked them, “When you put your faith in Jesus, were you given the Holy Spirit?” “No!” they answered. “We have never even heard of the Holy Spirit.”
translation_verse: lɛ eʋi ba si, “Agi mlɛziŋa ɛ, mlɛzi Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ na a?” Lɛ bebu le onu si, “Owo o, Kuʋínu ʋidi si Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: “Then why were you baptized?” Paul asked. They answered, “Because of what John taught.”
translation_verse: Lɛ eʋi ba si, “Nyaŋwɛ linyi me bɛkpɛ kuni i kimlɔ ɔ.” Lɛ bɛpani si, “Ní Yohanes yɛ linyi ne me e.”

english_verse: Paul replied, “John baptized people so they would turn to God. But he also told them someone else was coming, and they should put their faith in him. Jesus is the one that John was talking about.”
translation_verse: Paulo apani si, “Yohanes akpɛ kuni aki banama gi bɛyurɔ ba bede zimizi ba ɛ, lɛ asi ba si bazɛ ɔniliɛ gi atrába ní yɛ kede, aʋa ŋa a, yɛ onu Yesu u.”

english_verse: After the people heard Paul say this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Agi benu le e, bɛdimɛ lɛ bɛkpɛ kuni ki ba ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu linyi ne me e.

english_verse: Then Paul placed his hands on them. The Holy Spirit was given to them, and they spoke unknown languages and prophesied.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Paulo atrɔ awla ní ba aʋa ɛ, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛba ba aʋa, lɛ bɛpani siya ede ede gi bákpasi edi i, xe bɛpani eboe e, gi Aya aki si ba pani i.

english_verse: There were about twelve men in this group.
translation_verse: Lɛ ba pɔ́ beze benyime liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa.

english_verse: For three months Paul went to the synagogue and talked bravely with the people about God's kingdom. He tried to win them over,
translation_verse: Agi atrɛ ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, apani liboe le ni ɔna, lɛ abasi bidɔ badzidzi tiata zizi, gi azeekyi ba begyodi ba ní Aya kaʋaŋaa su u si liboe lɛ gi yɛ ɛpani i, lilɛ lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: but some of them were stubborn and refused to believe. In front of everyone they said terrible things about God's Way. Paul left and took the followers with him to the lecture hall of Tyrannus. He spoke there every day
translation_verse: Agi banɔatɔ ba ina nɛ mɛ idzu u, xe béxo yɛ liboe le oto o, pɔ̀ xe bɛzɛpani eboe zimizi ní Ɔkaɛ kudeo ni kɔ kudɔbasɛ yɔ su ní ɔnɔha lɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, asɛ ní basu, lɛ ebú baʋaziŋa ba ní ba kusi i, lɛ azeegu kunugu aki ba liwe kakaa ní Tirano yɛ lidɔkpasigba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: for two years, until every Jew and Gentile in Asia had heard the Lord's message. The Sons of Sceva
translation_verse: Lɛ azaabitɛ liboe lɛ ya agɔ taʋa nyɔŋ, niteme si banima pɔ́ ní Asia ɛ, Yudanɛ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ pɔ́, benu Ɔkaɛ yɛ liboe le. Skeva yɛ Bebi ba Bɛpɛ Aya yɛ Kigagla ɛ

english_verse: God gave Paul the power to work great miracles.
translation_verse: Aya aki Paulo kigagla si Paulo ɔbitɛ enugutseboe.

english_verse: People even took handkerchiefs and aprons that had touched Paul's body, and they carried them to everyone who was sick. All of the sick people were healed, and the evil spirits went out.
translation_verse: Liki si kuvlɔkpa ni eteme ewu ala su axwɛ gi awa kyɛ ɛ, xegi bɛkɔ bɛzɛɛgbɔni banyanya ba fɛ ɛ, bànya ʋa bizeblo, lɛ ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne fɛ izedo eza ní banima mɛ.

english_verse: Some Jewish men started going around trying to force out evil spirits by using the name of the Lord Jesus. They said to the spirits, “Come out in the name of that same Jesus that Paul preaches about!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ atɔ gi bɛzɛklanɔ ɔ, banama gi benu behwinete e, xe bizɛkpikpini ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne edono e, bekeni edi lɛ bekpese Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ linyi kuxwa kpɛ ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne si, “Ní Yesu ɔniliɛ su gi Paulo ɛɛpani i, linyi ne me, makpɛ ɔsã aki mlɔ si mledo aza ní yɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Seven sons of a high priest named Sceva were doing this,
translation_verse: Lɛ banama gi bɛɛbitɛ lɛ ya elu e, benu Yudanɛ kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye Skeva yɛ bebi, iyasɔʋi tigloele.

english_verse: when an evil spirit said to them, “I know Jesus! And I have heard about Paul. But who are you?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no ɛsi ba si, “Mete Yesu, lɛ Paulo fɛ ɛ mete liboetɔ ni yɛ su u, mlɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, mlɔ mlenu nyaŋwɛ ba ɛ?”

english_verse: Then the man with the evil spirit jumped on them and beat them up. They ran out of the house, naked and bruised.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ mɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ zimizi no eze e, ayɔ adɔ ba aʋa, xe ekusi ba, lɛ akpɛ ba ebi i, liki si bese edo ni kepea lia klɔ mɛ ni liya gi ika izedo ní ba su u.

english_verse: When the Jews and Gentiles in Ephesus heard about this, they were so frightened that they praised the name of the Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yudanɛ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ pɔ́, gi bali ní Efeso e, benu liboe lɛ ya, lɛ lifu liku ba petee e, lɛ bemuni Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ linyi ne e.

english_verse: Many who were followers now started telling everyone about the evil things they had been doing.
translation_verse: Lɛ banama gi bɛziŋa abla ɛ, beglegle bɛba lɛ bɛdra ba ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, xe bɛpani ni ba eboe zimizi na su iiki ɔnɔ kakaa ɛ.

english_verse: Some who had been practicing witchcraft even brought their books and burned them in public. These books were worth about
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔ glegle gi beze behwinete e, bɛmani ba kuplekpaa, bɛkpɛ kɔ likpɔ, lɛ bɛkani kɔ ní ba petee aŋu mɛ ɛ, gi bebù kɔ kudzi yo ní kibɔ mɛ ɛ, kwina kibɔ klosalo akpe avitetsu.

english_verse: So the Lord's message spread and became even more powerful. The Riot in Ephesus
translation_verse: Liki si Ɔkaɛ yɛ liboe le lihle lɛ limɔ kigagla ní ɔkɔ kakaa a.

english_verse: After all this had happened, Paul decided to visit Macedonia and Achaia on his way to Jerusalem. Paul had said, “From there I will go on to Rome.”
translation_verse: Gi eboa la ya ɛba pɔ́ ɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛbasɛ Paulo si ɔga Makedonia ni Axaya xe aatrɛ ní Yerusalem i. Lɛ Paulo apani si i, xe madzi niklɔ ʋa kede e liaziã si mitrɛ ní Roma fɛ.

english_verse: So he sent his two helpers, Timothy and Erastus, to Macedonia. But he stayed on in Asia for a while.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso akpɛ yɛ banɔpɔnɔ ba mɛ tiaʋa, Timoteo ni Erasto, atsisi Makedonia a, lɛ yɛ ɔtutɔ awɔ ní Asia kesea mɛ kí lipoetɔ onu i. Ozi Eku ní Efeso

english_verse: At that time there was serious trouble because of the Lord's Way.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, kunugu ʋidi utɔ kwidɔ ní Efeso ɔma nɔ mɛ ní baʋaziŋa ba bede ʋa su.

english_verse: A silversmith named Demetrius had a business that made silver models of the temple of the goddess Artemis. Those who worked for him earned a lot of money.
translation_verse: Klosalo ɔnana ɔtɔ gi yɛ linyi linu Demetrio o, gi izɛkɔ klosalo ena ɔbu yɛ Atemis yɛ isumu kɔ sisami nɛ kí kudada ɛ, xe aki si ba klosalo ye su baxwɛwa ba bɛzɛmɔ kibɔ glegle ní ba axwɛ na mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: Demetrius brought together everyone who was in the same business and said: Friends, you know we make a good living at this.
translation_verse: axwa yɛ baxwɛwa ba ni bedzedze ba ní ɔha lɔ klɔ mɛ akpɛ likpɔ lɛ asi ba si, “Benemi ba, mlete si kuzɛɛmɔ kibɔ glegle dɔni lixwɛ nɛ ya mɛ.

english_verse: But you have surely seen and heard how this man Paul is upsetting a lot of people, not only in Ephesus, but almost everywhere in Asia. He claims that the gods we humans make are not really gods at all.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlɛmɔ ɔ, xe mlenu fɛ ɛ si, aní Efeso ya tete, pɔ̀ ní Asia petee kloe kyɛ ɛ, Paulo liɛ ɛsi banɔ glegle, lɛ ekyi ba begyodi ʋa ni eboa la ya si, bidɔ gi bɛkɔ awla ɛbitɛ ɛ, bínu aya a.

english_verse: Everyone will start saying terrible things about our business. They will stop respecting the temple of the goddess Artemis, who is worshiped in Asia and all over the world. Our great goddess will be forgotten!
translation_verse: Lɛ aní lixwɛ lɛ ya tete lia lila lɛ linyi gbã nɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ biakpɛ blɔ onee Atemis ʋidi ye ni yɛ lisumugba lɛ kakpaa a, lɛ biakple kubùbù ní blɔ onee Atemis ɔtutɔ lɔ gi bizeesumu ní Asia ziziɛ ní kelea petee me su u.”

english_verse: When the workers heard this, they got angry and started shouting, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Agi ɔnɔha lɔ enu Dimetrio yɛ liboe le e, beyeʋi isu gagla, lɛ bekpese ozi kukpɛ si, Efesoanɛ ma Atemis ye, yɛ onu ɔbu ʋidi ye petee!

english_verse: Soon the whole city was in a riot, and some men grabbed Gaius and Aristarchus, who had come from Macedonia with Paul. Then everyone in the crowd rushed to the place where the town meetings were held.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔma zizi nɔ ɛpisa a, lɛ bɛyɔ xe bezeevu Gayo ni Aristarxo Paulo yɛ bagaglɔnɛ nɛ ba a gi bɛdɔ ní Makedonia a, lɛ behe ba ɛtsinɔ ní ɔma nɔ ɔtsaklɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Paul wanted to go out and speak to the people, but the Lord's followers would not let him.
translation_verse: Nya Paulo apɛ si yitrɛ ní ɔnɔha lɔ kusi i, pɔ̀ baʋaziŋa ba báki yɛ kudeo onu u.

english_verse: A few of the local officials were friendly to Paul, and they sent someone to warn him not to go.
translation_verse: Lɛ Asia kesea baʋadi atɔ gi benu Paulo yɛ baʋia ba fɛ, bɛkpɛ banɔ ɛtsisi yɛ, si bɛki yɛ litukpo, ɔkisi si yɛtrɛ ní ɔma nɔ ɔtsaklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Some of the people in the meeting were shouting one thing, and others were shouting something else. Everyone was completely confused, and most of them did not even know why they were there.
translation_verse: Ɔma zizi nɔ ɛpisa, banɔatɔ bɛkpɛ ozi sì nite, lɛ batɔ fɛ si nite:, lɛ banɔ glegle béte kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso bɛba ɔtsa klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Several of the Jewish leaders pushed a man named Alexander to the front of the crowd and started telling him what to say. He motioned with his hand and tried to explain what was going on.
translation_verse: Nite si Yudanɛ ma betutru Aleksandro kpɛ ɔnɔha lɔ ɔtɔ nɔ. Lɛ Aleksandro emuni yɛ ɔwla lɔ gi azaapɛ si yibu liboe lɛ gi liba lɛ mɛ, ese ɛki ɔnɔha lɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But when the crowd saw that he was Jewish, they all shouted for two hours, “Great is Artemis, the goddess of the Ephesians!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lipoe lɛ gi banima bɛmɔ egyesi si Yuda ɔnɛ onu i, ba petee bekpese ozi kukpɛ ni ole tole kɔki ŋwisi inyɔsasa tiʋa si, Efesoanɛ ma onee Atemis yɛ onu ɔbu ʋidi yɛ petee.

english_verse: Finally, a town official made the crowd be quiet. Then he said: People of Ephesus, who in the world doesn't know that our city is the center for worshiping the great goddess Artemis? Who doesn't know that her image which fell from heaven is right here?
translation_verse: Lɛ gi ɔma nɔ ɔdɔŋwlimi yɛ aki ɔnɔha lɔ ɛgɔ tsriɖi i, apani si mete si, “Efeso benyime ba a, ɔnɔ kakaa ete si, Efeso ɔma nɔ eedi Atemis ʋidi ye ni yɛ likla ɛnɛ gi liwɔli dɔni lifunu ne aʋa a.

english_verse: No one can deny this, and so you should calm down and not do anything foolish.
translation_verse: Abla gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani liboe lɛ ya kunada ɛ, linu si mlaplɛ litɔ kese tɔ mláki bitɛ kididɔ ní kusuyeʋi me e.

english_verse: You have brought men in here who have not robbed temples or spoken against our goddess.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si benyime ba ya gi mlɛgbani mani ya ɛ, bénu banɔ gi bézuru blɔ kusɔliɔ kididɔ lɛ bédo libusioboe yurɔ blɔ onee Atemis fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If Demetrius and his workers have a case against these men, we have courts and judges. Let them take their complaints there.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, xegi liboetɔ lilɛ ní Demetrio ni yɛ baxɛwa ba awla ní ɔnutɔ su i, etsixwawe la alɛ, lɛ ɔma nɔ betsixwa ba fɛ balɛ ɛ, mlaki tɔ bakɔ liboe ní banɔ ba klɔ ʋa su u.

english_verse: But if you want to do more than that, the matter will have to be brought before the city council.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi liboe dzedzeetɔ sanɔ lilɛ ní mlɔ awla ní kidɔ dzedzeetɔ su i, kɔlɔ biaxwa lɛ ní kutsatsa mɛ ní ɔsã nɔ onu.

english_verse: We could easily be accused of starting a riot today. There is no excuse for it! We cannot even give a reason for this uproar.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mlaaku kuxixa mɛ xegi bɛkɔ omono liboe lɛ ya ekɔ ɛtsa mlɔ onu ní ozikpɛkpɛ ɔlɔ ya su u, lɛ ese linu si liboeboe líma ní mlɔ awla gi lɛ mlakɔki akɔnta ní ozi ɔlɔ ya su u.”

english_verse: After saying this, he told the people to leave.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi apani nite pɔ́ ɛ, aki ɔnɔha lɔ ehle.

english_verse: When the riot was over, Paul sent for the followers and encouraged them. He then told them goodbye and left for Macedonia.
translation_verse: Agi bɛmɔni ozi lo kukpɛ ɛ, Paulo axwa baʋaziŋa ba mani yɛ klɔ, lɛ gi akpɛ ba kigagla ní ba kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, akla ba lɛ eku Makedonia kudeo me e.

english_verse: As he traveled from place to place, he encouraged the followers with many messages. Finally, he went to Greece
translation_verse: Gi aga kusio klɔ ɔ, xe akpɛ ba kigagla ni eboe glegle e, aba Hela kesea mɛ.

english_verse: and stayed there for three months. Paul was about to sail to Syria. But some of the Jewish leaders plotted against him, so he decided to return by way of Macedonia.
translation_verse: Lɛ eze niklɔ badzidzi tiata a, nya apɛ si yiku ohu lo ɛtrɛ ní Siria xe e, pɔ̀ gi amɔ si Yudanɛ ma bɛtrɔ liboe ní yɛ su i, ekyi yɛ begyodi ʋa lɛ azɛɛga Makedonia ɛ.

english_verse: With him were Sopater, son of Pyrrhus from Berea, and Aristarchus and Secundus from Thessalonica. Gaius from Derbe was also with him, and so were Timothy and the two Asians, Tychicus and Trophimus.
translation_verse: Lɛ banama gi bezeni yɛ kude e, ba benu, Sopatre, Piro gi ádɔ Beroia a yebi, Aristarxo ani Sekundo gi bɛdɔ Tesalonika a; Gayo gi ádɔ Derbe e, kɔpɔni Tixiko ni Trofimo gi bɛdɔ Asia ani Timoteo.

english_verse: They went on ahead to Troas and waited for us there.
translation_verse: Banɔ ba ya ɛ, betu ɔtɔ lɛ bezeepe blo ní Troa ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After the Festival of Thin Bread, we sailed from Philippi. Five days later we met them in Troas and stayed there for a week. Paul's Last Visit to Troas
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kwiku ohu lo ɛdɔni Filipi ní Yudanɛ ma Aklamɔnyasi na liwe le kede e, kwizɛtsani ba ní Troa liwe tsunɛ nɛ aʋa, lɛ kwize ní niklɔ ewe tegloele e. Paulo Adzɛ Troa Kugɔgɔɔ

english_verse: On the first day of the week we met to break bread together. Paul spoke to the people until midnight because he was leaving the next morning.
translation_verse: Ní ogle no liwe tɔpu nɛ aʋa a, gi kwitsa si kuŋa Ɔkaɛ yɛ kiŋaŋaɛ ɛ, Paulo apani aki banima lipoe gblamadza zɛkɔna ni elesi tetsu, lɛ ese linu si apɛ si, xe kelea kɛti i, yɛ aaku kudeo me.

english_verse: In the upstairs room where we were meeting, there were a lot of lamps.
translation_verse: Lɛ kani glegle bezekle e, ní abaa la ligba ɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ gi kwitsa ɛ.

english_verse: A young man by the name of Eutychus was sitting on a window sill. While Paul was speaking, the young man got very sleepy. Finally, he went to sleep and fell three floors all the way down to the ground. When they picked him up, he was dead.
translation_verse: Ɔyasɔʋi ɔtɔ, gi yɛ linyi linu Eutixo eze ní fɛsrɛ onu, gi Paulo eboe la edzè onu i, eku sra mɛ, lɛ awɔli adɔ ni abaa la ligbatrɔtrɔ lɛ litanɛ nɛ aʋa xe aba dɔ kesea ɛ, lɛ gi bezefeke ye e, bɛmɔ si etse.

english_verse: Paul went down and bent over Eutychus. He took him in his arms and said, “Don't worry! He's alive.”
translation_verse: Agi Paulo eple atrɛ ní kesea ɛ, ehe ye apɔni isu lɛ apani si, “Mlakikpɛ lɛ ozi i, ɔlɛ ligbã.”

english_verse: After Paul had gone back upstairs, he broke bread, and ate with us. He then spoke until dawn and left.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi edzì emu i, ayaʋi àklã na lɛ bɛta ɛ, lɛ apani aki ba zɛkɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi liwe le litu i, lɛ asɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then the followers took the young man home alive and were very happy. The Voyage from Troas to Miletus
translation_verse: Banima bɛgbani ɔyasɔʋi lɔ ɛtsini kepea mɛ ɔgbagbe, ni ɔna dɔdɔ glegle. Paulo Adɔ Troa Aba Malta

english_verse: Paul decided to travel by land to Assos. The rest of us went on ahead by ship, and we were to take him aboard there.
translation_verse: Kwiku ɔtɔ eku ohu lo me lɛ kwitrɛ ní Aso e: niklɔ kwiazi Paulo kpɛ ní ohu lo me e, abitɛ kutrɔtrɔ liɔ ya lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔtutɔ ɛ, ikli atrága.

english_verse: When he met us in Assos, he came aboard, and we sailed on to Mitylene.
translation_verse: Gi atsani blɔ ní Aso e, kwizi yɛ kpɛ ní ohu lo me lɛ kwiba Mitilene e.

english_verse: The next day we came to a place near Chios, and the following day we reached Samos. The day after that we sailed to Miletus.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kelea kɛti i, kwikpaʋi dɔni niklɔ lɛ kwiba Xio su i, lɛ lɛ keletitia mɛ ɛ, kwitipɔni Samo, gi kelea kɛti dzɛ ɛ, kwiba eku Malta.

english_verse: Paul had decided to sail on past Ephesus, because he did not want to spend too much time in Asia. He was in a hurry and wanted to be in Jerusalem in time for Pentecost. Paul Says Goodbye to the Church Leaders of Ephesus
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Paulo atrɔ si yidɔ Efeso eza, tɔ yikízizi lipoe ní Asia, lɛ ese linu si ɛɛkɔsi si xegi liasɔnɔ ɛ, tɔ yiku Yerusalem ní Pentekoste liwe le aʋa a. Paulo Akla Efesoanɛ ma Baka ba

english_verse: From Miletus, Paul sent a message for the church leaders at Ephesus to come and meet with him.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní Malta ɛ, akpɛ banɔ atsisi Efeso, lɛ bɛzɛxwa baʋaziŋa ba baka ba ɛmanɔ ɛ.

english_verse: When they got there, he said: You know everything I did during the time I was with you when I first came to Asia.
translation_verse: Agi bɛba yɛ klɔ ɛ, asi ba si, “Mlete kiliɛ gi meze e, xe mawa axwɛ na ní mlɔ kusi i, dɔni liwe tɔpu nɛ gi makpɛ ɔkli ní Asia ɛ,

english_verse: Some of the Jews plotted against me and caused me a lot of sorrow and trouble. But I served the Lord and was humble.
translation_verse: mesumu Ɔkaɛ ni kusububɔ ni kube gi kukenidi kwiba mɛ aʋa gani Yudanɛ ma batrɔtrɔ ʋa si beye me su u.

english_verse: When I preached in public or taught in your homes, I didn't hold back from telling anything that would help you.
translation_verse: Mlete si mákɔ liboeboe kpo mlo gi liakpɛ mlɔ kigagla a, mapani Aya yɛ liboe le, lɛ mabasɛ mlɔ bidɔ ní ɔha kusi ni dɔni kepeme tsini kepeme e,

english_verse: I told Jews and Gentiles to turn to God and have faith in our Lord Jesus.
translation_verse: mapani akí Yudanɛ ma ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ní ɔna mɛ kukyikyi su ki Aya a, ani tɔ bazɛ Ɔkaɛ Yesu aʋa ŋa.

english_verse: I don't know what will happen to me in Jerusalem, but I must obey God's Spirit and go there.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, dike, malɛ kunyanya mɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ gi mɛɛtrɛ ní Yerusalem i, lɛ méte liboe lɛ gi litraba mɛ aʋa niklɔ ɔ,

english_verse: In every city I visit, I am told by the Holy Spirit that I will be put in jail and will be in trouble in Jerusalem.
translation_verse: pɔ̀ mete si ní ɔma kakaa mɛ ɛ, Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛki mizeete si kunugu ni ligbã kuzeze kwipe me e.

english_verse: But I don't care what happens to me, as long as I finish the work the Lord Jesus gave me to do. And this work is to tell the good news about God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mákɔ lɛ si mɛ ligbã nɛ líŋa kedzi sanɔ ɔ, xegi maabloni mɛ kude gaga uliɔ gi mazi adɔ ni Ɔkaɛ Yesu kɔ ɛ, lɛ linu si misi banima liboe gbã nɛ ní Aya lipomepɛpɛ nɛ su u.

english_verse: I have gone from place to place, preaching to you about God's kingdom, but now I know that none of you will ever see me again.
translation_verse: “Lɛ abla ɛ, mete si, mlɔ banama petee kusi gi maga a, xe mazeebu Aya yɛ kukusi ŋaŋaa ese akɔ ɛ, mlátramɔ mɛ aŋu mɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I tell you today I am no longer responsible for any of you!
translation_verse: Lɛ loso, mɛɛsi mlɔ omono liwe le si, xe mlɔ mɛ ɔnutɔ aalila yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, mɛ onugu ɔ̀ma ní lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I have told you everything God wants you to know.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mékpo Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa su kudradra ní mlɔ su u.

english_verse: Look after yourselves and everyone the Holy Spirit has placed in your care. Be like shepherds to God's church. It is the flock he bought with the blood of his own Son.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mledi mlɔ isu ya aʋa mumɔ, lɛ mledi banama petee, gi Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛkpɛ ní mlɔ awla ɛ aʋa mumɔ ɔ, mleze banɔgbanɔ kí bebi ba ya gi Aya akpɛ ní mlɔ awla ɛ, gi edzì ni yɛ ɔtutɔ Yebi ye yɛ ika lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I know that after I am gone, others will come like fierce wolves to attack you.
translation_verse: Mete si ní mɛ kusɛsɛɔ kede e, kuŋwafudroʋi yo banyraga ʋa biaku mlɔ kusi, banama gi bátradi iʋesi le asigaga a,

english_verse: Some of your own people will tell lies to win over the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: lɛ ní mlɔatsali kusi i, benyime ba klɔ gi bizɛsɛ asia na ɛ, biayɔ, lɛ biahe baʋaziŋaatɔ bu ní mlɔ kusi gi bia zeni ba kude e.

english_verse: Be on your guard! Remember how day and night for three years I kept warning you with tears in my eyes.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mleze ni aŋu, tɔ mlaplɛ aŋu si, agɔ tata nyɔŋ gi meze ní mlɔ kusi i, elesi ni kemize, mámɔni mlɔ mɛ kakaa kugba ni kube e.

english_verse: I now place you in God's care. Remember the message about his gift of undeserved grace! This message can help you and give you what belongs to you as God's people.
translation_verse: “Lɛ abla ɛ, benemi ba a, makɔ mlɔ akpɛ ní Aya awla si odi mlɔ aʋa, yɛ liboe le liaki si mlatsi ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ aaki mlɔ ɔŋakɔ ní banama petee gi akya ɛ kusi.

english_verse: I have never wanted anyone's money or clothes.
translation_verse: Mázã kimbi ní ɔnɔnɔ yɛ klosalo loo sika, lo ewu su u.

english_verse: You know how I have worked with my own hands to make a living for myself and my friends.
translation_verse: Mlɔ tutɔ mlete si, mɛ iwlaɛ lɛ ya ʋa makɔ awa axwɛ na ki mɛ ni banama gi bɛpɔni mɛ ɛ, blɔ baziazia ʋa a.

english_verse: By everything I did, I showed how you should work to help everyone who is weak. Remember that our Lord Jesus said, “More blessings come from giving than from receiving.”
translation_verse: Ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, mabasɛ mlɔ si, linu si, ɔnɔ ɔwa axwɛ, tɔ aazi kí banama su gi kigagla kíma ɛ, tɔ mlaplɛ aŋu ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ eboa la gi yɛ ɔtutɔ asi mlɔ aʋa si, ‘Kukɔkɔ kunu kukrira sani kuzɛɛzɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Paul had finished speaking, he knelt down with all of them and prayed.
translation_verse: Agi apani la ya pɔ́ ɛ, atrɛ yɛ aklamasi na aʋa, lɛ yɛ ni ba petee bɛxwa Aya ɛ.

english_verse: They were especially sad because Paul had told them, “You will never see me again.” Then they went with him to the ship.
translation_verse: nì ɔna nyipu u, li lɛ gi liyeʋi ba linu liboe lɛ gi apani si, bátramɔ yɛ tɔ ɔ. Lɛ bɛya yɛ zɛna ni ohu lo me e.

english_verse: After saying goodbye, we sailed straight to Cos. The next day we reached Rhodes and from there sailed on to Patara.
translation_verse: Gi kwikla ba pɔ́ ɛ, kwimu iku ní ohu lo me lɛ kwikpaʋi i, xe kwidu aŋu na ni Kos i, lɛ gi kelea kɛti i, kwiba Rodo, lɛ dɔni niklɔ ʋa ɛ, kwiba Patara,

english_verse: We found a ship going to Phoenicia, so we got on board and sailed off.
translation_verse: niklɔ kwimɔ ohu ɔtɔ gi ɛɛtsa axu la ɛtrɛ ní Foinike e, lɛ kwiku lo e.

english_verse: We came within sight of Cyprus and then sailed south of it on to the port of Tyre in Syria, where the ship was going to unload its cargo.
translation_verse: Gi kwimɔ Kipro e, kwibu ye kpɛ kabaa mɛ lɛ kwitsa ɛza ɛtrɛ ní Siria ɛ, lɛ kwizɛtɔ ní Tiro e, lɛ ese linu si niklɔ ohu lo ɛtrábu adɔtu la.

english_verse: But when the week was over, we started on our way again. All the men, together with their wives and children, walked with us from the town to the seashore. We knelt on the beach and prayed.
translation_verse: Gi blɔ ewe la ɛna si kusɛ ɛ, kwitrɛ ní blɔ kude gaga aʋa, lɛ baʋaziŋa ba pɔ́, ni ba bedze ba kɔpɔni banuvɔ ba bɛgbani blɔ edono ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ gi kwina axuta la ɛ, blɔ pɔ́ kwidɔ blɔ aklamasi na aʋa xe kwixwa Aya ɛ,

english_verse: Then after saying goodbye to each other, we got into the ship, and they went back home.
translation_verse: gi kwikra blɔ ahanuglɔ ba pɔ́ ɛ, kwiku ohu lo lɛ ba kɔ ɛ bedzitrɛ ní ba kupeo me e.

english_verse: We sailed from Tyre to Ptolemais, where we greeted the followers and stayed with them for a day.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kwisɛ ní Tiro e, kwiba eku Ptolemai, lɛ kwipani benemi ba niklɔ ɛ, lɛ kwize ní basu liwe tile e.

english_verse: The next day we went to Caesarea and stayed with Philip, the preacher. He was one of the seven men who helped the apostles,
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, kwisɛ ɛba Kaisarea, lɛ kwitrɛ ní liboe gbã nɛ ɔpani pani yɛ ye Filipo kepea mɛ ɛ, yɛ eze banɔ tiegloele ba gi bɛkya ní Yerusalem si bapɔni baʋaziŋa ba ɛ, ba mɛ tole.

english_verse: and he had four unmarried daughters who prophesied.
translation_verse: Idisami tine gi ídi ni benyime e, xe izeedo eboe plɛ ɛ, ize ní yɛ awla.

english_verse: We had been in Caesarea for several days, when the prophet Agabus came to us from Judea.
translation_verse: Gi kwize ní níklɔ ewe atɔ ɛ, oboedoplɛ ɔtɔ, gi yɛ linyi linu si Agabo gi ádɔ Yudea ɛ aba.

english_verse: He took Paul's belt, and with it he tied up his own hands and feet. Then he told us, “The Holy Spirit says that some of the Jewish leaders in Jerusalem will tie up the man who owns this belt. They will also hand him over to the Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi aba blɔ klɔ ɛ, eble Paulo yɛ kasɛa mɛ ɔnyagbe no akɔ anya yɛ tutɔ yɛ ikli lɛ ni iwla lɛ, lɛ apani si, “Nite Aya Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani si, ‘Nite Yudanɛ ma bianya onyime kasɛa mɛ ɔnyagbeɔ nɔ ya onetee, ní Yerusalem, lɛ biakɔ yɛ ɔkpɛ awla kí banama gi bénu Yadanɛ ma ɛ.’ ”

english_verse: After Agabus said this, we and the followers living there begged Paul not to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Agi kwinu liboe lɛ ya ɛ, blɔ ni banama gi bali níklɔ ɛ, kwiki Paulo litukpo si ɔkitrɛ ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: But Paul answered, “Why are you crying and breaking my heart? I am not only willing to be put in jail for the Lord Jesus, but I am even willing to die for him in Jerusalem!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo ebu le onu lɛ apani si, ege kiliɛ ya gi mlɛɛbitɛ ɛ, xe mleebemi i, xe mleebu ɔna ní mɛ lipo te e? Lɛ ese linu si mɔkɔ ɛ, mabla isu ya aplɛ, aní si banya mɛ tete e, pɔ̀ tɔ mitse ní Yerusalem kɔkpɛ Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ linyi ne loso.

english_verse: Since we could not get Paul to change his mind, we gave up and prayed, “Lord, please make us willing to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kwimɔ si ɔ́trakyi yɛ batrɔtrɔ ba ɛ, kwigɔ tsriɖi lɛ kwipani si, Ɔkaɛ yɛ kupɛpɛɔ kuba kɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then we got ready to go to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní ewe ala ya kede e, kwibla lɛ kwitrɛ ní Yerusalem i.

english_verse: Some of the followers from Caesarea went with us and took us to stay in the home of Mnason. He was from Cyprus and had been a follower from the beginning. Paul Visits James
translation_verse: Lɛ baʋaziŋaatɔ fɛ gi bɛdɔ Kaisarea ɛ, bekuni blɔ kude trɛ, lɛ bɛgbani blɔ ɛtsinɔ ní Mnason kepea mɛ ɛ, tɔ kwiaya ní yɛ klɔ. Mnason eze ɔdɔkpasi kokoo gi ádɔ Kipro e. Paulo Adzɛ Yakobo klɔ

english_verse: When we arrived in Jerusalem, the Lord's followers gladly welcomed us.
translation_verse: Gi kwiku Yerusalem i, baʋaziŋa ba bɛzi blɔ ni isami.

english_verse: Paul went with us to see James the next day, and all the church leaders were present.
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, kwizeni Paulo kude trɛ ní Yakobo kepea mɛ, lɛ baʋaziŋa ba ba ɔnu bezeze ba petee bɛba niklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Paul greeted them and told how God had used him to help the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Agi apani ba ɛ, akla kidɔ kakaa gi Aya abitɛ ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, kusi i gani yɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ aki ba tile tile.

english_verse: Everyone who heard this praised God and said to Paul: My friend, you can see how many tens of thousands of our people have become followers! And all of them are eager to obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi benu le e, bɛkpɔ̀ Aya, lɛ bɛsi Paulo si, “Onemi, dani wɔɛmɔ ɛ, banɔ akpe akpe ba bɛziŋa ní Yudanɛ ma kusi i, lɛ ba petee bevu Mose Ɔsã nɔ mɛ gaglaa.

english_verse: But they have been told that you are teaching those who live among the Gentiles to disobey this Law. They claim that you are telling them not to circumcise their sons or to follow our customs.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ benu ní wɔ su si, wɔbasɛ Yudanɛ ba klɔ petee gi balɛ ní babubitɛ ba kusi i, si badɔedzi ni Mose ɔsã nɔ kude lɛ wɔ pani si, bakítsa ɔyaʋi akí ba bebi ba lɛ bakíga ní blɔ ikɔ lɛ onu u.

english_verse: What should we do now that our people have heard you are here?
translation_verse: Abla ɛ kitɛ kwiabitɛ ɛ? Kitɛkitɛ fɛ ɛ, ɔnɔha lɔ aakpɛ likpɔ tsã, lɛ ese linu si bianu godoo si wɔba.

english_verse: Please do what we ask, because four of our men have made special promises to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, bitɛ kidiliɛ gi kwiasi wɔ ɛ. Benyime tiene bali ní ya gi bɛbitɛ kakaplɛ kí Aya a.

english_verse: Join with them and prepare yourself for the ceremony that goes with the promises. Pay the cost for their heads to be shaved. Then everyone will learn that the reports about you are not true. They will know you do obey the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Gbani banɔ ba ya ʋa, tɔ plo isu ya ni ba, atɔ tsa kidiliɛ gi bizɛkɔ ɛ, ní ba litukpo o, tɔ batani ba etukpo la kuxwa, kɔlɔ ɛ banima petee biate si, eboa la gi benu ní wɔ su i, áma ní la mɛ ɛ, lɛ wɔ tutɔ ɛ, wuzɛɛga mumɔ, lɛ wovu Mose ɔsã nɔ ní awla a.

english_verse: Some while ago we told the Gentile followers what we think they should do. We instructed them not to eat anything offered to idols. They were told not to eat any meat with blood still in it or the meat of an animal that has been strangled. They were also told not to commit any terrible sexual sins.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe bɛziŋa ɛ, ba kwiŋwlimi kɔ, xe kwipani si tɔ, bekyri bidɔ gi bɛki babu ʋa, ni ika ani baga gi bɛwɔ ika mɛ ɛ, kɔpɔni kihasi.”

english_verse: The next day Paul took the four men with him and got himself ready at the same time they did. Then he went into the temple and told when the final ceremony would take place and when an offering would be made for each of them. Paul Is Arrested
translation_verse: Lɛ loso gi kelea kɛti i, Paulo ebu benyime ba klɔ apɔni yɛ isu ya, lɛ gi beplo isu ya pɔ́ ɛ, atrɛ ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ lɛ azaapani ní liwe lɛ gi kusuploo ewe la aablo su u, ni lipoe lɛ gi biaki bidɔ mɛ ɛ, xe biapɔʋi ní ba mɛ ɔkakaa litukpo e. Bevu Paulo ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ

english_verse: When the period of seven days for the ceremony was almost over, some of the Jewish people from Asia saw Paul in the temple. They got a large crowd together and started attacking him.
translation_verse: Gi ewe tegloele la eblo pɔ ɛ, Yudanɛ a ma gi bɛdɔ Asia kesea mɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ Paulo ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, bɛkpɛ ozi kpɛ ní ɔnɔha lɔ petee me, lɛ bevu ye e.

english_verse: They were shouting, “Friends, help us! This man goes around everywhere, saying bad things about our nation and about the Law of Moses and about this temple. He has even brought shame to this holy temple by bringing in Gentiles.”
translation_verse: Bɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Israelbanɛ ma, mlazi kí blɔ loo! Ɔniliɛ ya yɛ onu ɔniliɛ gi izɛbasɛ banima petee ní ɔkɔ kakaa bidɔ gi biyɔ kedu ní blɔakɔanɛ ma ni ɔsã nɔ ani lisɔligba ɛ lɛ ya su u. Lɛ sani lɛ ya ɛ, agbani Helanɛ loo Grikbanɛ atɔ fɛ amani lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, gi akɔ elulu ɔkɔ dradra kɔ lɔ ya ʋa su u.”

english_verse: Some of them thought that Paul had brought Trophimus from Ephesus into the temple, because they had seen them together in the city.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si bɛmɔ yɛ ni Trofimo, Efesoɔnɛɛ ní ɔma nɔ mɛ, lɛ begyo edi si Paulo agbani yɛ amani lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The whole city was in an uproar, and the people turned into a mob. They grabbed Paul and dragged him out of the temple. Then suddenly the doors were shut.
translation_verse: Lɛ ozi eku ní ɔma zizi nɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ banima bese ba lisɔligba lɛ mɛ, lɛ bevu Paulo o, xe behe ye dono ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛda ipupo le kyɔŋ ɔ.

english_verse: The people were about to kill Paul when the Roman army commander heard that all Jerusalem was starting to riot.
translation_verse: Gi bɛzɛpɛ si, beeye ye e, liboe le lizɛna baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye ní iklɔ si, Yerusalem ɔma zizi nɔ ɛpisa.

english_verse: So he quickly took some soldiers and officers and ran to where the crowd had gathered. As soon as the mob saw the commander and soldiers, they stopped beating Paul.
translation_verse: Ní ɔnyɔsasaa lɔ klɔ ɖii aʋa ɛ, agbani baguta ba ni baguta ba baka ba apɔni isu ya, lɛ bese eku ní ba kusi i, pɔ̀ gi banama gi bɛkpɛ ozi lo bɛmɔ baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye ni yɛ baguta ba ɛ, bɛmɔni Paulo kukusi.

english_verse: The army commander went over and arrested him and had him bound with two chains. Then he tried to find out who Paul was and what he had done.
translation_verse: Baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye, eti apɔni ba, lɛ evu ye e, lɛ akpɛ ɔsã si, banya yɛ ni akanya taʋa, lɛ eʋi ɔniliɛ elu gi onu u ani kidiliɛ gi abitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Part of the crowd shouted one thing, and part of them shouted something else. But they were making so much noise that the commander could not find out a thing. Then he ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress.
translation_verse: Lɛ linɔkpi le mɛ banɔatɔ bɛkpɛ ozi lo si nite, lɛ batɔ fɛ si nite, kɔ gi ɔ́tani anukwarɛ kunu kɔkpɛ ozi gagla lɔ loso e, akpɛ ɔsã si, bagbani yɛ atsini baguta ba kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: As they reached the steps, the crowd became so wild that the soldiers had to lift Paul up and carry him.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛna atrakpui ʋa aʋa ɛ, liba lɛ mɛ si, baguta ba befeke ye ní linɔkpi le agaglaboe la loso.

english_verse: The crowd followed and kept shouting, “Kill him! Kill him!” Paul Speaks to the Crowd
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si linɔkpi le likuni yɛ kude nì ozi si, “Mlaki tɔ kuye ye.” Paulo Ebu Isu ya onu

english_verse: When Paul was about to be taken into the fortress, he asked the commander, “Can I say something to you?” “How do you know Greek?” the commander asked.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi baguta ba si bɛkpɛ Paulo ní ligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, asi baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye si, “Kimɛ kudeonu tɔ misi wɔ liboetɔ.” Lɛ baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye eʋi ye si, “Wonu si Hela sɛ na a?”

english_verse: “No!” Paul replied. “I am a Jew from Tarsus, an important city in Cilicia. Please let me speak to the crowd.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo apani si, Mɔkɔ ɛ, Yudanɛ menu, dɔni Tarso ní Kilikia, ɔma gi ɛzi linyi, lɔ mɛ ɔnɛ menu pɔ̀ maki wɔ litukpo si kimɛ kudeonu tɔ mipani ki banima a.

english_verse: The commander told him he could speak, so Paul stood on the steps and motioned to the people. When they were quiet, he spoke to them in Aramaic:
translation_verse: Gi bɛki yɛ kudeo onu i. Paulo alɛɛ ní atrakpui ʋa aʋa, lɛ emuni yɛ awla la ní linɔkpi le aʋa, lɛ gi bɛgɔ tsriɖi i, asi ba ní Yudanɛ ma siya sɛ mɛ si,

english_verse: “My friends and leaders of our nation, listen as I explain what happened!”
translation_verse: “Mɛ baka ba ni mɛ benemi ba, mlata mɛ kutu tɔ mlaki tɔ mibu isu unu!”

english_verse: When the crowd heard Paul speak to them in Aramaic, they became even quieter. Then Paul said:
translation_verse: Gi benu gi ɛɛpani ki ba ni ba tutɔ ba siya sɛ mɛ ɛ, ba petee bɛdɔ zii sanɔ, lɛ Paulo atrɛ ní lɛ aʋa si,

english_verse: I am a Jew, born in the city of Tarsus in Cilicia. But I grew up here in Jerusalem where I was a student of Gamaliel and was taught to follow every single law of our ancestors. In fact, I was just as eager to obey God as any of you are today.
translation_verse: “Yuda ɔnɛ menu gi bɛpɔ ní Tarso ní Kilikia kesea mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ bɛgani mɛ ní Yerusalem gi meze ɔdɔkpasibi ní Gamaliɛl ese e. Behe me ní blɔ baka ba isã nɛ mɛ mɔmɔmɔ lɛ meze kpikpi aki Aya ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ petee mlalɛ omono e.

english_verse: I made trouble for everyone who followed the Lord's Way, and I even had some of them killed. I had others arrested and put in jail. I didn't care if they were men or women.
translation_verse: Meti banama gi beze Kristo yɛ baʋaziŋa ba kude e, lɛ maki si beye ba a. Mevu ba, badzɛ ni benyime akpɛ ligba.

english_verse: The high priest and all the council members can tell you this is true. They even gave me letters to the Jewish leaders in Damascus, so that I could arrest people there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished.
translation_verse: Kusɔliɔ ɔka ʋidi ye ni banɔtsitsia ba gi bizɛtsa ɛ, balɛ gi biasi mlɔ si anukwarɛ mɛɛpani i. Mazi kuplekpa adɔ ni baklɔ kí blɔ akɔanɛ ma ní Damasko, lɛ niklɔ mɛɛtrɛ si mizeevu banɔ ba ya tɔ mikpɛ ba bibɔ ʋɛ manɔ ní Yerusalem tɔ behe kutu aki ba.” Paulo Apani ní yɛ Ɔna mɛ Kukyikyi su

english_verse: One day about noon I was getting close to Damascus, when a bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
translation_verse: “Gi meze kudeo aʋa ŋwisi kemize inyɔ liɔfɔ ni tiʋa ɔza a, xe meeti ipɔni Damasko e, kyɔŋ koe kuklekle gagla atɔ kwidɔ lifunu ne kle klani mɛ.

english_verse: I fell to the ground and heard a voice asking, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me?”
translation_verse: Megleʋe adɔ kesea lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ gi eʋi me si, ‘Saulo, Saulo! Ege lɛ loso woeti me e?’

english_verse: “Who are you?” I answered. The Lord replied, “I am Jesus from Nazareth! I am the one you are so cruel to.”
translation_verse: Meʋi si, ‘Wɔ wonu nyaŋwɛ ɛ, Ɔkaɛ?’ “Ebu le onu si, ‘Mɛ menu Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ gi woeti i.’

english_verse: Those who were traveling with me saw the light, but did not hear the voice.
translation_verse: Banama gi mɛ ni ba kwizɛga ɛ, bɛmɔ kuklekleo pɔ̀ bénu eboa la gi ole lo ɛɛsi mɛ ɛ ese.

english_verse: I asked, “Lord, what do you want me to do?” Then he told me, “Get up and go to Damascus. When you get there, you will be told what to do.”
translation_verse: “Meʋi si, ‘Ege wɔsi mibitɛ ɛ, Ɔkaɛ?’ “Ɔkaɛ asi mɛ si, ‘Yɔ tɔ trɛ ní Damasko ɔma nɔ mɛ, biasi wɔ kidɔ kakaa liɛ gi Aya atrɔ aplɛ si wɔabitɛ ɛ.’

english_verse: The light had been so bright that I couldn't see. And the others had to lead me by the hand to Damascus.
translation_verse: Gi kuklekle gaglaa kwini mɛ bimbi ʋe loso e, banama gi blɔ ni ba kwizɛga ɛ, bevu mɛ ɔwla lɔ ɛgbani mɛ tsinɔ ní Damasko ɔma nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: In that city there was a man named Ananias, who faithfully obeyed the Law of Moses and was well liked by all the Jewish people living there.
translation_verse: “Lɛ onyime ɔtɔ gi eze ní ɔma lɔ klɔ mɛ ɛ, gi bizɛxwa si Anania a, eze ɔnɔ gi izesumu Aya ní kudeo onu u, xe izɛbitɛ ní blɔ isã nɛ aʋa a, lɛ Yudanɛ a ma petee gi beze ní ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ, bizɛkpɛ kubùbù ní yɛ su kpaŋu.

english_verse: He came to me and said, “Saul, my friend, you can now see again!” At once I could see.
translation_verse: Yɛ ɔba lɛ ní mɛ su lɛ asi mɛ si, ‘Onemi Saulo dra wɔ bimbi ʋe.’ Lipoe lɛ klɔ ʋa mekpese kumɔ lɛ mamɔ yɛ gi ɔlɛ ní mɛ su i.

english_verse: Then Ananias told me, “The God that our ancestors worshiped has chosen you to know what he wants done. He has chosen you to see the One Who Obeys God and to hear his voice.
translation_verse: “Apani dzɛ si, ‘Blɔ baka koko ba Aya yɛ akya wɔ si wɔate yɛ kupɛpɛɔ, tɔ mɔ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ, ani tɔ nu gi ɛɛpani ni yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ole lo o.

english_verse: You must tell everyone what you have seen and heard.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si wɔ wɔtraze yɛ Katsalia, lɛ wɔapani kí ɔnɔ kakaa kidiliɛ gi wɔmɔ ɔ, xe wonu i.’

english_verse: What are you waiting for? Get up! Be baptized, and wash away your sins by praying to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Lɛ abla ɛ, ege wɔepe e? ‘Yɔ, ki tɔ bakpɛ kuni aki wɔ, tɔ plo wɔ eboe zimizi na gani Aya kuxwaxwa mɛ.’ Bɛxwa Paulo Si Ɔpani Liboe Gbã nɛ ki Banama Gi Bénu Yudanɛ ɛ

english_verse: After this I returned to Jerusalem and went to the temple to pray. There I had a vision
translation_verse: “Gi medzi atrɛ ní Yerusalem i, xe mazɛɛxwa Aya ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, mamɔ kububasɛ

english_verse: of the Lord who said to me, “Hurry and leave Jerusalem! The people won't listen to what you say about me.”
translation_verse: ní kɔ mɛ ɛ, mamɔ Ɔkaɛ lɛ asi mɛ si ‘Kɔsi tɔ sɛ ní Yerusalem kyikya lɛ ese linu si banima niya ɛ, bátrazɛ wɔ liboe le ní mɛ su u.’

english_verse: I replied, “Lord, they know that in many of our synagogues I arrested and beat people who had faith in you.
translation_verse: “Mebu le onu si ‘Ɔkaɛ bete le si madzɛ Yudanɛ ma itsaklɔ lɛ mevu lɛ maki si bekusi banama gi bɛzi wɔ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Stephen was killed because he spoke for you, and I stood there and cheered them on. I even guarded the clothes of the men who murdered him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ lipoe lɛ gi bezeye wɔ katsalia Stefano e, mɛ ɔtutɔ malɛ niklɔ gi mazakpɛ banama gi beeye ye e, kigagla xe mazeedi banɔye ba ewu la aʋa a.’

english_verse: But the Lord told me to go, and he promised to send me far away to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ Ɔkaɛ asi mɛ si ‘Trɛ lɛ ese linu si matrakpɛ wɔ ɛtsisi ɔgblamadza kɔ ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma kɔ ɔ.’ ”

english_verse: The crowd listened until Paul said this. Then they started shouting, “Get rid of this man! He doesn't deserve to live.”
translation_verse: Banima bɛzɛta Paulo kutu kɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi apani liboe gɔgɔɛ lɛ ya ɛ; lɛ bekpese ozi lo kukpɛ tatata si “Mlasɛ ni yɛ niklɔ! Mleye ye! Ógyeno si oze ligbã!”

english_verse: They kept shouting. They waved their clothes around and threw dust into the air. Paul and the Roman Army Commander
translation_verse: Gi bɛzɛkpɛ ozi lo o, xe bɛzefu ba ewu la a, xe bɛzɛhali ovugbo lo etu ikpɛ liwɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: The Roman commander ordered Paul to be taken into the fortress and beaten with a whip. He did this to find out why the people were screaming at Paul.
translation_verse: Roma banɛ baguta ba ɔkaɛ aki si bedzini Paulo akpɛ ligba lɛ mɛ. Lɛ aki kyɛ si bafiaʋi yɛ litam nɛ tɔ beʋi ye kidiliɛ loso gi Yudanɛ ma bɛyɔ kedu ya ní yɛ su gbɛgbɛ te e.

english_verse: While the soldiers were tying Paul up to be beaten, he asked the officer standing there, “Is it legal to beat a Roman citizen before he has been tried in court?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi bɛnya yɛ sì bekpese yɛ bidɔ mɛ kufiaʋi i, Paulo eʋi baguta ba ɔka ɔtɔ gi alɛ ní klɔ si “Ɔsã nɔ ɛki kude si bafiaʋi Roma ɔnɛ gi bebu ye lipɔ si abitɛ liboe zimizi i, bidɔ na a?”

english_verse: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and said, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
translation_verse: Gi ɔkaɛ enu liboe le e, asɛ atrɛ ní ba ɔka ʋidi ye klɔ lɛ eʋi si “Ege mlɛbitɛ ɛ? onyime liɛ klɔ ʋa ɛ, onu Roma ɔnɛ.”

english_verse: The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” Paul answered.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso ɔka ʋidi ye atrɛ ní Paulo klɔ lɛ eʋi ye si, “Si mɛ anukwarɛɛ, Roma ɔnɛ wonu na a?” Ebu le onu si, “Ee Roma ɔnɛ menu.”

english_verse: The commander then said, “I paid a lot of money to become a Roman citizen.” But Paul replied, “I was born a Roman citizen.”
translation_verse: Ɔka ʋidi ye apani si, “Liʋɛ ʋidi matsa xe ableke medzi Roma ɔnɛ.” Paulo ebu le onu si, “Bɛpɔ mɛ Roma ɔnɛ.”

english_verse: The men who were about to beat and question Paul quickly backed off. And the commander himself was frightened when he realized that he had put a Roman citizen in chains. Paul Is Tried by the Council
translation_verse: Banama gi nya bɛtráʋi Paulo beʋiʋi ʋa ɛ, bedzi trɛ kede yɛ su kyɔŋ. Lɛ baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye elulu lipoe lɛ gi amɔ egyesi si Paulo onu Roma ɔnɛ ɛ, gi yɛ yɛ akpɛ bibɔ ʋɛ mɛ ɛ. Bɛmani Saulo ní Otsixwaklɔ

english_verse: The next day the commander wanted to know the real reason why the Jewish leaders had brought charges against Paul. So he had Paul's chains removed, and he ordered the chief priests and the whole council to meet. Then he had Paul led in and made him stand in front of them.
translation_verse: Baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye apɛ si yi nu lɛ mɛ tututu kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso Yudanɛ ma bɛyɔ kedu ní Paulo su i. Lɛ loso ní keletitia mɛ ɛ, aki bebu Paulo bibɔ ʋɛ mɛ lɛ aki si Kusumu yo baka ba ni Yudanɛ ma betsixwa ba petee bɛtsa a, lɛ bɛgbani Paulo e tsinɔ xe ɔzɛ ɔlɛɛ ni ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Paul looked straight at the council members and said, “My friends, to this day I have served God with a clear conscience!”
translation_verse: Paulo efeke aŋu na edi betsixwa ba lɛ apani si i, “Mɛ akɔanɛ Israɛlanɛ ma, mawa mɛ lixwɛ nɛ aki Aya gi mɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛdra ní kiliɛ gi meze ní Aya aŋu mɛ ga kɔna ni abla ɛ su.”

english_verse: Then Ananias the high priest ordered the men standing beside Paul to hit him on the mouth.
translation_verse: Kusɔliɔ ɔka ʋidi ye Anania asi banama gi bɛlɛɛ ní Paulo su si, basa yɛ bidɔ ní onugu lo aʋa a.

english_verse: Paul turned to the high priest and said, “You whitewashed wall! God will hit you. You sit there to judge me by the Law of Moses. But at the same time you order men to break the Law by hitting me.”
translation_verse: Paulo asi ye si, “Aya aasa wɔ bidɔ godoo wɔ ligli gi besi akalo ye kɔ ɛ. Wodi niklɔ ɛxwa litsi nì mɛ ní ɔsã nɔ onu pɔ̀ wɔɛta ɔsã nɔ ese gi wɔki ɔsã si basa mɛ bidɔ ɛ!”

english_verse: The men standing beside Paul asked, “Don't you know you are insulting God's high priest?”
translation_verse: Banama gi beti pɔni Paulo e, bɛsi yɛ si, “Aya yɛ kusɔliɔ Ɔka ʋidi ye wɔekpini te e?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “Oh! I didn't know he was the high priest. The Scriptures do tell us not to speak evil about a leader of our people.”
translation_verse: Paulo ebu le onu si, “Mɛ akɔanɛ Israɛlanɛ ma, Méte si yɛ onu kusɔliɔ ɔka ʋidi ye e, kuŋwlimi dradra kwipani si, ‘wukipani liboe zimizi ni ɔnɔ gi ɛɛŋa mlɔ akɔanɛ ma aʋa su u.’ ”
