english_verse: When Paul saw that some of the council members were Sadducees and others were Pharisees, he shouted, “My friends, I am a Pharisee and the son of a Pharisee. I am on trial simply because I believe that the dead will be raised to life.”
translation_verse: Gi Paulo amɔ si betsixwa ba mɛ batɔ benu Saduki banɛ ɛ, xe batɔ kyɛ benu Farisianɛ loso e, akpɛ ozi lo gagla ní litsi le me si, “Blɔ akɔanɛ Israɛlanɛ ma, menu Farisiɔnɛ, lɛ menu Farisianɛ ma obi. Bɛɛxwa litsi nì mɛ ni ya kɔkpɛ mɛ kudeonudidi si bekpo ʋa biayɔ lɛ biadzi ligbã a.”

english_verse: As soon as Paul said this, the Pharisees and the Sadducees got into a big argument, and the council members started taking sides.
translation_verse: Gi anya apani nite ko e, kuboehe kwiku ni Farisianɛ ma ni Saduki banɛ ma kusi. lɛ kutsatsaa kwilɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Sadducees do not believe in angels or spirits or that the dead will rise to life. But the Pharisees believe in all of these,
translation_verse: Saduki banɛ ma bizeedo si banima bátrayɔ ɛdɔni bekpo ba mɛ ɛ, lɛ bázɛ lɛ kyɛ si Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ balɛ ɛ, lɛ ifuɛfuɛ nɛ kyɛ ilɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ farisianɛ ma kɔ bɛzi bɛ petee aʋa ɛɛŋa a.

english_verse: and so there was a lot of shouting. Some of the teachers of the Law of Moses were Pharisees. Finally, they became angry and said, “We don't find anything wrong with this man. Maybe a spirit or an angel really did speak to him.”
translation_verse: Ozi lo ɛfiʋi kɔtɔɔ ní klɔ, ɔsã nɔ badɔbasɛ batɔ gi bali ní Farisianɛ ma ɔha lɔ mɛ ɛ, bɛyɔ ɛlɛ, lɛ behe eboe gagla si: “Kumɔ kididɔ gi kizizi ni onyime liɛ ya su u! Nyaŋwɛ ete xe Ɔfuɛfuɛ loo Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ɔtɔ anya egu kunugu aki yɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: The argument became fierce, and the commander was afraid that Paul would be pulled apart. So he ordered the soldiers to go in and rescue Paul. Then they took him back into the fortress.
translation_verse: Kuboeheo kwize gagla nite me si lifu liku baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye si biakyi Paulo ɔgbagbee. Lɛ loso asi yɛ baguta ba si bagbani Paulo edono ní banima kusi atsinɔ ni baguta ba kepea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: That night the Lord stood beside Paul and said, “Don't worry! Just as you have told others about me in Jerusalem, you must also tell about me in Rome.” A Plot To Kill Paul
translation_verse: Liwe lɛ klɔ elesi la ɛ, Ɔkaɛ alɛ ní Paulo su lɛ asi yɛ si, “Ɔna oze wɔ lipo. Kiliɛ gi wɔŋa katsalia ɔki mɛ ní Yerusalem i, nite ke wɔaŋa kimɛ ni Roma kyɛ.” Bɛbitɛ kunyanya Si Biaye Paulo

english_verse: Then some of them went to the chief priests and the nation's leaders and said, “We have promised God that we would not eat a thing until we have killed Paul.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛtrɛ ní Kusumu yo baka ba ni banɔtsitsi ba klɔ lɛ bɛsi ba si, “kwinya onugu ni kakaplɛ si kutraŋa kididɔ kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ gi kwiye Paulo e.

english_verse: You and everyone in the council must go to the commander and pretend that you want to find out more about the charges against Paul. Ask for him to be brought before your court. Meanwhile, we will be waiting to kill him before he gets there.”
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mlɔ ni betsixwa ba ɛ, mlakpɛ banɔ ní baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye klɔ tɔ ba si yɛ si, ɔgbani Paulo ɔmani mlɔ; mlɛpɛ si mlenu eboe atɔ mɛ sanɔ ɛdɔni yɛ kɔ. Pɔ̀ blɔ kɔ kwiaze ní kublabla mɛ si kwiaye ye xe aba eku ní ya a.”

english_verse: When Paul's nephew heard about the plot, he went to the fortress and told Paul about it.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo yenemidzɛ Yebi enu liboe le lɛ atrɛ ní baguta ba kepea mɛ ɛ, xe azɛɛsi Paulo e.

english_verse: So Paul said to one of the army officers, “Take this young man to the commander. He has something to tell him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo axwa baguta ba ɔka ɔtɔ lɛ asi yɛ si “gbani ɔyasɔʋi ɔ lɔ ya tsini baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye liboetɔ lilɛ yɛ awla gi si yisi yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The officer took him to the commander and said, “The prisoner named Paul asked me to bring this young man to you, because he has something to tell you.”
translation_verse: Baguta ba ɔkaɛ agbani yɛ atsini ɔka ʋidi ye lɛ asi yɛ si, “Paulo gi ɔkpasi ligba lɛ ɛ, axwa mɛ lɛ asi mɛ si, migbani ɔyasɔʋi ɔ lɔ ya mani wɔ, lɛ ese linu si liboetɔ lilɛ ní lɔ awla gi si lɔɛsi wɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: The commander took the young man aside and asked him in private, “What do you want to tell me?”
translation_verse: Baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye evu yɛ kawlakpɔa lɛ ebu ye kuka a, xe eʋi ye si i, “Liboe woli lilɛ gi wɔ si wusi mɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: He answered, “Some men are planning to ask you to bring Paul down to the Jewish council tomorrow. They will claim they want to find out more about him.
translation_verse: Ebu le onu si “Yudanɛ ma bɛtrɔ si biasi wɔ si gbani Paulo mani otsixwa klɔ kivo tɔ beʋi ye liboetɔ mɛ ɛdɔni yɛ kɔ.

english_verse: The commander sent the young man away after saying to him, “Don't let anyone know you told me this.” Paul Is Sent to Felix the Governor
translation_verse: Baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye asi yɛ si “Kúsi ɔnɔnɔ si wodo liboe lɛ ya ɔsi mɛ ɛ.” Lɛ akpɛ ɔyasɔʋi lɔ kude xe ɛsɛ ɛ. Bɛtsisi Paulo Ní Okusi ye Felikisi Klɔ

english_verse: Get a horse ready for Paul and make sure he gets safely through to Felix the governor.”
translation_verse: Mlapɛ isɔ batɔ aki Paulo gi aakpɛ ɛ, tɔ mledi le aʋa si eku Kesea Ɔʋadi ye Felikisi iwla mɛ ɛ liboeboe mɔbamɔba.”

english_verse: Greetings from Claudius Lysias to the Honorable Governor Felix:
translation_verse: Kɛ dɔ ní Claudio Lisias kɔ ɛki Kesea Ɔʋadi ye Obùbùnɛ Felikisi: Mapani wɔ.

english_verse: Some Jews grabbed this man and were about to kill him. But when I found out that he was a Roman citizen, I took some soldiers and rescued him.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ma bevu onyime liɛ gi bɛpɛ si beye ye e. Menu si onu Roma ɔnɛ lɛ matsini mɛ baguta ba xe kwizi yɛ ní ba awla ɛ.

english_verse: I wanted to find out what they had against him. So I brought him before their council
translation_verse: Mapɛ si mite kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso beehe eboe la ni yɛ ɛ, lɛ loso magbani yɛ atsini ba otsixwa klɔ.

english_verse: and learned that the charges concern only their religious laws. This man isn't guilty of anything for which he should die or even be put in jail.
translation_verse: Mamɔ egyesi si ɔ́bitɛ kididɔ gi kizia si otse e, loo bakpɛ yɛ ligba; kunutsatsa ní yɛ su kunu ba tutɔ ba ɔsã nɔ su eboe.

english_verse: As soon as I learned that there was a plot against him, I sent him to you and told their leaders to bring charges against him in your court.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bɛki mete si bɛtrɔ si beye ye e, matrɔ lɛ si maatsisi yɛ ní wɔ klɔ. Masi banama gi bɛyɔ kedu ya ní yɛ su si, bakɔ liboe le amani wɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The soldiers obeyed the commander's orders, and that same night they took Paul to the city of Antipatris.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso baguta ba bɛbitɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa. lɛ bɛgbani Paulo esɛnɔ elesi la a, xe bezeku Antipatri i.

english_verse: The next day the foot soldiers returned to the fortress and let the soldiers on horseback take him the rest of the way.
translation_verse: Kele titia mɛ ɛ, bagutaa ba gi bɛga ikli lɛ ɛ, bekyi dzi ba kepea mɛ, lɛ bɛyurɔ báklɔ gi bɛzɛ kpɛ isɔ ʋa a si ba gbani yɛ atsinɔ ní Kaisarea ɛ.

english_verse: When they came to Caesarea, they gave the letter to the governor and handed Paul over to him.
translation_verse: Gi beku Kaisarea ɛ, bɛkɔ keplekpa ɛki Kesea ɔʋadi ye lɛ bɛkɔ Paulo ɛkpɛ ní yɛ awla ɛ.

english_verse: The governor read the letter. Then he asked Paul and found out that he was from Cilicia.
translation_verse: Kesea ɔʋadi ye akla keplekpaa, lɛ eʋi Paulo si nifɔ ádɔ na a? Gi enu si ádɔ ní Kilikia ɛ,

english_verse: The governor said, “I will listen to your case as soon as the people come to bring their charges against you.” After saying this, he gave orders for Paul to be kept as a prisoner in Herod's palace.
translation_verse: asi yɛ si, “Maanu wɔ liboe le xegi banama gi bɛkɔ liboe ní wɔsu bɛ ba ɛ.” Lɛ aki ɔsã si bakpɛ Paulo ní ligba tɔ bazeepe pe ye ní Kepea lia gi Herodes atsa ɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Five days later Ananias the high priest, together with some of their leaders and a lawyer named Tertullus, went to the governor to present their case against Paul.
translation_verse: Ewe tetsu kede e, Kusumu yo ɔka ʋidi ye Anania, eple atrɛ ní Kaisarea. Ɔha lɔ banɔ tsitsi batɔ ni otsixwa ɔtɔ gi bizɛxwa si Tertulo o, ba bɛgbani yɛ tsinɔ. Bɛlɛɛ ní kesea ɔʋadi ye Felikisi aŋu mɛ lɛ bɛkɔ liboe ní Paulo su i.

english_verse: So Paul was called in, and Tertullus stated the case against him: Honorable Felix, you have brought our people a long period of peace, and because of your concern our nation is much better off.
translation_verse: Gi bɛxwa Paulo ɛmanɔ ɛ, Tertulo akɔ liboe Paulo su si; “Ɔka ʋidi ye Felikisi; Wɔ kukusi ŋaŋaa kwimani blɔ kusuyɔ lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ lɛ ya, lɛ wɔ kuboeteo kwiki si eboe glegle ekyi gi ɛki kupɛ̀pɛ ni blɔ kesea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All of us are always grateful for what you have done.
translation_verse: Kwizi lɛ ni iwla tiʋa ní ɔkɔ kakaa ni lipoe kakaa lɛ kwido wɔ ɖase popopo ní lɛ litukpo.

english_verse: I don't want to bother you, but please be patient with us and listen to me for just a few minutes.
translation_verse: Mápɛ si mizizi wɔ lipoe e, lɛ loso mɛɛki wɔ litukpo si ta blɔ liboe le kutu ní onyime liɛ ya su.

english_verse: This man has been found to be a real pest and troublemaker for our people all over the world. He is also a leader of a group called Nazarenes.
translation_verse: “Kwimɔ si onyime liɛ ya ɛ, onu ɔnɔ gi ɛɛkpɛ kunugu u, gi izɛɛpisa banɔ ɔ, lɛ izɛɛki si Yudanɛ ma bizɛyɔ kedu ní kelea petee me e. Yɛ onu Nazarene ɔha lɔ onu ozezee.

english_verse: When he tried to disgrace the temple, we arrested him. If you question him, you will find out for yourself that our charges are true.
translation_verse: Lisia aki ɔsã si banama gi liboetɔ lilɛ ní ba awla ní yɛ su i, baba wɔ klɔ. Xe woʋi onyime liɛ ya igbè e, wɔ tutɔ wɔamɔ eboa la gi la kwikɔ ní yɛ su i.”

english_verse: The Jewish crowd spoke up and agreed with what Tertullus had said. Paul Defends Himself
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ma kyɛ bɛtrɔ ní yɛ liboe le aʋa si lilɛ ní lɛ mɛ. Paulo Aŋwlɛ Isu Litukpo ní Felikisi aŋu mɛ

english_verse: The governor motioned for Paul to speak, and he began: I know that you have judged the people of our nation for many years, and I am glad to defend myself in your court.
translation_verse: Gi Kesea ɔʋadi ye abasi Paulo awla la si ɔpani i, Paulo apani si: “Mete si wonu otsixwa ni kesea lia aʋa agɔ glegle la ya, lɛ loso linu mɛ isami si miŋwlɛ isu litukpo ní wɔ aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: It was no more than twelve days ago that I went to worship in Jerusalem. You can find this out easily enough.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi ewe liɔfɔ ni taʋa ko la ya gi matrɛ ní Yerusalem si mizeesumu Aya a. Banɔ biatani likla kutrɔ lɛ aʋa kiwɔ ɔ,

english_verse: Never once did the Jews find me arguing with anyone in the temple. I didn't cause trouble in the synagogues or in the city itself.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛkɔ liboe ní mɛ su i, bámɔ si mɛ ni ɔnɔnɔ kwihe eboe ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, loo si mɛɛpisa ɔnɔha si ɔyɔ kedu ní ɔnɔnɔ su u, ní Yudanɛ ma ɔtsakɔ loo ní ɔkɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ní ɔma ʋidi no me.

english_verse: There is no way that they can prove these charges they are now bringing against me.
translation_verse: Lɛ bátratani likla kutrɔ ní eboa la gi la bɛkɔ ní mɛ su i aʋa.

english_verse: I admit that their leaders think the Lord's Way which I follow is based on wrong beliefs. But I still worship the same God that my ancestors worshiped. And I believe everything written in the Law of Moses and in the Prophets.
translation_verse: Mazi lɛ ní wɔ aŋu mɛ si meesumu Aya blɔ bakatsi ba Aya gani kude uliɔ gi bɛɛxwa si baʋaziŋa ba kudeo o, gi bɛɛsi sì kupɛ ɛ aʋa. Pɔ̀ mɛ fɛ mazi kidɔ kakaa a gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Mose ɔsã nɔ, ani beboedoplɛ ba kuplekpaa mɛ aʋa aŋa a.

english_verse: I am just as sure as these people are that God will raise from death everyone, good or evil.
translation_verse: Kudeonu didi uliɔ gi kuli ní mɛ awla ɛ, kɔ ke kuli ní banɔaya fɛ ba awla, si ɔnɔ kakaa; banɔ gbã ni banɔ zimizi ba pɔ́ biayɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And because I am sure, I try my best to have a clear conscience in whatever I do for God or for people.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mizeekeni idi lipoe kakaa si tɔ mikíta kese ní Aya nì ɔnɔ gbagbe aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After being away for several years, I returned here to bring gifts for the poor people of my nation and to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: “Gi masɛ ní Yerusalem agɔ glegle onu loso e, medzi atrɛ níklɔ ni kibɔ kí mɛ akɔanɛ ma ani tɔ mizɛɛki Aya bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi i.

english_verse: This is what I was doing when I was found going through a ceremony in the temple. I wasn't with a crowd, and there was no uproar.
translation_verse: Meblo ni isu ya kuploploo ɔkɔ lɔ xe bɛmɔ mɛ ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ. Ɔnɔha ɔtɔtɔ óze pɔni mɛ ɛ, lɛ ozi loo avitɛ kyɛ bíze niklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Some Jews from Asia were there at that time, and if they have anything to say against me, they should be here now.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yudanɛ atɔ gi bɛdɔ Asia ɛ, beze níklɔ, nya lilɛ si beze ní ya tɔ bakɔ ba kunutsatsa akí wɔ ba tutɔ, xe gi liboetɔ lilɛ ní ba awla ni mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Or ask the ones who are here. They can tell you that they didn't find me guilty of anything when I was tried by their own council.
translation_verse: Loo banama gi bali ní ya abla ɛ, bapani ní lipɔɛ lɛ gi maŋa lipoe lɛ gi malɛ ní Yudanɛ ma betsixwa ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The only charge they can bring against me is what I shouted out in court, when I said, “I am on trial today because I believe that the dead will be raised to life!”
translation_verse: Sani liboe tile gi me muni mɛ ole lo o, xe mapani i, gi malɛ ní ba aŋu mɛ ɛ, lɛ linu si: ‘mlɛɛxwa litsi ni mɛ omono kɔkpɛ mɛ kuziŋaa si bekpo ba biayɔ gi biadzi ligbã́ a.’ ”

english_verse: Felix knew a lot about the Lord's Way. But he brought the trial to an end and said, “I will make my decision after Lysias the commander arrives.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Felikisi gi ete baʋaziŋaa ba kudeo kpaŋu i, apani si “Xegi Lisia, baguta ba ɔka ʋidi ye aba ɛ, maatsa mlɔ liboe le e.” Lɛ aki si bɛyɔ ɛ.

english_verse: He then ordered the army officer to keep Paul under guard, but not to lock him up or to stop his friends from helping him. Paul Is Kept under Guard
translation_verse: Akpɛ ɔsã aki baguta ba ɔkaɛ gi eepe ye e, si odzini ye ɔkpɛ ligba lɛ, pɔ̀ tɔ ɔki kudeo onu si yɛ baʋianɛ ba biatani yɛ baziazia ʋa kubitɛ ɔki yɛ. Paulo ebu yɛ liboe le ese aki Felikisi ni Drusila

english_verse: Several days later Felix and his wife Drusilla, who was Jewish, went to the place where Paul was kept under guard. They sent for Paul and listened while he spoke to them about having faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Ewe atɔ kede e, Felikisi ni yedze Drusila gi onu Yudanɛ e, bɛba lɛ bɛkpɛ banɔ ní Paulo ese tɔ benu yɛ eboa la gi ɛɛpani ní kuziŋa ní Kristo Yesu su i.

english_verse: But Felix was frightened when Paul started talking to them about doing right, about self-control, and about the coming judgment. So he said to Paul, “That's enough for now. You may go. But when I have time I will send for you.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Paulo azɛɛpani ní ligbã́ gbã kuzeze, isu aʋa kuŋaŋa ni litsixwaxwa ɛnɛ gi litrába su i, Lifu liku Felikisi lɛ asi yɛ si, “Wɔatani kusɛ abla, xegi mamɔ lipoe lɛ kede e, maxwa wɔ dzɛ.”

english_verse: After this, Felix often sent for Paul and talked with him, because he hoped that Paul would offer him a bribe.
translation_verse: Gi azeegyo le edi si Paulo aaki yɛ kibɔ kitɔ ɛ, azaaxwa yɛ lɛ yɛ ni yɛ bezegu kunugu yo lipoe me ni lipoe me e.

english_verse: Two years later Porcius Festus became governor in place of Felix. But since Felix wanted to do the Jewish leaders a favor, he kept Paul in jail.
translation_verse: Agi agɔ taʋa ɛza ɛ, bebu Felikisi isɛnɔ lɛ bɛmani Pɔrsus Festo ní yɛ linyi ne me e. Ɔkpɛ gi Felikisi apɛ si yipɛ lipo me ɛki Yudanɛ ma loso e, aki si Paulo awɔ ligba lɛ.

english_verse: Three days after Festus had become governor, he went from Caesarea to Jerusalem.
translation_verse: Ni ewetata kede gi Festo aba eku yɛ kesea mɛ ɛ, ádɔ Kaisarea lɛ emu atrɛ ní Yerusalem i

english_verse: There the chief priests and some Jewish leaders told him about their charges against Paul. They also asked Festus
translation_verse: niklɔ kusumu yo baka ʋidi ba ni Yudanɛ ma ɔtɔ nɔ bezeze ba bɛzɛtsà Paulo onu. Bɛki Festo litukpo

english_verse: if he would be willing to bring Paul to Jerusalem. They begged him to do this because they were planning to attack and kill Paul on the way.
translation_verse: si ɔpɛ lipo me ɔki ba tɔ bamani Paulo ní Yerusalem, lɛ ese linu si bɛbitɛ kunyanya si biaye Paulo ní kudeo aʋa a.

english_verse: But Festus told them, “Paul will be kept in Caesarea, and I am soon going there myself.
translation_verse: Festo ebu le onu si, “Bɛkpɛ Paulo ní ligba ní Kaisarea, lɛ mɛ ɔtutɔ matradzi itrɛ niklɔ kpékpe e.

english_verse: If he has done anything wrong, let your leaders go with me and bring charges against him there.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlaki tɔ mlɔ ɔtɔ nɔ bezeze ba bekuni mɛ kude atrɛ ní Kaisarea atɔ bazɛtsà Paulo onu, xe kesetata eboe atɔ alɛ ní mlɔ awla ní yɛ su i.”

english_verse: Festus stayed in Jerusalem for eight or ten more days before going to Caesarea. Then the next day he took his place as judge and had Paul brought into court.
translation_verse: Festo eze ní ba kusi ewe ɔgɔtaʋa kɔtsini liɔfɔ, lɛ kede e edzi eple atrɛ ní Kaisarea. Kele titia mɛ ɛ, Festo azeeze ní yɛ litsixwagbo le aʋa lɛ aki ɔsã si bagbani Paulo amanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: As soon as Paul came in, the leaders from Jerusalem crowded around him and said he was guilty of many serious crimes. But they could not prove anything.
translation_verse: Agi Paulo aba eku i, Yudanɛ a ma gi bɛdɔ Yerusalem ba ɛ, bazɛlɛɛ klani yɛ lɛ bekpese eboe gagla la kukɔ ní yɛ su i, gi átani yɛ kuvu u.

english_verse: Then Paul spoke in his own defense, “I have not broken the Law of my people. And I have not done anything against either the temple or the Emperor.”
translation_verse: Paulo aŋwlɛ isu ya litukpo si, “Mábitɛ kididɔ ɔ, gi kiyɔ kedu ní Yudanɛ ma Ɔsã nɔ su loo ní Lisɔligba lɛ su loo ni Ɔʋaŋaɛ Kaisaro su u.”

english_verse: Festus wanted to please the leaders. So he asked Paul, “Are you willing to go to Jerusalem and be tried by me on these charges?”
translation_verse: Gi Festo apɛ si yiku Yudanɛ ma liŋu i, lɛ loso e eʋi Paulo si, “Wɔapɛ si wutrɛ ni Yerusalem atɔ mizɛɛxwa litsi ni wɔ ní eboa la gi bɛkɔ ní wɔ su ní mɛ aŋu mɛ na a?”

english_verse: Paul replied, “I am on trial in the Emperor's court, and that's where I should be tried. You know very well I have not done anything to harm the Jewish nation.
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo asi Festo si, “Owo malɛɛ ní Kaisaro ɔtutɔ yɛ otsixwa klɔ, niklɔ gi biaxwa litsi ni me e. Maʋíta Yudanɛ ma ese e, kiliɛ gi wɔ tutɔ wote mɔmɔmɔ ɛ.

english_verse: If I had done something deserving death, I would not ask to escape the death penalty. But I am not guilty of any of these crimes, and no one has the right to hand me over to these people. I now ask to be tried by the Emperor himself.”
translation_verse: Máta ɔsã nɔ ese e, pɔ̀ xe mábitɛ kiditɔ ɔ, gi linu si mitse e, méʋiʋi si mise ní bɛ su. Pɔ̀ xe anukwarɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́ma ní eboa la gi bɛkɔ ní mɛ su mɛ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani mɛ kukɔ ɔki ba a. Meʋi si Ɔʋaŋaɛ Kaisaro ɔtutɔ ɔxwa litsi ni me.”

english_verse: After Festus had talked this over with members of his council, he told Paul, “You have asked to be tried by the Emperor, and to the Emperor you will go!” Paul Speaks to Agrippa and Bernice
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Festo adzɛ agyina ni yɛ axɔtɔ ba pɔ̀ ɛ, apani si, “Agi wɔkɔ wɔ eboe la kpɛ ní Kaisaro iwla lɛ mɛ ɛ, kɔ Kaisaro klɔ ko wɔatrɛ.” Bɛ gbani Paulo mani Okusi ye Agripa ani Bernike aŋu mɛ

english_verse: A few days later King Agrippa and Bernice came to Caesarea to visit Festus.
translation_verse: Ní ewe atɔ kede e, Okusi ye Agripa ani Bernike bɛba Kaisarea si bɛɛpani Festo tɔ bɛɛkpɛ yɛ wolaa.

english_verse: They had been there for several days, when Festus told the king about the charges against Paul. He said: Felix left a man here in jail,
translation_verse: Agi bɛtráze níklɔ ewe glegle onu i, Festo apani ní Paulo su aki Okusi ye Agripa si, “Onyime ɔtɔ ɔlɛ ní ya gi Felikisi ayurɔ akpɛ ligba lɛ ɛ;

english_verse: and when I went to Jerusalem, the chief priests and the Jewish leaders came and asked me to find him guilty.
translation_verse: lɛ agi madzi Yerusalem i, Yudanɛ ma kusumu yo baka ʋidi ba ni banɔ tsitsi ba bɛtsà yɛ onu, lɛ beʋi si mibu ye lipɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I told them it isn't the Roman custom to hand a man over to people who are bringing charges against him. He must first have the chance to meet them face to face and to defend himself against their charges.
translation_verse: “Pɔ̀ masi ba si, blɔ Roma banɛ ma kɔ ɛ, líma blɔ ɔsã nɔ onu si kuyurɔ ɔnɔ ɔkɔ si ata kese e, xe ɔ́tsani banama gi bɛkɔ liboe ní yɛ su aŋu mɛ ni aŋu mɛ ɛ, tɔ ɔmɔ lipoe atɔ obu isu ya onu ní eboa la gi bɛkɔ ni yɛ su i su.

english_verse: So when they came here with me, I wasted no time. On the very next day I took my place on the judge's bench and ordered him to be brought in.
translation_verse: Agi bɛba ni ya ɛ, mézizi lipoe lɛ ní kele titia mɛ ɛ, meze ní mɛ litsixwagbo le aʋa lɛ makpɛ banɔ si bagbani yɛ amanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But when the men stood up to make their charges against him, they did not accuse him of any of the crimes that I thought they would.
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛyɔ kedu ya ní yɛ su i, bɛyɔ pɔ̀ ɛ, bátsa yɛ onu ni eboe zimizi gi mebu si biapani i.

english_verse: Instead, they argued with him about some of their beliefs and about a dead man named Jesus, who Paul said was alive.
translation_verse: Eboa la gi bɛmanɔ ko eze eboe hehe ní yɛ ní ba kusumu yo su ani ɔka ɔtɔ gi bɛxwa si Yesu u, gi etse koko o, pɔ̀ xe Paulo kɔ azɛ lɛ si ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ su u.

english_verse: Since I did not know how to find out the truth about all this, I asked Paul if he would be willing to go to Jerusalem and be put on trial there.
translation_verse: Agi méte kiliɛ gi maaga ní liboe zizi nɛ ya su i, meʋi Paulo xe aapɛ si yitrɛ ɛ, ní Yerusalem atɔ bazɛxwa litsi ni yɛ ní eboa la gi bɛkɔ ɛtsa yɛ onu su u.

english_verse: But Paul asked to be kept in jail until the Emperor could decide his case. So I ordered him to be kept here until I could send him to the Emperor.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo si yɛ atrɛ ní Kaisaro klɔ ɔ, ɔlɛ eʋi si bakɔ yɛ akpɛ ní banɔpe ba iwla mɛ ɛ ani tɔ baki Kaisaro ɔpani ní yɛ liboe le su. Lɛ maki ɔsã si bakpɛ yɛ awla ki banɔpe ba kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi maatani yɛ kutsisi Kaisaro klɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: Then Agrippa said to Festus, “I would also like to hear what this man has to say.” Festus answered, “You can hear him tomorrow.”
translation_verse: Agripa asi Festo si, “Maapɛ si mɛ ɔtutɔ minu ɔniliɛ klɔ yɛ kunugu yo.” Lɛ Festo ebu le onu si, “Wɔanu yɛ onu kivo.” Paulo alɛɛ ni Agripa Aŋu mɛ

english_verse: The next day Agrippa and Bernice made a big show as they came into the meeting room. High ranking army officers and leading citizens of the town were also there. Festus then ordered Paul to be brought in
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, Agripa ni Bernike bɛba ní sikusi me, lɛ banima bɛkpɛ kubùbù glegle ní basu u, gi beetiku ní ɔtsaklɔ ɛ, baguta ba baka ba kɔpɔni ɔma nɔ banɔ ʋidi ba beze ni ba kude. Festo aki ɔsã, lɛ bɛzɛmani Paulo e.

english_verse: and said: King Agrippa and other guests, look at this man! Every Jew from Jerusalem and Caesarea has come to me, demanding for him to be put to death.
translation_verse: Festo apani si, “Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa ani banama pɔ́ gi bɛtsa ní ya ni blɔ ɔ: Dani mlɛmɔ ɔniliɛ ya, gi yɛ Yudanɛ ma pɔ́ ɔ gi bali ní ya ni Yerusalem i bɛtsà yɛ onu kí mɛ ɛ. Bɛkpɛ lɛ ozi si líma si oze ligbã́ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I have not found him guilty of any crime deserving death. But because he has asked to be judged by the Emperor, I have decided to send him to Rome.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mámɔ kesetata katɔtɔ ní yɛ su u, gi kiaki si biatsa kutsépia ɔki yɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ gi yɛ ɔtutɔ eʋi si batsini yɛ ní Kaisaro klɔ ɛ, mazi lɛ si maatsisi yɛ.

english_verse: I have to write some facts about this man to the Emperor. So I have brought him before all of you, but especially before you, King Agrippa. After we have talked about his case, I will then have something to write.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mámɔ liboeboe e, gi maaŋwlimi ní yɛ su tsisi blɔ Okusi ʋidi ye Kaisaro o. Lɛ loso mamani yɛ ní mlɔ petee aŋu mɛ ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa atɔ xe kwipami yɛ liboe le me pɔ́ ɛ, maamɔ liboetɔ gi maaŋwlimi i.

english_verse: It makes no sense to send a prisoner to the Emperor without stating the charges against him.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mamɔ si bítratrɔ mɛ aʋa si mikɔ ɔnɔ gi bɛkpɛ ligba tsisi yɛ ɛ, xegi máŋwlimi kidiliɛ gi kɛ abitɛ ɛ, xe kɛ loso bevu ye kpɛ ligba lɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Paul, “You may now speak for yourself.” Paul stretched out his hand and said:
translation_verse: Agripa asi Paulo si, “Kudeonu kwidra ɛki wɔ si bu isu ya onu.” Lɛ loso Paulo edo ye ɖase lɛ ekpese isu ya onu kubu si:

english_verse: King Agrippa, I am glad for this chance to defend myself before you today on all these charges my own people have brought against me.
translation_verse: “Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa! Mebu isu ya si mɛ litukpo lipɛ si omete e, matrabu isu ya onu ní wɔ aŋu mɛ ní liboe kakaa gi Yudanɛ ma bɛkɔ ní mɛ su i su.

english_verse: You know a lot about our religious customs and the beliefs that divide us. So I ask you to listen patiently to me.
translation_verse: Li veve le linu si wote Yudanɛ ma ikɔ lɛ petee ni ba eboe hehe la ni ba kiboebitɛɛ. Meeʋi si ta mɛ eboe la kutu ni litɔ plɛplɛ kese.

english_verse: All the Jews have known me since I was a child. They know what kind of life I have lived in my own country and in Jerusalem. And if they were willing, they could tell you I was a Pharisee, a member of a group that is stricter than any other.
translation_verse: Bete lipoe kakaa, xe bɛpɛ si bɛpani ní mɛ su i, si dɔni okpese kɔ ke e, meze ɔnɔ gi mevu kusumu yo ikɔ lɛ mɛ ɖii ŋwisi Farisiɔnɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now I am on trial because I believe the promise God made to our people long ago.
translation_verse: Lɛ abla ɛ, malɛɛ ní ya gi bɛxwa litsi ni me e, kɔkpɛ mɛ kudeonudidi ní kakaplɛa lia gi Aya aka aplɛ aki blɔ baka ba a,

english_verse: Day and night our twelve tribes have earnestly served God, waiting for his promised blessings. King Agrippa, because of this hope, some of our leaders have brought charges against me.
translation_verse: kakaplɛa lia ɖii gi blɔ amade liɔfɔ ni taʋa la beedi kudeonu ikɔ si biamɔ, gi beesumu Aya elesi ni kemize e. Lɛ kɔkpɛ kudeonudidi uliɔ ya loso e, Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa, Yudanɛ ma beebu me lipɔ!

english_verse: Why should any of you doubt that God raises the dead to life?
translation_verse: Kitɛ lilɛ gagla ɛki mlɔ banama gi mlalɛ ni ya a si mlazi ŋa si Aya izɛyɔ ni bekpo ʋa na a?

english_verse: I once thought that I should do everything I could to oppose Jesus from Nazareth.
translation_verse: “Mɛ ɔtutɔ mebu le si lilɛ si mibitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi maatanɔ ɔ, gi kiyɔ kedu ní Yesu Nazaret ɔnɛɛ linyi ne su u.

english_verse: I did this first in Jerusalem, and with the authority of the chief priests I put many of God's people in jail. I even voted for them to be killed.
translation_verse: Nite ɖii mabitɛ ní Yerusalem. Mazi kigagla dɔni kusumu yo baka ba kɔ, lɛ makpɛ Aya yɛ banima ní ligba a; lɛ xe bɛtsa kutsépia ɛki ba ɛ, mizɛɛtrɔ awla ní lɛ aʋa.

english_verse: I often had them punished in our synagogues, and I tried to make them give up their faith. In fact, I was so angry with them, that I went looking for them in foreign cities.
translation_verse: Epoe glegle e, maki si behe kutu iiki ba ní Yudanɛ ma itsa klɔ, lɛ mekeni edi si míki tɔ banada ba kuziŋa a. Magɔni kifu ni ba si mezeni ba kude adzɛ ima ʋidi dzedze le me. Paulo apani ní yɛ kukyikyi yo su

english_verse: King Agrippa, one day I was on my way to Damascus with the authority and permission of the chief priests.
translation_verse: “Liboe lɛ ya loso, matrɛ ní Damasko gi mazi kigagla ni ɔsã adɔ ni kusumu yo baka ba klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: About noon I saw a light brighter than the sun. It flashed from heaven on me and on everyone traveling with me.
translation_verse: Lize kemize gi kwize ní kudeo aʋa ɛ, Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa, xe mamɔ kuklekle dɔni lifunu ne e, gi kuli gagla sani liwo le e, gi kwikle klani mɛ ɛ, ni banama gi mɛ ni ba kwiga kudeo e.

english_verse: We all fell to the ground. Then I heard a voice say to me in Aramaic, “Saul, Saul, why are you so cruel to me? It's foolish to fight against me!”
translation_verse: Blɔ pɔ́ kwigleʋe dɔ ní kesea, lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ gi ɛpani ki mɛ ní Hebri se me si, “Saulo, Saulo! Ege lɛ loso woeti me e? Wɔɛwa isu ya nyɔnyɔɛ gi wɔɛdzi kede ní mɛ su i, ŋwisi ligume gi likɔ ɔkli si lɛta lɛ onetee otséʋi lo e.”

english_verse: “Who are you?” I asked. Then the Lord answered, “I am Jesus! I am the one you are so cruel to.
translation_verse: Lɛ meʋi si, ‘Wɔ wonu Nyaŋwɛ ɛ, Ɔkaɛ?’ Ɔkaɛ ebu le onu si, “Mɛ menu Yesu gi woeti i.

english_verse: Now stand up. I have appeared to you, because I have chosen you to be my servant. You are to tell others what you have learned about me and what I will show you later.”
translation_verse: ‘Pɔ̀ yɔ tɔ lɛɛ ní wɔ ikli lɛ aʋa a. Mebu isu ya abasi wɔ tɔ mixwa wɔ ŋwisi mɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ kinɛ ɛ. Wɔtrasi banima kidiliɛ gi wɔmɔ ní mɛ su omono ni kidiliɛ gi wɔamɔ ní ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Lord also said, “I will protect you from the Jews and from the Gentiles that I am sending you to.
translation_verse: Maabu wo dɔni Israel bebi ba ni banama gi bénu u Yudanɛ gi ba klɔ makpɛ wɔ ɛ, iwla mɛ.

english_verse: I want you to open their eyes, so they will turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to God. Then their sins will be forgiven, and by faith in me they will become part of God's holy people.”
translation_verse: Atɔ dra ba bimbi ʋe tɔ kyi ba dɔni litumu ne me kɔmanɔ ní kuklekle mɛ, dɔni Satana kigaglaɛ ese kɔmanɔ ní Aya klɔ, atɔ gani ba kuziŋaa ní mɛ aʋa ɛ, biakɔ ba eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ ba, lɛ biamɔ ba izekɔ ní Aya yɛ banɔ kyakya ba kusi i.’ Paulo apani ní yɛ axwɛwawa la su

english_verse: First I preached to the people in Damascus, and then I went to Jerusalem and all over Judea. Finally, I went to the Gentiles and said, “Stop sinning and turn to God! Then prove what you have done by the way you live.”
translation_verse: Kutɔpu yɔ ní Damasko, lɛ Yerusalem i, lɛ kede e, ní Yudea pɔ́ lɛ ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma kusi i. Mapani liboe gbã nɛ si bekyi ní ba eboe zimizi na kude, tɔ bekyi evu Aya tɔ babitɛ eboa la gi aabasɛ si bekyi i.

english_verse: That is why some men grabbed me in the temple and tried to kill me.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ eboa la ya loso Yudanɛ ma bevu me gi meze ní Lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ bekeni edi si beeye me e.

english_verse: But all this time God has helped me, and I have preached both to the rich and to the poor. I have told them only what the prophets and Moses said would happen.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kɔna ni omete e, Aya apɔni mɛ lɛ loso malɛɛ ní ya a, xe mɛɛpani anukwarɛɛ ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa a, ki banuvɔ ni betsitsi petee. Eboa la gi mapani i, la ɖii Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba ni Mose bɛpani si ɛtrába la mɛ ɛ;

english_verse: I told them how the Messiah would suffer and be the first to be raised from death, so he could bring light to his own people and to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: si lilɛ si Kristo ye ɔmɔ kini tɔ yɛ oze ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ gi aayɔ dɔni kutse me e, tɔ aapani ní kuklekleo su ki Yudanɛ ma ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Before Paul finished defending himself, Festus shouted, “Paul, you're crazy! Too much learning has driven you out of your mind.”
translation_verse: Agi Paulo azeebu isu ya onu nite e, Festo akpɛ ozi yɛ aʋa si, “Wɔewle, Paulo! Wɔ keplekpa tetea kɛki si wɔ aŋu mɛ eezizi!”

english_verse: But Paul replied, “Honorable Festus, I am not crazy. What I am saying is true, and it makes sense.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo ebu le onu si, “Méʋiwle, mɛ Obùbù nɛ! Malɛ nì aŋu lɛ liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani i, linu anukwarɛ.

english_verse: None of these things happened off in a corner somewhere. I am sure that King Agrippa knows what I am talking about. That's why I can speak so plainly to him.”
translation_verse: Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa! Maatani kupani kiwɔ kede modi, lɛ ese linu si wote eboa la ya pɔ́. Maka lɛ aʋa si wɔkpɛ liboe kakaa lixaŋu, lɛ ese linu si liboe lɛ ya líba ní likɔtɔ mɛ loo kikpokpo me e.

english_verse: Then Paul said to Agrippa, “Do you believe what the prophets said? I know you do.”
translation_verse: Okusi ʋidi ye Agripa, wɔzi Aya yɛ beboedoplɛ ba aʋa ŋa na a? Mete si wɔzi ŋa!”

english_verse: Agrippa asked Paul, “In such a short time do you think you can talk me into being a Christian?”
translation_verse: Lɛ Agripa asi Paulo si, “Ní lipoe kpekpemi lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, kibu wobu si wɔakyi me Ɔʋaziŋa na a?”

english_verse: Paul answered, “Whether it takes a short time or a long time, I wish you and everyone else who hears me today would become just like me! Except, of course, for these chains.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Paulo ebu le onu si, “Xe ní lipoe kpekpemi loo lipoe gblamadza mɛ o, mɛɛxwa Aya si ɔki si wɔ ni banɔgɔgɔ ba gi mleenu liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani omono e, mleze ŋwisi mɛ bibɔɛ ʋɛ gi bilɛ ní mɛ su ko bitráze ní mlɔ su u!”

english_verse: Then King Agrippa, Governor Festus, Bernice, and everyone who was with them got up.
translation_verse: Lɛ Okusi ʋidi ye, Agripa, Roma banɛ ma kesea ɔʋadi ye, Bernike ani banɔ gɔgɔ ba bɛyɔ ɛ,

english_verse: But before they left, they said, “This man isn't guilty of anything. He doesn't deserve to die or to be put in jail.”
translation_verse: lɛ gi bɛsɛ ɛ, bɛpani ki ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Ɔniliɛ ya ɔ́bitɛ kididɔ gi kiaki si aatse loo biakpɛ yɛ ligba a.”

english_verse: Agrippa told Festus, “Paul could have been set free, if he had not asked to be tried by the Roman Emperor.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Agripa asi Festo si, “Ɔniliɛ ya ɛ, nya biatani yɛ kuyurɔ atɛsi ɔpani si Kaisaro asi yɛ liboe le ɔki yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: When it was time for us to sail to Rome, Captain Julius from the Emperor's special troops was put in charge of Paul and the other prisoners.
translation_verse: Agi bɛtrɔ lɛ si kwiaku onitsahu lo etrɛ ní Italia ɛ, bɛkɔ Paulo ni banɔ dzedze gi beze ligba lɛ, kpɛ awla ki Yulio, gi onu ɔka ní Roma baguta ba kusi i, gi bɛzɛxwa si i, “Roma Kesea Okusi ye yɛ baguta ba ɔha lɔ.”

english_verse: We went aboard a ship from Adramyttium that was about to sail to some ports along the coast of Asia. Aristarchus from Thessalonica in Macedonia sailed on the ship with us.
translation_verse: Kwiku onitsahu lo edɔni Adramitio axu la kutu gi bɛzɛɛtrɛ ní Asia kuseo me e. Aristarxo gi ádɔ Tesalonika a, gi ònu ɔma ní Makedonia ɛ, apɔni blɔ.

english_verse: The next day we came to shore at Sidon. Captain Julius was very kind to Paul. He even let him visit his friends, so they could give him whatever he needed.
translation_verse: Gi kelea kɛti i, kwizeku Sidon axu la kutu. Yulio apɛ lipo me aki Paulo lɛ loso aki yɛ kudeo si ɔzeedi yɛ baʋia ba, atɔ baki yɛ yɛ bidɔ ziazia ʋɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When we left Sidon, the winds were blowing against us, and we sailed close to the island of Cyprus to be safe from the wind.
translation_verse: Kwisɛ níklɔ, lɛ gi liwɔ lɛ liyɔ kedu ní blɔ su i, kwikyi klani Kipro kunisi yo kesea niklɔ gi liwɔ lɛ onu li bubɔ taʋɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then we sailed south of Cilicia and Pamphylia until we came to the port of Myra in Lycia.
translation_verse: Kwitsa Kilikia ani Pamfilia axu la lɛ kwiba Mira ní Likia ɛ.

english_verse: There the army captain found a ship from Alexandria that was going to Italy. So he ordered us to board that ship.
translation_verse: Niklɔ baguta ba ɔkaɛ amɔ onitsahu lo gi ɛdɔ Aleksandria a, xe ɛzɛɛtrɛ ní Italia ɛ, lɛ aki kwiku lo e.

english_verse: We sailed along slowly for several days and had a hard time reaching Cnidus. The wind would not let us go any farther in that direction, so we sailed past Cape Salmone, where the island of Crete would protect us from the wind.
translation_verse: Kwiga kuni yo aʋa blewu ewe glegle lɛ ní kupèpè glegle kede e, kwizeku Knido ɔma nɔ mɛ. Liwɔ lɛ onu lize gagla si kútani ɔtɔ kutrɛ ní kudeo liɔ klɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ, lɛ loso kwiga axu la kutu ní Kreta kunisi yo kesea aʋa, riɖiɖi kwidɔ Salmone ɛza.

english_verse: We went slowly along the coast and finally reached a place called Fair Havens, not far from the town of Lasea.
translation_verse: Kwize ní axu la aʋa gi kwitipɔni kesea lɛ nì kupèpè zizi kwiba eku ɔkutɔ gi bɛxwa si i, “Ɔyakɔ Gbã ki initsahu le” gi ódze ní Lasea ɔma nɔ su u.

english_verse: By now we had already lost a lot of time, and sailing was no longer safe. In fact, even the Great Day of Forgiveness was past.
translation_verse: Kwize níklɔ ewe glegle lɛ axu la aʋa kude gaga kwiku lifu itrɔ, lɛ ese linu si liwe lɛ gi bizɛnya onugu lo ɛki eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ fɛ liza kooko, lɛ loso Paulo akpɛ kiɖaŋui liɛ ya aki ba si,

english_verse: Then Paul spoke to the crew of the ship, “Men, listen to me! If we sail now, our ship and its cargo will be badly damaged, and many lives will be lost.”
translation_verse: “Benyime, mamɔ si blɔ kude gaga dɔni ní ya kɔtsinɔ ɛ kwitrazee gagla ɛpɔnɔ; bidɔ glegle biazizi aní adɔtu la tètè pɔ̀ onitsahu lo kyɛ ɛ, lɛ banima kyɛ biatse e.”

english_verse: But Julius listened to the captain of the ship and its owner, rather than to Paul.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ baguta ba ɔkaɛ ɔ́zɛ Paulo liboe le, boŋu i, eboa la gi ohu lo me baxwɛwa ba ɔkaɛ ni ohu lo onete bɛpani i, eku ye aŋu i sanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The harbor at Fair Havens wasn't a good place to spend the winter. Because of this, almost everyone agreed that we should at least try to sail along the coast of Crete as far as Phoenix. It had a harbor that opened toward the southwest and northwest, and we could spend the winter there. The Storm at Sea
translation_verse: Gi ohu lo ɔyakɔ lɔ gi beze ɔ́pɛ gi biaze ní kublɔ lipoe le loso e, ohu lo me banima kapɛ ʋidi a kɛkpɛ lɛ manɔ si kusɛ ni kugyodi si kwiiku Foinike tɔ kwiaze níklɔ kublɔ lipoe le. Foinike onu initsahu ɔyakɔ ní Kreta gi ekyi aŋu mɛ kpɛ keweku ya a. Liwɔ gagla lifu ní axu la aʋa

english_verse: When a gentle wind from the south started blowing, the men thought it was a good time to do what they had planned. So they pulled up the anchor, and we sailed along the coast of Crete.
translation_verse: Liwɔ bɔlɔɛtɔ tɔ likpese kufu dɔni kaala ɔza lɔ lɛ banima begyodi si kidiliɛ gi bɛpɛ ɛ, kɛ kiba kɛ mɛ. Lɛ loso behe igbé nɛ gi ivu onitsahu lo emuno lɛ ohu lo ɛkpaʋi i, pɔ̀ eze ní Kreta kesea su u.

english_verse: days you have been so worried that you haven't eaten a thing.
translation_verse: Lídze xe liwɔ gagla ɛtɔ ɔ gi bizɛxwa si, “Kewetu ya kaʋetu ya ɛ.” likpese kufu dɔni Kreta kesea aʋa.

english_verse: The wind struck the ship, and we could not sail against it. So we let the wind carry the ship.
translation_verse: Liwɔ lɛ lizɛhali onitsahu lo, lɛ gi líze liboe plɔplɔ si bekyi onitsahu lo akpɛ liwɔ lɛ mɛ tɔ ɛ, kwiyurɔ lɔ tɔ liwɔ lɛ lifu lo etsinɔ níklɔ gi lipɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We went along the island of Cauda on the side that was protected from the wind. We had a hard time holding the lifeboat in place,
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi kwina kunisi yo keseatɔ gi bizɛxwa si Klauda ka kaletu ya ɛ, gi liwɔ lɛ onu lika ɛyɔ taʋɛ ɛ, niklɔ bekeni edi ni kupepe xe kwitani onitsahu lo katapokpaa kunya a.

english_verse: but finally we got it where it belonged. Then the sailors wrapped ropes around the ship to hold it together. They lowered the sail and let the ship drift along, because they were afraid it might hit the sandbanks in the gulf of Syrtis.
translation_verse: Behe katapokpaa ɛkpɛ ní onitsahu lo me, lɛ bɛkɔ igbé ne enya klani onitsahu lo e. Bezese lifu si liwɔ lɛ liafu ohu lo zɛkpɛ siklami sɛ mɛ ní Libia axuta la, lɛ loso bepleni ohu lo basa ʋa tɔ liwɔ lɛ ligbani ohu lo kiliɛ gi lipɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The storm was so fierce that the next day they threw some of the ship's cargo overboard.
translation_verse: Liwɔ gagla lɛ lizeefu dzɛ, lɛ loso keletitia mɛ ɛ, bekpese ohu lo me adɔtu la kuŋwia kpɛ ní axu la mɛ.

english_verse: Then on the third day, with their bare hands they threw overboard some of the ship's gear.
translation_verse: Ní liwe tanɛ nɛ aʋa ɛ, bɛta ohu lo me bixwɛwadɔ mɛ bitɔ ɛŋwia ɛkpɛ ní axu la mɛ.

english_verse: For several days we could not see either the sun or the stars. A strong wind kept blowing, and we finally gave up all hope of being saved.
translation_verse: Kɔki ewe glegle e, kúmɔ liwo le loo badzidziebi ʋa, lɛ liwɔ lɛ lizeefu gagla niteme si gɔgɔ ɛ, kwilila kudeonudidi si blɔ mɛ ɔtɔ agɔ ligbã́.

english_verse: Since none of us had eaten anything for a long time, Paul stood up and told the men: You should have listened to me! If you had stayed on in Crete, you would not have had this damage and loss.
translation_verse: Agi banima bamɔ lipoe ŋa bidɔ lipoe gblamadza ɛ, Paulo ayɔ ɔlɛɛ ní banima aŋu mɛ lɛ apani si, “Benyime, nya mlegbe mɛ liboe le kunu si kukísɛ ní Kreta a. Nya eboa la ya ni bidɔzizi bɛ ya ni bidɔlilalila bɛ ya ʋa bítraba ba mlɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: But now I beg you to cheer up, because you will be safe. Only the ship will be lost.
translation_verse: Pɔ́ abla ɛ, maki mlɔ litukpo si mlevu ɔna! Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratse ní blɔ kusi i; onitsahu lo ko aayaʋi i.

english_verse: I belong to God, and I worship him. Last night he sent an angel
translation_verse: Kivoe elesi la ɛ, Aya gi yɛ ɔnɛ menu i, gi yɛ mizeesumu i, yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ aba mɔ klɔ

english_verse: to tell me, “Paul, don't be afraid! You will stand trial before the Emperor. And because of you, God will save the lives of everyone on the ship.”
translation_verse: lɛ apani si, ‘Paulo! Wukíse lifu u, Wɔtralɛɛ ní Kaisaro aŋu mɛ. Lɛ ní Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ kiwɔ onu i, ebu banama pɔ́ gi bɛɛga kudeo pɔni wɔ ɛ, ba agbã́ na.’

english_verse: Cheer up! I am sure that God will do exactly what he promised.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso, mlevu ɔna mɛ benemi ba! Lɛ ese linu si mazi Aya aʋa ŋa si litraze kiliɛ ɖi gi asi mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But we will first be shipwrecked on some island.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ liwɔ lɛ litrabu blo eplɛ plɛ ní kunisi yo keseatɔ aʋa a.”

english_verse: They cut the anchors loose and let them sink into the sea. At the same time they untied the ropes that were holding the rudders. Next, they raised the sail at the front of the ship and let the wind carry the ship toward the beach.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛtsa igbé ne gi bɛka bala ʋa ni lɛ onu gi bizɛ kɔ ɛtɔ initsahu le petee lɛ bɛyurɔ lɛ ɛkpɛ ní axu la mɛ ɛ, ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ke e, beble igbé nɛ gi lɛ ivu akufa ba a, gi bɛkɔ ɛgyɔ ohu lo e. Lɛ bemuni ohu lo basa ʋa ní ɔtɔ nɔ atɔ liwɔ lɛ lifu onitsahu lo atsini ɔtɔ nɔ, lɛ kwibu aŋu mɛ ɛkpɛ ní kuni yo kutu i.

english_verse: The sailors wanted to escape from the ship. So they lowered the lifeboat into the water, pretending that they were letting down some anchors from the front of the ship.
translation_verse: Lɛ ohu lo me baxwɛwa ba bɛpɛ si beese ni ohu lo su u, bepleni katapokpaa ɛkpɛ ní kuni yo me lɛ bɛkpɛ imɔ nɛ si bɛtrɛ ɛ pleni igbé ne ní ohu lo ɔtɔ nɔ kyɛ.

english_verse: The sailors were afraid that we might hit some rocks, and they let down four anchors from the back of the ship. Then they prayed for daylight.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi bezese lifu si onitsahu lo iihali aklakpɔ loso e, bepleni igbé tine gi bɛka bala ʋidi lɛ onu i, ní ohu lo kedea lɛ bɛxwa Aya si kelea kati i.

english_verse: But Paul said to Captain Julius and the soldiers, “If the sailors don't stay on the ship, you won't have any chance to save your lives.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo asi baguta ba ɔkaɛ ni baguta ba si, “Xe ohu lo baxwɛwa ba báma ní ohu lo me e, mlatrátani kubúbú kumɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: The soldiers then cut the ropes that held the lifeboat and let it fall into the sea.
translation_verse: Lɛ baguta ba bɛtsa igbé nɛ gi ivu katapokpaa a, xe kɛwɔli eku ní kuni yo me e.

english_verse: I beg you to eat something. Your lives depend on it. Do this and not one of you will be hurt.”
translation_verse: Mɛɛki mlɔ litukpo, mlaŋa kiditɔ; mlɛzia kiŋaŋaɛ tɔ mlakítse. Mlɔ kuya tule fɛ kutralila a.”

english_verse: After Paul had said this, he took a piece of bread and gave thanks to God. Then in front of everyone, he broke the bread and ate some.
translation_verse: Gi apani nite pɔ́ ɛ, Paulo akɔ lìklã ɛtɔ, edo Aya ɖase ní ba pɔ́ aŋu mɛ, lɛ ayaʋi lɛ xe ekpese aklã na kuta ɛ.

english_verse: They all felt encouraged, and each of them ate something.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔna eku ba lipo o, xe ba pɔ́ bɛŋa kiditɔ ɛ.

english_verse: and after everyone had eaten, they threw the cargo of wheat into the sea to make the ship lighter. The Shipwreck
translation_verse: Agi ɔnɔ kakaa aŋa bidɔ xe eke e, bɛŋwia kiŋaŋa gɔgɔɛ pɔ́ ɛkpɛ ní axu la mɛ tɔ ohu zizi nɔ odzi fotsofotso. Onitsahu lo ɛyaʋi

english_verse: Morning came, and the ship's crew saw a coast they did not recognize. But they did see a cove with a beach. So they decided to try to run the ship aground on the beach.
translation_verse: Agi kanɔa mɛ kɛdra ɛ, ohu lo me baxwɛwa ba bámɔ kesea egyesi pɔ̀ bɛmɔ kuni yo kutu itsisi ŋwisi ohu ɔyakɔ lɛ bɛtrɔ lɛ si xe liapɛ ɛ, biaseni onitsahu lo kpɛ ní siklami sɛ mɛ niklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But it ran aground on a sandbank. The front of the ship stuck firmly in the sand, and the rear was being smashed by the force of the waves.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ onitsahu lo ɛzɛɛhali siklami sɛ ní kuni yo me, lɛ ɛwɔ niklɔ ɛ; ohu lo ɔtɔ nɔ ewɔ kuni yo me gi ɔ́tani kuga tɔ ɔ, lɛ lɔ kedea kapɛa kɛzɛɛyaʋi kukpe kukpe ní axu la abla la kuwawa onu u.

english_verse: The soldiers decided to kill the prisoners to keep them from swimming away and escaping.
translation_verse: Lɛ baguta ba begyodi si biaye banama petee gi bɛkpɛ ligba ɛ, tɔ bakímɔ kudeo onu tɔ biase xegi bewlo etrɛ ní oge no o.

english_verse: But Captain Julius wanted to save Paul's life, and he did not let the soldiers do what they had planned. Instead, he ordered everyone who could swim to jump into the water and head for shore.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ baguta ba ɔkaɛ apɛ si yibu Paulo ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, lɛ loso ɔ́dimɛ ní lɛ aʋa a. Boŋu i, akpɛ ɔsã aki banama gi biatani kuwlo si bayɔ eku kuni yo me kpɛ ɔtɔ tɔ bewlo atrɛ oge no;

english_verse: Then he told the others to hold on to planks of wood or parts of the ship. At last, everyone safely reached shore.
translation_verse: banɔ gɔgɔ ba pɔ̀ ɛ, bekuni ba kude, ní kusepɛɔ loo ohu lo kupɛɔ aʋa. Nite lize xe blɔ petee kwiba eku oge no o xe ɔnɔnɔ otse e.

english_verse: When we came ashore, we learned that the island was called Malta.
translation_verse: Kwiku axu la kutu yɔ gi ɔnɔnɔ ótse e, xe banima bɛsi blɔ si bizɛxwa kunisi yo kesea lia klɔ si Malta a.

english_verse: The local people were very friendly, and they welcomed us by building a fire, because it was rainy and cold.
translation_verse: Kesea lia klɔ aʋa benete ba bɛwa sinemi nì blɔ. Bɛkpɛ kifu lɛ bɛzi blɔ petee lɛ ese linu si dzeni ye azɛɛdɔ lɛ kublɔ fɛ kwize e.

english_verse: After Paul had gathered some wood and had put it on the fire, the heat caused a snake to crawl out, and it bit him on the hand.
translation_verse: Paulo exogo inyi wli wli itɔ gi azaakpɛ lɛ ní kifu yɛ mɛ ɛ, ɔgblaga ɔtɔ edo ní inyi nɛ mɛ kɔkpɛ kifu yɛ kuhuhu yo loso, lɛ axla yɛ ɔwla lɔ xe ɔsunu lɔ su i.

english_verse: When the local people saw the snake hanging from Paul's hand, they said to each other, “This man must be a murderer! He didn't drown in the sea, but the goddess of justice will kill him anyway.”
translation_verse: Agi niklɔ benete ba bɛmɔ ɔgblagaɛ gi ɔsunu Paulo kawlakpɔa ɛ, bɛsi ba ahanuglɔ ba si, “Ɔniliɛ ya aaze ɔnɔye, gi ɔ́wɔ ní axu la mɛ kyɛ ɛ, babu ʋa bátraki si aaze ligbã́ a.”

english_verse: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and wasn't harmed.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Paulo esugu ɔgblagaɛ akpɛ ní kifu yɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔ́bitɛ yɛ kididɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The people kept thinking that Paul would either swell up or suddenly drop dead. They watched him for a long time, and when nothing happened to him, they changed their minds and said, “This man is a god.”
translation_verse: Banima bezeedi kudeonu si Paulo aaye loo aagleʋe lɛ aatse kpataa. Pɔ̀ gi beze lipoe gblamadzaɛtɔ ɔ, xe bɛmɔ si kididɔ kísa yɛ ɛ, bekyi ba begyodi ʋa lɛ bɛpani si, “Onu ɔbu!”

english_verse: The governor of the island was named Publius, and he owned some of the land around there. Publius was very friendly and welcomed us into his home for three days.
translation_verse: Inyrɔ ɛtɔ gi inu Publio kesea ɔʋadi ye inɛ ɛ, itipɔni kusio klɔ gi beze e. Yɛ azɛ blɔ akpɛ yɛ kepea mɛ lɛ kɔki ewe tata zizi i, edi blɔ aʋa mumɔ.

english_verse: His father was in bed, sick with fever and stomach trouble, and Paul went to visit him. Paul healed the man by praying and placing his hands on him.
translation_verse: Publio yɛka afɛ ní ɔnyanya ɔtɔsi aʋa ni kuwe ani lipo me tutu, lɛ loso Paulo azeedi ye ní yɛ kugbakeo me, lɛ gi axwa Aya ɛ, atrɔ awla yɛ aʋa, lɛ ayɔ ni yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After this happened, everyone on the island brought their sick people to Paul, and they were all healed.
translation_verse: Agi liba lɛ mɛ nite e, banyanya gɔgɔ ba ní kesea klɔ aʋa ɛ, bɛba lɛ ayɔni ba petee e.

english_verse: The people were very respectful to us, and when we sailed, they gave us everything we needed. From Malta to Rome
translation_verse: Bɛki blɔ bidɔ glegle lɛ gi blɔ lisɛpoe le lina ɛ, bɛki blɔ kidɔ kakaa iliɛ gi kwiazia ní blɔ kude gaga mɛ ɛ. Dɔni Malta trɛ ní Roma

english_verse: Three months later we sailed in a ship that had been docked at Malta for the winter. The ship was from Alexandria in Egypt and was known as “The Twin Gods.”
translation_verse: Kwize níklɔ badzidzi tiata, lɛ kwiku onitsahu ɔlɔ gi bɛzɛxwa si, “Babu Tiaʋa ʋɛ,” ohu lo ɛdɔ ní Aleksandria lɛ eze ni kunisi yo kesea lia klɔ aʋa ní kublɔ lipoe le me.

english_verse: We arrived in Syracuse and stayed for three days.
translation_verse: Kwizeku Sirakusa ɔma ʋidi no me lɛ kwize ní niklɔ ewe tata.

english_verse: From there we sailed to Rhegium. The next day a south wind began to blow, and two days later we arrived in Puteoli.
translation_verse: Dɔni níklɔ ʋa ɛ, kwisɛ dzɛ lɛ kwizeku Regio ɔma ʋidi no me. Ní liwe ʋla we le aʋa ɛ, liwɔ lɛ likpese kufu dɔni kaletu ya lɛ ní ewe taʋa kede e, kwizeku Puteoli ɔma nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: There we found some of the Lord's followers, who begged us to stay with them. A week later we left for the city of Rome.
translation_verse: Kwitsani baʋaziŋaatɔ niklɔ lɛ beʋi dɔni blɔ kɔ si kuze ní basu ogle tole. Lɛ kwizeku Roma ɛ.

english_verse: Some of the followers in Rome heard about us and came to meet us at the Market of Appius and at the Three Inns. When Paul saw them, he thanked God and was encouraged. Paul in Rome
translation_verse: Lɛ gi baʋaziŋa ba ní Roma benu blɔ onu i, bɛba ɛtsani blɔ ni Apía kedzia mɛ ani Izekɔ Tita lɛ̀ ɔma nɔ mɛ. Agi Paulo amɔ ba ɛ, edo Aya ɖase lɛ ɔna eku yɛ lipo e. Paulo eku Roma

english_verse: We arrived in Rome, and Paul was allowed to live in a house by himself with a soldier to guard him.
translation_verse: Agi kwiku Roma ɛ, bɛki Paulo kude si oze ní yɛ isuwla mɛ atɔ ɔguta tole oze pe ye.

english_verse: Three days after we got there, Paul called together some of the Jewish leaders and said: My friends, I have never done anything to hurt our people, and I have never gone against the customs of our ancestors. But in Jerusalem I was handed over as a prisoner to the Romans.
translation_verse: Ní ewe tata kede e, Paulo axwa Yudanɛ ma baka ba ní Roma aki kutsatsa. Agi bɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛ asi ba si, “Mɛ benemi Israelbanɛ ma, gi máyɔ kedu ni blɔ benete ba su loo ní ikɔ lɛ gi kwizɛ ɛdɔni blɔ baka ba klɔ fɛ ɛ, bɛkpɛ mɛ ligba ní Yerusalem lɛ bɛkpɛ mɛ awla ɛki Roma banɛ ma ɛ.

english_verse: They looked into the charges against me and wanted to release me. They found that I had not done anything deserving death.
translation_verse: Agi beʋi me eboe pɔ́ ɛ, Roma banɛ ma bɛpɛ si bɛɛyurɔ mɛ lɛ ese linu si, bɛmɔ si mábitɛ kididɔ gi kibasɛ si ligyeno si mitse e.

english_verse: The Jewish leaders disagreed, so I asked to be tried by the Emperor. But I don't have anything to say against my own nation.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Yudanɛ ma bɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su i, limi mɛ aʋa si miʋi si batsini mɛ ní Kaisaro klɔ, linya ɛli lɛ mɛ si liboeboe líma ní mɛ awla gi makɔ ní mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ benete ba su u.

english_verse: I am bound by these chains because of what we people of Israel hope for. This is why I have called you here to talk about this hope of ours.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso meʋi si mitsani mlɔ atɔ migu kunugu yo ni mlɔ ɔ. Ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛkpɛ mɛ bibɔ kɔkpɛ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi Israelbanɛ ma beedi kudeonu ikɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: The leaders replied, “No one from Judea has written us a letter about you. And not one of them has come here to report on you or to say anything against you.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi Paulo si, “Blɔ fɛ ɛ kúzi kuplekpa ku tɔtɔ adɔ ni Yudanɛ ma klɔ ní Yudea ní wɔ su u. Lɛ blɔ benete batɔ gi bɛdɔ niklɔ ɛba fɛ ɛ, bámani liboetɔtɔ loo liboe zimizi litɔtɔ ní wɔ su u.

english_verse: But we would like to hear what you have to say. We understand that people everywhere are against this new group.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kwipɛ si kunu wɔ begyodi ʋa, lɛ ese linu si kwite si ní ɔkɔ kakaaɛ banima bɛyɔ kedu ní wɔ ɔha lɔ su.”

english_verse: They agreed on a time to meet with Paul, and many of them came to his house. From early morning until late in the afternoon, Paul talked to them about God's kingdom. He used the Law of Moses and the Books of the Prophets to try to win them over to Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛtrɔ liwe ni Paulo, lɛ ní liwe lɛ klɔ aʋa ɛ, banɔ glegle bɛba Paulo klɔ ní niklɔ gi eze e. Dɔni livlɛ kɔkpɛ ni kulagɔ ɛ, Paulo ebu liboe me aki ba ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa su, lɛ ekeni edi si yidra ba aŋu na ní Yesu su u, gani eboe dɔni Mose Ɔsã nɔ ni Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ba bidɔŋwlimiŋwlimi ʋɛ ni Yesu su u mɛ.

english_verse: Some of the leaders agreed with what Paul said, but others did not.
translation_verse: Bamɛ batɔ bɛzi yɛ liboe le aʋa ɛɛŋa pɔ̀ batɔ kɔ bázɛ ŋa a.

english_verse: Since they could not agree among themselves, they started leaving. But Paul said, “The Holy Spirit said the right thing when he sent Isaiah the prophet
translation_verse: Eboe ese kumonumonu kwiku ba kusi lɛ bekpese kusɛ ɛ, gi Paulo apani yɛ liboe gɔgɔ lɛ si i, “Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛpani mumɔ gani Aya yɛ Ɔxwɛdzɛ Yesaya aʋa ɛki mlɔ baka ba!

english_verse: to tell our ancestors, ‘Go to these people and tell them: You will listen and listen, but never understand. You will look and look, but never see.
translation_verse: “ ‘Si, Zɛpani kí banɔaya ba si, “Mlaata kutu dzini kuta pɔ̀ mlátranu lɛ ese e; mlaadi dzini kudi, pɔ̀ mlátramɔ ɔ,”

english_verse: All of you have stubborn hearts. Your ears are stopped up, and your eyes are covered. You cannot see or hear or understand. If you could, you would turn to me, and I would heal you.’ ”
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si banɔaya ba begyodi ʋa bɛ bɛgɔ̀ ɔ, lɛ betsí ba atukpa la a, lɛ bɛda ba bimbi ʋe e. Xe aní nite e, ba bimbi ʋe biamɔ, lɛ ba batu ʋa bianu u, ba begyodi ʋa bianu eboe ese, lɛ biakyi vu me kɔ maayɔ ni ba ɛ.’

english_verse: Paul said, “You may be sure that God wants to save the Gentiles! And they will listen.”
translation_verse: Agi apani nite e, Yudanɛ ma bɛsɛ, gi bezehe eboe ní ba atsali kusi i.”

english_verse: For two years Paul stayed in a rented house and welcomed everyone who came to see him.
translation_verse: Kɔki agɔ taʋa ɛ, Paulo eze niklɔ gi ahayi aki isu ya ɛ, lɛ niklɔ azaazɛ banama gi bɛzɛba yɛ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: He bravely preached about God's kingdom and taught about the Lord Jesus Christ, and no one tried to stop him.
translation_verse: Apani liboe le ní Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa su lɛ abasi bidɔ ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo su lifumose lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ótsi kude ɔ́ki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: From Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus. God chose me to be an apostle, and he appointed me to preach the good news
translation_verse: Keplekpa lia ya kɛdɔ ní mɛ Paulo kɔ gi menu Kristo Yesu yɛ ɔnɔsumu u, ni ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ gi Aya akya a si mipani yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ.

english_verse: that he promised long ago by what his prophets said in the holy Scriptures.
translation_verse: Liboe Gbã nɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ kooko gani yɛ beboedoplɛ ba aʋa, kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi lɛ ɛkpɛ ní Aya yɛ Liboe le keplekpaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This good news is about his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ! As a human, he was from the family of David. But the Holy Spirit proved that Jesus is the powerful Son of God, because he was raised from death.
translation_verse: lɛ ní Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, Aya ebu abasɛ ni kigagla ʋidi si onu yebinyime gi yɛ ayɔnɔ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus was kind to me and chose me to be an apostle, so that people of all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Gani yɛ aʋa a, lɛ kɔki yɛ linyi ne loso e, Aya apɛ lipo me aki blɔ, lɛ aki si kudzi banɔkpɛkpɛ, tɔ kugbani banima dɔni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma kusi i, tɔ bedzi banɔ gi bizenu kutu gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You are some of those people chosen by Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlɔ fɛ ɛ, mlakpasi ní banama gi bɛxwa si bedzi Yesu Kristo banɛ ɛ, kusi.

english_verse: This letter is to all of you in Rome. God loves you and has chosen you to be his very own people. I pray that God our Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! A Prayer of Thanks
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mɛɛŋwlimi keplekpaa lia ɛki mlɔ banama petee gi mlalɛ ní Roma a, gi Aya alɔ ɔ, xe axwa si mledzi baʋaziŋa ɛ. Atɔ Aya blɔ Ɔka ani Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo baki mlɔ lipomepɛpɛ ni kusuyɔ. Paulo apɛ veve si yɛ atrɛ ní Roma

english_verse: First, I thank God in the name of Jesus Christ for all of you. I do this because people everywhere in the world are talking about your faith.
translation_verse: Kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, medo mɛ Aya ɖase ní mlɔ petee litukpo gani Yesu Kristo aʋa, lɛ ese linu si kelea petee keenu mlɔ kuziŋaa ní Kristo me onu.

english_verse: God has seen how I never stop praying for you, while I serve him with all my heart and tell the good news about his Son.
translation_verse: Aya gi yɛ meesumu u ni mɛ ɔna nɔ petee gi mɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ní Yebi ye su i, yɛ onu mɛ katsalia si liboe lɛ gi maapani i linu anukwarɛɛ, yɛ ete si mɛɛplɛ aŋu ní mlɔ aʋa lipoe kakaa

english_verse: In all my prayers, I ask God to make it possible for me to visit you.
translation_verse: gi mɛɛxwa Aya ɛ. Mizeeʋi ye si xegi linu yɛ kupɛpɛ ɛ, ɔki tɔ lidɔ ní lɛ aʋa kimɛ tɔ migadi mlo ablɔ kɔ.

english_verse: I want to see you and share with you the same blessings that God's Spirit has given me. Then you will grow stronger in your faith.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mapɛ vevee si mimɔ mlɔ, tɔ maapɔni mlɔ tɔ mlamɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kukrira gi kwiakpɛ mlɔ kigagla a.

english_verse: What I am saying is that we can encourage each other by the faith that is ours.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani linu si maakpɛ mlɔ kigagla a, lɛ mlɔ fɛ mlaakpɛ mɛ kigagla a, lɛ ese linu si mɛ kuziŋaa kwiakpɛ mlɔ kigagla lɛ mlɔ kuziŋaa fɛ kwiakpɛ mɛ kigagla a.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that I have often planned to come for a visit. But something has always kept me from doing it. I want to win followers to Christ in Rome, as I have done in many other places.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, mapɛ si mlete si lipoe glegle e, mapɛ si miba adi mlo, pɔ̀ kiditɔ kiitsí kudeonu ɛki mɛ gakɔ nani abla. Mapɛ si míbu banima tɔ bazɛ aŋa ní mlɔ kyɛ kusi ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mabitɛ ní iho dzedze elɛ gi ínu Yudanɛ ɛ ba kusi.

english_verse: It doesn't matter if people are civilized and educated, or if they are uncivilized and uneducated. I must tell the good news to everyone. That's why I am eager to visit all of you in Rome. The Power of the Good News
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ kimbi kizã si mipani Liboe Gbã nɛ ki mlɔ banama gi mlalɛ ní Roma fɛ ɛ. Liboe Gbã nɛ lɛ kigagla ɛ

english_verse: I am proud of the good news! It is God's powerful way of saving all people who have faith, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Liboe gbã nɛ líʋiku mɛ kuye e; lɛ ese linu si lɛ linu Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ ɛ, gi kiabu banama pɔ́ ɔ, gi bɛziŋa ɛ, ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, kí Yudanɛ ma lɛ kede e, kí banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The good news tells how God accepts everyone who has faith, but only those who have faith. It is just as the Scriptures say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.” Everyone Is Guilty
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ní liboe gbã nɛ mɛ ɛ, Aya yɛ kude gyɔgyɔ kwiŋwi. Aya yɛ kude gyɔgyɔ kwiga kuziŋa mɛ dɔni okpese kɔ kɔtsinɔ oblono kɔ ɔ. Kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ keplekpaa mɛ si i, “Gani kuziŋa mɛ banama gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ní Aya kɔ ɛ, biaze ligbã́.” Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio ní ɔnɔ gbagbe su

english_verse: From heaven God shows how angry he is with all the wicked and evil things that sinful people do to crush the truth.
translation_verse: Aya ɛɛbasɛ yɛ kusuyeʋio dɔni lifunu ne ní banama petee gi béte Ye e, ni ba eboe zimizi na su gi ba bizeetsi kude ɛki banima si bakíte yɛ anukwarɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They know everything that can be known about God, because God has shown it all to them.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi lilɛ si bete ní Aya su i, kɛ mɛ kidra ɛki ba lɛ ese linu si Aya ɔtutɔ adra kɛ mɛ aki ba a.

english_verse: God's eternal power and character cannot be seen. But from the beginning of creation, God has shown what these are like by all he has made. That's why those people don't have any excuse.
translation_verse: Dɔni okpese klɔ gi Aya ebu kelea mɛ ɛ, yɛ kizezee me gi baʋímɔ ɛ, yɛ kigaglaɛ liɛ gi odukɔ ɔma ɔki kɛ, lɛ onu Aya ní yɛ kilɛlɛ mɛ ɛ, biŋwi gaã gi bizeenu u bɛmɛ gani bidɔ bɛ ya gi Aya abitɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso e, banɔ baklɔ ba ɛ, isu unu kububu kutɔtɔ kúma kí ba tɔɔ ɔ, kɔ kpɛ kidiliɛ gi bɛbitɛ ɛ!

english_verse: They know about God, but they don't honor him or even thank him. Their thoughts are useless, and their stupid minds are in the dark.
translation_verse: Bete Aya, pɔ̀ báki yɛ kubùbù liɔ klɔ gi kunu yɛ kunɛ ɛ, lɛ baʋído ye ɖase e. Boŋu i, ba begyodi ʋa bedzi bepluplu, lɛ ba ina nɛ mɛ idzi litumu u.

english_verse: They claim to be wise, but they are fools.
translation_verse: Bɛɛmɔ isu ya si bete liboe, pɔ̀ benu kunukpaʋi nì kunukpadze e.

english_verse: They don't worship the glorious and eternal God. Instead, they worship idols that are made to look like humans who cannot live forever, and like birds, animals, and reptiles.
translation_verse: Agi biasumu Aya gi oʋítse liwewe onu i, beesumu bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ gi biŋwi ŋwisi ɔnɔ gbagbe e, loo kudzɔʋi, loo baga ʋa, aloo baga gi bizeetì ní ba epò la aʋa ɛ, ŋwisi bagblaga kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So God let these people go their own way. They did what they wanted to do, and their filthy thoughts made them do shameful things with their bodies.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Aya ayurɔ banɔ ba klɔ ba kɔ si babitɛ eyekubo ala gi ba ina nɛ idimɛ nɔ ɛ, lɛ bɛɛbitɛ anyaniboe ni ba ahanuglɔ ba a, gi la izɛɛkpɛ ba kakpaa ɛ.

english_verse: They gave up the truth about God for a lie, and they worshiped God's creation instead of God, who will be praised forever. Amen.
translation_verse: Bɛtsrɛ Aya yɛ anukwarɛɛ liboe le ni asia na; lɛ beesumu u, xe bɛɛdzi axwɛ ɛki Aya yɛ awla la onu bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ ɛ, sani si besumu Aya ɔtutɔ lɔ, gi yɛ linu si kukɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ akɔ ɛ, kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ! Tɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: God let them follow their own evil desires. Women no longer wanted to have sex in a natural way, and they did things with each other that were not natural.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ kiboebitɛ liɛ ya loso e, Aya ayurɔ ba aki eyeyeboe ni kusuŋwasi. Lɛ ba badzɛ ba kyɛ ɛ, bɛyurɔ benyime ba, lɛ bɛɛbitɛ eyeyeboe la ni badzɛ dzedze ba ɛ.

english_verse: Men behaved in the same way. They stopped wanting to have sex with women and had strong desires for sex with other men. They did shameful things with each other, and what has happened to them is punishment for their foolish deeds.
translation_verse: Niteme kee, benyime ba kyɛ bɛyurɔ ɔdzɛ ni onyime kigaga lɛ bizɛsrɔ̀ nì kulekuku zimizi ki ba ahanuglɔ benyime ba a, gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, Aya ehe kutu gi kwigyeno ɛki ba a, kɔkpɛ ba eboe zimizi na loso.

english_verse: Since these people refused even to think about God, he let their useless minds rule over them. That's why they do all sorts of indecent things.
translation_verse: Agi banɔ baklɔ ba bápɛ si biakɔ Aya yɛ kutete gbã ɛkpɛ, ní ba begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ, lɛ loso ayurɔ ba akpɛ ní ba begyodi zimizi ba mɛ ɛ, atɔ babitɛ kidiliɛ gi líma si babitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They are evil, wicked, and greedy, as well as mean in every possible way. They want what others have, and they murder, argue, cheat, and are hard to get along with. They gossip,
translation_verse: Litukpo me gagla kakaa elu lilɛ ní ba mɛ, bɛ binu kuzimizi, kuŋazi ani kimbi kyikyi; ba kiboebitɛ kiyí ni kaŋuzã, kunɔye, kugɔni, kunɔmeno ani onugu kedzi kugaga. Bizegu kunugu ni banɔ su,

english_verse: say cruel things about others, and hate God. They are proud, conceited, and boastful, always thinking up new ways to do evil. These people don't respect their parents.
translation_verse: lɛ bizezizi ba ahanuglɔ ba su u; bekyri Aya, bizedu onugu, bizefeke isu ya lɛ benu basudɔ ɔ. Bizɛpɛ bede ʋa ya onu gi biakɔbitɛ eboe zimizi i, lɛ báʋibu ba bapɔnɛ ba a.

english_verse: They are stupid, unreliable, and don't have any love or pity for others.
translation_verse: Béte lidzegaga ní eboe gbã ni eboe zimizi kusi i; baʋíga ní ba kakaplɛ aʋa a, lɛ báʋidi banɔ asigaga a, lɛ baʋíkɔkyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They know God has said that anyone who acts this way deserves to die. But they keep on doing evil things, and they even encourage others to do them.
translation_verse: Bete si Aya ɔsã nɔ ɛpani si banama gi balɛ ligbã ɛnɛ ya elu e, begyeno ki kutse. Pɔ̀, bɛtrɛ ní ba ligbã́ɛ nɛ klɔ aʋa a, lɛ bɛtrɔ ní banama gi bɛbitɛ niteme eboa la fɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: Some of you accuse others of doing wrong. But there is no excuse for what you do. When you judge others, you condemn yourselves, because you are guilty of doing the very same things.
translation_verse: Lɛ klɔ loso e, mlɔ banama gi mlɛzɛɛxwa etsi ni banima ɛ, kunububu utɔtɔ kúma ní mlɔ awla a. Lɛ ese linu si xegi mlɔ kiboebitɛ kíga lidze ní ba kiniɛ su i, kɔ mleebu isu ya lipɔ ɛ.

english_verse: We know that God is right to judge everyone who behaves in this way.
translation_verse: Kwite anukwarɛɛ si Aya linɛ ligyɔ xe axwa litsi ni banɔ ba klɔ gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboa la ya elu i.

english_verse: You surely don't think much of God's wonderful goodness or of his patience and willingness to put up with you. Don't you know that the reason God is good to you is because he wants you to turn to him?
translation_verse: Aloo kikɔ mlɛkɔ yɛ asigagadidi ʋidi la, kiliɛ gi akɔ lɛ mɛ aki blɔ ɔ, xe aplɛ litɔ kese aki blɔ ɛ, si bínu bidɔ vevee na a. Mlɛ nya tè si Aya apɛ lipo me, lɛ ese linu si eekeni idi si yipɔni mlɔ tɔ mlekyi ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But you are stubborn and refuse to turn to God. So you are making things even worse for yourselves on that day when he will show how angry he is and will judge the world with fairness.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ mlɔ kutu gaglaa ni kiliɛ gi mlékyi mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, mlɛbitɛ mlɔ kutuheheo si kuze gagla sanɔ ní liwe lɛ klɔ gi biabu Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio ni litsi gyɔgyɔ lɛ kuxwaxwaa basɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God will reward each of us for what we have done.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya atrátsa liʋɛ ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa ní yɛ axwɛwawa la onu u.

english_verse: He will give eternal life to everyone who has patiently done what is good in the hope of receiving glory, honor, and life that lasts forever.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ bɛɛtrɛ lɛ aʋa ɛbitɛ eboe gbã na, lɛ bɛɛpɛ kuklekle, kubùbù yo ani ligbã́ moblo le e; lɛ ki banɔ ba klɔ ba ɛ Aya aaki ba ligbã́ moblo le e.

english_verse: But he will show how angry and furious he can be with every selfish person who rejects the truth and wants to do evil.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ ba klɔ gi benu bamimi nɛ ɛ, xe bapɛ eboe gbã a, si tɔ biaze ni eboe zimizi na kude e; ní banɔ ba klɔ aʋa ɛ, Aya aatrɔ yɛ kusuyeʋi gaglaa.

english_verse: All who are wicked will be punished with trouble and suffering. It doesn't matter if they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Aya aaki ɔziã mɔmɔ ni kuyeʋiyeʋi ki ɔnɔ kakaa gi izɛbitɛ eboe zimizi i, ki Yudanɛ ma ní ɔtɔpu kɔ, lɛ kede e, ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But all who do right will be rewarded with glory, honor, and peace, whether they are Jews or Gentiles.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ki banama gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboe gbã ɛ, Aya aaki ba kuklekle, kubùbù ani kusuyɔ, ki Yudanɛ ma ní ɔtɔpu kɔ, lɛ kede e, ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Those people who don't know about God's Law will still be punished for what they do wrong. And the Law will be used to judge everyone who knows what it says.
translation_verse: Banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, xe béte Mose Ɔsã nɔ ɔ, xe bɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ, bialila gani ɔsã nɔ kede e. Pɔ́ Yudanɛ ma gi bete Mose Ɔsã nɔ ɔ, xe bɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ, Aya aaxwa litsi ni ba ní ɔsã nɔ onu u.

english_verse: God accepts those who obey his Law, but not those who simply hear it.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si aní ɔsã nɔ kununu yo kuzɛɛki si ɔnɔ aaze ligbã́ gyɔgyɔ nì Aya a, pɔ̀ banama gi bɛɛbitɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: This proves that the conscience is like a law written in the human heart. And it will show whether we are forgiven or condemned,
translation_verse: Ba kizezee me kibasɛ si kidiliɛ gi Ɔsã nɔ ɛɛpɛ ɛ, bɛŋwlimi kɛ ɛkpɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ. Ba ina nɛ fɛ iibasɛ si lilɛ lɛ mɛ, agi ba begyodi ʋa bizebu ba lipɔ epoe atɔ ɔ, lɛ bizɛŋwlɛ ba litukpo lipoe dzedze e.

english_verse: when God appoints Jesus Christ to judge everyone's secret thoughts, just as my message says. The Jews and the Law
translation_verse: Lɛ ni Liboe Gbã nɛ gi masɔli mɛ ɛ, nite litraze ní liwe lɛ klɔ gi Aya a, gani Yesu Kristo aʋa aaxwa litsi ni mlɔ begyodi kpokpo ʋa. Yudanɛ ma nì ɔsã nɔ

english_verse: Some of you call yourselves Jews. You trust in the Law and take pride in God.
translation_verse: Lɛ wɔ xwɛ ɛ, xegi wɔɛxwa isu ya si Yuda ɔnɛ ɛ; xe wovu ɔsã nɔ mɛ awla a, xe wɔɛkɔ Aya ɛdɔ isu ya ɛ;

english_verse: By reading the Scriptures you learn how God wants you to behave, and you discover what is right.
translation_verse: xe wote kidiliɛ gi Aya apɛ si bitɛ ɛ, xe wɔkpasi dɔni ɔsã nɔ mɛ si kya eboe gbã na ɛ;

english_verse: You are sure that you are a guide for the blind and a light for all who are in the dark.
translation_verse: xe wɔka ní lɛ aʋa si wɔɛbasɛ kuŋupoo kudeo o, xe wonu kani ɔki banama a, gi balɛ ní litumu ne me e,

english_verse: And since there is knowledge and truth in God's Law, you think you can instruct fools and teach young people.
translation_verse: xe wonu ɔɖaŋukpɛ ɔki banama gi béte liboe e, ni ɔdɔbasɛ ɔki banama gi bénu eboe ese e. Wɔka ní lɛ aʋa si ní ɔsã nɔ mɛ ɛ, wɔzɛ kuboeteo ni anukwarɛ ɛ ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But how can you teach others when you refuse to learn? You preach it is wrong to steal. But do you steal?
translation_verse: Agi mlɛɛbasɛ banima bidɔ mɛ ɛ, ege lɛ loso mlatrábasɛ isu ya bidɔ ɛ? Mlɛzɛɛpani si, “Wukízuru u” pɔ̀ dani mleezezuru i?

english_verse: You say people should be faithful in marriage. But are you faithful? You hate idols, yet you rob their temples.
translation_verse: Mlɛzɛɛpani si, “Wukíwa kihasi i” pɔ̀ dani mlazɛɛwa kihasi ye e? Mládimɛni kubusumu yo o, pɔ̀ dani mlezezuru ní abugba la mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: You take pride in the Law, but you disobey the Law and bring shame to God.
translation_verse: Mlɛzɛɛdɔ̀ isu ya si Aya ɔsã nɔ ɔlɛ ní mlɔ awla, pɔ̀ dani mlɛɛzɛɛkpɛ Aya kuye gani yɛ ɔsã nɔ ese kutata mɛ na a?

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures tell us, “You have made foreigners say insulting things about God.”
translation_verse: Aya liboe le lipani si, “Kɔkpɛ mlɔ Yudanɛ ma loso ɛ, banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, bɛɛpani eboe zimizi na ní Aya su.”

english_verse: Being circumcised is worthwhile, if you obey the Law. But if you don't obey the Law, you are no better off than people who are not circumcised.
translation_verse: Xegi mlɛɛbitɛ ní Ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, mlɔ ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ ebu libi; pɔ̀ xegi mlɛta ɔsã nɔ ese e, kɔlɔ xegi bátsa ɔyaʋi aki mlɔ ɛ, lipɛ ɛsanɔ.

english_verse: So everyone who obeys the Law, but has never been circumcised, will condemn you. Even though you are circumcised and have the Law, you still don't obey its teachings.
translation_verse: Banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, gi bátsa ɔyaʋi i, pɔ̀ bizɛɛbitɛ ni ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, biabú mlɔ Yudanɛ ma lipɔ gani lɛ mɛ si mlɔ bɛki ɔsã nɔ xe bɛŋwlimi lɔ ɛplɛ ɛ, xe mlɛtsa ɔyaʋi lɔ kyɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ mlaʋíbitɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: To be a real Jew you must obey the Law. True circumcision is something that happens deep in your heart, not something done to your body. And besides, you should want praise from God and not from humans.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, Yuda ɔnɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ yɛ onu Yudanɛ dɔni ɔna nɔ mɛ, gi yɛ onu ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔ, gi yɛ ekyi ɔna mɛ ɛ, gi yɛ ɔna nɔ bɛkpɛ gyesi si onu Aya ɔnɛ ɛ. Lɛ lɛya linu Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ axwɛwawa la, lɛ aní ní Ɔsã nɔ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ. Ɔniliɛ klɔ ʋa aazi yɛ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɔ dɔni Aya kɔ aní dɔni banima kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: What good is it to be a Jew? What good is it to be circumcised?
translation_verse: Xegi lilɛ nite e, kude woli Yuda ɔnɛ amɔ ní ɔniliɛ gi ónu Yuda ɔnɛ aʋa ɛ? lɛ kedzizɛzɛ woli kalɛ ní ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ su u?

english_verse: It is good in a lot of ways! First of all, God's messages were spoken to the Jews.
translation_verse: Ní kapɛ kakaa mɛ ɛ, lipɔnɔ koŋ! Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, Aya akɔ yɛ liboe le akpɛ ní Yudanɛ ma iwla mɛ.

english_verse: It is true that some of them did not believe the message. But does this mean that God cannot be trusted, just because they did not have faith?
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi kuziŋa kúze ní bamɛ batɔ awla xwɛ ɛ? Ba kumɔziŋaa kwiaki si Aya ɔ́trabitɛ ní yɛ anukwarɛɛ aʋa na a?

english_verse: No, indeed! God tells the truth, even if everyone else is a liar. The Scriptures say about God, “Your words will be proven true, and in court you will win your case.”
translation_verse: Lítraze nite kura a! Lilɛ si Aya oze anukwarɛɛ onete, xegi ɔnɔ kakaa onu ɔsia nɛ kyɛ ɛ. Kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le keplekpaa mɛ si i, “Lilɛ si babasɛ si wɔ linɛ ligyɔ xegi wɔpani i; lɛ lilɛ si wɔaŋwɛ oyi xegi bɛxwa litsi iiki wɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: If our evil deeds show how right God is, then what can we say? Is it wrong for God to become angry and punish us? What a foolish thing to ask.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe blɔ eboe zimizi na aabu Aya yɛ eboe gyɔgyɔ la kubasɛ sanɔ ɛ, kɔ liboe woli kwiapani i? Kibu kwiabu Aya lipɔ si ehe kutu aki blɔ a? Lɛ ya liaze kuʋiʋi uliɔ gi biaʋi i.

english_verse: But the answer is, “No.” Otherwise, how could God judge the world?
translation_verse: Liwewe! Xegi Aya onu ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bágyɔ ɛ, kitɛ aaxwa litsi ni kelea mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Since your lies bring great honor to God by showing how truthful he is, you may ask why God still says you are a sinner.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe mɛ asia na aaki si Aya yɛ kusudraa ni yɛ anukwarɛɛ aaŋwi mɔmɔmɔ ɛ, ege lɛ loso biazebu mɛ lipɔ kokoko ŋwisi ɔnɔ zimizi kinɛ ɛ?

english_verse: You might as well say, “Let's do something evil, so that something good will come of it!” Some people even claim that we are saying this. But God is fair and will judge them as well. No One Is Good
translation_verse: Ege lɛ loso mlatrápani si, lɛ kɔ mlaki tɔ kubitɛ eboe zimizi tɔ eboe gbã na aado dɔ ní la mɛ ɛ? Koko e, banɔ batɔ beekpini me lɛ beebu me lipɔ si mapani liboe lɛ ya ɖii! Biabu ba lipɔ kiliɛ gi liziã ɛ. Ɔnɔnɔ Ɔ́na

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean that we Jews are better off than the Gentiles? No, it doesn't! Jews, as well as Gentiles, are ruled by sin, just as I have said.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ kitɛ kwiatsa lɛ mɛ ɛ? Blɔ Yudanɛ ma kulɛ ní kizeze me gbã mɛ sani banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma na a? Líma lɛmɛ ɛ! Mabasɛ kooko si Yudanɛ ma ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ pɔ́ bákpasi ni kuzimizi yo kigaglaɛ ese e.

english_verse: Not one of them understands or even searches for God.
translation_verse: ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ma gi enu Aya yɛ anukwarɛɛ ese e, loo ɛɛpɛ si yite Aya.

english_verse: They have all turned away and are worthless. There isn't one person who does right.
translation_verse: Ba pɔ́ bekyi kede kpɛ Aya a; ba pɔ́ bápɛ ɛki kididɔ ɔ; ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ʋibitɛ eboe gbã, ani tole e.

english_verse: Their words are like an open pit, and their tongues are good only for telling lies. Each word is as deadly as the fangs of a snake,
translation_verse: Ba ile le me ilɛ ŋwisi ikpodrɔ gi bɛdra edu u; lɛ ba binemi ʋe e, bizɛɛsɛ asia lɛ egle ʋa aɖi yɛ ɔlɛ ní ba onu.

english_verse: and they say nothing but bitter curses.
translation_verse: Ba enugumeboe la enu ifixoxo nì kuyeʋiyeʋi.

english_verse: These people quickly become violent.
translation_verse: Ba ikli lɛ izɛɛkɔsi si lɛɛbali inɔ́ka kese;

english_verse: Wherever they go, they leave ruin and destruction.
translation_verse: Lɛ bidɔ zizizizi ni ɔzia izeze ni ba kude e.

english_verse: They don't know how to live in peace.
translation_verse: lɛ béte kusuyɔ kude e,

english_verse: They don't even fear God.”
translation_verse: lɛ Aya kusese kúma ní ba aŋu mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: We know that everything in the Law was written for those who are under its power. The Law says these things to stop anyone from making excuses and to let God show that the whole world is guilty.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kwite si kidɔ kakaa ní Ɔsã nɔ mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ ki banama gi balɛ ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ ese e, tɔ banima eboe onu kububu kumɔni atɔ biaamani kelea petee ní Aya litsi xwaxwa nɛ ese.

english_verse: God doesn't accept people simply because they obey the Law. No, indeed! All the Law does is to point out our sin. God's Way of Accepting People
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔnɔ linɛ lígyɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ, xe abitɛ kidiliɛ gi Ɔsã nɔ eʋi kyɛ ɛ; boŋu kidiliɛ gi Ɔsã nɔ izɛɛbitɛ ɛ, kɛ kinu si izɛɛki si tɔ kute si kwibitɛ eboe zimizi. Kiliɛ gi Aya izɛɛlulɔ blɔ ni yɛ kusi i

english_verse: Now we see how God does make us acceptable to him. The Law and the Prophets tell how we become acceptable, and it isn't by obeying the Law of Moses.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, Aya ebu bede ba klɔ aʋa gi izɛga a, xe aaki si banima bede ʋa biagyɔ ɛ abasɛ ɛ. Ɔsã nɔ ɔma ní lɛ mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ Mose Ɔsã nɔ nì beboedoplɛ ba bɛŋa katsalia ɛki lɛ.

english_verse: All of us have sinned and fallen short of God's glory.
translation_verse: ɔnɔ kakaa abitɛ eboe zimizi, lɛ kulɛ ní ɔdzage ní Aya yɛ kubúbú yo su u.

english_verse: But God treats us much better than we deserve, and because of Christ Jesus, he freely accepts us and sets us free from our sins.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gani Aya yɛ bidɔ kɔkɔ gi aki blɔ faã ní yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ onu i, aki si ba linɛ ligyɔ gani Yesu Kristo aʋa gi yɛ aki ba ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ ɛ.
