english_verse: God sent Christ to be our sacrifice. Christ offered his life's blood, so by faith in him we could come to God. And God did this to show that in the past he was right to be patient and forgive sinners. This also shows that God is right when he accepts people who have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: tɔ yibu yɛ kude gyɔgyɔ ɛbasɛ abla. Gani lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, Aya izɛɛbasɛ si yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ alɛ ní yɛ kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa, lɛ izɛɛtsini ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ Yesu aʋa aŋa ɛ, ní kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa.

english_verse: What is left for us to brag about? Not a thing! Is it because we obeyed some law? No! It is because of faith.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ ege aʋa kwiafeke isu ya ɛ? Kididɔ! Lɛ ege lɛ loso e? Aní gi kwibitɛ ní Ɔsã nɔ aʋa loso e? Kura!, pɔ̀ gi kwiziŋa ɛ loso.

english_verse: We see that people are acceptable to God because they have faith, and not because they obey the Law.
translation_verse: Kwitsà lɛ si ɔnɔ kude kuzɛɛgyɔ ni Aya gani kuziŋaa tete ko me, lɛ aní gani Ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Does God belong only to the Jews? Isn't he also the God of the Gentiles? Yes, he is!
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya onu Yudanɛ ma tete ɔnɛ na a? Aní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ fɛ Aya onu na a? Kikakaa elu e, onu ba fɛ ɔnɛ.

english_verse: There is only one God, and he accepts Gentiles as well as Jews, simply because of their faith.
translation_verse: Abla gi Aya tole ɔlɛ ɛ, aamani Yudanɛ ma ní kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa gani ba kuziŋaa aʋa, lɛ aamani banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma fɛ ni kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa gani ba kuziŋaa liɔ ke me e.

english_verse: Do we destroy the Law by our faith? Not at all! We make it even more powerful.
translation_verse: Kɔ lɛ ese linu si gani kuziŋaa liɔ ya aʋa ɛ, kwiyurɔ Ɔsã nɔ na a? Kura aníte, boŋu i, kwivu Ɔsã nɔ gaglaa.

english_verse: If he became acceptable to God because of what he did, then he would have something to brag about. But he would never be able to brag about it to God.
translation_verse: Atɛsi yɛ eboe gbã kubitɛ mɛ Aya amɔ yɛ si yɛ bede bɛgyɔ ɛ, nya liboe lilɛ yɛ awla gi lɛ aʋa aafeke isu i; pɔ̀ aní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Money paid to workers isn't a gift. It is something they earn by working.
translation_verse: Ɔxwɛwa bizɛɛtsa liʋɛ ɛkɔ, ɔ́trakɔ lɛ ŋwisi bidɔ kɔkɔ binu u; boŋu i, binu liʋɛ gi bɛtsa ɛki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures David talks about the blessings that come to people who are acceptable to God, even though they don't do anything to deserve these blessings. David says,
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya su Dawid apani gi apani ní isami i nɛ gi ɔnɔ izɛmɔ su u, xe Aya azɛ yɛ si yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ, gi Aya ódi yɛ axwɛ ana gi awa kyɛ ɛ:

english_verse: “What a blessing when God forgives our sins and our evil deeds. What a blessing when the Lord erases our sins from his book.”
translation_verse: Ɔsamì nɛ onu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi Ɔkaɛ ɔ́trabu akɔnta ní yɛ kesetataa su u!”

english_verse: Are these blessings meant for circumcised people or for those who are not circumcised? Well, the Scriptures say that God accepted Abraham because Abraham had faith in him.
translation_verse: Isami inɛ su gi Dawid apani i, inu banama tètè gi bɛtsa ɔyaʋi ba inɛ na a? Kura! Ilɛ ɛki banama gi bátsa iyaʋi kɔ kyɛ ɛ. Kwipani si, Abraham azɛ Aya aʋa aŋa, lɛ gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Aya azɛ yɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But when did this happen? Was it before or after Abraham was circumcised? Of course, it was before.
translation_verse: Lipoe woli liboe lɛ ya liba lɛ mɛ ɛ? Liba ɛkpɛ ɔtɔ ɛki yɛ ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ loo lɛ kede na a? Liba ɛkpɛ ɔtɔ aní lɛ kede e.

english_verse: This also makes Abraham the father of everyone who is circumcised and has faith in God, as Abraham did before he was circumcised. The Promise Is for All Who Have Faith
translation_verse: Yɛ ke onu Ɔka ɔki banama gi bɛga ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ linu si kɔpɔni banama gi bɛga ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ mɛ ɛ, xe balɛ ní kuziŋa tuleo liɔ klɔ mɛ ɛ, gi blɔ ɔkaɛ Abraham eze e, xe ableke aga ɔyaʋi tsatsa lɔ mɛ ɛ. Abraham azɛ aya yɛ kakaplɛa gani kuziŋaa mɛ

english_verse: God promised Abraham and his descendants that he would give them the world. This promise wasn't made because Abraham had obeyed a law, but because his faith in God made him acceptable.
translation_verse: Gi Aya aka aplɛ aki Abraham ni yɛ besoebi ba si kelea mɛ kiadzi ba kaŋase e, abitɛ lɛ nite, aní gi Abraham aga ní Ɔsã nɔ aʋa loso o, pɔ̀ gani kude gyɔgyɔ liɔ gi kuzɛɛba gani kuziŋaa mɛ loso o.

english_verse: If Abraham and his descendants were given this promise because they had obeyed a law, then faith would mean nothing, and the promise would be worthless.
translation_verse: Xegi banama gi bizɛga ni ɔsã nɔ aʋa ba bizɛzɛ kaŋasea ɛ, kɔlɔ kuziŋaa kúnu kididɔ ɔ, lɛ Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kébu libi tɔtɔ ɔ.

english_verse: God becomes angry when his Law is broken. But where there isn't a law, it cannot be broken.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔsã nɔ izɛɛmani Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio; pɔ̀ niklɔ gi Ɔsã nɔ ɔma ɛ, ɔsã nɔ ese kutata tɔtɔ kyɛ kúma a.

english_verse: Everything depends on having faith in God, so that God's promise is assured by his gift of undeserved grace. This promise isn't only for Abraham's descendants who have the Law. It is for all who are Abraham's descendants because they have faith, just as he did. Abraham is the ancestor of us all.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso Aya yɛ kakaplɛa, bɛtrɔ ka ní kuziŋaa aʋa, tɔ kakaplɛa keze liklatrɔtrɔ ŋwisi Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ bidɔkɔkɔ faã ki Abraham yɛ besoebi ba petee aní ki banɔ baklɔ gi bizɛɛga ní Ɔsã nɔ aʋa a, pɔ̀ ki banɔ baklɔ fɛ gi bɛziŋa ŋwisi Abraham kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si Abraham yɛ onu ɔka ki blɔ petee;

english_verse: The Scriptures say that Abraham would become the ancestor of many nations. This promise was made to Abraham because he had faith in God, who raises the dead to life and creates new things.
translation_verse: ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le me si, “Mabitɛ wɔ ɔka aki amade glegle.” Yɛ onu blɔ ɔka ní Aya aŋu mɛ, gi yɛ aʋa Abraham azɛ aŋa ɛ, Aya gi yɛ kigaglaɛ kizɛɛki bekpo ba bizeedzi ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ izɛɛki si kidɔ gi kíze edi i, si kiba kɛ mɛ.

english_verse: God promised Abraham a lot of descendants. And when it all seemed hopeless, Abraham still had faith in God and became the ancestor of many nations.
translation_verse: Abraham azɛ aŋa lɛ edi kudeonu, gi nya liboeboe líze e, gi liaki kudeonudidi uliɔ klɔ ɔ, gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ edzì “ɔka aki amade glegle e.” Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛsi yɛ si “nite wɔ besoebi ba kyɛ biasrɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: But Abraham never doubted or questioned God's promise. His faith made him strong, and he gave all the credit to God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔna ódo yɛ lipo o, boŋu i, evu Aya yɛ kakaplɛa mɛ gaglaa; lɛ yɛ kuziŋaa kwiki yɛ kigagla, lɛ aki Aya kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɔ.

english_verse: Abraham was certain that God could do what he had promised.
translation_verse: Aka ní lɛ aʋa mɔmɔmɔ si kigagla kilɛ Aya awla gi aabitɛ kidiliɛ gi aka aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: just as we read in the Scriptures. But these words were not written only for Abraham.
translation_verse: Eboe la gi ɛpani si i, “bɛzi yɛ” ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ báŋwlimi na aki yɛ tètè e.

english_verse: They were written for us, since we will also be accepted because of our faith in God, who raised our Lord Jesus to life.
translation_verse: Pɔ́ bɛŋwlimi na ɛki blɔ banama kyɛ gi biazɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ, gi kwizi yɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi ayɔ ni blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God gave Jesus to die for our sins, and he raised him to life, so that we would be made acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ blɔ eboe zimizi na loso e, Aya ayurɔ yɛ akɔ si otse, lɛ Aya ayɔni yɛ tɔ ɔlulɔ blɔ ni Aya kusi i.

english_verse: By faith we have been made acceptable to God. And now, thanks to our Lord Jesus Christ, we have peace with God.
translation_verse: Abla gi Aya azɛ blɔ gani kuziŋaa mɛ si blɔ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ, kwimɔ kusuyɔ ni Aya gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo aʋa.

english_verse: Christ has also introduced us to God's gift of undeserved grace on which we now take our stand. So we are happy, as we look forward to sharing in the glory of God.
translation_verse: Amani blɔ gani kuziŋaa mɛ gi kwimɔ kudeonu ni Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ gi kulɛ abla ɛ. Lɛ kwimɔ isami ní kudeonudidi uliɔ gi kulɛ blɔ awla a, si kwimɔ libi ní Aya yɛ kusudra mɛ.

english_verse: But that's not all! We gladly suffer, because we know that suffering helps us to endure.
translation_verse: Aní lɛ tètè, kwimɔ isami ní blɔ baxixa ʋa mɛ, lɛ ese linu si kwite si baxixa ʋa bizɛɛmani igyidodo,

english_verse: And endurance builds character, which gives us a hope
translation_verse: igyidodo izɛmani kizeze me gbã; lɛ kizeze me gbã kizɛki kudeonudidi i.

english_verse: that will never disappoint us. All of this happens because God has given us the Holy Spirit, who fills our hearts with his love.
translation_verse: Lɛ kudeonudidi uliɔ ya kuʋíbu ɔna ní blɔ lipo o, lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ akɔ yɛ kunɔlɔɔ akpɛ ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ gani Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ gi onu Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Christ died for us at a time when we were helpless and sinful.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi kibitɛ kíze ní blɔ su i, Kristo etse aki banama gi béte Aya a ní lipoe lɛ gi Aya akya aʋa a.

english_verse: No one is really willing to die for an honest person, though someone might be willing to die for a truly good person.
translation_verse: Linu liboe gagla si ɔnɔ otse ní ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa ɛ litukpo. Lɛ ƒaã xe ɔnɔ aasi si yitse ní ɔnɔ gbã litukpo o.

english_verse: But God showed how much he loved us by having Christ die for us, even though we were sinful.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya ebu abasɛ blɔ kiliɛ gbɛgbɛ gi alɔ blɔ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si lipoe lɛ gi kwize banɔ zimizi i, Kristo etse aki blɔ.

english_verse: But there is more! Now that God has accepted us because Christ sacrificed his life's blood, we will also be kept safe from God's anger.
translation_verse: Abla gi bebu blo egani yɛ ika lɛ mɛ ɛ, kitɛ gbɛgbɛ liaze xe yɛ kutseo kwiabu blo dɔni Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio me gani yɛ mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: Even when we were God's enemies, he made peace with us, because his Son died for us. Yet something even greater than friendship is ours. Now that we are at peace with God, we will be saved by the life of his Son.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ gi kwize Aya yɛ ahwafianɛ ma a, xe Aya edzini blɔ ni yɛ akpɛ likpɔ, gani Yebi ye yɛ kutseo me e, lɛ abla gi Aya alulɔ blɔ ni yɛ kusi i, kitɛ gbɛgbɛ, ɔ́traki blɔ ligbã́ gani Kristo yɛ ligba nɛ mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: And in addition to everything else, we are happy because God sent our Lord Jesus Christ to make peace with us. Adam and Christ
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ aní lɛ odukɔ lɔ klɔ ɔ, kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, kuzɛmɔ isami kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi Aya abitɛ ɛ, gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo me, gi abla ɛ, Aya akpɛ blɔ ni yɛ likpɔ. Gani Adam aʋa ɛ, kutseo kwiba, pɔ̀ gani Yesu aʋa ɛ, ligbã́ nɛ liba

english_verse: Sin was in the world before the Law came. But no record of sin was kept, because there was no Law.
translation_verse: Xe ableke biaaki ɔsã nɔ ɛ, eboe zimizi eze ní kelea mɛ. Pɔ̀ niklɔ gi ɔsã nɔ ɔma ɛ Aya óʋibú banɔ zimizi ba lipɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Yet death still had power over all who lived from the time of Adam to the time of Moses. This happened, though not everyone disobeyed a direct command from God, as Adam did. In some ways Adam is like Christ who came later.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ dɔni Adam lipoe le aʋa zɛɛkɔna ni Mose lipoe le e, kutseo kwiŋa banima petee aʋa a, banama gi bábitɛ eboe zimizi ŋwisi Adam kiniɛ gi ata Aya yɛ ɔsã nɔ ese kyɛ ɛ. Adam eze ɔnutɔ gi eyi ɔniliɛ gi atrába ɛ.

english_verse: But the gift of God's undeserved grace was very different from Adam's sin. That one sin brought death to many others. Yet in an even greater way, Jesus Christ alone brought God's gift of undeserved grace to many people.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ba tiaʋa bénu kidɔ tile e, lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ gi akɔ faã ɛ, bíma ŋwisi Adam yɛ liboe zimizi nɛ gi abitɛ ɛ. Lɛmɛ lidra si banɔ glegle betse kɔ kpɛ ɔnɔ tole liɛ klɔ yɛ eboe zimizi na. Pɔ̀ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ likeme sanɔ kooko lɛ nite ke yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ gi akɔ faã ki banɔ glegle ba gani Yesu Kristo yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ kyɛ bisrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: There is a lot of difference between Adam's sin and God's gift. That one sin led to punishment. But God's gift made it possible for us to be acceptable to him, even though we have sinned many times.
translation_verse: Lɛ lidzegaga lilɛ ní Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ni ɔnɔ tole yɛ eboe zimizi na kusi i, litsixwaxwa nɛ ligba eboe zimizi na ɛkpɛ ɛ; gi bebu ɔniliɛ klɔ ʋa lipɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ lɛ klɔ ʋa kede e, ni eboe zimizi glegle kede e, Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi kúgyeno e, biba gi bɛtsà litsi iiki blɔ si blɔ linɛ ligyɔ.

english_verse: Death ruled like a king because Adam had sinned. But that cannot compare with what Jesus Christ has done. God has treated us with undeserved grace, and he has accepted us because of Jesus. And so we will live and rule like kings.
translation_verse: Lilɛ lɛ mɛ si gani ɔnɔ tole yɛ liboe zimizi ne me e, kutseo kwikpese kaʋa kuŋa kɔkpɛ ɔnɔ tolee loso. Pɔ̀ lixwɛ nɛ gi ɔnɔ tolee Yesu Kristo awa ɛ, likeme sani kidiliɛ gi kiba kɛ mɛ kɔkpɛ ɔnɔ tolee yɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ. Kitɛ banɔ baklɔ petee gi bɛzi Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ srɛsrɛ ɛ, lɛ xe ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ, biaŋa kukusi ní ligbã nɛ mɛ gani ɔnɔ tolee Yesu Kristo aʋa a.

english_verse: Everyone was going to be punished because Adam sinned. But because of the good thing that Christ has done, God accepts us and gives us the gift of life.
translation_verse: Lɛ klɔ loso ɛ; kiliɛ gi bebu banima petee lipɔ kɔkpɛ ɔnɔ tole eboe zimizi na loso e, niteme ke gani kidɔ gbã tile kubitɛɔ mɛ ɛ, Aya aki si banima petee bede ʋa bɛgyɔ xe aki ba ligbã́ a.

english_verse: Adam disobeyed God and caused many others to be sinners. But Jesus obeyed him and will make many people acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi gani ɔnɔ tole yɛ kutumonumonu yo me e, banima petee bedzi banɔ zimizi i, nite ke kɔ gani ɔnɔ tole yɛ kutununu yo me e, Aya aaki si banima petee ba bede ʋa biagyɔ.

english_verse: The Law came, so that the full power of sin could be seen. Yet where sin was powerful, God's gift of undeserved grace was even more powerful.
translation_verse: Bɛkpɛ ɔsã nɔ tɔ eboe zimizi na aasrɛ apɔnɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ gi eboe zimizi na ɛsrɛ ɛ, Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ kyɛ lisrɛ ɛsanɔ.

english_verse: Sin ruled by means of death. But God's gift of grace now rules, and God has accepted us because of Jesus Christ our Lord. This means that we will have eternal life.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kuzimizi yo kwiŋa banima ligbã nɛ aʋa gani kutse me e, niteme ke Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ kyɛ liŋa aʋa gani kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa gi kwigbani blɔ ɛtsinɔ ní ligbã moblo le me gani Yesu Kristo blɔ Ɔkaɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: What should we say? Should we keep on sinning, so that God's gift of undeserved grace will show up even better?
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ liboe woli kwiapani i? Kwiatrɛ ní eboe zimizi na kubitɛɔ aʋa tɔ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lisrɛ pɔnɔ ɔ?

english_verse: No, we should not! If we are dead to sin, how can we go on sinning?
translation_verse: Lítraze nite kura! Lɛ ese linu si kwitse lɛ eboe zimizi na ámɔ kigagla ní blɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ, lɛ kitɛ mɛ kwiaze ní eboe zimizi na mɛ dzɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Don't you know that all who share in Christ Jesus by being baptized also share in his death?
translation_verse: Aloo mléte si gi bɛkpɛ kuni ki blɔ ɔ, xe kwidzi tole ni Kristo Yesu i, kikpɛ bɛkpɛ kuni ki blɔ ɔ, xe kwidzi tole ni yɛ kutseo na a?.

english_verse: When we were baptized, we died and were buried with Christ. We were baptized, so we would live a new life, as Christ was raised to life by the glory of God the Father.
translation_verse: Gani blɔ kuni kpɛkpɛɔ mɛ ɛ, blɔ fɛ kwitse lɛ bevive blo epɔni yɛ, tɔ kiliɛ gi bɛyɔni yɛ ɛdɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ gani Yɛka kusudra kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, nite blɔ fɛ kwiayɔ xe kwiaze ligbã vuvu nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If we shared in Jesus' death by being baptized, we will be raised to life with him.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kwidzi tole ni yɛ ní kutseo me e, gi etse e, nite ke kwiaze tole ni yɛ gi bɛyɔni blɔ ɔ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛyɔni yɛ kyɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We know that the persons we used to be were nailed to the cross with Jesus. This was done, so our sinful bodies would no longer be the slaves of sin.
translation_verse: Lɛ kwite si bɛkla blɔ kizeze me kokoe me ni Kristo o, ní yɛ osetsase lo su tɔ bikyrɔ blɔ kuzimizi yo kigaglaɛ atɔ kukíze banɔ dzidzi ɛki kuzimizi yo.

english_verse: We know sin doesn't have power over dead people.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi ɔnɔ etse e, izɛmɔ ɔwla edɔni kuzimizi yo kigaglaɛ ese e.

english_verse: As surely as we died with Christ, we believe we will also live with him.
translation_verse: Agi kwitse nì Kristo e, kwiziŋa si blɔ fɛ kwiaze ligbã nì yɛ.

english_verse: We know that death no longer has any power over Christ. He died and was raised to life, never again to die.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kwite si bɛyɔ nì Kristo edɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔ́tratse tɔ ɔ, kutseo kútraŋa yɛ aʋa tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: When Christ died, he died for sin once and for all. But now he is alive, and he lives only for God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, gi etse tule e, kigagla kíma ní kuzimizi yo awla ní yɛ aʋa tɔ ɔ; lɛ abla ɛ, ɔlɛ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya.

english_verse: In the same way, you must think of yourselves as dead to the power of sin. But Christ Jesus has given life to you, and you live for God.
translation_verse: Niteme ke lilɛ si mlebu isu ya sì mletse, lɛ eboe zimizi na ámɔ kigagla ní mlɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ mlalɛ ligbã ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Aya gani Kristo Yesu me e.

english_verse: Don't let sin rule your body. After all, your body is bound to die, so don't obey its desires
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlakíki si kuzimizi yo kuŋa aʋa ní mlɔ kisugya tsetsee liɛ ya mɛ ɛ, tɔ mlazɛyurɔ isu iiki mlɔ kusuŋwasi yɔ.

english_verse: or let any part of it become a slave of evil. Give yourselves to God, as people who have been raised from death to life. Make every part of your body a slave that pleases God.
translation_verse: Loo tɔ mlayurɔ mlɔ kisugyaɛ kapɛatɔtɔ kɔ ki eboe zimizi kubitɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, mlayurɔ isu ya aki Aya, ŋwisi banama gi bɛɛdɔnɔ ní bekpo ʋa mɛ mani ligbã nɛ mɛ kinɛ ɛ. Mlayurɔ mlɔ isu ya petee aki Aya tɔ ɔwa mlɔ su axwɛ ki eboe gi alɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: Don't let sin keep ruling your lives. You are ruled by God's undeserved grace and not by the Law. Slaves Who Do What Pleases God
translation_verse: Líma si kuzimizi kuŋa mlɔ aʋa a; lɛ ese linu si mláma ní ɔsã nɔ ese. Boŋu i, mlalɛ ní Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ ese e. Mleze banɔdzidzi aki kude gyɔgyɔɔ

english_verse: What does all this mean? Does it mean we are free to sin, because we are ruled by God's gift of undeserved grace and not by the Law? Certainly not!
translation_verse: Kɔ liboe woli kwiapani i? Kuzɛɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na gi kúma ní ɔsã nɔ ese e, xe kulɛ ní Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ ese na a? Lítraze nite e!

english_verse: Don't you know that you are slaves of anyone you obey? You can be slaves of sin and die, or you can be obedient slaves of God and be acceptable to him.
translation_verse: Sugba mlete si xe mlɛyurɔ isu ikɔ ŋwisi banɔdzidzi si mlaanu kutu ki onutɔ ɛ, mlenu banɔdzidzi ki ɔka liɛ klɔ gi yɛ axwɛ mlɛɛwa ɛ. Kɔ mlenu banɔdzidzi ki kuzimizi gi kwiagbani mlɔ kutsinɔ ní kutse me e, loo ki kutununu gi kwiagbani mlɔ kutsinɔ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: You used to be slaves of sin. But I thank God that with all your heart you followed the example set forth in the teaching you received.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kwido Aya ɖase! Gi lipoetɔ zanɔ ɛ mleze banɔdzidzi ki eboe zimizi i. Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, mleenu kutu ni mlɔ ɔna petee ɛki anukwarɛɛ liboe lɛ gi kwibasɛ mlɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Now you are set free from sin and are slaves who please God.
translation_verse: Bebú mlo liʋɛ mɔtsa ɛdɔni eboe zimizi na iwla mɛ, lɛ mledzi banɔdzidzi iiki kude gyɔgyɔ ɛ.

english_verse: I am using these everyday examples, because in some ways you are still weak. You used to let the different parts of your body be slaves of your evil thoughts. But now you must make every part of your body serve God, so that you will belong completely to him.
translation_verse: Mɛɛwa liwe kakaa siya sɛ su axwɛ ní ya tɔ mlaanu eboe la ese e. Lipoetɔ gi liza ɛ, mlɛyurɔ isu ya petee ŋwisi banɔdzidzi ki anyaniboe ni litukpo me gagla ni eboe zimizi kakaa elu. Niteme ke, abla ɛ, lilɛ si mlayurɔ isu ya petee akɔ ŋwisi banɔdzidzi ki kude gyɔgyɔɔ tɔ mlɔ isu ya idra aki Aya.

english_verse: When you were slaves of sin, you didn't have to please God.
translation_verse: Lipoe lɛ gi mleze banɔdzidzi ikí eboe zimizi na ɛ, mlɛmɔ ɔwla ɛdɔni kidiliɛ gi Aya apɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But what good did you receive from the things you did? All you have to show for them is your shame, and they lead to death.
translation_verse: Libi woli mlɛmɔ ɛdɔni eyeyeboe ala gi mlɛbitɛ zanɔ ɔ, xe eeku mlɔ kuyeo abla mɛ ɛ? Kutse kunu eboa la klɔ ʋa la libi le!

english_verse: Now you have been set free from sin, and you are God's slaves. This will make you holy and will lead you to eternal life.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, Aya ebu mlo adɔ ni eboe zimizi na iwla mɛ ɛ, lɛ mleedzi yɛ banɔdzidzi i. Mlɔ libi le linu ligbã́ gi bɛkɔ ɛki Aya petee e, lɛ lilɛ gi liado dɔni lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ linu ligbã moblo le.

english_verse: Sin pays off with death. But God's gift is eternal life given by Jesus Christ our Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ eboe zimizi na la liʋɛ nɛ linu kutse e, pɔ̀ Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi izɛɛkɔ faã ɛ, bɛ binu ligbã́ moblo le ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo Yesu blɔ Ɔkaɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you surely understand enough about law to know that laws only have power over people who are alive.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, lɛ mɛ lidra si mlaanu liboe lɛ gi matrapani i ese, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ pɔ́ mleete kiditɔ ní ɔsã nɔ su u. Ɔsã nɔ izɛɛgbani banɔ ligbã́ gi banɔ ba klɔ balɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: For example, the Law says that a man's wife must remain his wife as long as he lives. But once her husband is dead, she is free
translation_verse: Ŋwi liŋwitsɛ kinɛ ɛ, ɔdzɛ gi odi ni onyime e, ɔsã ɛnya ɔdzɛ ni yenye kpɛ likpɔ, xe onyimee ɔlɛ ligba nɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ xe yenye etse e, kɔ ɔdzɛɛ amɔ ɔwla ní ɔsã nɔ gi ɛnya yɛ ni onyimee iwla mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: to marry someone else. However, if she goes off with another man while her husband is still alive, she is said to be unfaithful.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xe azeeze ni onyime dzedze e, gi yenye ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, biapani si ɛɛsi enyimeboe; pɔ̀ xe yenye etse e, ɔdzɛɛ amɔ ɔwla ní ɔsã nɔ onu lɛ lítraze si ɛɛsi enyimeboe e, xe azeezeni onyime dzedze e.

english_verse: That is how it is with you, my friends. You are now part of the body of Christ and are dead to the power of the Law. You are free to belong to Christ, who was raised to life so we could serve God.
translation_verse: Nite ke lilɛ ki mlɔ mɛ benemi ba. Ni ɔsã nɔ onu i, lilɛ ŋwisi mlɔ fɛ mletse, lɛ ese linu si mlenu Kristo yɛ kisugyaɛ kapɛ katɔ; lɛ abla ɛ, mlenu yɛ banɛ gi bɛyɔnɔ ɛdɔni kutseo me e, tɔ kwiabu libi ní Aya yɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ.

english_verse: When we thought only of ourselves, the Law made us have sinful desires. It made every part of our bodies into slaves who are doomed to die.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kwize ligbã́ nɛ ní blɔ banɔ gbagbe ba kilɛlɛɛ mɛ onu i, ɔsã nɔ ɛɛhwani kulekuku zimizi yo ní blɔ mɛ si kubitɛ eboe zimizi na, lɛ lɔ izɛwa axwɛ na ní blɔ mɛ gi kidiliɛ gi kwibitɛ ɛ, kimani kutse e.

english_verse: But the Law no longer rules over us. We are like dead people, and it cannot have any power over us. Now we can serve God in a new way by obeying his Spirit, and not in the old way by obeying the written Law. The Battle with Sin
translation_verse: Abla kɔ ɛ, kwido ní Ɔsã nɔ ese, lɛ ese linu si kwitse ki kidiliɛ klɔ gi kɛ kinya blɔ ɛ. Kuʋísumu ní kude kokoo aʋa ní kiliɛ ɔsã nɔ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ, ɛpɛ si kuzeesumu aʋa tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ ní kude vuvu yɔ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛbasɛ si kuze aʋa a. Ɔsã nɔ ɛki si kwite blɔ eboe zimizi na

english_verse: Does this mean that the Law is sinful? Certainly not! But if it had not been for the Law, I would not have known what sin is really like. For example, I would not have known what it means to want something that belongs to someone else, unless the Law had told me not to do this.
translation_verse: Kɔ kipani kwiapani si, Ɔsã nɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ onu kidɔ zimizi na a? Kura lítraze nite! Pɔ̀ Aya ɔsã nɔ ɛki si mete kidiliɛ gi kuzimizi kunu u. Xe nya ɔsã nɔ ɔ́pani si, “Wukíkɔ kaŋuza ní kidiliɛ gi kinu ɔnutɔ kinɛ su i”, nya mátrate kidiliɛ gi kinu kaŋuzã a.

english_verse: It was sin that used this command as a way of making me have all kinds of desires. But without the Law, sin is dead.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gani ɔsãa lɔ klɔ aʋa ɛ, kuzimizi yo kwimɔ kɔ kudeonu lɛ kwihwa ni kaŋuza kakaa elu ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ. Xegi ɔsã nɔ ɔma ɛ, kuzimizi yo kunu bidɔ tsetse.

english_verse: Before I knew about the Law, I was alive. But as soon as I heard that command, sin came to life,
translation_verse: Mɛ ɔtutɔ fɛ meze ligbã́ gi ménu ɔsã nɔ ese e; pɔ̀ lipoe lɛ gi menu ɔsã nɔ ɛ, kuzimizi yo kwidzi ligbã́, lɛ metse e.

english_verse: and I died. The very command that was supposed to bring life to me, instead brought death.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔsã nɔ gi bɛki si ɔmani ligbã́ ɛ, ní mɛ kapɛa mɛ ɛ, ɛmani kutse.

english_verse: Sin used this command to trick me, and because of it I died.
translation_verse: Lɛ kuzimizi yo kwimɔ kɔ kudeonu u, lɛ gani ɔsã nɔ mɛ ɛ, kwimeni me lɛ kwiye me e.

english_verse: Still, the Law and its commands are holy and correct and good.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ, ɔsã nɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛdra, lɛ lɔ kukpɛkpɛɔ su kwidra, kwina, lɛ kwipɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Am I saying something good caused my death? Certainly not! It was sin that killed me by using something good. Now we can see how terrible and evil sin really is.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kɔ kibasɛ libasɛ si kidiliɛ klɔ gi kipɛ ɛ, kɛ kimani kutseo ɛki mɛ na a? Kura! Kuzimizi yo kwikɔ; gi kwiwa kidɔgbã su axwɛ ɛ, kuzimizi yo kwimani kutse ɛki mɛ ɛ, tɔ kɔ kizezee me ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, kwiaŋwi. Lɛ loso gani ɔsã nɔ mɛ ɛ, kuzimizi yo kwibu ibasɛ si kwinyani isanɔ ɔ! Kuŋwlɛŋwlɛ ni blɔ mɛ

english_verse: We know that the Law is spiritual. But I am merely a human, and I have been sold as a slave to sin.
translation_verse: Kwite si Ɔsã nɔ onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ bidɔ; pɔ̀ menu kisugya gi kuzimizi kuzɛɛŋa mɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: In fact, I don't understand why I act the way I do. I don't do what I know is right. I do the things I hate.
translation_verse: Maʋínu kidiliɛ gi mizɛɛbitɛ ɛ ese; pɔ̀ máʋibitɛ kidiliɛ gi maapɛ si mibitɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mizɛɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi mekyri i.

english_verse: Although I don't do what I know is right, I agree that the Law is good.
translation_verse: Agi kidiliɛ gi kɛ mizɛɛbitɛ ɛ, xe aní kɛ mizɛɛpɛ si mibitɛ ɛ, libasɛ si madimɛ si ɔsã nɔ ɛgyɔ ɔ.

english_verse: So I am not the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, aní mɛ mɛɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi mɛɛbitɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ kuzimizi uliɔ gi kukpasi ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, kɔ kwibitɛ lɛ.

english_verse: I know that my selfish desires won't let me do anything that is good. Even when I want to do right, I cannot.
translation_verse: Mete si bidɔ gbã bíma ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, ŋwisi ɔnɔ kinɛ ɛ. Gi mizɛɛpɛ si mibitɛ eboe gbã kyɛ ɛ, maʋítani la kubitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Instead of doing what I know is right, I do wrong.
translation_verse: Máʋibitɛ eboe gbã na gi mapɛ si míbitɛ ɛ; boŋu i, mizɛɛbitɛ eboe zimizia na gi maʋípɛ si mibitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And so, if I don't do what I know is right, I am no longer the one doing these evil things. The sin that lives in me is what does them.
translation_verse: Xe mabitɛ kidiliɛ gi mápɛ si mibitɛ tɔ ɔ; boŋu i, kuzimizi uliɔ klɔ gi kukpasi ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, kɔ kwibitɛ nite eboa la ya.

english_verse: The Law has shown me that something in me keeps me from doing what I know is right.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mamɔ si ɔsã nɔ ya ɛɛwa lɔ axwɛ na; xegi mapɛ si mibitɛ kidiliɛ gi kipɛ ɛ, kidiliɛ gi kinyani i, kɛ ko kilɛ gi maabitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: With my whole heart I agree with the Law of God.
translation_verse: Ní mɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, mizɛɛmɔ isami ní Aya yɛ ɔsã nɔ su.

english_verse: But in every part of me I discover something fighting against my mind, and it makes me a prisoner of sin that controls everything I do.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mamɔ ɔsã dzedze gi ɛɛwa axwɛ ní mɛ isu ya mɛ ɛ, ɔsã gi ɛɛta kigũ ni ɔsãa nɔ aʋa gi mɛ ɔna nɔ ɛtrɔ awla ɛ. Biki si medzi ɔnɔ gi bɛkpɛ ligba ɛki kuzimizi yo ɔsã nɔ gi ɛɛwa axwɛ na ní mɛ isu ya mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: What a miserable person I am. Who will rescue me from this body that is doomed to die?
translation_verse: Medzi ɔsigaganete! Nyaŋwɛ aabu mɛ ní mɛ kisugyaɛ liɛ ya awla gi kiitsini mɛ ní kutse mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Thank God! Jesus Christ will rescue me. So with my mind I serve the Law of God, although my selfish desires make me serve the law of sin.
translation_verse: Medo Aya ɖase si gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo aʋa ɛ, mamɔ kubúbú. Lɛ linu si ní mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ kapɛa mɛ ɛ, maatani Aya yɛ ɔsã nɔ kusumu ní mɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ, pɔ̀ me kisugyaɛ kɔ ɛ, kisumu kuzimizi yo ɔsã nɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit will give you life that comes from Christ Jesus and will set you free from sin and death.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɔsã nɔ, gi izɛɛmani blɔ ligbã́ nɛ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ nì Kristo Yesu i, ɛki mɛ ɔwlamɔmɔ dɔni ɔsãa nɔ gi lɔ izɛɛmani kuzimizi nì kutse me e.

english_verse: The Law of Moses cannot do this, because our selfish desires make the Law weak. But God set you free when he sent his own Son to be like us sinners and to be a sacrifice for our sin. God used Christ's body to condemn sin.
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ gi Aya yɛ Ɔsã nɔ gi aki Mose ɔ́tani kubitɛ ɛ, kɔkpɛ gi ɔnɔ gbagbe ɔ́tani ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ ɛ, kɛ Aya abitɛ. Aya ebu kuzimizi yo ní ɔnɔ gbagbe ligbã́zeze ne me lipɔ gi atsisi yɛ ɔtutɔ Yebi ye e, xe aba ní ɔnɔ kizeze me ŋwisi blɔ kuzimizi yo kizeze me. Aba si yikɔ isu ikyɛ ní banɔ dzedze ba eboe zimizi na litukpo.

english_verse: He did this, so that we would do what the Law commands by obeying the Spirit instead of our own desires.
translation_verse: Aya abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ ɔsã nɔ lɔ kupɛpɛ gyɔgyɔ kwiaba kɔ mɛ tututu ní blɔ banama gi kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ dradra lɔ kupɛpɛɔ onu u, lɛ aní ní kisugyaɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu u.

english_verse: If our minds are ruled by our desires, we will die. But if our minds are ruled by the Spirit, we will have life and peace.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ begyodi ʋa balɛ ní kidiliɛ ɛ, gi yɛ kisugyaɛ kiipɛ aʋa ɛ, izɛɛmɔ kutse e. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ begyodi ʋa balɛ ní Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa ɛ, izɛɛmɔ ligbã́ ni kusuyɔ.

english_verse: If we follow our desires, we cannot please God.
translation_verse: Banama gi bizeexo kisugyaɛ kutu i, batrátani Aya aŋu kuku.

english_verse: You are no longer ruled by your desires, but by God's Spirit, who lives in you. People who don't have the Spirit of Christ in them don't belong to him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mláma ligbã́ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɔ kisugyaɛ kizɛɛsi mlɔ si mlabitɛ ɛ; boŋu i, mlalɛ ligbã́ nɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛɛsi mlɔ ɛ. Ní anukwarɛ mɛ ɛ, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔkpasi ní mlɔ mɛ. Ɔniliɛ mɛ gi Kristo yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔma ɛ, ónu yɛ ɔnɛ.

english_verse: But Christ lives in you. So you are alive because God has accepted you, even though your bodies must die because of your sins.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi Kristo ɔlɛ ní wɔ mɛ kyɛ ɛ, wɔ kisugyaɛ kiatse kɔkpɛ kuzimizi yo loso. Pɔ̀ wɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ligbã́, lɛ ese linu si Aya aki si wɔ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ.

english_verse: My dear friends, we must not live to satisfy our desires.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba a, kwidɔ liʋɛ, pɔ̀ aní si kuze ligbã́ nɛ ní kisugyaɛ liɛ ya kupɛpɛɔ onu u.

english_verse: If you do, you will die. But you will live, if by the help of God's Spirit you say “No” to your desires.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi mlalɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní kiliɛ gi mlɔ kisugyaɛ kipɛ onu i, mlɛtratse; pɔ̀ xe Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛpɔni mlɔ ɔ, xe mleye mlɔ kiboebitɛ zimizi ye e, mlaze ligbã́.

english_verse: Only those people who are led by God's Spirit are his children.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si banama gi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛgbanɔ ɛ, ba benu Aya yɛ bebi ba a.

english_verse: God's Spirit doesn't make us slaves who are afraid of him. Instead, we become his children and call him our Father.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi Aya aki mlɔ ɛ, ɔ́bitɛ mlɔ banɔdzidzi gi aaki si mlaazese lifu u; boŋu i, Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ aabitɛ mlɔ Aya yɛ bebi, lɛ ni Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigaglaɛ kwixwa Aya si, “Ɔka! mɛ Ɔka!”

english_verse: God's Spirit makes us sure that we are his children.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ni blɔ ifuɛfuɛ nɛ izɛɛtrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si Aya yɛ bebi kunu u.

english_verse: His Spirit lets us know that together with Christ we will be given what God has promised. We will also share in the glory of Christ, because we have suffered with him. A Wonderful Future for God's People
translation_verse: Agi kunu yɛ bebi i, kwiamɔ kukrira liɔ gi abla aplɛ aki yɛ banima ɛ, lɛ blɔ fɛ kwiamɔ kɔpɔni Kristo kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya abla aplɛ aki yɛ ɛ; lɛ xe kwimɔ ɔŋakɔ ní Kristo yɛ ɔziã nɔ mɛ ɛ, blɔ fɛ kwiamɔ ɔŋakɔ ní yɛ kusudra mɛ ɛ. Kusudraa ní Ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ

english_verse: I am sure what we are suffering now cannot compare with the glory that will be shown to us.
translation_verse: Makɔ lɛ si blɔ kupɛpɛɔ ní lipoe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, batrátani kɔ kukɔsrɔ nɔ kusudraa liɔ gi Aya atrábu ibasɛ blɔ lɛ kede e.

english_verse: In fact, all creation is eagerly waiting for God to show who his children are.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ pɔ́ biidi kudeonu vevee iiki Aya si obu yɛ bebi ba basɛ.

english_verse: Meanwhile, creation is confused, but not because it wants to be confused. God made it this way in the hope
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya atrɔ lɛ si yɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ ɛ, bialila bɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso bɛbitɛ ʋɛ ɛ, aní ní bɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu u, pɔ̀ Aya ɔtutɔ atrɔ lɛ nite e. Pɔ̀ kudeonu didi uliɔ kwize,

english_verse: that creation would be set free from decay and would share in the glorious freedom of his children.
translation_verse: si Aya aabu yɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ liwetɔ dɔni bɛ ɔnɔdzidzi kuzeze me ki kutse ni kufumufumu mɛ lɛ Aya aaki biamɔ ba kapɛa ni Aya yɛ bebi ba ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ gi lɔ kudeonu beedi i me.

english_verse: We know that all creation is still groaning and is in pain, like a woman about to give birth.
translation_verse: Lɛ kwite si gakɔna nì abla ɛ, Aya yɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ petee biinimì ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizeenu kuyeʋiyeʋi ní kubipɔ mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The Spirit makes us sure about what we will be in the future. But now we groan silently, while we wait for God to show that we are his children. This means that our bodies will also be set free.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ aní yɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ tete ko biinimì i; blɔ banama awla gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ɛ, ŋwisi Aya yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ tɔpu mɛ fɛ ɛ, kuzeenimi ní isu ya mɛ, gi kwipe si Aya ɔbitɛ blɔ yɛ bebi ba ɛ, tɔ ɔki si blɔ inɔse zizi nɛ pɔ́ kubúbú u.

english_verse: And this hope is what saves us. But if we already have what we hope for, there is no need to keep on hoping.
translation_verse: Lɛ gani kudeonudidi me e, kwimɔ kubúbú yo; pɔ̀ xegi kwimɔ kidiliɛ gi kɛ kwidi kudeonu ikɔ ɛ, kɔlɔ bínu kudeonudidi tɔ ɔ. Lɛ blɔ mɛ owoli izeedi kudeonu iiki kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ ɛ?

english_verse: However, we hope for something we have not yet seen, and we patiently wait for it.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe kwidi i kudeonu iiki kidiliɛ gi kuʋímɔ ɛ, kwiapé ke ni litɔplɛplɛkese.

english_verse: In certain ways we are weak, but the Spirit is here to help us. For example, when we don't know what to pray for, the Spirit prays for us in ways that cannot be put into words.
translation_verse: Niteme Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ fɛ ibazɛ ɛpɔni blɔ, xe kigagla kíma blɔ mɛ fɛ ɛ. Lɛ kúte kiliɛ gi kwiaxwa Aya a; Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔtutɔ izeebu blɔ onu ki Aya ni kutaniɔ gi eboe atrátani kupani i.

english_verse: All of our thoughts are known to God. He can understand what is in the mind of the Spirit, as the Spirit prays for God's people.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya, gi ete kidiliɛ gi kikpasi ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ ke ete Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ begyodi ʋa kyɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izeebu yɛ banima onu iiki Aya ni Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu.

english_verse: We know that God is always at work for the good of everyone who loves him. They are the ones God has chosen for his purpose,
translation_verse: Lɛ kwite si ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, Aya izɛwa axwɛ ɛki banama gi bɛlɔ yɛ ɛ, ba ligbã, banama gi axwa ní yɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu i.

english_verse: and he has always known who his chosen ones would be. He had decided to let them become like his own Son, so his Son would be the first of many children.
translation_verse: Banama gi Aya ebu kooko e, ebu ba lidze tɔ biadzi ŋwisi Yebi ye e, tɔ Yebi ye aadzi obi tsitsi ye ní benemi glegle kusi i.

english_verse: God then accepted the people he had already decided to choose, and he has shared his glory with them. God's Love
translation_verse: Lɛ loso banama gi Aya ebu lidze e, ba axwa; lɛ banama gi axwa ɛ, aki si ba bede ʋa bɛɛgyɔ, lɛ aki si bamɔ ba kapɛa ní yɛ kusudraa mɛ ɛ. Aya yɛ kulɔlɔɔ ní Kristo Yesu me

english_verse: What can we say about all this? If God is on our side, can anyone be against us?
translation_verse: Ɔkpɛ la ya petee e, liboe woli kwiapani i? Xe Aya ɔlɛ ni blɔ kude e, nyaŋwɛ aayɔ kedu ní blɔ su u?

english_verse: God did not keep back his own Son, but he gave him for us. If God did this, won't he freely give us everything else?
translation_verse: Lilɛ́ nite si aní Aya a, gi ɔ́ka blɔ Yebi ye, pɔ̀ akɔ yɛ akɔ ní blɔ litukpo o! Gi akɔ Yebi ye aki blɔ ɛ, kigbe aagbe si yɛ ɔ́trakɔ kidɔ kakaa ɛki blɔ faã na a?

english_verse: If God says his chosen ones are acceptable to him, can anyone bring charges against them?
translation_verse: Nyaŋwɛ aakɔ̀ liboe ní Aya yɛ banɔ kyakya ba su u? Aya ɔtutɔ apani si báŋa lipɔ ɔ!

english_verse: Or can anyone condemn them? No indeed! Christ died and was raised to life, and now he is at God's right side, speaking to him for us.
translation_verse: Kɔ nyaŋwɛ ɔlɛ gi aabu ba lipɔ ɛ? Aní Kristo Yesu gi etse e, aloo gi bɛyɔni ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, xe ɔlɛ ní Aya yɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ, xe ɛɛŋwlɛ blɔ litukpo o.

english_verse: Can anything separate us from the love of Christ? Can trouble, suffering, and hard times, or hunger and nakedness, or danger and death?
translation_verse: Kɔ nyaŋwɛ aatani blɔ ni Kristo yɛ kunɔlɔɔ kusi kulɛ ɛ? Kunugu kwiatani lɛ kubitɛ ɛ? loo baxixa loo keduyɔyɔ loo kuʋɛ loo lipi loo ɔxa loo kutse na a?

english_verse: It is exactly as the Scriptures say, “For you we face death all day long. We are like sheep on their way to be butchered.”
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi Aya liboe le lipani i si, “Kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso e, kwize ní kutse ɔxa mɛ ɛ, lipoe kakaa; bɛbitɛ blɔ ŋwisi iʋesi gi bɛtrɛtsà kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: In everything we have won more than a victory because of Christ who loves us.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀, ní la ya petee me e, kwimɔ kaʋaŋaŋa petee gani Yesu mɛ, yɛ gi alɔ blɔ ɛ!

english_verse: I am sure that nothing can separate us from God's love—not life or death, not angels or spirits, not the present or the future,
translation_verse: Lɛ maka ní lɛ aʋa si kididɔ kíma gi kialɛ blɔ ni yɛ kunɔlɔɔ kusi i: kutse loo ligbã́, Aya baxwɛdzɛ loo ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne gi iŋa aʋa ní lifunu ne loo kigagla dzedze etɔtɔ, abla loo ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ o,

english_verse: and not powers above or powers below. Nothing in all creation can separate us from God's love for us in Christ Jesus our Lord!
translation_verse: kididɔ ní ayɛɛ mɛ loo kesea ese, ni anukwarɛɛ mɛ kididɔ kíma ní kelea mɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ kusi gi kiatani blɔ kufili ní Aya yɛ kunɔlɔɔ gi kunu blɔ kunɛ ɛ, gani Kristo Yesu blɔ Ɔkaɛ aʋa su u.

english_verse: I am a follower of Christ, and the Holy Spirit is a witness to my conscience. So I tell the truth and I am not lying when I say
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi matrapani i, linu anukwarɛ; menu Kristo ɔnɛ lɛ máʋisɛ asia. Mɛ ɔna nɔ gi lɔ aʋa Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛŋa kyɛ ɛ, ɛɛka ní lɛ aʋa ɛki mɛ si máʋisɛ asia.

english_verse: my heart is broken and I am in great sorrow.
translation_verse: Asigaga ɛɛga mɛ lɛ ɔna ɛɛta mɛ lipoe kakaa a.

english_verse: I would gladly be placed under God's curse and be separated from Christ for the good of my own people.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si nya maapɛ si mɛ ɔtutɔ mize ní Aya yɛ ifixoxo ese e, tɔ mɛ ni Kristo kusi kùlɛ kɔkpɛ mɛ benemi ba, mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ akɔanɛ ma loso.

english_verse: They are the descendants of Israel, and they are also God's chosen people. God showed them his glory. He made agreements with them and gave them his Law. The temple is theirs and so are the promises that God made to them.
translation_verse: Ba benu Israel bebi ba; Aya azɛ ba si yɛ bebi lɛ ebu yɛ kusudraa ni kaʋaŋaa abasɛ ba a; anya onugu ni ba, lɛ aki ba Ɔsã nɔ ɔ; lɛ Aya abasɛ ba kiliɛ gi biasumu ye ní kude gbã aʋa a, lɛ bɛzi Aya yɛ kakaplɛa a.

english_verse: They have those famous ancestors, who were also the ancestors of the Christ. I pray that God, who rules over all, will be praised forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Bɛgyɔ ɛdɔ ní Yudanɛ ma baka ba okú gi ɛzi linyi me e, lɛ Kristo gi aba ŋwisi ɔnɔ gbagbe e, agyɔ ádɔ ní ba okú no me. Atɔ Aya gi ɛɛŋa aʋa ní ɔkɔ kakaa ɛ, ɔzɛ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ tsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: It cannot be said that God broke his promise. After all, not all of the people of Israel are the true people of God.
translation_verse: Meʋípani si Aya ɔ́bitɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa a; lɛ ese linu si aní Israel bebi ba pɔ́ benu Aya yɛ bebi ba.

english_verse: In fact, when God made the promise to Abraham, he meant only Abraham's descendants by his son Isaac. God was talking only about Isaac when he promised
translation_verse: Lɛ libasɛ si bebi gi bɛpɔ ní kupɔpɔdeo onu i, bénu Aya yɛ bebi i; boŋu i, bebi ba ya gi bɛpɔ ní Aya yɛ kakaplɛa onu i, ba bɛkɔ si benu besoebi ba ɔtutɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Sarah, “At this time next year I will return, and you will already have a son.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya aka aplɛ ni eboa la ya si, “Ni lipoe gbã nɛ aʋa ɛ, maadzi ba, lɛ Sarai aapɔ obinyime e.”

english_verse: That's why the Scriptures say that the Lord liked Jacob more than Esau.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi Kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si i, “Malɔ Yakob pɔ̀ mekyri Esau.”

english_verse: The Lord told Moses that he has pity and mercy on anyone he wants to.
translation_verse: Lɛ apani aki Mose si i; “Maadi ɔniliɛ gi mapɛ ɛ, asigaga; lɛ mɛ ɔna nɔ aavu ɔniliɛ gi mapɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Everything then depends on God's mercy and not on what people want or do.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kididɔ kidɔ ní kidiliɛ gi blɔ banɔ gbagbe ba kwipɛ́ loo kwibitɛ su u, pɔ̀ ní Aya tètè yɛ asigaga didi la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says to the king of Egypt, “I let you become king, so that I could show you my power and be praised by all people on earth.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ní Aya yɛ liboe le keplekpaa mɛ ɛ, Aya apani aki Farao Egipteanɛ ma Okusi yɛ si, “Medu wo kukusi tɔ gani wɔ aʋa ɛ, maabasɛ mɛ kigaglaɛ atɔ biaanu mɛ onu ní kelea petee me e.”

english_verse: Everything depends on what God decides to do, and he can either have pity on people or make them stubborn. God's Anger and Mercy
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, Aya izedi ɔnɔ kakaa gi apɛ́ ɛ, asigaga lɛ izɛbitɛ ɔnɔ kakaa gi apɛ́ ɛ ɔtumonu u. Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio ni yɛ asigagadidi ki banima

english_verse: Someone may ask, “How can God blame us, if he makes us behave in the way he wants us to?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mlɔ mɛ tole aasi mɛ si, “Xe nite lilɛ ɛ, kitɛ Aya aabu ɔnutɔ lipɔ ɛ? Nyaŋwɛ aatani kedu kuyɔ ní Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ su i?”

english_verse: But, my friend, I ask, “Who do you think you are to question God? Does the clay have the right to ask the potter why he shaped it the way he did?
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ nyaŋwɛ wonu u, si wɔabu le onu ki Aya a? Kefukpo kaʋíʋi ka ɔʋɔʋɔ yɛ si, “Ege lɛ loso xe nite wɔʋɔ mɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: God wanted to show his anger and reveal his power against everyone who deserved to be destroyed. But instead, he patiently put up with them.
translation_verse: Lɛ nite ke lilɛ ki kidiliɛ gi Aya abitɛ ɛ. Apɛ si yibasɛ yɛ kusuyeʋio tɔ ɔki si bete yɛ kigaglaɛ. Pɔ̀ aplɛ litɔ kese koŋu aki banama gi bɛkpɛ yɛ kusuyeʋi yo o, gi akpɛ ba lixaŋu si ba oblo kɔ onu si biazizi petee e.

english_verse: He did this by showing how glorious he is when he has pity on the people he has chosen to share in his glory.
translation_verse: Lɛ apɛ́ fɛ si yibu yɛ kusudraa ní kɔ kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛbasɛ ɛ, gi kɔ enye abali ní blɔ banama a, gi blɔ binɛ binu yɛ asigagadidi la a, blɔ kunu banama gi abla si kwiazi yɛ kusudraa a.

english_verse: just as the Lord says in the book of Hosea, “Although they are not my people, I will make them my people. I will treat with love those nations that have never been loved.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya Aya apani ní Hosea keplekpaa mɛ si, “Banama gi béze mɛ banɛ ɛ, Maaxwa ba si ‘Mɛ banɔ.’ maadimɛ ni banama gi mádimɛ nɔ ɛ ‘lɛ maaxwa ba si mɛ banɔ ɔ.’ ”

english_verse: “Once they were told, ‘You are not my people.’ But in that very place they will be called children of the living God.”
translation_verse: Lɛ “niklɔ ɖii gi bɛsi ba si, ‘Mlénu mɛ banɔ ɛ,’ “niklɔ biaxwa ba si Aya gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, yɛ bebi ba.”

english_verse: And this is what the prophet Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “The people of Israel are as many as the grains of sand along the beach. But only a few who are left will be saved.
translation_verse: Lɛ Yesaya akpɛ lɛ ozi ní Israel su si i, “Xe Israel banɛ ma bɛsrɛ ŋwisi siklami ní axuta la kyɛ ɛ, bamɛ basisami batɔ ko biabu u;

english_verse: The Lord will be quick and sure to do on earth what he has warned he will do.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ aakɔsi lɛ aahe kutu ki banima ní kelea mɛ kyikya.”

english_verse: Isaiah also said, “If the Lord All-Powerful had not spared some of our descendants, we would have been destroyed like the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah.” Israel and the Good News
translation_verse: Lilɛ ko ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Yesaya apani azanɔ si i, “Xe nya blɔ Ɔka Kigaglaɛ petee onetee ɔ́ki blɔ besoebi batɔtɔ ɛ, nya kwiadzi ŋwisi Sodom, nya kwiaze ŋwisi Gomora.” Paulo yɛ Aya kuxwaxwa ki Israel

english_verse: What does all of this mean? It means that the Gentiles were not trying to be acceptable to God, but they found that he would accept them if they had faith.
translation_verse: Kɔ lɛ loso kwiapani si banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, gi bazetutru si bialulɔ ba ni Aya kusi i, Aya aki si ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ lɛ ese linu si bɛzi Aya aʋa ɛɛŋa a,

english_verse: It also means that the people of Israel were not acceptable to God. And why not? It was because they were trying to be acceptable by obeying the Law instead of by having faith in God. The people of Israel fell over the stone that makes people stumble,
translation_verse: Lɛ ege lɛ loso e? Lɛ ese linu si bápɛ lɛ gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ gani ba axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso bezetù bikliʋi ʋɛ ní “likliʋitukla nɛ su.”

english_verse: just as God says in the Scriptures, “Look! I am placing in Zion a stone to make people stumble and fall. But those who have faith in that one will never be disappointed.”
translation_verse: Gi lɛ su Aya liboe le lipani si i, “Dike, Maplɛ likla ní Zion, likla gi liaki si banima biatu bikliʋi, liklakpɔ gi liaki si biagleʋe e. Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi azɛ yɛ aʋa ŋa ɛ, yɛ ɔna ɔ́tragbɔ̀ ɔ.”

english_verse: Dear friends, my greatest wish and my prayer to God is for the people of Israel to be saved.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, megyo le edi ni mɛ ɔna petee e, si mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ banima gi benu Yudanɛ ma ɛ, biamɔ kubúbú! Kiliɛ gi nya mizɛɛxwa Aya ní ba litukpo e!

english_verse: But Christ makes the Law no longer necessary for those who become acceptable to God by faith. Anyone Can Be Saved
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Kristo aki si Ɔsã nɔ odu, tɔ ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ ŋa ɛ, biaki si yɛ bede ʋa biagyɔ ni Aya. Kubúbú yo kulɛ ki ɔnɔ kakaa

english_verse: Moses said a person could become acceptable to God by obeying the Law. He did this when he wrote, “If you want to live, you must do all the Law commands.”
translation_verse: Mose aŋwlimi kiliɛ gi ɔnɔ bede ʋa biagyɔ ɔ, xegi aga ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, gi aŋwlimi si ɔnɔ kakaa gi aabitɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, aaze ligbã́.

english_verse: Neither will they ask, “Who will go down into the world of the dead to raise him to life?”
translation_verse: “Aloo tɔ oʋi si, ‘Nyaŋwɛ aaple ku ní bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ?’ “gi lɛ linu si tɔ iimuni Kristo edɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All who are acceptable because of their faith simply say, “The message is as near as your mouth or your heart.” And this is the same message we preach about faith.
translation_verse: Aya liboe le lipani si; “Aya yɛ ole lo eti ipɔni wɔ, ɔlɛ ní wɔ onu ani ní wɔ ɔna nɔ mɛ,” lɛ linu kuziŋaa liboe lɛ gi lɛ kwiipani i.

english_verse: So you will be saved, if you honestly say, “Jesus is Lord,” and if you believe with all your heart that God raised him from death.
translation_verse: Xegi wɔdra lɛ mɛ si “Yesu yɛ onu Ɔkaɛ ɛ,” xe wɔzi lɛ aʋa ŋa si, Aya ayɔni yɛ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, Aya aabu wo,

english_verse: God will accept you and save you, if you truly believe this and tell it to others.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si gani blɔ kuziŋaa mɛ blɔ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ; lɛ gani blɔ liboe le me kudradra mɛ ni blɔ inugu le e, kwiamɔ kubúbú yo.

english_verse: The Scriptures say no one who has faith will be disappointed,
translation_verse: Liboe le lipani si, “Ɔniliɛ gi azɛ yɛ aʋa ŋa ɛ, ɔ́tramɔ aŋu mɛ tsetse.”

english_verse: no matter if that person is a Jew or a Gentile. There is only one Lord, and he is generous to everyone who asks for his help.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa ɔlɛ ní lɛ mɛ, lɛ ese linu si lidzegaga ɛtɔtɔ líma ní Yudanɛ loo banama gi bénu Yudanɛ kusi i; Aya yɛ onu Ɔka tole liɛ klɔ ki ɔnɔ kakaa, lɛ izɛki banama gi bizɛɛxwa yɛ ɛ, yɛ bidɔ gbã mɛ ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ.

english_verse: How can people have faith in the Lord and ask him to save them, if they have never heard about him? And how can they hear, unless someone tells them?
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kitɛ ɛ biaxwa yɛ xe bázɛ yɛ aʋa ŋa ɛ? Lɛ kitɛ biatani yɛ aʋa kuziŋa xe bénu liboe le e? Lɛ kitɛ bianu liboe le xe ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́pani ní liboe le su i si ba ɛ?

english_verse: And how can anyone tell them without being sent by the Lord? The Scriptures say it is a beautiful sight to see even the feet of someone coming to preach the good news.
translation_verse: Lɛ kitɛ biapani liboe le xe bákpɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ɛ? ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ liboe le lipani i si, “Baxwɛdzɛa ba gi bɛmani liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, ba kuklikpɔ kwizã lo!”

english_verse: Yet not everyone has believed the message. For example, the prophet Isaiah asked, “Lord, has anyone believed what we said?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ aní Israɛlanɛ ma pɔ́ bɛzi Liboe Gbã nɛ ɛ. Yesaya ɔtutɔ apani si, “Ɔkaɛ nyaŋwɛ azɛ blɔ liboe le aʋa aŋa ɛ?”

english_verse: But am I saying that the people of Israel did not hear? No, I am not! The Scriptures say, “The message was told everywhere on earth. It was announced all over the world.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ miʋii lo o; kɔ linya ɛli lɛ mɛ si bénu liboe le na a? Ee benu le, kiliɛ gi liboe le lipani si, “Ba ile le iidzi kelea mɛ petee; ba eboe la ɛɛna kesea odu klɔ.”

english_verse: Did the people of Israel understand or not? Moses answered this question when he told that the Lord had said, “I will make Israel jealous of people who are a nation of nobodies. I will make them angry with people who don't understand a thing.”
translation_verse: Miʋi dzɛ: Kɔ Israel bebi ba ɛ, kɔ kinu bénu liboe le ese na a? Mose ɔtutɔ eze ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ gi ebu le onu si, “Maawà limade nɛ gi línu Yudanɛ su axwɛ ɛ, tɔ mikɔ ɛkpɛ mɛ banima kaŋuzã; lɛ kɔgani limade gi linu kunukpaʋi aʋa ɛ, Maakpɛ mɛ banima kusuyeʋi.”

english_verse: Isaiah was fearless enough to tell that the Lord had said, “I was found by people who were not looking for me. I appeared to the ones who were not asking about me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔna eze Yesaya lipo koŋ gi apani si i, “Banama gi baʋípɛ mɛ ɛ, ba bɛmɔ mɛ: Mebu isu abasɛ banama gi béʋi mɛ litukpo e.”

english_verse: And Isaiah said about the people of Israel, “All day long the Lord has reached out to people who are stubborn and refuse to obey.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní Israel su i, Aya apani si, “Liwe zizi nɛ petee mene mɛ iwla lɛ mɛ si míkpɛ limade gi linu batumonu ba ni banɔ gi bizɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su i, wolaa.”

english_verse: Am I saying that God has turned his back on his people? Certainly not! I am one of the people of Israel, and I myself am a descendant of Abraham from the tribe of Benjamin.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ meeʋi si: kiyurɔ Aya ayurɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ banima na a? Lima nite kura! Mɛ ɔtutɔ fɛ Yuda ɔnɛ me nu, Abraham yɛ osobi gi madɔ ní Benyamin okú no me e.

english_verse: God did not turn his back on his chosen people. Don't you remember reading in the Scriptures how Elijah complained to God about the people of Israel?
translation_verse: Aya ɔ́yurɔ yɛ banima, gi akya kpɛ ɔtɔ ɔ. Mlete kidiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le keplekpaa mɛ ɛ, lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Eliya aki Aya litukpo ní Israel su u, gi asi Aya si i;

english_verse: He said, “Lord, they killed your prophets and destroyed your altars. I am the only one left, and now they want to kill me.”
translation_verse: “Ɔkaɛ beye wɔ beboedoplɛ ba, lɛ bɛyaʋi wɔ idɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɛ ɛ; Mɛ tètè ko magɔ, lɛ bɛɛpɛ si beeye mɛ fɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: This means that only a chosen few of the people of Israel found what all of them were searching for. And the rest of them were stubborn,
translation_verse: Kɔ kitɛ ɛ? Israel bebi ba bamɔ kidiliɛ gi bɛɛpɛ ni kimbi kyikyi i. Likpɔkpɛkpɛ sisami lɛ klɔ gi Aya akya ɛ, lɛ ko limɔ bɛ; banɔ gɔgɔ ba ɛ, ba atukpa la mɛ etsí ní Aya kuxwaxwaa su u.

english_verse: It is the same way now. God treated the people of Israel with undeserved grace, and so a few of them are still his followers.
translation_verse: Nite ke lilɛ abla: banɔ kisrɛsrɛ sisami itɔ kìgɔ ní banama gi Aya akya aki isu ya kusi i, kɔkpɛ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ loso.

english_verse: This happened because of God's undeserved kindness and not because of anything they have done. It could not have happened except for God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ loso akya ba, aní ní kidiliɛ gi bɛbitɛ loso o. Lɛ xe Aya kukyakyaa kwidɔ ní banima kiboebitɛɛ mɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lítráze lipomepɛpɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “God made them so stupid that their eyes are blind, and their ears are still deaf.”
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le me si i, “Aya abitɛ lɛ si ba begyodi ʋa ni ba ina nɛ biʋíwa axwɛ ɛ, loso kɔna ni omono e, báʋimɔ ɔ, lɛ baʋínu eboe ese e.”

english_verse: Then David said, “Turn their meals into bait for a trap, so that they will stumble and be given what they deserve.
translation_verse: Lɛ Dawid apani si, “Atɔ ba eweŋaŋa na edzì kupe aki ba; atɔ Aya ɔki tɔ betu bikliʋi tɔ ohe kutu ɔki ba!

english_verse: Blindfold their eyes! Don't let them see. Bend their backs beneath a burden that will never be lifted.” Gentiles Will Be Saved
translation_verse: Atɔ Aya oni ba bimbi ʋe tɔ bakímɔ ɔ; tɔ ba kiboebitɛ zimizi ye kidzi lidɔtu gi biaxa ní lɛ ese lipoe kakaa a.”

english_verse: Do I mean that the people of Israel fell, never to get up again? Certainly not! Their failure made it possible for the Gentiles to be saved, and this will make the people of Israel jealous.
translation_verse: Meeʋi dzɛ si: Agi Yudanɛ ma bɛmlima ɛ, begleʋe gi batráyɔ tɔɔ na a? Líma nite e! Pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ ba eboe zimizi na loso e, kubúbú kwiba ɛki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, tɔ Yudanɛ ma kimbi kizã a.

english_verse: But if the rest of the world's people were helped so much by their sin and loss, they will be helped even more by their full return.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ma kuzimizi yo kwimani kukrira zikedzi iiki kelea mɛ, lɛ ba Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ lipi le liki banama a, gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ɛ, kukrira ʋidi. Kɔlɔ kitɛ gbɛgbɛ kukriraa kwiazɛ ɛ, xegi bɛkɔ Yudanɛ ma kiklaklaɛ mɛ petee pɔnɔ ɔ! Banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, biamɔ kubúbú

english_verse: I am now speaking to you Gentiles, and as long as I am an apostle to you, I will take pride in my work.
translation_verse: Mɛɛpani ki mlɔ banama gi mlénu Yudanɛ ɛ; agi manya enu ɔnɔkpɛkpɛ ki banama a, gi bénu Yudanɛ ko e, maamɔ isami ní mɛ axwɛ la su.

english_verse: I hope in this way to make some of my own people jealous enough to be saved.
translation_verse: Nyafɛ maatani kúkɔ si, banima ní mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ benete ba kusi i, ba kimbi kiazã atɔ Aya aabu bamɛ batɔ.

english_verse: When Israel rejected God, the rest of the people in the world were able to turn to him. So when God makes friends with Israel, it will be like bringing the dead back to life.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si gi bɛyurɔ ba ɛ, Aya alulɔ yɛ ni kelea banɔ dzedze ba petee kusi. Kitɛ liaze, xe Aya edzini ba kuzɛ ɛ? Liaze ŋwisi ligbã́ ki bekpo ʋa a!

english_verse: If part of a batch of dough is made holy by being offered to God, then all of the dough is holy. If the roots of a tree are holy, the rest of the tree is holy too.
translation_verse: Xegi bɛkɔ lìklã nɛ kapɛ tɔpu ya ɛki Aya ɛ, kɔ lìklã zizi nɛ kyɛ linu yɛ linɛ ɛ; lɛ xegi isegu nɛ su idra ɛ, kɔ aya la su fɛ ɛdra ɛ.

english_verse: You Gentiles are like branches of a wild olive tree made to be part of a cultivated olive tree. You have taken the place of some branches that were cut away from it. And because of this, you enjoy the blessings that come from being part of that cultivated tree.
translation_verse: Bɛyasi ayaatɔ ní ose gi bɛkpɛ su u, lɛ bɛkɔ liŋwafu nɛ mɛ ose lo aya la ɛtsa ní lɔ onu i. Mlalɛ ko ŋwisi liŋwafu mɛ ise le lɛ aya a, lɛ abla ɛ, mlɛŋwɛ kuni dɔni ose lo igu ne me e.

english_verse: But don't think you are better than the branches that were cut away. Just remember you are not supporting the roots of this tree. Its roots are supporting you.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ mlakídi banama gi bɛyasi zanɔ ŋwisi aya la kinɛ ɛ, ba aŋu mɛ. Kitɛ mlaadɔ̀ isu i? lise ya ko mlenu; mlaʋíki igu ne kiŋaŋa a, igu ne lɛ iitu mlo.

english_verse: Maybe you think those branches were cut away, so you could be put in their place.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ wɔapani si i, “Ee, pɔ̀ bɛyasi aya la tɔ mimɔ oti oku kɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: That's true enough. But they were cut away because they did not have faith, and you are where you are because you do have faith. So don't be proud, but be afraid.
translation_verse: Linu anukwarɛ, si bɛyasi aya la, lɛ ese linu si báziŋa a, pɔ̀ mlɔ kɔ mlɛdzi enu ose lo kapɛ katɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlɛɛzi ŋa. Pɔ̀ mlakídɔ isu ya ní lɛ su u; boŋu i, lifu liku mlo.

english_verse: If God cut away those natural branches, couldn't he do the same to you?
translation_verse: Lɛ xe Aya ódi Yudanɛ ma asigaga a, gi balɛ ko ŋwisi eseya la ɔtutɔ ɔ; kigyo mlegyo le edi si aadi mlɔ kɔ asigaga na a?

english_verse: Now you see both how kind and how hard God can be. He was hard on those who fell, but he was kind to you. And he will keep on being kind to you, if you keep on trusting in his kindness. Otherwise, you will be cut away too.
translation_verse: Ní ya kwiamɔ kiliɛ gi Aya izeedi asigaga ni kiliɛ gi yɛ eboe la alɛ gagla a. Ódi banama gi bɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na asigaga a, pɔ̀ apɛ lipo me aki mlɔ ɔ, xegi mlɛɛtrɛ lɛ aʋa ní yɛ asigagadidi la mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ xe mlegbe e, biayasi mlɔ fɛ zanɔ ní ose lo su.

english_verse: If those other branches will start having faith, they will be made a part of that tree again. God has the power to put them back.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi Yudanɛ ma bɛyurɔ ba kumɔziŋa ɛ, biadzini ba kutsa ní ose lo su; lɛ ese linu si Aya aatani lɛ kubitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After all, it wasn't natural for branches to be cut from a wild olive tree and to be made part of a cultivated olive tree. So it is much more likely that God will join the natural branches back to the cultivated olive tree. The People of Israel Will Be Brought Back
translation_verse: Mlɔ banama gi mlénu Yudanɛ ɛ, mlalɛ ko ŋwisi liya ní liŋwafu nɛ mɛ ose lo su, gi bɛyasi i, xe bedzini tsa ní ose kpɛkpɛ lɔ su u; gi nya ní eboe la onu i, litráze nite xe e. Yudanɛ ma balɛ ŋwisi ose kpɛkpɛɔ lɔ aya kinɛ ɛ, lɛ lilɛ plɔplɔ kí Aya si odzini ayaa la gi eboli i, kutsala ni ose lo dzɛ. Aya aadi ɔnɔ kakaa asigaga

english_verse: My friends, I don't want you Gentiles to be too proud of yourselves. So I will explain the mystery of what has happened to the people of Israel. Some of them have become stubborn, and they will stay like that until the complete number of you Gentiles has come in.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, anukwarɛ kpokpo ɔtɔ gi béte tsa ɛ, ɔlɛ gi mapɛ si mlete e, lɛ ese linu si aaki si mlatrámɔ isu ya si mlete liboe. Lɛ linu si Israel bebi ba kutumonu yo kutráze etsinɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ kwitraze kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, ba kiklaklaɛ mɛ kiana, xe biaba Aya klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: In this way all of Israel will be saved, as the Scriptures say, “From Zion someone will come to rescue us. Then Jacob's descendants will stop being evil.
translation_verse: Lɛ kude uliɔ ya aʋa Israel petee aamɔ kubúbú. Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ Aya yɛ liboe le keplekpaa mɛ si i, “Ɔnɔbu ye aadɔ ní Zion lɛ aabu litukpomegagla lɛ ní Yakob besoebi ba mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This is what the Lord has promised to do when he forgives their sins.”
translation_verse: Maabitɛ kunyanya liɔ ya ni ba xe mebu ba eboe zimizi na azanɔ ɛ.”

english_verse: The people of Israel are treated as God's enemies, so the good news can come to you Gentiles. But they are still the chosen ones, and God loves them because of their famous ancestors.
translation_verse: Agi bazɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ ɛ, lɛ Yudanɛ ma bedzi Aya yɛ ahwafianɛ ma ɛ, kɔkpɛ mlɔ banama gi mlénu Yudanɛ ma loso o. Pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ gi Aya akya ba loso e, bedzi yɛ baʋia kɔkpɛ ba baka ba loso.

english_verse: God doesn't take back the gifts he has given or disown the people he has chosen.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya ɔʋítsrɛ yɛ begyodi ʋa ní ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aakya a, xe akrira su u.

english_verse: At one time you Gentiles rejected God. But now Israel has rejected God, and you have been shown mercy.
translation_verse: Mlɔ banama gi mlénu Yudanɛ kɔ ɛ, mlénu kutu aki Aya ba zanɔ ɔ; pɔ̀ abla ɛ, mlɛɛzi Aya yɛ asigagadidi la, lɛ ese linu si Yudanɛ ma ɛ, bénu kutu u.

english_verse: And because of the mercy shown to you, they will also be shown mercy.
translation_verse: Niteme ke, kɔkpɛ asigaga ala gi mlɛzɛ abla ɛ, Yudanɛ ma bénu kutu aki Aya abla a, atɔ bafɛ ɛ, biaazɛ Aya yɛ asigagadidi la.

english_verse: All people have disobeyed God, and that's why he treats them as prisoners. But he does this, so that he can have mercy on all of them.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya abitɛ banima petee ligbã bezeze ki kutumonumonu, tɔ aadi ba petee asigaga. Mla ki tɔ kukpɔ̀ Aya

english_verse: Who can measure the wealth and wisdom and knowledge of God? Who can understand his decisions or explain what he does?
translation_verse: Aya yɛ exo la ekeme lo! Aya yɛ kuboeteo ni yɛ kuboesenu yo kwidzu! Nyaŋwɛ aatani wɔ batrɔtrɔ ba ese kubu u? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aatani wɔ bede ʋa ese kunu u?

english_verse: Has anyone loaned something to the Lord that must be repaid?”
translation_verse: “Nyaŋwɛ aki Aya kiditɔ edi i, gi kizia si odzini kɛ kutsa ɔki yɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Everything comes from the Lord. All things were made because of him and will return to him. Praise the Lord forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ ebu kidɔ kakaa a, lɛ kidɔ kakaa kilɛ gani yɛ aʋa a, lɛ binu yɛ binɛ ɛ, Mlaki tɔ kukpɔ̀ Aya kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Dear friends, God is good. So I beg you to offer your bodies to him as a living sacrifice, pure and pleasing. That's the most sensible way to serve God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba a, kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ asigaga glegle la ki blɔ onu i, mɛɛki mlɔ litukpo kpaŋu si: Mlakɔ isu aki Aya ŋwisi bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi bilɛ ligbã́ kinɛ ɛ. Mlakɔ isu aki yɛ axwɛ na tɔ mleku yɛ aŋu u. Lɛ ya linu Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kusumu yo ɔtutɔ lɔ gi lilɛ si mlakɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Don't be like the people of this world, but let God change the way you think. Then you will know how to do everything that is good and pleasing to him.
translation_verse: Mlakízɛɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi kelea lia mɛ banima bizɛbitɛ tɔ ɔ, boŋu i, mlaki tɔ Aya okyi mlɔ kimɛ mɛ gani mlɔ begyodi ʋa kukyíkyi petee me. Atɔ mlaatani Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ kute, kidiliɛ gi kipɛ ɛ, xe kiku yɛ aŋu u, xe kina ɛ.

english_verse: I realize God has treated me with undeserved grace, and so I tell each of you not to think you are better than you really are. Use good sense and measure yourself by the amount of faith that God has given you.
translation_verse: Lɛ kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ bidɔkɔkɔ gi aki mɛ onu i, mɛɛsi mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa si: Mlakímuni isu ya asani kiliɛ gi liziã si lize e. Boŋu i, mlabubɔ isu ya ní mlɔ begyodi ʋa mɛ, tɔ mlaxwa litsi ni isu ya ní kuziŋa liɔ gi Aya aki mlɔ onu u.

english_verse: A body is made up of many parts, and each of them has its own use.
translation_verse: Lɛ kiliɛ gi ɔnɔ kakaa yɛ kisugyaɛ kiilɛ ní kupɛ glegle me e, xe kapɛ kakaa ka lixwɛ nɛ liga lidze e;

english_verse: That's how it is with us. There are many of us, but we each are part of the body of Christ, as well as part of one another.
translation_verse: Niteme ke gi kunu banɔ lidze lidze e, xe kwisrɛ kyɛ ɛ, kunu kisugya tile ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo, lɛ blɔ petee kwikpɛ likpɔ ni blɔ ahanuglɔ ba a, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kisugyaɛ kupɛɔ kwikpɛ likpɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God has also given each of us different gifts to use. If we can prophesy, we should do it according to the amount of faith we have.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso lilɛ si kuwa blɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ede ede na su axwɛ ní lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi Aya aki blɔ ɛ onu. Xegi blɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ binu si kupani Aya liboe le e, lilɛ si kupani lɛ plɛ ní kuziŋaa liɔ gi kulɛ ní blɔ awla onu u.

english_verse: If we can serve others, we should serve. If we can teach, we should teach.
translation_verse: Xegi linu si kusumu i, lilɛ si kusumu; xegi blɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ binu kudɔbasɛ ɛ, lilɛ si kubasɛ bidɔ;

english_verse: If we can encourage others, we should encourage them. If we can give, we should be generous. If we are leaders, we should do our best. If we are good to others, we should do it cheerfully. Rules for Christian Living
translation_verse: Xegi blɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ binu si kukpɛ banɔ kigagla ɛ, lilɛ si kukpɛ ba kigagla. Ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛlɛ bidɔ ni banima ɛ, ɔbitɛ lɛ ni ɔna faã; ɔniliɛ gi odi ní banɔ onu i, lilɛ si ɔwa yɛ axwɛ na gaglaa, ɔniliɛ gi izeedi banɔ asigaga ɛ, ɔbitɛ lɛ ni aŋu mɛ dradra.

english_verse: Be sincere in your love for others. Hate everything that is evil and hold tight to everything that is good.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ kudɔ ní ɔna mɛ. Mlekyri eboe zimizi, tɔ mlevu kidiliɛ gi kipɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Love each other as brothers and sisters and honor others more than you do yourself.
translation_verse: Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba ŋwisi baʋaziŋa kinɛ ɛ, tɔ lifa mlɔ mɛ tɔ mlakpɛ kubùbù ní mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba su.

english_verse: Never give up. Eagerly follow the Holy Spirit and serve the Lord.
translation_verse: Mlawa axwɛ na gaglaa tɔ mlakíkpɛ asrã. Mlesumu Ɔkaɛ ni kusukɔkyɛ.

english_verse: Let your hope make you glad. Be patient in time of trouble and never stop praying.
translation_verse: Mlaki mlɔ kudeonudidi yo kukpɛ mlɔ isami, mlaplɛ litɔ kese ní mlɔ baxixa ʋa mɛ, ani tɔ mlaxwa Aya lipoe kakaa.

english_verse: Take care of God's needy people and welcome strangers into your home.
translation_verse: Mlalɛ mlɔ bidɔ mɛ ni mlɔ ahanuglɔ baʋaziŋa ba gi kididɔ kima ní ba awla ɛ, tɔ mladra mlɔ kupeo aki badɔbadɔba ba.

english_verse: Ask God to bless everyone who mistreats you. Ask him to bless them and not to curse them.
translation_verse: Mlakrira banama gi baʋípɛ mlɔ ligbã ɛ, mlakrira ba mlakíxo ifi akpɛ ba a.

english_verse: When others are happy, be happy with them, and when they are sad, be sad.
translation_verse: Mlamɔ isami ni banama gi bɛɛmɔ isami nɛ ɛ, mlebemi ni banama gi beebemi i.

english_verse: Be friendly with everyone. Don't be proud and feel that you know more than others. Make friends with ordinary people.
translation_verse: Mlakɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ aki ɔnɔ kakaa tɔ mlɔ begyodi ʋa beze tule. Mlakífeke isu u, boŋu i, mlawa lixwɛ kakaa elu. Mlakíbù isu ya si mlete liboe e.

english_verse: Don't mistreat someone who has mistreated you. But try to earn the respect of others,
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ ata wɔ ese e, wukídzini liboe zimizi itsa ɛki yɛ ɛ. Kenidi tɔ bitɛ kidiliɛ gi ɔnɔ kakaa ebu si kipɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and do your best to live at peace with everyone.
translation_verse: Bitɛ kidɔ kakaa gi wɔatanɔ ɛ ní wɔ kapɛa mɛ tɔ ze ní kusuyɔ mɛ ni ɔnɔ kakaa.

english_verse: Dear friends, don't try to get even. Let God take revenge. In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I am the one to take revenge and pay them back.”
translation_verse: Mɛ ɔʋia yɛ, wukídzini liboe zimizi itsa ɛki ɔnɔnɔ, boŋu i, ki tɔ Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio kubitɛ lɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si nite bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le me si, “Maabu kibu ye, Madzini le kutsa”, nite Ɔka Aya apani.

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “If your enemies are hungry, give them something to eat. And if they are thirsty, give them something to drink. This will be the same as piling burning coals on their heads.”
translation_verse: Boŋu kiliɛ gi Aya liboe le lipani si i; “Xe kuʋɛ kwiiku wɔ ahwafianɛ ma ɛ, ki ba kiŋaŋa a; xe olile eeku ba ɛ, ki ba kuni tɔ baŋwɛ; gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, wɔakpɛ ba kuye.”

english_verse: Don't let evil defeat you, but defeat evil with good.
translation_verse: Mlakíki si kuzimizi kuŋa mlɔ aʋa a; boŋu i, mlaŋa kuzimizi aʋa ni eboe gbã.

english_verse: Obey the rulers who have authority over you. Only God can give authority to anyone, and he puts these rulers in their places of power.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa ɔkpɛ kubùbù ní kesea mɛ baʋaŋa ba su, lɛ ese linu si kaʋaŋaa tɔtɔ káma gi Aya ɔ́ki ka kudeonu u, lɛ abla kaʋaŋaa ɛ, Aya yɛ edu ka.

english_verse: Rulers are a threat to evil people, not to good people. There is no need to be afraid of the authorities. Just do right, and they will praise you for it.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si banama gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboe gbã ɛ, baʋíse baʋaŋa ba, pɔ̀ banama gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ, ba bizese ba. Xegi mlápɛ si mleese banama gi benu baʋaŋa ba ɛ, kɔlɔ mlabitɛ kidiliɛ gi kipɛ ɛ, tɔ biakpɔ mlɔ,

english_verse: After all, they are God's servants, and it is their duty to help you. If you do something wrong, you ought to be afraid, because these rulers have the right to punish you. They are God's servants who punish criminals to show how angry God is.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si benu Aya yɛ banɔsumu gi bɛɛwa axwɛ ɛki mlɔ kupɛ̀pɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ xe mlɛbitɛ eboe zimizi i, kɔlɔ lilɛ si mlese ba lɛ ese linu si ba kigagla ɛ, si biahe kutu ki mlɔ ɛ, kilɛ ɛ. Benu Aya yɛ banɔsumu lɛ biakɔ Aya yɛ kutuheheo ɔki banama gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛ.

english_verse: But you should obey the rulers because you know it is the right thing to do, and not just because of God's anger.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ lɛ ya loso e, lilɛ si mlenu kutu aki baʋaŋa ba; aní kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ kutuheheo tètè loso o, pɔ̀ ki liboe lɛ gi mlɔ ina nɛ iasi mlɔ kyɛ loso o.

english_verse: You must also pay your taxes. The authorities are God's servants, and it is their duty to take care of these matters.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlazɛtsa mlɔ kagyɔa kyɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si baʋaŋa ba ɛ, bɛɛwa axwɛ na ɛki Aya xegi bɛɛŋa kesea aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Pay all that you owe, whether it is taxes and fees or respect and honor. Love
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlatsa kidiliɛ gi lilɛ si mlatsa ɛ, mlatsa mlɔ kagyɔa ni kesea liʋɛ nɛ, lɛ mlakɔ kubùbù ni kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ aki bamɛ ɔkakaa a. Mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba

english_verse: Let love be your only debt! If you love others, you have done all that the Law demands.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ liʋɛ likíwɔ ní mlɔ su mɔtsamɔtsa, sani lɛ ya gi liʋíblo o gi lɛ linu si mlalɔ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba a. Ɔniliɛ gi alɔ yɛ ɔhanuglɔ yɛ ɛ, abitɛ ní ɔsã nɔ aʋa.

english_verse: In the Law there are many commands, such as, “Be faithful in marriage. Do not murder. Do not steal. Do not want what belongs to others.” But all of these are summed up in the command that says, “Love others as much as you love yourself.”
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ gi bɛkpɛ si i, “Wukíwa kihasi i,” “Wukíye ɔnɔ ɔ,” “Wukízuru u,” “Wukíkɔ kaŋuza ni kidiliɛ gi kinu ɔnɔ dzedze kinɛ su u,” la ya petee ni edzedze la gi ɛsrɔni la ɛ, ɛkpɛ likpɔ ní ɔsã toleɔ lɔ ya mɛ si, “Lɔ wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ ɔtutɔ wɔ isu ya.”

english_verse: No one who loves others will harm them. So love is all that the Law demands. The Day When Christ Returns
translation_verse: Kunɔlɔɔ kuʋíkɔ si babitɛ eboe zimizi amani ɔhanuglɔ su u; lɛ loso e, kunɔlɔɔ kunu ɔsã zizi nɔ aʋa kubitɛ ɔ.

english_verse: You know what sort of times we live in, and so you should live properly. It is time to wake up. You know that the day when we will be saved is nearer now than when we first put our faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Linu si mlabitɛ lɛ nite, lɛ ese linu si mlete si lipoe le lina gi mlanyime dɔni mlɔ sra sɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ lipoɛ lɛ klɔ mɛ gi kwiamɔ kubúbú i, litipɔnɔ abla sani lipoe tɔpu inɛ gi kwizi ŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Night is almost over, and day will soon appear. We must stop behaving as people do in the dark and be ready to live in the light.
translation_verse: Elesi la iha za, kelea kɛti pɔ́. Mlaki tɔ kumɔni litumu ne kiboebitɛɛ kubitɛ ɛ, atɔ mlaki tɔ kukɔ kigũ yɛ bitadɔ mɛ, tɔ kuta kigũ yɛ ní kuklekleo onu, ŋwisi banɔ gi balɛ ni kuklekleo me kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So behave properly, as people do in the day. Don't go to wild parties or get drunk or be vulgar or indecent. Don't quarrel or be jealous.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kuvù isu ya mumɔ, ŋwisi banama gi balɛ ní liwo le kuklekleo onu kinɛ ɛ; ahasiboe loo adaŋwɛmuboe, anyaniboe loo eyeyeboe, akpafu loo kaŋuza akíze ní blɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Let the Lord Jesus Christ be as near to you as the clothes you wear. Then you won't try to satisfy your selfish desires.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mlaki tɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo oze ni mlɔ su ŋwisi liwu kinɛ ɛ, tɔ mlamɔni mlɔ isu ya mɛ kuzimizi yo kutu kuta a, tɔ mlamɔni kɔ bapɛ́pɛ ʋa aʋa kubitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Welcome all the Lord's followers, even those whose faith is weak. Don't criticize them for having beliefs that are different from yours.
translation_verse: Mlazi banama gi ba kuziŋa kúma gagla ɛ, lɛ mlakíhe eboe ni ba ní ba begyodi ʋa su u.

english_verse: Some think it is all right to eat anything, while those whose faith is weak will eat only vegetables.
translation_verse: Banɔ batɔ ba kuziŋaa kwiki ba kudeonu si baŋa kidɔ kakaa elu, pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ kuziŋaa kúma gagla ɛ, izɛŋa siga tètè ko.

english_verse: What right do you have to criticize someone else's servants? Only their Lord can decide if they are doing right, and the Lord will make sure that they do right.
translation_verse: Nyaŋwɛ wònu si xwa litsi ni ɔnɔ dzedze yɛ ɔnɔsumu ye e? Yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ yɛ ɔkaɛ ko aatsa lɛ mɛ xegi abitɛ mumɔ loo ɔ́bitɛ mumɔ ɔ. Lɛ aaŋa aʋa lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ aatani kubitɛ si tɔ ɔŋa aʋa a.

english_verse: Some of the Lord's followers think one day is more important than another. Others think all days are the same. But each of you should make up your own mind.
translation_verse: Ɔnutɔ izeegyoedi si liwetɔ liziã ɛsani ewe gɔgɔ la, lɛ ɔnutɔ fɛ izegyoedi si ewe la pɔ́ ɛɛsɔ ɔ. Blɔ mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔtrɔ likla ní yɛ begyodi ʋa aʋa.

english_verse: Any followers who count one day more important than another day do it to honor their Lord. And any followers who eat meat give thanks to God, just like the ones who don't eat meat.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi izɛkpɛ kubùbù ní liwe tile litɔ su i, izɛbitɛ nite si yikɔ ɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Aya su; ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛŋa kidɔ kakaa ɛ, abitɛ lɛ tɔ yikɔ ɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Ɔkaɛ su, lɛ ese linu si izeedo Aya ɖase ní kiŋaŋaɛ litukpo. Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔ́pɛ si yiŋa kiditɔ ɛ, abitɛ lɛ nite tɔ yikɔ ɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Ɔkaɛ su, lɛ izedo Aya ɖase e.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for God, rather than for ourselves.
translation_verse: Blɔmɛ ɔ́tɔtɔ ɔ́ma ligbã́ ɛki isu tètè e, nite ke blɔmɛ ɔ́tɔtɔ oʋítse kí isu ya tètè e.

english_verse: Whether we live or die, it must be for the Lord. Alive or dead, we still belong to the Lord.
translation_verse: Xegi kulɛ ligbã́ ɛ, Ɔkaɛ loso kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ, lɛ xegi kwitse kyɛ ɛ, Ɔkaɛ loso kwitse e, lɛ loso xegi kwitse loo kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, Ɔkaɛ yɛ banɛ kunu.

english_verse: This is because Christ died and rose to life, so that he would be the Lord of the dead and of the living.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya loso Kristo etse, xe ayɔ ɔ, xe edzì ligbã́ a, tɔ aadzi Ɔka ɔki banama gi balɛ ligbã́ a, ni banama gi betse e.

english_verse: Why do you criticize other followers of the Lord? Why do you look down on them? The day is coming when God will judge all of us.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlɔ banama gi siga sɛ ko mlɛzɛŋa ɛ, ege lɛ loso mlɛzɛɛxwa litsi ni mlɔ benemi ba a? Lɛ mlɔ banama gi mlɛzɛŋa kidɔ kakaa ɛ, ege lɛ loso mlɛzɛɛkpɛ mlɔ benemi ba kakpaa ɛ? Mlaplɛ aŋu si blɔ petee kwitralɛɛ ní Aya aŋu mɛ gi aaxwa litsi ni blo o.

english_verse: And so, each of us must give an account to God for what we do. Don't Cause Problems for Others
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, lilɛ si blɔ mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa a, aaki akɔnta ní yɛ isu ya su ki Aya. Mlakíki tɔ mlɔ benemi ba begleʋe

english_verse: We must stop judging others. We must also make up our minds not to upset anyone's faith.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlaki tɔ kumɔni etsi kuxwa ni blɔ ahanuglɔ ba. Boŋu i, mlatrɔ lɛ si mlatrábitɛ kididɔ gi kiaki si mlɔ benemi ba e, biamlima loo tɔ biawɔli ku kuzimizi me e.

english_verse: The Lord Jesus has made it clear to me that God considers all foods fit to eat. But if you think some foods are unfit to eat, then for you they are not fit.
translation_verse: Mɛ likpɔkpɛkpɛ ni Ɔkaɛ Yesu i, liki si maka ní lɛ aʋa si kiŋaŋaɛ tɔtɔ kíma gi kɛsu kídra ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu u; pɔ̀ xe mlɛziŋa si kiŋaŋaɛ tɔ kɛsu kídra ɛ, kɔ kɛsu kítradra ɛki mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: If you are hurting others by the foods you eat, you are not guided by love. Don't let your appetite destroy someone Christ died for.
translation_verse: Xe kiditɔ gi wɔŋa a kikpɛ onemi nɛ kunugu i, kɔlɔ mlaʋíga ní kunɔlɔ mɛ o. Mlakíki si kiŋaŋaɛ liɛ gi mlɛɛŋa ɛ, kizizi ɔnɔ gi yɛ loso Kristo etse e!

english_verse: Don't let your right to eat bring shame to Christ.
translation_verse: Mlakíki kidiliɛ gi mlɛkɔ lɛ si kipɛ ɛ, kizi linyi nyani nyani i.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't about eating and drinking. It is about pleasing God, about living in peace, and about true happiness. All this comes from the Holy Spirit.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ Kaʋaŋaa ɛ, kénu kiŋaŋa loo bidɔŋwɛŋwɛ liboe e, pɔ̀ kenu kude dradra, kusuyɔ ni isami gi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛɛkɔ ɛ.

english_verse: If you serve Christ in this way, you will please God and be respected by people.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso xe banima beesumu Kristo ní kudeo liɔ ya aʋa ɛ, bizeeku Aya aŋu lɛ bizɛɛkɔ si banima biazɛ ba liboe le.

english_verse: We should try to live at peace and help each other have a strong faith.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso lipoe kakaa ɛ, lilɛ si kutí bidɔ bɛklɔ gi bizɛɛmani kusuyɔ ɔ, xe bizɛɛkpɛ blɔ ahanuglɔ ba kigagla ní kuziŋa mɛ.
