english_verse: Don't let your appetite destroy what God has done. All foods are fit to eat, but it is wrong to cause problems for others by what you eat.
translation_verse: Mlakízizi kidiliɛ gi Aya abitɛ ɛ, kɔkpɛ kiŋaŋa loso o. Mlaatani kidɔ kakaa kuŋa, pɔ̀ lípɛ si mlaŋa kidiliɛ gi kiaki si ɔnutɔ aawɔli ku kuzimizi me e.

english_verse: It is best not to eat meat or drink wine or do anything else that causes problems for other followers of the Lord.
translation_verse: Kidɔ gbã ɛliɛ klɔ gi mlaabitɛ ɛ, kɛ kinu si, mlakíta kigyɛ a, ŋwɛ kuda, loo bitɛ kidiliɛ gi kiaki si wɔ onemi nɛ ɔʋaziŋa awɔli i.

english_verse: What you believe about these things should be kept between you and God. You are fortunate, if your actions don't make you have doubts.
translation_verse: Mlabla kidiliɛ aʋa gi mlɛziŋa a, ní liboe lɛ ya su ní mlɔ ni Aya kusi. Basami nɛ benu banama gi baʋibu isu ya lipɔ ɔ, xe bɛbitɛ kidiliɛ gi bɛmɔ si kipɛ ɛ!

english_verse: But if you do have doubts about what you eat, you are going against your beliefs. And you know that is wrong, because anything you do against your beliefs is sin.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi ɔ́ka a, ní kidiliɛ gi ɛɛŋa aʋa ɛ, Aya aabu ye lipɔ xe aŋa kɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si yɛ kiboebitɛ ɛ, kíga kuziŋa mɛ ɛ. Lɛ kidɔ kakaaɛ liɛ gi kíga kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, kinu kuzimizi.

english_verse: If our faith is strong, we should be patient with the Lord's followers whose faith is weak. We should try to please them instead of ourselves.
translation_verse: Blɔ banama gi kulɛ gagla ní kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, lilɛ si kuplɛ litɔ kese ki banama gi báma ɛgagla ní kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, tɔ betu ba adɔtu la. Líma si kuku isu ya aŋu u.

english_verse: We should think of their good and try to help them by doing what pleases them.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, lilɛ si blɔ pɔ́ kuku blɔ benemi ba aŋu ki ba tutɔ ba kupɛ̀pɛ ɛ, tɔ kuki tɔ betsi ní ba kuziŋaa mɛ.

english_verse: Even Christ did not try to please himself. But as the Scriptures say, “The people who insulted you also insulted me.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Kristo kyɛ ɛ, óku isu ya aŋu u. Boŋu i, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi liboe le lipani si i, “Bekpinia ʋa gi bekpini mlo e, bɛdɔ ní mɛ aʋa.”

english_verse: God is the one who makes us patient and cheerful. I pray that he will help you live at peace with each other, as you follow Christ.
translation_verse: Atɔ Aya gi yɛ kɔ litɔplɛplɛkese ni kigagla kpɛkpɛ kizɛɛdɔ ɛ, ɔki mlɔ kugyodi tule gi mlakɔze ni Kristo Yesu yɛ liŋwitsɛ lɛ kude e,

english_verse: Then all of you together will praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ. The Good News Is for Jews and Gentiles
translation_verse: atɔ mlɔ petee mlaakpɛ likpɔ nì ole tole tɔ mlakpɔ̀ Aya, blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ Ɔka. Liboe Gbã nɛ ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ

english_verse: Honor God by accepting each other, as Christ has accepted you.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlaazi mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba, ki Aya yɛ kusudraa, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Kristo azɛ mlɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I tell you that Christ came as a servant of the Jews to show that God has kept the promises he made to their famous ancestors. Christ also came,
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛɛsi mlɔ si Kristo yɛ ligbã́ nɛ mɛ axwɛwawa la ɛ, eze ki Yudanɛ ma tɔ libasɛ si Aya onu anukwarɛ onete, gi izɛɛki si yɛ kakaplɛa a, gi aka aplɛ aki ba bakatsi ba ɛ, kizɛɛba ka mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: so that the Gentiles would praise God for being kind to them. It is just as the Scriptures say, “I will tell the nations about you, and I will sing praises to your name.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bizɛɛki si banama gi bénu Yudanɛ fɛ ɛ, bamɔ kudeonu tɔ bakpɔ̀ Aya kɔkpɛ yɛ asigaga la loso o. Kiliɛ gi liboe le lipani si i, “Lɛ loso maakpɔ wɔ ní Banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma kusi i; Maaza kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ idzi ki wɔ.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say to the Gentiles, “Come and celebrate with God's people.”
translation_verse: Lɛ lipani dzɛ si i, “Mlamɔ isami, mlɔ banama gi mlénu Yudanɛ ɛ; mlamɔ isami mlɔ banama gi mlenu Yadanɛ nì Aya yɛ banima a!”

english_verse: Again the Scriptures say, “Praise the Lord, all you Gentiles. All you nations, come and worship him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ dzɛ ɛ, “Mlakpɔ Ɔkaɛ, mlɔ Banama petee gi mlénu Yudanɛ ɛ; mlakpɔ̀ yɛ, mlɔ banima petee!”

english_verse: Isaiah says, “Someone from David's family will come to power. He will rule the nations, and they will put their hope in him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ dzɛ ɛ, Yesaya apani si, “Yese yɛ osobi aaŋwi; yɛ aaba lɛ aaŋa banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ aʋa, lɛ biakɔ ba kudeonudidi yo trɔ ní yɛ aʋa.”

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives hope, will bless you with complete happiness and peace because of your faith. And may the power of the Holy Spirit fill you with hope. Paul's Work as a Missionary
translation_verse: Atɔ Aya, gi onu kudeonudidi yo ɔdɔklɔ ɛ, ɔki tɔ mleyi nì isami, ni kusuyɔ gani mlɔ kuziŋa ní yɛ mɛ ɛ, tɔ mlɔ kudeonudidi yo kutrɛ ní kɔ aʋa tɔ kutsi gani Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigagla ɛ mɛ. Paulo ebu isu unu kidiliɛ loso gi aŋwlimi keplekpaa lia lifumose e.

english_verse: My friends, I am sure that you are very good and that you have all the knowledge you need to teach each other.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba: Mɛ ɔtutɔ maka ní lɛ aʋa si mlete eboe gbã, lɛ kuboete kakaa kulɛ ní mlɔ awla, lɛ mlɛɛtani mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba bidɔ kubasɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But I have spoken to you plainly and have tried to remind you of some things. God treated me with undeserved grace!
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní keplekpaa lia ya mɛ ɛ, medu aŋu ní eboe atɔ su gi la aʋa maplɛ aŋu aki mlɔ ɛ. Medu aŋu na kɔkpɛ kudeo liɔ onu gi Aya aki mɛ ɛ loso,

english_verse: He chose me to be a servant of Christ Jesus for the Gentiles and to do the work of a priest in the service of his good news. God did this so that the Holy Spirit could make the Gentiles into a holy offering, pleasing to him.
translation_verse: gi menu Kristo Yesu yɛ ɔnɔsumu i, tɔ miwa axwɛ na aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ. Mizeesumu ŋwisi kusumu ɔka kinɛ ɛ, ní kiliɛ gi mizɛɛpani liboe Gbã nɛ ɛ, gi lidɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ, tɔ banama gi bénu Yudanɛ fɛ ɛ, biaaze bidɔkɔkɔ gi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ebu lidze e, xe biaku Aya aŋu i.

english_verse: Because of Christ Jesus, I can take pride in my service for God.
translation_verse: Gi malɛ likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo Yesu loso e, maatani isu kudɔ ní mɛ kusumu yo ki Aya su u.

english_verse: In fact, all I will talk about is how Christ let me speak and work, so that the Gentiles would obey him.
translation_verse: Maapani tètè aŋu mɛ modi ni kidiliɛ gi Kristo abitɛ gani mɛ aʋa a, gi meze ni ɔtɔ ɔ, aki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, tɔ benu kutu aki Aya. Abitɛ lɛ nite gani eboe, ni kiboebitɛ mɛ,

english_verse: Indeed, I will tell how Christ worked miracles and wonders by the power of the Holy Spirit. I have preached the good news about him all the way from Jerusalem to Illyricum.
translation_verse: gani enugutseboe ni eboe mamɔ edi ni gani Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso ní mɛ kudegagaa dɔni Yerusalem kɔtsini Iliriko kesea mɛ ke e, mebu liboe gbã nɛ ní Kristo su abasɛ mɔmɔɔmɔ.

english_verse: But I have always tried to preach where people have never heard about Christ. I am like a builder who doesn't build on someone else's foundation.
translation_verse: Mɛ kupɛpɛɔ kwize lipoe kakaa si mibu liboe gbã nɛ onu ní ikɔɛ lɛ klɔ gi bénu Kristo onu ʋidi i, tɔ mikíwa ɛtrɔ ní ɔxɔ gi ɔnutɔ akpɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: It is just as the Scriptures say, “All who haven't been told about him will see him, and those who haven't heard about him will understand.” Paul's Plan To Visit Rome
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Aya yɛ liboe le me si, “Banama gi básɛ liboeboe ni yɛ su i biaamɔ, lɛ banama gi bénu i biaanu le ese.” Paulo atrɔ si yɛ atrɛ ní Roma

english_verse: My work has always kept me from coming to see you.
translation_verse: Axwɛ ana la etsi kudeonu iiki mɛ lipoe glegle si mikiba mlɔ klɔ.

english_verse: Now there is nothing left for me to do in this part of the world, and for years I have wanted to visit you.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla gi meblo ni mɛ axwɛ na ní besi ba ya ɛ, lɛ gi mazɛɛpɛ ní agɔ glegle mɛ si miba tɔ mimɔ mlɔ ɛ,

english_verse: So I plan to stop off on my way to Spain. Then after a short, but refreshing, visit with you, I hope you will quickly send me on.
translation_verse: meedi kudeonu si miba abla, maapɛ si mimɔ mlɔ xe mɛɛtrɛ ní Spania a, tɔ mlaapɔni mɛ ní mɛ niklɔ kutrɛtrɛ mɛ xegi meze ní mlɔ su taʋɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am now on my way to Jerusalem to deliver the money that the Lord's followers in Macedonia and Achaia collected for God's needy people.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si isɔli ha lɛ ní Makedonia ni Axaya bɛkɔ ɔna faã si biaagyɔ bidɔ tɔ biaakɔ pɔni bazianete ba ní baʋaziŋa ba kusi ní Yerusalem.

english_verse: This is something they really wanted to do. But sharing their money with the Jews was also like paying back a debt, because the Jews had already shared their spiritual blessings with the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Kibitɛɛ liɛ klɔ kidɔ batutɔ ba kɔ; pɔ̀ ni anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, línu ba lixwɛ si bapɔni ba. Gi Yudanɛ ma bɛlɛ ba Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kukriraa nì banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, lilɛ si banama gi bénu Yudanɛ kyɛ bakɔ ba kelea mɛ bidɔmɔmɔ ʋɛ kukriraa tɔ bakɔ apɔni Yudanɛ ma.

english_verse: After I have safely delivered this money, I will visit you and then go on to Spain.
translation_verse: Xegi meblo ni lixwɛ ɛnɛ ya ɛ, xe makɔ bibɔɛ ʋɛ petee gi bɛgyɔ ɛki ba tsini ba ɛ, maasɛ trɛ ní Spania lɛ maabadi mlo ní mɛ kudeo aʋa xe mɛɛtrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And when I do arrive in Rome, I know it will be with the full blessings of Christ.
translation_verse: Xegi maaba mlɔ klɔ ɛ, mete si maaba ni Kristo yɛ kukriraa petee ki mlɔ.

english_verse: My friends, by the power of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the love that comes from the Holy Spirit, I beg you to pray sincerely with me and for me.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba mɛɛkpɛ mlɔ kigagla si, gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo ni kunɔlɔɔ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛɛkɔ mɛ ɛ; mlaapɔni mɛ ní mɛ kuŋwlɛwlɛɔ mɛ gani Aya kuxwaxwa mɛ lipoe kakaa ní mɛ litukpo.

english_verse: Pray that God will protect me from the unbelievers in Judea, and that his people in Jerusalem will be pleased with what I am doing.
translation_verse: Mlaxwa Aya tɔ obu me ní banama gi bázɛ Aya aʋa ŋa ní Yudea ɛ, iwla mɛ, atɔ mɛ axwɛwawa la ní Yerusalem i, eku Aya yɛ banima aŋu ní niklɔ.

english_verse: Ask God to let me come to you and have a pleasant and refreshing visit.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, maaba mlɔ klɔ ni isami glegle xe linu Aya yɛ kupɛpɛ ɛ, tɔ mɛ mlɔ kɔ kubabaa kwiakpɛ blɔ pɔ́ isami i, xe maba ku i.

english_verse: I pray that God, who gives peace, will be with all of you. Amen.
translation_verse: Atɔ Aya, blɔ kusuyɔɔ ɔdɔklɔ, oze ni mlɔ petee. Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: I have good things to say about Phoebe, who is a leader in the church at Cenchreae.
translation_verse: Meebu blɔ onemidzɛɛ Foibe, gi izeesumu ni Kenkrea baʋaziŋa ba ɔha lɔ mɛ ɛ, basɛ mlɔ.

english_verse: Welcome her in a way that is proper for someone who has faith in the Lord and is one of God's own people. Help her in any way you can. After all, she has proved to be a respected leader for many others, including me.
translation_verse: Meeʋi si mlazi yɛ ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ banima biazɛ yɛ ɛ, tɔ mlaki yɛ kupɔnɔ kakaa gi aazia dɔni mlɔ klɔ ɛ; lɛ ese linu si, yɛ ɔtutɔ ɛ, apɔni banɔ glegle kɔpɔni mɛ fɛ.

english_verse: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila. They have not only served Christ Jesus together with me,
translation_verse: Mlapani Priskila ni Akwila gi benu mɛ baxwɛwaglɔnɛ ní Kristo Yesu axwɛ na mɛ ɛ, aki mɛ;

english_verse: but they have even risked their lives for me. I am grateful for them and so are all the Gentile churches.
translation_verse: bɛkpɛ ba ligbã́ nɛ ní kuxixa mɛ ní mɛ litukpo. Medo ba ɖase, aní mɛ tete, pɔ̀ isɔlihaɛ lɛ petee gi ínu Yudanɛ kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Greet the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who was the first person in Asia to have faith in Christ.
translation_verse: Mlapani baʋaziŋa ba ɔha lɔ klɔ gi izɛɛtsa ní ba kepea mɛ ɛ. Mapani mɛ ɔʋia vevee Apaineto, gi yɛ eze ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ ní Asia kesea aʋa gi azɛ Kristo aʋa aŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
translation_verse: Mlapani Maria, gi yɛ awa axwɛ gagla ni mlɔ kusi i.

english_verse: Greet my relatives Andronicus and Junia, who were in jail with me. They are highly respected by the apostles and were followers of Christ before I was.
translation_verse: Mlapani Androniko ni Yunio gi benu Yudanɛ ŋwisi mɛ kinɛ ɛ, xe beze ní ligba pɔni mɛ ɛ; benu banɔ tètè ní banɔxwaxwa ba kusi, lɛ bedzi baʋaziŋa kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛki mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend whose faith is in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mlapani Ampliatus, gi madimɛnɔ ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me e.

english_verse: Greet Urbanus, who serves Christ along with us. Greet my dear friend Stachys.
translation_verse: Mlapani Urbano fɛ, blɔ ɔhanɛ ɔxwɛwa ní Kristo ɔha lɔ mɛ, ani Staxis mɛ ɔʋia vevee.

english_verse: Greet Apelles, a faithful servant of Christ. Greet Aristobulus and his family.
translation_verse: Mlapani Apeles, ɔniliɛ gi yɛ isu kukɔ ki Kristo e, kwimɔ si kúʋikyi i. Mlapani banama petee gi benu Aristobulo yɛ baŋanɛ ba ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Herodion, who is a relative of mine. Greet Narcissus and the others in his family, who have faith in the Lord.
translation_verse: Mlapani Herodion gi yɛ fɛ onu Yudanɛ kɔpɔni mɛ ɛ, ani baʋaziŋa ba ní Narkiso yɛ okú no me, gi benu baʋaziŋa ni Ɔkaɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who work hard for the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis. She also works hard for the Lord.
translation_verse: Mlapani Trifena ni Trifosa, badzɛ gi bɛɛwa Ɔkaɛ axwɛ na ɛ, ani ɔdzɛɛ Persis mɛ ɔʋia vevee, gi awa axwɛ glegle aki Ɔkaɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Rufus, that special servant of the Lord, and greet his mother, who has been like a mother to me.
translation_verse: Mlapani Rufo, gi Aya akya si yɛ axwɛwawa la ɛki Ɔkaɛ ɛ, ɛdzi aŋu mɛ, ani yene gi akɔ mɛ ŋwisi yebinyime kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, and Hermas, as well as our friends who are with them.
translation_verse: Mlapani Asinkrito, Flego, Herme, Patroba, Herma, ni baʋaziŋa dzedze ba gi bɛpɔni ba ɛ.

english_verse: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all of God's people who are with them.
translation_verse: Mlapani Filologo ni Yulia, kupaniɔ kwitrɛ ki Nerea ni yenemidzɛ, ki Olimpa ani ki Aya yɛ banima gi balɛ pɔni ba ɛ.

english_verse: Be sure to give each other a warm greeting. All of Christ's churches greet you.
translation_verse: Mlakɔ ɔna dradra akpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba wolaa tɔ mlapani ba, isɔli ha lɛ petee ni Kristo me e, ipani mlɔ. Batrɔtrɔ gɔgɔ ʋa

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to watch out for anyone who causes trouble and divides the church by refusing to do what all of you were taught. Stay away from them!
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba mɛɛgba mlɔ; tɔ mlevu aŋu ní banama gi bizɛɛmani kulɛlɛ ɛ, xe bizɛɛpisa banima kuziŋaa a, xe bizɛɛyɔ kedu ní kudɔbasɛ uliɔ gi kwibasɛ mlɔ su i. Mladzi kede ní ba su!

english_verse: They want to serve themselves and not Christ the Lord. Their flattery and fancy talk fool people who don't know any better.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si banɔ baklɔ gi bizɛɛbitɛ eboa la elu i, aní Kristo blɔ Ɔkaɛ beesumu, pɔ̀ batutɔ ba epò la beesumu u. Bizɛɛkɔ ba eboe panipani la ni kinemi gyigyi emeni banama gi béte liboeboe e.

english_verse: I am glad that everyone knows how well you obey the Lord. But still, I want you to understand what is good and not have anything to do with evil.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa enu mlɔ kutununu yo kí liboe le onu. Lɛ kɔkpɛ liboe lɛ ya loso e, mamɔ isami ni mlɔ. Mapɛ si mlete liboe ní kidiliɛ gi kipɛɛ su u, pɔ̀ mápɛ si mlɔ aŋu adra ní kuzimizi su u.

english_verse: Then God, who gives peace, will soon crush Satan under your feet. I pray that our Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya gi yɛ onu blɔ kusuyɔ ɔdɔklɔ ɛ, aakpɛ Satana ní mlɔ kukliseo tɔ mlaaŋa yɛ aʋa kpekpemi. Blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lize ni mlo.

english_verse: Timothy, who works with me, sends his greetings, and so do my relatives, Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater.
translation_verse: Timoteo, gi onu mɛ ɔxwɛwa hanuglɔ ɛ, apani mlɔ; nite ke Lukio, Yason ani Sosipatro blɔ akɔanɛ Yudanɛ ma kyɛ bɛpani mlɔ.

english_verse: I, Tertius, also send my greetings. I am a follower of the Lord, and I wrote this letter.
translation_verse: Mɛ Tertio ɔniliɛ gi aŋwlimi keplekpaa lia kyɛ ɛ, mapani mlɔ ní Ɔkaɛ linyi ne me.

english_verse: Praise God! He can make you strong by means of my good news, which is the message about Jesus Christ. For ages and ages this message was kept secret,
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mlaki tɔ kukɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ aki Aya! Aatani kukɔ si mlalɛɛ gagla ní mlɔ kuziŋaa mɛ, kɔkpɛ liboe gbã nɛ gi mapani aki mlɔ ní Yesu Kristo su i, kɔkpɛ eboe kpokpo la gi Aya ebu abasɛ ɛ, eboa la gi bɛkɔ ekpo lipoe gblamadza a, gi liza ɛ.

english_verse: but now at last it has been told. The eternal God commanded his prophets to write about the good news, so that all nations would obey and have faith.
translation_verse: Abla kɔ ɛ, anukwarɛɛ edo oŋwe no gani bidɔ mɛ gi beboedoplɛ ba bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ mɛ; lɛ gani batrɔtrɔ ʋa gi Aya a, gi oʋítse e, akpɛ ní mɛ ɛ. Aya ebu le abasɛ amade na petee, tɔ ɔnɔ kakaa aaziŋa tɔ aabitɛ ní lɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: And now, because of Jesus Christ, we can praise the only wise God forever! Amen.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kukpɔ̀ Aya tolee, gi yɛ tètè ko ete liboe kakaa ɛ, mlakɔ kusudra aki yɛ gani Yesu Kristo aʋa kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Christ Jesus, and from Sosthenes, who is also a follower.
translation_verse: Keplekpaa lia kɛdɔ ní mɛ Paulo, gi bɛxwa a, gani Aya yɛ kupɛ́pɛ onu si mize Yesu Kristo yɛ ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ ani dɔni blɔ onemi ye Sostenes klɔ.

english_verse: My prayer is that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Tɔ Aya blɔ Ɔka ni Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo baki mlɔ lipomepɛpɛ ni kusuyɔ. Paulo edo Aya ɖase

english_verse: I never stop thanking my God for treating you with undeserved grace by giving you Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Mizeedo Aya ɖase lipoe kakaa ní mlɔ litukpo kɔkpɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi aki mlɔ ɔ, gani Kristo Yesu aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Now you are certain that everything we told you about our Lord Christ Jesus is true.
translation_verse: Aya atrɔ likla lɛ aʋa aki mlɔ si, liboe lɛ gi kwisi mlɔ ní Kristo su i, linu anukwarɛ.

english_verse: And until the day Christ does return, he will keep you completely innocent.
translation_verse: Yɛ aakpɛ mlɔ kigagla zɛkɔna ni ni ɔgù klɔ ɔ, gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trabu mlɔ lipɔ ɔ liwe lɛ gi blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo aadzi ba ɛ.

english_verse: God can be trusted, and he chose you to be partners with his Son, our Lord Jesus Christ. Taking Sides
translation_verse: Aya gi onu anukwarɛ onete e, yɛ axwa mlɔ si mleze likpɔ nì Yebi ye Yesu Kristo blɔ Ɔkaɛ. Kulɛlɛ ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ mɛ

english_verse: My dear friends, as a follower of our Lord Jesus Christ, I beg you to get along with each other. Don't take sides. Always try to agree in what you think.
translation_verse: Meeʋi idɔni mlɔ klɔ mɛ benemi ba gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo linyi ne me si, mlenu mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba ese e. Mlakíki si kulɛlɛ kuba mlɔ kusi i. Boŋu i, mlakpɛ likpɔ nì kugyodi tule, mleze likpɔ ní kugyodi tule ní kutrɔtrɔ tule me.

english_verse: Several people from Chloe's family have already reported to me that you keep arguing with each other.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, mɛ benemi ba, banɔatɔ gi bɛdɔ Kloe yɛ Kepea mɛ ɛ, bɛsi mɛ si eboe hehe alɛ ní mlɔ kusi.

english_verse: They have said that some of you claim to follow me, while others claim to follow Apollos or Peter or Christ.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani i, lɛ linu si mlɔ mɛ ɔnutɔ ɛɛpani si; “Mɛ malɛ ni Paulo kude,” ɔtɔ fɛ si, “Malɛ ni Apolo kude,” loo si, “Malɛ ni Petro kude,” lɛ ɔtɔ kyɛ si ì, “Malɛ ni Kristo tètè kude.”

english_verse: I thank God that I didn't baptize any of you except Crispus and Gaius.
translation_verse: Medo Aya ɖase si mákpɛ kuni iiki mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔtɔ sani Krispo ani Gayo ko o.

english_verse: Not one of you can say that you were baptized in my name.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kupani si, bɛkpɛ kuni iiki ba ní mɛ linyi ne me e.

english_verse: I did baptize the family of Stephanas, but I don't remember if I baptized anyone else.
translation_verse: Oo, ma plɛ aŋu si, makpɛ kuni akí Stefana nì yɛ kepea, pɔ máplɛ aŋu xe makpɛ kuni aki ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Christ did not send me to baptize. He sent me to tell the good news without using words that sound wise and would make the cross of Christ lose its power. Christ Is God's Power and Wisdom
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Kristo ɔ́tsisi mɛ si tɔ mizɛɛkpɛ kuni ɔki banɔ ɔ, boŋu i tɔ mizɛɛpani yɛ liboe Gbã nɛ. Lɛ aní ni ɔnɔ gbagbee yɛ kuboete onu atɔ gani lɛ mɛ ɛ, osetsase lo aalila lɔ kigagla ɛ. Kristo yɛ onu Aya yɛ kuboeteo ni yɛ kigaglaɛ

english_verse: The message about the cross doesn't make any sense to lost people. But for those of us who are being saved, it is God's power at work.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si osetsase lo su liboe le e, linu ligaboe kí banama gi bɛɛlila ɛ; pɔ kí blɔ banama gi beebu i, lɛ linu Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ.

english_verse: As God says in the Scriptures, “I will destroy the wisdom of all who claim to be wise. I will confuse those who think they know so much.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si bɛŋwlimi nɛ ɛplɛ si, “Maazizi beboete ba ba kuboete o lɛ maabu basɛ si beplekpate ba ba kuboesenu yo kúnu kididɔ.”

english_verse: What happened to those wise people? What happened to those experts in the Scriptures? What happened to the ones who think they have all the answers? Didn't God show that the wisdom of this world is foolish?
translation_verse: Kɔ nifɔ oboete ye ɔlɛ ɛ? Lɛ nifɔ oplekpate ye ɔlɛ ɛ? Lɛ nifɔ ɔniliɛ gi izeehe eboe la ní omono kelea mɛ ɔlɛ ɛ? Aya abasɛ si kelea lia ya mɛ kuboeteo e kunu linukpaboe!

english_verse: God was wise and decided not to let the people of this world use their wisdom to learn about him. Instead, God chose to save only those who believe the foolish message we preach.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya ní yɛ kuboeteo me e, abitɛ lɛ si banima bakíte ye gani batutɔ ba kuboeteo me, boŋu i, liku yɛ liŋu si gani anukpaboe ala gi kwipani mɛ ɛ, yɛ aabu banama gi bɛziŋa ɛ.

english_verse: Jews ask for miracles, and Greeks want something that sounds wise.
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ma bɛɛpɛ enugutseboe lɛ Helanɛ ma kɔ ɛ bɛɛpɛ kelea mɛ kuboeteo.

english_verse: But we preach that Christ was nailed to a cross. Most Jews have problems with this, and most Gentiles think it is foolish.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kwipani ní Kristo gi bɛkla ní ose lo su u: linu liboe gi liitu Yudanɛ ma ina nɛ, lɛ linu linukpaboe ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ɛ.

english_verse: Our message is God's power and wisdom for the Jews and the Greeks that he has chosen.
translation_verse: Pɔ ki banama gi Aya axwa ɛ, ki Yudanɛ ma ni Helanɛ ma ɛ, liboe lɛ ya linu Kristo, gi yɛ onu Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ ni Aya yɛ kuboeteo.

english_verse: Even when God is foolish, he is wiser than everyone else, and even when God is weak, he is stronger than everyone else.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi kinu linukpaboe kí Aya ɛ, kinu kuboete gluglu i sani ɔnɔ gbagbe yɛ kuboeteo, lɛ niklɔ gi Aya ebotso e, niklɔ ɔlɛ gagla sani ɔnɔ gbagbe yɛ kigaglaɛ.

english_verse: My dear friends, remember what you were when God chose you. The people of this world didn't think that many of you were wise. Only a few of you were in places of power, and not many of you came from important families.
translation_verse: Benemi ba, mlaplɛ aŋu kiliɛ gi mleeze e, xe Aya axwa mlɔ ɛ. Ní banima kugyodi yo onu i, mlɔ me banɔ glegle mlɔ aŋu ádra, lɛ ozekɔ óze ní mlɔ mɛ beglegle ba awla a, lɛ mládɔ kupe ʋidi me kyɛ.

english_verse: But God chose the foolish things of this world to put the wise to shame. He chose the weak things of this world to put the powerful to shame.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya akya kelea lia mɛ banama gi báma liboeboe me e, tɔ yikɔ ba kpɛ beboete ba kuye, lɛ akya kela lia mɛ bidɔ bɛklɔ gi kigagla kima bɛ awla ɛ, si yikɔ bɛ ɛkpɛ banama gi bebu si bali gagla ɛ, kuye e.

english_verse: What the world thinks is worthless, useless, and nothing at all is what God has used to destroy what the world considers important.
translation_verse: Akya kidiliɛ gi kelea mɛ kébu si kinu kididɔ ɛ, bɛ klɔ gi bɛkpɛ kakpaa ɛ, tɔ yikɔ bɛ zizi kidiliɛ gi kelea mɛ kebu si kiliɛ veve e.

english_verse: God did all this to keep anyone from bragging to him.
translation_verse: Atɔ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkitani isu kudɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: You are God's children. He sent Christ Jesus to save us and to make us wise, acceptable, and holy.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ yɛ loso mlalɛ ní Kristo Yesu me, gi yɛ edzì kuboeteo aki blɔ dɔni Aya klɔ ɔ. Yɛ onu blɔ kude gyɔgyɔɔ, blɔ kusudraa, ani blɔ kubúbú yo.

english_verse: So if you want to brag, do what the Scriptures say and brag about the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi nɛ ɛplɛ ɛ, “Ɔniliɛ gi apɛ si yidɔ isu i, ɔdɔ isu ya ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Friends, when I came and told you the mystery that God had shared with us, I didn't use big words or try to sound wise.
translation_verse: Benemi ba, agi maba mlɔ klɔ ɔ si mipani Aya yɛ liboe kpòkpò le si mlɔ ɛ, máwa enugumeboe ʋidi ni keplekpa mɛ kuboete ʋidi su axwɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In fact, while I was with you, I made up my mind to speak only about Jesus Christ, who had been nailed to a cross.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi meze ní mlɔ su i, matrɔ lɛ si mátrate kididɔ sani Yesu Kristo o, gi beku ní ose lo su u.

english_verse: At first, I was weak and trembling with fear.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi maba mlɔ klɔ ɛ, kigagla kíze ní mɛ su, lɛ mazeesugu ni lifu glegle e.

english_verse: When I talked with you or preached, I didn't try to prove anything by sounding wise. I simply let God's Spirit show his power.
translation_verse: Mɛ kunugu yo ni mɛ liboedodo le e, bíga eboeteboe ni eboe gi aameni banɔ onu me e, boŋu i, kidiliɛ gi mabitɛ ɛ, kibasɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ lɔ kigagla ɛ.

english_verse: That way you would have faith because of God's power and not because of human wisdom.
translation_verse: tɔ mlɔ kuziŋaa kukíze ní kelea mɛ kuboeteo aʋa a, boŋu i, tɔ kwiaze ní Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ. Kuboete dɔni Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ

english_verse: We do use wisdom when speaking to people who are mature in their faith. But it isn't the wisdom of this world or of its rulers, who will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kwipani eboeteboe ki banama gi betsi ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ aní kelea lia mɛ kuboete aloo baʋaŋaa ba a, gi bɛɛŋa kelea aʋa a, gi ba onu ɛɛtraza ɛ.

english_verse: We speak of God's hidden and mysterious wisdom that God decided to use for our glory long before the world began.
translation_verse: Owo, kwipani ní Aya yɛ kuboete kpòkpò su, kuboete gi bɛkɔ ɛ kpò o, gi kɔ Aya akya a aplɛ aki blɔ kusudraa a, xe ableke kelea kekpese e.

english_verse: The rulers of this world didn't know anything about this wisdom. If they had known about it, they would not have nailed the glorious Lord to a cross.
translation_verse: Kelea lia mɛ baʋaŋa ba ɔtɔtɔ ónu kɔ ese e, xegi nya benu kɔ ese e, nya bákla kuklekleo Ɔkaɛ ní ose lo su u.

english_verse: But it is just as the Scriptures say, “What God has planned for people who love him is more than eyes have seen or ears have heard. It has never even entered our minds!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ, “Kimbi kímɔ ɔ, lɛ kutu fɛ kúnu u, lɛ ɔ́nɔnɔ oʋígyo le edi i, kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya abla aplɛ aki banama gi bɛlɔ yɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: God's Spirit has shown you everything. His Spirit finds out everything, even what is deep in the mind of God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya ebu le abasi blɔ gani Yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ aʋa a. Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛɛpamí kidɔ kakaa, Aya yɛ bidɔ dzudzu kpokpo ʋe fɛ.

english_verse: You are the only one who knows what is in your own mind, and God's Spirit is the only one who knows what is in God's mind.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si nyaŋwɛ ɔlɛ mlɔ kusi i, gi aatè yɛ ɔhanuglɔ yɛ begyodi ʋa sani ɔniliɛ klɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ; niteme ke e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trate Aya yɛ begyodi ʋa sani yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But God has given us his Spirit. This is why we don't think the same way that the people of this world think. This is also why we can recognize the blessings God has given us.
translation_verse: Kúzɛ kelea lia ya mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ; boŋu i, kwizɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ ɔnɔ gi Aya atsisi i, tɔ kwiate kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya akɔ akyɛ blɔ faã ɛ.

english_verse: Every word we speak was taught to us by God's Spirit, not by human wisdom. And this same Spirit helps us teach spiritual things to spiritual people.
translation_verse: Eboa la ya kwiipani, lɛ enu eboe gi kelea lia ya mɛ kuboeteo kwibasɛ blɔ ɔ, boŋu i, enu eboe gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛbasɛ blɔ ɔ gi ɛɛbasɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ anukwarɛ gani Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ eboe la aʋa a.

english_verse: This is why only someone who has God's Spirit can understand spiritual blessings. Anyone who doesn't have God's Spirit thinks these blessings are foolish.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ awla gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔma ɛ, ɔ́tratani bidɔ gi bidɔ ní Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ ɛ kuzɛ, lɛ ese linu si binu anukpaboe aki yɛ, lɛ ɔ́tratani bɛ ese kunu u, lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ko biaga, xe bianu bɛ ese e.

english_verse: People who are guided by the Spirit can make all kinds of judgments, but they cannot be judged by others.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, izɛta liboe kakaa odi i, pɔ̀ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani yɛ kuta odi i.

english_verse: The Scriptures ask, “Has anyone ever known the thoughts of the Lord or given him advice?” But we understand what Christ is thinking.
translation_verse: “Nyaŋwɛ ete Ɔkaɛ yɛ begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ? xe aatani yɛ bidɔ kubasɛ ɛ?” Pɔ̀ blɔ xunyɔ ɛ, Kristo yɛ begyodi ʋa bali ní blɔ awla.

english_verse: My friends, you are acting like the people of this world. That's why I could not speak to you as spiritual people. You are like babies as far as your faith in Christ is concerned.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, mátratani kupani ki mlɔ ŋwisi banɔ gi balɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, lize si mipani ki mlɔ ŋwisi kelea mɛ banɔ kinɛ ɛ, ŋwisi bebi ní Kristo me kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So I had to treat you like babies and feed you milk. You could not take solid food, and you still cannot,
translation_verse: Magani mlɔ nì kumwani yɔ, aní ni bidɔ ŋaŋa nyipu ʋe e, lɛ ese linu si mlábla aki bidɔ nyipu kuŋaŋa a. Ni abla kyɛ ɛ, mlamá kublabla mɛ aki ʋɛ ɛ.

english_verse: because you are not yet spiritual. You are jealous and argue with each other. This proves you are not spiritual and you are acting like the people of this world.
translation_verse: Lɛ mledzi alɛ ní kelea mɛ ko. Lɛ ese linu si kaŋuza ni avitɛ na alɛ ní mlɔ kusi i, kɔ aní banama gi bɛkɔ ba aŋu na ɛtrɔ ni kelea mɛ bidɔ mɛ aʋa ko mledzi enu i? Mlɔ kiboebitɛ kiibasɛ si kelea me banɔ ko mledzi enu u.

english_verse: Some of you say you follow me, and others claim to follow Apollos. Isn't this how ordinary people behave?
translation_verse: Xe mlɔ mɛ tole apani si, “Malɛ nì Paulo kude e” xe ɔʋla yɛ kyɛ apani si “Malɛ ni Apolo kude e” kɔ aní kelea mɛ banɔ ko mlenu i?

english_verse: Apollos and I are merely servants who helped you to have faith. It was the Lord who made it all happen.
translation_verse: Nyaŋwɛ onu Apolo o? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ onu Paulo o? Aya yɛ banɔsumu ko kunu, lɛ gani blɔ aʋa mlɛziŋa a. Blɔ mɛ ɔkakaa izɛɛwa lixwɛ nɛ klɔ gi Ɔkaɛ aki yɛ si ɔwa ɛ.

english_verse: I planted the seeds, Apollos watered them, but God made them sprout and grow.
translation_verse: Makpɛ lisebi le, Apolo yɛ akpɛ lɛ kuni yo, pɔ̀ Aya yɛ aki si lisebi le litsi i.

english_verse: The one who plants is just as important as the one who waters. And each one will be paid for what they do.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi yɛ akpɛ lisebi le ni ɔniliɛ klɔ gi yɛ akpɛ lɛ kuni yo e, kutrɔtrɔ tule ko kuli ní ba awla, lɛ ɔkakaa azɛ yɛ liʋɛ tsatsa lɛ ní yɛ axwɛ ana gi awa onu u.

english_verse: Apollos and I work together for God, and you are God's garden and God's building. Only One Foundation
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kunu Aya yɛ baxwɛwaglɔnɛ lɛ mlɔ mlenu Aya yɛ ɔnyrɔ nɔ, lɛ mlɔ mlenu yɛ ligba lɛ gi ɛɛwa a.

english_verse: God treated me with undeserved grace and let me become an expert builder. I laid a foundation on which others have built. But we must each be careful how we build,
translation_verse: Ni Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛɛ nɛ gi aki mɛ onu i, makpɛ ɔxɔ lɔ ŋwisi ɔgbawa gbã kinɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ dzedze ɛɛwa ɛtrɔ ɛ. Pɔ̀ lilɛ si ɔnɔ kakaa odi mumɔ kiliɛ gi ɛɛwa ɛtrɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Whatever we build on this foundation will be tested by fire on the day of judgment. Then everyone will find out if we have used gold, silver, and precious stones, or wood, hay, and straw.
translation_verse: yɛ lixwɛ nɛ liaŋwi, lɛ ese linu si liwe le liabu le kubasɛ ɛ. Ní Litsixwawe le aʋa ɛ, kifu yɛ kiabu ɔnɔ kakaa yɛ axwɛwawa la kubasɛ; kifu yɛ kiabasɛ lixwɛ nɛ gi ɔnɔ kakaa awa ɛ, la kigbãɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We will be rewarded if our building is left standing.
translation_verse: Xegi kidiliɛ gi awa kitani kulɛ ɛ, ní kifu yɛ onu i, ɔgbawa yɛ aazi yɛ liʋɛ nɛ.

english_verse: But if it is destroyed by the fire, we will lose everything. Yet we ourselves will be saved, like someone escaping from flames.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ yɛ axwɛwawa la ɛkani i, kɔlɔ aalila yɛ liʋɛ nɛ; pɔ̀ yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, aamɔ kubúbú, ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi aga kifu yɛ mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All of you surely know you are God's temple and his Spirit lives in you.
translation_verse: Kimɔ mlaʋímɔ egyesi si mlɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, mlɔ mlenu Aya yɛ Lisɔligba lɛ na a? Lɛ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔkpasi ní mlɔ mɛ na?

english_verse: Together you are God's holy temple, and God will destroy anyone who destroys his temple.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔnutɔ aazizi Aya yɛ Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, Aya aazizi ɔniliɛ klɔ ʋa. Lɛ Aya yɛ Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, lɛ su lidra, lɛ mlɔ mlenu yɛ Lisɔligba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves! If any of you think you are wise in the things of this world, you will have to become foolish before you can be truly wise.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́kimeni yɛ isu ya a. Xe ɔnutɔ ní mlɔ kusi ebu si yete liboe ní kelea lia ya mɛ kilɛlɛɛ onu i, odzi “kanukpaʋi” tɔ aatani oboete ɔtutɔ kudzi i.

english_verse: This is because God considers the wisdom of this world to be foolish. It is just as the Scriptures say, “God catches the wise when they try to outsmart him.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kelea lia ya mɛ kuboeteo kunu kuga ní Aya aŋu mɛ. Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ si, “Aya izɛpunu beboete ba evu ní ba kuboeteo me.”

english_verse: The Scriptures also say, “The Lord knows that the plans made by wise people are useless.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛŋwlimi ni ɔkɔ dzedze dzɛ si i, “Ɔkaɛ ete beboete ba begyodi ʋa; ete si bénu kididɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: So stop bragging about what anyone has done. Paul and Apollos and Peter all belong to you. In fact, everything is yours, including the world, life, death, the present, and the future. Everything belongs to you,
translation_verse: Xegi Paulo o, Apolo loo Petro o; loo kelea lia ya mɛ o, ligbã́ loo kutse o, abla loo ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ o, bɛ petee binu mlɔ binɛ.

english_verse: and you belong to Christ, and Christ belongs to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlenu Kristo banɛ ɛ, lɛ Kristo onu Aya yɛ ɔnɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Think of us as servants of Christ who have been given the work of explaining God's mysterious ways.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso lilɛ si i, banima bamɔ blɔ si kunu Kristo yɛ banɔsumu, ni banɔ gi ba awla akɔ Aya yɛ eboe kpokpo la akpɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And since our first duty is to be faithful to the one we work for,
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kidiliɛ gi Aya aziã adɔ ni banɔ ba klɔ awla a, gi akpɛ yɛ eboe kpokpo la ɛ, kɛ kinu si liziã si babasɛ si benu anukwarɛ benete.

english_verse: it doesn't matter to me if I am judged by you or even by a court of law. In fact, I don't judge myself.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mákɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ, xe mlɛxwa litsi ni me e, loo xe bɛxwa litsi ni me ní kelea lia ya mɛ otsixwa kɔ ɔtɔtɔ ɔ; ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, maʋíxwa litsi ni mɛ isu ya kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I don't know of anything against me, but this doesn't prove I am right. The Lord is my judge.
translation_verse: Mɛ begyodi ʋa mɛ bɛdra, pɔ̀ aní lɛ libasɛ si mɛ linɛ ligyɔ ɔ. Ɔkaɛ yɛ izɛɛwxa litsi nì me e.

english_verse: So don't judge anyone until the Lord returns. He will show what is hidden in the dark and what is in everyone's heart. Then God will be the one who praises each of us.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlakíxwa litsi ni ɔnɔnɔ ɔ, xe ableke lipoe le liana a; mlepe Ɔkaɛ yɛ kubabaa. Yɛ aabu eboa la gi bekpo ní litumu ne me manɔ ni kuklekleo onu u, lɛ aabu banima ba begyodi ʋa a, gi bàkpasi ní ba ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, ku basɛ. Lɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ kakaa aazi yɛ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ɔ dɔni Aya klɔ.

english_verse: Friends, I have used Apollos and myself as examples to teach you the meaning of the saying, “Follow the rules.” I want you to stop saying one of us is better than the other.
translation_verse: Abla benemi ba, kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso e, makɔ la ya petee akɔ asrɔ ni Apolo ni mɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ mɛ isu ya, atɔ mlaanu liboe lɛ ya ese aki mlɔ kupɛ̀pɛ si, “Mlakídɔ kidiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ, ɛza.” Mlɔ mɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkifeke isu ní ɔnɔ tole ɔtɔtɔ loso o, tɔ aaplɛ ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ ní kese e.

english_verse: What is so special about you? What do you have that you were not given? And if it was given to you, how can you brag?
translation_verse: Nyaŋwɛ abitɛ mlɔ si mlɛga lidze ní banɔ dzedze ba su u? Lɛ ege kilɛ ní mlɔ awla a, gi aní kiki bɛki mlɔ kɛ ɛ? Lɛ xegi kiki bɛki mlɔ kɛ ɛ, ege lɛ loso mlɛdɔ isu ya ŋwisi aní kiki bɛki mlɔ kɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Are you already satisfied? Are you now rich? Have you become kings while we are still nobodies? I wish you were kings. Then we could have a share in your kingdom.
translation_verse: Kidɔ kakaa gi mlɛzia ɛ, kilí ní mlɔ awla kooko. Mledzi bexonete kooko! Lɛ mlɛbitɛ isu ya kokoo si mledzi bekusi i, ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi blɔ kɔ ɛ, kuʋídzi bekusi i. Nya maapɛ si mlanya enu bekusi kokoo tɔ blɔ kyɛ kwiaze bekusi ni mlo!

english_verse: It seems to me that God has put us apostles in the worst possible place. We are like prisoners on their way to death. Angels and the people of this world just laugh at us.
translation_verse: Lɛ libitɛ ɛki mɛ si Aya akɔ blɔ yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba akɔ ŋwisi banɔ gi bɛtsá ní ɔga nɔ lɔ ɔgɔgɔ klɔ ní line ne me e, banɔ gi bɛtsa kutsépia ekɔ kinɛ ɛ. Abitɛ blɔ si kwidzi bidɔdidi ki kelea petee, kwidzi bidɔdidi iiki Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba ni banima pɔ́ ɔ.

english_verse: Because of Christ we are thought of as fools, but Christ has made you wise. We are weak and hated, but you are powerful and respected.
translation_verse: Kwidzi kunukpaʋi kɔkpɛ Kristo loso; pɔ̀ mlenu beboete ní Kristo me! Kwibotso, pɔ̀ mlɔ kɔɔ mlalɛ gagla a! Bɛkpɛ kubùbù ní mlɔ su, pɔ̀ blɔ kɔ ɛ, bedi blɔ aŋu mɛ!

english_verse: Even today we go hungry and thirsty and don't have anything to wear except rags. We are mistreated and don't have a place to live.
translation_verse: Gakɔna ni abla ɛ, kuzɛɛza kuʋɛ lɛ olile izeku blo o; kukpasi ní asiapɛ mɛ; banima bizekusi blo, lɛ kupe kúma ní blɔ awla a.

english_verse: We work hard with our own hands, and when people abuse us, we wish them well. When we suffer, we are patient.
translation_verse: Kuzɛɛwa axwɛ gagla ni blɔ awla la. Xegi bexo ifi le kpɛ blɔ ɛ, kuzɛɛkrira; xegi bɛyɔ kedu ní blɔ su i, kuzɛɛlɛɛ ní lɛ onu u.

english_verse: When someone curses us, we answer with kind words. Until now we are thought of as nothing more than the trash and garbage of this world.
translation_verse: Xegi bezizi blɔ su i, kuzebu le onu nì ɔnà gbã. Gakɔna ni abla ɛ, kwidzi simlu iiki kelea mɛ, kwidzi sivu se ɔ̀bali kɔ ɛki kelea mɛ.

english_verse: I am not writing to embarrass you. I want to help you, just as parents help their own dear children.
translation_verse: Máʋiŋwlimi eboa la ya si míkɔ la ɛkpɛ mlɔ kuye e, pɔ̀ tɔ migba mlɔ ŋwisi mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ bebi gi malɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Ten thousand people may teach you about Christ, but I am your only father. You became my children when I told you about Christ Jesus,
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi baʋadi akpe liɔfɔ bali ní mlɔ awla ní Kristo me kyɛ ɛ, Ɔka tole kpoo ko ɔlɛ ní mlɔ awla. Lɛ ní Kristo Yesu me e, medzi mlɔ ɔka gi ma mani mlɔ Liboe Gbã nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and I want you to be like me.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛɛkpɛ mlɔ sise si mlakpasi mɛ kizezee me.

english_verse: This is why I sent Timothy to you. I love him like a son, and he is a faithful servant of the Lord. Timothy will tell you what I do to follow Christ and how it agrees with what I always teach about Christ in every church.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ lɛ ya loso e, mɛɛtsisi Timoteo, gi onu mɛ obi ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ gi malɔ ɛ, xe onu anukwarɛɛ onete ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ ɛ, ní mlɔ klɔ. Yɛ aaplɛ aŋu kí mlɔ, mɛ bede ʋa ya kude gi mezeno ní mɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní Kristo Yesu me e, gi lisrɔ ni kidiliɛ ɛ, gi mabasɛ ní isɔliha lɛ mɛ ní ɔkɔ kakaa ɛ.

english_verse: Some of you think I am not coming for a visit, and so you are bragging.
translation_verse: Mlɔ mɛ banɔatɔ mlekpese isu kufeke gi mlɛbu si mátraba edi mlo tɔ ɛ.

english_verse: But if the Lord lets me come, I will soon be there. Then I will find out if the ones who are doing all this bragging really have any power.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe Ɔkaɛ adimɛ ɛ, maaba mlɔ klɔ kpekpemi, lɛ niklɔ maate kigaglaɛ liɛ klɔ gi kiliɛ ní banɔ basudɔ ba ya ʋa awla a, ní kiliɛ gi beegu kunugu yo su u.

english_verse: God's kingdom isn't just a lot of words. It is power.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa ɛ kénu enugumeboe, pɔ̀ enu kigagla eboe e.

english_verse: What do you want me to do when I arrive? Do you want me to be hard on you or to be kind and gentle?
translation_verse: Liwoli mlɛpɛ́ ɛ? Miba mlɔklɔ ni kiseʋi loo miba ni kulɔlɔ ni Ɔfuɛfuɛ gi ɛplɛ litɔ kese na a?

english_verse: I have heard terrible things about some of you. In fact, you are behaving worse than Gentiles. A man is even sleeping with his own stepmother.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, bɛtrɔ likla ní kunutsatsa aʋa si kihasi ye kikpasi ní mlɔ kusi gbɛgbɛ nite si babubitɛ ba kura kyɛ ɛ, líma ní ba kusi nite e. Bɛsi mɛ si onyime ɔtɔ ɛɛfɛ ni yɛkadze!

english_verse: You are proud, when you ought to feel bad enough to chase away anyone who acts like this.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlɛkɔ lɛ dɔ isu ya a? Aní asigaga mɛ boŋu mlaaze e, xe mlaabu onyime liɛ ɛ, gi abitɛ liboe lɛ ya ní mlɔ kusi i?

english_verse: I am with you only in my thoughts. But in the name of our Lord Jesus I have already judged this man, as though I were with you in person. So when you meet together and the power of the Lord Jesus is with you, I will be there too.
translation_verse: Xegi mletsa ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ linyi ne me e, lilɛ si mlete si malɛ nì mlɔ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ lɛ blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ kigaglaɛ kyɛ kilɛ ní mlɔ kusi i,

english_verse: You must then hand that man over to Satan. His body will be destroyed, but his spirit will be saved when the Lord Jesus returns.
translation_verse: mlayurɔ ɔniliɛ klɔ akpɛ ni Satana awla tɔ ozizi yɛ kule kuku zimizi yo pɔ̀ tɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kɔ aamɔ kubúbú yo ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe le aʋa.

english_verse: Stop being proud! Don't you know how a little yeast can spread through the whole batch of dough?
translation_verse: Lípɛ si mlazadɔ isu ya! Kite mléte si, “kuɖeme sisami ɔtɔ ko izɛki si amɔwɔ yɛ pɔ́ izeeho na a?”

english_verse: Get rid of the old yeast! Then you will be like fresh bread made without yeast, and this is what you are. Our Passover lamb is Christ, who has already been sacrificed.
translation_verse: Mlayurɔ kuɖeme koko ye ní mlɔ kusi i, tɔ mledzi ŋwisi amɔwɔ vuvu gi kuɖeme ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́ma ní yɛ mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ gi lilɛ si mleze e, lɛ ese linu si bɛkɔ blɔ kutseolidɔzawe le lɛ keʋesibi ya gi kenu Kristo e kɔ.

english_verse: So don't celebrate the festival by being evil and sinful, which is like serving bread made with yeast. Be pure and truthful and celebrate by using bread made without yeast.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlaki tɔ kuŋa liwe le aní ni kuɖeme koko liɛ klɔ gi onu kupɛ́pɛ zimizi ni litukpomegagla a, pɔ̀ ni àklã gi kuɖeme ɔ́ma ní la mɛ ɛ, ligbã́ ɛ nɛ klɔ gi lɛ su lidra a, xe linu anukwarɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In my other letter I told you not to have anything to do with immoral people.
translation_verse: Ní keplekpaa lia klɔ gi matsisi mlɔ mɛ ɛ, magba mlɔ sì mlakíkpɛ ɔhá nì bahasi nɛ ba a.

english_verse: But I wasn't talking about the people of this world. You would have to leave this world to get away from everyone who is immoral or greedy or who cheats or worships idols.
translation_verse: Maʋípani ní babubitɛ ba ní kelea lia mɛ gi bɛɛwa kihasi ye su u, loo baŋazinɛ loo banama gi bizezuru su u, loo bizesumu babu ʋa. Xegi nya wɔaya kí banɔ ba ya elu i, lilɛ si wɔado ní kelea lia mɛ petee.

english_verse: God judges everyone else. The Scriptures say, “Chase away any of your own people who are evil.”
translation_verse: Aya aaxwa litsi ni banɔ ba klɔ gi báma ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ mɛ ɛ. “Mlebu ɔnɔ zimizi ye ní mlɔ kusi.”

english_verse: When one of you has a complaint against another, do you take your complaint to a court of sinners? Or do you take it to God's people?
translation_verse: Xegi liboe liku ní wɔ ni ɔʋaziŋa ɔtɔ kusi i, kude woli wɔmɔ si wɔakɔ lɛ ɔtsinɔ kelea mɛ banɔ si basɛ ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi baʋaziŋa ba balɛ na a?

english_verse: Don't you know that God's people will judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, can't you settle small problems?
translation_verse: Kite mléte si baʋaziŋa ba ba bɛtráxwa litsi le ni kelea mɛ na a? Lɛ xegi mlaaxwa litsi ni kelea mɛ ɛ, kitɛ xe mlatráxwa litsi ni eboe tsɛ́ɛ la na a?

english_verse: Don't you know we will judge angels? And if this is so, we can surely judge everyday matters.
translation_verse: Lɛ kite mléte si blɔ kwitraxwa litsi nì Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba na a? Lɛ kitɛ xe mlátraxwa litsi ni kelea lia ya mɛ eboe la ɛ?

english_verse: Why do you take everyday complaints to judges who are not respected by the church?
translation_verse: Lɛ xe eboe hehe eku ní mlɔ kusi ní eboa la ya elu su i, mlakya banɔ gi bénu kididɔ ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ mɛ ɛ, tɔ basɛ la a.

english_verse: I say this to your shame. Aren't any of you wise enough to act as a judge between one follower and another?
translation_verse: Mapani nite tɔ mikɔ ɛkpɛ mlɔ kuye. Kɔ kisɛ mlɛsɛ sì ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ma ní mlɔ kusi gi ete liboe e, gi aaxwa litsi ní baziŋa ba kusi na a?

english_verse: Why should one of you take another to be tried by unbelievers?
translation_verse: Xe lɛ loso o, onemi ɔʋaziŋa tole aakɔ̀ ɔsã kude ní yɛ ɔhanuglɔɛ su u, ní banama gi báziŋa ɛ, ba aŋu mɛ na!

english_verse: When one of you takes another to court, all of you lose. It would be better to let yourselves be cheated and robbed.
translation_verse: Agi mlɛkɔ ɔsã kude ní mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba su i, libasɛ si bɛŋa mlɔ aʋa kooko. Ege loso mlatráki si bata mlɔ ese na a? Ege loso mlátraki si babà mlɔ ɔ?

english_verse: Don't you know that evil people won't have a share in the blessings of God's kingdom? Don't fool yourselves! No one who is immoral or worships idols or is unfaithful in marriage or is a pervert or behaves like a homosexual
translation_verse: Kite mléte si litukpo me gagla benete ba ɛ, bátramɔ kaŋase ní Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa mɛ na a? Mlakímeni isu u: bahasi nɛ loo babubitɛ loo benyime ba ya gi bɛgbani badzɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe bizɛlɔ̀ ádzɛboe e, loo benyime ba ya gi badzɛ ba bizɛɛtsa kibɔɛ kɔ si bafɛ ni ba a, loo benyime ba ya gi bizɛɛfɛ ni ba ahanuglɔ benyime e, ni badzɛ ba ya gi bizɛpɛ ba ahanuglɔ badzɛ ba a,

english_verse: Some of you used to be like that. But now the name of our Lord Jesus Christ and the power of God's Spirit have washed you and made you holy and acceptable to God. Honor God with Your Body
translation_verse: Lɛ ligbã́ɛ nɛ klɔ mlɔ mɛ mlɔ banɔatɔ mleze e. Pɔ̀ beplo mlo o, bɛdra mlɔ su, bɛbitɛ mlɔ si mlɔ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ní Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ linyi ne me gani blɔ Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ. Mlɔ kisugyaɛ kinu Aya yɛ lisɔligba lɛ

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do anything we want to.” But I tell you not everything is good for us. So I refuse to let anything have power over me.
translation_verse: “Kidɔ kakaa su kidra ɛki mɛ si mibitɛ ɛ,” “pɔ̀ aní kidɔ kakaa kibu libi ɛki mɛ ɛ.” Bɛdra kudeonu iiki mɛ si mibitɛ kidɔ kakaa, pɔ̀ ani kidɔ kakaa kiatani mɛ aʋa kuŋa a.

english_verse: You also say, “Food is meant for our bodies, and our bodies are meant for food.” But I tell you that God will destroy them both. We are not supposed to do indecent things with our bodies. We are to use them for the Lord who is in charge of our bodies.
translation_verse: “Kiŋaŋaɛ kilɛ ki lipo le, lɛ lipo le fɛ lilɛ ɛki kiŋaŋaɛ.” Pɔ̀ Aya atrázizi bɛ tuʋa a. Kisugyaɛ, bábitɛ kɛ aki kihasi ye e, pɔ̀ bɛbitɛ kɛ si kisumu Ɔkaɛ ɛ, lɛ Ɔkaɛ fɛ aaki kɛ kidɔ kakaaɛ liɛ gi kizia kɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God will raise us from death by the same power he used when he raised our Lord to life.
translation_verse: Lɛ gani yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, Aya ayɔni Ɔkaɛ adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ lɛ aayɔni blɔ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't you know that your bodies are part of the body of Christ? Is it right for me to join part of the body of Christ to a prostitute? No, it isn't!
translation_verse: Kite mléte si mlɔ inɔse le e, inu Kristo yɛ isu ya kapɛatɔ na a? Kɔ maakɔ Kristo yɛ kapɛ katɔ gi miikɔ ka ɛkpɛ likpɔ ni ɔhasinɛ na a? Litrába lɛ mɛ ɛ!

english_verse: Don't you know that a man who does that becomes part of her body? The Scriptures say, “The two of them will be like one person.”
translation_verse: Kɔ kite mléte si ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛkpɛ ɔhasinɛ ɔha ɛ, edzì kidɔ tile ni yɛ ní kisugyaɛ mɛ na a? Lɛ ese linu si bɛpani si, “Ba tiaʋa ɛ, biadzi kisugya tile.”

english_verse: But anyone who is joined to the Lord is one in spirit with him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ isu likpɔ ni Ɔkaɛ ɛ, aadzi kidɔ tile nì yɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ.

english_verse: Don't be immoral in matters of sex. That is a sin against your own body in a way no other sin is.
translation_verse: Mlese ní kihasi ye su, liboe zimizi kakaa elu gi ɔnɔ aabitɛ ɛ, ɔʋíbitɛ lɛ ɔmanɔ ní yɛ isu ya su u; pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi abitɛ lihasiboe e, abitɛ liboe zimizi amani yɛ isu ya su u.

english_verse: You surely know that your body is a temple where the Holy Spirit lives. The Spirit is in you and is a gift from God. You are no longer your own.
translation_verse: Mléte si mlɔ kisugyaɛ kinu Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ Lisɔligba lɛ na a, gi ɔkpasi ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ, gi lɔ mlɛzi dɔni Aya klɔ na a? Mláma ní mlɔ tutɔ mlɔ isu wla mɛ ɛ;

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So use your body to honor God.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ edzi mlo kedzi zɛzɛ. Lɛ loso e, mlakɔ mlɔ kisugyaɛ akpɛ kubùbù ní Aya su u.

english_verse: Now I will answer the questions you asked in your letter. You asked, “Is it best for people not to marry?”
translation_verse: Abla ní eboa la su gi mlɛpani ní mlɔ keplekpaa mɛ ɛki mɛ ɛ, lipɛ xe onyime ɔ́gbani ɔdzɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Well, having your own husband or wife should keep you from doing something immoral.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi kihasi ye kisrɛ ní mlɔ mɛ onu i, lilɛ sì onyime kakaa ɔgbani yɛ ɔtutɔ yedze, tɔ ɔdzɛ kakaa ozeni yɛ ɔtutɔ yenye.

english_verse: Husbands and wives should be fair with each other about having sex.
translation_verse: Onyimee ɔwa yɛ axwɛ na ŋwisi ɔdzɛnye kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔdzɛɛ fɛ ɛ, ɔwa yɛ kapɛa ŋwisi onyimedze kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: A wife belongs to her husband instead of to herself, and a husband belongs to his wife instead of to himself.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ yɛ kisugyaɛ kínu yɛ tete kinɛ ɛ, boŋu i, kinu yenye kyɛ kinɛ ɛ. Nite ke onyimee yɛ kisugyaɛ kínu yɛ tete kinɛ ɛ, boŋu i kinu yedze kyɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So don't refuse sex to each other, unless you agree not to have sex for a little while, in order to spend time in prayer. Then Satan won't be able to tempt you because of your lack of self-control.
translation_verse: Mlakíkyi kede kpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba a, xe ani gani kesenunu mɛ kpɛ ɔtɔ lɛ ki lipoetɔ ko o, atɔ mlaakɔ mlɔ isu ya petee ɔki Aya kuxwaxwa; pɔ̀ lɛ kede e, mledzi akpɛ likpɔ dzɛ. Atɔ Satana okífeke mlo edi kɔkpɛ gi mlátani isu aʋa kuŋa loso o.

english_verse: In my opinion that is what should be done, though I don't know of anything the Lord said about this matter.
translation_verse: Mɛɛpani lɛ ya aní si linu ɔsã a, pɔ̀ linu kude kɔkɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I wish all of you were like me, but God has given different gifts to each of us.
translation_verse: Nya maapɛ si onyime kakaa oze ŋwisi mɛ kiniɛ gi mágbani ɔdzɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ onyime kakaa azɛ bidɔ kɔkɔ lidze lidze adɔ ni Aya klɔ ɔ, ɔnutɔ ɛ, bidɔkɔkɔɛ bɛ ya, ɔnɔ dzedze e, bidɔkɔkɔ dzedze.

english_verse: My advice for the unmarried and for widows is that it is acceptable for them to remain single, just as I am.
translation_verse: Abla, ki banama gi benu itrè le e, ni badzɛa ya gi ba benye ba betse e, mɛɛsi ba si liapɛ ki ba si bakígbani badzɛ loo zeni benyime e, tɔ beze ŋwisi kiliɛ gi malɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But if you don't have enough self-control, then go ahead and get married. After all, it is better to marry than to burn with desire.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe bátratani isu aʋa kuŋa ɛ, lɛ kɔ bagbani badzɛ ani tɔ bezeni benyime lɛ ese linu si liapɛ sanɔ si bagbani badzɛ sani si isu ya mɛ izɛɛŋwasi ba ɔkpɛ kulekuku liɔ gi kuli ní ba mɛ loso o.

english_verse: I instruct married couples to stay together, and this is exactly what the Lord himself taught. A wife who leaves her husband
translation_verse: Lɛ ki banama gi bɛgbanɔ badzɛ ɛ, loo gi bedini benyime e, mɛɛki ba ɔsãa nɔ, aní mɛ, pɔ̀ Aya a: Líma si ɔdzɛ ɔsɛ ní yenye su u.

english_verse: should either stay single or go back to her husband. And a husband should not leave his wife.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe asɛ ɛ, líma si ozeni onyime e, loo lilɛ si odziba ní yenye klɔ ɔ; lɛ onyime kyɛ ɛ, líma si ɔyurɔ yedze e.

english_verse: I don't know of anything else the Lord said about marriage. All I can do is to give you my own advice. If your wife isn't a follower of the Lord, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce her.
translation_verse: Kí banɔ gɔgɔ ba ɛ, mɛɛpani si, : xe onyime gi asɔli i, agbani ɔdzɛ gi ónu ɔsɔlisɔli i, pɔ̀ adimɛ si yɛ aze ní onyimee su i, lɛ kɔɔ onyimee ɔkiyurɔ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If your husband isn't a follower, but is willing to stay with you, don't divorce him.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔdzɛ, odini onyime gi ónu ɔsɔlisɔli i, pɔ̀ adimɛ si yɛ nì ɔdzɛɛ biaze e, lɛ kɔɔ ɔkisɛ ní ɔdzɛɛ su u.

english_verse: Your husband or wife who isn't a follower is made holy by having you as a partner. This also makes your children holy and keeps them from being unclean in God's sight.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si onyimee gi ónu ɔʋaziŋa ɛ, Aya adra yɛ su gani yedze aʋa a, lɛ ɔdzɛ gi ónu ɔʋaziŋa kyɛ ɛ, Aya adra yɛ su gani yenye gi onu ɔʋaziŋa aʋa a. Xe aníte e, mlɔ bebi ba su batrádra a, pɔ̀ kiliɛ gi lilɛ ɛ, ba su ɛɛdra.

english_verse: If your husband or wife isn't a follower of the Lord and decides to divorce you, then you should agree to it. You are no longer bound to that person. After all, God chose you and wants you to live at peace.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnɔ gi ɔ́ziŋa asɛ ɛ, mlaki tɔ ɔtrɛ. Ní la ya mɛ ɛ, kididɔ kínya ɔʋaziŋaɛ gi onu onyimedze e, loo ɔdzɛnye e. Aya yɛ axwa mlɔ si mleze ní kusuyɔ mɛ.

english_verse: And besides, how do you know if you will be able to save your husband or wife who isn't a follower? Obeying the Lord at All Times
translation_verse: Kitɛ mlatani kute xe wɔ onyimedzee wɔabu wonye e, loo wɔ ɔdzɛnyee wɔabu wodze e?

english_verse: In every church I tell the people to stay as they were when the Lord Jesus chose them and God called them to be his own. Now I say the same thing to you.
translation_verse: Ní liboe lɛ ya kede e, lilɛ si ɔnɔ kakaa oze ligbã́ nɛ ní niklɔ gi Ɔkaɛ aki yɛ onu u, ani kí niklɔ gi Aya axwa yɛ akɔ onu u. Lɛ ya linu kutrɔtrɔ liɔ gi mizɛɛbasɛ ní ɔsɔli ha kakaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Being circumcised or uncircumcised isn't really what matters. The important thing is to obey God's commands.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔnɔ atsà ɔyaʋi loo ɔ́tsa ɔyaʋi i, línu kididɔ; kidiliɛ gi kisazia ɛ, kɛ ko kinu si ɔbitɛ ní Aya yɛ isã nɛ aʋa.

english_verse: So don't try to change what you were when God chose you.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ kakaa oze kiliɛ gi eze e, xe Aya axwa yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Are you a slave? Don't let that bother you. But if you can win your freedom, you should.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔdzidzi woze xe Aya axwa wɔ na a? Wukíki si likpɛ wɔ kunugu u; pɔ̀ xegi kudeonu kulɛ ɛki wɔ ɛ, zɛ wɔ isuwla mɛ kuzezeo.

english_verse: God paid a great price for you. So don't become slaves of anyone else.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ edzi mlo kedzi ʋidi i; lɛ loso e, mlakídzì banɔdzidzi ɛki banima a.

english_verse: I don't know of anything the Lord said about people who have never been married. But I will tell you what I think. And you can trust me, because the Lord has treated me with kindness.
translation_verse: Abla kí badzɛa ba gi béte benyime e, mázi liboeboe adɔ ni Ɔkaɛ kɔ aki ba a, pɔ̀ gani Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ, aki mɛ kuboete gi biaka ní kɔ aʋa a, lɛ maaki mlɔ mɛ begyodi ʋa ní lɛ su u.

english_verse: We are now going through hard times, and I think it is best for you to stay as you are.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ abla baxixa ʋa loso e, megyo le edi si liapɛ xegi mledzi alɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ ɛ gi mlalɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you are married, stay married. If you are not married, don't try to get married.
translation_verse: Xegi ɔdzɛ ɔlɛ ní wɔ awla ɛ, wukíyurɔ yɛ ɛ. Xegi wɔ́gbani ɔdzɛ ɛ, wukípɛ ɔdzɛ si wugbanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: It isn't wrong to marry, even if you have never been married before. But those who marry will have a lot of trouble, and I want to protect you from this.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi wɔgbani ɔdzɛ fɛ ɛ, wɔ́bitɛ liboe zimizi i. Pɔ̀ xegi ɔdzɛ ɛ, gi ɔbitɛ enyimeboe odi odini onyime e, ɔ́bitɛ liboe zimizi i. Pɔ̀ banama a gi bɛgbanɔ badzɛ ba ɛ, biamɔ ɔzia ní ligbã́ɛ lɛ ya mɛ, lɛ nya mapɛ si mibu mlo ní lɛ mɛ xe e.

english_verse: My friends, what I mean is that the Lord will soon come, and it won't matter if you are married or not.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani i, lɛ ese linu si lipoe le lilɛ kpekpemi kí Ɔkaɛ yɛ kubaba a. Lɛ loso dɔni abla kɔtsinɔ ɛ, banama awla gi badzɛ balɛ ɛ, beze ligba nɛ ŋwisi ɔdzɛ ɔma ní ba awla kinɛ ɛ;

english_verse: It will be all the same if you are crying or laughing, or if you are buying or are completely broke.
translation_verse: banama gi beebemi i, beze ŋwisi baʋíbemi i; banama gi bɛɛmɔ isami i, beze ŋwisi baʋímɔ isami i; banama gi beedzi bidɔ ɛ, beze ŋwisi kidiliɛ gi beedzi i, aní ba kinɛ kinu u;

english_verse: It won't make any difference how much good you are getting from this world or how much you like it. This world as we know it is now passing away.
translation_verse: banama gi bɛwa kelea lia mɛ bidɔ mɛ su axwɛ ɛ, bakídzi tole nì bɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si kelea lia kiliɛ gi kwite ka abla ɛ, ka onu kɛɛza.

english_verse: I want all of you to be free from worry. An unmarried man worries about how to please the Lord.
translation_verse: Mapɛ si mlɔ ina idɔ. Onyime gi ɔ́gbani ɔdzɛ ɛ, tɔ yɛ begyodi ʋa beze ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ axwɛ na su ani kiliɛ gi aaku Ɔkaɛ aŋu i.

english_verse: But a married man has more worries. He must worry about the things of this world, because he wants to please his wife.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ onyime gi agbani ɔdzɛ kɔ ɛ, yɛ begyodi ʋa bali ní kelea mɛ eboe la su, lɛ ese linu si aapɛ si yiku yedze aŋu;

english_verse: So he is pulled in two directions. Unmarried women and women who have never been married worry only about pleasing the Lord, and they keep their bodies and minds pure. But a married woman worries about the things of this world, because she wants to please her husband.
translation_verse: lɛ yɛ bapɛ́pɛ ʋa bɛlɛ ɛ. Ɔdisami ni ɔdzɛ gi ódini onyime e, izɛɛpɛ si yiwa Ɔkaɛ yɛ axwɛ na, yɛ kupɛpɛ kunu si yikɔ yɛ kisugyaɛ nì yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ pɔ́ ɛki Ɔkaɛ. Pɔ̀ ɔdzɛ gi odini onyime e, izɛɛpɛ kelea mɛ eboe la, kiliɛ gi aaku yenye aŋu i.

english_verse: What I am saying is for your own good—it isn't to limit your freedom. I want to help you to live right and to love the Lord above all else.
translation_verse: Mɛɛpani la ya ɛsi mlɔ kí mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlɔ kupɛ̀pɛ aní si tɔ mitsi kudeonu iiki mlɔ ɔ, boŋu tɔ mleze ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa tɔ mlakɔ mlɔ aŋu na petee atrɔ ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ kusumu yo aʋa.

english_verse: But suppose you are engaged to someone old enough to be married, and you want her so much that all you can think about is getting married. Then go ahead and marry. There is nothing wrong with that.
translation_verse: Xe onyime ɔtɔ gi akpɛ ɔdisami ɔtɔ kuwlawla a, xe amɔ si yaʋíga mumɔ, lɛ xe ɔdisami nɔ gi akpɛ kuwlawlaa eetsi ní agɔ mɛ ɛ, xe amɔ si liziã si yigbani yɛ ɛ, lilɛ si ɔbitɛ ní yɛ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa tɔ ɔgbani yɛ. Kesetataa tɔtɔ káma ni lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: It is perfectly all right to marry, but it is better not to get married at all.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso onyime liɛ klɔ gi agbani ɔdisami nɔ ɔ, gi bɛkpɛ kuwlawla abitɛ lɛ mumɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ ɛ, gi ɔ́gbani yɛ kuwlawlakpɛdzɛ ɛ, abitɛ lɛ mumɔ asanɔ.

english_verse: A wife should stay married to her husband until he dies. Then she is free to marry again, but only to a man who is a follower of the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛ gi odini onyime e, xe yenye ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, ɔwlamɔmɔ ɔma ní yɛ su u; pɔ̀ xe yenye etse e, kɔ lɔ amɔ ɔwla gi aazeni onyime kakaa a, gi apɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ lilɛ si oze Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔnɛ ɛ.

english_verse: However, I think I am obeying God's Spirit when I say she would be happier to stay single.
translation_verse: Ní mɛ kumɔmɔ onu i, aaze ɔsamì nɛ ɔsã nɔ, xegi aaze ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔlɛ kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ megyo le edi si mɛ fɛ ɛ, Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔkpasi ní mɛ mɛ̀.

english_verse: In your letter you asked me about food offered to idols. All of us know something about this subject. But knowledge makes us proud of ourselves, while love makes us helpful to others.
translation_verse: Abla ní bidɔŋaŋa gi bizɛɛki babu ʋa su i: Kwite si kuboete kuli ní blɔ petee awla. Kuboete kuzɛɛkɔ si banima bizeemuni isu u, pɔ̀ kulɔlɔɔ kuzɛɛkpɛ blɔ kigagla a.

english_verse: In fact, people who think they know so much don't know anything at all.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi egyoedi si yete kiditɔ ɛ, óte kidiliɛ gi linu si ote e.

english_verse: But God has no doubts about who loves him.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi alɔ Aya ɛ, Aya ete ye.

english_verse: Even though food is offered to idols, we know that none of the idols in this world are alive. After all, there is only one God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso ní bidɔŋaŋa gi bɛki babu ʋa su i; kwite si ɔbu yɛ ónu kididɔ kura a, lɛ ní kelea lia ya mɛ ɛ, Aya tole ko ɔlɛ.

english_verse: Many things in heaven and on earth are called gods and lords, but none of them really are gods or lords.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi babu ʋa balɛ ní lifunu ne e, loo ní kesea aʋa, lɛ “babu” ʋa ni “ifuɛfuɛ gagla lɛ kpaŋu gi banima bizesumu i,”

english_verse: We have only one God, and he is the Father. He created everything, and we live for him. Jesus Christ is our only Lord. Everything was made by him, and by him life was given to us.
translation_verse: blɔ kɔ ɛ Aya tole ko ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla, Ɔka yɛ, gi yɛ abitɛ kidɔ kakaa ɛ, xe yɛ loso kuli ligbã́ nɛ ɛ; lɛ Ɔkaɛ tole gi onu Yesu Kristo e, yɛ ko ɔlɛ, lɛ gani yɛ aʋa bɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa a, lɛ gani yɛ aʋa kuli ligbã́ nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Not everyone knows these things. In fact, many people have grown up with the belief that idols have life in them. So when they eat meat offered to idols, they are bothered by a weak conscience.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ aní ɔnɔ kakaa ete anukwarɛ liɛ ya a. Banɔatɔ ɛ, kububitɛɔ kwita eku ní ba mɛ gbɛgbɛ nite me si xegi bɛɛŋa bidɔ ʋɛ ya ɛ, bizɛɛbitɛ ɛki ba ŋwisi bidɔŋaŋa gi bɛki ɔbu yɛ ɛ; lɛ gi ba ina nɛ ibotso loso e, ba ina nɛ mɛ ilulù.

english_verse: But food doesn't bring us any closer to God. We are no worse off if we don't eat, and we are no better off if we do.
translation_verse: Bidɔŋaŋa ʋɛ kɔ e, bitrátini blo epɔni Aya a; kutrálila kididɔ ɔ, xe kúŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ kutrámɔ kididɔ ɛdɔni lɛ mɛ ɛ, xe kwiŋa bidɔ ʋɛ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for someone with a weak conscience, just because you have the right to eat anything.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mledi isu aʋa mumɔ si mlɔ ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ ɔkiki si banama gi ba kuziŋaa kúma ɛ gagla bawɔli eku ní eboe zimizi me e.

english_verse: You know all this, and so it doesn't bother you to eat in the temple of an idol. But suppose a person with a weak conscience sees you and decides to eat food that has been offered to idols.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔnutɔ ɔ, gi yɛ kuziŋaa kúma ɛ gagla a, amɔ wɔ ɔniliɛ gi wote liboe e lɛ ya si wɔɛŋa bidɔ ní libugba lɛ mɛ ɛ, lítrakpɛ ɔna ní yɛ lipo o, xe yɛ kyɛ aaŋa bidɔŋaŋa a, gi bɛki babu ʋa na a?

english_verse: When you sin by hurting a follower with a weak conscience, you sin against Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mlɛbitɛ eboe zimizi yurɔ mlɔ benemi ba nite e, xe mlɛkpɛ ba kuziŋaa a, gi kúma ɛ gagla ebi i, mlɛta Kristo ese.

english_verse: So if I hurt one of the Lord's followers by what I eat, I will never eat meat as long as I live.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso xegi kidiliɛ ɛ, gi mɛɛŋa kiaki si mɛ onemi ye aabitɛ liboe zimizi i, kɔlɔ mátratà kigya liwewe tɔ ɔ, tɔ maaki mɛ onemi dzedze aawɔli ku ní liboe zimizi i tɔtɔ mɛ tɔ.

english_verse: I am free. I am an apostle. I have seen the Lord Jesus and have led you to have faith in him.
translation_verse: Dani malɛ ní mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ isuwla mɛ ɛ? Aní ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ menu i? Lɛ dani mamɔ Yesu blɔ Ɔkaɛ ɛ? Lɛ aní mlɔ mlenu axwɛ a na gi mawa aki ɔkaɛ la libi le e?

english_verse: Others may think that I am not an apostle, but you are proof that I am an apostle to you.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ménu ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ aki banɔatɔ kyɛ ɛ, menu ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ ɔki mlɔ xunyɔ, Lɛ ese linu si mlɔ mlenu mɛ axwɛwawa la gi mawa aki Ɔkaɛ la liklatrɔtrɔ lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When people question me, I tell them
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya linu mɛ kunububu yo kí banama gi bizɛxwa litsi ni mɛ.

english_verse: that Barnabas and I have the right to our food and drink.
translation_verse: Kɔ kude kúma ní blɔ awla si kwiaŋa bidɔ tɔ kwiaŋwɛ bidɔ ní mlɔ kupeo me na a?

english_verse: We each have the right to marry one of the Lord's followers and to take her along with us, just as the other apostles and the Lord's brothers and Peter do.
translation_verse: Lɛ kɔ kima líma si kwiagbani blɔ bedze ba gi benu baʋaziŋa si beze ni blɔ kude ŋwisi kiliɛ gi banɔkpɛkpɛ dzedze ba ni Ɔkaɛ yɛ benemi ba ni Petro kyɛ bizɛbitɛ na a.

english_verse: Are we the only ones who have to support ourselves by working at another job?
translation_verse: Aloo mɛ ni Barnaba tètè ko linu si kuwa axwɛ na tɔ kwiakɔdi isu aʋa na a?

english_verse: I am not saying this on my own authority. The Law of Moses tells us not to muzzle an ox when it is grinding grain. But was God concerned only about an ox?
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Mose ɔsã nɔ mɛ si, “Wukínya ligumee nɛ gi liigu amu na a, lɛ onugu lo o.” Abla ɛ, Aya akɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ aki egume na loo kitɛ ɛ?

english_verse: No, he wasn't! He was talking about us. This was written in the Scriptures so that all who plow and all who grind the grain will look forward to sharing in the harvest.
translation_verse: Apani liboe lɛ ya aki blɔ aníte e? Ee, bɛŋwlimi liboe lɛ ya ɛki blɔ, lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛsɔ amu na a, ni ɔniliɛ gi egu amu na ɛ, beedi kudeonu si biamɔ ba kapɛa ní ɔnyrɔ nɔ mɛ bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀.

english_verse: When we told the message to you, it was like planting spiritual seed. So we have the right to accept material things as our harvest from you.
translation_verse: Xegi kwikpɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ esebi ní mlɔ kusi i, kisani lisani mlɔ si kwiamɔ kelea lia mɛ bidɔ dɔni mlɔ klɔ na a?

english_verse: If others have the right to do this, we have an even greater right. But we haven't used this right of ours. We are willing to put up with anything to keep from causing trouble for the message about Christ.
translation_verse: Xegi banɔatɔ kɔɔ ɔŋakɔ lɔ ya elu ɔlɛ ni ba awla si mlaaki ba kupɔnɔ ɛ, ɔŋakɔ ɔlɔ ya elu ɔma ní blɔ awla sani ba na a? Pɔ̀ blɔ kɔɔ kúwa ɔŋakɔ lɔ ya su axwɛ ɛ, boŋu i, kwizi liboe kakaa tɔ kukídzi ɔvia loo banɔ gi beetsi kudeonu iiki Kristo yɛ liboe gbã nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't you know that people who work in the temple make their living from what is brought to the temple? Don't you know that a person who serves at the altar is given part of what is offered?
translation_verse: Kite mléte si banɔaklɔ gi bizɛɛwa axwɛ na ní asɔligba la mɛ ɛ, bizɛɛmɔ ba kiŋaŋaɛ dɔni asɔligba la mɛ na a? Lɛ banɔaklɔ gi bizeesumu ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ su i, bizɛɛmɔ ba ɔgyɔ lɔ ní kidiliɛ gi bɛkɔ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa na a?

english_verse: In the same way, the Lord wants everyone who preaches the good news to make a living from preaching this message.
translation_verse: Niteme kee, Ɔkaɛ aki ɔsã si banama gi bizɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, biazɛ ba kiŋaŋaɛ dɔni liboe gbã nɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But I have never used these privileges of mine, and I am not writing this because I want to start now. I would rather die than have someone rob me of the right to take pride in this.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ máwa mɛ ɔŋakɔ lɔ ya ɔtɔtɔ su axwɛ, lɛ máʋiŋwlimi liboe lɛ ya ni kugyodi si mlaabitɛ niteme eboe aki mɛ ɛ. Maapɛ boŋ si mitse sani si ɔnutɔ ɔzɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso madɔ isu ya ní mɛ awla a.

english_verse: I don't have any reason to brag about preaching the good news. Preaching is something God told me to do, and if I don't do it, I am doomed.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe mɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, mátratani isu kudɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si Aya emi mɛ aʋa si mipani liboe le e. Baba kimɛ xegi mápani liboe gbã nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If I preach because I want to, I will be paid. But even if I don't want to, it is still something God has sent me to do.
translation_verse: Xegi mapani liboe gbã nɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi akɔ isu akɔ kinɛ ɛ, liaziã si batsa liʋɛ aki mɛ; pɔ̀ xe aní mɛ kupɛ́pɛ onu mɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, kɔ lixwɛ nɛ gi Aya aka mɛ aʋa si mawa ɛ, lɛ mɛɛwa.

english_verse: What pay am I given? It is the chance to preach the good news free of charge and not to use the privileges that are mine because I am a preacher.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ ɛ, ege kinu libi le ɛki mɛ ɛ? Lɛ ko linu si: gi mɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, mɛɛpani lɛ faã kibɔ mozɛ, atɔ mikíkɔ mɛ ɔŋa klɔ ɛtrɔ ní mlɔ aʋa a, xe mɛɛpani liboe gbã nɛ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I am not anyone's slave. But I have become a slave to everyone, so I can win as many people as possible.
translation_verse: Linya ɛli lɛ mɛ si malɛ ní mɛ isuwla mɛ, lɛ ménu ɔnɔnɔ ɔnɛ kyɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ mabitɛ mɛ isu ya si midzi ɔnɔdzidzi kí ɔnɔ kakaa atɔ migbani banɔ glegle mani Yesu klɔ.

english_verse: When I am with the Jews, I live like a Jew to win Jews. They are ruled by the Law of Moses, and I am not. But I live by the Law to win them.
translation_verse: Gi mazɛɛwa axwɛ na ní Yudanɛ ma kusi i, medzi ŋwisi Yuda ɔnɛ, tɔ mimɔ Yudanɛ ma ɛki Ɔkaɛ. Ki banama gi bali ní ɔsã nɔ ese e, medzi ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní ɔsã nɔ ese e, nya mɛ ɔtutɔ máma ní ɔsã nɔ ese xe e, atɔ mimɔ banama gi bali ní ɔsã nɔ ese aki Kristo.

english_verse: And when I am with people who are not ruled by the Law, I forget about the Law to win them. Of course, I never really forget about the law of God. In fact, I am ruled by the law of Christ.
translation_verse: Ki banama awla gi ɔsã nɔ ɔma ɛ, medzi ŋwisi ɔniliɛ awla gi ɔsã nɔ ɔ́ma ɛ, gani lɛ mɛ máma ní ɔwlamɔmɔ mɛ ní Aya ɔsã nɔ ese kyɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ malɛ ní Kristo yɛ ɔsã nɔ ese e, atɔ mihe banɔaklɔ gi ɔsã nɔ ɔma ní ba awla ɛ, ɛmanɔ ní Kristo klɔ.

english_verse: When I am with people whose faith is weak, I live as they do to win them. I do everything I can to win everyone I possibly can.
translation_verse: Kí banama gi bebotso ní kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, mɛ fɛ mebotso atɔ migbani banama gi bebotso ní kuziŋaa mɛ mani Kristo klɔ. Medzi kidɔ kakaa akí banima petee atɔ gani kude kakaa onu i, maabu bamɛ batɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I do all this for the good news, because I want to share in its blessings. A Race and a Fight
translation_verse: Mabitɛ la ya petee kɔkpɛ liboe gbã nɛ loso, tɔ mɛ fɛ maamɔ ɔŋakɔ ní lɛ kukriraa mɛ.

english_verse: You know that many runners enter a race, and only one of them wins the prize. So run to win!
translation_verse: Aloo kite mléte si xegi banima beese e, bamɛ tole ko izɛɛŋa aʋa na a? Kɔlɔ ɛ, mlese ní kudeo liɔ klɔ onu gi mlaŋa aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Athletes work hard to win a crown that cannot last, but we do it for a crown that will last forever.
translation_verse: Osese kakaa gi ɔkpasi ní besese ba kusi i, izɛɛga kuhehe gagla mɛ ɛ, bizɛɛbitɛ lɛ nite atɔ biazɛ likusikuto elɛ gi liʋíkle e, xe lizee zizi i; pɔ̀ kuzɛɛbitɛ lɛ tɔ kwiazɛ likusikuto elɛ gi litrázizi liwewe e.

english_verse: I don't run without a goal. And I don't box by beating my fists in the air.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e maʋíse ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi eese e, gi litukpo líma ni yɛ kuseseo onu u; lɛ loso maʋíta akpafu íkpɛ liwɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I keep my body under control and make it my slave, so I won't lose out after telling the good news to others.
translation_verse: Owo, mizeebu kiní iiki mɛ kisugyaɛ atɔ kidzi ɔnɔdzidzi kí mɛ atɔ xegi mapani Aya yɛ liboe le asi banima pɔ́ ɛ, bátrabu me ní lɛ mɛ si mégyeno o.

english_verse: Friends, I want to remind you that all our ancestors walked under the cloud and went through the sea.
translation_verse: Mápɛ si mleze ní litumu me ní liboe lɛ ya su u, mɛ benemi ba, blɔ bakatsitsi ba petee beze ní liplɛ lɛ gi lɛ lizɛgbani ba ese e; lɛ ba pɔ́ bɛtsa axu la a.

english_verse: All of them also ate the same spiritual food
translation_verse: Ba pɔ́ bɛŋa Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ bidɔŋaŋa lide tile;

english_verse: and drank the same spiritual drink, which flowed from the spiritual rock that followed them. That rock was Christ.
translation_verse: lɛ bɛŋwɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kuni yo; lɛ ese linu si bɛŋwɛ kuni yo ɛdɔni Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ liklakpɔ ɛlɛ gi lizeni ba kude me e, lɛ liklakpɔ ɛlɛ klɔ linu Kristo.

english_verse: But most of them did not please God. So they died, and their bodies were scattered all over the desert.
translation_verse: Gi liba lɛ mɛ nite kyɛ ɛ, Aya ɔ́mɔ isami ní bamɛ beglegle ba, ba bekpo ʋa bɛwɔ ní ɔyaya klɔ.

english_verse: What happened to them is a warning to keep us from wanting to do the same evil things.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, eboa la ya ɛɛba la mɛ ŋwisi liŋwitsɛ ki blɔ, tɔ kukíki si blɔ ina nɛ izeni eboe zimizi na kude kiliɛ gi bɛbitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They worshiped idols, just as the Scriptures say, “The people sat down to eat and drink. Then they got up to dance around.” So don't worship idols.
translation_verse: Mlakíze babubitɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bamɛ batɔ beze kinɛ ɛ. Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi si i: “Banima beze kesea lɛ bɛŋa bidɔ ɔ, xe bɛŋwɛ bidɔ ɔ, lɛ bɛyɔ ɛ, xe bekpese abúvu na kuyo e.”

english_verse: Some of you say, “We can do whatever we want to!” But I tell you not everything may be good or helpful.
translation_verse: Mlɛzɛpani si “Kudeo onu kuli ki kidɔ kakaa” pɔ̀ mɛ Paulo mɛɛpani si aní kidɔ kakaa kizebu libi i. Mlɛzɛɛpani si “Bebu kudeo onu ɛki kidɔ kakaa, pɔ̀ mɛ Paulo mɛɛpani si aní kidɔ kakaa kizɛɛki kupɔnɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: And don't try to test Christ, as some of them did and were later bitten by poisonous snakes.
translation_verse: Líma si kufeke Ɔkaɛ odi i, kiliɛ gi batɔ bɛbitɛ ɛ, xe bagblaga ʋa benemi ba a, xe betse e.

english_verse: Don't even grumble, as some of them did and were killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Lɛ líma si kutɛ̀, kiliɛ gi bamɛ batɔ bɛbitɛ ɛ, xe Aya yɛ kutseo ɔxwɛdzɛɛ akyrɔ ba a.

english_verse: These things happened to them as a warning to us. All this was written in the Scriptures to teach us who live in these last days.
translation_verse: Eboa la ya ɛba ba aʋa ŋwisi liŋwitsɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ bɛŋwlimi la ɛplɛ ɛki blɔ ŋwisi kugbagba kinɛ ɛ, blɔ banama gi blɔ kulɛ ligba nɛ ɛ, xe kelea ka ligù nɛ liana a.

english_verse: Even if you think you can stand up to temptation, be careful not to fall.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso xegi wogyo odi si wɔlɛ gagla ɛ, di le aʋa si wukígleʋe e.

english_verse: You are tempted in the same way that everyone else is tempted. But God can be trusted not to let you be tempted too much, and he will show you how to escape from your temptations.
translation_verse: Kufekedi utɔtɔ kúba mlɔ aʋa gi kuʋíba banɔdzedze ba aʋa eʋídi. Pɔ̀ Aya onu anukwarɛ onete e, gi izɛɛbitɛ ɛ ní liboe lɛ gi apani i aʋa. Lɛ ɔ́traki si biafeke mlo odi sani kiliɛ gi mlɔatani kutu u. Pɔ̀ xegi beefeke mlo edi i, yɛ aaki mlɔ kude kutɔ onu gi mlɔatani kɔ onu kulɛ ɛ.

english_verse: My friends, you must keep away from idols.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ baʋia veve ba a, mlebú isu ya ní kubusumu yo me.

english_verse: I am speaking to you as people who have enough sense to know what I am talking about.
translation_verse: Mɛɛpani iiki mlɔ banɔ gi bete liboe e; mlegyo eboa la gi mɛɛpani iiki mlɔ edi.

english_verse: When we drink from the cup we ask God to bless, isn't that sharing in the blood of Christ? When we eat the bread we break, isn't that sharing in the body of Christ?
translation_verse: Kepliʋia gi ka loso kuzedo Aya ɖase e, xe ka su axwɛ kuzɛɛwa ní ɔkplɔ nɔ su i, Ɔkaɛ yɛ ika lɛ su axwɛ kuzɛɛwa. Lɛ àklã na gi la kuzɛɛyaʋi i, aní Kristo yɛ kisugyaɛ kɛ kapɛatɔ kunu na a?

english_verse: By sharing in the same loaf of bread, we become one body, even though there are many of us.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si dɔni liklã̀ tile lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, blɔ banama gi kwisrɛ ɛ, kunu kisugya tile lɛ ese linu si kuzɛɛlɛ liklã̀ tile lɛ klɔ ʋa.

english_verse: Aren't the people of Israel sharing in the worship when they gather around the altar and eat the sacrifices offered there?
translation_verse: Bù Israelbanɛ ma su di; banɔaklɔ gi bizɛɛŋa bidɔ bɛklɔ gi bɛkɔ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa ɛ, bali likpɔ gani kidiliɛ klɔ gi bɛki Aya mɛ ɛ lɛ benu tole ni Aya a.

english_verse: Am I saying that either the idols or the food sacrificed to them is anything at all?
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ ɛ kipani mlɛɛpani si, kidɔ gi bɛki babu ʋa ɛ, bínu kiditɔ na a? Aloo babu ʋa benu kiditɔ na a?

english_verse: No, I am not! This food is really sacrificed to demons and not to God. I don't want you to have anything to do with demons.
translation_verse: Owo! Pɔ̀ kidɔ gi babubitɛ ʋa bizɛɛkɔ ɛ, bizɛɛkɔ ʋɛ ɛki ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne, lɛ aní kí Aya a, lɛ loso mápɛ si mleze tole ni ifuɛfuɛ zimizi i.

english_verse: You cannot drink from the cup of demons and still drink from the Lord's cup. You cannot eat at the table of demons and still eat at the Lord's table.
translation_verse: Mlatrátani Ɔkaɛ yɛ kepliʋia kuŋwɛ ɛ, xe lɛ kede e, mlaŋwɛ bidɔ ní ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne kepliʋia kyɛ mɛ ɛ, mlatrátani bidɔ kuŋa ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔkplɔ nɔ su u, xe mlazɛɛŋa bidɔ ni ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We would make the Lord jealous if we did this. And we are not stronger than the Lord. Always Honor God
translation_verse: Kikpɛ kwikpɛ Aya kimbi kyikyi na a? Lɛ kibu kwibù si kuli gagla sani yɛ na a? Ɔʋaziŋaɛ yɛ ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ

english_verse: We should think about others and not about ourselves.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔkizeedi yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛ̀pɛɔ ko, pɔ̀ ɔpɛ banɔ dzedze ba bapɛ́pɛ ʋa.
