english_verse: However, when you buy meat in the market, go ahead and eat it. Keep your conscience clear by not asking where the meat came from.
translation_verse: Mlaŋa kidɔ kakaa gi bɛɛda ní kigyɛ kedzia mɛ ɛ, tɔ mlakízeʋi beʋiʋi i, gi biakpɛ mlɔ begyodi ʋa kunugu u.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “The earth and everything in it belong to the Lord.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, “Kesea ni kidɔ kakaa ní ka mɛ ɛ, kinu Ɔkaɛ yɛ kinɛ.”

english_verse: If an unbeliever invites you to dinner, and you want to go, then go. Eat whatever you are served. Don't cause a problem for someone's conscience by asking where the food came from.
translation_verse: Xegi ɔnɔ gi ɔ́ziŋa axwa wɔ si gaŋa bidɔ ni yɛ ɛ, xe wɔdimɛ si wɔatrɛ ɛ, ŋa kidiliɛ gi bɛkɔ edu ní wɔ ketu ya ɛ, wukíʋi liboeboe gi liakpɛ wɔ ɔna nɔ kunugu u.

english_verse: But if you are told it has been sacrificed to idols, don't cause a problem by eating it. I don't mean a problem for yourself, but for the one who told you. Why should my freedom be limited by someone else's conscience?
translation_verse: aní wɔ ɔna nɔ loso o, pɔ̀ ɔnɔ ʋlaɛ yɛ ɔna nɔ loso o. Lɛ ese linu si ege loso ɔnɔ dzedze yɛ ɔna nɔ azɛɛxwa litsi ni mɛ ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ ɛ?

english_verse: If I give thanks for what I eat, why should anyone accuse me of doing wrong?
translation_verse: Xegi medo Aya ɖase aki mɛ kiŋaŋaɛ ɛ, ege loso ɔnutɔ aabu me lipɔ ɔkpɛ kiŋaŋaɛ gi kɛ loso medo Aya ɖase e?

english_verse: When you eat or drink or do anything else, always do it to honor God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kidɔ kakaa gi wɔŋa loo wɔŋwɛ ɛ, loo kidɔ kakaa gi wɔbitɛ ɛ, bitɛ la pɔ́ kí Aya yɛ kusudra a.

english_verse: Don't cause problems for Jews or Greeks or anyone else who belongs to God's church.
translation_verse: Wukíki si ɔnɔnɔ otu kikliʋi xegi Yuda ɔnɛ loo Hela ɔnɛ aloo Aya yɛ ɔsɔli ha lɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I always try to please others instead of myself, in the hope that many of them will be saved.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mekeni edi si miku ɔnɔ kakaa aŋu ní kude kakaa onu u. Lɛ ese linu si maʋípɛ mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ linɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ mɛɛpɛ ligbã ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa atɔ biamɔ kubúbú u.

english_verse: You must follow my example, as I follow the example of Christ. Rules for Worship
translation_verse: Mlakpasi mɛ kiboebitɛɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi makpasi Kristo yɛ kiboebitɛɛ ɛ. Kiboebitɛ gbã ɛ ní ɔsɔli klɔ

english_verse: I am proud of you, because you always remember me and obey the teachings I gave you.
translation_verse: Makpɔ mlɔ lɛ ese linu si mlɛzɛɛplɛ aŋu ni mɛ aʋa ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ni kiliɛ gi mlevu mɛ bidɔ basɛbasɛ ʋɛ ni awla a, kiliɛ gi makɔ ʋɛ aki mlɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that Christ is the head of all men, and a man is the head of a woman. But God is the head of Christ.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mapɛ si mlete si Kristo yɛ onu onyime kakaa yɛ litukpo le, lɛ ɔdzɛ kakaa yɛ litukpo le linu yenye e, lɛ Aya yɛ onu Kristo yɛ litukpo le e.

english_verse: This means that any man who prays or prophesies with something on his head brings shame to his head.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso onyime kakaa gi ɛɛxwa Aya loo ɛɛpani liboe plɛ ɛ, xe akɔ bidɔ ewe yɛ litukpo le e, ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔ́kpɛ kubùbù ní yɛ litukpo le su u.

english_verse: But any woman who prays or prophesies without something on her head brings shame to her head. In fact, she may as well shave her head.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛ kakaa gi ɛɛxwa Aya loo ɛɛpani liboe plɛ ɛ, gi ówe bidɔ ni yɛ litukpo le aʋa ɛ, akpɛ yɛ litukpo le kuye, lidzegaga ɛ tɔtɔ líma ní yɛ ni ɔdzɛ gi axwã yɛ litukpo le kusi i.

english_verse: A woman should wear something on her head. It is a disgrace for a woman to shave her head or cut her hair. But if she refuses to wear something on her head, let her cut off her hair.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔdzɛ ówe bidɔ yɛ litukpo le e, liapɛ si ɔxwa yɛ litukpo le. Lɛ xegi linu kuye ki ɔdzɛ si ɔhà loo ɔxwa yɛ litukpo le e, kɔlɔ lilɛ si owe bidɔ ní yɛ litukpo le.

english_verse: Men were created to be like God and to bring honor to God. This means a man should not wear anything on his head. Women were created to bring honor to men.
translation_verse: Líma si onyime owe bidɔ yɛ litukpo le e, lɛ ese linu si yɛ ɔnɔɛ yɛ onu Aya yɛ kizeze me ni yɛ kusudra a; Pɔ̀ ɔdzɛɛ yɛ izeebu onyimee yɛ kukɔkɔ ɛbasɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It was the woman who was made from a man, and not the man who was made from a woman.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si onyimee ɔ́dɔ ni ɔdzɛɛ mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ ɔdzɛ ádɔ ní onyimee me.

english_verse: He wasn't created for her. She was created for him.
translation_verse: Lɛ, báʋɔ onyimee ɔ́ki ɔdzɛɛ, boŋu i, bɛʋɔ ɔdzɛɛ ki onyimee.

english_verse: And so, because of this, and also because of the angels, a woman ought to wear something on her head, as a sign of her authority.
translation_verse: Ɔkpɛ lɛ ya loso o, lɛ kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba loso e, lilɛ si ɔdzɛɛ ɔbasɛ gyesi ɔtɔ si yalɛ ní kigagla ɛtɔ ese e.

english_verse: It is true that the first woman came from a man, but all other men have been given birth by women. Yet God is the one who created everything.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kiliɛ gi ɔdzɛɛ ádɔ ni onyimee me e, nite kee ɔdzɛɛ yɛ apɔ onyimee. Pɔ̀ kidɔ kakaa kidɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Ask yourselves if it is proper for a woman to pray without something on her head.
translation_verse: Mlata lɛ edi si kɔɔ, lipɛ si ɔdzɛɛ ɔxwa Aya gi ówe bidɔ ní yɛ litukpo le na a?

english_verse: This is how things are done in all of God's churches, and this is why none of you should argue about what I have said. Rules for the Lord's Supper
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ apɛ si yihe liboe e, maapani ko si ɔkɔ ɔtɔtɔ ɔmáni blɔ awla, lɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔma ní Aya yɛ isɔli ha lɛ kyɛ mɛ ɛ. Kutrɔtrɔ ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ ɔkplɔ nɔ su kiŋaŋaɛ su

english_verse: Your worship services do you more harm than good. I am certainly not going to praise you for this.
translation_verse: Mátrakpɔ̀ mlɔ ní batrɔtrɔ ʋa ya gi bɛtrába mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ batsátsa ʋa bizeezizi bidɔ ɛsanɔ si balulɔ bɛ.

english_verse: I am told you can't get along with each other when you worship, and I am sure that some of what I have heard is true.
translation_verse: Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, menu si kulɛlɛ kuli ní mlɔ kusi xegi mlɛtsa ŋwisi ɔsɔli ha kinɛ ɛ, lɛ maapɛ si mizɛ lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa a.

english_verse: You are bound to argue with each other, but it is easy to see which of you have God's approval.
translation_verse: Lilɛ si kulɛlɛ kuze ní mlɔ kusi tɔ biamɔ mlɔ banama aʋa gi Aya yɛ liklatrɔtrɔ lɛ lilɛ ɛ kugyesi.

english_verse: You even start eating before everyone gets to the meeting, and some of you go hungry, while others get drunk.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xe mlɛɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, mlɔmɛ ɔkakaa izɛɛtrɛ lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa bidɔ mɛ gi ɔ́trape bagɔgɔ ba a, liki si banɔatɔ bizeeze ni kuʋɛ kɔ batɔ pɔ̀ bɛŋwɛ emu i.

english_verse: The Lord meant that when you eat this bread and drink from this cup, you tell about his death until he comes.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si lipoe kakaa gi mlɛɛta aklã na ya a, xe mlɛŋwɛ ní kaŋwɛpliʋia lia ya onu i, mlɛɛklá Ɔkaɛ yɛ kutseo zɛkɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi aadzi ba ɛ.

english_verse: If you fail to understand that you are the body of the Lord, you will condemn yourselves by the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔnutɔ ónu Ɔkaɛ yɛ kisugyaɛ ese e, xe ata lìklã nɛ ɛ, xe aŋwɛ ní kaŋwɛpliʋia onu i, aamani litsixwaxwa ní yɛ isu ya aʋa a.

english_verse: After the meal, Jesus took a cup of wine in his hands and said, “This is my blood, and with it God makes his new agreement with you. Drink this and remember me.”
translation_verse: Niteme kee, agi bɛŋa bidɔ mɛ pɔ ɛ, akɔ kaŋwɛpliʋia lɛ apani si, “Kaŋwɛpliʋia lia kenu kunyanya vuvu yɔ ní mɛ ika lɛ mɛ; xegi mlɛɛŋwɛ ka onu i, atɔ mlakɔ aplɛ aŋu ní mɛ aʋa.”

english_verse: I have already told you what the Lord Jesus did on the night he was betrayed. And it came from the Lord himself. He took some bread in his hands.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi mazɛ adɔ ni Ɔkaɛ klɔ ɛ, kɛ mɛ kyɛ makɔ aki mlɔ: si Ɔkaɛ Yesu i, ní elesi ala klɔ mɛ gi bebu ye ekɔ ɛ, akɔ lìklã nɛ,

english_verse: Then after he had given thanks, he broke it and said, “This is my body, which is given for you. Eat this and remember me.”
translation_verse: lɛ gi edo Aya ɖasee ní lɛ aʋa ɛ, ayaʋi lɛ lɛ apani si, “Lɛ ya linu mɛ kisugyaɛ, gi bɛkɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ. Mlabitɛ lɛ tɔ mlakɔ aplɛ aŋu ní mɛ aʋa.”

english_verse: But if you eat the bread and drink the wine in a way that isn't worthy of the Lord, you sin against his body and blood.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xe ɔnutɔ atà Ɔkaɛ yɛ lìklã nɛ loo aŋwɛ dɔni yɛ kaŋwɛpliʋia onu ní kude gi kúgyɔ onu i, aŋa lipɔ gi abitɛ liboe zimizi ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ kisugyaɛ ni yɛ ika lɛ su u.

english_verse: This is why you must examine the way you eat and drink.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ lilɛ si ɔnɔ ɔtapo yɛ isu ya odi xe ableke aatà lìklã nɛ ɛ, xe ableke aaŋwɛ ní kaŋwɛpliʋia lia onu u.

english_verse: This is why many of you are sick and weak and why a lot of others have died.
translation_verse: Lɛ klɔ ʋa loso mlɔmɛ banɔ glegle mlebotso lɛ mlɛdɔ bànya ʋa a, xe banɔ glegle fɛ betse e.

english_verse: If we carefully judge ourselves, we won't be punished.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xegi kwitapo isu ya edi kpɛ ɔtɔ ɛ, kutrába Aya yɛ litsixwaxwa nɛ ese e.

english_verse: But when the Lord judges and punishes us, he does it to keep us from being condemned with the rest of the world.
translation_verse: Xegi Ɔkaɛ axwa litsi ni blɔ ɛ, kihe eehe blo, atɔ bakíbu blo lipɔ kɔpɔni kelea mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: My dear friends, you should wait until everyone gets there before you start eating.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba a, xe mlɛtsa si mlɛɛŋa Ɔkaɛ yɛ kiŋaŋaɛ ɛ, mlepe mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba.

english_verse: If you really are hungry, you can eat at home. Then you won't condemn yourselves when you meet together. After I arrive, I will instruct you about the other matters.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi kuʋɛ kwiku ɔnutɔ ɛ, ɔŋa bidɔ ní yɛ kepea mɛ, atɔ xegi mlɛtsa si mlɛŋa bidɔ mɛ ɛ, likímani Ɔkaɛ yɛ litsixwaxwa ni mlɔ aʋa a. Lɛ xegi maba ɛ, maaki mlɔ batrɔtrɔ gɔgɔ ʋa a.

english_verse: I want you to remember that before you became followers of the Lord, you were led in all the wrong ways by idols that cannot even talk.
translation_verse: Mlete si gi mleze ní kububitɛɔ mɛ ɛ, gani bedeatɔ onu i, bɛŋa mlɔ aʋa lɛ bɛgbani mlɔ ɛlila ɛtsini babua ʋa gi baʋígu kunugu kɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Now I want you to know that if you are led by God's Spirit, you will say that Jesus is Lord, and you will never curse Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mapɛ si misi mlɔ si ɔnutɔ gi ɛɛpani ní Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ kigagla ese e, ɔ́tratani kupani si “Yesu onu obusionɛ”, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kupani si “Yesu yɛ onu Ɔkaɛ”, xe aní Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛgbani yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: There are different kinds of spiritual gifts, but they all come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Bidɔkɔkɔ ede ede na alɛ, pɔ̀ Ɔfuɛfuɛ toleɔ lɔ klɔ ko ɛki bɛ petee e.

english_verse: There are different ways to serve the same Lord,
translation_verse: Axwɛwawa la bede ede ede alɛ, pɔ̀ Ɔka tole ko banima beesumu u.

english_verse: and we can each do different things. Yet the same God works in all of us and helps us in everything we do.
translation_verse: Kitèbitɛ ede ede na alɛ, pɔ̀ Aya tole ko izɛki kitèbitɛ ki ɔnɔ kakaa ki lixwɛ nɛ gi ɛɛwa a.

english_verse: The Spirit has given each of us a special way of serving others.
translation_verse: Abla kí ɔnɔ kakaa ɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ lɔ kibabaɛ kibasɛ isu ya ní kude kutɔ aʋa kí ɔnɔ kakaa yɛ kupɛ̀pɛ.

english_verse: Some of us can speak with wisdom, while others can speak with knowledge, but these gifts come from the same Spirit.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛɛki ɔnutɔ kuboesenu, lɛ ki ɔnɔ dzedze dɔni Ɔfuɛfuɛ tole lɔ klɔ kee me e, izɛki kuboete.

english_verse: To others the Spirit has given great faith or the power to heal the sick
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛki ɔnɔ tole e, kuziŋa lɛ ki ɔnɔ dzedze e, izɛki kigagla tɔ aakɔ̀ yɔni banima.

english_verse: or the power to work mighty miracles. Some of us are prophets, and some of us recognize when God's Spirit is present. Others can speak different kinds of languages, and still others can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛɛki si ɔnutɔ aabitɛ enugutseboe; lɛ ki ɔnɔ dzedze e, izɛki si odo eboe ɔplɛ; lɛ ki ɔnɔ dzedze e, tɔ ɔtani lidzegaga kukpɛ ní ifuɛfuɛ nɛ kusi i. Ki ɔnutɔ ɛ, ɛki kupani ni siya ede ede me, lɛ ki ɔnɔ dzedze dzɛ ɛ, ɛki siya ede ede na ese kububu yo.

english_verse: But it is the Spirit who does all this and decides which gifts to give to each of us. One Body with Many Parts
translation_verse: La ya petee enu Ɔfuɛfuɛ toleɔ lɔ klɔ lɔ axwɛwawa, lɛ izɛɛki bidɔ kiliɛ gi ɛɛpɛ ɛ. Kisugya tile gi kilɛ ní kupɛ̀ kupɛ̀ mɛ

english_verse: The body of Christ has many different parts, just as any other body does.
translation_verse: Kisugyaɛ kinu kidɔ tile ko, pɔ̀ kilɛ̀ ní kupɛ̀ kupɛ̀ mɛ, lɛ gi kɛ kupɛɔ kwisrɛ kyɛ ɛ, kisugya tile ko kinu. Lɛ nite Kristo kyɛ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: Some of us are Jews, and others are Gentiles. Some of us are slaves, and others are free. But God's Spirit baptized each of us and made us part of the body of Christ. Now we each drink from that same Spirit.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛkpɛ kuni iiki blɔ pɔ́ si kudzi kisugya tile, xegi Yuda ɔnɛ loo Hela ɔnɛ, banɔdzidzi loo banɔ gi bali ní ba tutɔ ba isuwla mɛ, blɔ petee Ɔfuɛfuɛ toleɔ lɔ klɔ ko Aya aki blɔ.

english_verse: Our bodies don't have just one part. They have many parts.
translation_verse: Kisugyaɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, ani kapɛ tiele ko kinu, pɔ̀ kɛ kupɛɔ kwisrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Suppose a foot says, “I'm not a hand, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the foot still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Xe ɔkli lɔ aapani si, “Agi ménu ɔwla loso e, ménu kisugyaɛ kapɛa tɔtɔ ɛ,” litráki si ɔ́traze kisugyaɛ kapɛatɔ tɔɔ.

english_verse: Or suppose an ear says, “I'm not an eye, and so I'm not part of the body.” Wouldn't the ear still belong to the body?
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi kutu yɔ kwiapani si, “Agi ménu kimbi ye loso e, ménu kisugyaɛ kapɛa tɔtɔ ɛ,” lítraki si kútraze kisugyaɛ kapɛatɔtɔ tɔɔ.

english_verse: If our bodies were only an eye, we couldn't hear a thing. And if they were only an ear, we couldn't smell a thing.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe kisugyaɛ petee kinu kimbi i, kitɛ kiabitɛ xe kianu i? Lɛ xe kisugyaɛ petee kinu kutu yɔ tete e, kitɛ kiabitɛ xe kianu ɔfu nɔ ɛ?

english_verse: But God has put all parts of our body together in the way that he decided is best.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, Aya akpɛ kisugyaɛ kapɛ kakaa ní kisugyaɛ mɛ kiliɛ gi apɛ́ si kize e.

english_verse: A body isn't really a body, unless there is more than one part.
translation_verse: Xegi si kisugyaɛ petee kize kapɛ tiele ko e, kítraze kisugya tɔɔ ɔ.

english_verse: It takes many parts to make a single body.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi lilɛ ɛ, kisugyaɛ kupɛɔ kwisrɛ, pɔ̀ kisugya tile ko kinu u.

english_verse: That's why the eyes cannot say they don't need the hands. That's also why the head cannot say it doesn't need the feet.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kimbi ye kítratani ɔwla lɔ kusɛ si, “Mázia wɔ ɔ!” Lɛ litukpo le lítratani kuklikpɔɔ kusɛ si “mázia mlɔ ɔ!”

english_verse: In fact, we cannot get along without the parts of the body that seem to be the weakest.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, kisugyaɛ kupɛ uliɔ su gi banima bɛmɔ si kigagla kíma ɛ, kɔ kwisazia.

english_verse: We take special care to dress up some parts of our bodies. We are modest about our personal parts,
translation_verse: Lɛ kisugyaɛ kùpɛ uliɔ gi kuzɛɛŋwi ŋwisi kubùbù kúma ní kɔ su i, kɔsu kuzɛɛkpɛ kubùbù i sanɔ. Lɛ kupɛ uliɔ gi kúpɛ si bamɔ ɛ, kɔ kuzekpo xe kɔsu kuzɛɛkpɛ kubùbù yo o.

english_verse: but we don't have to be modest about other parts. God put our bodies together in such a way that even the parts that seem the least important are valuable.
translation_verse: Kùpɛ uliɔ gi kwipedi i, kúzia si kukyì awla ní kɔ su u. Aya ɔtutɔ akpɛ kùpɛ uliɔ klɔ ʋa likpɔ lɛ akpɛ kubùbù ʋidi ní kɔ su kiliɛ gi liziã ɛ.

english_verse: He did this to make all parts of the body work together smoothly, with each part caring about the others.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kulɛlɛ kúma ní kisugyaɛ su, boŋu i, lilɛ si kisugyaɛ kapɛ kakaa kakɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ aki kapɛ kakaa.

english_verse: If one part of our body hurts, we hurt all over. If one part of our body is honored, the whole body will be happy.
translation_verse: Xe kapɛ tiele kɛmɔ kini i, kùpɛ gɔgɔ fɛ kwiamɔ kinie ni ka. lɛ xe bɛkpɔ̀ kapɛ tiele e, kùpɛ gɔgɔɔ fɛ kwiamɔ isami nɛ ni ka.

english_verse: Together you are the body of Christ. Each one of you is part of his body.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mlɔ mlenu Kristo yɛ kisugyaɛ, lɛ mlɔmɛ kakaa onu yɛ kapɛatɔ.

english_verse: First, God chose some people to be apostles and prophets and teachers for the church. But he also chose some to work miracles or heal the sick or help others or be leaders or speak different kinds of languages.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ mɛ ɛ, Aya edu ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, banɔkpɛkpɛ ba, ní ɔʋla kɔ ɛ, beboedoplɛ ba, ní ɔtanɛ kɔ ɛ, badɔbasɛ ba; lɛ banama gi bizɛɛbitɛ enugutseboe la ɛ, ní ba kede e, banama gi bɛki kigaglaɛ si bayɔni banyanya ba a, lɛ banama gi bɛɛpɔni banima a, lɛ banama gi bizedi axwɛ na aʋa ɛ, lɛ banɔatɔ gi bizɛpani siya ede ede na a.

english_verse: Not everyone is an apostle. Not everyone is a prophet. Not everyone is a teacher. Not everyone can work miracles.
translation_verse: Banima petee benu banɔkpɛkpɛ ba na a? Banima pɔ́ benu beboedoplɛ ba na a? Banima pɔ́ benu badɔbasɛ ba na a? Banima pɔ́ bɛki kigaglaɛ si babitɛ enugutseboe la na a?

english_verse: Not everyone can heal the sick. Not everyone can speak different kinds of languages. Not everyone can tell what these languages mean.
translation_verse: Bamɛ ɔkakaa awla kigaglaɛ kilɛ si bayɔni banyanya ba na a? Aloo tɔ bapani siya ede ede na na a? Lɛ ba pɔ́ bizebu siya sɛ ese na a?

english_verse: I want you to desire the best gifts. So I will show you a much better way.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, mlapɛ ní mlɔ ina nɛ petee bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi bimu isanɔ ɛ. Lɛ abla ɛ, matrábasɛ mlɔ kude gbã liɔ gi kwisapɛ ɛ.

english_verse: What if I could speak all languages of humans and even of angels? If I did not love others, I would be nothing more than a noisy gong or a clanging cymbal.
translation_verse: Xegi mapani banima siya ede ede na ni Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba siya sɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe kunɔlɔ kúma ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ, mɛ kunugu yo kunu kamasa aloo ɔnyɔróroe.

english_verse: What if I could prophesy and understand all mysteries and all knowledge? And what if I had faith that moved mountains? I would be nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Xegi kuboedoplɛ kɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ bilɛ ní mɛ awla a, xe mizeenu eboe kpokpo la petee ese e, xe mete eboe la petee e, lɛ xe kuziŋa gi kwiahwani eto la kuli ní mɛ awla a, pɔ̀ xe kunɔlɔ kúma ní mɛ awla ɛ, mɛ́nu kididɔ ɔ.

english_verse: What if I gave away all that I owned and let myself be burned alive? I would gain nothing, unless I loved others.
translation_verse: Xegi makɔ mɛ kidɔ kakaa makɔ akyɛ bepiplinete ba a, xe makɔ isu akɔ si bakpɛ mɛ kifu u, pɔ̀ xe kunɔlɔ kúma ní mɛ awla ɛ, mátramɔ kididɔ ɔ.

english_verse: rude. Love isn't selfish or quick tempered. It doesn't keep a record of wrongs that others do.
translation_verse: Kunɔlɔɔ kuʋídu onugu, lɛ kuʋípɛ isu ya línɛ ɛ, lɛ dzɛ ɛ, kuʋíyeʋi isu kise ise, loo bla eboe aplɛ aki banɔ.

english_verse: Love rejoices in the truth, but not in evil.
translation_verse: Kunɔlɔɔ kuʋímɔ isami nì eboe zimizi i, pɔ̀ kuzɛɛmɔ isami ni anukwarɛɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Love never fails! Everyone who prophesies will stop, and unknown languages will no longer be spoken. All that we know will be forgotten.
translation_verse: Kunɔlɔɔ kɔ onu kuʋídu. Pɔ̀ niklɔ gi kuboedoplɛ kulɛ ɛ, kwiadu; niklɔ gi siya ede ede si lɛ ɛ, sia mɔni, niklɔ gi kuboete kulɛ ɛ, kɔ onu kwiaza.

english_verse: We don't know everything, and our prophecies are not complete.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si liboe le kapɛmi ko kwite e, lɛ kuzeedo eboe plɛ kapɛ ni kapɛ ɛ;

english_verse: But what is perfect will someday appear, and what isn't perfect will then disappear.
translation_verse: pɔ̀ xe lipoe lɛ gi kidɔ ɔ gi kina kɛ lipoe le lina ɛ, kɔlɔ kidiliɛ gi kína ɛ, kialila.

english_verse: When we were children, we thought and reasoned as children do. But when we grew up, we quit our childish ways.
translation_verse: Agi meze onuvɔ ɛ, mapani ŋwisi ɔnuvɔ kinɛ, mɛ begyodi ʋa beze ɔnuvɔ banɛ, lɛ mabitɛ eboe la ŋwisi ɔnuvɔ kinɛ ɛ. Abla gi metsi i, xe medzi onyime e, mayurɔ anuvɔboe la.

english_verse: Now all we can see of God is like a cloudy picture in a mirror. Later we will see him face to face. We don't know everything, but then we will, just as God completely understands us.
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ abla ɛ, kilɛ ko ŋwisi bidɔ gi kwimɔ funufunu ní azinzi yɛ mɛ kinɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ kwitramɔ bɛ aŋu mɛ ni aŋu mɛ. Abla ɛ, mete bidɔ mɛ kapɛmiatɔ ko; pɔ̀ mátrate kidɔ kakaa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya ete me kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: For now there are faith, hope, and love. But of these three, the greatest is love.
translation_verse: Lɛ eboe tata la ya ko ɛɛgɔ kɔtsinɔ ɔ, la enu; kuziŋa, kudeonudidi, ni kunɔlɔɔ. Lilɛ gi lisa keme ní la kusi i, lɛ linu Kunɔlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Love should be your guide. Be eager to have the gifts that come from the Holy Spirit, especially the gift of prophecy.
translation_verse: Mleze ni kunɔlɔɔ kɔ kudeo kude, atɔ mlakpɛ ikũ̀ nɛ tɔ mleʋi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ, ŋwisi kuboedoplɛɔ kɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you speak languages that others don't know, God will understand what you are saying, though no one else will know what you mean. You will be talking about mysteries that only the Spirit understands.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛpani siya dzedze se e, ɔ́ʋipani iiki banɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ ɛɛpani iiki Aya a. Ni anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tranu liboe lɛ gi ɛɛpani i lɛ ese e, izɛɛpani eboe kpokpo ni yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ.

english_verse: But when you prophesy, you will be understood, and others will be helped. They will be encouraged and made to feel better.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi eedo liboe plɛ ɛ, ɛɛpani iiki banima ki ba kigagla kukpɛkpɛ, sise kukpɛkpɛ ni ba ɔna kuyɔyɔ.

english_verse: By speaking languages that others don't know, you help only yourself. But by prophesying you help everyone in the church.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛpani siya dzedze se e, izɛkpɛ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ isu kigagla, pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi eedo liboe plɛ ɛ, ɛɛkpɛ ɔsɔli ha lɔ petee kigagla.

english_verse: I am glad for you to speak unknown languages, although I prefer that you would prophesy. In fact, prophesying does much more good than speaking unknown languages, unless someone can help the church by explaining what you mean.
translation_verse: Maapɛ si mlɔ pɔ́ mlapani ní siya ede ede me, pɔ̀ liaze mɛ ole sanɔ ɔ, xegi mlɔ pɔ́ mlado eboe plɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi eedo eboe plɛ ɛ, emu asani ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛpani siya ede ede na ɛ, sani si eebu eboa la gi ɛɛpani i, ese ɛki ɔsɔli ha lɔ gi biaki ɔha lɔ aatsi i.

english_verse: My friends, what good would it do, if I came and spoke unknown languages to you and didn't explain what I meant? How would I help you, unless I told you what God had shown me or gave you some knowledge or prophecy or teaching?
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba, xe maba mlɔ klɔ ɔ, xe maba ɛpani siya ede ede na ɛki mlɔ ɛ, libi woli maabu ki mlɔ ɔ? Sani si maba ɛpani si mlɔ ní kubububasɛ, loo kuboete, loo kuboedoplɛ, loo isã itɔ su u, lɛ ko mlaanu mɛ ese e.

english_verse: If a bugle call isn't clear, how would you know to get ready for battle?
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ɔniliɛ gi eeme kakuklui ya a, xe oʋíme ka a, xe ka mɛ kaʋídra ɛ, nyaŋwɛ aabla ɔki kigũ yɛ na a?

english_verse: This is how it is when you speak unknown languages. If no one can understand what you are talking about, you will only be talking to the wind.
translation_verse: Nite ke lilɛ ki mlɔ kyɛ, kitɛ ɔnutɔ aanu liboe lɛ klɔ gi wɔɛpani i, xegi wɔ siya sɛ mɛ silídra na a? Wɔazɔ pani kpɛ ní liwɔ lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But if I don't understand the language that someone is using, we will be like foreigners to each other.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi ménu kuyaa klɔ gi ɔnutɔ ɛɛpani mɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ klɔ ʋa gi ɛɛpani i, aadzi ɔbaba ɔki mɛ, lɛ mɛ fɛ maadzi ɔbaba ɔki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you really want spiritual gifts, choose the ones that will be most helpful to the church.
translation_verse: Nite lilɛ ki mlɔ kyɛ, xegi liʋítsɔ ɛki mlɔ si mlazi ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛ, lilɛ si mlekeni edi tɔ mlabitɛ asanɔ ní bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi biakpɛ ɔha lɔ kigagla mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When we speak languages that others don't know, we should pray for the power to explain what we mean.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ɔniliɛ klɔ gi izɛɛpani siya sitɔ ɛ, ɔxwa Aya tɔ ɔki si aatani eboa la gi ɛɛpani i, ese kubu.

english_verse: For example, if I use an unknown language in my prayers, my spirit prays but my mind is useless.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi mɛɛxwa Aya ní siya ede ede na mɛ ɛ, mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛxwa Aya. Mɛ begyodi ʋa kɔ ɛ, baʋíbu libí i tɔtɔ.

english_verse: Then what should I do? There are times when I should pray with my spirit, and times when I should pray with my mind. Sometimes I should sing with my spirit, and at other times I should sing with my mind.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ ege kɛ maabitɛ ɛ? Maaxwa Aya ni mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, pɔ̀ ɛ, maaxwa Aya ni mɛ begyodi ʋa kyɛ. Maaza idzi nɛ ni mɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, pɔ̀ maaza idzi nɛ ni mɛ begyodi ʋa kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Suppose some strangers are in your worship service, when you are praising God with your spirit. If they don't understand you, how will they know to say, “Amen”?
translation_verse: Xegi wɔɛkpɔ̀ Aya ni wɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛ, kitɛ ɔnɔ gi ɔlɛ ní wɔ su u, pɔ̀ xe óʋinu wɔ liboe lɛ gi woedo ese e, kitɛ aado si liba lɛ mɛ ɛ?, xegi wɔedo Aya ɖase e gi ónu liboe le me e?

english_verse: You may be worshiping God in a wonderful way, but no one else will be helped.
translation_verse: Wɔanya zedo Aya ɖase gi lipɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ litrákpɛ ɔnɔ dzedze e, gi ɔlɛ ní wɔ su i, kigagla.

english_verse: I thank God that I speak unknown languages more than any of you.
translation_verse: Medo Aya ɖase si mizɛɛpani siya ede ede sani mlɔ petee.

english_verse: But words that make sense can help the church. This is why in church I would rather speak five words that make sense than to speak
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní ɔha lɔ kusi i, maapɛ si mipani eboe tetsu gi banima bianu la ese e, tɔ mikɔ ɛbasɛ banima bidɔ, sani si tɔ mipani eboe akpe liɔfɔ ni siya dzedze mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, stop thinking like children. Think like mature people and be as innocent as tiny babies.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, mlakize ŋwisi banuvɔ ní mlɔ begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ, mlaze ŋwisi banuvɔ ní eboe zimizi na mɛ baa, pɔ̀ mleze banɔtsitsi ni mlɔ begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures the Lord says, “I will use strangers who speak unknown languages to talk to my people. They will speak to them in foreign languages, but still my people won't listen to me.”
translation_verse: Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní ɔsã nɔ mɛ si, “Gani banama gi bizɛɛpani siya ede ede na aʋa ɛ, Lɛ gani bababa ba ba inugu le me e, Maapani ki banɔaya ʋa, pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, batráta mɛ liboe le kutu u.” Nite Ɔkaɛ apani i.

english_verse: Languages others don't know may mean something to unbelievers, but not to the Lord's followers. Prophecy, on the other hand, is for followers, not for unbelievers.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kupaniɔ ní siya ede ede na mɛ ɛ, bínu gyesi iiki baʋaziŋa ba a, boŋu i, binu gyesi iiki banama gi báziŋa ɛ; Lɛ kuboedoplɛ ɛ, kunu gyesi iiki baʋaziŋa ba lɛ aní ki banama gi báziŋa a.

english_verse: Suppose everyone in your worship service started speaking unknown languages, and some outsiders or some unbelievers come in. Won't they think you are crazy?
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔsɔli ha lɔ pɔ́ ɛtsa a, xe lɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ekpese kupani ní siya ede ede na mɛ ɛ, xe banɔatɔ gi bénu bɛ ese e, aloo xe banɔatɔ ɔ, gi báziŋa bɛ ba ɛ, bátraʋi si mlalɛ mumɔ abla na a?

english_verse: But suppose all of you are prophesying when those unbelievers and outsiders come in. They will realize that they are sinners, and they will want to change their ways because of what you are saying.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnɔ kakaa eedo Aya liboe le eplɛ ɛ, xe ɔnɔ gi ɔ́zɛ ɔŋa loo ɔnɔ gi onu liboe le ese aba ɛ, lɛ mɛ liadra kí yɛ si yenu ɔnɔ zimizi i, lɛ eboa la petee gi enu i, aabasɛ yɛ si Aya aaxwa litsi ni yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They will tell what is hidden in their hearts. Then they will kneel down and say to God, “We are certain that you are with these people.” Worship Must Be Orderly
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ begyodi kpokpo ʋa biado oŋwe no, lɛ aadɔ kesea gi aasumu Aya a, lɛ aadra lɛ mɛ si, “Anukwarɛ linu si Aya ɔlɛ ní mlɔ kusi!” Kutrɔtrɔ ní Kusɔli yɔ mɛ

english_verse: My friends, when you meet to worship, you must do everything for the good of everyone there. That's how it should be when someone sings or teaches or tells what God has said or speaks an unknown language or explains what the language means.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, liboe woli kwiapani i? Xegi mlɛtsa si mleesumu i, ɔnɔ kakaa ɛɛpɛ si yibitɛ kiditɔ, ɔnutɔ aapɛ si yiza ɔdzi, loo aapɛ si yibasɛ bidɔ, loo aapɛ si yisi mlɔ kubububasɛ gi Aya ebu abasi yɛ ɛ, loo si yipani siya ede ede, loo yɛ aapɛ si yibu sɛ ese. Pɔ̀ kidɔ kakaa gi mlɛbitɛ ɛ, lilɛ si kikpɛ mlɔ baʋaziŋa ba pɔ́ kigagla.

english_verse: No more than two or three of you should speak unknown languages during the meeting. You must take turns, and someone should always be there to explain what you mean.
translation_verse: Xegi banɔatɔ bɛpɛ si bɛɛpani ní siya ede ede me e, mlaki tiaʋa xe bɛsanɔ ɛ, tiata tɔ bapani. Lɛ lilɛ si beegu kunugu yo tole, tole ní ba ahanuglɔ ba kede e, tɔ ɔnutɔ obu eboa la gi bɛpani i, ese.

english_verse: If no one can explain, you must keep silent in church and speak only to yourself and to God.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́ma a, gi aabu eboe la ese e, kɔlɔ ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɛɛpani i, ɔdɔ zĩi ní kutsatsaa mɛ atɔ aapani kí yɛ isu ya nì Aya tètè.

english_verse: Two or three persons may prophesy, and everyone else must listen carefully.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ beboedoplɛ tiaʋa loo tiata ba pani tɔ beboedoplɛ dzedze ba bazɛɛta liboe lɛ gi bɛɛpani edi i mɔmɔmɔ.

english_verse: If someone sitting there receives a message from God, the speaker must stop and let the other person speak.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ ɛɛpani i, xe ɔnɔ dzedze gi odi kesea azɛ kubububasɛ adɔ ni Aya klɔ ɛ, lɛkɔ ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ gi ɛɛpani i, lilɛ si ɔgɔ tsriɖi.

english_verse: Let only one person speak at a time, then all of you will learn something and be encouraged.
translation_verse: Gani kude uliɔ ya onu i, mlɔ petee mlaapani Aya liboe le tole tole, lɛ mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa aakpasi bidɔ ɔ, lɛ ɔna aaze yɛ lipo o.

english_verse: God wants everything to be done peacefully and in order. When God's people meet in church,
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya ónu kumasɔmasɔ Aya a, pɔ̀ onu kusuyɔ Aya a. Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi lilɛ ní Aya yɛ baʋaziŋa ba kutsatsa kakaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: the women must not be allowed to speak. They must keep quiet and listen, as the Law of Moses teaches.
translation_verse: Badzɛ ba bagɔ tsriɖi ní kutsatsaa mɛ. Kudeonu kúma si bapani i. Lilɛ si babùbɔ isu ya ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔsã nɔ ɛpani i.

english_verse: If there is something they want to know, they can ask their husbands when they get home. It is disgraceful for women to speak in church.
translation_verse: Xegi kuʋiʋi kutɔ kulɛ ní ba awla ɛ, beʋi ba benye ba ní ba kupeo me, lɛ ese linu si linu kuye si badzɛ ba bapani ní ɔsɔli ha lɔ batsátsa ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: God's message did not start with you people, and you are not the only ones it has reached.
translation_verse: Aloo kibu mlebu si Aya yɛ liboe le e, mlɔ Korintoanɛ ma aʋa likpese na? Aloo mlɔ tètè ko liboe le lina na a?

english_verse: If you think of yourself as a prophet or a spiritual person, you will know I am writing only what the Lord has commanded.
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ akɔ lɛ si ye nu Aya yɛ oboedoplɛ ɛ, aloo akɔ lɛ si Aya aki yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ɛ, kɔ lilɛ si ɔmɔ ogyesi si liboe lɛ gi mɛɛŋwlimi i, linu ɔsã dɔni Ɔkaɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: So don't pay attention to anyone who ignores what I am writing.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔ́zɛ liboe lɛ ya ɛ, kɔlɔ mlɔ fɛ mlakíkɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ aki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: My friends, be eager to prophesy and don't stop anyone from speaking languages that others don't know.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba a, mlapɛ ni mlɔ ina nɛ petee si mlapani Aya yɛ liboe le, lɛ mlakítsi kude ɔki banama gi bizɛpani siya ede ede na a.

english_verse: But do everything properly and in order.
translation_verse: Mledi le aʋa si mlɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa mumɔ ani ní kude gbã onu.

english_verse: You will be saved by this message, if you hold firmly to it. But if you don't, your faith was all for nothing.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya linu liboe gbãɛ nɛ gi lɛ libu mlo o, xegi mlɛtrɛ lɛ aʋa a, xe mlɛzi liboe lɛ ɛ, gi mapani aki mlɔ aʋa ɛɛŋa ɛ. Pɔ̀ xegi aní nite e, kɔ mlɔ kuziŋaa kunu kufiteete.

english_verse: I told you the most important part of the message exactly as it was told to me. This part is: Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Maki mlɔ kidiliɛ gi kisaɛ veve e, gi kɛ mɛfɛ mazɛ ɛ. Kristo etse aki blɔ eboe zimizi na, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní kuŋwlimi dradraa mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He was buried, and three days later he was raised to life, as the Scriptures say.
translation_verse: Bevive ye lɛ bɛyɔni yɛ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ ní liwe tanɛ nɛ aʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi nɛ ɛkpɛ ní Kuŋwlimi dradraa mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Christ appeared to Peter, then to the twelve.
translation_verse: Ebu isu abasi Petro, lɛ klɔ ʋa kede e, banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba bɛmɔ yɛ.

english_verse: After this, he appeared to more than five hundred other followers. Most of them are still alive, but some have died.
translation_verse: Lɛ klɔ ʋa kede e, ebu isu abasi benemi gi bɛsani banɔ alafa tietsu ní lipoe tile me e, gi ba kapɛ ʋidi a kedzi alɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ bamɛ batɔ kɔɔ betse e.

english_verse: He also appeared to James, then to all of the apostles.
translation_verse: Lɛ ebu isu ya abasi Yakobo o, lɛ kede e, yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba petee e.

english_verse: Finally, he appeared to me, even though I am like someone who was born at the wrong time.
translation_verse: Lɛ gɔgɔ ɛ, mɛ fɛ mamɔ yɛ, mɛ gi bɛpɔ ŋwisi limɛ gi liwɔli kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am the least important of all the apostles. In fact, I caused so much trouble for God's church that I don't even deserve to be called an apostle.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mɛ menu ɔsisami yɛ ní yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba kusi i, gi nya mégyeno si baxwa mɛ si yɛ ɔnɔkpɛkpɛ kura kiliɛ gi meti Aya yɛ ɔha lɔ loso o.

english_verse: But God treated me with undeserved grace! He made me what I am, and his grace wasn't wasted. I worked much harder than any of the other apostles, although it was really God's grace at work and not me.
translation_verse: Gani Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ, menu kidiliɛ gi menu abla ɛ, lɛ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ libu libi kí mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si mawa axwɛ gagla sani yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba petee e, lɛ aní mɛ kigagla onu u, pɔ̀ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛɛ nɛ ɛ, gi lilɛ ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛ.

english_verse: But it doesn't matter if I preached or if they preached. All of you believed the message just the same. God's People Will Be Raised to Life
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, xegi mɛ mɛɛsɔli loo ba bɛɛsɔli i, liboe tilee lɛ klɔ gi mlɛzi ŋa ɛ, lɛ ko blɔ petee kwisɔli i. Banima kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ

english_verse: If we preach that Christ was raised from death, how can some of you say the dead will not be raised to life?
translation_verse: Xegi kwisɔli si Aya ayɔni Kristo adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, liboe woli lɛ loso mlɔ mɛ banɔatɔ bɛɛpani si banima bátrayɔ ɛdɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ?

english_verse: If they won't be raised to life, Christ himself wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si, xegi banɔ bátrayɔ ɛdɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, lɛ kɔ Aya ɔ́yɔni Kristo ɔ́dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ o.

english_verse: And if Christ wasn't raised to life, our message is worthless, and so is your faith.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi Aya ɔ́yɔni Kristo ɔ́dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ ɔ liboe lɛ gi kwipani si mlɔ ní Aya su i, línu kididɔ ɔ, lɛ mlɔ kuziŋaa kyɛ kúbu libi i.

english_verse: If the dead won't be raised to life, we have told lies about God by saying he raised Christ to life, when he really did not.
translation_verse: Sanɔ ɛ, libasɛ si, kwiŋa asia katsali ní Aya su u, lɛ ese linu si kwipani si Aya ayɔni Kristo adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ. Pɔ̀ lɛ ɔ́yɔni yɛ ɛ, xegi linu anukwarɛ ɛ, si banɔ bátrayɔ ɛdɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So if the dead won't be raised to life, Christ wasn't raised to life.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi Aya ɔ́yɔni bekpo ʋa ɛ, kɔlɔ ɔ́yɔni Kristo fɛ o.

english_verse: Unless Christ was raised to life, your faith is useless, and you are still living in your sins.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔ́yɔni Kristo e, kɔlɔ mlɔ kuziŋaa kúbu libi o, kɔlɔ mledzi alɛ ní mlɔ eboe zimizi na mɛ.

english_verse: And those people who died after putting their faith in him are completely lost.
translation_verse: Libasɛ si banama kyɛ gi bɛzi Yesu aʋa ɛɛŋa a, xe betse e, bɛlila!

english_verse: If our hope in Christ is good only for this life, we are worse off than anyone else.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi blɔ kudeonudidi yo ní Kristo me kunu ki ligbã́ lɛ ya ko e, kɔlɔ kunu basigaganete sani ɔnɔ kakaa ní kelea mɛ.

english_verse: But Christ has been raised to life! And he makes us certain that others will also be raised to life.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ anukwarɛɛ linu si Aya ayɔni Kristo adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ. Yɛ onu ɔtɔpu yɛ gi onu liklatrɔtrɔ si biayɔ ni banama petee gi betse e.

english_verse: Just as we will die because of Adam, we will be raised to life because of Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kutse kwiba kelea mɛ gani ɔnɔ aʋa ɛ, kuyɔyɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ kyɛ kwiba gani ɔnɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: But we must each wait our turn. Christ was the first to be raised to life, and his people will be raised to life when he returns.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔnɔ kakaa ní yɛ lipoe le aʋa a: Kristo yɛ beetu kuyɔnɔ ɔ, lɛ kede e, biayɔni banama petee gi benu Kristo yɛ banɛ ɛ, xegi edzì aba ɛ.

english_verse: Then after Christ has destroyed all powers and forces, the end will come, and he will give the kingdom to God the Father.
translation_verse: Lɛ klɔ ʋa kede e, kelea kiagù, lɛ aadzi ni kaʋaŋaa kí yɛ ka Aya a, xegi ezizi ɔʋaŋa kakaa ni ozekɔ kakaa ni kigagla kakaa kede e.

english_verse: Christ will rule until he puts all his enemies under his power,
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si lilɛ si Kristo ɔŋa kukusi ga kɔkpɛni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi aakpɛ yɛ ahwafiɔnɛ kakaa ní yɛ kukliseo e.

english_verse: and the last enemy he destroys will be death.
translation_verse: Lɛ ahwafiɔnɛ gɔgɔ ɛ, gi yɛ aʋa aaŋa ɛ, yɛ onu kutseo.

english_verse: When the Scriptures say he will put everything under his power, they don't include God. It was God who put everything under the power of Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si, “Aya akɔ kidɔ kakaa akpɛ ní yɛ kukliseo.” Abla xegi liboe li lipani si “kidɔ kakaa bɛkpɛ kɛ ní yɛ ese e,” lɛ mɛ lidra sì Aya ɔtutɔ lɔ ɔ, gi yɛ akɔ kidɔ kakaa akpɛ ní Kristo ese e, ɔ́ma ni lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After everything is under the power of God's Son, he will put himself under the power of God, who put everything under his Son's power. Then God will mean everything to everyone.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe Aya abitɛ la ya petee e, kɔlɔ yɛ Yebi ye ɔtutɔ ɛ, aakɔ̀ yɛ isu ya kpɛ ní Aya ese e, yɛ gi yɛ akɔ kidɔ kakaa akpɛ ni Kristo ese e; lɛ Aya aaŋa kukusi yo ní kidɔ kakaa aʋa a.

english_verse: If the dead are not going to be raised to life, what will people do who are being baptized for them? Why are they being baptized for those dead people?
translation_verse: Abla xegi batráyɔni bekpo ʋa ɛ, liboe woli liaba banama gi bɛkpɛ kuni kɔ ki banama gi betse e aʋa? Lɛ xegi bekpo ʋa batráyɔ ɛ, ege loso bɛɛkpɛ kuni ki banima ní ba linyi ne me e?

english_verse: and face death every day? The pride that I have in you because of Christ Jesus our Lord is what makes me say this.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, maka si mizɛɛmɔ kutseo aŋu mɛ liwe kakaa! Liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani linu si, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mizeefeke isu ni mlɔ aʋa ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ Kristo Yesu me.

english_verse: Don't fool yourselves. Bad friends will destroy you.
translation_verse: Mlakíki tɔ bemeni mlo, “Ɔha nyani nyani mɛ kuzeze kuze zizi ligbã́zeze gbã.”

english_verse: Be sensible and stop sinning. You should be embarrassed that some people still don't know about God. What Our Bodies Will Be Like
translation_verse: Mlenyime ní mlɔ begyodi ʋa mɛ kiliɛ gi lilɛ si lize e, tɔ mlamɔni eboe zimizi na kubitɛ ɛ. Lɛ linu kuye ɛki mlɔ si, mipani sì mlɔ mɛ banɔatɔ ɛ, béte Aya a. Kisugyaɛ kuyɔyɔ

english_verse: Some of you have asked, “How will the dead be raised to life? What kind of bodies will they have?”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔnutɔ aaʋi si, “Kitɛ biayɔ ni bekpo ʋa a, xe biadzi ligbã ɛ? Kisugya wolilu kiaze ní ba awla a?”

english_verse: Don't be foolish. A seed must die before it can sprout from the ground.
translation_verse: Linukpaboe woli lɛ ya! Kidiliɛ gi mlɛkpɛ ɛ, kiʋídzi ligbã́ sani si kitse xe ɛ.

english_verse: Wheat seeds and all other seeds look different from the sprouts that come up.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mlɛkpɛ bidɔ ɛ, aní ose ɔlɔ gi aaze e, lɔ mlɛkpɛ pɔ̀ lisebi i, nyafɛ adeʋi loo esebi dzedze.

english_verse: This is because God gives everything the kind of body he wants it to have.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya aki lɛ kilɛlɛ liɛ mɛ gi apɛ si ose lo aaze e; lɛ ki lisebi kakaa ɛ, izɛki lɛ lɛ kilɛlɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Everything in the heavens has a body, and so does everything on earth. But each one is very different from all the others.
translation_verse: Lɛ bisugya ʋɛ bilɛ ní lifunu ne fɛ, lɛ bisugya ʋɛ bilɛ ní kesea aʋa; kupedi uliɔ klɔ gi kuli ní lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ su i, kunu lide tile lɛ kupedi uliɔ gi kuli ní kesea ʋa bidɔ mɛ su i, kunu kidɔ dzedze.

english_verse: The sun isn't like the moon, the moon isn't like the stars, and each star is different.
translation_verse: Liwo le lɛ kupedi yo kunu lide tile, ɔdzidzi yɛ yɛ kupedi yo kunu kudzedze, lɛ badzidziebi ʋa fɛ ba kupedi yo kunu kudzedze. Lɛ badzidziebi bi ʋa ba kupedi yo kulɛ lidze lidze.

english_verse: That's how it will be when our bodies are raised to life. These bodies will die, but the bodies that are raised will live forever.
translation_verse: Nite litraze xe bɛyɔni bekpo ʋa a, xe bedzi ligbã́ a. Kisugyaɛ liɛ gi bizɛkpɛ ní sise se me xe gi kwitse e, kizeezizi pɔ̀ xegi bɛyɔni kɛ ɛ, kiaze ligbã́ nɛ gi kitrazizi i.

english_verse: As surely as there are physical bodies, there are spiritual bodies. And our physical bodies will be changed into spiritual bodies.
translation_verse: Bevive ke kesea ʋa ya kisugya; pɔ̀ biayɔni kɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ mɛ kisugya a. Lɛ ese linu si xegi kelea mɛ kisugyaɛ kilɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kisugyaɛ fɛ kilɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The first man was named Adam, and the Scriptures tell us that he was a living person. But Jesus, who may be called the last Adam, is a life-giving spirit.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛŋwlimi si “Ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ Adam edzì kidɔ gbagbe,” Pɔ̀ Adam gɔgɔɛ gi yɛ onu Kristo ye e, onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ gi izɛki ligbã́ a.

english_verse: We see that the one with a spiritual body did not come first. He came after the one who had a physical body.
translation_verse: Aní kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kitu ba a, pɔ̀ kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní kelea lia mɛ kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ kede e, lɛ kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ kɛ kigba ɛkpɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The first man was made from the dust of the earth, but the second man came from heaven.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ tɔpu yɛ ɛ, sise bɛkɔ ɛ ʋɔ yɛ, lɛ ádɔ kesea mɛ ɛ; pɔ̀ ɔnɔ ʋla yɛ ádɔ ní lifunu ne e.

english_verse: Everyone on earth has a body like the body of the one who was made from the dust of the earth. And everyone in heaven has a body like the body of the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Banama gi bali kesea aʋa ɛ, balɛ ŋwisi ɔniliɛ klɔ gi sise se bɛkɔ ɛ ʋɔ yɛ ɛ; lɛ banama gi bali ní lifunu ne e, ba kyɛ ba lɛ ŋwisi ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ádɔ ní lifunu ne e.

english_verse: Just as we are like the one who was made out of earth, we will be like the one who came from heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ kiliɛ gi kulɛ abla ŋwisi ɔniliɛ gi ádɔ ní kesea aʋa ɛ, niteme ke kwitraze ŋwisi ɔniliɛ gi ádɔ ní lifunu ne e.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that our bodies of flesh and blood will decay. This means they cannot share in God's kingdom, which lasts forever.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba a, liboe lɛ gi mɛɛpani linu si blɔ kesea aʋa ya kisugyaɛ ni ika lɛ ɛ, bítratani ɔŋakɔ kumɔ ní Aya yɛ kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ. Bidɔ gi bizeezizi i, bítratani ɔŋakɔ kumɔ ní bidɔ gi bitrázizi i me.

english_verse: I will explain a mystery to you. Not every one of us will die, but we will all be changed.
translation_verse: Mlata kutu tɔ mido liboe kpokpo gi litu banima kugyɔ ɛ si mlɔ. Blɔ petee kutrátse e, pɔ̀ bɛtrákyi blɔ petee blɔ kilɛlɛɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It will happen suddenly, quicker than the blink of an eye. At the sound of the last trumpet the dead will be raised. We will all be changed, so we will never die again.
translation_verse: Liaba lɛ mɛ ní lipoe kpekpe etɔ mɛ, kilɛ gi ɔnɔ aada yɛ bimbi ʋe ʋleʋleʋle e, xegi beme kakuklui gɔgɔa ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si xegi beme kakuklui ya ɛ, banama gi betse e, biayɔni ba gi batrátse tɔ ɔ. Lɛ blɔ banama gi kuli ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, biakyi blɔ kyɛ blɔ kizezee me.

english_verse: The bodies we now have are weak and can die. But they will be changed into bodies that are eternal. Then the Scriptures will come true, “Death has lost the battle!
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi blɔ bisugyaɛ ʋɛ gi bizeezizi i, xegi bekyi ʋe e, xe bidzi bisugya gi bitrázizi liwewe e, xe kisugyaɛ liɛ gi kizeetse e, xe kidzi kisugya gi kitrátse liwewe e, kɔ Aya yɛ liboe lɛ gi bɛŋwlimi plɛ ɛ, liba lɛ mɛ si: “Bɛmlɔ kutseo ní kaʋaŋaa mɛ ɛ!”

english_verse: Where is its victory? Where is its sting?”
translation_verse: “Oo Kutse nifɔ wɔ kuʋaŋaa kulɛ ɛ? Kutseo nifɔ wɔ ome no ɔlɛ ɛ?”

english_verse: Sin is what gives death its sting, and the Law is the power behind sin.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kutseo kɔ ome no onu kuzimizi yo, lɛ kuzimizi yo kuzɛɛzi kɔ kigaglaɛ dɔni ɔsã nɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But thank God for letting our Lord Jesus Christ give us the victory!
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kwido Aya ɖase gi yɛ aki si kwiŋa aʋa gani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo me e!

english_verse: My dear friends, stand firm and don't be shaken. Always keep busy working for the Lord. You know that everything you do for him is worthwhile.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi lɔlɔ ba a, mlalɛɛ gaglaa tɔ mlakíki si kididɔ kihwani mlɔ ɔ. Mlakɔ mlɔ isu ya petee aki Ɔkaɛ yɛ axwɛ na lipoe kakaa, lɛ ese linu si mlete si mlɔ axwɛ na ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ ɛ, atráze efiteete e.

english_verse: When you collect money for God's people, I want you to do exactly what I told the churches in Galatia to do.
translation_verse: Abla ní mlɔ beʋiʋi ʋa ní bidɔ kɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi bɛkpɛ likpɔ ɛki Aya yɛ banima su i, lilɛ si mlabitɛ kiliɛ gi masi ɔsɔli ha lɔ ní Galatia si babitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: That is, each Sunday each of you must put aside part of what you have earned. If you do this, you won't have to take up a collection when I come.
translation_verse: Mlɔ mɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔbla kibɔɛ liɛ gi mlɛmɔ ɛ, kɛ kapɛatɔ ní ogle no lɔ liwe tɔpu nɛ aʋa a, atɔ likízia tɔɔ si mlaazɛ gyɔ bibɔ xe maba a.

english_verse: Choose some followers to take the money to Jerusalem. I will send them on with the money and with letters which show that you approve of them.
translation_verse: Xegi maba ɛ maŋwlimi kuplekpa gi kwiabu banama a, gi mlaakya ɛbasɛ ɛ, lɛ ba biakɔ mlɔ bidɔ kɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛtsini Yerusalem.

english_verse: If you think I should go along, they can go with me. Paul's Travel Plans
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi liaziã ɛ, mɛ ni ba kwiaga kudeo trɛ. Paulo yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛpɛ ɔ

english_verse: After I have gone through Macedonia, I hope to see you
translation_verse: Maaba mlɔ klɔ xegi mazɛga Makedonia ɛ, lɛ ese linu si mabitɛ kutrɔtrɔ si mizɛɛga Makedonia.

english_verse: and visit with you for a while. I may even stay all winter, so that you can help me on my way to wherever I will be going next.
translation_verse: Nyafɛ maaze ní mlɔ su taʋɛ, loo nyafɛ kublɔ lipoe le petee, atɔ mlaatani mɛ kupɔnɔ ní mɛ kude gaga mɛ tsinɔ níklɔ gi matradzɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If the Lord lets me, I would rather come later for a longer visit than to stop off now for only a short visit.
translation_verse: Mápɛ si miba edi mlo ko o, tɔ mitsa ɛza; mapɛ si miba eze ní mlɔ su lipoe gblamadza a, xe Ɔkaɛ aki kude e.

english_verse: I will stay in Ephesus until Pentecost,
translation_verse: Maaze ni ya ní Efeso zɛkɔna ni Pentekoste liwe le aʋa.

english_verse: because there is a wonderful opportunity for me to do some work here. But there are also many people who are against me.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kudeonu kwidra ɛki mɛ ni ya kí axwɛ gbã kuwawa, linya ɛli lɛ mɛ si banɔ glegle bɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su.

english_verse: When Timothy arrives, give him a friendly welcome. He is doing the Lord's work, just as I am.
translation_verse: Xegi Timoteo aba mlɔ klɔ ɛ, mlakíki lifu liku ye e, gi ɔlɛ ní mlɔ kusi i, lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ yɛ axwɛ ɛɛwa, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mɛ fɛ mɛɛwa la kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't let anyone mistreat him. I am looking for him to return to me together with the other followers. So when he leaves, send him off with your blessings.
translation_verse: Mlakígbe si mlátrazi yɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mlapɔni yɛ tɔ odziba ní mɔ klɔ ní kusuyɔ mɛ; meedi yɛ kudeonu si aaba nì baʋaziŋa dzedze ba.

english_verse: I have tried hard to get our friend Apollos to visit you with the other followers. He doesn't want to come just now, but he will come when he can. Personal Concerns and Greetings
translation_verse: Abla ní blɔ onemi ye Apolo su i, mɛɛkpɛ yɛ kunugu yo veve si tɔ ɔbadi mlo ni baʋaziŋa gɔgɔ ba, pɔ̀ ɔ́pɛ si yiba ní lipoe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ. Xegi amɔ kudeonu dzedze e, aaba di mlo.

english_verse: Keep alert. Be firm in your faith. Stay brave and strong.
translation_verse: Mleze ní aŋu, tɔ mlalɛɛ mumɔ ní kuziŋaa mɛ, ɔna oze ní mlɔ lipo tɔ mleze gagla.

english_verse: Show love in everything you do.
translation_verse: Mlabitɛ kidɔ kakaa ní kulɔlɔ mɛ.

english_verse: You know that Stephanas and his family were the first in Achaia to have faith in the Lord. They have done all they can for God's people. My friends, I ask you
translation_verse: Mlete sì Stefana ni yɛ kepea ɛ; ba beze baʋaziŋa tɔpu ba ní Axaya, lɛ bɛkɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki Aya yɛ banima a. Maki mlɔ litukpo, mɛ benemi ba si,

english_verse: to obey leaders like them and to do the same for all others who work hard with you.
translation_verse: mlakɔ isu akpɛ ní banɔaklɔ elu ese e, ani ɔnɔ kakaa gi ɛɛwa axwɛ na a, xe eesumu ni ba ese e.

english_verse: I was glad to see Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus. Having them here was like having you.
translation_verse: Isami iku me si Stefana ni Fortunato ani Axaiko bɛba lɛ ese linu si bɛmani mɛ kidiliɛ gi kíma ní mɛ awla ɛ.

english_verse: They made me feel much better, just as they made you feel better. You should appreciate people like them.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkpɛ mɛ ɔna nɔ nì mlɔ ina nɛ kyɛ isami i. Banɔaklɔ ʋa elue e, lilɛ si ba kiboebitɛɛ kiku aŋu u. Paulo yɛ kunɔpanɔ gɔgɔɔ

english_verse: Greetings from the churches in Asia. Aquila and Priscilla, together with the church that meets in their house, send greetings in the name of the Lord.
translation_verse: Isɔliha lɛ ní Asia kesea mɛ ɛ, ipani mlɔ, Akwila ni Priskila ani ɔha lɔ gi izɛɛtsa ní ba kepea mɛ ɛ, bɛpani mlɔ mɔmɔmɔ ni isami.

english_verse: All the Lord's followers send their greetings. Give each other a warm greeting.
translation_verse: Benemi ba pɔ́ ní ya a, bɛpani mlɔ. Mlakpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba wolaa ní sinemi me.

english_verse: I am signing this letter myself: PAUL.
translation_verse: Mɛ Paulo mɛ maŋwlimi kunɔpanɔ liɔ ya nì mɛ awla la.

english_verse: I pray that God will put a curse on everyone who doesn't love the Lord. And may the Lord come soon.
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ ɔ́lɔ blɔ Ɔkaɛ ɛ, ifixoxo igbani yɛ kpɛ. Oo Ɔka ba!

english_verse: I pray the Lord Jesus will be kind to you.
translation_verse: Blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lize ni mlo.

english_verse: I love everyone who belongs to Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Mɛ kulɔlɔ ki mlɔ petee ní Kristo Yesu me e. Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: From Paul, chosen by God to be an apostle of Jesus Christ, and from Timothy, who is also a follower. To God's church in Corinth and to all of God's people in Achaia.
translation_verse: Keplekpaa lia ya kɛdɔ ní mɛ Paulo gi menu Kristo Yesu yɛ ɔnɔkpɛkpɛ ɛ, gani Aya yɛ kupɛ́pɛ onu ani blɔ onemi ye Timoteo klɔ. Kí Aya yɛ ɔsɔli ha lɔ ní Korinto kɔpɔni kí Aya yɛ banima petee ní Axaya kesea mɛ:

english_verse: I pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace! Paul Gives Thanks
translation_verse: Atɔ Aya blɔ Ɔka ani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo baki mlɔ lipomepɛpɛ ni kusuyɔ. Aya yɛ Izɛyɔ Ɔnɔ Kakaa yɛ Ɔna

english_verse: Praise God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ! The Father is a merciful God, who always gives us comfort.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kudo Aya gi onu blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo Yɛka ɖase e. Ɔka gi izedi banɔ asigaga a, xe yɛ izɛki ɔnɔ kakaa ɔnadɔdɔ ɛ!

english_verse: He comforts us when we are in trouble, so that we can share this same comfort with others in trouble.
translation_verse: Yɛ izɛɛpɔni blɔ ní blɔ baxixa ʋa petee me tɔ blɔ kwitani banama gi beku baxixa ede ede na mɛ ɛ, kupɔnɔ, kiliɛ gi blɔ fɛ Aya apɔni blɔ ɛ.

english_verse: We share in the terrible sufferings of Christ, but also in the wonderful comfort he gives.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kiliɛ gi Kristo yɛ ɔzia mɔmɔ nɔ ɛgbigba eku kpaŋu ní blɔ agbã́ na mɛ ɛ, nite ke gani Kristo me e, blɔ ɔnadɔdɔ lɔ aagbigba.

english_verse: We suffer in the hope that you will be comforted and saved. And because we are comforted, you will also be comforted, as you patiently endure suffering like ours.
translation_verse: Xegi kuli ní kuyeʋiyeʋi me e, linu mlɔ ɔnadɔdɔ ni kubúbú; lɛ xegi bɛyɔ blɔ ɔna ɛ, linu ɔnadɔdɔ ɛki mlɔ, gi lɔ izɛɛmani litɔplɛplɛkese ní igyidodo me ki mlɔ ní ɔzia nɔ ke gi kwimɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You never disappoint us. You suffered as much as we did, and we know that you will be comforted as we were.
translation_verse: Lɛ blɔ kudeonudidi yo ki mlɔ kuli gagla lɛ ese linu si kwite si ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɛɛmɔ ɔzia nɔ ɛpɔni blɔ ɛ, nite ke mlamɔ ɔnadɔdɔ ni blɔ.

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know what a hard time we had in Asia. Our sufferings were so horrible and so unbearable that death seemed certain.
translation_verse: Kúpɛ si mleze ní litumu me benemi ba a, ní kupepe uliɔ gi kwiba blɔ aʋa ní Asia kesea mɛ su u. Bemi ní blɔ aʋa gbɛgbɛ gi lisani blɔ kututu si ɔna óze ni blɔ lipo tɔ ɔ, si kwiaze ligbã́ kyɛ.

english_verse: In fact, we felt sure we were going to die. But this made us stop trusting in ourselves and start trusting God, who raises the dead to life.
translation_verse: Ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, kwimɔ egyesi si bɛtsa kutsépia ɛki blɔ. Pɔ̀ liboe lɛ ya liba lɛ mɛ nite tɔ kukíkɔ aŋu atrɔ ní blɔ isu ya aʋa a, pɔ̀ tɔ kukɔ trɔ ní Aya aʋa a, gi yɛ izɛɛyɔni bekpo ʋa ɛ.

english_verse: God saved us from the threat of death, and we are sure that he will do it again and again.
translation_verse: Yɛ ebu blo adɔ ni kuxixa ʋidi uliɔ ya mɛ ɛ, lɛ aabu blo o. Lɛ loso e, kwikɔ blɔ kudeonudidi yo ɛtrɔ ní yɛ aʋa si aatrɛ lɛ aʋa gi aabu blo o,

english_verse: Please help us by praying for us. Then many people will give thanks for the blessings we receive in answer to all these prayers. Paul's Change of Plans
translation_verse: kiliɛ gi mlɛpɔni blɔ ɔ, gani mlɔ Aya kuxwaxwa mɛ. Kɔ banɔ glegle biado ɖase ní blɔ litukpo kɔkpɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ kupɔnɔ liɔ gi bɛki blɔ ɔ, gi banɔ glegle bɛxwa Aya loso o. Paulo Atsrɛ Yɛ Batrɔtrɔ ʋa

english_verse: We can be proud of our clear conscience. We have always lived honestly and sincerely, especially when we were with you. And we were guided by God's gift of undeserved grace instead of by the wisdom of this world.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, liboe lɛ ya aʋa kwidɔ isu ya; blɔ ina nɛ iisi blɔ si kwivu isu ya mumɔ ní kelea lia ya mɛ. Liveve le, ní blɔ ni mlɔ siŋanɛ sɛ mɛ, ní kudradra ni anukwarɛɛ gi bidɔ ní Aya kɔ mɛ ɛ. Kwibitɛ lɛ aní gani kelea mɛ kuboete me e, pɔ̀ gani Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am not writing anything you cannot read and understand. I hope you will understand it completely,
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si aní eboe dzedze gi mlatrátani la kukla a loo gi mlatrátani la ese kunu i, la kwiŋwlimi kí mlɔ. Lɛ meedi kudeonu si i

english_verse: just as you already partly understand us. Then when our Lord Jesus returns, you can be as proud of us as we are of you.
translation_verse: ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlenu blɔ ese kapɛ ni kapɛ ɛ, mlaba nu blɔ ese mɔmɔmɔ tɔ mlaatani isu kudɔ ní blɔ aʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kwiadɔ isu ní mlɔ aʋa a, ní Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe le aʋa kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I was so sure of your pride in us that I had planned to visit you first of all. In this way you would have the blessing of two visits from me—
translation_verse: Gi maka ní lɛ aʋa loso e, matrɔ si maaba di mlo kpɛ ɔtɔ, atɔ mlɔamɔ ebibubu taʋa.

english_verse: once on my way to Macedonia and again on my return from there. Then you could send me on to Judea.
translation_verse: Matrɔ si maaba di mlo xegi mɛɛtrɛ ní Makedonia ɛ, lɛ xegi madɔ ní Makedonia ɛ, maga mlɔ klɔ tɔ mlaakpɛ mɛ kude ní mɛ kudegaga trɛ ní Yudea mɛ.

english_verse: Do you think I couldn't make up my mind about what to do? Or do I seem like someone who says “Yes” or “No” simply to please others?
translation_verse: Gi mɛɛbitɛ batrɔtrɔ ba ya ʋa ɛ, mábitɛ lɛ ŋwisi liwaʋíboe kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ mábitɛ mɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa ní kelea lia mɛ bapɛ́pɛ ʋa onu u, xe maatani kupani ní onugu tole ɔlɔ klɔ mɛ si Ee, Ee, loo Owo, owo o.

english_verse: God can be trusted, and so can I, when I say our answer to you has always been “Yes” and never “No.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kikakaa elue e, gi Aya onu anukwarɛ onete e, blɔ liboe le kí mlɔ línu Ee ni Owo.

english_verse: This is because Jesus Christ the Son of God is always “Yes” and never “No.” And he is the one Silas, Timothy, and I told you about.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya Yebi ye Yesu Kristo, gi Sila, Timoteo ani mɛ kwisɔli ní mlɔ kusi i, ónu ɔnɔ gi onu “Ee” ani “Owo”, boŋu i, yɛ liboe le lipoe kakaa linu si “Ee.”

english_verse: Christ says “Yes” to all God's promises. This is why we have Christ to say “Amen” for us to the glory of God.
translation_verse: Lɛ líbitɛ liboeboe xe Aya aka aplɛ tasɛ tasɛ ɛ, la petee enu “Ee” ní Kristo me. Lɛ loso e, gani Yesu Kristo me e, kuzeedo si “Liba lɛ mɛ” ki Aya yɛ kusudra a.

english_verse: And so God makes it possible for you and us to stand firmly together with Christ. God is also the one who chose us
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, Aya izɛki si blɔ ni mlɔ kuzɛɛlɛɛ gagla ní Kristo me. Yɛ asra kumu yɔ aki blɔ

english_verse: God is my witness that I stayed away from Corinth, just to keep from being hard on you.
translation_verse: Maxwa Aya si yɛ ɔŋa katsalia ɔki mɛ si médzi aba Korinto tɔ ɔ, atɔ mibu mlo ní kunugu me.

english_verse: We are not bosses who tell you what to believe. We are working with you to make you glad, because your faith is strong.
translation_verse: Aní kipɛ́ kwipɛ si kutrɔ ɛki mlɔ kidiliɛ gi kɛ aʋa mlaziŋa; pɔ̀ kwiwa axwɛ na ni mlɔ kí mlɔ isami i, lɛ ese linu si gani kuziŋaa mɛ mlalɛɛ gagla a.

english_verse: I have decided not to make my next visit with you so painful.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso matrɔ lɛ si mátraba ɛkpɛ mlɔ ɔnatata tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: If I make you feel bad, who would be left to cheer me up, except the people I had made to feel bad?
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi makpɛ mlɔ kuyeʋiyeʋi i, nyaŋwɛ aagɔ gi yɛ aakpɛ mɛ isami i, sani mlɔ banɔaklɔ ke gi mlɔ maki si mlenu kuyeʋiyeʋi yo e?

english_verse: The reason I want to be happy is to make you happy. I wrote as I did because I didn't want to visit you and be made to feel bad, when you should make me feel happy.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso maŋwlimi keplekpaa kiliɛ gi maŋwlimi ka ɛ, tɔ xegi maba ɛ, lítraze ɔna nyipu iiki mɛ ɛ, ní mlɔ banama gi lilɛ si mlakpɛ mɛ isami nɛ ɛ, mlɔ aŋu mɛ tɔɔ. Lɛ maka ní mlɔ petee aʋa si xegi mɛɛmɔ isami nɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ mlɔ petee fɛ, isami nɛ iaku mlo.

english_verse: At the time I wrote, I was suffering terribly. My eyes were full of tears, and my heart was broken. But I didn't want to make you feel bad. I only wanted to let you know how much I cared for you. Forgiveness
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si maŋwlimi aki mlɔ ni ɔna nyipu ni ɔnatata ani kube; aní mɛ kutrɔtrɔ linu si mikpɛ mlɔ asigaga a, pɔ̀ tɔ mibu mɛ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ kidzudzu ye me basɛ mlɔ ɔ. Paulo abasɛ bidɔ ní kiliɛ gi biakɔ eboe zimizi na kɔkyɛ su u

english_verse: I don't want to be hard on you. But if one of you has made someone feel bad, I am not really the one who has been made to feel bad. Some of you are the ones.
translation_verse: Abla xe ɔnutɔ aki si ɔnutɔ ɔmɔ ɔnatata ɛ, ɔ́bitɛ lɛ mani mɛ tete su u, pɔ̀ abitɛ lɛ amani mlɔ petee su u, lɛ ní kude kutɔ onu i, mápɛ si mibitɛ lɛ si linyani isanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Most of you have already pointed out the wrong that person did, and this is punishment enough for what was done.
translation_verse: Lisrɛ si mlɔ banima kapɛ ʋidi a mlehe kutu kí oniliɛ klɔ.

english_verse: When people sin, you should forgive and comfort them, so they won't give up in despair.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, lilɛ si mlakɔ akyɛ yɛ tɔ mlayɔ yɛ ɔna, atɔ ɔki wɔ ní asigaga la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You should make them sure of your love for them.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, maki mlɔ litukpo si mlatrɔ likla ní lɛ aʋa aki yɛ si mlɛlɔ yɛ.

english_verse: I also wrote because I wanted to test you and find out if you would follow my instructions.
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso maŋwlimi keplekpaa lia aki mlɔ ɛ, kɛ ese kinu si, mapɛ si mite xe mlɛtani kulɛɛ ní kufekedi uliɔ ya mɛ ɛ, ani xe mlenu kutu ní liboe kakaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I will forgive anyone you forgive. Yes, for your sake and with Christ as my witness, I have forgiven whatever needed to be forgiven.
translation_verse: Xegi mlɛkɔ ɔnutɔ yɛ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ yɛ ɛ, mɛ fɛ makɔ la kukyɛ yɛ. Lɛ kidiliɛ gi makɔ akyɛ ɛ, xe kiditɔ kilɛ gi maakɔ kyɛ ɛ, makɔ kɛ akyɛ ní Kristo aŋu mɛ kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso,

english_verse: I have done this to keep Satan from getting the better of us. We all know what goes on in his mind.
translation_verse: atɔ mitsi kude kí Ozimizi ye tɔ ɔkiŋa blɔ aʋa a. Lɛ ese linu si kwite yɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa a. Paulo nì Kunyanya Vuvu Yɔ ní Troa

english_verse: When I went to Troas to preach the good news about Christ, I found that the Lord had already prepared the way.
translation_verse: Agi mazeku Troa si mipani Liboe Gbã nɛ ní Kristo su i, mamɔ si Ɔkaɛ adra kudeonu akí axwɛ na ní klɔ.

english_verse: But I was worried when I didn't find my friend Titus there. So I left the other followers and went on to Macedonia.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛ begyodi ʋa mɛ bádɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si mámɔ mɛ onemi ye Tito ní klɔ ɔ. Lɛ loso maklá banima ní iklɔ lɛ maza atrɛ ní Makedonia ɛ.

english_verse: I am grateful that God always makes it possible for Christ to lead us to victory. God also helps us spread the knowledge about Christ everywhere, and this knowledge is like the smell of perfume.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kwido Aya ɖase e! Gi yɛ izɛgbani blɔ lipoe kakaa a, ní ɔga nɔ mɛ ní Kristo yɛ kuʋaŋaa kukli yɔ mɛ ɛ. Lɛ gani blɔ aʋa ɛ, Aya izeehle Kristo yɛ kisuwa flafla kɛ gi kinu yɛ kuteteo ní ɔkɔ kakaa a.

english_verse: In fact, God thinks of us as a perfume that brings Christ to everyone. For people who are being saved, this perfume has a sweet smell and leads them to a better life. But for people who are lost, it has a bad smell and leads them to a horrible death. No one really has what it takes to do this work.
translation_verse: Kí ɔnutɔ ɛ, kunu ɔfu zimizi gi aaki kutse e; pɔ̀ ki ɔnɔ dzedze e, kunu ɔfu flafla gi aaki ligbã a. Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aatani lixwɛ nɛ ya onu kulɛ ɛ?

english_verse: A lot of people try to get rich from preaching God's message. But we are God's sincere messengers, and by the power of Christ we speak our message with God as our witness.
translation_verse: Kúma ŋwisi banɔ glegle dzedze ba ɛ, kuʋída Aya yɛ liboe le ki kibɔ ɔ, boŋu i, ní Kristo me e, kuzɛɛpani Aya yɛ liboe le ní Aya aŋu mɛ ni anukwarɛɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi bɛkpɛ dɔni Aya kɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Are we once again bragging about ourselves? Do we need letters to you or from you to tell others about us? Some people do need letters telling about them.
translation_verse: Kɔ isu kwikpese kukpɔ̀ dzɛ a? Loo ŋwisi banɔ dzedzeatɔ kinɛ ɛ, kizia kwizia kuplekpa gi kwipani ní blɔ su kí mlɔ ɔ, loo dɔni mlɔ klɔ ki banɔ dzedze ba na a?

english_verse: But you are our letter, and you are in our hearts for everyone to read and understand.
translation_verse: Mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlenu blɔ keplekpaa gi bɛŋwlimi ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, gi ɔnɔ kakaa ete e, xe ɔnɔ kakaa aatani kukla a.

english_verse: You are like a letter written by Christ and delivered by us. But you are not written with pen and ink or on tablets made of stone. You are written in our hearts by the Spirit of the living God.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlɛbasɛ si mlenu keplekpa gi kɛdɔ ní Kristo klɔ ɔ, libii lɛ gi lido ɛdɔni blɔ axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ, gi aní kiŋwlimiseʋi i, gi biatani kutsoli bɛkɔ ɛ ŋwlimi ka a, pɔ̀ Aya gbagbe yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, lɛ báŋwlimi ka atrɔ ní akla aʋa a, pɔ̀ bɛŋwlimi ka ɛtrɔ ní banɔ gbagbe ba ba ina nɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: We are sure about all this. Christ makes us sure in the very presence of God.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya linu ɔna gi ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo ní Kristo me ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We don't have the right to claim that we have done anything on our own. God gives us what it takes to do all we do.
translation_verse: Aní si kwiatani kiditɔ kubitɛ ní blɔ ɔtutɔ isuwla mɛ ɛ, boŋu i, kidɔ kakaa gi kuzɛɛbitɛ ɛ, kidɔ ní Aya klɔ.

english_verse: He makes us worthy to be the servants of his new agreement that comes from the Holy Spirit and not from a written Law. After all, the Law brings death, but the Spirit brings life.
translation_verse: Yɛ aki blɔ blɔ kutanɔɔ tɔ kuwa yɛ axwɛ na ŋwisi yɛ banɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, ní kunyánya vuvu yɔ mɛ. Lɛ kunyanya vuvu liɔ ya ɛ, aní ní kɔ kuŋwlimi yɔ onu u, pɔ̀ ní kɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ onu u; lɛ ese linu si kuŋwlimi yɔ kuzɛɛmani kutse, pɔ̀ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ izɛmani ligbã́ nɛ ɛ. Kunyanya vuvu yɔ kɔ Kudradraa

english_verse: The Law of Moses brought only the promise of death, even though it was carved on stones and given in a wonderful way. Still the Law made Moses' face shine so brightly the people of Israel could not look at it, even though it was a fading glory.
translation_verse: Abla xegi Kutseo kɔ axwɛ ana gi bɛwa ŋwisi ye banɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, gi kɔ isã nɛ bɛŋwlimi trɔ ní akla na aʋa, kwimani kutse e, gi nya kɔ kudradra kwiki si Mose yɛ aŋu na mɛ ezeekle e, xe Israɛl bebi ba bázɛtani yɛ aŋu na mɛ kudi i, kɔkpɛ la kuklekleo gi nya kwiklo kyɛ xe e,

english_verse: So won't the agreement the Spirit brings to us be even more wonderful?
translation_verse: kɔlɔ kudradra ʋidi kwiaze ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ axwɛwawa la mɛ sanɔ ɔ!

english_verse: If something that brings the death sentence is glorious, won't something that makes us acceptable to God be even more glorious?
translation_verse: Xegi kusumu liɔ gi kuzebu banɔ lipɔ su kwidra ɛ, kitɛ xe kusumu uliɔ gi kuzɛɛki banɔ su izɛdra kutrámani kudradra ɛsanɔ ɔ?

english_verse: In fact, the new agreement is so wonderful that the Law is no longer glorious at all.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi kɛsu kidra ɛzanɔ ɛ, kɛsu kídra tɔ ɔ, xegi kwikɔ kɛ srɔni kudradra ʋidi uliɔ gi kulɛ abla a.

english_verse: The Law was given with a glory that faded away. But the glory of the new agreement is much greater, because it will never fade away.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi kidiliɛ gi kiikpã kiba ni kudradra ɛ, kitɛ kuklekleo kiaze ki kidiliɛ gi kilɛɛ tsinɔ ɔ?

english_verse: This wonderful hope makes us feel like speaking freely.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso gi kudeonudidi uliɔ kuli ní blɔ awla ɛ, ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo.

english_verse: We are not like Moses. His face was shining, but he covered it to keep the people of Israel from seeing the brightness fade away.
translation_verse: Kúma ŋwisi Mose kiniɛ, gi akɔ kavlɔʋi ewe yɛ aŋu na mɛ ɛ, tɔ Israel banɛ ma bamɔni kuklekleo kudi kɔkpɛ lipoe lɛ gi kwiakpã ɛ.

english_verse: The people were stubborn, and something still keeps them from seeing the truth when the Law is read. Only Christ can take away the covering that keeps them from seeing.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ba begyodi ʋa mɛ bádra a, lɛ ese linu si gakɔkpɛ ni omono e, bewe bidɔ bɛklɔ xegi bɛkla kunyanya kokoo e. Báti kɔ azanɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si gani Kristo aʋa ko biati kɔ.

english_verse: When the Law of Moses is read, they have their minds covered over
translation_verse: Kɔna ni omono kruaa kyɛ ɛ, lipoe kakaa gi bɛkla Mose kuplekpaa ɛ, kavlɔʋia keedziwe ba ina nɛ dzɛ.

english_verse: with a covering that is removed only for those who turn to the Lord.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ ekyi evu Ɔkaɛ ɛ, kɔ bɛti kavlɔʋia ɛ.

english_verse: The Lord and the Spirit are one and the same, and the Lord's Spirit sets us free.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, Ɔkaɛ yɛ onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ, lɛ niklɔ gi Ɔkaɛ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔlɛ ɛ, niklɔ ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ ɔlɛ.

english_verse: So our faces are not covered. They show the bright glory of the Lord, as the Lord's Spirit makes us more and more like our glorious Lord.
translation_verse: Blɔ banɔaya gi bɛti kavlɔʋia ní blɔ aŋu na mɛ ɛ, kwiimɔ xe kwibu Ɔkaɛ yɛ kusudraa ɛbasɛ ɛ, beekyi blo tɔ kudzi ŋwisi yɛ gi bɛki blɔ kusudra a, gi kwikeme sanɔ ɔ, gi kwidɔ ni Ɔkaɛ ɛ, gi onu Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: God has been kind enough to trust us with this work. This is why we never give up.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso gi gani Aya yɛ asigagadidi la mɛ ɛ, xe aki blɔ axwɛ ana ya si kuwa ɛ, blɔ ɔna ɔ́tragbɔ̀ ɔ.

english_verse: We don't do shameful things that must be kept secret. And we don't try to fool anyone or twist God's message around. God is our witness that we speak only the truth, so others will be sure we can be trusted.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, kwibu eyekuboe la ni eboe kpokpo la ɛzanɔ, kuʋímeni banɔ ɔ, lɛ kuʋíkyi Aya yɛ liboe le kyɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, gi kwiibu anukwarɛɛ basɛ wee e, kwiikpɔ isu ya ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa si biate blo ní ba ina nɛ mɛ ní Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: If there is anything hidden about our message, it is hidden only to someone who is lost.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe liboe gbã nɛ gi kwiipani i, xe lɛ mɛ lídra kyɛ ɛ, banama gi bɛɛkyrɔ ɛ, ba ko lɛ mɛ lídra ɛkɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The god who rules this world has blinded the minds of unbelievers. They cannot see the light, which is the good news about our glorious Christ, who shows what God is like.
translation_verse: Ozimizi ye, kesea ʋa ya ka aya yɛ akpɛ banama gi báziŋa ɛ, ba begyodi ʋa ní litumu me. Lɛ loso baʋímɔ Liboe gbã nɛ ɛ, lɛ kusudraa kɔ kuklekleo ní Kristo su gi yɛ onu Aya yɛ kizeze me e.

english_verse: We are not preaching about ourselves. Our message is that Jesus Christ is Lord. He also sent us to be your servants.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si aní blɔ ɔtutɔ isu ya kwisɔli i; kwisɔli Yesu Kristo si onu Ɔka, lɛ blɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ kɔ ɛ, kunu mlɔ banɔsumu kɔkpɛ Yesu loso.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God commanded light to shine in the dark.” Now God is shining in our hearts to let you know that his glory is seen in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya gi yɛ apani si i, “Kuklekle kudo dɔni litumu ne me e,” yɛ aki si yɛ kuklekleo kukle ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ. Atɔ kuki si kute Aya yɛ kusudraa kɔ kuklekleo ní Kristo yɛ aŋu na mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We are like clay jars in which this treasure is stored. The real power comes from God and not from us.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ exo ala alɛ ní blɔ awla ní sise kufukpo me e, tɔ libasɛ si kigagla ʋidi liɛ kidɔ ní Aya kɔ lɛ aní blɔ kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: We often suffer, but we are never crushed. Even when we don't know what to do, we never give up.
translation_verse: Baxixa ʋa bali ní blɔ su ní kapɛ kakaa mɛ, pɔ̀ baʋíŋa blɔ aʋa a, lɛ kuzɛɛpisa pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, blɔ ɔna ɔʋígbɔ̀ ɔ.

english_verse: In times of trouble, God is with us, and when we are knocked down, we get up again.
translation_verse: Banima bizɛyɔ kedu ní blɔ su pɔ̀ Aya ɔ́yurɔ blɔ ɔ; bizɛpɛ si beye blo pɔ̀ kuʋítse e.

english_verse: We face death every day because of Jesus. Our bodies show what his death was like, so his life can also be seen in us.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ blɔ gi kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, kulɛ ní kuxixa mɛ lipoe kakaa kɔkpɛ Yesu loso, atɔ biamɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní blɔ kisugyaɛ liɛ gi kizetse e, me.

english_verse: This means that death is working in us, but life is working in you.
translation_verse: Libasɛ si kutseo kwiiwa axwɛ na ní blɔ mɛ, pɔ̀ ligbã́ nɛ liiwa axwɛ na ní mlɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In the Scriptures it says, “I spoke because I had faith.” We have this same kind of faith. So we speak
translation_verse: Bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní kuŋwlimi dradraa mɛ si, “Mazi lɛ aʋa aŋa lɛ loso Mapani.” Ni kuziŋa Ɔfuɛfuɛ tole ɔlɔ klɔ ʋa ɛ, blɔ fɛ kwiziŋa lɛ loso kwiipani;

english_verse: because we know that God raised the Lord Jesus to life. And just as God raised Jesus, he will also raise us to life. Then he will bring us into his presence together with you.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si kwite si Aya, gi yɛ ayɔni Yesu Kristo adɔ ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, aayɔ ni blɔ fɛ kɔpɔni Yesu lɛ aakɔ̀ blɔ pɔni mlɔ, manɔ ní yɛ aŋu mɛ.

english_verse: All of this has been done for you, so more and more people will know how kind God is and will praise and honor him. Faith in the Lord
translation_verse: Eboa la petee gi ɛɛba blɔ aʋa ɛ, alɛ aki mlɔ libibubu; lɛ loso Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi liina banima ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛ, liaki ɖasedodo gi aagbigba ki Aya yɛ kusudraa a.

english_verse: We never give up. Our bodies are gradually dying, but we ourselves are being made stronger each day.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ɔna oʋído ní blɔ lipo o. Linya ɛli ní lɛ mɛ si blɔ inɔse le iiblo, pɔ̀ ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ ɛ, beedzini blo kigagla ɛkpɛ liwe kakaa.

english_verse: These little troubles are getting us ready for an eternal glory that will make all our troubles seem like nothing.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si blɔ baxixa sisami ʋa ya gi kwimɔ abla ɛ, bɛtráze lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko, lɛ bɛɛki si kwiimɔ kusudra gi kɔ onu kutraza liwewe e, gi kwipɛ sani baxixa ya ʋa petee e.

english_verse: Things that are seen don't last forever, but things that are not seen are eternal. This is why we keep our minds on the things that cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso kúkɔ blɔ aŋu na atrɔ ní kudɔkpaa liɔ klɔ gi kuzɛɛmɔ aʋa a, pɔ̀ kwikɔ la ɛtrɔ ní kudɔkpaa liɔ klɔ gi kuʋímɔ ɛ aʋa. Lɛ ese linu si kidiliɛ gi bizɛɛmɔ ɛ, kizeeze lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko, pɔ̀ kidiliɛ klɔ gi baʋímɔ ɛ, kɛ kizeeze kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_verse: Our bodies are like tents that we live in here on earth. But when these tents are destroyed, we know that God will give each of us a place to live. These homes will not be buildings someone has made, but they are in heaven and will last forever.
translation_verse: Abla kwite si kusaa akpandɔ liɛ mɛ gi kukpasi i, gi onu blɔ kesea aʋa ya kisugyaɛ ɛ; xegi ezizi i, ligba lilɛ ní blɔ awla dɔni Aya klɔ. Ligba gi liaze ní blɔ awla ní lifunu ne e, gi aní ɔnɔ awla bɛkɔ ɛwa lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: While we are here on earth, we sigh because we want to live in that heavenly home.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, kwigù hũu u, gi kwipɛ si kwiaze ní blɔ lifunu ne kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We want to put it on like clothes and not be naked.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si xegi kwikpɛ lifunu ne kisugyaɛ ɛ, bátramɔ blɔ si kwibu liya a.

english_verse: These tents we now live in are like a heavy burden, and we groan. But we don't do this just because we want to leave these bodies that will die. It is because we want to change them for bodies that will never die.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si gi kukpasi ní kelea mɛ kusa akpandɔ liɛ ya mɛ ɛ, kwiitàni ní isu ya mɛ, lɛ lidɔtu ʋidi litini ní blɔ aʋa a, lɛ ese linu si kúpɛ si bebu blo liya a, pɔ̀ kwipɛ si kuze ní blɔ lifunu ne kisugyaɛ mɛ atɔ kidiliɛ gi kinu kidɔ tsetse e, biakyi ke tɔ kiadzi ligbã́.

english_verse: God is the one who makes all this possible. He has given us his Spirit to make us certain he will do it.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, Aya yɛ abitɛ blɔ akí liboe lɛ ya, xe aki blɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ŋwisi bidɔ gi bɛtsa ɛplɛ ɛ, gi kiitrɔ likla a ní kidiliɛ gi kitraba ba ɛ, aʋa.

english_verse: So always be cheerful! As long as we are in these bodies, we are away from the Lord.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla lipoe kakaa, lɛ kwite si gi kuli kepe ní kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, kuli ní ɔdzage ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ su.

english_verse: But we live by faith, not by what we see.
translation_verse: Kuli ligbã́ nɛ ní kuziŋa mɛ aní ní kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ onu u.

english_verse: We should be cheerful, because we would rather leave these bodies and be at home with the Lord.
translation_verse: Ɔna ɔlɛ ní blɔ lipo lɛ mɛɛpani, lɛ kwiapɛ si kwiayurɔ blɔ kisugyaɛ tɔ kwiaze kepea ni Ɔkaɛ.

english_verse: But whether we are at home with the Lord or away from him, we still try our best to please him.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kwitrɔ lɛ si kwiabitɛ kidɔ kakaa tɔ kwiaku yɛ aŋu, xegi kulí kepe ní kisugyaɛ mɛ ni ya a, loo ní ɔdzage ni kɛ su.

english_verse: We know what it means to respect the Lord, and we encourage everyone to turn to him. God himself knows what we are like, and I hope you also know what kind of people we are.
translation_verse: Abla gi kwite kidiliɛ ɛ, gi linu tɔ sé Ɔkaɛ ɛ, lɛ loso kwikeni idi tɔ kuliɔ̀ʋi banima tɔ benu blɔ ese e. Aya ete kidiliɛ gi kunu i, lɛ medi kudeonu si lɛ mɛ lidra ɛki mlɔ ina nɛ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We are not trying once more to brag about ourselves. But we want you to be proud of us, when you are with those who are not sincere and brag about what others think of them.
translation_verse: Kuʋí keni idi dzɛ si kukpɔ̀ isu iiki mlɔ ɔ; pɔ̀ kwikenidi tɔ kwiaki mlɔ kudeonu tɔ mladɔ isu ní blɔ aʋa a, atɔ mlatani lɛ onu kubu kí banama gi bizeefeke isu ya a, ní kidiliɛ gi banima bizɛɛmɔ aʋa a sani kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ aʋa.

english_verse: If we seem out of our minds, it is between God and us. But if we are in our right minds, it is for your good.
translation_verse: Xegi kwiiwle e, Aya loso kwiiwle, pɔ̀ xegi kulɛ mumɔ ɛ, kulɛ mumɔ kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso.

english_verse: We are ruled by Christ's love for us. We are certain that if one person died for everyone else, then all of us have died.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Kristo yɛ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ kwiŋa blɔ aʋa lɛ ese linu si lɛ mɛ lidra ɛki blɔ si ɔnɔ tole etse aki ɔnɔ kakaa lɛ loso e, ɔnɔ kakaa etse.

english_verse: And Christ did die for all of us. He died so we would no longer live for ourselves, but for the one who died and was raised to life for us.
translation_verse: Etse akí banima petee, tɔ banama gi bali ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, batráze ligbã́ nɛ ɛki isu ya tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ biaze ligbã́ kí ɔniliɛ klɔ gi yɛ etse aki ba ɛ, xe bedzini yɛ yɔnɔ ɛ.
