english_verse: We are careful not to judge people by what they seem to be, though we once judged Christ in this way.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso dɔni abla ɛ, kuʋímɔ ɔnɔnɔ dɔni kelea mɛ bede ʋa onu tɔ ɔ. Nya lipoe tɔ zanɔ ɛ, nite kwizɛmɔ Kristo pɔ̀ kuʋímɔ yɛ nite tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Anyone who belongs to Christ is a new person. The past is forgotten, and everything is new.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso xe ɔnutɔ akpɛ likpɔ ni Kristo e, edzì ɔnɔ vuvu; bidɔ koko ʋe biza; bidɔ vuvu ʋɛ biba.

english_verse: God has done it all! He sent Christ to make peace between himself and us, and he has given us the work of making peace between himself and others.
translation_verse: La petee ɛdɔ Aya klɔ lɛ gani Kristo me e, akpɛ blɔ ni Yɛ likpɔ lɛ akpɛ lixwɛ ní blɔ awla si tɔ kukpɛ Yɛ ni banɔdzedze ba kyɛ likpɔ.

english_verse: What we mean is that God was in Christ, offering peace and forgiveness to the people of this world. And he has given us the work of sharing his message about peace.
translation_verse: Aya ɛɛkpɛ kelea mɛ ni Yɛ isu ya likpɔ gani Kristo aʋa; lɛ Aya ɔʋíbla banima ba eboe zimizi na ɛki ba a. Lɛ akɔ likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ odu lo akpɛ ní blɔ awla aki banima.

english_verse: We were sent to speak for Christ, and God is begging you to listen to our message. We speak for Christ and sincerely ask you to make peace with God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso kulɛɛ ní Kristo linyi ne me ŋwisi Aya eeʋi gani blɔ aʋa kinɛ ɛ, kwiki mlɔ litukpo ní Kristo yɛ linyi ne me si: Mlaki tɔ bakpɛ mlɔ ni Aya likpɔ.

english_verse: Christ never sinned! But God treated him as a sinner, so Christ could make us acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Aya aki si Kristo gi eboe zimizi áma ní yɛ su i, odzi eboe zimizi kí blɔ tɔ gani yɛ aʋa ɛ, blɔ eboe aagyɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We work together with God, and we beg you to make good use of God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Ŋwisi Aya yɛ baxwɛwa glɔnɛ kinɛ ɛ, kwiki mlɔ litukpo si mlakíkɔ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi mlɛzɛ ɛ, ŋwisi kidɔ gi kítrabu libi i.

english_verse: In the Scriptures God says, “When the time came, I listened to you, and when you needed help, I came to save you.” That time has come. This is the day for you to be saved.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si apani si, “Gi lipoe le lina si mipɛ̀ lipo me ki mlɔ ɛ, mata mlɔ kutu u, lɛ gi liwe lɛ aʋa a, gi lilɛ si mibu mlo lina ɛ, mapɔni mlɔ.” Mɛsi mlɔ si abla Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ liwe le lina, abla yɛ kubúbú yo liwe le lina. Paulo Yɛ Baxixa ʋa

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with our work, and so we try hard not to cause problems.
translation_verse: Kuʋíplɛ likla ni mlɔ kude me si mletu kikliʋi, tɔ biamɔ kunutsatsa tɔtɔ ní blɔ axwɛwawa la su.

english_verse: But in everything and in every way we show we truly are God's servants. We have always been patient, though we have had a lot of trouble, suffering, and hard times.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ŋwisi Aya yɛ banɔsumu kinɛ ɛ, kuzɛɛkpɔ̀ isu ya ní kude kakaa onu; ní igyidodo me; ní baxixa ʋa mɛ, ní kupepe ni ɔna kugbɔ̀gbɔ̀ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We have been beaten, put in jail, and hurt in riots. We have worked hard and have gone without sleep or food.
translation_verse: Kuzɛɛkpɔ̀ isu ní kukusì me, ligba kuzeze me, ní kedu kuyɔyɔ mɛ; ní axwɛ gagla la kuwawa mɛ, sramɔdɔmɔdɔ elesi ni kuʋɛ mɛ;

english_verse: But we have kept ourselves pure and have been understanding, patient, and kind. The Holy Spirit has been with us, and our love has been real.
translation_verse: kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, kuzɛɛbasɛ blɔ kusudraa ni eboe ese kununu yo, litɔplɛplɛkese kese ani asigagadidi; ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ mɛ, lɛ kuzɛɛbasɛ blɔ ɔna mɛ kunɔlɔ;

english_verse: We have spoken the truth, and God's power has worked in us. In all our struggles we have said and done only what is right.
translation_verse: ní anukwarɛ kudodo me, ni ní Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, kwivu kude gyɔgyɔ kɔ bigutadɔ mɛ ní kaŋawla ni kaba mɛ.

english_verse: Whether we were honored or dishonored or praised or cursed, we always told the truth about ourselves. But some people said we did not.
translation_verse: Lɛ gani kusudra ani kuyé me e, kunutsatsa nyani nyani ni kunutsatsa gbã mɛ ɛ; anukwarɛɛ benete pɔ̀ bɛxwa blɔ si basia nɛ ɛ;

english_verse: We are unknown to others, but well known to you. We seem to be dying, and yet we are still alive. We have been punished, but never killed,
translation_verse: bɛbitɛ ŋwisi béte blo o, pɔ̀ ba pɔ́ bete blo o; bɛsi si kwitse, pɔ̀ mledi kuli ligbã́ nɛ ɛ; bekusi blo pɔ̀, baʋíye blo o.

english_verse: and we are always happy, even in times of suffering. Although we are poor, we have made many people rich. And though we own nothing, everything is ours.
translation_verse: Kuli ní asigaga mɛ, pɔ̀ lipoe kakaa kuli ní isami mɛ ɛ; kwiku lipi le, pɔ̀ kwibitɛ banɔ glegle e, gi bedzi bexonete e; kwiŋwi ŋwisi kididɔ kíma ní blɔ awla a, pɔ̀ kidɔ kakaa kilɛ ní blɔ awla a.

english_verse: Friends in Corinth, we are telling the truth when we say there is room in our hearts for you.
translation_verse: Kwipani iiki mlɔ ni ɔna faã Korintoanɛ ma, lɛ kwidra blɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛki mlɔ.

english_verse: We are not holding back on our love for you, but you are holding back on your love for us.
translation_verse: Kúkɔ blɔ kunɔlɔɔ kokpo mlo o, pɔ̀ mlɛ kɔ mlɔ kuni yɔ ɛkɔ ɛ kpo blo o.

english_verse: I speak to you as I would speak to my own children. Please make room in your hearts for us. The Temple of the Living God
translation_verse: Ŋwisi kutsrɛtsrɛ gbã kinɛ ɛ; mɛɛpani ki mlɔ ŋwisi kí mɛ bebi kinɛ ɛ; lɛ loso e mlɔfɛ mladra mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ aki blɔ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi kwidra blɔ ini nɛ mɛ ɛki mlɔ kinɛ ɛ. Mlakíkpɛ ɔha ni banama gi báziŋa a

english_verse: Stay away from people who are not followers of the Lord! Can someone who is good get along with someone who is evil? Are light and darkness the same?
translation_verse: Mlakíkpɛ likpɔ ni banama gi báziŋa a. Lɛ ese linu si siŋanɛ woli lu si lɛ ní banama gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ, ni banama gi benu litukpomegagla benete kusi i? loo ɔha kpɛkpɛ woli lu ɔlɛ ní kuklekle ni litumu ne kusi i?

english_verse: Is Christ a friend of Satan? Can people who follow the Lord have anything in common with those who don't?
translation_verse: Likpɔkpɛkpɛ woli lu lilɛ ní Kristo ni Belial kusi i? Liboe woli lilɛ ní ɔʋaziŋaɛ ni ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́zɛ ŋa ɛ kusi i?

english_verse: Do idols belong in the temple of God? We are the temple of the living God, as God himself says, “I will live with these people and walk among them. I will be their God, and they will be my people.”
translation_verse: Kunyánya woli lu kulɛ ní Aya yɛ lisɔligba lɛ ni babu ʋa kusi i? Lɛ ese linu si blɔ kunu Aya gbagbee yɛ lisɔligba lɛ. Ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Aya apani si i, “Maaze nì ba lɛ maaga ní ba kusi, lɛ maaze bá Aya, lɛ biaaze mɛ banɔ.”

english_verse: The Lord also says, “Leave them and stay away! Don't touch anything that isn't clean. Then I will welcome you
translation_verse: “Lɛ loso e, mledò ní ba kusi tɔ mlebú lidze, nite Ɔkaɛ apani. Mlakígbɔni bidɔ gi bɛsu bídra a, atɔ maazi mlɔ.”

english_verse: and be your Father. You will be my sons and my daughters, as surely as I am God, the All-Powerful.”
translation_verse: “Maaze Ɔka ki mlɔ, lɛ mlaaze mɛ bebinyime ni mɛ bebidzɛ, nite kigaglaɛ petee Ɔkaɛ Aya apani.”

english_verse: My friends, God has made us these promises. So we should stay away from everything that keeps our bodies and spirits from being clean. We should honor God and try to be completely like him. The Church Makes Paul Happy
translation_verse: Agi eboa la ya a gi Aya aka aplɛ aki blɔ alɛ ní blɔ awla ɛ, mɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, mlaki tɔ kudra blɔ isu ya su dɔni kidɔ kakaa gi kialulu blɔ isu ya a, ni blɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ mɛ̀. Atɔ mlaki tɔ blɔ kudradraa kuna ki Aya yɛ kubùbù nì yɛ kusese. Paulo Yɛ Isami nɛ

english_verse: Make a place for us in your hearts! We haven't mistreated or hurt anyone. We haven't cheated anyone.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ blɔ kyɛ kumɔ ozekɔ ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ. Kuʋíta ɔnɔnɔ ese e, kuʋíkyi ɔnɔnɔ ɛxla a, kuʋítafa ɔnɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I am not saying this to be hard on you. But, as I have said before, you will always be in our thoughts, whether we live or die.
translation_verse: Maʋípani nite tɔ mikɔ ebú mlɔ lipɔ ɔ; mapani azanɔ si mleŋa kedzi ní blɔ ina nɛ mɛ si kwiaze ligba loo kwiatse ni mlo.

english_verse: I trust you completely. I am always proud of you, and I am greatly encouraged. In all my trouble I am still very happy.
translation_verse: Maka ní mlɔ aʋa mɔmɔmɔ; mizɛɛdɔ isu ní mlɔ aʋa. Mlɛkpɛ mɛ kigagla mɔmɔmɔ; lɛ ní blɔ baxixa ʋa petee me e, odukɔ ɔma ɔki mɛ isami nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After we came to Macedonia, we didn't have any chance to rest. We were faced with all kinds of problems. We were troubled by enemies and troubled by fears.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kwiba Makedonia ɛ, blɔ kisugyaɛ liɛ ya kímɔ kusuyɔ; bɛzɛkpɛ blɔ kunugu yo ní kapɛ kakaa mɛ, gani kuboehe ní oge no, lifu ne ní kimɛ mɛ.

english_verse: But God cheers up people in need, and this is what he did when he sent Titus to us.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya gi yɛ izɛɛyɔ ɔna ɛki banama a, gi ba ɔna ɛgbɔ ɛ, ayɔ blɔ ɔna gi Titus aba blɔ klɔ ɛ,

english_verse: Of course, we were glad to see Titus, but what really made us glad is the way you cheered him up. He told how sorry you were and how concerned you were about me. And this made me even happier.
translation_verse: lɛ aní yɛ kubaba tètè e, pɔ̀ ɔnayɔyɔ lɔ gi mlɛki yɛ ɛ. Asi blɔ kiliɛ gi mlɛpɛ si mlɛmɔ mɛ ɛ, mlɔ ɔna tata nɔ, kiliɛ gi mlɛkɔ lɛ mɛ ɛki mɛ ɛ, gi biki si mɛ isami nɛ isrɛ sanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I don't feel bad anymore, even though my letter hurt your feelings. I did feel bad at first, but I don't now. I know that the letter hurt you for a while.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi mɛ keplekpaa kákpɛ mlɔ isami kyɛ ɛ, litráve me e. Nya live me lipoe lɛ klɔ ɔkpɛ gi mamɔ si mɛ keplekpaa kákpɛ mlɔ isami kɔ ki lipoe kpekpemi itɔ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now I am happy, but not because I hurt your feelings. It is because God used your hurt feelings to make you turn back to him, and none of you were harmed by us.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla ɛ, isami iiku me, ani gi keplekpaa kákpɛ mlɔ isami loso o, pɔ̀ mlɔ asigaga la ɛki si mlekyi ɔna mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si mledzi basigaga nɛ kiliɛ gi Aya apɛ ɛ, lɛ loso e, ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́kpɛ mlɔ libi ní kude kutɔtɔ onu gani blɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: When God makes you feel sorry enough to turn to him and be saved, you don't have anything to feel bad about. But when this world makes you feel sorry, it can cause your death.
translation_verse: Xegi Aya aki si mleku ní asigaga mɛ ɛ, lɛ lizɛɛmani ɔna mɛ kukyikyi i, gi kɔ kuzɛɛki blɔ kubúbú yo o, xe kuʋímani “atɛsi metete” e, pɔ̀ kelea mɛ asigaga la ɛ, la izɛɛmani kutse.

english_verse: When I wrote you, it wasn't to accuse the one who was wrong or to take up for the one who was hurt. I wrote, so God would show you how much you do care for us.
translation_verse: Loso agi maŋwlimi akí mlɔ ɛ, ani ɔniliɛ klɔ gi abitɛ liboe zimizi i, loo ɔniliɛ gi yɛ su bɛbitɛ liboe zimizi i manɔ, pɔ̀ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ, mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlaamɔ kiliɛ gi mlɛkɔ isu ya ɛki blɔ ɛ.

english_verse: And we were greatly encouraged. Although we were encouraged, we felt even better when we saw how happy Titus was, because you had shown he had nothing to worry about.
translation_verse: Gani lɛ petee me e, bɛkpɛ blɔ kigagla. Kɔpɔni blɔ ɔtutɔ blɔ ɔnadɔdɔ lɔ ɛ, lize blɔ isami koŋ si kwimɔ Titus yɛ isami nɛ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ petee mlɛkpɛ yɛ ɔna nɔ isami.

english_verse: We had told him how much we thought of you, and you did not disappoint us. Just as we have always told you the truth, so everything we told him about you has also proved to be true.
translation_verse: Madɔ isu aki yɛ ní mlɔ su, lɛ mlákpɛ mɛ kuye e. Pɔ̀ lɛ kiliɛ gi liboe kakaaɛ lɛ ɛ, gi kwipani si mlɔ lilɛ ní lɛ mɛ ɛ, niteme ke blɔ kusudɔɔ ní mlɔ su kí Tito fɛ ɛ, kɔ kyɛ kunu anukwarɛ.

english_verse: Titus loves all of you very much, especially when he remembers how you obeyed him and how you trembled with fear when you welcomed him.
translation_verse: Lɛ yɛ kudimɛnɔɔ ki mlɔ ɛ, kwikeme pɔnɔ xegi aplɛ aŋu si mlɔ petee mlenu kutu u, lɛ mlɛzi yɛ ni lifu ani kusugu u.

english_verse: It makes me really glad to know I can depend on you.
translation_verse: Isami iku me si maaka ni mlɔ petee aʋa.

english_verse: My friends, we want you to know that the churches in Macedonia have shown others God's gift of undeserved grace.
translation_verse: Benemi ba, kwipɛ si mlete lipomepɛpɛɛ nɛ gi Aya akí Makedonia isɔliha lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Although they were going through hard times and were very poor, they were glad to give generously.
translation_verse: Dɔni kukenidi gagla mɛ ɛ, ba isami nɛ ni ba ɔzia nɔ bigbigba, gi bidzi bidɔkɔkɔ faã a, gi biku kumu yɔ ɔ.

english_verse: They gave as much as they could afford and even more, simply because they wanted to.
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛɛŋa katsalia ɛki ba si, bɛki bidɔ kiliɛ onu gi biatanɔ, sani ba kutanɔ kyɛ. Ní ba ɔtutɔ ba isuwla mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: They even asked and begged us to let them have the joy of giving their money for God's people.
translation_verse: beʋi dɔni blɔ iwla mɛ veve si kuki ba kudeonu tɔ ba kyɛ bamɔ ɔwakɔ ní lixwɛ nɛ ya mɛ ki banɔ dradra ba.

english_verse: And they did more than we had hoped. They gave themselves first to the Lord and then to us, just as God wanted them to do.
translation_verse: Lɛ bábitɛ kiliɛ gi kwidi kudeonu ikɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, bɛkɔ ba isu ya ɛki Ɔkaɛ, lɛ bɛkɔ isu ya ɛki blɔ ní Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ onu.

english_verse: Titus was the one who got you started doing this good thing, so we begged him to help you finish what you had begun.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso kwikpɛ Tito kigagla gi ekpese lixwɛ nɛ ya kooko e, tɔ aablo ni lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lixwɛ nɛ ya kyɛ ní mlɔ kapɛa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You do everything better than anyone else. You have stronger faith. You speak better and know more. You are eager to give, and you love us better. Now you must give more generously than anyone else.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kiliɛ gi mlɛbitɛ sanɔ ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, ní kuziŋaa mɛ, ní kupaniɔ mɛ, ní kuboeteo mɛ, ní anukwarɛ zizi mɛ ani mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ kí blɔ mɛ ɛ, mledi le ɔza si mlɔ fɛ mlɛbitɛ sanɔ ní lipomepɛpɛ nɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ ya mɛ.

english_verse: I am not ordering you to do this. I am simply testing how real your love is by comparing it with the concern that others have shown.
translation_verse: Aní ɔsã mɛɛkpɛ ki mlɔ, pɔ̀ mapɛ si mífeke mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ kɔ anukwarɛ edi i, tɔ míkɔ kɔ ɛsrɔni banɔ gɔgɔ ba ba anukwarɛɛ.

english_verse: You know our Lord Jesus Christ treated us with undeserved grace by giving up all his riches, so you could become rich.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mlete blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ gi nya eze oxonete xe e, pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ mlɔ loso e, edzì opiplinete tɔ mlɔ kyɛ gani yɛ lipi le me e, mladzi bexonete.

english_verse: A year ago you were the first ones to give, and you gave because you wanted to. So listen to my advice.
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛ kugbagba ki mlɔ ní kidiliɛ gi kisapɛ̀ ní liboe lɛ ya mɛ kinu si i, galigɔ lɛ ɛ, mlɔ mleeze banɔ tɔpu ba aní ní bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ mɛ tètè pɔ̀ ní kupɛ́pɛ si mlaabitɛ nite me kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I think you should finish what you started. If you give according to what you have, you will prove you are as eager to give as you were to think about giving.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mlebloni lixwɛ nɛ, atɔ la kublono e, kwiaze ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlɛpɛ si mlɛbitɛ lɛ kinɛ ɛ, ní kiliɛ gi mlaatanɔ onu u.

english_verse: It doesn't matter how much you have. What matters is how much you are willing to give from what you have.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe kupɛ́pɛɔ kulɛ́ ɛ, biaazɛ mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛ, ní kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ni ɔnɔ kakaa awla ɛ onu, aní ní kidiliɛ gi kíma ní yɛ awla onu u.

english_verse: I am not trying to make life easier for others by making life harder for you. But it is only fair
translation_verse: Aní blɔ kupɛ́pɛ linu si banɔatɔ bamɔ ɔnadɔdɔ tɔ mlɔ pɔ̀ bemi mlo akpɛ ɛ, boŋu i, tɔ mlɔ pɔ́ mleze ní kisɔsɔ mɛ.

english_verse: for you to share with them when you have so much, and they have so little. Later, when they have more than enough, and you are in need, they can share with you. Then everyone will have a fair share,
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, mlɔ bidɔ srɛsrɛ ʋɛ ɛ, biana ki ba baziazia ʋa atɔ xe mlɔ kyɛ mleku ní ɔzia mɛ ɛ, ba bidɔ srɛsrɛ ʋɛ biana ki mlɔ baziazia ʋa. Atɔ mlaze ni kisɔsɔ mɛ.

english_verse: just as the Scriptures say, “Those who gathered too much had nothing left. Those who gathered only a little had all they needed.” Titus and His Friends
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ni Aya liboe le me si i, “Ɔniliɛ gi atá yɛ ní kisrɛsrɛ mɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔni yɛ ɔ́sa srɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔniliɛ gi atá sisami kyɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔni yɛ ɔ́saɛ sisami.” Bɛkpɛ Tito ní Korinto

english_verse: I am grateful that God made Titus care as much about you as we do.
translation_verse: Medo Aya ɖase gi akpɛ lɛ ní Tito ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, si ɔkɔ litɔ ní lɛ mɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi makɔ litɔ ní lɛ mɛ aki mlɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When we begged Titus to visit you, he said he would. He wanted to because he cared so much for you.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Tito ɔ́zɛ liboe le gi kwisi yɛ si ɔbitɛ tètè e, boŋu i, atrába nì yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛpɛ vevee si tɔ yipɔni mlɔ nì yɛ ɔna nɔ petee e.

english_verse: With Titus we are also sending one of the Lord's followers who is well known in every church for spreading the good news.
translation_verse: Kɔpɔni yɛ ɛ, kwitsisi onemi ye gi yɛ isɔliha lɛ petee iikpɔ̀ kɔkpɛ yɛ ikù nɛ kukpɛkpɛɔ ki Liboe Gbã nɛ loso o.

english_verse: The churches chose this follower to travel with us while we carry this gift that will bring praise to the Lord and show how much we hope to help.
translation_verse: Lɛ kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, isɔliha lɛ iikya yɛ si ɔpɔni blɔ gi kwiikɔ ba bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ tsinɔ ɔ, gi blɔ ɔtutɔ kwitrɔ ɔ si kwikɔ ɛkpɛ kubùbù ní Ɔkaɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ su ani tɔ kubasɛ si kwikɔ lɛ mɛ si kwipɔnɔ ɔ.

english_verse: We don't want anyone to find fault with the way we handle your generous gift.
translation_verse: Kúpɛ si ɔnɔnɔ ɔki kunutsatsa zimizi utɔtɔ ní kiliɛ gi kwialɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ ya a, gi bɛkɔ nì ɔna faã su u.

english_verse: But we want to do what pleases the Lord and what people think is right.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kwipɛ si kuga kude gbã aní ní Aya tètè aŋu mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ ní banima kyɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We are also sending someone else with Titus and the other follower. We approve of this man. In fact, he has already shown us many times that he wants to help. And now he wants to help even more than ever, because he trusts you so much.
translation_verse: Kɔpɔnɔ ɛ, kwiikpɛ blɔ onemi ye nì ba kude; gi ebu abasi blɔ lipoe glegle ni ní bede glegle onu si kifu kilɛ ní yɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ sanɔ ɛ, abasɛ si yɛ azɛ mlɔ aʋa aŋa.

english_verse: Titus is my partner, who works with me to serve you. The other two followers are sent by the churches, and they bring honor to Christ.
translation_verse: Tito kɔ ɛ, yɛ onu mɛ ɔgaglɔnɛɛ nì mɛ ɔxwɛwaglɔnɛ ní mlɔ kusi, blɔ benemi dzedze ba ɛ, ba bali ní isɔliha lɛ linyi ne me lɛ benu kubùbù kí Kristo.

english_verse: Treat them in such a way that the churches will see your love and will know why we bragged about you.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlabasɛ benyime ba ya mlɔ kunɔlɔɔ, nì kidiliɛ loso gi kuzɛɛdɔ isu ní mlɔ aʋa a, tɔ isɔliha lɛ petee biamɔ nɛ.

english_verse: I don't need to write you about the money you plan to give for God's people.
translation_verse: Nya líziã si miŋwlimi ki mlɔ ní bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi mlɛtsisi banɔ dradra ba su u.

english_verse: I know how eager you are to give. And I have proudly told the Lord's followers in Macedonia that you people in Achaia have been ready for a whole year. Now your desire to give has made them want to give.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mete si mlɛpɛ veve si mlɛɛpɔnɔ, lɛ makɔ mlɔ adɔ isu akí Makedonia banɛ ma. Lɛ masi ba si, “Benemi ba ní Axaya ɛ, bɛbla si biaki kupɔnɔ dɔni galigɔ lɛ ke.” Mlɔ kupɛ́pɛɔ kwihwa ni bamɛ beglegle si ba kyɛ babitɛ kiditɔ.

english_verse: This is why I am sending Titus and the two others to you. I want you to be ready, just as I promised. This will prove we were not wrong to brag about you.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mɛɛtsisi benemi ba, tɔ blɔ kusufekeo ní eboa la ya mɛ ɛ, kukíze kupluplu u. Pɔ̀, tɔ mlaaze ní kublabla mɛ ni mlɔ kupɔnɔɔ kiliɛ gi mapani si mlaapɔnɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Some followers from Macedonia may come with me, and I want them to find that you have the money ready. If you don't, I would be embarrassed for trusting you to do this. But you would be embarrassed even more.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi mɛ ni banama a, gi bɛdɔ Makedonia kwiaba a, xe biamɔ si mlamá ní kublabla mɛ ɛ, kwiaŋa kuye gi nya kwika ní mlɔ aʋa gbɛgbɛ te e. Xegi kútrapani ní mlɔ kuyeo su i.

english_verse: So I have decided to ask Titus and the others to spend some time with you before I arrive. This way they can arrange to collect the money you have promised. Then you will have the chance to give because you want to, and not because you feel forced to.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, megyo le edi si liziã si mikpɛ benemi ba kigagla tɔ betù ɔtɔ aki mɛ tɔ biaakpɛ mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ likpɔ ɔ. Ɔna mɛ faã bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi mleka ɛplɛ si mlaakɔ ɛ. Lɛ biakpɛ ʋɛ likpɔ xe maaba a, lɛ biabasɛ si kipɛ́ mlɛpɛ si mlɛɛki bɛ ɛ, lɛ bínu bidɔ gi bemi ní mlɔ aʋa si mlakɔ ɔ. Mlakpɛ bidɔ ni ɔna faã

english_verse: Remember this saying, “A few seeds make a small harvest, but a lot of seeds make a big harvest.”
translation_verse: Mlaplɛ aŋu si ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ bidɔ sisami i, yɛ aamɔ bidɔ sisami; lɛ ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ bidɔ glegle e, yɛ aamɔ bidɔ glegle.

english_verse: Each of you must make up your own mind about how much to give. But don't feel sorry that you must give and don't feel you are forced to give. God loves people who love to give.
translation_verse: Mlɔmɛ ɔnɔ kakaa ɔkɔ kidiliɛ gi atrɔ ní yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ si yɛ akɔ ɛ, aní ni aŋu mɛ nyasi nyasi loo ŋwisi kimi bemi ní yɛ aʋa kinɛ ɛ; lɛ ese linu si Aya izɛɛlɔ ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛki bidɔ ni isami i.

english_verse: God can bless you with everything you need, and you will always have more than enough to do all kinds of good things for others.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya aatani mlɔ yɛ lipomepɛpɛ kakaa kukɔ niteme si ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ ni lipoe kakaa mɛ ɛ, mlamɔ mlɔ baziazia ba petee lɛ mlɔ eboe gbã na aasrɛ.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “God freely gives his gifts to the poor, and always does right.”
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si i, “Izeehle yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛki bazianete ba ni ɔna faã; lɛ yɛ kude gyɔgyɔɔ kulɛ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.”

english_verse: God gives seed to farmers and provides everyone with food. He will increase what you have, so you can give even more to those in need.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya gi yɛ izɛɛki esebi la ɛki ɔnyrɔmɛdzɛɛ ni àklã na ki kidɔŋaŋa ɛ, yɛ kee aaki mlamɔ eboe gyɔgyɔ la gi aasrɛ. Lɛ aaki si kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ni mlɔ awla ɛ, kiasrɛ.

english_verse: You will be blessed in every way, and you will be able to keep on being generous. Then many people will thank God when we deliver your gift.
translation_verse: Aaki si mlaaze bexonete ní kude kakaa onu gi mlɔ fɛ mlaaki bidɔ ni ɔna faã lipoe kakaa a, lɛ gani blɔ aʋa ɛ, mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ biaki si banima biado Aya ɖase.

english_verse: What you are doing is much more than a service that supplies God's people with what they need. It is something that will make many others thank God.
translation_verse: Lixwɛ nɛ gi mlɛwa ɛ, liʋíki Aya yɛ banima ba bidɔ ziazia ʋɛ tètè ko, boŋu i, lɛ liiki si banɔ glegle bedo Aya ɖase.

english_verse: The way in which you have proved yourselves by this service will bring honor and praise to God. You believed the message about Christ, and you obeyed it by sharing generously with God's people and with everyone else.
translation_verse: Lɛ kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi mlɔ axwɛwawa la ebu ibasɛ ɛ, banima bíakɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ki Aya kɔkpɛ mlɔ isu ya kukɔkpɛ ní Kristo yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ mɛ ɛ, gi mleenu kutu loso o. Lɛ ya lipɔni mlɔ bidɔkɔkɔ ni ɔna faã gi mlɛlɛ ʋɛ ni ba ni ɔnɔ kakaa fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now they are praying for you and want to see you, because God used you to bless them so very much.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso, ní ba Aya kuxwaxwa ní mlɔ litukpo me e, ba ina nɛ iigyo mlo edi kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ ʋidi lɛ gi aki mlɔ loso o.

english_verse: Thank God for his gift that is too wonderful for words!
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kudo Aya ɖase ki yɛ bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani kusrɔ loso o.

english_verse: Do you think I am a coward when I am with you and brave when I am far away? Well, I ask you to listen, because Christ himself was humble and gentle.
translation_verse: Gani Kristo yɛ kusububɔ ni yɛ litɔplɛplɛkese kese me e, mɛ Paulo, gi mlɛzɛɛsɛ si menu ɔnɔ gi onu ofufluse e, xegi malɛ ní mlɔ kusi i, pɔ̀ xe maʋíse ɔnɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, xegi malɛ ni ɔdzage e, meʋi dɔni mlɔ iwla mɛ si

english_verse: Some people have said we act like the people of this world. So when I arrive, I expect I will have to be firm and forceful in what I say to them. Please don't make me treat you that way.
translation_verse: mɛɛki mlɔ litukpo si xegi maba mlɔ klɔ ɛ, litrázia si mizeehe eboe gagla a, ni banama gi begyo le edi si kelea lia mɛ bede ʋa onu kulɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We live in this world, but we don't act like its people
translation_verse: Gi kulɛ ní kelea mɛ kyɛ ɛ, kuʋíta kigũ ŋwisi kelea mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: or fight our battles with the weapons of this world. Instead, we use God's power that can destroy fortresses. We destroy arguments
translation_verse: Bigutadɔɛ mɛ su axwɛ gi kwiiwa ɛ, bínu kelea ya mɛ bigutadɔ pɔ̀ binu Aya yɛ bigutadɔ gi kigagla kiliɛ ní bɛ awla a, gi biazizi ahwafiɔnɛ yɛ izekɔ gagla lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and every bit of pride that keeps anyone from knowing God. We capture people's thoughts and make them obey Christ.
translation_verse: Kuzeezizi kuboehe kakaa; ni imɔkpɛkpɛ kakaa gi iyɔ kedu ya ní Aya kuteteo su u; kuzeevu kugyodi kakaa tɔ kuki si kukpɛ litukpo ní Kristo ese.

english_verse: And when you completely obey him, we will punish anyone who refuses to obey.
translation_verse: Lɛ kuli ní kublabla mɛ si kwiahe kutu kí kutu monumonu kakaa xegi mlɛbasɛ si mlalɛ ní kutununu me petee ɛki Kristo e.

english_verse: You judge by appearances. If any of you think you are the only ones who belong to Christ, then think again. We belong to Christ as much as you do.
translation_verse: Mleedi eboa la gi alɛ ní mlɔ aŋu na mɛ ɛ. Xegi mlɔmɛ onutɔ aka ní yɛ isu aʋa si yenu Kristo ɔnɛ ɛ, lilɛ si odzini le kutapo odi mumɔ si blɔ kyɛ ɛ, kunu Kristo banɛ ŋwisi yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ kiniɛ na a.

english_verse: Maybe I brag a little too much about the authority that the Lord gave me to help you and not to hurt you. Yet I am not embarrassed to brag.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi maasa awla kakɔ ɔ, ní kigaglaɛ liɛ gi Ɔkaɛ aki blɔ si tɔ kukɔ gani mlɔ tɔ mletsi i sani si tɔ kuzizi mlo kyɛ ɛ, kuye kútraku me e.

english_verse: And I am not trying to scare you with my letters.
translation_verse: Mápɛ si miŋwi ŋwisi meekeni idi si míkpɛ mlɔ lifu ni mɛ kuplekpaa a, gi maŋwlimi aki mlɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Some of you are saying, “Paul's letters are harsh and powerful. But in person, he is a weakling and has nothing worth saying.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si banɔatɔ bɛɛpani si, “Paulo yɛ kuplekpaa gi aŋwlimi i, kɔmɛ eboe la alɛ nyipu lɛ alɛ gagla a, pɔ̀ xe yɛ ɔtutɔ ɔlɛ ní blɔ su i, ónu ɔnɔ gagla a, lɛ yɛ eboe la ɛ, enu kididɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: Those people had better understand that when I am with you, I will do exactly what I say in my letters.
translation_verse: Linu si banɔ ba klɔ ba bamɔ egyesi si lidzegaga ɛtɔtɔ líma ní kidiliɛ gi kwiŋwlimi ní blɔ kuplekpaa mɛ ɛ, xe kúma ní mlɔ su i, ani kidiliɛ gi kwiabitɛ ɛ, xe kuli ní mlɔ kusi i me.

english_verse: We won't dare compare ourselves with those who think so much of themselves. But they are foolish to compare themselves with themselves.
translation_verse: Kútrakeni idi si kúkla isu ya kpɛ loo tɔ kúkɔ isu ya ɛsrɔni banama gi bizɛkpɔ̀ isu ya a. Gi beegyigye batutɔ ba isu ya a nì ba isu ya, xe bɛɛkɔ ba isu ya ɛsrɔni ni ba ahanuglɔ ba ɛ, bétè liboe.

english_verse: We won't brag about something we don't have a right to brag about. We will only brag about the work God has sent us to do, and you are part of that work.
translation_verse: Blɔ kɔ ɛ, blɔ kusudɔɔ kútradɔ kudeo ɛza a; pɔ̀ blɔ kusudɔɔ kwiaze ní axwɛ ana gi Aya akpɛ ní blɔ awla ɛ aʋa, lɛ axwɛ ana klɔ ɛna mlɔ kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We are not bragging more than we should. After all, we did bring the message about Christ to you.
translation_verse: Lɛ kuʋísa kutrɛ ní blɔ kusudɔɔ mɛ ɛ, kiliɛ gi liaze e, xegi kúba mlɔ klɔ ni Liboe Gbã nɛ ní Kristo su u. Lɛ ese linu si kwitani mlɔ kuna ni Kristo yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ.

english_verse: We don't brag about what others have done, as if we had done those things ourselves. But I hope as you become stronger in your faith, we will be able to reach many more of the people around you. That has always been our goal.
translation_verse: Lɛ kuʋídɔ kudeo ɛza si kwidɔ isu ní axwɛ gi banɔ dzedze bɛwa aʋa a. Blɔ kudeonudidi kunu si kiliɛ gi mlɔ kuziŋaa kwiitsi ipɔnɔ ɛ, blɔ axwɛ na kètu ya ni mlɔ kusi kiane sanɔ.

english_verse: Then we will be able to preach the good news in other lands where we cannot take credit for work someone else has already done.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ kwiatani Liboe Gbã nɛ kupani ní kuse dzedzeo aʋa. Lɛ ese linu si kúpɛ si kwidɔ isu u ní axwɛ gi bɛwa kokoo ní ɔnɔ dzedze yɛ kètu ya mɛ ɛ aʋa.

english_verse: You may brag about yourself, but the only approval that counts is the Lord's approval.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si aní ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛkpɔ isu ya ɛ, yɛ onu ɔnɔ gbã ɛ, pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ akpɔ̀ ɛ.

english_verse: Please put up with a little of my foolishness.
translation_verse: Megyo le edi si mlaazi mɛ xegi mɛɛdɔ kuga taʋɛ fɛ ɛ; Mlɛɛbitɛ lɛ nite kokoo!

english_verse: I am as concerned about you as God is. You were like a virgin bride I had chosen only for Christ.
translation_verse: Mɛɛgɔni kìfu ní mlɔ onu lɛ kìfui liɛ gi mɛgɔni i, kidɔ ní Aya klɔ. Mlalɛ ko ŋwisi ɔdisami gi makpɛ kuwlawla akɔ akí onyime tole e, Kristo kinɛ.

english_verse: But now I fear that you will be tricked, just as Eve was tricked by that lying snake. I am afraid that you might stop thinking about Christ in an honest and sincere way.
translation_verse: Meese lifu ne si biapisa mlɔ begyodi ʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ɔgblagaɛ akɔ ɔdaye lo emeni Eva kiniɛ ɛ, lɛ mlɔ begyodi ʋa biasɛ ní mlɔ ɔna mɛ dradra ni kulɔlɔ petee aki Kristo su u.

english_verse: We told you about Jesus, and you received the Holy Spirit and accepted our message. But you let some people tell you about another Jesus. Now you are ready to receive another spirit and accept a different message.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi mlezenu banama a, gi bɛba ɛzɛpani ní Yesu dzedze su liboe ɛki mlɔ ɔ, gi liga lidze ní Yesu liɛ ɛ, gi yɛ su liboe kwiba ɛpani ɛki mlɔ ɛ, loo mlɛzi Ɔfuɛfuɛ dzedze gi ɛga lidze ní lɔ klɔ ɔ, gi mlɛzɛ su i, loo mlenu Liboe Gbã gi liga lidze ní Liboe Gbãɛ nɛ gi mlɛzɛ su i, mlalɛ ní kublabla mɛ si mlazi lɛ kyɛ!

english_verse: I think I am as good as any of those super apostles.
translation_verse: Mámɔ lɛ si “banɔkpɛkpɛ dzi aŋu mɛ” ba klɔ ʋa, bɛɛpɛ sani mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I may not speak as well as they do, but I know as much. And this has already been made perfectly clear to you.
translation_verse: Nyafɛ béhe me ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi aapani ní banɔ kusi i, pɔ̀ kuboesenu kulɛ ní mɛ awla a; lɛ kwidra lɛ mɛ ki mlɔ lipoe kakaa gani kude kakaa onu u.

english_verse: Was it wrong for me to lower myself and honor you by preaching God's message free of charge?
translation_verse: Liboe zimizi linu si mibubɔ isu tɔ mimuni mlo gani Aya yɛ liboe le gi mapani aki mlɔ faã a, gi mázi kibɔ ní mlɔ awla na a?

english_verse: I robbed other churches by taking money from them to serve you.
translation_verse: Lize ŋwisi mazeezuru isɔliha dzedze le gi mazi kupɔnɔ adɔ ni ba klɔ ɔ, xe makɔ kɔ apɔni mlɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Even when I was in need, I still didn't bother you. In fact, some of the Lord's followers from Macedonia brought me what I needed. I have not been a burden to you in the past, and I will never be a burden.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi mazɛɛwa axwɛ na ní mlɔ kusi i, xe mazia kiditɔ ɛ, méze lidɔtu iiki ɔnɔnɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si benemi ba ya gi bɛdɔ ní Makedonia ɛ, bɛzɛɛki mɛ kidiliɛ gi mazia ɛ. Medi le aʋa si méze lidɔtu iiki mlɔ ní kude utɔtɔ onu u, lɛ maadi le aʋa si maatrɛ ní lɛ aʋa nite e.

english_verse: As surely as I speak the truth about Christ, no one in Achaia can stop me from bragging about this.
translation_verse: Ní Kristo yɛ anukwarɛɛ liɛ gi ɔlɛ ní mɛ mɛ̀ onu i, ɔnɔnɔ ni Axaya kesea mɛ ɛ, ɔ́tratsi kude ɛki mɛ kusufeke uliɔ ya a.

english_verse: And it isn't because I don't love you. God himself knows how much I do love you.
translation_verse: Ege loso e? kilɔ málɔ mlɔ loso a? Aya ete si malɔ mlɔ!

english_verse: I plan to go on doing just what I have always done. Then those people won't be able to brag about doing the same things we are doing.
translation_verse: Matratrɛ ní lɛ aʋa tɔ mibitɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ mɛɛbitɛ abla ɛ, atɔ mitsi kudeonu ikí banɔkpɛkpɛ ba klɔ gi bɛɛpɛ kudeonu u, si tɔ bamɔ ba si blɔ ni ba kwisɔ ní eboa la gi bɛɛpani i mɛ.

english_verse: Anyway, they are no more than false apostles and dishonest workers. They only pretend to be apostles of Christ.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si banɔ báklɔ ba ɛ, benu asia banɔkpɛkpɛ, benu baxwɛwa gi bizeemeni banɔ ɔ, gi bekyi isu ya a, ŋwisi Kristo yɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And it is no wonder. Even Satan tries to make himself look like an angel of light.
translation_verse: Lɛ likítu mlo kugyɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si Satana ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, abitɛ yɛ isu ya ŋwisi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gi izekle kinɛ ɛ!

english_verse: So why does it seem strange for Satan's servants to pretend to do what is right? Someday they will get exactly what they deserve. Paul's Sufferings for Christ
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, línu liboe gi liatu ɔnɔ kugyɔ ɔ si yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba fɛ, bɛtsrɛ ba isu ya tɔ baŋwi ŋwisi banɔsumu gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ. Ba oblono kɔ aaze kidiliɛ ɖii gi ba kiboebitɛɛ kiʋi i. Paulo adɔ isu ya ní yɛ Ɔzia mɔmɔ nɔ aʋa

english_verse: I don't want any of you to think I am a fool. But if you do, then let me be a fool and brag a little.
translation_verse: Meedzini ipani si: ɔnɔnɔ ɔki gyo le edi si menu kanukpaʋi i. Pɔ̀ xe wɔzi lɛ nite e, lɛ kɔ mlazi mɛ ŋwisi kanukpaʋi atɔ midɔ̀ isu ya taʋɛ.

english_verse: When I do all this bragging, I do it as a fool and not for the Lord.
translation_verse: Ní mɛ kusudɔɔ liɔ ya mɛ ɛ, máʋipani ŋwisi kiliɛ gi Ɔkaɛ aapani i, boŋu i, mɛɛpani ŋwisi kanukpaʋi kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Yet if others want to brag about what they have done, so will I.
translation_verse: Agi banɔ glegle bɛɛdɔ isu ya ní kelea mɛ bede ʋa onu i, mɛ fɛ maadɔ isu ya.

english_verse: And since you are so smart, you will gladly put up with a fool.
translation_verse: Mlɛplɛ litɔ kese ki kunukpaʋiɔ ni isami lɛ ese linu si mlete liboe koŋ!

english_verse: In fact, you let people make slaves of you and cheat you and steal from you. Why, you even let them strut around and slap you in the face.
translation_verse: Ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, mlɛzɛɛzi ɔnɔ kakaa elue gi aabitɛ mlɔ yɛ banɔdzidzi loo ɔnɔ gi aakpɛ mlɔ klanɔ ɛ, loo amɔ ɔŋakɔ ní mlɔsu u, loo izeefeke isu ní mlɔ aʋa a, loo aasa mlɔ bidɔ ní aŋu na mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am ashamed to say we are too weak to behave in such a way. If they can brag, so can I, but it is a foolish thing to do.
translation_verse: Linu mɛ kuye si mizɛ lɛ si kwize befufluse gbɛgbɛ nite gi kwibitɛ eboa la ya ɛ! Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ aakenidi si yidɔ isu ya ní kiditɔ su i, mɛ fɛ maadɔ isu ní kɛ su u; mɛɛpani ŋwisi kanukpaʋi kinɛ.

english_verse: Are they Hebrews? So am I. Are they Jews? So am I. Are they from the family of Abraham? Well, so am I.
translation_verse: Hebrianɛ benu na a? Mɛ fɛ Hebriɔnɛ menu. Israelbanɛ benu na a? Mɛ fɛ Israelɔnɛ menu. Abraham yɛ besoebi benu na a? Mɛ fɛ Abraham yɛ osobi menu.

english_verse: Are they servants of Christ? I am a fool to talk this way, but I serve him better than they do. I have worked harder and have been put in jail more times. I have been beaten with whips more and have been in danger of death more often.
translation_verse: Kristo yɛ banɔsumu benu na a? Masani ba. . Mawa axwɛ gagla sani ba, meze ligba lipoe glegle, bekusi me mɔmɔɔmɔ, lɛ meze kutse iwla mɛ lipoe glegle e.

english_verse: Five times my own people gave me
translation_verse: Yudanɛ ma bɛfiaʋi mɛ biseʋi avitata ni ɔgɔtile afiaʋi tetsu zizi;

english_verse: Three times the Romans beat me with a big stick, and once my enemies stoned me. I have been shipwrecked three times, and I even had to spend a night and a day in the sea.
translation_verse: Tatá zizi bɛfiaʋi mɛ biseʋi ʋe; lɛ bɛsa mɛ akla tule e. Onitsahu lo ɛyaʋi nì blɔ tatá, gi mawɔ ní axu la mɛ elesi zizi nì liwe tile e.

english_verse: During my many travels, I have been in danger from rivers, robbers, my own people, and foreigners. My life has been in danger in cities, in deserts, at sea, and with people who only pretended to be the Lord's followers.
translation_verse: Mazɛ aklanɔ lipoe kakaa, lɛ meze kuxixa mɛ ɛ, dɔni inipe le kɔ, dɔni banama gi bɛtsa bede ʋa ɛki mɛ kɔ ɔ, dɔni blɔ ɔtutɔ blɔ akɔanɛ ma iwla mɛ ni banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ba iwla mɛ ɛ, baxixa ʋa beze ní ima ʋidi ne me, ni kumami yɔ mɛ, ní axu la aʋa, ani banama gi benu asia baʋaziŋa ba klɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I have worked and struggled and spent many sleepless nights. I have gone hungry and thirsty and often had nothing to eat. I have been cold from not having enough clothes to keep me warm.
translation_verse: Mawa axwɛ ni kupèpè; gi lipoe glegle e, mázadɔ sra; Kuʋɛ ni olile eku me; lɛ lipoe glegle e, máŋa kididɔ ɔ, lɛ kublɔ kwizeku me gi mámɔ ɔ ewu gi maakpɛ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Besides everything else, each day I am burdened down, worrying about all the churches.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní la klɔ ʋa kede e, mɛ begyodi ʋa mɛ báʋidɔ liwewe gi mɛ aŋu me éple kese kí isɔli ha lɛ petee loso o.

english_verse: When others are weak, I am weak too. When others are tricked into sin, I get angry.
translation_verse: Nyaŋwɛ ebotso xe mɛ mébotso o; lɛ xegi bɛgbani ɔnutɔ ɛkpɛ ní eboe zimizi me e, mizɛɛnu kuyeʋiyeʋi ní mɛ isu ya mɛ.

english_verse: If I have to brag, I will brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Xe linu si midɔ̀ isu ya ɛ, maadɔ isu ní kudɔkpaa liɔ gi kwibasɛ si kigagla kíma ní mɛ awla ɛ su.

english_verse: God, the Father of our Lord Jesus, knows I am not lying. And God is to be praised forever!
translation_verse: Aya gi onu Ɔka kí Ɔkaɛ Yesu u, gi yɛ biakpɔ̀ tsinɔ ní kanɔmɛ ɛ, ete si máʋisɛ asia a.

english_verse: The governor of Damascus at the time of King Aretas had the city gates guarded, so he could capture me.
translation_verse: Agi meze ní Damasko e, ɔma nɔ ɔʋadi ye gi eze ní Okusi Areta ese e, akpɛ banɔpe ba ní ɔma ʋidi no lɔ kuvunu yo tɔ bevu me e.

english_verse: But I escaped by being let down in a basket through a window in the city wall.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bepleni me ní kasɔʋi mɛ gani ligli le fɛsrɛ ɔtɔ me, lɛ mese ní yɛ su i.

english_verse: I have to brag. There is nothing to be gained by it, but I must brag about the visions and other things that the Lord has shown me.
translation_verse: Lilɛ si mitrɛ ní lɛ aʋa tɔ midɔ̀ isu ya, xegi lítrabu libi fɛ ɛ. Maapani ní eboa la gi Ɔkaɛ aki si mamɔ ní kubububasɛ uliɔ gi aki mɛ ɛ su.

english_verse: I am planning to visit you for the third time. But I still won't make a burden of myself. What I really want is you, and not what you have. Children are not supposed to save up for their parents, but parents are supposed to take care of their children.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, malɛ ní kublabla mɛ si míba edi mlo kutanɛ, lɛ mátraze lidɔtu iiki mlɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ mɛɛpɛ, aní kidiliɛ gi kiliɛ ní mlɔ awla a. Líziã si banuvɔ ba babla bidɔ aplɛ aki ba bapɔnɛ ba a, pɔ̀ linu si bapɔnɛ ba babla bidɔ aplɛ akí ba bebi ba.

english_verse: As I said, only God really knows if this man was in his body at the time.
translation_verse: Lɛ mete si ɔniliɛ ya ɛ, xe kisugyaɛ mɛ eze loo óze ní kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ, méte; Aya ko ete.

english_verse: I will brag about that man, but not about myself, except to say how weak I am.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ ya elu sú maadɔ isu ya, pɔ̀ mátradɔ̀ isu ya ní mɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ mɛ isu ya su u, sani si bidɔ bɛklɔ gi biabasɛ si mebotso e.

english_verse: Yet even if I did brag, I would not be foolish. I would simply be speaking the truth. But I will try not to say too much. That way, none of you will think more highly of me than you should because of what you have seen me do and say.
translation_verse: Xe nya mapɛ si midɔ̀ isu ya ɛ, mátraze kanukpaʋi i, lɛ ese linu si maazɛpani anukwarɛɛ. Pɔ̀ mátradɔ̀ isu ya a, lɛ loso ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tramɔ mɛ ɔnɔ ʋidi sani kiliɛ gi amɔ mɛ kidiliɛ gi mabitɛ ɛ, ni eboa la gi mapani i.

english_verse: Of course, I am now referring to the wonderful things I saw. One of Satan's angels was sent to make me suffer terribly, so that I would not feel too proud.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ tɔ mikíze ní kusufekeo ni kusudɔɔ mɛ ɛ, kɔkpɛ eboe ʋidi a la gi bebu ibasi mɛ loso ɛ, bɛkpɛ kunya gi kulɛ ŋwisi kibu kinɛ ɛ, ní mɛ isu ya mɛ, dɔni Ozimizi ye yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ klɔ gi kwiwa axwɛ na tɔ mikifeke isu ya a.

english_verse: Three times I begged the Lord to make this suffering go away.
translation_verse: Tatá zizi maxwa Ɔkaɛ si ɔti lidɔtu i lɛ ya ní mɛ aʋa.

english_verse: But he replied, “My gift of undeserved grace is all you need. My power is strongest when you are weak.” So if Christ keeps giving me his power, I will gladly brag about how weak I am.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ebu le onu si; “Mɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lisrɛ ɛki wɔ, lɛ ese linu si mɛ kigaglaɛ kizɛɛna kɛ ɔna klɔ ní kubotsobotso me.” Lɛ loso maadɔ isu isanɔ nì isami ní mɛ kubotsobotsoo me e, atɔ Kristo yɛ kigaglaɛ kiaze ní mɛ aʋa.

english_verse: Yes, I am glad to be weak or insulted or mistreated or to have troubles and sufferings, if it is for Christ. Because when I am weak, I am strong. Paul's Concern for the Lord's Followers at Corinth
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔkpɛ Kristo loso e, lilɛ mɛ ole xegi mebotso o, xegi beekpini me e, xegi malɛ ní kuxixa mɛ ɛ, xegi bɛyɔ kedu ní mɛ su u, xegi eboe la aʋítrɛ lɛ aʋa ɛki mɛ mumɔ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si, xegi mebotso e, kɔ malɛ gagla ɛ. Paulo Akɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ akí Korintoanɛ ma

english_verse: I have been making a fool of myself. But you forced me to do it, when you should have been speaking up for me. I may be nothing at all, but I am as good as those super apostles.
translation_verse: Mabitɛ isu ya kanukpaʋi, pɔ̀ mlɔ mlɛkɔ ɔ, xe mabitɛ nite e. Mlɔ nya lilɛ si mlakpɔ mɛ xe e, Lɛ ese linu si aní ɔnɔ fiteete menu, xe bɛkɔ mɛ srɔni “banɔkpɛkpɛ gbã ba,” gani lɛ mɛ si ménu kididɔ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: When I was with you, I was patient and worked all the powerful miracles and signs and wonders of a true apostle.
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ gi kizɛkpɛ banɔkpɛkpɛ ba gyesi i, gi kinu lixaŋu nɛ, enugutseboe, ni eboe mamɔ edi i, mabitɛ ʋɛ ní mlɔ kusi ni litɔplɛplɛkese kese glegle.

english_verse: You missed out on only one blessing the other churches received. That is, you didn't have to support me. Forgive me for doing you wrong.
translation_verse: Kiboebitɛ kitɔtɔ kilɛ gi mabitɛ aki isɔliha dzedze le e, gi mábitɛ aki mlɔ sani si mákɔ isu atrɔ ní mlɔ aʋa na a? Mlakɔ kesetataa lia akyɛ mɛ!

english_verse: So I will gladly give all I have and all I am. Will you love me less for loving you too much?
translation_verse: Liaze mɛ isami si miwa mɛ kidɔ kakaa su axwɛ aki mlɔ, kɔpɔni mɛ ɔtutɔ mɛ isu ya kyɛ. Lɛ xegi malɔ mlɔ asanɔ ɛ, mlaatí ní mlɔ kulɔlɔɔ kimɛ aʋa na a?

english_verse: You agree that I wasn't a burden to you. Maybe that's because I was trying to catch you off guard and trick you.
translation_verse: Kiliɛ gi lilɛ ɛ, mlɛdimɛ si ménu lidɔtu aki mlɔ. Pɔ̀ ɔmɔnɛɛ gi menu i, mapunu mlɔ lɛ mevu mlo e.

english_verse: Were you cheated by any of those I sent to you?
translation_verse: Maba mlɔ gani benyime ba ya gi makpɛ mlɔ klɔ aʋa na a?

english_verse: I urged Titus to visit you, and I sent another follower with him. But Titus didn't cheat you, and we felt and behaved the same way he did.
translation_verse: Makpɛ litukpo kese akí Tito si ɔba mlɔ klɔ, lɛ makpɛ mɛ onemi ye nì yɛ kude e. Tito ɔ́ba mlɔ, loo aba mlɔ na a? Aní Ɔfuɛfuɛ tole ni kude tule aʋa kwiga na?

english_verse: Have you been thinking all along that we have been defending ourselves to you? Actually, we have been speaking to God as followers of Christ. But, my friends, we did it all for your good.
translation_verse: Kibu mleebu si gakɔna ni abla ɛ, isu litukpo kwiŋwlɛ ki mlɔ na a? Aní nite, kwipani ní Aya aŋu mɛ ŋwisi banɔ gi balɛ ní Kristo me e; lɛ baʋia dimɛnɔ ba, kidɔ kakaa gi kwibitɛ ɛ, kwibitɛ kɛ ɛki mlɔ kigagla kukpɛkpɛ.

english_verse: I am afraid when I come, we won't be pleased with each other. I fear that some of you may be arguing or jealous or angry or selfish or gossiping or insulting each other. I even fear that you may be proud and acting like a mob.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mese lifu si xegi meku ni klɔ ʋa ɛ, mátramɔ mlɔ kiliɛ gi maapɛ si mleze e, lɛ mlɔ fɛ ɛ, mlátramɔ mɛ kiliɛ gi mlaapɛ si mize e. Meese lifu ne si maamɔ avitɛ, kaŋuzá, kusuyeʋi, kulɛlɛ, banɔ su kuzizi, kunugu kugugu ní banɔ su, kusudɔ ni kapisa ní mlɔ ɔma nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am afraid God will make me ashamed when I visit you again. I will feel like crying because many of you have never given up your old sins. You are still doing things that are immoral, indecent, and shameful.
translation_verse: Meese lifu si xegi maba mlɔ klɔ dzɛ ɛ, mɛ Aya aaple nì me ní mlɔ aŋu mɛ, lɛ ɔna aata mɛ ɔkpɛ banɔ glegle ba ya gi bɛta kese zanɔ ɛ, xe békyi ɔna mɛ ní ba anyaniboe la mɛ, ba kihasi ye ni kuda ŋwɛŋwɛ kpaŋukpaŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I am on my way to visit you for the third time. And as the Scriptures say, “Any charges must be proved true by at least two or three witnesses.”
translation_verse: Kutánɛ kɔya gi matradi mlo o. Kwite si “Liboe kakaa ɛ, linu si banɔ tiaʋa loo tiata beze gi biaŋa katsalia ki lɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: During my second visit I warned you that I would punish you and anyone else who doesn't stop sinning. I am far away from you now, but I give you the same warning.
translation_verse: Magba mlɔ lipoe lɛ gi maba mlɔ klɔ kuʋla a. Meedzini lɛ ɛpani abla gi máma ní mlɔ kusi i, si; xegi madziba dzɛ ɛ, mátradi banama gi bɛbitɛ eboe zimizi na ɛzanɔ ɔ, loo ɔnɔ dzedze ɔ́tɔtɔ asigaga.

english_verse: This should prove to you that I am speaking for Christ. When he corrects you, he won't be weak. He will be powerful!
translation_verse: Agi mlɛpɛ si mleete si Kristo eegu kunugu igani mɛ aʋa ɛ, lɛ ya linu likla trɔtrɔ si ónu ɔnɔ gi kigagla kíma ní yɛ awla a, xe abitɛ kiditɔ aki mlɔ ɔ; boŋu i, abasi yɛ kigaglaɛ ní mlɔ kusi.

english_verse: Although he was weak when he was nailed to the cross, he now lives by the power of God. We are weak, just as Christ was. But you will see that we will live by the power of God, just as Christ does.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si tɔ kuka lɛ aʋa ɛ, beku ye ní ose lo su ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi kigagla kíma ní yɛ su kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ gani Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ. Nite me ke blɔ kyɛ ɛ, ní likpɔ kpɛkpɛ mɛ nì yɛ ɛ, kigagla kíma ní blɔ su u; pɔ̀ gani Aya yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, kwiaze ligbã́ ni yɛ tɔ kwiasumu mlo.

english_verse: Test yourselves and find out if you really are true to your faith. If you pass the test, you will discover that Christ is living in you. But if Christ isn't living in you, you have failed.
translation_verse: Mlatapo mlɔ isu ya edi si mlalɛ ní kuziŋaa mɛ na a; tɔ mlaxwa litsi ni ìsu ya a. Kɔ mlamɔ ogyesi si Kristo Yesu ɔlɛ ní mlɔ mɛ na a? Kɔ kiwɔli mlɛ wɔli petee, lɛ ko mlatrámɔ egyesi i.

english_verse: I hope you will discover we have not failed.
translation_verse: Maka lɛ aʋa si mlaamɔ egyesi si kúwɔli ní kufekedi yo me e.

english_verse: We pray you will stop doing evil things. We don't pray like this to make ourselves look good, but to get you to do right, even if we are failures.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kwixwa Aya si mlakíbitɛ liboe zimizi i tɔtɔ. Aní si tɔ banima biamɔ ogyesi si mlɛŋa kufekedi yo aʋa loso o, pɔ̀ tɔ̀ mlabitɛ kidiliɛ gi kinu kigbã ɛ, xegi liiŋwi ki banima si kwiwɔli kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All we can do is to follow the truth and not fight against it.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kutrátani kedu kuyɔ ní anukwarɛɛ su u, sani si kulɛ ni anukwarɛɛ kude.

english_verse: Even though we are weak, we are glad that you are strong, and we pray you will do even better.
translation_verse: Isami izeeku blo xe kigagla kíma ní blɔ mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ xe mlɔ pɔ̀ mlalɛ gagla ɛ; lɛ loso kuzɛɛxwa Aya si tɔ mlana a.

english_verse: I am writing these things to you before I arrive. This way I won't have to be hard on you when I use the authority the Lord has given me. I was given this authority, so I could help you and not destroy you.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ ya loso maŋwlimi eboa la ya aki mlɔ gi máma ní mlɔ su u; tɔ xegi maba ɛ, litrázia si mihe eboe gagla ni mlɔ ní mɛ ozeklɔ onu. Ozekɔ ɔlɔ gi Ɔkaɛ aki mɛ ki mlɔ kutsitsi i, aní si tɔ mikɔ zizi mlo o. Kunɔpanɔ gɔgɔɔ

english_verse: Goodbye, my friends. Do better and pay attention to what I have said. Try to get along and live peacefully with each other. Now I pray that God, who gives love and peace, will be with you.
translation_verse: Tɔ miblono e, mɛ benemi ba, mleze mumɔ! Mlekeni edi tɔ mlana; mlenu mɛ liboe le; mleze ni kugyodi tule; atɔ mleze ní kusuyɔ mɛ. Tɔ kunɔlɔɔ ni kusuyɔɔ Aya oze ni mlo.

english_verse: Give each other a warm greeting. All God's people send their greetings.
translation_verse: Mlakpɛ mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba wolaa ni ɔna dradra.

english_verse: I pray that God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ will be kind to you and will bless you with peace!
translation_verse: Maxwa Aya blɔ Ɔka ní blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo tɔ baki mlɔ lipomepɛpɛ ni kusuyɔ.

english_verse: Christ obeyed God our Father and gave himself as a sacrifice for our sins to rescue us from this evil world.
translation_verse: Kristo akɔ yɛ isu akɔ ní blɔ eboe zimizi na litukpo gi ayurɔ yɛ isu ya akpɛ ní blɔ Ɔka Aya kupɛ́pɛɔ ese e, tɔ yiki blɔ ɔwlamɔmɔ dɔni lipoe zimizi i lɛ ya mɛ.

english_verse: God will be given glory forever and ever. Amen. The Only True Message
translation_verse: Atɔ mlaki tɔ kukpɔ̀ Aya dɔni kanɔmɛ tsini kanɔmɛ! Tɔ liba lɛ mɛ. Liboe Gbã dzedze etɔtɔ líma

english_verse: I am shocked that you have so quickly turned from God, who chose you with his gift of undeserved grace. You have believed another message,
translation_verse: Litu me kugyɔ si ní lipoe kpekpemi lɛ ya mɛ ɛ, mlɛyurɔ Aya gi yɛ axwa mlɔ gani Kristo yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ mlɛzi kudɔbasɛ dzedze ko e,

english_verse: when there is really only one true message. But some people are causing you trouble and want to make you turn away from the good news about Christ.
translation_verse: gi ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ, línu Liboe Gbã. Pɔ̀ lɛ mɛ lidra si banɔ batɔ balɛ gi bɛɛpisa mlɔ ɔ, lɛ beekeni idi tɔ beekyi Kristo yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I pray that God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from our message to you! It doesn't matter if that person is one of us or an angel from heaven.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe blɔ ɔ, loo ɔxwɛdzɛ dɔni lifunu ne aasɔli Liboe Gbã dzedzeetɔ ki mlɔ sani liboe lɛ gi kwisi mlɔ ɛ, tɔ bebu ye lipɔ tratratra, tɔ bakɔ yɛ akpɛ ní kifumoni ye me e!

english_verse: I have said it before, and I will say it again. I hope God will punish anyone who preaches anything different from what you have already believed.
translation_verse: Kwipani lɛ edi, lɛ mɛɛpani lɛ abla dzɛ si: xe ɔnutɔ aasɔli Liboe Gbã dzedze ki mlɔ sani lɛ klɔ gi mlɛnu u, xe mlɛzɛ ɛ, atɔ bebu ye lipɔ tratratra tɔ bakɔ yɛ akpɛ ní kifumoni ye me e!

english_verse: I am not trying to please people. I want to please God. Do you think I am trying to please people? If I were doing that, I would not be a servant of Christ. How Paul Became an Apostle
translation_verse: Kibasɛ libasɛ si mɛɛpɛ si miku ɔnɔ gbagbe loo Aya liŋu na a? Aloo kipɛ mɛɛpɛ si miku banima aŋu na a? Xe nya mapɛ si miku banima aŋu i, mátraze Kristo yɛ ɔnɔsumu u. Kiliɛ gi Lize xe Paulo Edzì Ɔnɔ kpɛkpɛ ɛ

english_verse: My friends, I want you to know that no one made up the message I preach.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, mlaki tɔ misi mlɔ si, Liboe Gbãɛ nɛ gi masɔli i, lídɔ ɔnɔ kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: It wasn't given or taught to me by some mere human. My message came directly from Jesus Christ when he appeared to me.
translation_verse: Mázi lɛ adɔ ni ɔnɔ gbagbe ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ɔ, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́basɛ mɛ ɛ. Boŋu Yesu Kristo yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ebu le abasɛ mɛ.

english_verse: You know how I used to live as a Jew. I was cruel to God's church and even tried to destroy it.
translation_verse: Bɛsi mlɔ kiliɛ gi meze ligba nɛ ɛ, ní Yudanɛ ma kusumu yo onu, kiliɛ gi makɔ keduyɔyɔ ni litukpomegagla alɛɛ ní Aya yɛ baʋaziŋa ba ɔha lɔ su u, xe mekeni edi si miyaʋi lɔ kikaka elu u.

english_verse: I was a much better Jew than anyone else my own age, and I obeyed every law our ancestors had given us.
translation_verse: Lɛ ni blɔ kesea mɛ ɛ, meze ní ɔtɔ akí mɛ ahanuglɔ Yadanɛ glegle ní Yudanɛ ma kusumu yo ikɔ lɛ onu, lɛ mevu blɔ baka ba batrɔtrɔ ʋa mɛ asani ba.

english_verse: to show me his Son, so I would announce his message to the Gentiles. I didn't talk this over with anyone.
translation_verse: si yibu Yebi ye e, gi ɔlɛ ní mɛ mɛ̀ ɛbasɛ mɛ ɛ, tɔ maasɔli yɛ Liboe Gbã nɛ ní yɛ su kí banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ɛ, mádzɛ ɔnɔnɔ kɔ si mizɛ kiɖaŋu u.

english_verse: I didn't say a word, not even to the men in Jerusalem who were apostles before I was. Instead, I went at once to Arabia, and afterwards I returned to Damascus.
translation_verse: Lɛ mátrɛ ní Yerusalem tɔ mizɛɛmɔ banɔkpɛkpɛa ba gi beze ɔtɔ ɛki mɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, masɛ kyɔŋ atrɛ ní Arabia lɛ kede e, medzi aba Damasko.

english_verse: Three years later I went to visit Peter in Jerusalem and stayed with him for
translation_verse: Agɔ tata kede xe memu atrɛ ní Yerusalem tɔ mizeete Petro o, lɛ meze yɛ klɔ ewe liɔfɔ ni tetsu u.

english_verse: The only other apostle I saw was James, the Lord's brother.
translation_verse: Mámɔ ɔnɔkpɛkpɛ ɔtɔtɔ tɔɔ sani Yakobo, gi onu Ɔkaɛ yeneminyime e.

english_verse: And in the presence of God I swear I am telling the truth.
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ gi maŋwlimi i, kilɛ ni kɛ mɛ, Aya ete si máʋisɛ asia!

english_verse: Later, I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
translation_verse: Lɛ kede e, madzi ikɔ́ lɛ ní Siria nì Kilikia.

english_verse: But no one who belonged to Christ's churches in Judea had ever seen me in person.
translation_verse: Ni lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, baʋaziŋa ba ní Kristo me ba iha lɛ mɛ bezeze ba ní Yudea ɛ, béte me aŋu mɛ nì aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They had only heard that the one who had been cruel to them was now preaching the message that he had once tried to destroy.
translation_verse: Eboa la gi banima bɛzɛɛpani ní mɛ su u ko béte si, “Onyime liɛ klɔ gi ayɔ kedu ya ní blɔ su azanɔ ɛ, yɛ ɛɛsɔli kuziŋaa klɔ gi ekeni edi si yiyaʋi i!”

english_verse: And because of me, they praised God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e bɛkpɔ̀ Aya kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi abitɛ agani mɛ aʋa a.

english_verse: Fourteen years later I went to Jerusalem with Barnabas. I also took along Titus.
translation_verse: Ní agɔ liɔfɔ ni tene kede e, memu atre ní Yerusalem ni Banaba, lɛ magbani Tito kyɛ apɔni isu ya ɛ.

english_verse: But I went there because God had told me to go, and I explained the good news I had been preaching to the Gentiles. Then I met privately with the ones who seemed to be the most important leaders. I wanted to make sure my work in the past and my future work would not be for nothing.
translation_verse: Madzɛ lɛ ese linu si Aya ebu abasɛ mɛ si linu si mitrɛ. Ní kikpokpo me kutsátsa mɛ ɛ mebú eboe mɛ, aki banama gi bɛŋwi ŋwisi ba beze ba onu i, mápɛ si axwɛ ana gi mawa zanɔ ɔ loo laklɔ gi mɛɛwa abla ɛ, adɔ limu.

english_verse: Titus went to Jerusalem with me. He was a Greek, but still he wasn't forced to be circumcised.
translation_verse: Mɛ ɔgaglɔnɛ Tito, gi onu Hela ɔnɛ kruaa kyɛ ɛ, bemi yɛ aʋa si ɔtsà ɔyaʋi.

english_verse: We went there because of those who pretended to be followers and had sneaked in among us as spies. They had come to take away the freedom Christ Jesus had given us, and they were trying to make us their slaves.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ liboe lɛ ya likpɛ ketui dono o, lɛ ese linu si banɔ baklɔ ʋa bɛbitɛ isu ya ŋwisi baʋaziŋa kinɛ ɛ, bekpo etiku blɔ mɛ, tɔ bɛɛpami tɔ beedi ɔwlamɔmɔ nɔ klɔ gi kwimɔ ɛ, gani blɔ likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo Yesu u. Bɛpɛ si bɛɛbitɛ blɔ banɔdzidzi.

english_verse: But we wanted you to have the true message. This is why we didn't give in to them, not even for a second.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ tɔ kubla Liboe Gbã nɛ lɛ anukwarɛɛ akí mlɔ ɛ, kúkiba kudeonu lipoe tɔtɔ fɛ.

english_verse: Some of them were supposed to be important leaders, but I didn't care who they were. God doesn't have any favorites! None of these so-called special leaders added anything to my message.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ baklɔ gi bɛŋwi ikí ba si ba bedi ní ba onu i, mámɔ lidzegaga ɛtɔtɔ ní ba kusi i. Lɛ ese linu si Aya ɔʋíxwa litsi ní banɔ kiŋwiŋwi mɛ su, lɛ banɔ baklɔ ba bákɔ liboeboe apɔni liboe lɛ gi mabasɛ si mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: They realized God had sent me with the good news for Gentiles, and he had sent Peter with the same message for Jews.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, bɛmɔ si Aya aki mɛ lixwɛ si misɔli liboe le ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma a, kiliɛ ɖii gi aki Petro lixwɛ nɛ si ɔsɔli liboe le ɔki Yudanɛ ma ɛ.

english_verse: God, who had sent Peter on a mission to the Jews, was now using me to preach to the Gentiles.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kiliɛ gi Aya awa axwɛ ní Petro yɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ ki Yudanɛ ma ɛ, nite ke ɛɛwa axwɛ na ní mɛ axwɛ na mɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔkpɛkpɛ ki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma a.

english_verse: James, Peter, and John realized that God had given me the message about his gift of undeserved grace. And these men are supposed to be the backbone of the church. They even gave Barnabas and me a friendly handshake. This was to show that we would work with Gentiles and that they would work with Jews.
translation_verse: Yakobo, Petro, nì Yohanes gi bɛŋwi si ba bedi ní ba onu i, bɛkpɛ lɛ gyesi ye si Aya aki mɛ lixwɛ dzedze lɛ ya; lɛ loso bɛki mɛ nì Banaba awla ní lɛ aʋa a, gi binu gyesi si baxwɛwaglɔnɛ kunu u. Kwidimɛ si Banaba nì Mɛ kwiawa axwɛ na ní banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma kusi i, tɔ ba fɛ biawa axwɛ na ní Yudanɛ ma kusi.

english_verse: They only asked us to remember the poor, and this was something I had always been eager to do. Paul Corrects Peter at Antioch
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ ko gi beʋi kinu si tɔ kuplɛ aŋu ní bazianete ba ní bá likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ mɛ aʋa a, gi lɛ linu kidiliɛ ɖii gi mazɛɛpɛ si mibitɛ ɛ. Paulo Atɛni Petro Ní Antioxia

english_verse: When Peter came to Antioch, I told him face to face that he was wrong.
translation_verse: Gi Petro aba Antioxia ɛ, mayɔ kedu ní yɛ su aŋu mɛ ni aŋu mɛ lɛ ese linu si, lɛ mɛ lidra si ata kese e.

english_verse: He and the others hid their true feelings so well that even Barnabas was fooled.
translation_verse: Lɛ benemi Yudanɛ dzedze ba fɛ ɛ, bɛkɔ ɔŋufa ɛpɔni Petro; lɛ bemeni Banaba fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But when I saw they were not really obeying the truth that is in the good news, I corrected Peter in front of everyone and said: Peter, you are a Jew, but you live like a Gentile. So how can you force Gentiles to live like Jews?
translation_verse: Agi mamɔ si baʋíga ní kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa ni Liboe Gbã nɛ lɛ anukwarɛɛ aʋa ɛ, masi Petro ní ba petee aŋu mɛ si, “Yuda ɔnɛ wonu pɔ̀ woeze ligbã́ nɛ ko ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi ónu Yuda ɔnɛ kinɛ ɛ. Kitɛmɛ wɔakyi Babubitɛ ba tɔ beze ni Yudanɛ ma ikɔ lɛ kude e?”

english_verse: But we know that God accepts only those who have faith in Jesus Christ. No one can please God by simply obeying the Law. So we put our faith in Christ Jesus, and God accepted us because of our faith.
translation_verse: Lɛ kwite si ɔnɔ liboe litrágyɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ, gani ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ mɛ, boŋu i, gani kuziŋa ní Yesu Kristo me. Blɔ fɛ kwizi Kristo Yesu aʋa ɛɛŋa, tɔ blɔ linɛ liagyɔ ní Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ, lɛ aní kɔgani kidiliɛ gi Mose Ɔsã nɔ eʋi me e. Lɛ ese linu si gani Mose ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ mɛ ɛ, ɔnɔnɔ linɛ litrágyɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: When we Jews started looking for a way to please God, we discovered that we are sinners too. Does this mean that Christ is the one who makes us sinners? No, it doesn't!
translation_verse: “Kɔlɔ xegi kwipɛ si blɔ linɛ ligyɔ ní Aya kɔ ɔ, gani likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo e, xe bɛɛmɔ blɔ si kwibitɛ eboe zimizi ŋwisi kiliɛ gi banama a, gi bénu Yudanɛ ma balɛ kinɛ ɛ, kɔ kibasɛ libasɛ si Kristo ɛɛkpɛ eboe zimizi na kubitɛ kigagla na a? Owo líma lɛ mɛ nite e!

english_verse: But if I tear down something and then build it again, I prove that I was wrong at first.
translation_verse: Xegi medzini kiditɔ ɔ, gi mayaʋi i, xe medzini kɛ awa ɛ, kɔlɔ mɛɛbasɛ isu ya si, menu ɔnutɔ gi ɛɛta Ɔsã nɔ ese e.

english_verse: It was the Law itself that killed me and freed me from its power, so I could live for God. I have been nailed to the cross with Christ.
translation_verse: Tsã ɛ, meze ní ɔsã nɔ ese, pɔ̀ máma ní ɔsã nɔ ese tɔ ɔ, lɛ malɛ ligbã́ nɛ aki Aya. Beye mɛ ligbã́ koko le nì Kristo ní yɛ osetsase lo su,

english_verse: I have died, but Christ lives in me. And I now live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave his life for me.
translation_verse: lɛ loso aní mɛ malɛ ligbã́ nɛ tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ Kristo yɛ ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní mɛmɛ ɛ. Ligbã́ɛ nɛ gi malɛ abla ɛ, malɛ ní kuziŋaa mɛ ní Aya Yebi ye me, ɔniliɛ gi alɔ mɛ ɛ, xe akɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ akyɛ ní mɛ onu i.

english_verse: I don't turn my back on God's gift of undeserved grace. If we can be acceptable to God by obeying the Law, it was useless for Christ to die.
translation_verse: Mátradimɛ si mikyí kede ekpɛ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ xe ɔnutɔ linɛ liagyɔ ní Aya aŋu mɛ gani Mose Ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, kɔ libasɛ si Kristo etse fiteete!”

english_verse: You stupid Galatians! I told you exactly how Jesus Christ was nailed to a cross. Has someone now put an evil spell on you?
translation_verse: Mlɔ Galatia banɛ kunukpaʋi! Nyaŋwɛ akpɛ mlɔ kudamadamaa liɔ ɔ? Ní mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, bɛsrɔ mlɔ Yesu Kristo yɛ kutseo ní osetsase lo su!

english_verse: I want to know only one thing. How were you given God's Spirit? Was it by obeying the Law of Moses or by hearing about Christ and having faith in him?
translation_verse: Mlasi mɛ liboe tilee lɛ ya; kizi mlɛzi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gani Mose Ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ mɛ loo gani liboe le gi mlenu u, xe mlɛzi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa me na a?

english_verse: How can you be so stupid? Do you think that by yourself you can complete what God's Spirit started in you?
translation_verse: Lɛ kitɛ mlɛdɔ kunukpaa ŋwisi baga ʋa nite e! Agi mlekpese mlɔ ligbã́ nɛ ni Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛ; kipɛ́ mlɛpɛ si mleebloni le ní mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlɔ kigagla mɛ na a?

english_verse: Have you gone through all of this for nothing? Is it all really for nothing?
translation_verse: Kɔ eboe gleglea la mɛ gi mlɛga ɛ, enu kididɔ kura aki mlɔ na a?

english_verse: God gives you his Spirit and works miracles in you. But does he do this because you obey the Law of Moses or because you have heard about Christ and have faith in him?
translation_verse: Kikɔ́ Aya akɔ yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ akí mlɔ ɔ, xe abitɛ enugutseboe ala a, gi abitɛ ɛ ní mlɔ kusi kɔkpɛ gi mlɛbitɛ ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ lɔ kupɛ́pɛɔ aʋa a, loo kɔkpɛ gi mlenu Liboe Gbã nɛ ɛ, xe mlezi lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa na a?

english_verse: And so, you should understand that everyone who has faith is a child of Abraham.
translation_verse: Linu si mlete si banama awla gi kuziŋa kulɛ ɛ, bá benu Abraham yɛ bebi ba.

english_verse: Long ago the Scriptures said God would accept the Gentiles because of their faith. This is why God told Abraham the good news that all nations would be blessed because of him.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ Liboe le lipani plɛ si Aya atráki si banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ma ɛ, ba bede ʋa biagyɔ gani kuziŋa mɛ. Lɛ loso, Aya yɛ Liboe le lidra Liboe Gbã nɛ mɛ ɛki Abraham si, “Gani wɔ aʋa ɛ, Aya aakrira kelea amade na petee.”

english_verse: This means everyone who has faith will share in the blessings given to Abraham because of his faith.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso ɔnɔ kakaa gi aziŋa ɛ, Aya aakrira yɛ ni Abraham. Abraham yɛ onu kuziŋaa ɔkaɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who tries to please God by obeying the Law is under a curse. The Scriptures say, “Everyone who doesn't obey everything in the Law is under a curse.”
translation_verse: Banama gi bɛkɔ ba begyodi ʋa etrɔ ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, balɛ ní ifixoxo ese. Lɛ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si, “Ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́zɛ kidɔ kakaa gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ keplekpaa mɛ ɛ, xe ɔʋíbitɛ ni lɔ aʋa ɛ, ɔkpasi ní Aya yɛ ifixoxo ese!”

english_verse: No one can please God by obeying the Law. The Scriptures also say, “The people God accepts because of their faith will live.”
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, lɛmɛ lidra si ɔnɔnɔ linɛ litrágyɔ nì Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ ɛ, gani Ɔsã nɔ aʋa kubitɛ mɛ. Lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si i, “Ɔniliɛ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, yɛ ko aaze ligbã.”

english_verse: But Christ rescued us from the Law's curse, when he became a curse in our place. This is because the Scriptures say that anyone who is nailed to a tree is under a curse.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Kristo edzì ifixoxo ní blɔ onu i, Kristo ebú blo adɔ ni Ɔsã nɔ lɔ ifixoxo le ese; lɛ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si i, “Ɔnɔ kakaa gi bɛkú ní ose su i, ɔlɛ ní Aya yɛ ifixoxo ese.”

english_verse: And because of what Jesus Christ has done, the blessing promised to Abraham was taken to the Gentiles. This happened so that by faith we would be given the promised Holy Spirit. The Law and the Promise
translation_verse: Kristo abitɛ lɛ si tɔ kukrira liɔ klɔ gi Aya aka aplɛ akí Abraham si i, biaki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ fɛ gani Kristo Yesu me e, atɔ gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, kwiazi Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ gi aka aplɛ ɛ. Lidzegaga nɛ ní Mose Ɔsã nɔ ni Aya yɛ Kakaplɛa kusi

english_verse: My friends, I will use an everyday example to explain what I mean. Once someone agrees to something, no one else can change or cancel the agreement.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, matraki mlɔ liŋwitsɛ dɔni liwe kakaa ligbã́zeze me; kiliɛ gi ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́trata liboetɔ ɔ, gi bɛpani i, xe bɛnya nɛ ɛplɛ ese e, loo aakɔ̀ kididɔ pɔni lɛ tɔ ɛ, nite lilɛ ní kapɛa lia ya kyɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: That is how it is with the promises God made to Abraham and his descendant. The promises were not made to many descendants, but only to one, and that one is Christ.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, Aya abitɛ yɛ kakaplɛa akí Abraham ni yɛ osobi ye. Aya yɛ liboe le lípani si “ni wɔ besoebi ba,” gi libasɛ si banɔ srɛsrɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ lipani si, “ni wɔ osobi ye e,” gi libasɛ si ɔnɔ tole kpoo, gi yɛ onu Kristo ye e.

english_verse: If we have to obey the Law in order to receive God's blessings, those blessings don't really come to us because of God's promise. But God was kind to Abraham and made him a promise.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe Aya bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ bika ni Ɔsã nɔ su i, kɔlɔ bíka ní yɛ kakaplɛa su tɔ ɔ. Pɔ̀ kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ loso e, akɔ bidɔkɔkɔɛ klɔ ʋa aki Abraham gani yɛ kakaplɛa mɛ.

english_verse: What is the use of the Law? It was given later to show that we sin. But it was only supposed to last until the coming of that descendant who was given the promise. In fact, angels gave the Law to Moses, and he gave it to the people.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ ege loso bɛkpɛ Ɔsã nɔ ɛ? Bɛkɔ lɔ ɛpɔnɔ, tɔ ɔbasɛ blɔ, tɔ kute kidiliɛ gi eboe zimizi na enu i, lɛ linu si ozè zɛkɔna ni Abraham yɛ osobi ye kibabaɛ, gi yɛ loso Aya aki kakaplɛa ɛ. Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba aʋa Ɔsã nɔ ɛga xe eba a, lɛ bɛkɔ lɔ ɛki ɔnɔ gbagbe ɔguvu u, gi ɔlɛ ni Aya yɛ baxɛdzɛ ba ni blɔ kusi i, xe ableke ɛna banɔ gbagbe ba a.

english_verse: There is only one God, and the Law did not come directly from him. Slaves and Children
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, ɔguvu ye ónu ɔnɔ tole ɔnɛ ɛ, lɛ Aya tole ko ɔlɛ ɛ. Kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso Ɔsã nɔ ɛba ɛ.

english_verse: Does the Law disagree with God's promises? No, it doesn't! If any law could give life to us, we could become acceptable to God by obeying that law.
translation_verse: Kibasɛ libasɛ si Ɔsã nɔ ɛyɔ kedu ní Aya yɛ kakaplɛa su na a? Aníte koŋ! Lɛ xe ɔnɔ azɛ ɔsã nɔ klɔ gi aaki ligbã ɛ, kɔlɔ ɔnɔ kakaa kude kwiagyɔ gani lɔ aʋa kugaga mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But the Scriptures say that sin controls everyone, so that God's promises will be for anyone who has faith in Jesus Christ.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani kí kelea mɛ petee si kakpasi ní kuzimizi yo kigaglaɛ ese; lɛ loso kakaplɛa bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ní kuziŋa ní Yesu Kristo me e, bɛ bɛkɔ ɛki banama gi bɛziŋa a.

english_verse: The Law controlled us and kept us under its power until the time came when we would have faith.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe able ke kuziŋaa kwiaba ɛ, Ɔsã nɔ evu blo enya ŋwisi ligba bezeze kinɛ, kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi biabu kuziŋaa basɛ ɛ.

english_verse: In fact, the Law was to be our teacher until Christ came. Then we could have faith and be acceptable to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, Ɔsã nɔ ɛzɛɛgbani blɔ ɛtsinɔ ní Kristo klɔ atɔ Aya alulɔ blɔ ni yɛ kusi gani kuziŋa ní Kristo me aʋa a.

english_verse: But once a person has learned to have faith, there is no more need to have the Law as a teacher.
translation_verse: Abla gi kuziŋaa lipoe le lina ɛ, Ɔsã nɔ ɔma ní blɔ onu tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: All of you are God's children because of your faith in Christ Jesus.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, mlɔ pɔ́ mledzi Aya ye bebi gani likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ nì Kristo Yesu u.

english_verse: And when you were baptized, it was as though you had put on Christ in the same way you put on new clothes.
translation_verse: Gi bɛkpɛ kuni kimlɔ ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo e, mlɛkpɛ Kristo ɔtutɔ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ ŋwisi awu kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Faith in Christ Jesus is what makes each of you equal with each other, whether you are a Jew or a Greek, a slave or a free person, a man or a woman.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, lidzegaga ɛtɔtɔ líma ní Yuda ɔnɛ ni Hela ɔnɛ kusi, ɔnɔdzidzi ni isuwla mɛ ozeze kusi, benyime ni badzɛ kusi; mlɔ pɔ́ mledzi kidɔ tile ní likpɔkpɛkpɛ mɛ ni Kristo Yesu u.

english_verse: So if you belong to Christ, you are now part of Abraham's family, and you will be given what God has promised.
translation_verse: Xegi mlenu Kristo banɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ mlenu Abraham yɛ besoebi, lɛ mlaazɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya aka aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Children who are under age are no better off than slaves, even though everything their parents own will someday be theirs.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mɛɛpani si, xegi obi liɛ gi aaŋa yɛ ka bidɔ mɛ ɛ odzi onu obi i, lidzegaga ɛtɔtɔ líma ní yɛ nì ɔnɔdzidzi ye kusi i, ɔkpɛ gi oʋítsi loso o, xe kidɔ kakaa kinu yɛ kínɛ fɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This is because children are placed in the care of guardians and teachers until the time their parents have set.
translation_verse: Agi oʋítsi i, banima balɛ gi biadi ye aʋa a, lɛ biadi yɛ bidɔ mɛ kyɛ aʋa kɔzɛna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi yɛ ka atrɔ aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This is how it was with us. We were like children ruled by the powers of this world.
translation_verse: Niteme ké, gi blɔ fɛ kwize banuvɔ ɛ, kwize banɔdzidzi kí kalea lia mɛ ifuɛfuɛ nɛ gi iiŋa aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: But when the time was right, God sent his Son, and a woman gave birth to him. His Son obeyed the Law,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi lipoe gbã nɛ lina ɛ, Aya atsisi yɛ ɔtutɔ yebinyime e. Aba ŋwisi ɔnɔ Yebi gi ɔdzɛ apɔ ɛ, lɛ eze ní Yudanɛ ma Ɔsã nɔ ese e,

english_verse: so he could set us free from the Law, and we could become God's children.
translation_verse: atɔ aabu banama gi beze ní Ɔsã nɔ ese e, tɔ kwiazi blɔ ɔŋa klɔ ŋwisi blɔ Ɔkaɛ yɛ bebi kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Now that we are his children, God has sent the Spirit of his Son into our hearts. And his Spirit tells us that God is our Father.
translation_verse: Tɔ bibasɛ si mlenu yɛ bebi i, Aya atsisi Yebi ye yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ní blɔ ìna nɛ mɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ ɔnɔ klɔ gi izɛɛkpɛ ozi si, “Kakaa,” mɔka.

english_verse: You are no longer slaves. You are God's children, and you will be given what he has promised. Paul's Concern for the Galatians
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, wónu ɔnɔdzidzi tɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ obi i. Lɛ gi wonu yɛ obi i, Aya aaki wɔ kidɔ kakaa gi kilɛ ní yɛ awla kí yɛ bebi ba ɛ. Paulo akɔ litɔ lɛ mɛ aki Galatianɛ ma

english_verse: Before you knew God, you were slaves of gods that are not real.
translation_verse: Tsã́ gi mléte Aya ɛ, mleze banɔdzidzi ki babu ʋa a.

english_verse: But now you know God, or better still, God knows you. How can you turn back and become the slaves of those weak and pitiful powers?
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ abla gi mlete Aya a, loo, mipani si abla gi Aya ete mlo e, kítɛ xe mlɛɛpɛ si mleedzini isu ikpɛ ni ifuɛfuɛ nɛ awla gi kigagla kíma a, pɔ́ xe iŋa aʋa ɛ awla.? Ege lɛ loso mlɛpɛ si mleedzi banɔdzidzi ki lɛ dzɛ ɛ?

english_verse: You even celebrate certain days, months, seasons, and years.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlezeevu aŋu ní ewe veve la, badzidzi ʋa, epoe la ni agɔ la su mɔmɔɔmɔ.

english_verse: I am afraid I have wasted my time working with you.
translation_verse: Likpɛ mɛ ɔna nɔ kunugu! Liibasɛ si mɛ axwɛ ana gi mawa akí mlɔ ɛ, edzì efiteete.

english_verse: My friends, I beg you to be like me, just as I once tried to be like you. Did you mistreat me
translation_verse: Maki mlɔ litukpo, mɛ benemi ba, mleze ko ŋwisi kiliɛ gi malɛ ɛ. Kiliɛ gi mɛ fɛ malɛ ko ŋwisi mlɔ kinɛ ɛ. Aní kita mlɛta mɛ ese e.

english_verse: when I first preached to you? No you didn't, even though you knew I had come there because I was sick.
translation_verse: Mlaplɛ aŋu ní kidiliɛ gi kɛ loso o, masɔli liboe gbã nɛ aki mlɔ kutɔpu yɔ ɛ; lɛ ese linu si mɛ isu mɛ ízɛɛdra.

english_verse: My illness must have caused you some trouble, but you didn't hate me or turn me away because of it. You welcomed me as though I were one of God's angels or even Christ Jesus himself.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi mɛ ɔnɔse lo eze lidɔtu kí mlɔ fɛ ɛ, mládzi kede ní mɛ su loo yurɔ mɛ ɛ. Boŋu i, mlɛzi mɛ kiliɛ gi mlazi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ dɔni lifunu ne kinɛ ɛ; ŋwisi kiliɛ gi mlaazi Kristo Yesu ɔtutɔ lɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Where is that good feeling now? I am sure if it had been possible, you would have taken out your own eyes and given them to me.
translation_verse: Isami iize ní mlɔ aʋa! Lɛ liboe woli liba ɛ? Mɛ ɔtutɔ maatani kupani si mlaapɛ si mlɛɛkpaʋi mlɔ bimbi ʋe tɔ mlɛkɔ ʋɛ ɛki mɛ ɛ, xe liaze plɔplɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Am I now your enemy, just because I told you the truth?
translation_verse: Lɛ abla ɛ, kidzí medzi mlɔ ahwafiɔnɛ ɛ, gi masi mlɔ anukwarɛɛ loso na a?

english_verse: Those people may be paying you a lot of attention, but it isn't for your good. They only want to keep you away from me, so you will pay them a lot of attention.
translation_verse: Banɔ dzedzea klɔ ba ɛ, ba bimbi bizã ní mlɔ su, pɔ̀ ba begyodi ʋa bápɛ ɛ. Ba kupɛ́pɛ ko kunu si tɔ bafili mlɔ ní blɔ su, atɔ mladimɛ nì ba ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛdimɛ ni mlɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It is always good to give your attention to something worthwhile, even when I am not with you.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, lipɛ si kupɛ́pɛ dzudzu uliɔ klɔ kuze, xe begyodi ʋa bɛ pɛ ɛ, lilɛ si lize nite lipoe kakaa, lɛ aní si xe malɛ ní mlɔ su ko o.

english_verse: I wish I were with you now. Then I would not have to talk this way. You really have me puzzled. Hagar and Sarah
translation_verse: Nya liku mɛ kule si mize ní mlɔ su abla, atɔ maabu ɔkli dzedze ní mlɔ su. Lɛ ese linu si mɛɛgani aŋu ní mlɔ su! Hagar Ani Sara Ba liŋwitsɛɛ lɛ

english_verse: Some of you would like to be under the rule of the Law of Moses. But do you know what the Law says?
translation_verse: Mlakí tɔ miʋi mlo banama gi mlɛpɛ si mleeze ní Ɔsã nɔ ese e; Dani mleenu kiliɛ gi Ɔsã nɔ ɛɛpani na a?

english_verse: In the Scriptures we learn that Abraham had two sons. The mother of one of them was a slave, while the mother of the other one had always been free.
translation_verse: Ɛɛpani si bebinyime tiaʋa Abraham apɔ, tole ɔdzɛɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye apɔ yɛ, lɛ ɔʋla ɛ, ɔdzɛɛ gi agbanɔ ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu i, yɛ apɔ yɛ.

english_verse: The son of the slave woman was born in the usual way. But the son of the free woman was born because of God's promise.
translation_verse: Yebinyime gi ɔdzɛɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye apɔ akí yɛ ɛ, bɛpɔ yɛ ní kiliɛ gi banima bizɛɛpɔ̀ onu u, pɔ̀ yebinyime gi ɔdzɛɛ ɛ, gi agbanɔ ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu apɔ aki yɛ ɛ, bɛpɔ yɛ ní Aya yɛ kakaplɛa onu.

english_verse: All of this has another meaning as well. Each of the two women stands for one of the agreements God made with his people. Hagar, the slave woman, stands for the agreement that was made at Mount Sinai. Everyone born into her family is a slave.
translation_verse: Bíanu eboa la ya ʋa ese gani abee me; badzɛ tiaʋa ya ʋa ɛ, balɛɛ kí banyánya tiaʋa. Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi apɔ yɛ bebi ba ní kunɔdzi yo me e, yɛ onu Hagar, lɛ ɔlɛɛ kí kunyánya uliɔ klɔ gi bɛbitɛ ní Sinai Lito le aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Hagar also stands for Mount Sinai in Arabia and for the present city of Jerusalem. She and her children are slaves.
translation_verse: Hagar, gi ɔlɛɛ kí Sinai Lito le ní Arabia ɛ, yɛ onu abee kí omono Yerusalem ɔma ʋidi no gi lɔ mɛ banima pɔ́ balɛ ni kunɔdzi me e.

english_verse: But our mother is the city of Jerusalem in heaven above, and she isn't a slave.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ lifunu ne Yerusalem yɛ kɔ ɛ, yɛ ɔlɛɛ ɔki ɔdzɛɛ gi bɛgbanɔ ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu u, lɛ yɛ onu blɔ one e.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about her, “You have never had children, but now you can be glad. You have never given birth, but now you can shout. Once you had no children, but now you will have more children than a woman who has been married for a long time.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani si, “Mɔ isami, wɔ ɔdzɛ gi bebi báma ní wɔ awla a! wɔ́ gi woʋípɔ̀ oʋídi i, kpɛ ɔdzi ni ole gagla, wɔ gi woʋínu kuyeʋiyeʋi ní kubipɔ mɛ oʋidi i; lɛ ese linu si ɔdzɛ liɛ gi yɛ tete ɔlɛ ɛ, yɛ bebi ba bɛsrɛ sani ɔdzɛ liɛ gi odi ní kenyime pe me e.”

english_verse: My friends, you were born because of this promise, just as Isaac was.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, mlɔ mɛ benemi ba, ŋwisi Isak kinɛ ɛ, mledzi kakaplɛa ka bebi.

english_verse: But the child who was born in the natural way made trouble for the child who was born because of the Spirit. The same thing is happening today.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, obi ye gi bɛpɔ ní kelea mɛ kigyɔgyɔɛ onu i, aayɔ kedu ní obi liɛ klɔ gi bɛpɔ gani Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ su u; lɛ nite lilɛ abla a.

english_verse: The Scriptures say, “Get rid of the slave woman and her son! He won't be given anything. The son of the free woman will receive everything.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kitɛ Aya yɛ liboe le lipani i? Lipani si, “Kpɛ ɔdzɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye ni Yebi ye kude tɔ basɛ; lɛ ese linu si ɔdzɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye Yebi ye e, ɔ́tramɔ ɔŋakɔ ní yɛka bidɔ mɛ mɛ̀ ŋwisi ɔdzɛɛ gi bɛgbanɔ ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu u Yebi ye kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: My friends, we are children of the free woman and not of the slave.
translation_verse: Loso, mɛ benemi ba, kúnu ɔdzɛ ɔnɔdzidzi ye yɛ Yebi i, pɔ̀ kunu ɔdzɛɛ gi bɛgbanɔ ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu yɛ bebi i.

english_verse: Christ has set us free! This means we are really free. Now hold on to your freedom and don't ever become slaves of the Law again.
