english_verse: We wish each of you would always be eager to show how strong and lasting your hope really is.
translation_verse: Linu blɔ kupɛ́pɛ ʋidi si mlɔ mɛ ɔkakaa okeni odi nì litɔplɛplɛkese zɛɛkɔna ni lɛ ɔgù kɔ tɔ mlaamɔ kidiliɛ gi kɛ kudeonu mleedi i.

english_verse: Then you would never be lazy. You would be following the example of those who had faith and were patient until God kept his promise to them. God's Promise Is Sure
translation_verse: Kúpɛ si mledzi basranɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mlakpasi banama gi bɛziŋa a, xe bɛplɛ litɔ kese e, tɔ biazɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kiaba ka mɛ

english_verse: “I, the Lord, will bless you with many descendants!”
translation_verse: lɛ apani si, “Maakrira wɔ kikakaa elue, lɛ maaki wɔ besoebi glegle.”

english_verse: Then after Abraham had been very patient, he was given what God had promised.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi Abraham aplɛ litɔ kese e, xe epe Aya lipoe zizi i, Aya yɛ kakaplɛa kɛka yɛ awla.

english_verse: When anyone wants to settle an argument, they make a vow by using the name of someone or something greater than themselves.
translation_verse: Xegi banima bɛka kitamu i, bizɛɛka kɛ ni linyi nɛ gi limu isani ba lini nɛ ɛ, kɔ kuboehe kakaa kwitsa ɛ.

english_verse: So when God wanted to prove for certain his promise to his people could not be broken, he made a vow.
translation_verse: Lɛ ki banama gi biazɛ Aya yɛ kakaplɛa ɛ, Aya apɛ si yidra lɛ mɛ si yɛ ɔ́trakyi yɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa liwewe e, lɛ loso aka kitamu yɛ apɔni yɛ kakaplɛ a.

english_verse: God cannot tell lies! And so his promises and vows are two things that can never be changed. We have run to God for safety. Now his promises should greatly encourage us to take hold of the hope that is right in front of us.
translation_verse: Aya abitɛ eboe taʋa la ya, gi atrátani kukyi i, lɛ ese linu si Aya ɔ́tratani asia kusɛ ɛ. Lɛ loso blɔ banɔaya gi kwise trɛ ní yɛ kɔ ɔ, si kuvu kudeonudidi uliɔ gi bɛkɔ ɛplɛ ní blɔ aŋu mɛ awla ɛ, liakpɛ ɔna ní blɔ lipo.

english_verse: This hope is like a firm and steady anchor for our souls. In fact, hope reaches behind the curtain and into the most holy place.
translation_verse: Kudeonudidi uliɔ ya kulɛ ní blɔ awla gi kunu ŋwisi ɔxɔ ki blɔ agbã́ na a. Kulɛ mumɔ lɛ kulɛ gagla gi kuzɛɛtani lisɔligba lɛ mɛ kusaa mɛ kuga a, gi kwiatiku ní ɔdradra klɔ ke e.

english_verse: Jesus has gone there ahead of us, and he is our high priest forever, just like Melchizedek.
translation_verse: Niklɔ gi Yesu eku blɔ ɔtɔ atrɛ ní blɔ onu u, gi yɛ edzì kusɔliɔ Ɔka ʋidi ye ní Melxizedek yɛ ɔga nɔ onu kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_verse: Melchizedek was both king of Salem and priest of God Most High. He was the one who went out and gave Abraham his blessing, when Abraham returned from killing the kings.
translation_verse: Melxizedek liɛ ɛ, yɛ eze Salem ɔma nɔ okusi ye ani Aya gi emu asani ɔnɔ kakaa ɛ, yɛ kusɔliɔ Ɔkaɛ, yɛ azɛɛtsani Abraham lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ gi edzì adɔ ni kigũ yɛ ɛ, agi azɛɛŋa bekusi ba aʋa a, lɛ akrira yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then Abraham gave him a tenth of everything he had. The meaning of the name Melchizedek is “King of Justice.” But since Salem means “peace,” he is also “King of Peace.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Abraham akɔ bidɔ bɛ ya petee gi amɔ adɔ ni ní kigũ yɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa aki yɛ. Melxizedek yɛ linyi ne ese ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, linu si “Okusi gi ɔlɛ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa a”, Lɛ gi onu okusi ni Salɛm ɔma nɔ aʋa loso e, yɛ linyi ne ese kibubu dzedze kinu si “Kusuyɔ Okusi”.

english_verse: We are not told he had a father or mother or ancestors or beginning or end. He is like the Son of God and will be a priest forever.
translation_verse: Lɛ báŋwlimi Melxizedek yɛka loo yene, loo yɛkatsitsi ɔtɔtɔ liboe aplɛ ɛ, lɛ kididɔ kíma ní yɛ kupɔpɔ loo kutse su u. Ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi Aya Yebi gi onu yɛ kusɔliɔ ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Notice how great Melchizedek was! Our famous ancestor Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had taken from his enemies.
translation_verse: Lɛ mledi ɔnɔ ʋidi liɛ elu gi Melxizedek eze e, blɔ ɔkatsitsi ye Abraham akɔ bidɔɛ mɛ gi amɔ adɔ ni kigũ yɛ ɛ, bɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa akɔ aki yɛ.

english_verse: The Law teaches that even Abraham's descendants must give a tenth of what they possess. And they are to give this to their own relatives, who are the descendants of Levi and are priests.
translation_verse: Lɛ Lewi lipo le me bebia ba gi benu kusɔliɔ baka ba ɛ, ɔsã nɔ ɛɛki ba kude si bazɛ bidɔ mɔmɔ ʋɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa dɔni banima awla gi benu ba benemi i. Sro si ba kyɛ benu Abraham yɛ lipo me bebi xe e.

english_verse: Although Melchizedek wasn't a descendant of Levi, Abraham gave him a tenth of what he had. Then Melchizedek blessed Abraham, who had been given God's promise.
translation_verse: Melxizedek ónu Levi yɛ lipo me obi i, pɔ̀ azɛ Abraham yɛ bidɔ mɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa a, lɛ akrira Abraham i, yɛ ɔniliɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ akɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Everyone agrees a person who gives a blessing is greater than the one who receives the blessing.
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛ lidra si ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛkrira ɛ, ekeme asani ɔniliɛ gi bɛɛkrira ɛ.

english_verse: Priests are given a tenth of what people earn. But all priests die, except Melchizedek, and the Scriptures teach that he is alive.
translation_verse: Ní kapɛ tiele me e, bidɔ mɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa ɛ, banama gi bizɛɛzi bɛ fɛ ɛ, betse. Pɔ̀ ní Melxizedek kapɛa mɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ kɔtsinɔ ɛ, yɛ azɛ bɛ.

english_verse: Levi's descendants are now the ones who receive a tenth from people. We could even say that when Abraham gave Melchizedek a tenth, Levi also gave him a tenth.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso biatani kupani si; gi Abraham atsa bidɔ mɛ kapɛ liɔfɔa ɛ, Lewi gi yɛ izɛɛzi kapɛ liɔfɔa ɛ, yɛ fɛ atsa bɛ gani Abraham aʋa a.

english_verse: This is because Levi was born later into the family of Abraham, who gave a tenth to Melchizedek.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si gi bápɔ Lewi kyɛ ɛ, eze ní Abraham yɛ lipo le me lipoe lɛ gi Abraham ni Melxizedek bɛtsa ɛ.

english_verse: Even though the Law of Moses says the priests must be descendants of Levi, those priests cannot make anyone perfect. So there needs to be a priest like Melchizedek, rather than one from the priestly family of Aaron.
translation_verse: Lewi yɛ lipo me bebi ba ba kusumu yo kuka ŋaŋaa kɔ onu bɛki ɔsã nɔ. Lɛ xegi nya Lewianɛ ma ba kusumu yo kuka ŋaŋaa kwina ɛ, nya lítrazia si kusumu yo ɔka dzedze ɔŋwi i, gi onu kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ní Melxizedek yɛ ɔga nɔ onu u, lɛ ani Aron yɛ lipo me bebi ba ɔni nɔ onu u.

english_verse: And when the rules for selecting a priest are changed, the Law must also be changed.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi bɛtsrɛ kusumu yo kuka ŋaŋaa ɛ, kɔ lilɛ si batsrɛ ɔsã nɔ fɛ.

english_verse: The person we are talking about is our Lord, who came from a tribe that had never had anyone to serve as a priest at the altar.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ su bɛpani liboe lɛ ya ɛ, ádɔ ní limade dzedze me, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́dɔ limade lɛ ya mɛ ɔ́ŋa kusumu yo Ɔka ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa oʋídi i.

english_verse: All of this becomes clearer, when someone who is like Melchizedek is appointed to be a priest.
translation_verse: Lɛ liboe lɛ gi kwipani i, lɛ mɛ liadra ɛsanɔ; kusumu yo Ɔka dzedze aŋwi gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi Melxizedek kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: That person wasn't appointed because of his ancestors, but because his life can never end.
translation_verse: Onu kusumu yo Ɔka aní gani ɔsã ní ɔnɔ kigyɔgyɔɛ mɛ onu, pɔ̀ gani Ligbã́ nɛ kigagla a, gi bátratani kuzizi me e.

english_verse: The Scriptures say about him, “You are a priest forever, just like Melchizedek.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si bɛpani lɛ wee si, “Wonu kusumu yo Ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ní Melxizedek ɔga nɔ onu.”

english_verse: In this way a weak and useless command was put aside,
translation_verse: Lɛ loso bɛyurɔ ɔsã koko lo eplɛ kapɛ agi kigagla kíma ní lɔ su u, xe oʋíbu libiitɔtɔ loso o.

english_verse: because the Law cannot make anything perfect. At the same time, we are given a much better hope, and it can bring us close to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ɔsã nɔ ɔ́tani kididɔ kubitɛ si kina a, pɔ̀ abla ɛ, bɛki blɔ kudeonudidi gi kwipɛ sanɔ ɔ, gi gani kɔ aʋa ɛ, kwitipɔni Aya.

english_verse: God himself made a promise when this priest was appointed. But he did not make a promise like this when the other priests were appointed. The promise he made is, “I, the Lord, promise that you will be a priest forever! And I will never change my mind!”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Yesu kɔ edzì kusumu yo Ɔka gi Aya aka kitamu si, “Aya aka aplɛ lɛ ɔ́trakyi yɛ begyodi ʋa a: ‘Wonu kusumu yo Ɔka kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.’ ”

english_verse: This means that Jesus guarantees us a better agreement with God.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ kitamu kiliɛ ya loso e, Yesu edzì ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛɛ kede ɔki blɔ ní kunyanya a, gi kwipɛ sanɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: There have been a lot of other priests, and all of them have died.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, Kusumu yo baka báklɔ ba bɛsrɛ lɛ ese linu si betse lɛ batrátani ba axwɛ na aʋa kutrɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But Jesus will never die, and so he will be a priest forever!
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Yesu kɔ ɔlɛ ligbã́ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ, yɛ kusumu yo ɔka kuzeze e, kútratrɛ ní ɔnɔ dzedze ɔtɔtɔ kɔ ɔ.

english_verse: He is forever able to save the people he leads to God, because he always lives to speak to God for them.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso aatani kuzɛ lipoe kakaa ɔki banama gi bɛga yɛ aʋa a, xe bɛba Aya klɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si ɔlɛ ligbã lipoe kakaa gi aazi ba onu ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Jesus is the high priest we need. He is holy and innocent and faultless, and not at all like us sinners. Jesus is honored above all beings in heaven,
translation_verse: Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ŋwisi Yesu i, yɛ aatani blɔ baziazia ʋa aʋa kubitɛ ɔki blɔ. Yɛ su ɛɛdra, kesetataa tɔtɔ káma ní yɛ su u, lɛ eboe zimizi áma ní yɛ su u; bebu ye lidze ní banɔ zimizi ba su, lɛ bemuni ye gi emu asani lifunu ne e.

english_verse: and he is better than any other high priest. Jesus doesn't need to offer sacrifices each day for his own sins and then for the sins of the people. He offered a sacrifice once for all, when he gave himself.
translation_verse: Ɔ́ma ŋwisi kusumu yo baka ʋidi dzedze ba a, gi liziã si bapɔʋi bidɔ aki Aya liwe kakaa ki ba tutɔ ba eboe zimizi na kpɛ ɔtɔ ɔ, xe ableke biapɔʋi bidɔ ɔki Aya ni banɔ dzedze ba onu u. Aki yɛ bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi ʋɛ tule ní banima petee litukpo gi akɔ isu ya akɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The Law appoints priests who have weaknesses. But God's promise, which came later than the Law, appoints his Son. And he is the perfect high priest forever.
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ izɛɛkɔ banama gi bána ɛ, du si beze kusumu yo baka a. Pɔ̀ Aya yɛ kakaplɛa a, gi aka aplɛ ni kitamu i, xe kiba ní ɔsã nɔ kede e, yɛ akya Yebi ye gi abitɛ si obloni yɛ axwɛ na a kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_verse: What I mean is we have a high priest who sits at the right side of God's great throne in heaven.
translation_verse: Liboe lɛ gi lɛ su kwipani i, lɛ linu si: kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi liɛ gi ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla a, eze ní Lifunu ne kukusi yo kɔ likusigbo le kaŋawla mɛ.

english_verse: He also serves as the priest in the most holy place inside the real tent there in heaven. This tent of worship was set up by the Lord, not by humans.
translation_verse: Eesumu ní Ɔdradra klɔ ní lisumugba lɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ gi Aya awa mɛ ɛ, gi aní banɔ gbagbe awla bɛkɔ ɛwa lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If he were here on earth, he would not be a priest at all, because here the Law appoints other priests to offer sacrifices.
translation_verse: Xegi kesea lia ya aʋa ɔlɛ ɛ, nya ɔ́traze kusumu yo Ɔka koŋ, lɛ ese linu si kusumu yo baka ba balɛ gi bizɛɛki bidɔ ní ɔsã nɔ onu u.

english_verse: But the tent where they serve is just a copy and a shadow of the real one in heaven. Before Moses made the tent, he was told, “Be sure to make it exactly like the pattern you were shown on the mountain!”
translation_verse: Axwɛ ana gi bizɛɛwa ní Ɔdradra klɔ ɔ, ŋwisi kusumu yo baka kinɛ ɛ, enu kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní lifunu ne e, kɛ liŋwitsɛ nì kɛ kuyeo ko. Mose, lipoe lɛ gi atráwa lisɔligba lɛ ɛ, Aya aki yɛ ɔsãa nɔ si, “Di le aʋa si wɔbitɛ kidɔ kakaa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛbasɛ wɔ ní lito le aʋa kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: If the first agreement with God had been all right, there would not have been any need for another one.
translation_verse: Atɛsi kesetata káma ní kunyanya tɔpu yɔ su i, nya kunyanya ʋla kutrázia tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But the Lord found fault with it and said, “I tell you the time will come, when I will make a new agreement with the people of Israel and the people of Judah.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Aya amɔ kesetata ní yɛ banima su gi apani si i, “Me Aya mɛɛpani si: Lipoe le litrába, gi maabitɛ kunyanya vuvu ni Israɛl kepea ani Yuda kepea a.

english_verse: It won't be like the agreement that I made with their ancestors, when I took them by the hand and led them out of Egypt. They broke their agreement with me, and I stopped caring about them!
translation_verse: Kútraze ŋwisi kunyanyaa liɔ gi mabitɛ ni ba bakatsi ba a, liwe lɛ gi mevu ba ɔwla lɔ ɔ, xe magbani ba adɔnɔ ní Egipte e. Béɖi anukwarɛɛ aki kunyanyaa liɔ gi mabitɛ ni ba a, lɛ loso mekyi ní ba su, nite Ɔkaɛ apani.

english_verse: “But now I tell the people of Israel this is my new agreement: ‘The time will come when I, the Lord, will write my laws on their minds and hearts. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso, kunyanyaa liɔ ya gi mátrabitɛ ni Israelbanɛ ma ni ewe a la gi ɛtrába mɛ ɛ, nite Ɔkaɛ apani. Maakpɛ mɛ isã nɛ ní ba begyodi ʋa mɛ lɛ maaŋwlimi nɛ ɔkpɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ. Maaze ba Aya, lɛ biaze mɛ banɔ.

english_verse: Not one of them will have to teach another to know me, their Lord.’ “All of them will know me, no matter who they are.
translation_verse: Lítrazia si bamɛ ɔtɔtɔ ɔbasɛ yɛ ɔhanuglɔ bidɔ ɔ, loo ɔnɔnɔ ɔsi yenemi si, ‘Te Ɔkaɛ,’ lɛ ese linu si ba petee biate me, dɔni ɔgɔgɔɛ aʋa zɛɛkɔna ni ɔnɔ ʋidi ye aʋa ɛ,

english_verse: I will treat them with kindness, even though they are wicked. I will forget their sins.”
translation_verse: Maakɔ ba eboe zimizi na kɔkyɛ ba lɛ mátraplɛ aŋu ní ba kesetata atɔtɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: When the Lord talks about a new agreement, he means that the first one is out of date. And anything that is old and useless will soon disappear.
translation_verse: Gi apani ní kunyanya vuvu yɔ su i, Aya abitɛ lɛ si kunyanya tɔpu yɔ kwidzi kukoko, lɛ kidiliɛ gi kitsi i, xe kikpã ɛ, kialila kpekpemii.

english_verse: The first promise included rules for worship and a tent for worship here on earth.
translation_verse: Kunyanya tɔpu yɔ ɛ, kɔ isã nɛ ki kusumu yo kwize, lɛ Ɔdradra kɔ kyɛ eze gi bɛbitɛ ní kesea ya aʋa a.

english_verse: The first part of the tent was called the holy place, and a lampstand, a table, and the sacred loaves of bread were kept there.
translation_verse: Bɛwa Aya yɛ lisumugba lɛ gi linu kusa akpandɔ ɔ. Bɛlɛ lisumugba lɛ ní kupɛ tuʋa mɛ. Ní kapɛ tɔpu ya gi bɛɛxwa si Ɔdradra klɔ mɛ ɛ, kani ose, ani ɔkplɔ gi lɔ aʋa bɛzɛɛtrɔ aklã na a, gi bɛdra la su iiki Aya ɛ.

english_verse: Behind the curtain was the most holy place.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní ligba lɛ mɛ kutsasa ʋlaa kede e, níklɔ bizɛɛxwa si Ɔdradra klɔ petee.

english_verse: The gold altar for burning incense was in this holy place. The gold-covered sacred chest was also there, and inside it were three things. First, there was a gold jar filled with manna. Then there was Aaron's walking stick that sprouted. Finally, there were the flat stones with the Ten Commandments written on them.
translation_verse: Ní niklɔ ɛ, bedu sika yɛ ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ gi lɔ aʋa bizɛɛkpɛ lifi ye kifu yɛ, ani sika yɛ kunyanya adaka yɛ, gi yɛ me sika kefukpo a, gi kamɛ bɛkpɛ mana yɛ keze e. Aron yɛ ose vuga vuga lɔ gi ɛɛta awlakpa la ɛ, kɔpɔni akla na gi la aʋa bɛŋwlimi Mose ɔsã nɔ ɛtrɔ ɛ, la kyɛ akpasi ní adaka yɛ mɛ.

english_verse: On top of the chest were the glorious creatures with wings opened out above the place of mercy. Now isn't the time to go into detail about these things.
translation_verse: Ní adaka yɛ aʋa ɛ, Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba gi bizɛxwa si Kerubim ba a, gi bɛɛbasɛ si Aya ɔlɛ ní niklɔ ɛ balɛ. Ba siwlasasa sɛ ɛ, siwe niklɔ gi bizɛɛkɔ eboe zimizi na ɛkyɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ kutrátani eboa la petee me kutrɛ kpaŋu abla a.

english_verse: But this is how everything was when the priests went each day into the first part of the tent to do their duties.
translation_verse: Xegi bedu bidɔ mɛ nite pɔ́ ɛ, Kusumu yo baka ba bizeetiku ní lisumugba lɛ kapɛ tɔpu ya mɛ liwe kakaa lɛ bizɛwa ba axwɛ na.

english_verse: However, only the high priest could go into the second part of the tent, and he went in only once a year. Each time he carried blood to offer for his sins and for any sins that the people had committed without meaning to.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye ko izeetiku ní lisumugba lɛ kimɛ ɛmɛ, tule ní ligɔ lɛ mɛ. Izeevu ikaɛ lɛ gi izɛɛki Aya ní yɛ ɔtutɔ ni banɔ dzedze ba eboe zimizi na gi béte si bɛɛbitɛ ɛ onu ní awla.

english_verse: All of this is the Holy Spirit's way of saying no one could enter the most holy place while the tent was still the place of worship.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ ebu le basɛ tratratra dɔni la ya mɛ si gi batrɔtrɔ ba ya balɛ onu i, baʋíbu kudeo onu ɛki niklɔ gi Ɛsadra petee ɛki ɔnɔnɔ gi lisumugba tɔpu nɛ lidzi lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: These rules are merely about such things as eating and drinking and ceremonies for washing ourselves. And rules about physical things will last only until the time comes to change them for something better.
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si binu kiŋaŋa ni kiŋwɛŋwɛ ni kusuplo egoʋi ede ede ko. Bɛ ya petee binu aŋu mɛ isã ko gi ilɛ lɛ ozekɔ ɔ, kɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi Aya aamani yɛ kutrɔtrɔ vuvu yɔ ɔ. Yesu yɛ ika lɛ iplo blɔ eboe zimizi na ɛzanɔ

english_verse: Christ came as the high priest of the good things that are now here. He also went into a much better tent that wasn't made by humans and that doesn't belong to this world.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Kristo aba ŋwisi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye e, gi yɛ aki bidɔ gbã ɛ mɛ ɛ, gi bilɛ niya kooko e, aga lisɔligba ɛlɛ gi liŋa kedzi sanɔ ɔ, xe lina ɛsanɔ mɛ ɛ. Lisumugba ɛlɛ ya ɛ, aní banɔ awla bɛkɔ ɛwa lɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si línu kesea aʋa ya bidɔ bitɛbitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then Christ went once for all into the most holy place and freed us from sin forever. He did this by offering his own blood instead of the blood of goats and bulls.
translation_verse: Gi eti eku ní Ɔdradra klɔ petee tule uliɔ klɔ ʋa ɛ, ɔ́tsini bamu ni egumenyime ika a, boŋu i, akɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ika lɛ, lɛ azɛ kubúbú gi kútrablo ɛki blɔ ɛ.

english_verse: According to the Law of Moses, those people who become unclean are not fit to worship God. Yet they will be considered clean, if they are sprinkled with the blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a sacrificed calf.
translation_verse: Bamu ʋa ni egumenyime na ika lɛ ani egumedzɛ la, la kusuʋɔtɔɔ bizeehle ʋe ɛbali ní banama gi basu ɔ́dra ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu i, aʋa kɔ basu ɛdra ní ɔkɔ lɔ onu i.

english_verse: But Christ was sinless, and he offered himself as an eternal and spiritual sacrifice to God. This is why his blood is much more powerful and makes our consciences clear. Now we can serve the living God and no longer do things that lead to death.
translation_verse: Xegi lilɛ ní lɛ mɛ nite e, ege kɛ kiakɔ si Kristo yɛ ika lɛ, gi akɔ isu aki Aya a, ŋwisi bidɔ pɔʋi pɔʋi gi bina kinɛ ɛ, gani Ɔfuɛfuɛ moblo lo me e, ítraplo blɔ begyodi ʋa su dɔni eboa la gi izɛɛmani kutse me e, gi la aaki si kwiawa axwɛ na ki Aya gbagbe e?

english_verse: In fact, making an agreement of this kind is like writing a will. This is because the one who makes the will must die before it is of any use.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ní kupɛpɛɔ liɔ gi bizɛŋwlimi plɛ onu i, liziã si batrɔ likla lɛ aʋa si ɔniliɛ gi aŋwlimi kɔ aplɛ ɛ, etse xe e.

english_verse: In other words, a will doesn't go into effect as long as the one who made it is still alive.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si gi ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, xe aŋwlimi kɔ akpɛ ɛ, kigaglaɛ tɔtɔ kíma yɛ kupɛpɛɔ awla a, gakɔ kpɛ ni lipoe lɛ gi etse e, xe ableke kwiawa lixwɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Moses told the people all the Law said they must do. Then he used red wool and a hyssop plant to sprinkle the people and the book of the Law with the blood of bulls and goats and with water.
translation_verse: Gi Mose apani ní isã nɛ su ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛkpɛ lɛ ɛki banima petee kede e, akɔ egume na, ni bamu ʋa ika lɛ gi bɛpisa ni kuni yo o, lɛ efuflu ʋe ní ɔsã nɔ keplekpaa ni banima petee aʋa ni hisope yɛ awlakpa la gi bɛnya ni sigapi kyikyi sɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He told the people, “With this blood God makes his agreement with you.”
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si, “Bɛ ya binu ika lɛ gi ibasɛ kunyanya liɔ gi kɔ ɔsã Aya akpɛ aki mlɔ si mlevu u.”

english_verse: Moses also sprinkled blood on the tent and on everything else used in worship.
translation_verse: Niteme ke Mose akɔ ika lɛ ehle ní Aya yɛ lisumugba lɛ ni bisumudɔ kakaa aʋa.

english_verse: The Law says that almost everything must be sprinkled with blood, and no sins can be forgiven unless blood is offered. Christ's Great Sacrifice
translation_verse: Lɛ ní anukwarɛɛ mɛ ní ɔsã nɔ onu i, kidɔ kakaa kloe kiziã si beplo kɛ su ni ika, lɛ ese linu si kukɔkyɛ kúma xegi ika íbali kese e. Yesu Yɛ isu kukɔkyɛ kuzebu eboe zimizi na zanɔ

english_verse: These things are only copies of what is in heaven, and so they had to be made holy by these ceremonies. But the real things in heaven must be made holy by something better.
translation_verse: Liziã si beplo bidɔ bɛ ya gi bilɛ ŋwisi lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ su gani bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi bɛ ya onu u, pɔ̀ lifunu ne bidɔ mɛ bɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ, biziã bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi bipɛ sani bɛ ya ɛ.

english_verse: This is why Christ did not go into a tent made by humans and was only a copy of the real one. Instead, he went into heaven and is now there with God to help us.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Kristo óti iku ní Ɔdradra kɔ gi bɛkɔ awla la ɛwa a, gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi lifunu ne ɔni nɔ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɔ. Eti eku ní lifunu ne ɔtutɔ lɔ, niklɔ gi ɔlɛ abla ní Aya aŋu mɛ ní blɔ onu u.

english_verse: Christ did not have to offer himself many times. He wasn't like a high priest who goes into the most holy place each year to offer the blood of an animal.
translation_verse: Kristo óti iku si yikɔ isu ikɔ tasɛ tasɛ ŋwisi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye gi izeetiku ní Ɔdradra klɔ tule ní ligɔ kakaa mɛ ɛ, ni ika gi ínu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ inɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If he had offered himself every year, he would have suffered many times since the creation of the world. But instead, near the end of time he offered himself once and for all, so he could be a sacrifice that does away with sin.
translation_verse: Xegi nite lilɛ ɛ, nya lilɛ ki yɛ si ɔzɛmɔ ɔziã nɔ tasɛ tasɛ dɔni kelea okpese klɔ kee; pɔ̀ abla ní lipoe lɛ gi kelea kɛtrɛ ní ka ɔgù kɔ ɛ, aba tule si tɔ yɛ aabu eboe zimizi na zanɔ gani yɛ isu kukɔkyɛ mɛ.

english_verse: So Christ died only once to take away the sins of many people. But when he comes again, it will not be to take away sin. He will come to save everyone who is waiting for him.
translation_verse: niteme ke Kristo fɛ, akɔ isu akyɛ tule tɔ yibu banɔ glegle ba eboe zimizi na ɛzanɔ. Aadzi ba kuʋla aní si tɔ yimɔ kini kpɛ eboe zimizi na tɔ ɔ, boŋu i, si tɔ yibu banama gi beepe ye e.

english_verse: The Law of Moses is like a shadow of the good things to come. This shadow isn't the good things themselves, because it cannot free people from sin by the sacrifices offered year after year.
translation_verse: Ɔsã nɔ ɛ, eboe gbã ana gi ɛtrába ɛ, la kuyeo ko ɛbasɛ, aní eboe la kizeze me ɔtutɔ lɔ. Ní ɔsã lɔ klɔ ese e, bizɛɛkɔ bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi bɛ klɔ edzini kɔ ligɔ kakaa. Pɔ̀ bítratani banama gi beti apɔnɔ tɔ beesumu Aya ɛ, basu kudra xe biana a.

english_verse: If there were worshipers who already have their sins washed away and their consciences made clear, there would not be any need to go on offering sacrifices.
translation_verse: Xegi bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ʋɛ klɔ biatani banama gi beesumu Aya su kudra sugba ɛ, nya bɛmɔni bidɔ mɛ kupɔʋi, lɛ ese linu si banama gi ba beesumu Aya ɛ, basu ɛdra petee lɛ ba ina nɛ ítrabu ba lipɔ ɔ, kɔkpɛ ba eboe zimizi na loso.

english_verse: But the blood of bulls and goats cannot take away sins. It only reminds people of their sins from one year to the next.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si egume na ni bamu ʋa ika lɛ ɛ, ítratani eboe zimizi na kubu zanɔ liwewe.

english_verse: When Christ came into the world, he said to God, “Sacrifices and offerings are not what you want, but you have given me my body.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya loso gi Kristo aaba kelea mɛ ɛ, asi Aya si, “Wɔ́ziã bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ni bidɔkɔkɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ wɔki mɛ kisugya.

english_verse: No, you are not pleased with animal sacrifices and offerings for sin.”
translation_verse: Baga zizi gi bɛpɔʋi ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa ni bidɔ kɔkɔ gi biabu eboe zimizi zanɔ kyɛ ɛ, bíku wɔ aŋu u.

english_verse: Then Christ said, “And so, my God, I have come to do what you want, as the Scriptures say.”
translation_verse: Lɛ mapani si, ‘Imɔ mɛ, bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní keplekpaa mɛ ní mɛ su si Oo Aya, maba si mibitɛ ní wɔ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa a.’ ”

english_verse: The Law teaches that offerings and sacrifices must be made because of sin. But why did Christ mention these things and say that God did not want them?
translation_verse: Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, apani si “Bidɔkɔkɔ ni bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi loo baga gi bɛpɔʋi ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa a, loo bidɔkɔkɔ ki eboe zimizi bíku wɔ aŋu lɛ wɔ́pɛ ʋɛ ɛ.” Apani nite gi nya ɔsã nɔ ɛki si bazaaki bidɔ bɛ ya xe e.

english_verse: Well, it was to do away with offerings and sacrifices and to replace them. This is what he meant by saying to God, “I have come to do what you want.”
translation_verse: Lɛ apani si, “Imɔ mɛ maba si mibitɛ ní wɔ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa” Lɛ loso Aya ebu kunyanya tɔpu yɔ azanɔ lɛ akɔ kunyanya ʋlaa akpɛ ní kɔ ozeklɔ ɛ.

english_verse: The priests do their work each day, and they keep on offering sacrifices that can never take away sins.
translation_verse: Kusɔliɔ ɔka kakaa izɛɛwa yɛ axwɛ na liwe kakaa, lɛ izɛɛkɔ bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi ʋɛ lide tilee lɛ klɔ lipoe kakaa pɔ̀ bidɔpɔʋipɔʋii ʋɛ klɔ bítratani eboe zimizi na kubu zanɔ liwewe e.

english_verse: But Christ offered himself as a sacrifice that is good forever. Now he is sitting at God's right side,
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ gi Kristo aki bidɔ mɛ tule ki eboe zimizi na ɛ, bidɔkɔkɔɛ ʋɛ klɔ biwà axwɛ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ, lɛ eze ní Aya yɛ kaŋaa mɛ.

english_verse: and he will stay there until his enemies are put under his power.
translation_verse: Lɛ dɔni lipoe lɛ klɔ aʋa kɔtsinɔ ɛ, eepe Aya gi yɛ aakɔ̀ yɛ ahwafianɛ ma kpɛ yɛ kukliseo e.

english_verse: By his one sacrifice he has forever set free from sin the people he brings to God.
translation_verse: Nì bidɔkɔkɔ tule e, aki si banama su gi iidra dɔni ba eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ, bɛna kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_verse: The Holy Spirit also speaks of this by telling us that the Lord said,
translation_verse: Lɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ Dradra lɔ kyɛ ɛɛŋa katsalia ɛki blɔ. Ní ɔtɔpu kɔ ɛ, ɛpani si,

english_verse: “When the time comes, I will make an agreement with them. I will write my laws on their minds and hearts.
translation_verse: “Lɛ ya linu kunyanya liɔ gi maabitɛ nì ba a, ní ewe ala ya gi ɛtrába mɛ ɛ, nite Ɔkaɛ apani, Maakpɛ mɛ isã nɛ ní ba ina nɛ mɛ, lɛ maaŋwlimi nɛ kpɛ ní ba begyodi ʋa mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Then I will forget about their sins and no longer remember their evil deeds.”
translation_verse: Lɛ apani dzɛ si, “Mátraplɛ aŋu liwewe ní ba eboe zimizi na ni ba ɔsã nɔ ese kutataa tɔtɔ aʋa tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: When sins are forgiven, there is no more need to offer sacrifices. Encouragement and Warning
translation_verse: Lɛ loso xegi bɛkɔ kiboebitɛ liɛ ya ɛkyɛ bá ɛ, bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi biabu eboe zimizi zanɔ ɛ, bíziã tɔ ɔ. Mlaki tɔ kukpɛ ikù nɛ

english_verse: My friends, the blood of Jesus gives us courage to enter the most holy place
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mɛ benemi ba a, agi kudeonu kulɛ si kwiatiku ní Ɔdradra klɔ gani Yesu kutseo me e,

english_verse: by a new way that leads to life! And this way takes us through the curtain that is Christ himself.
translation_verse: yɛ adra kude vuvu gi kwiaki blɔ ligbã́ a, gani kusa uliɔ gi kwitsa ligba lɛ mɛ ɛ, gi kunu yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kisugyaɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: We have a great high priest who is in charge of God's house.
translation_verse: Gi kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla a, gi yɛ odi ní Aya yɛ lisɔligba lɛ onu i,

english_verse: So let's come near God with pure hearts and a confidence that comes from having faith. Let's keep our hearts pure, our consciences free from evil, and our bodies washed with clean water.
translation_verse: mlaki tɔ kuti pɔni Aya ni ɔna gi lɔ mɛ ɛdra ɛ, ni kuziŋa zizi nì ina gi lɛ su idra ɛ, gani Yesu yɛ ika lɛ gi ehle abali ní blɔ aʋa a, xe iiki si blɔ su ɛdra dɔni lipɔ kububu me e, nì blɔ kisugya gi eplo nì kuni i gi kɔmɛ kwidra ɛ.

english_verse: We must hold tightly to the hope we say is ours. After all, we can trust the one who made the agreement with us.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kuvu kudeonudidi uliɔ gi kɔ su kwipani ki banima a, si kulɛ ní blɔ awla gagla, lɛ ese linu si kwiatani kuka ní Aya aʋa si aabitɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa.

english_verse: We should keep on encouraging each other to be thoughtful and to do helpful things.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kupɛ bede ʋa ya onu gi kwiakpɛ kifu ní blɔ ahanuglɔ ba mɛ, tɔ balɔ ani tɔ babitɛ eboe gbã.

english_verse: Some people have given up the habit of meeting for worship, but we must not do that. We should keep on encouraging each other, especially since you know that the day of the Lord's coming is getting closer.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlakíkɔ si kuyurɔ blɔ batsátsa ʋa ŋwisi kiliɛ gi banɔ batɔ bɛɛbitɛ kinɛ ɛ, boŋu i, mlaki tɔ kukpɛ ɔna ní blɔ ahanuglɔ ba lipo sanɔ, abla gi kwimɔ si Ɔkaɛ yɛ liwe le liiti pɔnɔ ɛ.

english_verse: No sacrifices can be made for people who decide to sin after they find out about the truth.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si xegi kwitrɛ ní blɔ eboe zimizi na aʋa ɔŋwadzu i, bidɔkɔkɔɛ tɔtɔ bíma a, gi bɛ biabu eboe zimizi na zanɔ ɔ, abla gi mlɛzɛ anukwarɛɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ ɛ.

english_verse: They are God's enemies, and all they can look forward to is a terrible judgment and a furious fire.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ kidiliɛ gi kigɔ ko kinu si kuze ní lifu kusese me kí litsixwaxwa ɛnɛ ɛ, gi lɛ kudeonu kwidi i, ani kifu ʋidi iliɛ gi kitrazizi banɔ ba ya gi benu Aya yɛ ahwafianɛ ma ɛ!

english_verse: If two or more witnesses accused someone of breaking the Law of Moses, that person could be put to death.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi ɔ́dimɛ si yibitɛ ní Mose ɔsã nɔ aʋa ɛ, bizeye ye asigaga modi xegi bɛtsa litsi ki yɛ si aŋa lipɔ ɔ, xegi banɔ tiaʋa loo sani nite bɛŋa katsalia ɛki liboe le e.

english_verse: But it is much worse to dishonor God's Son and to disgrace the blood of the promise that made us holy. And it is just as bad to insult the Holy Spirit, who shows us mercy.
translation_verse: Kɔ kitɛ liaze ki ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ Aya Yebi ye kakpaa ɛ? Ɔniliɛ gi akɔ Aya yɛ kunyanya vuvu yɔ ika lɛ ŋwisi biwaʋídɔ gi bɛ bizɛɛdra yɛ su idɔní yɛ eboe zimizi na mɛ ɛ? Gi yɛ izeekpini Aya yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, gi lɔ izɛki lipomepɛpɛ nɛ ɛ? Mlegyo le edi kiliɛ gi kutuheheo liɔ ɔ, gi kwigyeni ye, kwianyani sanɔ ɛ!

english_verse: We know that God has said he will punish and take revenge. We also know that the Scriptures say the Lord will judge his people.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kwite ɔniliɛ klɔ gi apani si, “Maabitɛ ɔkpɛ lɛ onu, maadzini liʋɛ nɛ kutsa,” gi yɛ apani dzɛ si i, “Ɔkaɛ aaxwa litsi ni yɛ banima a.”

english_verse: It is a terrible thing to fall into the hands of the living God!
translation_verse: Linu lifu liboe si ɔnutɔ ɔwɔli okú ní Aya gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, yɛ iwla mɛ.

english_verse: Sometimes you were abused and mistreated in public, and at other times you shared in the sufferings of others.
translation_verse: Lipoe tɔ onu i, bekpini mlo, lɛ bɛzɛɛwa mlɔ nyɔnyɔɛ ní ɔha lɔ kusi i, lɛ ní lipoe dzedze me e, mlɛlɛ nì banɔ baklɔ gi ba bɛzɛɛwa nyɔnyɔɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You were kind to people in jail. And you gladly let your possessions be taken away, because you knew you had something better, something that would last forever.
translation_verse: Mlɔ kyɛ mlemɔ ɔziã nɔ ɛpɔni ba ŋwisi banama gi bɛkpɛ ligba lɛ ɛ, lɛ gi bɛɛzi mlɔ bidɔmɔmɔ ʋɛ petee kyɛ ɛ, mlɛzi lɛ ni isami, lɛ ese linu si mlɔ ɔtutɔ mlete si bidɔ gi bipɛ sanɔ ɛ, bilɛ ní mlɔ awla gi bitrázizi liwewe e.

english_verse: Keep on being brave! It will bring you great rewards.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso ɔna okído ní mlɔ lipo o, lɛ ese linu si biamani mlɔ libibubu ʋidi.

english_verse: Learn to be patient, so you will please God and be given what he has promised.
translation_verse: Liziã si mlaplɛ litɔ ní kese atɔ xe mlɛbitɛ Aya yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa ɛ, mlazi kidiliɛ gi kɛ aka aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: As the Scriptures say, “God is coming soon! It won't be very long.
translation_verse: Ní lipoe kpekpemi itɔ mɛ ɛ, “Ligɔ taʋɛ ko ɔniliɛ gi atrába ɛ aaba, lɛ ɔ́trawɔ kede e.

english_verse: The people God accepts will live because of their faith. But he isn't pleased with anyone who turns back.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mɛ banama gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɛ, biaze ligbã́ ní ba kuziŋaa mɛ. pɔ̀ xe bamɛ ɔtɔ ekyi kede akpɛ mɛ ɛ, isami itraku me ni ye e.”

english_verse: We are not like those people who turn back and get destroyed. We will keep on having faith until we are saved.
translation_verse: Kúma ní banɔ baklɔ gi bizekyi kede e, xe bizɛɛkyrɔ kusi i. Boŋu i, kulɛ ní banɔ baklɔ gi bɛziŋa a, xe Aya ebu i, ba kusi.

english_verse: Faith makes us sure of what we hope for and gives us proof of what we cannot see.
translation_verse: Abla ɛ, kuziŋaa kunu likla trɔtrɔ ní kidiliɛ gi kɛ kudeonu kwidi aʋa a, ani kukaka ni kidiliɛ gi kuʋímɔ ɛ aʋa.

english_verse: It was their faith that made our ancestors pleasing to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si gani kuziŋa mɛ kanaa mɛ banima bɛzi kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀.

english_verse: Because of our faith, we know that the world was made at God's command. We also know that what can be seen was made out of what cannot be seen.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ kwinu lɛ mɛ si Aya abitɛ kelea gani yɛ liboe panipani mɛ ɛ, gi lɛ loso o Aya abitɛ kidiliɛ gi kwimɔ ɛ dɔni kidiliɛ gi baʋímɔ ɛ mɛ.

english_verse: Because Abel had faith, he offered God a better sacrifice than Cain did. God was pleased with him and his gift, and even though Abel is now dead, his faith still speaks for him.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ Abel aki Aya bidɔpɔʋipɔʋi gi bipɛ sani Kain binɛ ʋɛ ɛ. Gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Aya akpɔ yɛ ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ɔ, gi Aya akpɔ yɛ bidɔkɔkɔ ʋɛ ɛ. Gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Abɛl ɛɛpani omono gi etse kyɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Enoch had faith and did not die. He pleased God, and God took him up to heaven. This is why his body was never found.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ Aya efeke Henox asɛ nɔ gi ótse e, xe bámɔ yɛ tɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si Aya efeke ye asɛ nɔ. Kpɛ ɔtɔ kí lipoe lɛ gi biafeke Henox sɛnɔ ɛ, Aya akpɔ yɛ si onu ɔnɔ gi eku Ye aŋu u.

english_verse: But without faith no one can please God. We must believe that God is real and rewards everyone who searches for him.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani Aya aŋu kuku xegi kuziŋa kúma ní lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ se linu si ɔnɔ kakaa gi aaba Aya kɔ ɛ, lilɛ si ɔniliɛ klɔ ɔzɛ lɛ aʋa ŋa si Aya ɔlɛ, lɛ izɛɛtsa liʋɛ ɛki banama gi bɛpɛ́ yɛ veve e.

english_verse: Because Noah had faith, he was warned about something that had not yet happened. He obeyed and built a boat that saved him and his family. In this way the people of the world were judged, and Noah was given the blessings that come to everyone who pleases God.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ Noa gi Aya agba yɛ mɔmɔmɔ ní kidiliɛ gi kiʋíba kɛ mɛ sukɔ su i, enu kutu aki Aya lɛ awa onitsahu lo gi lɔ mɛ yɛ ni yɛ kepea petee bɛwɔ ligbã nɛ ɛ. Gani yɛ kuziŋaa mɛ ebu kelea petee lipɔ, lɛ Noa edzì ɔniliɛ gi gani yɛ kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, azɛ kaŋase ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛgyɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Abraham had faith and obeyed God. He was told to go to the land that God had said would be his, and he left for a country he had never seen.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ Abraham enu kutu, lipoe lɛ gi Aya axwa yɛ ɛ, xe asi yɛ si, ɔsɛ tɔ ɔtrɛ ní kese dzedze e, gi kenu kaŋase e, gi Aya aka aplɛ si yɛ akɔ ki yɛ aʋa a. Asɛ lɛ atrɛ niklɔ gi óte e.

english_verse: Because Abraham had faith, he lived as a stranger in the promised land. He lived there in a tent, and so did Isaac and Jacob, who were later given the same promise.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ eze ŋwisi ɔbaba kinɛ ɛ, ní kesea lia klɔ gi Aya aka aplɛ aki yɛ aʋa a. Eze ní basa akpandɔ mɛ, nite ke Isak ni Yakob gi ba kyɛ biaŋa kaŋasea lia klɔ ɔ, gi ka Aya aka aplɛ ɛ, beze.

english_verse: Abraham did this, because he was waiting for the eternal city God had planned and built.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Abraham azeedi kudeonu aki ɔmaa lɔ klɔ su gi Aya ekyi awla a, xe atsà a, ɔma gi lɔ ɔxɔ lɔ ɔ́trakyi liwewe e.

english_verse: Even when Sarah was too old to have children, she had faith that God would do what he had promised, and she had a son.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ Abraham edzì obika gi nya yɛ Abraham etsi i, xe yedze Sara kyɛ eze okonɔ xe e, lɛ ese linu si ebù ɔniliɛ gi aka aplɛ aki yɛ si aabitɛ ní yɛ kakaplɛa aʋa a.

english_verse: Her husband Abraham was almost dead, but he became the ancestor of many people. In fact, there are as many of them as there are stars in the sky or grains of sand along the seashore.
translation_verse: Gi etsi i, xe ɔlɛ ní kutsede onu kyɛ ɛ, dɔnɔ ní ɔnɔ tole liɛ ya mɛ bebi gi bɛsrɛ ɛ, ŋwisi badzidziebi ʋa ní liwɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ, xe ba kiklakla mɛ kisrɛ ɛ, ŋwisi siklami sɛ ní axuta la a, gi batrátani kukla ɛ, mɛ bɛdɔ.

english_verse: Every one of those people died. But they still had faith, even though they had not received what they had been promised. They were glad just to see these things from far away, and they agreed that they were only strangers and foreigners on this earth.
translation_verse: Banɔ ba ya petee pɔ́ beze ní ba kuziŋaa aʋa a, xe betse e, gi ba ɔwla ɔ́ka kidiliɛ ɛ, gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ bɛmɔ ʋɛ dɔni ɔgblamadza kɔ, lɛ bɛkpɛ ʋɛ wolaa ɛdɔni ɔdzage no, lɛ bedra lɛ mɛ si benu bababa ni bedega ní kesea lia ya aʋa a.

english_verse: When people talk this way, it is clear they are looking for a place to call their own.
translation_verse: Lɛ banama gi bizɛɛpani eboa la ya elu i, bɛdra lɛ mɛ si bɛɛpɛ ba tutɔ ba kese.

english_verse: If they had been talking about the land where they had once lived, they could have gone back at any time.
translation_verse: Baʋíbù kesea lia klɔ mɛ gi bedo ɛdɔ ɛ su, xe aní nite e, nya bemɔ kudeonu si biadzitrɛ ní niklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But they were looking forward to a better home in heaven. This is why God wasn't ashamed for them to call him their God. He even built a city for them.
translation_verse: Boŋu i, bɛɛpɛ kese gi kɛpɛ sanɔ ɛ, gi kenu lifunu ne kanɛ ɛ. Lɛ líku Aya kuye si baxwa yɛ si ba Aya a, lɛ ese linu si abla ɔma ʋidi aplɛ aki ba.

english_verse: Abraham had been promised that Isaac, his only son, would continue his family. But when Abraham was tested, he had faith and was willing to sacrifice Isaac,
translation_verse: gi Aya asi yɛ si, “Gani Isak aʋa wɔamɔ wɔ besoebi ba a, xe wɔ linyi ne litrálila a.”

english_verse: Isaac had faith, and he promised blessings to Jacob and Esau.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ, Isak aka kukrira aplɛ aki Yakob ni Esau ní ɔtɔ nɔ ewe la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Later, when Jacob was about to die, he leaned on his walking stick and worshiped. Then because of his faith he blessed each of Joseph's sons.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋaa mɛ ɛ Yakob, gi etsi xe atrátse e, akrira Yosef yɛ bebinyime tiaʋa ba gi aziɔni yɛ osevugavuga lɔ ɔ, xe esumu Aya a.

english_verse: And right before Joseph died, he had faith that God would lead the people of Israel out of Egypt. So he told them to take his bones with them.
translation_verse: Ní kuziŋa mɛ, lipoɛ lɛ gi Yosef ihatse e, apani ní Israel bebi ba kudodoo ní Egipte kesea mɛ su u, xe akpɛ ɔsã ní kiliɛ gi biagani yɛ axwa la su u.

english_verse: Because Moses' parents had faith, they kept him hidden until he was three months old. They saw that he was a beautiful child, and they were not afraid to disobey the king's orders.
translation_verse: Kuziŋaa, kwikɔ si gi bɛpɔ Mose e, yɛ bapɔnɛ ba bekpo ye badzidzi tiata. Lɛ ese linu si bɛmɔ si, ónu obi ko, lɛ bése okusi ye yɛ ɔsã nɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Then after Moses grew up, his faith made him refuse to be called the king's grandson.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋaa mɛ Mose gi etsi i, ɔ́dimɛ si baxwa yɛ si Farao yebidzɛɛ Yebi tɔ ɔ;

english_verse: He chose to be mistreated with God's people instead of having the good time that sin could bring for a little while.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso akya si yɛ, yɛ amɔ ɔziã pɔni Aya yɛ banima sani si tɔ yimɔ isami lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ni eboe zimizi.

english_verse: Moses knew that the treasures of Egypt were not as wonderful as what he would receive from suffering for the Messiah, and he looked forward to his reward.
translation_verse: Akpɛ lɛ lixaŋu si tɔ yimɔ kuye ní Kristo loso e, biŋa kedzi isani Egipteanɛ ma exo la. Lɛ ese linu si eedi kudeonu ki yɛ liʋɛtsatsa lɛ ní ɔtɔ nɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Because of his faith, Moses left Egypt. Moses had seen the invisible God and wasn't afraid of the king's anger.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋaa mɛ asɛ ní Egipte gi óse Egipteanɛ ma okusi ye yɛ kusuyeʋio o. Lize ŋwisi amɔ Aya gi baʋímɔ ɛ, lɛ loso ódi kede e.

english_verse: His faith also made him celebrate Passover. He sprinkled the blood of animals on the doorposts, so that the first-born sons of the people of Israel would not be killed by the destroying angel.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ, edu kutseo lidɔzawe le, xe befuflu ika lɛ ní ipupo le su u, gi lɛ loso kutseo ɔxwɛdzɛɛ óye Israel bebi ba ba bebinyime tɔpu ba a.

english_verse: Because of their faith, the people walked through the Red Sea on dry land. But when the Egyptians tried to do it, they were drowned.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ banima bɛga Axu kyikyi na mɛ ɛza ŋwisi kese kpaakpa aʋa kinɛ ɛ, lɛ gi Egipteanɛ ma fɛ bekeni edi si bɛɛbitɛ nite e, kuni yo kwivu ba.

english_verse: God's people had faith, and when they had walked around the city of Jericho for seven days, its walls fell down.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ Yerixo egli la ɛkpɔtɔ eku gi banima bɛga ɛklani la ewe tegloele e.

english_verse: Rahab had been a prostitute, but she had faith and welcomed the spies. So she wasn't killed with the people who disobeyed.
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ béye Raxab ɔwawadzee gi ápɔni banama gi bénu kutu aki Aya a, lɛ ese linu si yɛ azɛ Israel bebi beboesega ba ni iwla tiʋa a.

english_verse: What else can I say? There isn't enough time to tell about Gideon, Barak, Samson, Jephthah, David, Samuel, and the prophets.
translation_verse: Lɛ liboe woli maapani dzɛ ɛ? Lɛ ese linu si lipoe le lisrɛ ɛki mɛ tɔɔ si mipani ní Gideon, Barak, Simson, Yefta, Dawid, Samuel ani beboedoplɛ ba su u;

english_verse: Their faith helped them conquer kingdoms, and because they did right, God made promises to them. They closed the jaws of lions
translation_verse: Gani kuziŋa mɛ ɛ, bɛta bigu xe bɛŋa bekusi ba aʋa a. Bɛxwa etsi gyɔgyɔ ɛki banama gi ba linɛ ligyɔ ɛ, lɛ loso bɛzɛ kidiliɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ. Bɛkpɛ gyata ʋa inugu le bibɔ ʋɛ,

english_verse: and put out raging fires and escaped from the swords of their enemies. Although they were weak, they were given the strength and power to chase foreign armies away.
translation_verse: beni bifu gagla ʋɛ, lɛ bedo ní banama gi bɛpɛ si beye ba ni ihɛɛ gblamadza ʋa iwla mɛ ɛ. Béze gagla pɔ̀ kigagla kiku ba mɛ, lɛ bedzi baguta ʋidi gi bɛŋa kùse dzedze baguta ba aʋa a.

english_verse: Some women received their loved ones back from death. Many of these people were tortured, but they refused to be released. They were sure they would get a better reward when the dead are raised to life.
translation_verse: Badzɛ ba bɛzɛ ba baŋanɛa ba gi betse e, xe bedzini ba ɛyɔnɔ ɛ. Banɔ dzedze fɛ gi bezee bu kini ikɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ xe badimɛ si bayurɔ ba a, lɛ loso betse ní kinie me, pɔ̀ bɛbitɛ nite tɔ biayɔ dzi ligbã́ gi lipɛ sanɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Others were made fun of and beaten with whips, and some were chained in jail.
translation_verse: Batɔ fɛ bɛkpɛ ba kakpaa lɛ bekusi ba a, lɛ batɔ fɛ ɛ, bɛkpɛ ba bibɔ lɛ bɛgbani ba ɛtsinɔ ɛzɛkpɛ ligba a.

english_verse: Still others were stoned to death or sawed in two or killed with swords. Some had nothing but sheep skins or goat skins to wear. They were poor, mistreated, and tortured.
translation_verse: Bɛsa ba akla, bɛlɛ ba ní kupɛ tuʋa mɛ, beye ba nì ihɛ gblamadza ʋa, bɛzɛga ɛklanɔ ní bamu ʋa ni iʋesi le kugaplekpaa mɛ, lɛ banima bezeebu kini ikí ba, lɛ bɛzɛɛwa ba nyɔnyɔɛ ɔkpɛ gi kididɔ kíze ní ba awla loso o,

english_verse: The world did not deserve these good people, who had to wander in deserts and on mountains and had to live in caves and holes in the ground.
translation_verse: kelea mɛ kádɔ aki ba! Lɛ bɛzɛga ɛklanɔ ní ɔyaya klɔ ni kutokpaa aʋa a. Lɛ ipɛgla nɛ mɛ, ni ezokpo la mɛ eze ba izeklɔ ɔ.

english_verse: All of them pleased God because of their faith! But still they died without being given what had been promised.
translation_verse: Lɛ banɔ ba ya petee bɛzɛ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɔkpɛ ba kuziŋaa, pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, bázɛ kidiliɛ gi kɛ Aya aka aplɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This was because God had something better in store for us. And he did not want them to reach the goal of their faith without us.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya abitɛ kutrɔtrɔ gbã aki blɔ. Yɛ kutrɔtrɔ kunu si blɔ kwiapɔni ba xe ableke biana a.

english_verse: Such a large crowd of witnesses is all around us! So we must get rid of everything that slows us down, especially the sin that just won't let go. And we must be determined to run the race that is ahead of us.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso gi banima lihakpɔ ʋidi ilɛ gi liiŋa katsalia ɛki blɔ liklani blɔ ɛ, mlaki tɔ kuyurɔ kidɔ kakaa gi kiitsí blɔ kudeonu ni eboe zimizi ana gi ɛnya blɔ gagla tɔ mlaki tɔ kuse nì kimbi kyíkyĩ kuseseo liɔ klɔ gi kulɛ ní blɔ ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ.

english_verse: We must keep our eyes on Jesus, who leads us and makes our faith complete. He endured the shame of being nailed to a cross, because he knew later on he would be glad he did. Now he is seated at the right side of God's throne!
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kukɔ blɔ aŋu na trɔ ní Yesu aʋa, gi yɛ onu blɔ liŋwitsɛ lɛ ɛ, xe yɛ aaki si blɔ kuziŋaa kwina ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si gi ete isamii nɛ ɛ, gi aamɔ dɔni Aya kɔ ɛ, evu ɔna akpɛ lipo lɛ amɔ ɔziã nɔ, lɛ osetsase lo su kutseo kúku ye kuye e. Lɛ eze ní Aya yɛ likusigbo le lɛ kaŋawla mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: So keep your mind on Jesus, who put up with many insults from sinners. Then you won't get discouraged and give up.
translation_verse: Mlebù eboa la mɛ gi aga aza a, dɔni banɔ zimizi ba iwla mɛ, kiliɛ gi bɛtrɔ yɛ ahwafi i! atɔ mlakípè tɔ ɔna okído ní mlɔ lipo o.

english_verse: But you have forgotten that the Scriptures say to God's children, “When the Lord punishes you, don't make light of it, and when he corrects you, don't be discouraged.
translation_verse: Lɛ kidze mledze liboe lɛ gi Aya asi mlɔ gi akpɛ mlɔ sise ŋwisi yɛ bebi ba kinɛ na a? Gi lipani si, “Mobi ye, wukíkɔ Ɔkaɛ yɛ kutuheheo ŋwisi kidɔ tsɛ́ɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ likípè wo xegi aka aŋu mɛ aki wɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The Lord corrects the people he loves and disciplines those he calls his own.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ izɛɛka aŋu mɛ ɛki ɔnɔ kakaa gi alɔ ɛ. Lɛ izeehe kutu iiki ɔnɔ kakaa gi azɛ si Yebi i.”

english_verse: Be patient when you are being corrected! This is how God treats his children. Don't all parents correct their children?
translation_verse: Mlevu ɔna ní ɔziã mɔmɔ mɛ ŋwisi kuhehe kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya eehe mlo ŋwisi yɛ bebi kinɛ ɛ, obi woli ɔlɛ gi yɛka óʋihe ye e?

english_verse: God corrects all his children, and if he doesn't correct you, then you don't really belong to him.
translation_verse: Xegi baʋíhe kutu iiki mlɔ ɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bizehe ki ɔnɔ kakaa kinɛ ɛ, kɔ libasɛ si ɔma mɛ bebi mlenu i, kɔ lɔ mlénu yɛ ɔtutɔ lɔ yɛ bebii o.

english_verse: Our earthly fathers correct us, and we still respect them. Isn't it even better to be given true life by letting our spiritual Father correct us?
translation_verse: Kɔpɔni lɛ ya ɛ, blɔ kisugyaɛ mɛ baka ba gi bizeehe kutu iiki blɔ ɛ, dani kuzɛɛkpɛ kubùbù yo ní ba su i? Lɛ ege loso kutrábubɔ isu ya ɛkpɛ ní Aya blɔ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ Ɔkaɛ ese e, xe kwiaze ligbã́ nɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Our human fathers correct us for a short time, and they do it as they think best. But God corrects us for our own good, because he wants us to be holy, as he is.
translation_verse: Blɔ kisugyaɛ mɛ baka ba behe kutu iiki blɔ lipoe kpekpemi itɔ ko, ní kiliɛ gi begyodi si kipɛ ɛ onu, pɔ̀ Aya kɔ ɛ izeehe kutu iiki blɔ ki blɔ kupɛ̀pɛ ɛ, atɔ blɔ kyɛ kudra ŋwisi yɛ kiniɛ.

english_verse: It is never fun to be corrected. In fact, at the time it is always painful. But if we learn to obey by being corrected, we will do right and live at peace.
translation_verse: Xegi beehe kutu iiki blɔ ɛ, lizɛɛbitɛ ɛki blɔ si kwinu kuyeʋiyeʋi lɛ aní isami i; pɔ̀ lɛ kede e, banama gi bɛga kutuheheo liɔ ya mɛ ɛ, bizɛmɔ si balɛ ni kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa lɛ bizɛmɔ kusuyɔ ni ligbã́zeze gbã.

english_verse: and walk a straight path. Then lame people will be healed, instead of getting worse. Warning against Turning from God
translation_verse: atɔ mlebu bede gyɔgyɔ ʋa aki mlɔ ikli lɛ, tɔ banama gi bɛgbà fɛ ɛ, bakígleʋe pɔ̀ tɔ biayɔ ɔ. Mlata Aya kutu

english_verse: Try to live at peace with everyone! Live a clean life. If you don't, you will never see the Lord.
translation_verse: Mlapɛ tɔ mleze ní kusuyɔ nì ɔnɔ kakaa, atɔ mleze ligbã́ dradra fɛ; lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tratani Ɔkaɛ kumɔ ɔ, xegi ɔ́dra a.

english_verse: Make sure no one misses out on God's wonderful kindness. Don't let anyone become bitter and cause trouble for the rest of you.
translation_verse: Mledi mumɔ, tɔ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkidzi kede ní Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ su u. Mlɔmɛ ɔnɔnɔ okídzi osegu tiitii gi aakpɛ kunugu mlɔ kusi i, xe lɔ aɖi ye aazizi banɔ glegle e.

english_verse: Watch out for immoral and ungodly people like Esau, who sold his future blessing for only one meal.
translation_verse: Mledi le aʋa si ɔnɔnɔ okídzi ɔhasi nɛ, loo ɔnɔ gi óse Aya a, ŋwisi Esau kinɛ ɛ, gi adà yɛ otsitsizeklɔ kɔkpɛ bidɔŋaŋa kezi tiele loso o.

english_verse: You know how he later wanted it back. But there was nothing he could do to change things, even though he begged his father and cried.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si mlete si, lɛ kede e, agi apɛ si yizɛ yɛ kaŋasea nì kukrira ɛdɔni yɛka klɔ ɛ, ɔ́kɔ ʋɛ ɔ́ki yɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ɔ́tani kidiliɛ gi abitɛ ɛ, kukyi gi nya apɛ kukriraa nì kube xe e.

english_verse: You have not come to a place like Mount Sinai that can be seen and touched. There is no flaming fire or dark cloud or storm
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si aní Sinai lito le gi biagbɔnɔ ɔ, loo lito gi liisɔ ɔ, kanɔmɛ gi kɛbitɛ yɔɔ ɔ, loo litumu ni liwɔ gagla su mlaba lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: or trumpet sound. The people of Israel heard a voice speak. But they begged it to stop,
translation_verse: Mlénu kakuklui ya ole gagla lɔ, loo ole gi ɛɛpani eboe gi banama gi benu la ɛ, bɛki litukpo si bakípani liboeboe asi ba tɔɔ ɔ.

english_verse: because they could not obey its commands. They were even told to kill any animal that touched the mountain.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si bátani ɔsã nɔ gi akpɛ aki ba ɛ, lɔ onu kulɛ. “Xegi ɔga asasani lito le fɛ ɛ, lilɛ si basa yɛ akla tɔ otse.”

english_verse: The sight was so frightening that Moses said he shook with fear.
translation_verse: Kidiliɛ gi amɔ níklɔ ʋa ɛ, kikɔ si Mose apani si: “Lifu liku me gi meesugu u.”

english_verse: You have now come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem. This is the city of the living God, where thousands and thousands of angels have come to celebrate.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mlalɛɛ ní Zion lito le su, gi linu Yerusalem gi ɔlɛ ní lifunu ne e, gi onu Aya a, gi ɔlɛ ligbã nɛ ɛ, yɛ ɔma ʋidi no. Mlɛba Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ akpe akpe ʋa ba isami nɛ kutsatsa mɛ.

english_verse: Here you will find all of God's dearest children, whose names are written in heaven. And you will find God himself, who judges everyone. Here also are the spirits of those good people who have been made perfect.
translation_verse: Mlɛba ɛtsani Aya yɛ banima gi balɛ ŋwisi bebi tɔpu ma a, gi ba enyi na bɛŋwlimi ní lifunu ne e. Mlɛba ɛtsani Aya gi yɛ aaxwa litsi ni ɔnɔ kakaa a, mlɛba ɛtsani banama gi ba bede ʋa bɛgyɔ ba ifuɛfuɛ nɛ ɛ, gi Aya aki si ina ɛ.

english_verse: And Jesus is here! He is the one who makes God's new agreement with us, and his sprinkled blood says much better things than the blood of Abel.
translation_verse: Mlɛba ɛtsani Yesu, gi yɛ onu ɔguvu ye kí kunyanya vuvu yɔ ɔ, ni ika lɛ gi befuflu u, gi ipani eboe gbã ɛ sani Abel ini nɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Make sure you obey the one who speaks to you. The people did not escape, when they refused to obey the one who spoke to them at Mount Sinai. Do you think you can possibly escape, if you refuse to obey the one who speaks to you from heaven?
translation_verse: Mledi mumɔ, tɔ mlakíkyi kede ekpɛ ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛpani ki mlɔ ɛ. Xe banɔ baklɔ, gi bekyi kedea ɛkpɛ ɔniliɛ ɛ, gi ɛɛka aŋu mɛ ɛki ba ní kesea aʋa a, xe bátani kuse ní ɔniliɛ klɔ su i, kitɛ liaze xe kwiase ní yɛ ɔniliɛ ɛ, gi ɛɛka aŋu mɛ ɛki blɔ ɛdɔni lifunu ne su u?

english_verse: When God spoke the first time, his voice shook only the earth. This time he has promised to shake the earth once again, and heaven too.
translation_verse: Yɛ ole lo esugu kesea ní lipoe lɛ klɔ mɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ abla ɛ, aka aplɛ si, “Matrahwani aní kesea tètè, pɔ̀ lifunu ne kyɛ tule dzɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The words “once again” mean that these created things will someday be shaken and removed. Then what cannot be shaken will last.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kuyaa gi apani si “tule dzɛ”, kwibasɛ si bidɔ bɛ klɔ gi abitɛ ɛ, xe biasugu i, aabu ʋe zanɔ, atɔ bidɔ bɛ klɔ gi bítrasugu i, tɔ biaze.

english_verse: We should be grateful we were given a kingdom that cannot be shaken. And in this kingdom we please God by worshiping him and by showing him great honor and respect.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso agi mlɛɛzi kaʋaŋaa klɔ ɔ, gi kátrásugu i, mlaki tɔ kudo Aya ɖase, tɔ kusumu Aya gani bede ba ya gi biaku yɛ aŋu i aʋa, kɔpɔni kubùbù ani lifu kusese e.

english_verse: Our God is like a destructive fire!
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si blɔ Aya ɔlɛ ŋwisi kifu, gi kizɛɛkani bidɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Keep being concerned about each other as the Lord's followers should.
translation_verse: Mlatrɛ lɛ aʋa tɔ mlalɔ mlɔ benemi ba!

english_verse: Be sure to welcome strangers into your home. By doing this, some people have welcomed angels as guests, without even knowing it.
translation_verse: Mlaplɛ aŋu tɔ mlazɛ bababa ba; lɛ ese linu si gani kiboebitɛ liɛ ya mɛ ɛ, banɔ batɔ bɛzɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba gi béte e.

english_verse: Remember the Lord's people who are in jail and be concerned for them. Don't forget those who are suffering, but imagine you are there with them.
translation_verse: Mlaplɛ aŋu ní banama gi bakpasi ní agba la aʋa a, ŋwisi mlɔ fɛ ligba lɛ mlalɛ apɔni ba kinɛ ɛ, ani banama gi beebu kini ikɔ fɛ ɛ ŋwisi banama gi ba fɛ bɛɛmɔ kini ye pɔni ba kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Have respect for marriage. Always be faithful to your partner, because God will punish anyone who is immoral or unfaithful in marriage.
translation_verse: Mlɔ pɔ́ mlakpɛ kubùbù yo ní kudzɛgbanɔɔ nì kunyimezenoo su, lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkido ní yɛ ɔhanuglɔɛ aʋa si yizɛɛŋa kuglɔ ɔ; lɛ ese linu si Aya aaxwa litsi ni bahasi nɛ ba ni bawawadze ba ani banama gi bizɛga ɔma mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't fall in love with money. Be satisfied with what you have. The Lord has promised that he will not leave us or desert us.
translation_verse: Mlakíki tɔ mlɔ agbã́ na ezè ní kibɔɛ kudimɛnɔɔ su, lɛ kidiliɛ gi kilɛ ní mlɔ awla ɛ, mlaki tɔ kiku mlɔ aŋu lɛ ese linu si Aya apani si, “Mátrayurɔ mlɔ liwewe mátrakyi kede ekpɛ mlɔ liwewe.”

english_verse: This should make you feel like saying, “The Lord helps me! Why should I be afraid of what people can do to me?”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso kwipani nì likla trɔtrɔ si, “Ɔkaɛ yɛ onu mɛ ɔpɔnɔpɔnɔɛ, matrase lifu u; ege ɔnɔ aatani mɛ kubitɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: Don't forget about your leaders who taught you God's message. Remember what kind of lives they lived and try to have faith like theirs.
translation_verse: Mlaplɛ aŋu ní mlɔ onu bezeze ba aʋa, banama gi bɛpani Aya yɛ Liboe le ɛsi mlɔ ɛ, lɛ mledi kidiliɛ gi kidoedɔ ba agbã́ na mɛ ɛ, tɔ mlakpasi ba kuziŋaa.

english_verse: Jesus Christ never changes! He is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
translation_verse: Yesu Kristo onu ɔnɔ gi oʋikyi i, kiliɛ gi eze azanɔ ɛ, nite ɔlɛ abla lɛ nite ke atráze kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.

english_verse: Don't be fooled by any kind of strange teachings. It is better to receive strength from God's gift of undeserved grace than to depend on certain foods. After all, these foods don't really help the people who eat them.
translation_verse: Mlakíki bidɔbasɛbasɛ dzedze ʋe bikyí mlɔ begyodi ʋa; lɛ ese linu si lipɛ si Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ likpɛ blɔ ina nɛ kigagla, aní isã aʋa kubitɛ ní bidɔŋaŋa ikɔ gi iʋibu libiitɔtɔ ɛki banama gi bɛŋa ʋɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But we have an altar where even the priests who serve in the place of worship have no right to eat.
translation_verse: Ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla, gi kusɔliɔ baka ʋa a, gi bizeesumu ní lisumugba lɛ mɛ ɛ, bámɔ kude gi biaŋa bidɔ ɔ gi bɛkɔ ní lɔ aʋa a.

english_verse: After the high priest offers the blood of animals as a sin offering, the bodies of those animals are burned outside the camp.
translation_verse: Kusumu yo Ɔka ʋidi ye e, izɛɛkɔ bagaa ʋa gi bɛkɔ ɛki banima eboe zimizi na kukɔkyɛ ɛ, ba ika lɛ tsini ɔdradra klɔ petee, pɔ̀ bizɛkani baga zizi ʋa ní ɔma nɔ kutu yɔ.

english_verse: Jesus himself suffered outside the city gate, so his blood would make people holy.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, Yesu fɛ amɔ ɔziã ní ɔma nɔ kutu yɔ, tɔ yikɔ yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ ika lɛ kɔɛdra yɛ akɔanɛ ma su u.

english_verse: This is why we should go outside the camp to Jesus and share in his disgrace.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlaki tɔ kutrɛ yɛ klɔ ní ɔma nɔ ese, tɔ blɔ kyɛ bakpɛ blɔ kuyeo ŋwisi kiliɛ gi bɛkpɛ yɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: On this earth we don't have a city that lasts forever, but we are waiting for such a city.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si ní kesea aʋa ya ɛ, ɔma gi aakyrɔ ɛ, lɔ ɔlɛ ní blɔ awla, pɔ̀ kwidi kudeonu iiki ɔma gi ɔ́trakyrɔ ɔ.

english_verse: But don't forget to help others and to share your possessions with them. This too is like offering a sacrifice that pleases God.
translation_verse: Lɛ mlakídze eboe gbã kubitɛ ni bidɔ mɛ kulɛlɛ nì mlɔ ahanuglɔ ba, lɛ ese linu si bidɔkɔkɔɛ bɛ ya elue bizeeku Aya aŋu.

english_verse: Obey your leaders and do what they say. They are watching over you, and they must answer to God. So don't make them sad as they do their work. Make them happy. Otherwise, they won't be able to help you at all.
translation_verse: Mlenu kutu aki mlɔ onu bezeze ba, lɛ mlakpɛ litukpo ni ba ese, lɛ ese linu si ba beepe mlɔ su, ŋwisi banama gi bɛtráki akɔnta kí Aya kinɛ ɛ. Mlenu kutu aki ba tɔ bawa ba axwɛ na nì isami, atɔ akíze ɔnatata ɛki ba; lɛ ese linu si lítrabu libiitɔtɔ ɛki mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Pray for us. Our consciences are clear, and we always try to live right.
translation_verse: Mlaxwa Aya ní blɔ litukpo; kwika lɛ aʋa si blɔ begyodi ʋa mɛ bɛdra, lɛ kwipɛ si kwiaze ligbã́ gi lipɛ ní kapɛ kakaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I especially want you to pray that I can visit you again soon. Final Prayers and Greetings
translation_verse: Lɛ mɛɛki mlɔ litukpo glegle sanɔ si, mlaxwa Aya ní mɛ litukpo tɔ Aya aadzi ni me kumanɔ ní mlɔ klɔ kpékpe e.

english_verse: God gives peace, and he raised our Lord Jesus Christ from death. Now Jesus is like a Great Shepherd whose blood was used to make God's eternal agreement with his flock.
translation_verse: Atɔ kusuyɔ Aya gi gani kunyanya vuvu yɔ gi kulɛ tsinɔ ɛ, kɔ ika lɛ mɛ, xe agbani blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu adɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛ, yɛ Yesu gi onu iʋesi le ɔgbanɔgbanɔ ʋidi ye,

english_verse: I pray God will make you ready to obey him and that you will always be eager to do right. May Jesus help you do what pleases God. To Jesus Christ be glory forever and ever! Amen.
translation_verse: ɔki mlɔ kidɔ gbã kakaa gi kiaziã tɔ mlabitɛ ní yɛ kupɛpɛɔ aʋa; atɔ ɔbitɛ kidiliɛ gi kiku yɛ aŋu ní blɔ mɛ gani Yesu Kristo aʋa, gi yɛ kwiakɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ kɔ dɔni kanɔmɛ kɔtsinɔ kanɔmɛ! Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, I have written only a short letter to encourage you, and I beg you to pay close attention to what I have said.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, mɛɛki mlɔ litukpo tɔ mlazɛ kugbagba liɔ ya gi mɛkɔ ɛkpɛ mlɔ kigagla ɛ, lɛ ese linu si keplekpa kpékpe ko maŋwlimi atsisi mlɔ ɔ.

english_verse: By now you surely must know that our friend Timothy is out of jail. If he gets here in time, I will bring him with me when I come to visit you.
translation_verse: Mɛɛki mlɔ kutete si bebu blɔ onemi ye Timoteo ní ligba lɛ, xegi atsɔ ní ya kuba ɛ, kɔlɔ mɛ nì yɛ kwiana mlɔ.

english_verse: Please give my greetings to your leaders and to the rest of the Lord's people. His followers from Italy send you their greetings.
translation_verse: Mlapani mlɔ onu bezeze ba petee ani Aya yɛ banima petee. Baʋaziŋa ba gi bɛdɔ Italia ɛ, bɛpani mlɔ.

english_verse: I pray that God will be kind to all of you!
translation_verse: Aya yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lize nì mlɔ petee!

english_verse: From James, a servant of God and of our Lord Jesus Christ. Greetings to the twelve tribes scattered all over the world. Faith and Wisdom
translation_verse: Keplekpaa lia kɛdɔ ní mɛ Yakobo ɔniliɛ gi izeesumu Aya ni Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo kɔ ɔ. Mapani mlɔ amade liɔfɔ ni taʋa la gi ɛlɛ eku kelea petee me e. Kukenidi ni kufekedi bɛ onu kulɛ́lɛɛ

english_verse: My friends, be glad, even if you have a lot of trouble.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, isami iku mlo xe kufekedi ede ede ɛba mlɔ bede ʋa mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: But you must learn to endure everything, so you will be completely mature and not lacking in anything.
translation_verse: Mledi lɛ aʋa si mlɔ eboe la onu kulɛlɛɔ kwigbani mlɔ ɛtsinɔ ní ɔtɔ, tɔ mlakígleʋe e, tɔ mlaatsì, atɔ mlana ní kidɔ kakaa mɛ.

english_verse: If any of you need wisdom, you should ask God, and it will be given to you. God is generous and won't correct you for asking.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe kuboete kúma ní mlɔ mɛ ɔtɔ awla ɛ, ɔniliɛ klɔ oʋi Aya tɔ aaki yɛ, lɛ ese linu si Aya izɛɛkɔ ɔnɔ kakaa faã ni lipomepɛpɛ.

english_verse: But when you ask for something, you must have faith and not doubt. Anyone who doubts is like an ocean wave tossed around in a storm.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe eʋi i, lilɛ si ɔzɛ ɔŋa tɔ ɔkidi kede kura. Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi eedi kede e, ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi axu la gi liwɔ lɛ lifu iklanɔ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Any of God's people who are poor should be glad he thinks so highly of them.
translation_verse: Lilɛ si isami iku ɔsɔlisɔli liɛ klɔ gi onu opiplinete e, xe Aya emuni ye e,

english_verse: But any who are rich should be glad when God makes them humble. Rich people will disappear like wild flowers
translation_verse: lɛ oxonete ɔsɔlisɔli yɛ fɛ, ɔmɔ isami xe Aya epleni ye e. Lɛ ese linu si oxonete yɛ ligbã́ nɛ liaza ŋwisi liŋwafu mɛ ose kufùfù kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: scorched by the burning heat of the sun. The flowers lose their blossoms, and their beauty is destroyed. This is how the rich will disappear, as they go about their business. Trials and Temptations
translation_verse: Liwo le lizeetu nì lɛ kuhuhu gaglaa, kuzɛɛkani siwa sɛ, kɔ sɛ bifùfù ʋe biwɔli nite me si sɛ kupedi yo kwizizi i. Nite ke onyime oxonetee fɛ aazizi gi eekyi awla ní yɛ axwɛ na su u. Kufekedi ni kukenidi kúdɔ Aya kɔ ɔ

english_verse: God will bless you, if you don't give up when your faith is being tested. He will reward you with a glorious life, just as he rewards everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Isami iku ɔniliɛ klɔ gi aalɛ ní yɛ kuziŋa aʋa a, xe ɔlɛ kufekedi me e, lɛ ese linu si xe aŋa kufekedi uliɔ ya aʋa ɛ, aazɛ ŋwisi yɛ liʋɛ tsatsa kinɛ ɛ, ligbã́ɛ nɛ gi Aya aka aplɛ ɛ, aki banama gi bɛlɔ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Don't blame God when you are tempted! God cannot be tempted by evil, and he doesn't use evil to tempt others.
translation_verse: Xe kufekedi uliɔ ya elu kwiba ɔnutɔ aʋa ɛ, ɔkipani si, “Aya eefeke ye edi i.” Lɛ ese linu si bátratani Aya kufekedi nì eboe zimizi i, lɛ yɛ ɔtutɔ fɛ, óʋifeke ɔnɔnɔ odi i.

english_verse: We are tempted by our own desires that drag us off and trap us.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kufekedi kuzɛɛba ɔnɔ aʋa, xe yɛ ɔtutɔ bapɛ́pɛ nyani nyani ʋa bemeni ye e, xe behe ye zɛkpɛ kufekedi yo me e.

english_verse: Our desires make us sin, and when sin is finished with us, it leaves us dead.
translation_verse: Kɔ yɛ kupɛ́pɛ nyani nyani yɔ gi kuli yɛ ɛ, kwimani kuzimizi, lɛ xe kuzimizi yo kwisrɛ ɛ, kɔ kɔ fɛ kwimani kutse.

english_verse: Don't be fooled, my dear friends.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi balɔlɔ nɛ ba, mlakíki tɔ ɔnɔnɔ omeni mlo o!

english_verse: Every good and perfect gift comes down from the Father who created all the lights in the heavens. He is always the same and never makes dark shadows by changing.
translation_verse: Kidɔkɔkɔ gbã kakaa ni kidɔflɔflɔ kakaa kidɔ ní lifunu ne, lɛ kiple edɔ ɛdɔni Aya kɔ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ abitɛ lifunu ne kuklekleo o, gi oʋítsrɛ ŋwisi kuklekle ní litumu kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: He wanted us to be his own special people, and so he sent the true message to give us new birth. Hearing and Obeying
translation_verse: Gani yɛ ɔtutɔ yɛ kupɛ́pɛ mɛ ɛ, akɔ anukwarɛ liboe le apɔ blɔ, tɔ kuze batɔpu ní yɛ bidɔbitɛbitɛ ʋɛ petee kusi. Kututata ni Liboe le aʋa kubitɛbitɛ

english_verse: If you are angry, you cannot do any of the good things God wants done.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Ɔnɔ yɛ kusuyeʋi kuʋíkɔ si babitɛ Aya yɛ kupɛ́pɛ dradra aʋa a.

english_verse: You must stop doing anything immoral or evil. Instead be humble and accept the message planted in you to save you.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlayurɔ ligbã́ kluklu ni litukpo me gagla kakaa. Mlepleni isu ya akpɛ ní Aya ese tɔ mlazɛ yɛ liboe le gi akpɛ ní mlɔ ina nɛ mɛ ɛ, gi lɛ liatani mlɔ kubu i.

english_verse: Obey God's message! Don't fool yourselves by just listening to it.
translation_verse: Mlakímeni isu gani yɛ liboe le tètè kununu me ko o, boŋu i, mlakɔ lɛ akpɛ axwɛwawa mɛ.

english_verse: If you hear the message and don't obey it, you are like people who stare at themselves in a mirror
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi izenu liboe le e, xe ɔʋíwa lɛ su axwɛ ɛ, ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi onyime ɔtɔ gi izedi azinzi yɛ mɛ ɛ, xe izɛmɔ isu ya kiliɛ gi ɔlɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and forget what they look like as soon as they leave.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe edi isu ya mɔmɔmɔ ɔ, xe asɛ ɛ, kɔ edze yɛ kilɛlɛɛ kyɔŋ.

english_verse: But you must never stop looking at the perfect law that sets you free. God will bless you in everything you do, if you listen and obey, and don't just hear and forget.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔniliɛ gi izɛtapo ɔsã flɔflɔ lɔ edi mɔmɔmɔ ɔ, gi izɛki banɔ ɔwlamɔmɔ ɔ, xe izexo lɔ kutu i, xe óʋinu lo ko o, xe aadze lo o, pɔ̀ izɛkɔ lɔ ɛkpɛ axwɛwawa mɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ klɔ Aya aakrira ní yɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ.

english_verse: If you think you are being religious, but can't control your tongue, you are fooling yourself, and your religion is useless.
translation_verse: Xe ɔnutɔ egyoedi si yɛaflɔ ní kusɔli mɛ ɛ, pɔ xe ɔ́ŋa yɛ kinemi ye aʋa ɛ, emeni isu lɛ yɛ kusɔliɔ kunu kupluplu u.

english_verse: Religion that pleases God the Father must be pure and spotless. You must help needy orphans and widows and not let this world make you evil.
translation_verse: Kusɔli uliɔ gi Aya, blɔ Ɔkaɛ akɔ si kwidra a, xe kesetata káma ní kɔsu i, kɔ kunu si, tɔ mledi bebi ba gi ba bapɔnɛ ba betse e, ni batrɛtrɛ ba bedze ba aʋa, ní ba ɔzia nɔ mɛ, ani tɔ mlebu isu adɔni kelea mɛ kuzimizi kakaa mɛ.

english_verse: My friends, if you have faith in our glorious Lord Jesus Christ, you won't treat some people better than others.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, ŋwisi banɔ gi bɛzi blɔ Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo o, gi onu kuklekleo Ɔkaɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa kinɛ ɛ, mlakíkpɛ lidzegaga ní banima kusi ní ba kiŋwiŋwi yɛ mɛ onu u.

english_verse: Suppose a rich person wearing fancy clothes and a gold ring comes to one of your meetings. And suppose a poor person dressed in worn-out clothes also comes.
translation_verse: Kɔ lɛ si oxonete gi akpɛ sika asukpɛɛ ɛ, nì ewu gbã na aba mlɔ ɔtsaklɔ, lɛ ɔzianete ní ewu pamipami mɛ fɛ aba ɛ.

english_verse: You must not give the best seat to the one in fancy clothes and tell the one who is poor to stand at the side or sit on the floor.
translation_verse: Xe wɔki ɔniliɛ gi akpɛ ewu gbã na kubùbù sanɔ ɔ, lɛ wɔsi yɛ si, “Ze ní ligbo gbã ɛ nɛ ya aʋa a”, pɔ̀ lɛ wɔsi ɔzianete si, “Lɛɛ ní kɔlɔ ɔ,” loo wɔsi yɛ si “ze keseatsalia ní mɛ ikli lɛ onu i,”

english_verse: This is the same as saying that some people are better than others, and you would be acting like a crooked judge.
translation_verse: Kɔlɔ wɔnyata kese gi wɔmani kulɛlɛ ní mlɔ kusi i, lɛ wɔxwa litsi nì begyodi nyani nyani i.

english_verse: My dear friends, pay attention. God has given a lot of faith to the poor people in this world. God has also promised them a share in his kingdom that he will give to everyone who loves him.
translation_verse: Mlata kutu mɛ benemi lɔlɔ nɛ ba! Aya akya bepiplinete ba ní kelea lia mɛ tɔ beze bexonete ní kuziŋa mɛ atɔ bazɛ kaʋaŋaa ŋwisi ba kaŋase gi aka aplɛ aki banama a, gi bɛlɔ yɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: You mistreat the poor. But isn't it the rich who boss you around and drag you off to court?
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ mlákpɛ kubùbù ní bepiplinete ba su u! Fɔanɛ bizɛtafa mlɔ xe bizehe mlo ɛtsini betsixwa ba a? Ba benu bexonete ba!

english_verse: You will do all right, if you obey the most important law in the Scriptures. It is the law that commands us to love others as much as we love ourselves.
translation_verse: Wɔli ní kude gbã aʋa xe wɔbitɛ ní kaʋaŋaa ɔsã nɔ aʋa a, gi ɔkpasi ní kuŋwlimi dradra mɛ ɛ si, “Lɔ wɔ ɔhanuglɔɛ ŋwisi wɔ ɔtutɔ wɔ isu ya kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: But if you treat some people better than others, you have done wrong, and the Scriptures teach that you have sinned.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe wɔbitɛ ɔyurɔ banima ní ba kiŋwiŋwi yɛ mɛ onu i, wɔta kese, lɛ ɔsã nɔ ebú wo lipɔ ŋwisi ɔtumonu kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you obey every law except one, you are still guilty of breaking them all.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi ata ɔsã tole ese e, aŋa lipɔ ŋwisi ɔnɔ gi ata lɛ petee ese kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The same God who told us to be faithful in marriage also told us not to murder. So even if you are faithful in marriage, but murder someone, you still have broken God's Law.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi yɛ apani si, “Wukíwa kihasi i” yɛ ké apani si, “Wukíye ɔnɔ ɔ.” Xe wɔ́wa kihasi fɛ ɛ, wɔta ɔsã nɔ ese xegi woye ɔnɔ ɛ.

english_verse: Speak and act like people who will be judged by the law that sets us free.
translation_verse: Lɛ loso mlapani tɔ mlabitɛ eboe ŋwisi banama gi biaxwa litsi kɔ ní ɔsã nɔ gi izɛki banɔ ɔwlamɔmɔ onu i.

english_verse: Do this, because on the day of judgment there will be no pity for those who have not had pity on others. But even in judgment, God is merciful! Faith and Works
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya ɔ́tradi asigaga xe axwa litsi ni ɔniliɛ gi ódi banɔ asigaga a, pɔ̀ Aya ɔ́trabu ɔniliɛ gi izeedi banɔ asigaga ɛ lipɔ. Kuziŋa gi axwɛwawa áma ní kɔsu i, kunu kuziŋa pluplu

english_verse: My friends, what good is it to say you have faith, when you don't do anything to show you really do have faith? Can this kind of faith save you?
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, liboe gbã woli linu xe ɔnutɔ apani si, kuziŋa kulɛ ní yɛ awla pɔ̀ xe kuziŋa lixwɛwawa líma yɛ awla a? Kuziŋa liɔ klɔ kwiatani yɛ kubu na a?

english_verse: If you know someone who doesn't have any clothes or food,
translation_verse: Kɔlɛ si beneminyime loo benemidzɛ bɛziã ewu, lɛ kiŋaŋaɛ fɛ kísrɛ gi biaŋa a.

english_verse: you shouldn't just say, “I hope all goes well for you. I hope you will be warm and have plenty to eat.” What good is it to say this, unless you do something to help?
translation_verse: Liboe gbã woli linu, xe wɔsi ba si, “Aya ɔkrira mlɔ! Kublɔ kukíku mlo o, lɛ mlaŋa tɔ mleke!” xe wɔ́ki ba ligbã́ nɛ bidɔ ziazia ʋɛ ɛ?

english_verse: Faith that doesn't lead us to do good deeds is all alone and dead!
translation_verse: Nite kuziŋaa kulɛ, xe axwɛwawa ápɔni kɔ ɛ, kɔlɔ kunu kupluplu.

english_verse: Suppose someone disagrees and says, “It is possible to have faith without doing kind deeds.” I would answer, “Prove that you have faith without doing kind deeds, and I will prove that I have faith by doing them.”
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔnutɔ aapani si, “Kuziŋa kulɛ ní ɔnutɔ awla, lɛ ɔnɔ dzedze fɛ axwɛwawa alɛ yɛ awla a.” Maabu lɛ onu si, “Basɛ mɛ kiliɛ gi kuziŋa kwiaze ni ɔnutɔ awla xe axwɛwawa ápɔnɔ ɛ. Lɛ maabasɛ mlɔ mɛ kuziŋaa gani mɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: You surely believe there is only one God. That's fine. Even demons believe this, and it makes them shake with fear.
translation_verse: Dani wɔzi lɛ aʋa ŋa si Aya tole ko ɔlɛ ɛ? Lipɛ! Ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne fɛ izɛ lɛ aʋa ɛɛŋa lɛ izeesugu ni lifu u.

english_verse: Does some stupid person want proof that faith without deeds is useless?
translation_verse: Ɔga! Kipɛ wɔpɛ si babasɛ wɔ si kuziŋa gi axwɛwawa ápɔni kɔ ɛ, kúnu kididɔ na a?

english_verse: Well, our ancestor Abraham pleased God by putting his son Isaac on the altar to sacrifice him.
translation_verse: Dani bɛmɔ blɔ ɔkatsitsi ye Abraham si eze ɔnɔ gbã ní Aya aŋu mɛ gani yɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ gi akɔ Yebi ye Isak akɔ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ aʋa na a?

english_verse: Now you see how Abraham's faith and deeds worked together. He proved his faith was real by what he did.
translation_verse: Mlámɔ si yɛ kuziŋaa ni yɛ axwɛwawa la ɛkpɛ likpɔ na a? Lɛ yɛ kuziŋaa kwiflɔ gani yɛ axwɛwawa la mɛ na a?

english_verse: This is what the Scriptures mean by saying, “Abraham had faith in God, and God accepted him.” That's how Abraham became God's friend.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya kuŋwlimi yɔ kwiba kɔ mɛ gi kwipani i si, “Abraham azɛ Aya aʋa ŋa lɛ kɔkpɛ yɛ kuziŋaa loso e, Aya azɛ yɛ si onu ɔnɔ gi aflɔ ɔ.” Lɛ loso axwa Abraham si yɛʋia a.

english_verse: You can now see that we please God by what we do and not only by what we believe.
translation_verse: Wɔmɔ si gani axwɛwawa mɛ ɛ, ɔnɔ izeedzì ɔnɔgbà ní Aya aŋu mɛ, aní gani kuziŋa tete me e.

english_verse: For example, Rahab had been a prostitute. But she pleased God when she welcomed the spies and sent them home by another way.
translation_verse: Nite ké lize ni Raxab ɔwawadzee, gani yɛ axwɛwawa mɛ ɛ, Aya amɔ yɛ si eze ɔnɔ gbã gi azɛ Israel bebi badɔwá ba a, lɛ apɔni ba xe bese ga kude dzedze sɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Anyone who doesn't breathe is dead, and faith that doesn't do anything is just as dead!
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, kiliɛ gi ɔnɔ ɔlɛ xe Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔma ní yɛ mɛ ɛ, xe izedzì kidɔ tsetse e, niteme ke kuziŋa gi axwɛwawa ápɔnɔ ɛ, kunu kuziŋa tsetse.

english_verse: My friends, we should not all try to become teachers. In fact, teachers will be judged more strictly than others.
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, aní mlɔ petee mladzì badɔbasɛ ɛ. Lɛ ese linu si mlete si biaxwa litsi tsiitsii ni blɔ badɔbasɛ ba sani banɔ gɔgɔ ba.

english_verse: All of us do many wrong things. But if you can control your tongue, you are mature and able to control your whole body.
translation_verse: Blɔ petee kwita kese. Pɔ̀ xe ɔnɔ ɔlɛ gi ɔʋíta kese liwewe ní yɛ kipaniɛ mɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ aflɔ lɛ aatani yɛ isu aʋa kuŋa a.

english_verse: By putting a bit into the mouth of a horse, we can turn the horse in different directions.
translation_verse: Xe kwikpɛ bala ʋa ní isɔ ʋa inugu le me tɔ benu kutu aki blɔ ɛ, kuzɛɛtani ba tsinɔ ní niklɔ gi kwipɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It takes strong winds to move a large sailing ship, but the captain uses only a small rudder to make it go in any direction.
translation_verse: Loo mlegyo initsahu le edi, kiliɛ gi ikeme e, xe awɔ gagla la izeku le fɛ ɛ, akufa sisami atɔ ko bizɛkɔɛ gyɔ lɛ ɛtsini niklɔ gi ohuku ye apɛ si itrɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Our tongues are small too, and yet they brag about big things. It takes only a spark to start a forest fire!
translation_verse: Niteme ke kinemi ye kilɛ. Kinu kisugyaɛ kapɛ sisami atɔ, pɔ̀ kizɛtani isu kudɔ̀ ni eboe ʋidi. Gyo le di kiliɛ gi ɔfinyimi aatani liŋwafu ʋidi kifu kukpɛ ɛ!

english_verse: The tongue is like a spark. It is an evil power that dirties the rest of the body and sets a person's entire life on fire with flames that come from hell itself.
translation_verse: Lɛ kinemi ye kilɛ ŋwisi kifu kinɛ ɛ. Kinu kidɔ zimizi gi kikpasi ní blɔ isu ya mɛ bidɔ mɛ kusi i, kizezizi ɔnɔse lo, lɛ kizɛkpɛ blɔ ligbã́ nɛ mɛ bede ʋa kifu, lɛ kifu moni ye kizɛkpɛ kinemi ye kifu yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All kinds of animals, birds, reptiles, and sea creatures can be tamed and have been tamed.
translation_verse: Ɔnɔ gbagbe aatani kumla, lɛ amla baga ede ede, kudzɔʋi yɔ, bagaa ʋa gi bizeti i, ani akpa la.

english_verse: But our tongues get out of control. They are restless and evil, and always spreading deadly poison.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani kinemi ye kumla a. Ki nyani, lɛ báma kɛ aʋa kuŋa mɛ ɛ, kinu aɖi zimizi.

english_verse: My dear friends, with our tongues we speak both praises and curses. We praise our Lord and Father, and we curse people who were created to be like God, and this isn't right.
translation_verse: Ɖase kudodo ni ifi kuxoxo bizɛdɔ onugu tole lɔ ya ké me. Mɛ benemi ba, líma si lize nite e.

english_verse: Can clean water and dirty water both flow from the same spring?
translation_verse: Kenipeʋiatɔ kalɛ gi kizɛɛki kumwɛni ani kuni gyigyi dɔni linitɔ tile me na a?

english_verse: Can a fig tree produce olives or a grapevine produce figs? Does fresh water come from a well full of salt water? Wisdom from Above
translation_verse: Mɛ benemi ba, ɔmumɔɛ ose ɔ́tratani mangɔ kuyo o, lɛ wain ehuʋi la átratani atɔ kuyo o, lɛ kumwɛ linitɔ kyɛ ɛ litrátani kuni gyigyi kukɔ ɔ. Kuboete ede taʋa

english_verse: Are any of you wise or sensible? Then show it by living right and by being humble and wise in everything you do.
translation_verse: Banɔatɔ balɛ ni mlɔ kusi gi béte liboe, xe benu eboe ese na a? Bebu le abasɛ gani ba agbã́ gbã na ni ba kiboebitɛ gi bɛbitɛ ní kusububɔ ni kuboete me.

english_verse: But if your heart is full of bitter jealousy and selfishness, don't brag or lie to cover up the truth.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ xe ní wɔ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, kaŋuzã, kutitii ani amimi bilɛ wɔ ɛ, kɔlɔ wukíta kese ní anukwarɛ su gani kusufeke ni wɔ kuboeteo me e.

english_verse: This kind of wisdom doesn't come from above. It is earthly and selfish and comes from the devil himself.
translation_verse: Kuboeteo klɔ elue kúdɔ lifunu ne e, kelea me kunɛ kunu, kisugya mɛ ni ozimizi ye kunɛ.

english_verse: Whenever people are jealous or selfish, they cause trouble and do all sorts of cruel things.
translation_verse: Niklɔ gi kaŋuza ni amimi bilɛ ɛ, niklɔ kapisa ni kuzimizi kakaa elu bilɛ.

english_verse: When peacemakers plant seeds of peace, they will harvest justice.
translation_verse: Lɛ Ligbã́ flɔflɔ lɛ ɛ, linu kidiliɛ gi kido edɔni esebi ala gi baguvu ba bɛkpɛ ní kusuyɔ mɛ ɛ.
