translation_verse: Lɛ ba ipi ne izɛɛta ŋwisi kutapiadzɔ ini nɛ kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ní ba ipi ne me kigaglaɛ kilɛ si biabu kini kí banɔ badzidzi tietsu.

english_verse: Their king was the angel in charge of the deep pit. In Hebrew his name was Abaddon, and in Greek it was Apollyon.
translation_verse: Lɛ okusi liɛ gi eze ba onu i, ye onu lizokpo gluglu le me ɔxwɛdzɛɛ, gi bizɛxwa ní si Hebri se me si Abadon, lɛ ní si Hela sɛ mɛ ɛ bizɛxwa yɛ si Apolion.

english_verse: The first horrible thing has now happened! But wait. Two more horrible things will happen soon.
translation_verse: “Babaa” tɔpu yɛ onu aza; bigɔ baba tiaʋa gi bɛtrába ɛ.

english_verse: Then the sixth angel blew his trumpet. I heard a voice speak from the four corners of the gold altar that stands in the presence of God.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ glonɛɛ eme yɛ kakuklui ya ɛ, lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ gi ɛɛpani dɔni sika yɛ ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ, gi ɔlɛ ní Aya aŋu mɛ ɛ, lɔ asia tene na mɛ.

english_verse: The voice spoke to this angel and said, “Release the four angels who are tied up beside the great Euphrates River.”
translation_verse: Ɛɛpani ki Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ glonɛɛ gi evu kakuklui ya ní awla la si, “Ble baxwɛdzɛ tiene ba, gi bɛnya ní Eufrate onipe ʋidi lo kutu i.”

english_verse: The four angels had been prepared for this very hour and day and month and year. Now they were set free to kill a third of all people.
translation_verse: Lɛ beble baxwɛdzɛ tiene ba gi balɛ ní kublabla mɛ ɛki ɔnyɔsasaa nɔ ya tututu u, ni liwe lɛ ya, ni ɔdzidzi liɛ ya, ani ligɔɛ lɛ ya aʋa a, tɔ biaye banima kapɛ tanɛa.

english_verse: By listening, I could tell there were more than
translation_verse: Menu gi bɛxwa ba baguta ba kiklaklaɛ mɛ si benu isɔ bakpɛkpɛ akpe akpe ede alafa tiaʋa.

english_verse: In my vision their riders wore fiery-red, dark-blue, and yellow armor on their chests. The heads of the horses looked like lions, with fire and smoke and sulfur coming out of their mouths.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ isɔ ʋa ni banama gi bɛkpɛ ʋa a, ní kububasɛɔ mɛ, baguta ba bɛkpɛ bala kakɔ mɛ ewu gi ɛzã kyikyi ŋwisi kifu, ni bluu ŋwisi axu la, ni bitɔ gi biizã ŋwisi sɔlfa loo ɔmumɔɛ kinɛ ɛ. Ba isɔ ʋa etukpo la eze ŋwisi gyata kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kifu yɛ ni kunyɔɔ ani sɔlfa biido edɔ ní ba inugu le me e.

english_verse: One third of all people were killed by the three terrible troubles caused by the fire, the smoke, and the sulfur.
translation_verse: Lɛ banima kapɛ tanɛa ní kesea aʋa betse gani baxixa tiata ʋa ya mɛ, gi benu kifu yɛ, ni kunyɔɔ ani sɔlfa yɛ gi biido ɛdɔni ba inugu le me loso o.

english_verse: The people who lived through these terrible troubles did not turn away from the idols they had made, and they did not stop worshiping demons. They kept on worshiping idols that were made of gold, silver, bronze, stone, and wood. Not one of these idols could see, hear, or walk.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ banɔ gɔgɔa ba, gi bétse gani nyɔnyɔɛ biwàdɔ tutaɛ ʋɛ ya mɛ kyɛ ɛ, badime si bekyi ní ba ina nɛ mɛ, dɔni ba ɔwla onu axwɛwawa la mɛ ɛ, bámɔni ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne, sika yɛ ni klosalo ye, ni ɔla kyikyi yɛ ani akla kɔpɔni ise le babu ʋa kusumu u. Babua ʋa gi batrátani bidɔ kumɔ ɔ, loo eboe kunu loo kuga a.

english_verse: No one stopped murdering or practicing witchcraft or being immoral or stealing.
translation_verse: Lɛ báyurɔ ba kunɔyeo, ba ehwiboe la, ba enyimeboe la ni ádzɛboe la, ni ba ezeboe la.

english_verse: I saw another powerful angel come down from heaven. This one was covered with a cloud, and a rainbow was over his head. His face was like the sun, his legs were like columns of fire,
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gagla dzedze gi eeple dɔni lifunu ne e. Aplɛ la ɛklani yɛ ŋwisi liwu kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔkũ yɛ ɔlɛ ní yɛ litukpo le, yɛ aŋu na mɛ eekle ŋwisi liwo le kinɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ ikli lɛ ilɛ ŋwisi igbase gi iisɔ kinɛ ɛ,

english_verse: and with his hand he held a little scroll that had been unrolled. He stood there with his right foot on the sea and his left foot on the land.
translation_verse: evu keplekpa sisami gi bɛdra ɛ, ní yɛ kawla mɛ. Edu yɛ kaŋa mɛ ɔkli lɔ ní axu la aʋa, lɛ akɔ yɛ kaba ɔkli lɔ edu ní kesea aʋa ɛ,

english_verse: Then he shouted with a voice that sounded like a growling lion. Thunder roared seven times.
translation_verse: lɛ akpɛ ozi ni ole gagla ɔtɔ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi gyata yɛ izɛfami kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ gi akpɛ ozi lo e, dzeni ye egu tegloele ŋwisi kunububu kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: After the thunder stopped, I was about to write what it had said. But a voice from heaven shouted, “Keep it secret! Don't write these things.”
translation_verse: Lɛ gi dzenigugu tiegloele ʋa ba ile le ipani i, mapɛ si miŋwlimi ba liboe le plɛ ɛ: pɔ̀ lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ ɛdɔ ní lifunu ne gi ɛɛpani si, “Dà bidɔ ní eboa la gi dzenigugu tiegloele ba bɛpani i onu, atɔ wukíŋwlimi na ɛplɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The angel I had seen standing on the sea and the land then held his right hand up toward heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ liɛ, gi mamɔ ɔ, xe ɔlɛɛ kedu ya ní axu la ni kesea aʋa ɛ, emuni yɛ kaŋawla ní lifune.

english_verse: He made a promise in the name of God who lives forever and who created heaven, earth, the sea, and every living creature. The angel said, “You won't have to wait any longer.
translation_verse: Lɛ atàni ɔniliɛ gi ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ, ɔniliɛ gi yɛ abitɛ lifunu ne ni kidɔ kakaa a, gi kikpasi ni bɛ mɛ ɛ, ani kesea ni kidɔ kakaa a, gi kilɛ ní ka aʋa a, kɔpɔni axu la ni kidɔ kakaa a, gi kikpasi ní la mɛ ɛ, lɛ apani si “Batrázizi lipoe tɔtɔ tɔɔ ɔ!

english_verse: God told his secret plans to his servants the prophets, and it will all happen by the time the seventh angel sounds his trumpet.”
translation_verse: Xe lipoe le lina si Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gloele e ome yɛ kakuklui ya ɛ, Aya aagu yɛ eboe kpokpo la ŋwisi kiliɛ gi asi yɛ banɔsumu ba gi benu beboedoplɛ ba ɛ.”

english_verse: Once again the voice from heaven spoke to me. It said, “Go and take the open scroll from the hand of the angel standing on the sea and the land.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ole ɔklɔ gi menu adɔ ni lifunu ne e, egu kunugu ni me dzɛ si, “Trɛ tɔ zɛɛzɛ keplekpaʋia lia gi bɛdra ɛ, gi kalɛ ní Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ɛ, gi ɔlɛɛ kedu ya ní axu la ni kesea aʋa ɛ awla.”

english_verse: When I went over to ask the angel for the little scroll, the angel said, “Take the scroll and eat it! Your stomach will turn sour, but the taste in your mouth will be sweet as honey.”
translation_verse: Lɛ matrɛ ní Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ klɔ ɔ, xe meʋi ye si ɔkɔ keplekpaʋia ɔkimɛ ɛ. Lɛ asi mɛ si, “Izɛ, tɔ tà ka, kianyasi ní wɔ lipo le me, pɔ̀ kiaze ŋanaŋana ní wɔ onugu lo me ŋwisi kibú kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: I took the little scroll from the hand of the angel and ate it. The taste was sweet as honey, but my stomach turned sour.
translation_verse: Lɛ mazɛ keplekpaʋia ní Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ awla a, lɛ matà ka ɛ. Kezee ŋanaŋana ní mɛ onugu lo me ŋwisi kibu ye kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ gi matà ka ɛ, kɛnyasi ní mɛ lipo le me.

english_verse: Then some voices said, “Keep on telling what will happen to the people of many nations, races, and languages, and also to kings.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛsi mɛ si, “Lilɛ kí wɔ si pani liboe plɛ dzɛ ní banɔ glegle, ni kuse glegle, siya ede ede ni bekusi ede ede su.”

english_verse: But don't measure the courtyard outside the temple building. Leave it out. It has been given to those people who don't know God, and they will trample all over the holy city for
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ wukígyigye kepea aŋu na mɛ ɛ, níklɔ gi egli la ɛklanɔ ɔ; lɛ ese linu si bɛkɔ níklɔ ɛki banama gi bénu Yudanɛ ɛ, lɛ biaga lɛ biazizi ɔma ʋidi dradra lɔ badzidzi avitene ni tiaʋa a.

english_verse: These two witnesses are the two olive trees and the two lampstands that stand in the presence of the Lord who rules the earth.
translation_verse: Banɔ tiaʋa ba ya gi bɛŋa katsalia ɛ, benu kumu yɔ ise loo olif ye ise tiʋa ni kaniɛ ise tiʋa ɛlɛ gi ilɛɛ ní kesea Ɔkaɛ yɛ aŋu na mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: They have the power to lock up the sky and to keep rain from falling while they are prophesying. And whenever they want to, they can turn water to blood and cause all kinds of terrible troubles on earth.
translation_verse: Kigagla kilɛ ní banɔ ba ya awla si biatsi lifune onu, tɔ dzeni ye ɔkidɔ ní ewe ala klɔ mɛ gi bɛɛpani eboe plɛ ɛ, lɛ kigagla kilɛ ní ba awla si biakyi bèni ʋa gi biadzi ika a, lɛ biaki kunya kakaa elu kwiaba kesea aʋa lipoe kakaa elu gi bɛpɛ́ ɛ.

english_verse: After the two witnesses have finished preaching God's message, the beast that lives in the deep pit will come up and fight against them. It will win the battle and kill them.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi bɛŋa ba katsalia pɔ́ ɛ, kɔ ɔnyraga liɛ klɔ gi adɔ edo ní lizokpo gluglu le me e, aata kigũ ni ba, lɛ aaŋa ba aʋa, lɛ aaye ba.

english_verse: Their bodies will be left lying in the streets of the same great city where their Lord was nailed to a cross. And this city is spiritually like the city of Sodom or the country of Egypt.
translation_verse: Lɛ ba bekpo ʋa biafɛ ní ɔma ʋidi lo lɔ ablɔ ʋa aʋa a, niklɔ gi bɛkla ba Ɔkaɛ ní ose lo su i. Ɔma lɔ klɔ gi bizɛxwa ní abee me si; Sodom ni Egipte e.

english_verse: For three and a half days the people of every nation, tribe, language, and race will stare at the bodies of these two witnesses and refuse to let them be buried.
translation_verse: Lɛ kɔki ewe tata ni kapɛ ɛ, banima dɔni banɔ kakaa mɛ, limade kakaa mɛ, siya ede ede me, ni kese kakaa aʋa biamɔ ba bekpo ʋa gi bafini i, pɔ̀ bátravive ba a.

english_verse: Everyone on earth will celebrate and be happy. They will give gifts to each other, because of what happened to the two prophets who caused them so much trouble.
translation_verse: Lɛ kesea aʋa bezeze ba biamɔ isami ɔkpɛ ba loso, lɛ biaŋa liwe gi biatsisi ba ahanuglɔ ba bidɔ; lɛ ese linu si beboedoplɛ tiaʋa ba ya ɛ, bebu kini ɛki banama gi balɛ ligbã́ nɛ ní kesea aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: But three and a half days later, God will breathe life into their bodies. They will stand up, and everyone who sees them will be terrified.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ ní ewe tata ni kapɛ kede e, Aya afuɛ yɛ ligbã́ nɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ akpɛ ní ba mɛ, lɛ bɛyɔ xe balɛɛ ní ba ikli lɛ aʋa ɛ, lɛ lifu ʋidi itɔ lidɔ ní banama gi bɛmɔ ba aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: The witnesses then heard a loud voice from heaven, saying, “Come up here.” And while their enemies were watching, they were taken up to heaven in a cloud.
translation_verse: Lɛ benu ole gagla ɔtɔ dɔni lifunu ne gi ɛɛpani si, “Mlemu aba niya a.” Lɛ bemu itrɛ lifunu ne ní aplɛ la mɛ ɛ, lɛ ba ahwafianɛ ma beedi ba ɛtsisi i.

english_verse: At that same moment there was a terrible earthquake that destroyed a tenth of the city. Seven thousand people were killed, and the rest were frightened and praised the God who rules in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ ní ɔnyɔsasa lɔ klɔ mɛ ɛ, kesea kɛhwa gaglaa, lɛ ɔma ʋidi no kapɛ liɔfɔa kɛkpa ɛdɔ kesea a, lɛ banɔ akpe tiegloele betse ní kesea kuhwahwa mɛ ɛ, lifu ne liku banɔ gɔgɔ ba nyani nyani, lɛ bɛkɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ɛki lifunu ne Aya ɛ.

english_verse: The second horrible thing has now happened! And the third one will be here soon. The Seventh Trumpet
translation_verse: Baba ʋlaɛ yɛ onu aza, lɛ ɔtanɛ ɛ atrába kpekpemi. Beme Kakuklui gloelea

english_verse: At the sound of the seventh trumpet, loud voices were heard in heaven. They said, “Now the kingdom of this world belongs to our Lord and to his Chosen One! And he will rule forever and ever!”
translation_verse: Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gloelee eme yɛ kakuklui ya, lɛ ile gagla iyi ní lifunu ne gi iipani si, “Kesea aʋa kukusi yo kwidzi blɔ Ɔkaɛ ni yɛ Kristo ye kunɛ ɛ, lɛ aaŋa aʋa kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.”

english_verse: They said, “Lord God All-Powerful, you are and you were, and we thank you. You used your great power and started ruling.
translation_verse: lɛ bɛpani si, “Kwido wɔ ɖase Ɔkaɛ, Aya kigaglaɛ petee onete e, wɔ ɔniliɛ gi wɔlɛ ɛ, xe woze e, lɛ ese linu si wɔkɔ wɔ kigagla ʋidi ye, lɛ wokpese kukusi yo kuŋa a.

english_verse: When the nations got angry, you became angry too! Now the time has come for the dead to be judged. It is time for you to reward your servants the prophets and all your people who honor your name, no matter who they are. It is time to destroy everyone who has destroyed the earth.”
translation_verse: Kesea aʋa kuseo kwiyeʋi isu, lɛ ese linu si wɔ kusuyeʋi gaglaa kɔ lipoe le lina. Lipoe le lina gi wɔaxwa litsi ni bekpo ʋa a; gi wɔatsa liʋɛ ki wɔ banɔsumu ba a, gi benu beboedoplɛ ba ɛ, ni wɔ banɔ dradra ba ani banama gi bizɛkpɛ kubùbù ní wɔ linyine su u, banuvɔ ni banɔtsitsi, tɔ wɔakyrɔ banama gi bezizi kesea ɛ.”

english_verse: The door to God's temple in heaven was then opened, and the sacred chest could be seen inside the temple. I saw lightning and heard roars of thunder. The earth trembled and huge hailstones fell to the ground.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛdra Aya yɛ lisumugba ɛlɛ, gi lilɛ ní lifunu ne e, lɛ ní lisumugba lɛ mɛ ɛ, bɛmɔ yɛ kunyanyaa kɔ aɖaka yɛ. Lɛ dzeni ye atsí kifu yɛ lɛ dzeni ye alàʋi lɛ egu i, lɛ kesea kɛhwa xe liwɔ ʋidi itɔ ni amukla na kyɛ bidɔ ɛ.

english_verse: She was about to give birth, and she was crying because of the great pain.
translation_verse: limɛ lilɛ ní yɛ su, lɛ azeenimi gi si yipɔ̀ loso o.

english_verse: Something else appeared in the sky. It was a huge red dragon with seven heads and ten horns, and a crown on each of its seven heads.
translation_verse: Lɛ gyesi dzedze aŋwi ní lifune dzɛ ɛ, kenia mɛ ɔgblaga kyikyi ʋidi ɔtɔ gi etukpo tegloele ni asia liɔfɔ alɛ yɛ ɛ, ekume ekusikuto tegloele, tile ní yɛ litukpo kakaa.

english_verse: With its tail, it dragged a third of the stars from the sky and threw them down to the earth. Then the dragon turned toward the woman, because it wanted to eat her child as soon as it was born.
translation_verse: Akɔ yɛ opi no ehe badzidziebi ʋa kapɛ tanɛa ní lifune aŋwia ɛkpɛ ní kesea. Lɛ kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ ɔlɛɛ ní ɔdzɛɛ gi atrápɔ ɛ, yɛ ɔtɔ nɔ tɔ yivu obi ye tà xegi bɛpɔ yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The woman gave birth to a son, who would rule all nations with an iron rod. The boy was snatched away. He was taken to God and placed on his throne.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ apɔ obinyime, gi yɛ atráŋa banima petee aʋa ni ɔwla gagla a. Pɔ̀ bɛʋã obi ye tsinɔ ní Aya klɔ ní Aya yɛ likusigbo le su u.

english_verse: The woman ran into the desert to a place that God had prepared for her. There she would be taken care of for
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔdzɛɛ ese atrɛ ní ɔyaya klɔ niklɔ gi Aya abla aplɛ aki yɛ ɛ, gi biagani yɛ ɛ, ewe akpe tole alafa tiaʋa ni aviteglo.

english_verse: A war broke out in heaven. Michael and his angels were fighting against the dragon and its angels.
translation_verse: Lɛ kigũ kidɔ ní lifunu ne e: Mixael ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba bɛta kigũ yɛ ni kenia mɛ ɔgblaga ʋidi ye, lɛ ɔgblaga ʋidi ye ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba kyɛ bɛta kigũ yɛ ni ba ɛ.

english_verse: But the dragon lost the battle. It and its angels were forced out of their places in heaven
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kigaglaɛ kíze ní kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ su ebono lɛ bɛkpikpini ba ní ba ozeklɔ ní lifunu ne e.

english_verse: and were thrown down to the earth. Yes, that old snake and his angels were thrown out of heaven! That snake, who fools everyone on earth, is known as the devil and Satan.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛŋwia kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba petee ekpɛ ní kesea ɛ. Yɛ onu ɔgblaga kokoe, gi bizɛxwa si: ozimizi ye loo Satana a, gi yɛ eemeni kelea petee e. Bɛŋwia yɛ ɛkpɛ kesea aʋa, yɛ ni yɛ baxwɛdzɛ ba petee.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven shout, “Our God has shown his saving power, and his kingdom has come! God's own Chosen One has shown his authority. Satan accused our people in the presence of God day and night. Now he has been thrown out!
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole gagla ɔtɔ ní lifunu ne gi ɛɛpani si, “Abla ɛ, blɔ Aya yɛ kubúbú yo ni yɛ kigaglaɛ ni yɛ kaʋaŋaa kwiba, lɛ yɛ Kristo ye yɛ kigaglaɛ ki ba a. Lɛ ese linu si ɔniliɛ gi izɛɛtsa blɔ benemi ba onu i, gi izɛɛtsa ba onu ní blɔ Aya yɛ aŋu mɛ elesi ni kemize e, bɛŋwia yɛ ɛkpɛ ní kesea.

english_verse: “Our people defeated Satan because of the blood of the Lamb and the message of God. They were willing to give up their lives.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛŋa yɛ aʋa gani keʋesibi ya ka ika lɛ ani gani ba katsalia eboe la mɛ ɛ, lɛ bálɔ ba ligbã́ nɛ gbɛgbɛ si biase ní kutseo onu u.

english_verse: “The heavens should rejoice, together with everyone who lives there. But pity the earth and the sea, because the devil was thrown down to the earth. He knows his time is short, and he is very angry.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, isami iku mlo, mlɔ lifunu ne kuseo ni mlɔ banama gi mlalɛ ní kɔ mɛ ɛ. Pɔ̀ babaa ki kesea ni axu la: lɛ ese linu si Ozimizi ye eple eku ní mlɔ aʋa a! lɛ kusuyeʋio kwiku ye nyani nyani; lɛ ese linu si, ete si yɛ lipoe le lídze tɔ ɔ.”

english_verse: When the dragon realized it had been thrown down to the earth, it tried to make trouble for the woman who had given birth to a son.
translation_verse: Lɛ gi kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ amɔ si, bɛŋwia yɛ ɛkpɛ ní kesea aʋa ɛ, eti ɔdzɛɛ gi apɔ obinyime e lideke.

english_verse: But the woman was given two wings like those of a huge eagle, so she could fly into the desert. There she would escape from the snake and be taken care of for a time, two times, and half a time.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bɛkɔ kadzi ʋidi atɔ ka siwlasasa sɛ ede taʋa ɛki ɔdzɛɛ, tɔ aapruru trɛ ní ɔyaya kɔ ɔkutɔ, níklɔ gi bɛbla ɛplɛ ki yɛ ɛ, xe biadi yɛ aʋa a agɔ tata ni kapɛ, níklɔ gi kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ ɔ́tratani yɛ kuna ɛ.

english_verse: But the earth helped her and swallowed the water that had come from the dragon's mouth.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ kesea kɛɛpɔni ɔdzɛɛ, gi kesea kɛla onugu lo me, lɛ kɛŋwɛ kuni yo gi kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ api adɔ ni yɛ onugu lo me e.

english_verse: This made the dragon terribly angry with the woman. So it started a war against the rest of her children. They are the people who obey God and are faithful to what Jesus did and taught.
translation_verse: Lɛ isu iyeʋi ɔgblaga yɛ nyani nyani ni ɔdzɛɛ, lɛ asɛ atrɛ si yizɛɛta kigũ ni ɔdzɛɛ yɛ lipo me bebi gɔgɔ ba ɛ, ba benu banama gi bizenu kutu iiki Aya yɛ isã nɛ ɛ, xe bevu Yesu yɛ katsalia mɛ ní awla ɛ.

english_verse: The dragon stood on the beach beside the sea.
translation_verse: Lɛ kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ alɛɛ ní axu la kutu yɔ ɛ.

english_verse: I looked and saw a beast coming up from the sea. This one had ten horns and seven heads, and a crown was on each of its ten horns. On each of its heads were names that were an insult to God.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ ɔnyraga ɔtɔ gi eedo edɔ ní axu la mɛ ɛ. Asia liɔfɔ ni etukpo tegloele alɛ yɛ, lɛ ekusikuto liɔfɔ alɛ ní yɛ asia na aʋa a, lɛ ní yɛ litukpo kakaa aʋa ɛ, bɛŋwlimi libusionyi itrɔ.

english_verse: The beast I saw had the body of a leopard, the feet of a bear, and the mouth of a lion. The dragon handed over its own power and throne and great authority to this beast.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔga liɛ gi mamɔ ɛ, ɔlɛ ko ŋwisi abosokpo ye kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ yɛ ikli lɛ ilɛ ŋwisi sisiblisi ye yɛ ikli kinɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ onugu lo ɔlɛ ŋwisi gyata yɛ onugu kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ kenia mɛ ɔgblaga ʋidi ye e, akɔ yɛ kigaglaɛ, yɛ likusigbo le, ni yɛ ɔlɛɛkɔ gyɔgyɔ lɔ aki ɔnyraga yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: One of its heads seemed to have been fatally wounded, but now it was well. Everyone on earth marveled at this beast,
translation_verse: Ɔgaɛ yɛ litukpo tile le e, liŋwi ŋwisi libi gi nya liaki si aatse kinɛ ɛ bɛkpɛ yɛ, pɔ̀ libi le lítse e. Lɛ kelea petee onugu ètse lɛ bekuni ɔnyraga yɛ kude e.

english_verse: and they worshiped the dragon who had given its authority to the beast. They also worshiped the beast and said, “No one is like this beast! No one can fight against it.”
translation_verse: Lɛ banima bɛkpɛ litukpo kese ɛki kenia mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ, lɛ ese linu si akɔ yɛ kigaglaɛ aki ɔnyraga yɛ, lɛ bɛkpɛ litukpo kese ɛki ɔnyraga yɛ kyɛ lɛ beʋi si, nyaŋwɛ ɔlɛ ŋwisi ɔnyraga yɛ kinɛ ɛ? Lɛ nyaŋwɛ aatani kigũ kuta ni yɛ ɛ?

english_verse: The beast cursed God, and it cursed the name of God. It even cursed the place where God lives, as well as everyone who lives in heaven with God.
translation_verse: Lɛ adra yɛ onugu lo xe ekpese ebusioboe la kupani ní Aya su u, lɛ akpɛ Aya yɛ linyi ne, nì yɛ ozeklɔ, kɔpɔni banama petee gi balɛ ní lifune e, kakpaa.

english_verse: It was allowed to fight against God's people and defeat them. It was also given authority over the people of every tribe, nation, language, and race.
translation_verse: Bɛki yɛ kigaglaɛ tɔ ɔta kigũ ni banɔ dradra ba, atɔ ɔŋa ba aʋa, lɛ bɛki yɛ kigagla ní limade kakaa, ni banɔ kakaa, ni siya kakaa, kɔpɔni kese kakaa aʋa.

english_verse: The beast was worshiped by everyone whose name wasn't written before the time of creation in the book of the Lamb who was killed.
translation_verse: Lɛ kesea aʋa bezeze ba petee biasumu ye e. Ba benu banama petee gi báŋwlimi ba enyi na ákpɛ ní ligbã́ nɛ keplekpaa mɛ, dɔni kelea okpese klɔ ɔ. Keplekpaa lia kenu keʋesibi ya lia gi beye e, kanɛ.

english_verse: If you have ears, then listen!
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ klɔ gi kutu kulɛ yɛ ɛ, tɔ onu!

english_verse: If you are doomed to be captured, you will be captured. If you are doomed to be killed by a sword, you will be killed by a sword. This means God's people must learn to endure and be faithful!
translation_verse: Xe lilɛ si ɔnutɔ ɔtrɛ ligba ɛ, ligba lɛ ko aatrɛ. xe lilɛ si beye ɔnutɔ ni ihɛ gblamadzaa ɛ, ihɛ gblamadzaa yɛ ko biakɔ ye ye. Liboe lɛ ya liziã litɔplɛplɛkese ni igyidodo ani anukwarɛɛ dɔni banɔdradra ba klɔ. Ɔnyraga gi edo ádɔ ní Kesea mɛ ɛ

english_verse: I now saw another beast. This one came out of the ground. It had two horns like a lamb, but spoke like a dragon.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ ɔnyraga dzedze gi edo adɔ ní kesea mɛ ɛ. Asia taʋa alɛ yɛ ŋwisi keʋesibi kinɛ ɛ, pɔ̀ apani ŋwisi kenia mɛ ɔgblaga ʋidi kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: It worked for the beast whose fatal wound had been healed. And it used all its authority to force the earth and its people to worship that beast.
translation_verse: Lɛ awa ɔnyraga tɔpu yɛ yɛ kigaglaɛ petee su axwɛ ní yɛ onu u. Lɛ aki si kesea ni ka aʋa banima, besumu ɔnyraga tɔpu yɛ gi yɛ bɛkpɛ lìbi le e, gi nya liaki si otse xe e, pɔ̀ xe otse e.

english_verse: It worked mighty miracles, and while people watched, it even made fire come down from the sky.
translation_verse: Lɛ abitɛ enugutseboe ʋidi na, gi aki si kifu kidɔ ní lifunu ne dɔ ní kesea aʋa ní banima aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: This second beast fooled people on earth by working miracles for the first one. Then it talked them into making an idol in the form of the beast that did not die after being wounded by a sword.
translation_verse: Kɔkpɛ gi bɛki yɛ kigaglaɛ si ɔbitɛ eboa la ya elu ní ɔnyraga tɔpu yɛ onu loso e, emeni kesea aʋa banima. Aki ba ɔsã si bedu litugu kí ɔnyraga tɔpu yɛ gi yɛ su bɛkpɛ libi le ni ihɛ gblamadza yɛ pɔ̀ xe ɔlɛ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, tɔ bakɔ akpɛ kubùbù ní yɛ su u.

english_verse: It was allowed to put breath into the idol, so it could speak. Everyone who refused to worship the idol of the beast was put to death.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛki yɛ kigagla si ɔki litugu inɛ gi bedu iiki ɔnyraga tɔpu yɛ, Ɔfuɛfuɛ tɔ lipani i, ani tɔ liki si beye banama petee badimɛ si beesumu ɔnyragaɛ yɛ litugu ne e.

english_verse: All people were forced to put a mark on their right hand or forehead. Whether they were powerful or weak, rich or poor, free people or slaves,
translation_verse: Lɛ emi ní ɔnɔ kakaa aʋa, banɔtsitsi ni banuvɔ, bexonete ni bazianete, banɔdzidzi ni banɔ gi balɛ ní ba ɔtutɔ isuwla mɛ, lɛ aki si bakpɛ ɔnɔ kakaa lixaŋu ní yɛ kaŋawla mɛ loo ní yɛ ketu ya,

english_verse: they all had to have this mark, or else they could not buy or sell anything. This mark stood for the name of the beast and for the number of its name.
translation_verse: atɔ ɔnɔnɔ ɔkitani bidɔ kudzi loo tɔ ɔdà bidɔ ɔ, dzaa si ɔnyraga yɛ yɛ lixaŋu nɛ gi linu yɛ linyine e loo yɛ aŋwi na ɛ, alɛ ní ɔniliɛ klɔ ʋa su u.

english_verse: You need wisdom to understand the number of the beast! But if you are smart enough, you can figure this out. Its number is
translation_verse: Lɛ ya liziã kuboete. Xe kuboesenu kulɛ ní ɔnutɔ awla ɛ, ogyo ɔnyraga yɛ yɛ aŋwi na odi i, lɛ ese linu si linu ɔnɔ linyi gi lɛ aŋwi na enu alafa tieglo aviteglo ni teglo.

english_verse: Then I heard a sound from heaven that was like a roaring flood or loud thunder or even like the music of harps.
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ gi ɛdɔ ní lifunu ne ŋwisi onipe gi ɛdɔ limu kinɛ ɛ, ni dzeni kugugu gagla ɔtɔ kinɛ ɛ. Ole lo gi menu i, ɔlɛ ŋwisi ɔsangu beʋéʋe gi beeʋe ba isangu ne kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And a new song was being sung in front of God's throne and in front of the four living creatures and the elders. No one could learn that song, except the
translation_verse: Bɛzɛzà ɔdzi vuvu ɔtɔ ní likusigbo le ɔtɔ nɔ ní bidɔgbagbe tune ʋe kɔpɔni banɔtsitsi ba aŋu mɛ. Lɛ ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani ɔdzi nɔ kukpasi i, sani banɔ akpe alafa tole avitene ni tiene ba gi bebu idɔni kesea aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: All of these are pure virgins, and they follow the Lamb wherever he leads. They have been rescued to be presented to God and the Lamb as the most precious people on earth.
translation_verse: Banɔ ba ya benu banama gi bákpɛ ɔdzɛ ha, lɛ bélulu isu ya ni badzɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si ba isu ya idra a. Banɔ ba ya ɛ, bizeekuni Keʋesibi ya kude tsinɔ ní ɔkɔ kakaa gi izɛtrɛ ɛ. Banɔ ba ya bedzi dɔni banima kusi i, xe bɛkɔ ba ŋwisi bidɔkɔkɔ tɔpu ki Aya ni Keʋesibi ya a.

english_verse: I saw another angel. This one was flying across the sky and had the eternal good news to announce to the people of every race, tribe, language, and nation on earth.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ dzedze gi eepruru ní liwɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ, gi evu liboe gbã moblo le ní awla a, si yipani iiki banama gi balɛ ní kesea aʋa a, ki kese kakaa, ki limade kakaa, ki siya lide kakaa ni banɔ kakaa.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Worship and honor God! The time has come for him to judge everyone. Kneel down before the one who created heaven and earth, the oceans, and every stream.”
translation_verse: Lɛ apani ni ole gagla si, “Mlese Aya, lɛ mlakɔ kusudraa aki yɛ; lɛ ese linu si yɛ litsixwaxwa nɛ lipoe le lina, lɛ mlesumu ɔniliɛ klɔ gi yɛ abitɛ lifunu ne e, ni kesea ani axu la kɔpɔni inide ede ede ne.”

english_verse: A second angel followed and said, “The great city of Babylon has fallen! This is the city that made all nations drunk and immoral. Now God is angry, and Babylon has fallen.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ʋla ɛ, ekuni yɛ kude lɛ apani si, “Egleʋe, Egleʋe, Babilonia ʋidi ye Egleʋe, yɛ gi yɛ aki si kuseo petee kwiŋwɛ yɛ kihasiwawaɛ kɛ kuwleda ɛ.”

english_verse: Finally, a third angel came and shouted: Here is what will happen if you worship the beast and the idol and have the mark of the beast on your hand or forehead.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ tanɛ, ekuni ba kude lɛ apani ni ole gagla si, “Xe ɔnutɔ esumu ɔnyraga yɛ ni yɛ litugu ne e, xe azɛ yɛ lixaŋu nɛ ní yɛ ketu ya loo ní yɛ kawla mɛ ɛ,

english_verse: You will have to drink the wine that God gives to everyone who makes him angry. You will feel his mighty anger, and you will be tortured with fire and burning sulfur, while the holy angels and the Lamb look on.
translation_verse: Aya aayeʋi isu ni ye, lɛ yɛ kyɛ aŋwɛ Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio kuda, gi bátsa ní yɛ kusuyeʋio kepliʋia mɛ ɛ. Lɛ biakɔ kifu ni kifu akla kɔwa yɛ nyɔnyɔɛ ní Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ dradra ba ani keʋesibi ya aŋu mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: If you worship the beast and the idol and accept the mark of its name, you will be tortured day and night. The smoke from your torture will go up forever and ever, and you will never be able to rest.
translation_verse: Lɛ ba kinie xunyɔ ɛ, kwiado trɛ ní lifunu ne tsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ. Lɛ kuzemɔni kúma elesi ni kemize ki banama gi besumu ɔnyragaɛ ni yɛ litugu ne e, loo ki banama gi bɛzɛ yɛ linyi ne lɛ lixaŋu nɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: God's people must learn to endure. They must also obey his commands and have faith in Jesus.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya liziã litɔplɛplɛkese kese ni igyidodo dɔni banɔ dradra ba, gi bizeenu kutu iiki Aya isã nɛ ɛ, xe bevu kuziŋa ni Yesu me ní awla ɛ.

english_verse: Then I heard a voice from heaven say, “Put this in writing. From now on, the Lord will bless everyone who has faith in him when they die.” The Spirit answered, “Yes, they will rest from their hard work, and they will be rewarded for what they have done.” The Earth Is Harvested
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ dɔni lifunu ne gi ɛɛpani si i, “Ŋwlimi si: Biakrira banɔ ba ya gi betse ní Ɔkaɛ mɛ ɛ, dɔni lɛ ya aʋa kɔtsinɔ ɛ.” “Ee” Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛɛpani si, “biaazemɔni ní ba axwɛwawa la mɛ, lɛ ese linu si ba axwɛwawa la aazeni ba kude.” Litsi ʋidi le

english_verse: I looked and saw a bright cloud, and someone who seemed to be the Son of Man was sitting on the cloud. He wore a gold crown on his head and held a sharp sickle in his hand.
translation_verse: Gi meedi i, mamɔ liplɛ pititi itɔ, lɛ ɔnutɔ odi ní lɛ aʋa ŋwisi Ɔnɔɛ Yebi ye kinɛ ɛ, sika yɛ likuto le lilɛ ní yɛ litukpo le, lɛ evu ihɛ gi bizɛkɔ ɛtsa amu ní awla la a.

english_verse: The one on the cloud swung his sickle and harvested the crops.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi odi ní liplɛ lɛ aʋa ɛ, aŋwia yɛ ihɛ atsisi ní kesea aʋa ɛ, lɛ atsa kesea aʋa bidɔkpɛkpɛ ʋɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Another angel with a sharp sickle then came out of the temple in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ dzedze edo ádɔ ní lisɔligba ɛlɛ, gi lilɛ ní lifunu ne me e, gi yɛ fɛ evu kaláʋi nunu atɔ ní awla a.

english_verse: After this, an angel with power over fire came from the altar and shouted to the angel who had the sickle. He said, “All grapes on earth are ripe! Harvest them with your sharp sickle.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ dzedze ɔtɔ dzɛ, gi yɛ awla kigaglaɛ kilɛ ní kifu yɛ aʋa ɛ, edo ádɔ ní ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ su, lɛ akpɛ ozi ni ole gagla atsisi ɔniliɛ klɔ gi evu kaláʋia ní awla la si, “Kɔ̀ wɔ kaláʋia tɔ gu kesea aʋa wain yoyoe yɛ asà la; lɛ ese linu si wain yɛ esebi la ɛzã!”

english_verse: The angel swung his sickle on earth and cut off its grapes. He threw them into a pit where they were trampled on as a sign of God's anger.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ aŋwia yɛ kaláʋia atsisi ní kesea aʋa ɛ, lɛ agu kesea aʋa wain yoyoe, lɛ bɛkɔ yɛ ɛkpɛ ní Aya yɛ kusuyeʋio kɔ wain yɛ ɔka kɔ ʋidi lo me e.

english_verse: The pit was outside the city, and when the grapes were mashed, blood flowed out. The blood turned into a river that was about
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛpita wain yoyoe ni ikli lɛ ní ɔma nɔ ese e, lɛ ika lɛ ido ní wain yɛ ɔka klɔ ɛ, gi idzu ŋwisi ɔnɔ liŋwladɔ̃ kinɛ ɛ, gi bɛ kidzedzee me kina itiplɛkɔ alafa tiaʋa a.

english_verse: After this, I looked at the sky and saw something else that was strange and important. Seven angels were bringing the seven last terrible troubles. When these are ended, God will no longer be angry.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ gyesi ʋidi dzedze ní lifunu ne, gi liki si mɛ onugu etse e. Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele gi bevu baxixa tiegloele gɔgɔ ʋa ní awlaa. Ba ya benu ba gɔgɔ ʋa lɛ ese linu si gani ba mɛ ɛ, kɔ Aya yɛ kusuyeʋi gaglaa kɔ onu kwidu i.

english_verse: Then I saw something that looked like a glass sea mixed with fire, and people were standing on it. They were the ones who had defeated the beast and the idol and the number that tells the name of the beast. God had given them harps,
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ kiditɔ gi kilɛ ŋwisi axu la a, pɔ̀ alɛ ŋwisi azinzi yɛ kinɛ ɛ, gi yɛ mɛ azã kyikyii ŋwisi kifu kinɛ ɛ. Lɛ mamɔ banɔ batɔ gi balɛ ni axu la kutu yɔ ɛ, ba benu banama gi bɛŋa ɔŋuga yɛ ni yɛ litugu ne ani yɛ linyi ne lɛ aaŋwi na aʋa a. Bevu isangu ne gi Aya aki ba ní awla a.

english_verse: and they were singing the song his servant Moses and the Lamb had sung. They were singing, “Lord God All-Powerful, you have done great and marvelous things. You are the ruler of all nations, and you do what is right and fair.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛza Mose, Aya yɛ ɔnɔsumu ye yɛ ɔdzi nɔ ani Keʋesibi ya ka ɔdzi nɔ si, “Ɔka, Aya kigaglaɛ petee onete, wɔ axwɛwawa la ekeme lɛ enu enugutseboe. Wɔ bede ʋa benu bede gi bɛgyɔ ɔ, lɛ benu anukwarɛɛ, Wɔ wonu kelea petee Okusi ye lipoe kakaa.

english_verse: Lord, who doesn't honor and praise your name? You alone are holy, and all nations will come and worship you, because you have shown that you judge with fairness.”
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ, nyaŋwɛ ɔlɛ gi ɔ́trase wo o, xe ɔ́trakpɔ̀ wɔ linyi ne e? Lɛ ese linu si wɔ tole ko wɔdra. Ima nɛ petee iaba wɔ aŋu mɛ ɛ, lɛ iaba sumu wo, lɛ ese linu si bebu wɔ kiboebitɛ gyɔgyɔ ɛbasɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: After this, I noticed something else in heaven. The sacred tent used for a temple was open.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, medi, lɛ mamɔ si bɛdra lisɔligba lɛ, gi linu kusɔliɔ kɔ likatsa lɛ ɛ.

english_verse: And the seven angels who were bringing the terrible troubles were coming out of it. They were dressed in robes of pure white linen and wore belts made of pure gold.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele ba, gi bevu baxixa tiegloele ʋa ní awla ɛ, bedo edɔ ní lisɔligba lɛ mɛ. Bɛkpɛ ewu pititi gi ɛɛdra a, xe eeŋle e, lɛ bɛkɔ sika asaba ɛklani ba ete na mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: One of the four living creatures gave each of the seven angels a bowl made of gold. These bowls were filled with the anger of God who lives forever and ever.
translation_verse: Lɛ bidɔgbagbe tune ʋe ɔmɛ tole akɔ sika kubɔziʋi tugloele aki Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele ba ɛ. Kubɔziʋi tugloele uliɔ ya mɛ Aya gi ɔlɛ ligba nɛ kɔtsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ, yɛ kusuyeʋio kukpasi.

english_verse: The temple quickly filled with smoke from the glory and power of God. No one could enter it until the seven angels had finished pouring out the seven last troubles.
translation_verse: Lɛ kunyɔɔ, gi kwidɔ Aya yɛ kuklekleo ni yɛ kigaglaɛ mɛ ɛ, kwiyi lisumugba lɛ mɛ, nite me si ɔnɔnɔ ɔ́tani lisɔligba lɛ mɛ kutiku, zɛkɔna ni lipoe lɛ gi baxixa ʋa gi Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele ba bevu i, ba onu ɛza.

english_verse: From the temple I heard a voice shout to the seven angels, “Go and empty the seven bowls of God's anger on the earth.”
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole gagla ɔtɔ dɔni lisɔligba lɛ mɛ gi ɛɛsi Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele ba si i, “Mlatrɛ tɔ mlazeenye Aya yɛ kusuyeʋi yo kubɔziʋi tugloele ko abali ní kesea aʋa.”

english_verse: The first angel emptied his bowl on the earth. At once ugly and painful sores broke out on everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ tɔpu yɛ atrɛ ɛ, lɛ enye yɛ kabɔziʋia abali ní kesea aʋa ɛ, lɛ banima bɛdɔ ebì gi ezeyeʋi gagla ɛ, ba banama su gi ɔŋuga yɛ yɛ gyesi ye ɔlɛ ɛ ani banama gi besumu yɛ litugu ne e.

english_verse: The second angel emptied his bowl on the sea. At once the sea turned into blood like that of a dead person, and every living thing in the sea died.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ ʋla yɛ enye yɛ kabɔziʋia abali ní axu la aʋa ɛ, lɛ kuni yo kwikyi dzi ika ŋwisi okpo ɔtɔ yɛ ika kinɛ ɛ, lɛ kidɔ gbagbe kakaa gi kilɛ ní axu la mɛ ɛ kitse.

english_verse: The third angel emptied his bowl into the rivers and streams. At once they turned to blood.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ tanɛɛ enye yɛ kabɔziʋia abali ní inipe le ani inide ne aʋa ɛ, lɛ bikyí dzi ika ɛ.

english_verse: Then I heard the angel, who has power over water, say, “You have always been, and you always will be the holy God. You had the right to judge in this way.
translation_verse: Lɛ menu gi Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ liɛ gi odi ní bèni ʋa onu i ɛɛpani si, “Wɔ linɛ ligyɔ ní litsi tsatsa ɛlɛ ya mɛ. Wɔ gi wɔlɛ ɛ, xe woze e, wɔ Ɔnɔ dradra yɛ, lɛ ese linu si nite wɔtsa litsi le e;

english_verse: They poured out the blood of your people and your prophets. So you gave them blood to drink as they deserve!”
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si bɛbali wɔ banɔ dradra ba ni wɔ beboedoplɛ ba ba ika lɛ, lɛ wɔki ba ika lɛ, tɔ baŋwɛ ŋwisi kiliɛ gi ligyeni ba kinɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: After this, I heard the altar shout, “Yes, Lord God All-Powerful, your judgments are honest and fair.”
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ dɔni ɔdɔpɔʋisuʋɔ lɔ mɛ gi ɛɛpani si i, “Ee, Ɔkaɛ Aya kigaglaɛ petee ɔkaɛ, wɔ etsixwaxwa la enu anukwarɛɛ ni eboe gyɔgyɔ.”

english_verse: The fourth angel emptied his bowl on the sun, and it began to scorch people like fire.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ néne ye enye yɛ kabɔziʋia abali ní liwo le aʋa ɛ, lɛ bɛki liwo le kigagla tɔ, likani banima nì kifu u.

english_verse: Everyone was scorched by its great heat, and all of them cursed the name of God who had power over these terrible troubles. But no one turned to God and praised him.
translation_verse: Lɛ liwo le lɛ kuhuhu yo kwikani banima ɛ, lɛ bexo ifi ikpɛ Aya yɛ linyi ne, Aya gi yɛ amɔ kigagla ní baxixa ʋa ya aʋa ɛ, pɔ̀ lɛ badimɛ si bekyi ní ba ina nɛ mɛ, xe biakɔ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀ ki yɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The fifth angel emptied his bowl on the throne of the beast. At once darkness covered its kingdom, and its people began biting their tongues in pain.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ tsunɛɛ enye yɛ kabɔziʋia abali ní ɔŋuga yɛ yɛ likusigbo le aʋa ɛ, lɛ yɛ kaʋaŋaa kɛdɔ litumu ne e. Lɛ banima bɛtà ba binemi ʋe kɔkpɛ ɔzia mɔmɔ nɔ loso o,

english_verse: And because of their painful sores, they cursed the God who rules in heaven. But still they did not stop doing evil things.
translation_verse: lɛ bexo ifi le ekpɛ lifunu ne Aya a, kɔkpɛ ba kuyeʋiyeʋi yo ni ba ebi la loso, pɔ̀ kyɛ ɛ, badimɛ si bekyi ní ba kiboebitɛɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The sixth angel emptied his bowl on the great Euphrates River, and it completely dried up to make a road for the kings from the east.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ glonɛɛ enye yɛ kabɔziʋia abali ní Eufrate onipe ʋidi lo aʋa ɛ, lɛ lɔ mɛ kuni yo kwiti i, tɔ bialulɔ kudeo aki kewetu ya bekusi ba.

english_verse: An evil spirit that looked like a frog came out of the mouth of the dragon. One also came out of the mouth of the beast, and another out of the mouth of the false prophet.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ ifuɛfuɛ zimizi tita, gi ilɛ ŋwisi akpɔkplɔ ʋa kinɛ ɛ, gi ido edɔ ní keni ya mɛ ɔgblaga yɛ yɛ onugu lo me e, ni ɔŋuga yɛ yɛ onugu lo me, ani asia na oboedoplɛɛ yɛ onugu lo me.

english_verse: These evil spirits had the power to work miracles. They went to every king on earth, to bring them together for a war against God All-Powerful. But that will be the day of God's great victory.
translation_verse: Inu ifuɛfuɛ zimizi gi izɛɛbitɛ enugutseboe la a, lɛ izɛɛtrɛ ní kelea mɛ bekusi ba klɔ ní kelea petee me e, tɔ bɛkpɛ ba likpɔ ɛki kigũ yɛ ní Aya kigaglaɛ petee pɔ́ Ɔkaɛ, yɛ liwe ʋidi le aʋa a.

english_verse: Remember Christ says, “When I come, it will surprise you like a thief! But God will bless you, if you are awake and ready. Then you won't have to walk around naked and be ashamed.”
translation_verse: “Dike, matraba ŋwisi oze kinɛ ɛ! Biakrira ɔniliɛ klɔ gi izeeze ni aŋu i, xe izɛɛkɔ yɛ ewu la ɛplɛ isu ya su u, tɔ ɔkiga liya, xe biamɔ yɛ kuye me ɛ.”

english_verse: Those armies came together in a place that in Hebrew is called Armagedon.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkpɛ bekusi ba likpɔ ní niklɔ gi bizɛxwa ní si Hebri se me si Armagedon.

english_verse: As soon as the seventh angel emptied his bowl in the air, a loud voice from the throne in the temple shouted, “It's done!”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gloele enye yɛ kabɔziʋia kpɛ ní liwɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ole gagla ɔtɔ eyi dɔni lisɔligba lɛ mɛ ní likusigbo le su si, “Bɛbitɛ lɛ pɔ!”

english_verse: The great city of Babylon split into three parts, and the cities of other nations fell. So God made Babylon drink from the wine cup filled with his anger.
translation_verse: Ɔma ʋidi no ɛlɛ ní kupɛ tuta mɛ, lɛ kesea aʋa ima ʋidi ne iiyaʋi. Lɛ Aya aplɛ aŋu ní Babilonia ʋidi ye aʋa ɛ, lɛ akɔ yɛ kepliʋia gi ka mɛ yɛ kusuyeʋio kuda kulɛ ɛ, lɛ akɔ ka aki yɛ si ɔŋwɛ.

english_verse: Every island ran away, and the mountains disappeared.
translation_verse: Lɛ kunisi yo kese kakaa kɛlila ɛ, lɛ bámɔ litoe tɔtɔ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: One of the seven angels who had emptied the bowls came over and said to me, “Come on! I will show you how God will punish that shameless prostitute who sits on many oceans.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele ba ya awla gi kubɔziʋi tugloeleo kwize e, ba mɛ tole apani asi mɛ si, “Ba tɔ mibasɛ wɔ kutuhehe kuliɔ gi bɛtráki ɔwawadze ʋidi ye e, gi odi ní bèni glegle ʋa aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: Every king on earth has slept with her, and her shameless ways are like wine that has made everyone on earth drunk.”
translation_verse: Kesea aʋa bekusi ba bɛwa kihasi ye ni yɛ, lɛ yɛ kihasi ye kuda ɛ, kwimu banama gi balɛ ní kesea aʋa ɛ.”

english_verse: With the help of the Spirit, the angel took me into a desert, where I saw a woman sitting on a red beast. The beast was covered with names that were an insult to God, and it had seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ efeke me ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ atsini ɔyaya klɔ, lɛ mamɔ ɔdzɛ ɔtɔ gi odi ní ɔŋuga kyikyi ɔtɔ, gi bɛŋwlimi ebusionyi na ní yɛ su petee e, xe etukpo tegloele ni asia liɔfɔ alɛ yɛ aʋa ɛ.

english_verse: The woman was dressed in purple and scarlet robes, and she wore jewelry made of gold, precious stones, and pearls. In her hand she held a gold cup filled with the filthy and nasty things she had done.
translation_verse: Ɔdzɛɛ enyime basa gi bɛzi kedzi, lɛ alulɔ isu ya ni sika ni akla gi ɛɛzi kedzi ani ihwia pititi, lɛ evu sika kadaŋwɛpliʋi, gi keyi ni anyaniboe ni yɛ kihasi ye kɛ eboe kluklu la.

english_verse: On her forehead a mysterious name was written: I AM THE GREAT CITY OF BABYLON, THE MOTHER OF EVERY IMMORAL AND FILTHY THING ON EARTH.
translation_verse: Linyii nɛ gi bɛŋwlimi ni yɛ ketu ya linu si: LIBOE KPOKPO BABILONIA ƲIDI YE BAWAWADZE BA PETEE ONEE NI ANYANIBOE LA NI KESEA AƲA LA ONEE.

english_verse: I could tell that the woman was drunk on the blood of God's people who had given their lives for Jesus. This surprising sight amazed me,
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ si ɔdzɛɛ emu kuda gi kunu banɔ dradra ba ba ika lɛ ɛ, banama gi bɛŋa katsalia ɛki Yesu i, ba ika lɛ. Gi mamɔ yɛ nite e, mawɔ yaa.

english_verse: and the angel said: Why are you so amazed? I will explain the mystery about this woman and about the beast she is sitting on, with its seven heads and ten horns.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ eʋi me si, “Ege lɛ loso wɔwɔ yaa nite e? Ki tɔ mibu liboe kpokpoe lɛ gi lilɛ ní ɔdzɛɛ ni ɔga liɛ gi yɛ aʋa odi i, gi etukpo tegloele ni asia liɔfɔ alɛ yɛ ɛ, ese aki wɔ.

english_verse: The beast you saw is one that used to be and no longer is. It will come back from the deep pit, but only to be destroyed. Everyone on earth whose names were not written in the book of life before the time of creation will be amazed. They will see this beast that used to be and no longer is, but will be once more.
translation_verse: Ɔŋuga yɛ gi wɔmɔ ɛ, eze azanɔ, pɔ̀ ɔ́ma tɔ ɔ, lɛ atrádo edɔ ní lizokpo gluglu le me, lɛ aatrɛ gi iikyrɔ ɛ. Lɛ banama gi balɛ ní kesea aʋa gi báŋwlimi ba enyi na akpɛ ní ligbã́ nɛ keplekpaa mɛ ɛ, dɔni lipoe lɛ gi bɛbitɛ kelea ɛ, ba inugu iatse xe bɛmɔ ɔŋuga yɛ, lɛ ese linu si ɔŋuga yɛ eze edi, pɔ̀ ɔ́ma tɔ ɔ, pɔ aadzi ba dzɛ.

english_verse: Anyone with wisdom can figure this out. The seven heads that the woman is sitting on stand for seven hills. These heads are also seven kings.
translation_verse: “Lɛ ya liziã kuboesenu nu: Etukpo tegloele la enu kutokpa tugloele, gi kɔ aʋa ɔdzɛɛ odi i. Edzì enu bekusi tiegloele:

english_verse: Five of the kings are dead. One is ruling now, and the other one has not yet come. But when he does, he will rule for only a little while.
translation_verse: Bamɛ tietsu bɛwɔli, tole ɔlɛ, ɔgɔgɔɛ ɔʋíba sukɔ, lɛ xegi aba ɛ, lilɛ si ɔŋa kukusi yo lipoe kpekpemi itɔ

english_verse: You also saw a beast that used to be and no longer is. That beast is one of the seven kings who will return as the eighth king, but only to be destroyed.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔŋuga yɛ, gi eze edi i, xe ɔ́ma tɔɔ ɛ, yɛ onu okusi ɔgɔtɔʋa yɛ. Onu tiegloele ba ɔmɛ tole lɛ ɛtrɛ ní kukyrɔkyrɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The ten horns you saw are ten more kings, who have not yet come into power, and they will rule with the beast for only a short time.
translation_verse: “Lɛ asia, liɔfɔa la gi wɔmɔ ɛ enu bekusi liɔfɔ pɔ̀ baʋízɛ ba kukusi yo sukɔ ɔ, pɔ̀ biaki ba kukusi ki ɔnyɔsasa tole ko lɛ biaŋa kukusi yo, kɔpɔni ɔŋuga yɛ.

english_verse: They all think alike and will give their power and authority to the beast.
translation_verse: Litukpo me trɔtrɔ tile lilɛ ní ba awla, lɛ linu si biakɔ ba kigaglaɛ ni ozeklɔ ki ɔŋuga yɛ.

english_verse: These kings will go to war against the Lamb. But he will defeat them, because he is Lord over all lords and King over all kings. His followers are chosen and special and faithful.
translation_verse: Biata kigũ ni Keʋesibi ya, pɔ̀ Keʋesibi ya kiaŋa ba aʋa a; lɛ ese linu si Yɛ onu Baka ba aʋa Ɔkaɛ ni Bekusi ba aʋa Okusi ye. Lɛ banama gi biapɔni yɛ ɛ, ba benu yɛ banɔxwaxwa ba ni yɛ banɔ kyakya ba kɔpɔni banama gi balɛ ni yɛ kude ni anukwarɛɛ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: The oceans you saw the prostitute sitting on are crowds of people from all races and languages.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ Ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi mɛ si, “Kuni yo gi wɔmɔ ɔ, gi kɔ aʋa ɔwawadzee odi i, kunu banɔ, linɔkpi ani kelea mɛ kuse ani siya ede ede.

english_verse: The ten horns and the beast will start hating the shameless woman. They will strip off her clothes and leave her naked. Then they will eat her flesh and throw the rest of her body into a fire.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔŋuga yɛ ni asia liɔfɔa la gi wɔmɔ ɛ, biatrɔ ɔwawadzee ahwafi. Biaki si aakyrɔ lɛ biabu ye liya a, biatà yɛ kisugyaɛ, lɛ biakpɛ yɛ kifu yɛ.

english_verse: God is the one who made these kings all think alike and decide to give their power to the beast. And they will do this until what God has said comes true.
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si Aya akpɛ lɛ ba ina nɛ mɛ, si babitɛ ní Aya yɛ batrɔtrɔ ʋa aʋa, gi ba petee biadimɛ tɔ biakɔ ba kaʋaŋaa aki ɔŋuga yɛ, zɛkɔna ni lipoe lɛ klɔ gi Aya yɛ eboe la aaba la mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The woman you saw is the great city that rules over all kings on earth.
translation_verse: “Ɔdzɛɛ gi wɔmɔ ɛ, yɛ onu ɔma ʋidi ɔ lɔ klɔ, gi ɛɛŋa kukusi yo ní kesea aʋa bekusi ba aʋa a.”

english_verse: I saw another angel come from heaven. This one had great power, and the earth was bright because of his glory.
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, mamɔ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ dzedze gi eeple edɔni lifunu ne e, lɛ ozekɔ ʋidi ɔlɛ ní yɛ awla a, lɛ yɛ kusudraa kwiki si kesea petee keekle e.

english_verse: The angel shouted, “Fallen! Powerful Babylon has fallen and is now the home of demons. It is the den of every filthy spirit and of all unclean birds, and every dirty and hated animal.
translation_verse: Akpɛ ozi ni ole gagla si, “Ayaʋi! Babilonia ʋidi ye ayaʋi! Lɛ ese linu si edzì ifuɛfuɛ zimizi ne e, lɛ ozekɔ, ni ɔfuɛfuɛ mɔdramɔdra kakaa lɔ okpo kɔ, ani kadzɔʋi kyrìkyrì nì ke kluklu kakaa ŋwisi opéte kinɛ ka ɔdɔ kɔ.

english_verse: Babylon's evil and immoral wine has made all nations drunk. Every king on earth has slept with her, and every merchant on earth is rich because of her evil desires.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ese linu si kesea petee kɛŋwɛ yɛ kihasi ye kɛ kuwledaa, lɛ kesea aʋa bekusi ba bɛwa kihasi ye ni yɛ, lɛ kesea aʋa bedzigà ba bedzi bexonete gani yɛ bikesinɔdɔ kpaŋukpaŋu ʋɛ mɛ.”

english_verse: Then I heard another voice from heaven shout, “My people, you must escape from Babylon. Don't take part in her sins and share her punishment.
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole dzedze dɔni lifunu ne gi ɛɛpani si i, “Mledo aza ní yɛ mɛ, mɛ banima, tɔ mlakíkpɛ ɔha ni yɛ kuzimizi yo, atɔ mláʋazɛ yɛ kinii tɔtɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Her sins are piled as high as heaven. God has remembered the evil she has done.
translation_verse: “Lɛ ese linu si yɛ kuzimizi yo kwimu ina lifunu ne e, lɛ Aya aplɛ aŋu ní yɛ eboe kyrikyri la aʋa.

english_verse: Treat her as she has treated others. Make her pay double for what she has done. Make her drink twice as much of what she mixed for others.
translation_verse: Mlabitɛ akpɛ ní lɛ onu aki yɛ, ŋwisi kiliɛ gi yɛ fɛ abitɛ lɛ kinɛ ɛ; mledzini atsa aki yɛ ede taʋa kɔkpɛ kidiliɛ gi abitɛ ɛ. Kaŋwɛpliʋia lia mɛ gi akpɛ aki mlɔ ɛ, mlaki yɛ kɔ tuʋa tɔ ɔŋwɛ.

english_verse: That woman honored herself with a life of luxury. Reward her now with suffering and pain. “Deep in her heart Babylon said, ‘I am the queen! Never will I be a widow or know what it means to be sad.’
translation_verse: Mlebu kinie aki yɛ atɔ mlaki yɛ ɔna tata ŋwisi kubùbù yo ni exo ala gi aki isu ya kinɛ ɛ. Ní yɛ ɔna nɔ mɛ ɛ, izeefeke isu ya si, ‘Medi ŋwisi okusidzɛ; Ménu ɔdzɛ gi yenye etse e, lɛ mátrabemi monye liwewe e.’

english_verse: And so, in a single day she will suffer the pain of sorrow, hunger, and death. Fire will destroy her dead body, because her judge is the powerful Lord God.”
translation_verse: Lɛ loso e, yɛ kini ye kiaba yɛ aʋa ní liwe tile aʋa a: kutse, kube ani kuʋɛ ɛ. Lɛ biakpɛ yɛ kifu gi kiakani yɛ petee, lɛ ese linu si Ɔka Aya, ɔniliɛ gi izɛxwa litsi ni yɛ ɛ, onu ɔnɔ gagla.

english_verse: Every king on earth who slept with her and shared in her luxury will mourn. They will weep, when they see the smoke from that fire.
translation_verse: “Lɛ xe kesea aʋa bekusi ba a, gi bɛwa kihasi ye ni yɛ ɛ, xe bɛŋa yɛ exo la nì yɛ ɛ, xe bɛmɔ yɛ kunyɔɔ ɔ, gi ɛɛsɔ ɛ, biabemi ye lɛ biawa awowoe la ní yɛ litukpo.

english_verse: Her sufferings will frighten them, and they will stand at a distance and say, “Pity that great and powerful city! Pity Babylon! In a single hour her judgment has come.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bialɛɛ ní ɔdzage no ke gi lifu ne liiku ba kɔkpɛ yɛ ɔzia mɔmɔ nɔ lɔ kinie e, gi ɛɛmɔ loso o, lɛ biabemi si, “Babaa, babaa kí wɔ, wɔ ɔma ʋidi no Wɔ Babilonia, wɔ gi wonu ɔma ʋidi ni ɔma gagla! Lɛ ese linu si wɔ kutuheheo kwiba ní ɔnyɔsasa tole me.

english_verse: Every merchant on earth will mourn, because there is no one to buy their goods.
translation_verse: “Lɛ kesea aʋa bedzigà ba biabemi, lɛ biawa awowoe la ní yɛ litukpo, lɛ ese linu si ɔnɔnɔ óʋidzi ba bidɔdada ʋɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: There won't be anyone to buy their gold, silver, jewels, pearls, fine linen, purple cloth, silk, scarlet cloth, sweet-smelling wood, fancy carvings of ivory and wood, as well as things made of bronze, iron, or marble.
translation_verse: Bidɔdadaɛ ʋɛ ya binu: Sika ni klosalo, ani akla gi ɛzi kedzi i, ni ihwia ni akla la plɔplɔ yɛ, ni basa gi bɛzi kedzi i, ni seda yɛ ni ise kakaa elu gi ilɛ flafla ɛ, ni kudɔkpa gi bɛbitɛ ni onyi ye line ne e, ni ise gi izɛ kedzi i, ni ɔla kyikyi yɛ ani ɔla kpikpi yɛ kɔpɔni akla dzedze la a;

english_verse: Babylon, the things your heart desired have all escaped from you. Every luxury and all your glory will be lost forever. You will never get them back.
translation_verse: “Lɛ biapani si ‘wɔ bilésedɔ mɛ, bido ní wɔ awla. Wɔ exo la petee ni wɔ isu ya bilulɔ dɔ mɛ petee bilila gi bitradzì ba liwewe tɔ ɔ.’

english_verse: The merchants had become rich because of her. But when they saw her sufferings, they were terrified. They stood at a distance, crying and mourning.
translation_verse: Bedzigà ba gi bɛzɛda kudɔkpaa liɔ ya ɛ, xe bɛmɔ ba exo la ɛdɔni yɛ klɔ ɛ, bialɛɛ ní ɔkutɔ ke gi lifu ne liiku ba a, gi bɛmɔ yɛ kinimɔmɔɛ ɛ. Biabemi, lɛ biawa awowoe la,

english_verse: Then they shouted, “Pity the great city of Babylon! She dressed in fine linen and wore purple and scarlet cloth. She had jewelry made of gold and precious stones and pearls.
translation_verse: lɛ biasa awla onu si, “ ‘Babaa, babaa ki ɔma ʋidi no! Wɔ gi wɔkpɛ aklala plɔplɔ yɛ ɛ, ni basa kakaa gi bɛzi kedzi i, xe wɔɛtsi kifu yɛ ni sika ni akla a, gi ɛzi kedzi ani ihwia lɛ ɛ!

english_verse: Yet in a single hour her riches disappeared.” Every ship captain and passenger and sailor stood at a distance, together with everyone who does business by traveling on the sea.
translation_verse: Wɔ exo glegle la ezizi ní ɔnyɔsasa tole me!’ “Lɛ initsahu le ɔka kakaa, ni ɔnɔ kakaa gi izekù initsahu le e, ni banama gi bizeku initsahu le e, ni kenia aʋa baxwɛwa ba, ani banama petee gi bizɛga kedzia ní axu la aʋa ɛ, bialɛɛ ní ɔkutɔ ke.

english_verse: When they saw the smoke from her fire, they shouted, “This was the greatest city ever!”
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi bɛmɔ kunyɔ ɛdɔ ni yɛ kukanikani yɔ mɛ ɛ, biakpɛ ozi si, ‘Ɔma ʋidi ɔtɔtɔ ɔ́srɔni ɔma lɔ ya a.’

english_verse: They cried loudly, and in their sorrow they threw dust on their heads, as they said, “Pity the great city of Babylon! Everyone who sailed the seas became rich from her treasures. But in a single hour the city was destroyed.
translation_verse: Lɛ biakpɛ ovugbo lo ní ba siya sɛ mɛ gi bɛwa awowoe la ɛ, lɛ biakpɛ ozi xe biazebemi si i, “‘Babaa! Babaa kí wɔ, ɔma ʋidi no, gi lɔ mɛ banama petee awla gi initsahu le ize ni axu la aʋa ɛ, bekyi edzì bexonete gani yɛ exo la aʋa! Lɛ ní ɔnyɔsasa tole me e, bɛkyrɔ yɛ.

english_verse: A powerful angel then picked up a huge stone and threw it into the sea. The angel said, “This is how the great city of Babylon will be thrown down, never to rise again.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ gagla ɔtɔ efeke liklakpɔ ʋidi itɔ gi likeme ŋwisi lihɔkla kinɛ ɛ, lɛ aŋwia lɛ akpɛ ní axu la mɛ, lɛ apani si: Anyra ala elu bɛtrákɔ ɛŋwia Babilonia ɔma ʋidi no ɛkpɛ kesea, lɛ batrámɔ nɔ liwewe tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The music of harps and singers and of flutes and trumpets will no longer be heard. No workers will ever set up shop in that city, and the sound of grinding grain will be silenced forever.
translation_verse: Lɛ batránu ɔsangu no beʋéʋe ba ni badziza ba, ni ketee bememe ba, ani kukuklui yɔ bememe ba ile le ní wɔ mɛ liwewe tɔ ɔ. Lɛ batrámɔ lixwɛxwɛ lɛ baxwɛwa ba ní wɔ mɛ tɔ ɔ. Lɛ batránu lihɔkla nɛ onu ní wɔ mɛ liwewe tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: Lamps will no longer shine anywhere in Babylon, and couples will never again say wedding vows there. Her merchants ruled the earth, and by her witchcraft she fooled all nations.
translation_verse: Kani yɛ kuklekleo kutrákle ní wɔ mɛ liwewe tɔ ɔ. Lɛ batránu kunyimezeno ni kudzɛgbanɔ ole ní wɔ mɛ liwewe tɔ ɔ. Wɔ bedzigà ba ba beze kesea aʋa banɔ ʋidi ba. Gani wɔ kunɔfanɔɔ mɛ ɛ, kelea ima nɛ petee ilila lɛ kudeo.

english_verse: On the streets of Babylon is found the blood of God's people and of his prophets, and everyone else.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛmɔ beboedoplɛ ba ni baʋaziŋa ba kɔpɔni banama petee gi beye ní kesea aʋa ɛ, ba ika lɛ ni wɔ mɛ ɛ.”

english_verse: After this, I heard what sounded like a lot of voices in heaven, and they were shouting, “Praise the Lord! To our God belongs the glorious power to save,
translation_verse: Lɛ ya kede e, menu ole gagla ɔtɔ gi ɔlɛ ŋwisi linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ ní lifunu ne lɛ ole gi liikpɛ ozi lo si, “Haleluya! Kubúbú ni kusudra ani kigaglaɛ binu blɔ Aya yɛ binɛ,

english_verse: because his judgments are honest and fair. That filthy prostitute ruined the earth with shameful deeds. But God has judged her and made her pay the price for murdering his servants.”
translation_verse: lɛ ese linu si yɛ etsixwaxwa la enu anukwarɛ lɛ ɛɛgyɔ ɔ. Ebu ɔwawadze ʋidi ye lipɔ gi yɛ ezizi kesea ni yɛ kihasi ye. Eʋi yɛ banɔsumu ba ba kuwleo adɔ ni yɛ iwla mɛ.”

english_verse: Then the crowd shouted, “Praise the Lord! Smoke will never stop rising from her burning body.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkpɛ ozi lo dzɛ si, “Haleluya! Ɔdzɛɛ yɛ kunyɔɔ kwiyɔ emu ní lifunu ne tsinɔ kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ.”

english_verse: From the throne a voice said, “If you worship and fear our God, give praise to him, no matter who you are.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ole eyi dɔni likusigbo le su si, “Mlakpɔ̀ blɔ Aya, mlɔ yɛ banɔsumu ba petee, mlɔ banama gi mlezeese ye e, mlɔ banuvɔ ni betsitsi.”

english_verse: Then I heard what seemed to be a large crowd that sounded like a roaring flood and loud thunder all mixed together. They were saying, “Praise the Lord! Our Lord God All-Powerful now rules as king.
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole ɔtɔ ŋwisi linɔkpi ʋidi itɔ ile, ŋwisi inipe glegle kuni kinɛ ɛ, ani ŋwisi dzeni kugugu gagla kinɛ gi bɛkpɛ ozi lo si, “Haleluya! Lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ, blɔ Aya kigaglaɛ petee onete ɛɛŋa kukusi yo.

english_verse: So we will be glad and happy and give him praise. The wedding day of the Lamb is here, and his bride is ready.
translation_verse: Mlaki tɔ kumɔ isami tɔ kuyo, tɔ mlaki yɛ kukpɔ̀kpɔ̀! Lɛ ese linu si keʋesibi ya ka kudzɛgbanɔɔ lipoe le lina, lɛ yɛ ɔdzɛɛ gi atrágbanɔ ɛ alulɔ yɛ isu ya aki lɛ.

english_verse: She will be given a wedding dress made of pure and shining linen. This linen stands for the good things God's people have done.”
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛki yɛ aklala pititi gi eeŋle si ɔkpɛ ɛ.” Aklala pititi yɛ onu banɔdradra ba eboe gyɔgyɔ la gi bɛbitɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then the angel told me, “Put this in writing. God will bless everyone who is invited to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” The angel also said, “These things God has said are true.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi mɛ si, “Ŋwlimi si, ‘Biakrira banama gi bɛxwa si ba ba Keʋesibi ya ka kudzɛgbanɔɔ ɔdɔŋakplɔ nɔ su i!’ ” Lɛ apɔnɔ dzɛ si, “Eboa la ya enu Aya yɛ anukwarɛɛ eboe.”

english_verse: I knelt at the feet of the angel and began to worship him. But the angel said, “Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you and everyone else who tells about Jesus. Don't worship anyone but God. Everyone who tells about Jesus does it by the power of the Spirit.” The Rider on the White Horse
translation_verse: Gi menu nite e, madɔ kesea ní yɛ ikli lɛ onu, tɔ misumu ye. Pɔ̀ asi mɛ si, “Wukíbitɛ lɛ ɛ! wɔ ɔhanuglɔ ɔnɔsumu menu, wɔ ni wɔ benemi ba gi mlevu Yesu yɛ katsalia mɛ ni awla ɛ. Sù Aya! Lɛ ese linu si anukwarɛ liɛ gi Yesu abasɛ ɛ, bɛ binu kidiliɛ gi Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɔ, gi eeze ní beboedoplɛ ba mɛ, ebu ibasɛ ɛ.” Isɔ pititi yɛ ɔkpɛkpɛɛ

english_verse: I looked and saw heaven was open, and a white horse was there. Its rider was called Faithful and True, and he is always fair when he judges or goes to war.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ lifunu ne gi lidra edu u, lɛ mamɔ isɔ pititi ɔtɔ, gi yɛ ɔkpɛkpɛɛ yɛ bizɛxwa si Anukwarɛɛ onete ni ɔnɔ aʋa gi biaziŋa ɛ. Izɛxwa litsi, lɛ izɛta kigũ ní kude gyɔgyɔ aʋa.

english_verse: He had eyes like flames of fire, and he was wearing a lot of crowns. His name was written on him, but he was the only one who knew what the name meant.
translation_verse: Yɛ bimbi ʋe bilɛ ŋwisi kifu gi kiisɔ gaglaa kinɛ ɛ, lɛ ekume ekusikuto glegle ní yɛ litukpo le. Bɛŋwlimi linyi itɔ ní yɛ su gi ɔnɔnɔ óte sani yɛ ɔtutɔ ko o.

english_verse: The rider wore a robe that was covered with blood, and he was known as “The Word of God.”
translation_verse: Akpɛ liwu gi bɛkpɛ ní ika lɛ mɛ ɛ, lɛ yɛ linyi ne linu si, Aya yɛ liboe le.

english_verse: He was followed by armies from heaven that rode on white horses and were dressed in pure white linen.
translation_verse: Lɛ lifunu ne baguta ba lihakpɔ lɛ gi bɛkpɛ isɔ pitititi ʋa ɛ, balɛ ni yɛ kude, lɛ bɛkpɛ aklala pititi ewu gi la su ɛɛdra a, xe eeŋle e.

english_verse: From his mouth a sharp sword went out to attack the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod and will show the fierce anger of God All-Powerful by trampling the grapes in the pit where wine is made.
translation_verse: Lɛ dɔni yɛ onugu lo me e, ihɛ inugu tiʋa izeedo gi yɛ izɛɛkɔ ɛɛŋa kuseo aʋa a. “Lɛ aaŋa amade na aʋa ni ɔwla gagla.” Yɛ izɛɛka wain yɛ ní Aya kigaglaɛ petee onetee yɛ kusuyeʋi gaglaa, kɔ wain yɛ ɔkaklɔ.

english_verse: On the part of the robe that covered his thigh was written, “KING OF KINGS AND Lord OF LordS.”
translation_verse: Lɛ ni yɛ liwu le ni yɛ litagba nɛ su i, bɛŋwlimi linyii nɛ ya; BEKUSI BA AƲA OKUSI NI Baka Ba aʋa Ɔka!

english_verse: I then saw an angel standing on the sun, and he shouted to all the birds flying in the sky, “Come and join in God's great feast!
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ tole ɔtɔ gi ɔlɛɛ ní liwo le me e, lɛ akpɛ ozi ni ole gaglaa atsisi kudzɔʋi yɔ petee gi kuze pruru u, ní liwɔ lɛ mɛ si, “Mlakpɛ isu likpɔ kí Aya yɛ ɔkplɔ ʋidi lo,

english_verse: I also saw the beast and all kings of the earth come together. They fought against the rider on the white horse and against his army.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ ɔŋuga yɛ ni kesea aʋa bekusi ba ani ba baguta ba gi bɛkpɛ isu likpɔ ɔ, si bɛta kigũ ni ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɔlɛ ní isɔɛ aʋa a ani yɛ baguta ba.

english_verse: But the beast was captured and so was the false prophet. This is the same prophet who had worked miracles for the beast, so he could fool everyone who had the mark of the beast and worshiped the idol. The beast and the false prophet were thrown alive into a lake of burning sulfur.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ bevu ɔŋuga yɛ ni asia na oboedoplɛɛ, gi yɛ azaabitɛ inugutseboe la ní yɛ onu i. Gani eboa la ya elu me e, emeni banama gi bɛzɛ ɔŋuga yɛ lixaŋu kpɛkpɛ lɛ ɛ, xe bɛkpɛ litukpo kese ɛki yɛ litugu ne e. Bɛkɔ ba bagbagbe tiaʋa ba ɛŋwia ɛkpɛ ní lifuha lɛ gi liisɔ ni kifu yɛ akla na mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: But the rest of their army was killed by the sword that came from the mouth of the rider on the horse. Then birds stuffed themselves on the dead bodies.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkɔ ihɛ gblamadza yɛ gi izedo edɔni ɔniliɛ klɔ gi ɛɛkpɛ isɔɛ yɛ onugu lo me e, bɛkɔ yɛ eye banɔ gɔgɔ ba ɛ, lɛ kudzɔʋi yɔ petee kwizɛɛta ba kigyɛ.

english_verse: I saw thrones, and sitting on those thrones were the ones who had been given the right to judge. I also saw the souls of the people who had their heads cut off because they had told about Jesus and preached God's message. They were the same ones who had not worshiped the beast or the idol, and they had refused to let its mark be put on their foreheads or hands. They will come to life and rule with Christ for
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ ekusigbo gi banama a, gi bɛki kigagla si baxwa etsi la ɛ, ba bedi ní la aʋa. Lɛ mamɔ banama onu gi bɛti etukpo la kɔkpɛ Yesu yɛ katsalia kuŋaŋaa ani kɔkpɛ Aya yɛ liboe le loso e, ba esobo la. Ba benu banama gi bésu ɔŋuga yɛ loo yɛ etugu na a, lɛ bázɛ yɛ lixaŋu kpɛkpɛ lɛ ní ba kutu yo loo ní ba kuwlakpɔɔ ɔ. Bedzi ligbã́ lɛ bɛŋa kukusi yo ni Kristo agɔ akpe tole.

english_verse: These people are the first to be raised to life, and they are especially blessed and holy. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and Christ and will rule with them for
translation_verse: Banɔ gi bɛkrira ni banɔ dradra ba benu banama gi biamɔ ba kapɛa ní kuyɔyɔ tɔpu yɔ dɔni bekpo ʋa mɛ̀ mɛ ɛ. Kutse ʋla kutrámɔ kigagla ní ba aʋa a, boŋu i, biadzi Aya ni Kristo yɛ kusɔliɔ kɔ baka ba, lɛ biaŋa kukusi yo ni yɛ kɔki agɔ akpe tole. Bɛŋa Satana aʋa

english_verse: He will fool the countries of Gog and Magog, which are at the far ends of the earth, and their people will follow him into battle. They will have as many followers as there are grains of sand along the beach,
translation_verse: lɛ aatrɛ, tɔ yizeemeni kelea petee, dɔni kesea igó tine ne aʋa a, gi inu, Gog ani Magog, tɔ biakpɛ ba likpɔ ki kigũ. Ba gi ba kisrɛsrɛ mɛ kiaze ŋwisi axuta la siklami sɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: and they will march all the way across the earth. They will surround the camp of God's people and the city God loves. But fire will come down from heaven and destroy the whole army.
translation_verse: Bɛga klani kesea petee lɛ bɛkpɛ Aya yɛ banima ba likpɔkpɛkpɛ lɛ ni yɛ ɔma ʋidi lo gi alɔ ni kusi yo e. Pɔ̀ kifu yɛ kidɔ ní lifunu ne lɛ kiba ɛkani ba ɛ.

english_verse: Then the devil who fooled them will be thrown into the lake of fire and burning sulfur. He will be there with the beast and the false prophet, and they will be in pain day and night forever and ever. The Judgment at the Great White Throne
translation_verse: Lɛ bevu Ozimizi ye gi yɛ emeni ba ɛ, ɛŋwia ɛkpɛ ní lifuha ɛlɛ gi liisɔ mɛ ɛ. Niklɔ gi bɛŋwia ɔŋuga yɛ ni asia na oboedoplɛɛ ɛkpɛ ɛ, lɛ biawa ba nyɔnyɔɛ elesi ni kemize tsini kanɔmɛ anɔmɛ ɛ. Aya axwa litsi ni bekpo ʋa

english_verse: I saw a great white throne with someone sitting on it. Earth and heaven tried to run away, but there was no place for them to go.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ likusigbo pititi ʋidi itɔ ni ɔniliɛ gi odi ní lɛ aʋa ɛ. Lifune ni kesea bise ní yɛ aŋu na mɛ, lɛ bámɔ ʋɛ tɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I also saw all the dead people standing in front of that throne. Every one of them was there, no matter who they had once been. Several books were opened, and then the book of life was opened. The dead were judged by what those books said they had done.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ bekpo ʋa, banɔtsitsi ni banuvɔ, gi balɛ ní likusigbo le ɔtɔ nɔ ɛ, lɛ bɛdra kuplekpaa ɛ. Lɛ bɛdra keplekpa dzedze gi ka kenu ligbã nɛ keplekpaa. Lɛ bɛxwa litsi ni bekpo ʋa ní kidiliɛ gi bɛŋwlimi i kpɛ ní kuplekpa liɔ klɔ mɛ ní ba axwɛwawa la su u.

english_verse: The sea gave up the dead people who were in it, and death and its kingdom also gave up their dead. Then everyone was judged by what they had done.
translation_verse: Lɛ axu la ebú banama ma gi bɛwɔ ní la mɛ ɛkɔ, lɛ kutseo ni bekpo ʋa mɛ kyɛ bebu banama gi balɛ ní bɛ mɛ ɛkɔ ɔ, lɛ bɛxwa litsi ni ɔnɔ kakaa ní kidiliɛ gi abitɛ ɛ onu.

english_verse: Afterwards, death and its kingdom were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death.
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkɔ kutseo ni bekpo ʋa mɛ ɛŋwia ɛkpɛ ní lifuha lɛ mɛ ɛ. Lifuha ɛlɛ ya linu kutse ʋlaa.

english_verse: Anyone whose name wasn't written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
translation_verse: Lɛ xegi báŋwlimi ɔnutɔ yɛ linyi ne akpɛ ní ligbã́ nɛ keplekpaa mɛ ɛ, kɔ bɛŋwia yɛ ɛkpɛ ní lifuha lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The first heaven and the first earth had disappeared, and so had the sea.
translation_verse: Lɛ mamɔ lifunu vuvu ni kese vuvu, lɛ ese linu si lifunu tɔpu nɛ ni kese tɔpu ya bɛ onu biza, lɛ axu la amá tɔɔ ɔ.

english_verse: I heard a loud voice shout from the throne: God's home is now with his people. He will live with them, and they will be his own. Yes, God will make his home among his people.
translation_verse: Lɛ menu ole gagla ɔtɔ ɛdɔni likusigbo le su si “Abla ɛ, Aya yɛ ozeklɔ ɔlɛ ní banima kusi lɛ yɛ aaze ní basu. Biaze yɛ banɔ, lɛ Aya ɔtutɔ lɔ aaze ní ba su lɛ aaze ba Aya.

english_verse: He will wipe all tears from their eyes, and there will be no more death, suffering, crying, or pain. These things of the past are gone forever.
translation_verse: Lɛ aatsoli kube kakaa kube ni ba aŋu mɛ. Kutse loo awowoe la kuwawa loo kubemi loo kuyeʋiyeʋi kútraze tɔɔ ɔ, lɛ ese linu si eboe kokoo la ɛɛza.”

english_verse: Then the one sitting on the throne said: I am making everything new. Write down what I have said. My words are true and can be trusted.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi odi ní likusigbo le aʋa ɛ, asi mɛ si, “Mɛɛbitɛ kidɔ kakaa si kidzi kivuvu u!” Lɛ apani si, “Ŋwlimi liboe lɛ ya plɛ, lɛ ese linu si eboe ala ya enu anukwarɛ eboe ni eboe gi biazɛ la aʋa ŋa a.”

english_verse: Everything is finished! I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will freely give water from the life-giving fountain to everyone who is thirsty.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi mɛ si, “Bɛbitɛ lɛ pɔ́! Mɛ menu Alfa ni Omega, gi lɛ ese linu si okpese klɔ ni ɔgù klɔ. Ɔniliɛ gi olile eeku i, maaki yɛ tɔ aaŋwɛ kuni liʋɛmotsa dɔni ligba nɛ kuni yo kɔ linitɔ lɛ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: All who win the victory will be given these blessings. I will be their God, and they will be my people.
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi aŋa aʋa ɛ, aazɛ kaŋase alia ya, lɛ maaze yɛ Aya, lɛ yɛ aaze mobi i.

english_verse: But I will tell you what will happen to cowards and to everyone who is unfaithful or dirty-minded or who murders or is sexually immoral or uses witchcraft or worships idols or tells lies. They will be thrown into that lake of fire and burning sulfur. This is the second death. The New Jerusalem
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ befufluse ba ni banama gi báziŋa a ani banama gi bizɛbitɛ anyaniboe la a, bakpɛnɛ ba, banama gi bizɛbitɛ ádzɛboe la ni enyimeboe la a, banama gi bizɛɛbitɛ ehwiboe la a, banama gi bizeesumu babu ʋa a, kɔpɔni basia nɛ ma petee ba ozeklɔ onu lifuha lɛ gi liisɔ ni kifu yɛ akla na a mɛ. Lɛ ya linu kutse ʋlaa a.”

english_verse: I saw one of the seven angels who had the bowls filled with the seven last terrible troubles. The angel came to me and said, “Come on! I will show you the one who will be the bride and wife of the Lamb.”
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ tiegloele ba, gi ba awla kubɔziʋi tugloeleo o, gi kwiyi ní baxixa tiegloele gɔgɔ ʋa ba mɛ tole aba, lɛ apani nì mɛ si, “Ba, tɔ mibasɛ wɔ ɔdzɛɛ gi bɛkpɛ kuwlawlaa gi onu Keʋesibi ya yedze e.”

english_verse: Then with the help of the Spirit, he took me to the top of a very high mountain. There he showed me the holy city of Jerusalem coming down from God in heaven.
translation_verse: Lɛ efeke me ní Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ mɛ atsini lito ʋidi gyɔgyɔ ɛtɔ aʋa ɛ, lɛ ebu ɔma dradra nɔ, Yerusalem, gi eeple dɔni lifunu ne ní Aya klɔ ɛ, abasɛ mɛ.

english_verse: The glory of God made the city bright. It was dazzling and crystal clear like a precious jasper stone.
translation_verse: Eekle ni Aya yɛ kusudraa, lɛ lɔ kuklekleo kulɛ ŋwisi líhuiʋi zi kedziatɔ kinɛ ɛ, ŋwisi yaspis likla, gi lɛ mɛ lidra ŋwisi azinzi kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The city had a high and thick wall with twelve gates, and each one of them was guarded by an angel. On each of the gates was written the name of one of the twelve tribes of Israel.
translation_verse: Ligli gyɔgyɔ ʋidi itɔ liklani lɔ, lɛ kuvunu liɔfɔ ni tuʋa kulɛ ní lɔ su, lɛ Aya yɛ baxwɛdzɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa balɛɛ ní lɔ kuvunu yo o. Bɛŋwlimi Israɛl bebi ʋa ba iku liɔfɔ ni tiʋa lɛ enyina ní lɔ kuvunu yo.

english_verse: Three of these gates were on the east, three were on the north, three more were on the south, and the other three were on the west.
translation_verse: Kuvunu tuta kulɛ ní kewetu ya kapɛa, tuta ni kaʋaa, tuta ní kalaa ni tuta ní keweku ya.

english_verse: The city was built on twelve foundation stones. On each of the stones was written the name of one of the Lamb's twelve apostles.
translation_verse: Ɔma ʋidi no lɔ egli la alɛɛ ní ixɔ liɔfɔ ni tiʋa aʋa, lɛ ni ixɔ liɔfɔ ni tiʋa lɛ aʋa ɛ, bɛŋwlimi Keʋesibi ya ka banɔkpɛkpɛ liɔfɔ ni tiaʋa ba enyi na ɛtrɔ ɔ.

english_verse: The angel who spoke to me had a gold measuring stick to measure the city and its gates and its walls.
translation_verse: Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛ liɛ gi apani ni mɛ ɛ, evu sika kigyigyeseʋi gi atrákɔ egyigyi ɔma ʋidi no ni lɔ kuvunu yo ni lɔ egli la a.

english_verse: The city was shaped like a cube, because it was just as high as it was wide. When the angel measured the city, it was about
translation_verse: Ɔma nɔ lɔ kidzedzee me ni lɔ kinenee me bisɔ̀. Egyigyi ɔma nɔ nì kigyigyeseʋi ye lɛ bize itiplɛkɔ akpe tole ni alafa tietsu; lɔ kidzedzee me ni kinenee me ani lɔ kimumu ye me bisɔ.

english_verse: The wall was built of jasper, and the city was made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Lɛ kidiliɛ gi bɛkɔ ɛtrɔ ligli le e, kinu yaspis likla, lɛ ɔma nɔ onu sika dradra yɛ, gi yɛ mɛ adra ŋwisi azinzi yɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Each of the twelve foundations was a precious stone. The first was jasper, the second was sapphire, the third was agate, the fourth was emerald,
translation_verse: Lɛ bɛkɔ akla zikedzi ede ede dzedze la, ɛkɔ ɛlulɔ ɔma nɔ lɔ ɔxɔ lɔ, gi lɔ aʋa egli la alɛ ɛ. Ɔxɔ kpɛkpɛ tɔpu nɔ onu yaspis, ɔʋla lɔ onu safir, ɔtanɛ nɔ onu xalkedon, onene no onu smaragd,

english_verse: the fifth was onyx, the sixth was carnelian, the seventh was chrysolite, the eighth was beryl, the ninth was topaz, the tenth was chrysoprase, the eleventh was jacinth, and the twelfth was amethyst.
translation_verse: otsunɛ nɔ onu sardonik, oglonɛ nɔ onu kanelia, ogloele lo onu krisolit, ɔgɔtɔʋa yɛ onu beril, ɔgɔtolee onu topas, liɔfɔɛ onu krisopras, liɔfɔ ni tolee onu hiakint, liɔfɔ ni ɔʋlaɛ onu ametis.

english_verse: Each of the twelve gates was a solid pearl. The streets of the city were made of pure gold, clear as crystal.
translation_verse: Lɛ kuvunu liɔfɔ ni tuʋa kunu ehuʋi pititi liɔfɔ ni taʋa, kevunu kakaa kenu lihuʋi pititi tile, lɛ ɔma nɔ ablɔ ʋa ɛ, benu sika gbã yɛ gi yɛ mɛ adra ŋwisi azinzi mɛ kinɛ ɛ.

english_verse: I did not see a temple there. The Lord God All-Powerful and the Lamb were its temple.
translation_verse: Lɛ mámɔ lisɔligba ɛ tɔtɔ ní lɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ese linu si Ɔka Aya, kigaglaɛ petee onetee ni Keʋesibi ya ba benu ba lisɔligba lɛ.

english_verse: And the city did not need the sun or the moon. The glory of God was shining on it, and the Lamb was its light.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɔma nɔ ɔ́ziã liwo le loo ɔdzidzi yɛ, tɔ aakle ɔbali ní lɔ aʋa, lɛ ese linu si Aya yɛ kusudraa kuzɛɛki lɔ kuklekle o, lɛ Keʋesibi ya kenu lɔ kani yɛ.

english_verse: Nations will walk by the light of that city, and kings will bring their riches there.
translation_verse: Lɛ kesea aʋa banima biaga ní lɔ kuklekleo onu u, lɛ kesea aʋa bekusi ba biakɔ ba exo la ni ba kusudraa mani lɔ mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Its gates are always open during the day, and night never comes.
translation_verse: Lɛ batráda lɔ ipupo le liwewe lɛ ese linu si kele kátrablo ní niklɔ ɔ,

english_verse: The glorious treasures of nations will be brought into the city.
translation_verse: Kesea aʋa banima biamani ba exo la ni ba kubùbù yo ní lɔ mɛ.

english_verse: But nothing unworthy will be allowed to enter. No one who is dirty-minded or who tells lies will be there. Only those whose names are written in the Lamb's book of life will be in the city.
translation_verse: Lɛ kidɔkakaa gi kɛsu kídra ɛ, kítrati iku ní lɔ mɛ ɛ, lɛ ɔnɔ gi izɛɛbitɛ anyaniboe loo izɛɛsɛ asia kyɛ ɔ́trátiiku isani banama tètè gi ba enyi na akpasi ní Keʋesibi ya ka ligbã́ nɛ keplekpaa mɛ ɛ.

english_verse: The angel showed me a river that was crystal clear, and its waters gave life. The river came from the throne where God and the Lamb were seated.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ abasi mɛ onipe gi lɔ kuni yo kuzɛɛkɔ ligbã́ nɛ ɛ, gi ɛɛtsi kifu yɛ ŋwisi azinzi kinɛ ɛ, gi ɛɛkpɛ dɔni Aya ni Keʋesibi ya ba likusigbo le su i.

english_verse: Then it flowed down the middle of the city's main street. On each side of the river are trees that grow a different kind of fruit each month of the year. The fruit gives life, and the leaves are used as medicine to heal the nations.
translation_verse: Lɛ ɛɛkpɛ ɛza ni ɔma ʋidi no lɔ ablɔ ʋidi ye yɛ kusio e. Lɛ ní onipe lo ige tiʋa lɛ aʋa ɛ, ligbã́ nɛ ose lo ɔlɛɛ, gi izeeyo lɔ esebi la eyo liɔfɔ ni taʋa ligɔ kakaa, tule ni ɔdzidzi kakaa mɛ ɛ. Lɛ ose lo lɔ awlakpa la ɛ, enu kuwa gi kwiaki amade na petee e aayɔ dɔni ba bànya ʋa mɛ.

english_verse: God's curse will no longer be on the people of that city. He and the Lamb will be seated there on their thrones, and its people will worship God
translation_verse: Lɛ ifixoxo etɔ́tɔ itráze tɔɔ ɔ, lɛ Aya ni Keʋesibi ya ba likusigbo le liaze ní ɔma ʋidi no me e, lɛ yɛ banɔsumu ba biasumu ye.

english_verse: and will see him face to face. God's name will be written on the foreheads of the people.
translation_verse: Biamɔ yɛ aŋu na mɛ, lɛ yɛ linyi ne liaze ní ba kutu yo.

english_verse: Never again will night appear, and no one who lives there will ever need a lamp or the sun. The Lord God will be their light, and they will rule forever. The Coming of Christ
translation_verse: Kele katráblo tɔɔ, lɛ batrázia kani kuklekle loo liwo le kuklekleo tɔɔ, lɛ ese linu si Ɔkaɛ Aya aaki ba kuklekle o. Lɛ biaŋa kukusi yo dɔni kanɔmɛ tsinɔ kanɔmɛ ɛ.

english_verse: Then I was told: These words are true and can be trusted. The Lord God controls the spirits of his prophets, and he is the one who sent his angel to show his servants what must happen very soon.
translation_verse: Lɛ Aya yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ asi mɛ si, “Eboa la ya ɛ, enu eboe aʋa gi biazɛ ŋa a, lɛ enu anukwarɛ eboe. Lɛ Ɔkaɛ Aya, gi yɛ izɛki yɛ beboedoplɛ ba yɛ Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ɛ, yɛ akpɛ yɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ tɔ obu eboa la gi ɛtrába la mɛ kpekpemii basɛ yɛ banɔsumu ba a.” Yesu atrába

english_verse: Remember, I am coming soon! God will bless everyone who pays attention to the message of this book.
translation_verse: “Dike, matraba kpekpemi i! Biakrira ɔniliɛ gi evu keplekpaa lia ya mɛ eboedoplɛ la ní awla ɛ.”

english_verse: My name is John, and I am the one who heard and saw these things. Then after I had heard and seen all this, I knelt down and began to worship at the feet of the angel who had shown it to me.
translation_verse: Mɛ Yohanes mɛ menu ɔniliɛ gi enu u, xe amɔ eboa la ya a. Lɛ gi menu u, xe mamɔ na ɛ, madɔ ní kesea ní ɔxwɛdzɛɛ ikli lɛ onu tɔ misù ye ɔniliɛ gi yɛ ɛbasɛ mɛ bidɔ bɛ ya ɛ.

english_verse: But the angel said, Don't do that! I am a servant, just like you. I am the same as a follower or a prophet or anyone else who obeys what is written in this book. God is the one you should worship.
translation_verse: Pɔ̀ asi mɛ si, “Wukíbitɛ lɛ ɛ, mɛ kyɛ wɔ ɔhanuglɔ ɔnɔsumu menu, mɛ ni wɔ benemi ba beboedoplɛ ba ani banama gi bevu keplekpaa lia ya mɛ eboe la ní awla ɛ. Sù Aya!.”

english_verse: Don't keep the prophecies in this book a secret. These things will happen soon.
translation_verse: Lɛ asi mɛ si, “Wukítsi eboa la gi akpasi ní kuboedoplɛ keplekpaa lia ya onu u, lɛ ese linu si lipoe le lina pɔ́.

english_verse: Evil people will keep on being evil, and everyone who is dirty-minded will still be dirty-minded. But good people will keep on doing right, and God's people will always be holy.
translation_verse: Mlaki si ɔniliɛ gi izɛbitɛ eboe zimizi i, ɔtrɛ ní yɛ eboe zimizi na aʋa a. Mlaki si ɔniliɛ gi izɛbitɛ anyaniboe ɔtrɛ ní yɛ anyaniboe la aʋa a. Mlaki si ɔniliɛ gi yɛ bede ʋa bɛɛgyɔ ɛ, oze ní yɛ kude gyɔgyɔɔ aʋa. Mlaki si ɔniliɛ gi adra ɛ, ɔtrɛ ni yɛ kudradraa aʋa a.”

english_verse: Then I was told: I am coming soon! And when I come, I will reward everyone for what they have done.
translation_verse: “Dike, matraba kpekpemii, lɛ matsini mɛ liʋɛ nɛ ɛmanɔ, lɛ maatsa liʋɛ ki ɔnɔ kakaa ní kidiliɛ gi abitɛ onu u.

english_verse: I am Alpha and Omega, the first and the last, the beginning and the end.
translation_verse: Mɛ menu Alfa ni Omega, Ɔtɔpu yɛ ni Ɔgɔgɔ yɛ, Okpese klɔ ni Ɔgù klɔ.

english_verse: God will bless all who have washed their robes. They will each have the right to eat fruit from the tree that gives life, and they can enter the gates of the city.
translation_verse: “Biakrira banama gi beplo ba ewu la, tɔ biamɔ kude tɔ biaŋa ligbã́ nɛ ose lo lɔ biyoyo ʋe, ani tɔ biaga kuvunu yo tɔ biati uku ní ɔma ʋidi no me e.

english_verse: But outside the city will be dogs, witches, immoral people, murderers, idol worshipers, and everyone who loves to tell lies and do wrong.
translation_verse: Banama gi balɛ ní ɔma ʋidi no kede e, ba benu kudroʋio, behwinete ba, banama gi bizɛbitɛ enyimeboe ni ádzɛboe, banama gi bizeye banɔ ɔ, banama gi bizesu babu ʋa a, kɔpɔni banama onu gi asia na izeeze ɔgyi i, xe bizɛɛsɛ asia na a.

english_verse: I am Jesus! And I am the one who sent my angel to tell all of you these things for the churches. I am David's Great Descendant, and I am also the bright morning star.
translation_verse: “Mɛ Yesu mɛ menu ɔniliɛ gi makpɛ mɛ ɔxwɛdzɛɛ si obu eboa la ya basɛ wɔ, tɔ wɔaŋa katsalia ki isɔliha lɛ ɛ. Mɛ menu Dawid yɛ ogù no ni yɛ osobi ye ani livlɛ lɛ ɔdzidziobi ye gi eekle e.

english_verse: The Spirit and the bride say, “Come!” Everyone who hears this should say, “Come!” If you are thirsty, come! If you want life-giving water, come and take it. It's free!
translation_verse: Ɔfuɛfuɛ nɔ ni ɔdzɛɛ gi bɛgbanɔ ɛ, bɛpani si, “Ba!” Lɛ ɔniliɛ gi eenu i, ɔpani si “Ba!” Ɔniliɛ gi olile eeku i, ɔba, ɔniliɛ gi apɛ́ ɛ, ɔzɛ ligbã́ nɛ kuni yo liʋɛmɔtsa. Ɔgù klɔ

english_verse: Here is my warning for everyone who hears the prophecies in this book: If you add anything to them, God will make you suffer all the terrible troubles written in this book.
translation_verse: Mɛɛgba ɔnɔ kakaa gi eenu kuboedoplɛ uliɔ ɔ, gi kulɛ ní keplekpaa lia mɛ si i: xe ɔnutɔ akɔ kiditɔ apɔni eboa la ya ɛ, Aya aakɔ̀ baxixa ʋa su gi bɛŋwlimi bidɔ mɛ ní keplekpaa lia ya mɛ ɛ, aakɔ̀ pɔnɔ ɔki yɛ.

english_verse: If you take anything away from these prophecies, God will not let you have part in the life-giving tree and in the holy city described in this book.
translation_verse: Lɛ xe ɔnutɔ aabu eboe atɔ ní kuboedoplɛ uliɔ gi kukpasi ní keplekpa lia ya mɛ ɛ, kɔlɔ Aya aabu yɛ ɔŋa klɔ kapɛa dɔni ligbã́ nɛ ose lo ní ɔma dradra lɔ gi bɛŋwlimi kpɛ ní keplekpaa lia ya mɛ ɛ, mɛ.

english_verse: The one who has spoken these things says, “I am coming soon!” So, Lord Jesus, please come soon!
translation_verse: Ɔniliɛ gi ɛɛŋa katsalia ɛki eboa la ya ɛ, apani si; “Ee, matraba kpekpemi!” Atɔ liba lɛ mɛ. Ɔkaɛ Yesu ba!

english_verse: I pray that the Lord Jesus will be kind to all of you.
translation_verse: Ɔkaɛ Yesu Kristo yɛ lipomepɛpɛ nɛ lize ni Aya yɛ banima. Tɔ liba lɛ mɛ.
